Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Waste Management - Version 1'
}

Table customer.customer_account {
  customer_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the Waste Management data platform. Primary key for the customer domain master entity.']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: Customer accounts that are EPA-registered hazmat generators require direct link for RCRA compliance tracking, manifest generation authority, biennial reporting, and regulatory status monitoring. Essen']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Commercial waste accounts require dedicated account managers for contract renewals, service optimization, rate negotiations, and billing escalations. Critical for customer retention, upsell opportunit']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Hazardous waste generator accounts enroll in specific safety programs (RCRA, DOT hazmat). Business need: Program eligibility verification, compliance tracking, regulatory reporting. Domain experts exp']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized account_segment STRING field to FK reference to customer_segment table. This is the primary segment classification for customer accounts and should reference the authoritative s']
  waste_generator_class_waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Customer accounts are classified by EPA waste generator class (LQG/SQG/CESQG) which directly determines eligible waste streams for regulatory compliance, service offerings, manifest requirements, and ']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the customer account. For residential accounts this is the householder full name; for commercial and municipal accounts this is the registered business or entity name. Used for billing, contracts, and correspondence.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable account number assigned to the customer by Waste Management. Used on invoices, service agreements, and customer communications. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer account. Prospect = pre-service onboarding; Active = services being rendered; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., non-payment, seasonal hold); Inactive = no current services but record retained; Closed = account permanently terminated and archived.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Customer value tier assigned by Waste Management based on revenue contribution, contract size, and strategic importance. Drives service priority, dedicated account management assignment, and pricing concessions. Strategic tier typically reserved for large municipal contracts and national commercial accounts.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the customer account indicating the nature of the customer relationship. Drives service offerings, pricing tiers, regulatory requirements, and reporting segmentation. Residential = B2C households; Commercial = B2B businesses; Industrial = large-volume generators; Municipal/Government = public sector contracts.']
  autopay_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is enrolled in automatic payment processing. When True, invoices are automatically charged to the customer\'s payment instrument on the due date via Oracle Revenue Management.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for invoice and billing correspondence delivery. Populated only when billing address differs from service address. Used by Oracle Revenue Management for invoice generation and AR collections.']
  billing_address_same_as_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s billing address is identical to the service address. When True, billing correspondence is sent to the service address. When False, a separate billing address is maintained in the billing system.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City for the customer\'s billing address. Used for invoice delivery and accounts receivable (AR) correspondence when billing address differs from service address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the customer\'s billing address. Used for invoice delivery and tax rate determination in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the customer\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and AR correspondence routing.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was permanently closed and all services terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis, win-back campaigns, and regulatory record retention compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the Waste Management data platform. Represents the record audit trail creation event. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable (AR) balance permitted for this customer account before service suspension is triggered. Expressed in USD. Set by the credit management team in Oracle Revenue Management based on payment history and account tier.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit standing of the customer account as assessed by Waste Management\'s accounts receivable (AR) and collections team. Good = no outstanding issues; Watch = elevated risk monitoring; Hold = service may be suspended pending payment; Collections = account referred to collections; Write_off = balance deemed uncollectable. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management.']
  customer_since_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was first activated and services commenced. Represents the start of the customer relationship lifecycle. Used for tenure analysis, loyalty programs, and contract renewal planning.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) or Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifying the primary industry of the commercial or industrial customer. Used for waste stream profiling, regulatory compliance determination (e.g., RCRA hazardous waste generator status), and market analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), data synchronization with source systems, and audit trail compliance.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to receive marketing and promotional communications from Waste Management, including service upgrade offers, sustainability program invitations, and recycling education materials. Managed per CAN-SPAM and CCPA consent requirements.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from the customer, ranging from 0 (extremely unlikely to recommend) to 10 (extremely likely to recommend). Promoters = 9-10; Passives = 7-8; Detractors = 0-6. Used for customer satisfaction analytics and service quality improvement initiatives.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this customer account. Used to determine survey recency and schedule follow-up surveys per the customer satisfaction program cadence.']
  paperless_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted into electronic (paperless) billing. When True, invoices are delivered via email or the Waste Logics customer portal rather than physical mail. Supports Waste Management\'s sustainability initiatives.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms governing when invoices are due for this customer account. Net_15/30/45/60 = days from invoice date; Prepaid = payment required before service; COD = cash on delivery. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management and SAP S/4HANA SD module.']
  portal_access_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has active access to the Waste Logics self-service customer portal for online service scheduling, container management, invoice viewing, and service history review.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred language for communications, invoices, and service notifications. Stored as IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, es-MX). Used to route multilingual customer service interactions and generate localized documents.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred method for remitting payment on invoices. ACH = automated clearing house bank transfer; Autopay = recurring automatic charge; Invoice = paper or electronic invoice with manual payment. Stored in Oracle Revenue Management. Actual payment instrument details are NOT stored here per PCI DSS compliance.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary point of contact for the customer account. For residential accounts this is the account holder; for commercial accounts this is the designated account manager or facility contact. Used for service communications, billing inquiries, and contract management.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the customer account used for billing notifications, service alerts, and marketing communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics customer portal. Subject to opt-in/opt-out preferences.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the customer account used for service scheduling, driver communication, and customer service interactions. Stored in E.164 international format. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics.']
  salesforce_account_reference varchar [note: 'Integration key linking this customer account to the corresponding Account record in Salesforce CRM. Used for bi-directional synchronization of customer profile, service requests, and contract data between the data platform and Salesforce.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'Customer master number assigned in SAP S/4HANA (SD and FI/CO modules). Used for order-to-cash processing, accounts receivable management, and financial reporting. Links this customer account to SAP billing documents and general ledger (GL) postings.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was most recently placed in suspended status, typically due to non-payment or service dispute. Null for accounts not currently or previously suspended. Used for AR collections workflow and service reinstatement tracking.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the most recent account suspension. Non_payment = outstanding AR balance; Customer_request = voluntary hold; Regulatory_hold = compliance investigation; Dispute = service or billing dispute; Seasonal = temporary seasonal service pause.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account holds a valid tax exemption certificate, exempting them from applicable sales or service taxes on waste management invoices. Typically applies to government, non-profit, and certain municipal accounts. Validated against exemption certificate records in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  tax_exemption_number varchar [note: 'Official tax exemption certificate number issued by the relevant tax authority for accounts with tax_exempt = True. Used to substantiate tax-exempt billing treatment during audits. Null for non-exempt accounts.']
  waste_generator_class varchar [note: 'EPA Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) hazardous waste generator classification for the customer account. LQG = Large Quantity Generator (>1,000 kg/month); SQG = Small Quantity Generator (100-1,000 kg/month); VSQG = Very Small Quantity Generator (<100 kg/month); Non_generator = no regulated hazardous waste. Determines regulatory compliance obligations and service requirements.']
  waste_logics_customer_reference varchar [note: 'Integration key linking this customer account to the corresponding customer record in the Waste Logics customer portal. Enables self-service scheduling, container management, and service history access for the customer.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all customer accounts served by Waste Management, encompassing residential households, commercial businesses, industrial clients, and municipal/government entities. Stores account identity, classification (B2C residential, B2B commercial, government), account status (prospect, active, suspended, inactive, closed), credit standing, account tier, NPS score, customer since date, preferred language, and integration keys to Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics portal. This is the master entity for the customer domain.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each contact record in the Waste Management customer domain. Primary key for the contact data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent customer account this contact is associated with. Links the contact to the customer master record in the customer domain.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized account_segment STRING field (semantic equivalent to customer_segment) to FK reference. Contacts should derive segment classification from their associated customer account via ']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts receive communications and can authorize service changes. Inactive contacts are retained for historical reference. Do_not_contact enforces opt-out compliance. Deceased and unverified statuses support data quality management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact record was first created in the Waste Management data platform (Silver layer). Represents the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit the contact belongs to within their company (e.g., Facilities, Finance, Operations, Environmental Health & Safety). Supports routing of service and billing inquiries to the correct team.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact is registered on the National Do Not Call Registry or has explicitly requested no outbound telemarketing calls. True = do not call for marketing purposes. Enforced by the Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM outbound dialing workflows.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has consented to receive marketing and promotional email communications from Waste Management. True = opted in; False = opted out or never consented. Distinct from transactional/service emails which are sent regardless of this flag.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact. Used for transmission of formal documents such as service agreements, hazardous waste manifests, and regulatory compliance correspondence where fax remains a legally accepted delivery method.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given (first) name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communications, billing correspondence, and service notifications.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive billing-related communications including invoices, payment reminders, Accounts Receivable (AR) notices, and rate change notifications. Supports Oracle Revenue Management billing workflow routing.']
  is_emergency_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should be notified in emergency situations such as hazardous waste spills, service safety incidents, or after-hours urgent service needs. Emergency contacts are prioritized in incident escalation workflows managed through Enviance EHS.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the customer account. Each customer account should have exactly one primary contact. Used to determine the default recipient for account-level communications when no specific role routing applies.']
  is_service_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive service-related communications including collection schedule notifications, service disruption alerts, Work Order (WO) confirmations, and container delivery/pickup notices.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Facilities Manager, Environmental Compliance Officer, Accounts Payable Specialist). Particularly relevant for commercial and municipal accounts.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family (last) name of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence and account management.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the contact\'s mailing address for physical correspondence including paper billing statements, regulatory notices, and formal contract documents. May differ from the service address on the customer account.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the contact\'s mailing address (suite, apartment, floor, unit number, or care-of designation). Supplements mailing_address_line1 for complete address formatting.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s mailing address. Used for physical mail delivery and geographic segmentation of contact communications.']
  mailing_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the contact\'s mailing address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Supports international commercial and industrial accounts and determines applicable privacy regulation framework.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the contact\'s mailing address. Used for mail delivery, geographic market analysis, and routing of state-specific regulatory communications.']
  mailing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory abbreviation for the contact\'s mailing address (e.g., TX, CA, FL). Follows USPS state code standards. Used for state-level regulatory compliance routing and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile/cell phone number for the contact. Supports SMS/text message notifications for service day reminders, route change alerts, and two-factor authentication for portal access. Formatted per E.164 standard.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from this contact, on a scale of 0–10. Promoters score 9–10, Passives 7–8, Detractors 0–6. Used to measure customer satisfaction and loyalty at the contact level for targeted retention and service improvement initiatives.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response was recorded for this contact. Used to determine survey recency and eligibility for follow-up surveys per survey cadence policies.']
  portal_access_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has been granted access to the Waste Logics self-service customer portal. True = portal account exists and is active. Linked to waste_logics_user_id when enabled. Supports self-service for service scheduling, container management, and billing review.']
  portal_last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the contact\'s most recent login to the Waste Logics customer portal. Used to identify inactive portal users, drive re-engagement campaigns, and support portal access audits. Null if the contact has never logged in.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Contact\'s stated preference for how Waste Management should reach them. Drives communication channel selection for service notifications, billing statements, and marketing communications. Supports Customer Relationship Management (CRM) personalization and compliance with communication preferences.']
  preferred_contact_time timestamp [note: 'Contact\'s preferred time window for receiving outbound calls or communications from Waste Management. Supports compliance with TCPA restrictions and improves customer satisfaction by respecting contact preferences.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., en, es, fr, zh-CN). Drives localization of billing statements, service notifications, and customer portal content.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the contact. Used for billing notifications, service confirmations, account alerts, and Customer Relationship Management (CRM) communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact Email field.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for reaching the contact. Formatted per E.164 international standard. Used for service scheduling confirmations, billing inquiries, and emergency notifications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact Phone field.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role this contact plays on the customer account. Determines routing of communications and authorizations: primary (main account holder), billing (receives invoices and AR communications), service (receives service notifications and scheduling), emergency (after-hours escalation), authorized_representative (can act on behalf of account holder).']
  salesforce_contact_reference varchar [note: 'External system identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver layer and the Salesforce Contact object.']
  secondary_email varchar [note: 'Alternate email address for the contact. Used as a fallback communication channel when the primary email is undeliverable or for routing specific communication types (e.g., service alerts to a work email, billing to a personal email).']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has provided express written consent to receive SMS/text message communications from Waste Management, including service reminders, route change alerts, and promotional messages. Required for TCPA compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during master data management, and audit traceability. Salesforce CRM is the primary source for commercial contacts; Waste Logics for portal-registered residential contacts.']
  title varchar [note: 'Honorific or salutation prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal written communications and billing correspondence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact record was most recently modified in the Waste Management data platform. Tracks the latest change for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact\'s identity and contact information was most recently verified. Used to enforce re-verification schedules and identify stale contact records requiring data quality remediation.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Identity and contact information verification status for this contact record. Verified = contact details confirmed via outbound validation or portal registration. Unverified = data entered but not yet confirmed. Pending = verification in progress. Failed = verification attempt unsuccessful. Supports data quality management and fraud prevention.']
  waste_logics_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the corresponding user account in the Waste Logics customer portal. Populated when the contact has registered for self-service portal access. Null if no portal account exists.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Business/office telephone number for the contact, including extension if applicable. Primarily used for commercial and municipal account contacts where direct-dial office numbers differ from mobile numbers.']
  work_phone_extension varchar [note: 'Telephone extension number associated with the work phone for the contact. Required for large commercial and municipal accounts where contacts are reached via a main switchboard.']

  Note: 'Individual persons associated with a customer account — primary account holders, billing contacts, service contacts, authorized representatives, and emergency contacts. Captures full name, title, role type (billing, service, primary, emergency), phone numbers, email addresses, preferred contact method, communication opt-in/opt-out flags, and portal login linkage. Supports both residential single-contact households and commercial multi-contact accounts. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object and Waste Logics portal user records.'
}

Table customer.service_address {
  service_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each physical service address where waste collection or environmental services are rendered. Primary key for the service_address entity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that owns or is associated with this service address. A single account may have multiple service addresses (e.g., a commercial chain with multiple sites). Populated from Salesforce CRM account object or Waste Logics customer record.']
  designated_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Service addresses are assigned a designated disposal facility (landfill, MRF, transfer station) where collected waste is delivered. Route planning, tipping fee calculation, and logistics optimization ']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Service addresses have documented site hazards (aggressive animals, low clearances, unstable ground, traffic patterns). Business need: Pre-route hazard communication, driver safety briefings, risk ass']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: Service addresses are physical generator sites requiring EPA ID registration, site-specific RCRA permits, accumulation area compliance, and emergency response planning. Critical for site-level regulat']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Service addresses require site-specific Job Hazard Analyses for collection operations (confined spaces, overhead hazards, access restrictions). Business need: Route planning, pre-shift crew safety bri']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Site-specific permits (on-site treatment, storage facilities, transfer stations) are issued to physical locations, not accounts. Service authorization, route planning, and regulatory inspections requi']
  primary_waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Service addresses must track primary waste stream for route planning, container type assignment, vehicle equipment requirements, and regulatory compliance. Drives operational decisions on collection f']
  recycling_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.recycling_program. Business justification: Service addresses participate in specific recycling programs based on municipality, franchise agreement, and service area. Required for routing, material acceptance rules, and municipal contract compl']
  access_code varchar [note: 'Gate code, keypad entry code, or access credential required to enter the service location (e.g., gated community code, commercial facility access PIN). Classified as confidential due to security sensitivity. Stored in Waste Logics and AMCS Platform for driver dispatch.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the physical service location, including street number and street name (e.g., \'1234 Industrial Blvd\'). Used for route planning, driver navigation, and service dispatch.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the service location, capturing suite numbers, building identifiers, unit numbers, or additional location descriptors (e.g., \'Suite 200\', \'Building C\', \'Dock 4\').']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service address. \'active\' indicates services are currently rendered; \'inactive\' indicates no current services; \'pending_validation\' indicates address requires verification; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily halted; \'demolished\' indicates physical location no longer exists.']
  address_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of postal address validation against USPS or third-party address verification service. \'validated\' confirms the address is deliverable and standardized; \'failed\' indicates the address could not be verified; \'overridden\' indicates a manual override was applied by an authorized user.']
  address_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the service address was last verified against USPS or third-party address validation service. Used to track address data quality and trigger re-validation workflows when addresses become stale.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name of the physical service location. Used for jurisdiction assignment, municipal contract billing, and regulatory reporting to Local Solid Waste Authorities.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Total number of waste containers (bins, carts, dumpsters, compactors) currently assigned and active at this service address. Used for container inventory management in Waste Logics and AMCS Platform, and for capacity planning.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the service location (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\'). Supports international operations and regulatory compliance determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service address record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance. Populated by Salesforce CRM or Waste Logics at record creation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this service address record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics, AMCS Platform, SAP S/4HANA). Used for data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during integration, and Silver Layer provenance auditing.']
  diversion_program_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service address is enrolled in a waste diversion program (recycling, composting, organics collection). Diversion Rate = percentage of waste diverted from landfill. Used for sustainability reporting, EPA diversion rate calculations, and GHG (Greenhouse Gas) emissions tracking.']
  elevation_ft decimal(7,2) [note: 'Elevation of the service address above sea level in feet. Used for route planning in hilly or mountainous terrain to optimize vehicle fuel consumption and assess CNG (Compressed Natural Gas) vehicle suitability for the route.']
  external_address_reference varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identifier for this service address as assigned by the source system of record (e.g., Waste Logics site ID, AMCS location code, or SAP SD ship-to party number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.']
  franchise_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the municipal franchise or exclusive service agreement under which waste collection services are provided at this address. Links to the applicable franchise contract terms, tipping fee schedules, and municipal reporting obligations.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Precision level of the latitude/longitude geocoding result for this service address. \'rooftop\' indicates exact parcel-level match; \'range_interpolated\' indicates interpolated from street range; \'approximate\' indicates centroid-level. Drives confidence in route optimization and dispatch accuracy.']
  is_gated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service address requires gate or controlled access entry. When true, drivers must use the access_code or follow special entry procedures. Used for route planning and driver briefing in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch.']
  is_hazardous_waste_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service address is designated for hazardous waste collection or handling services. When true, requires RCRA-compliant manifest tracking, specialized vehicle assignment, and Enviance EHS compliance workflows. Triggers additional regulatory reporting obligations.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which a waste collection or environmental service was successfully completed at this address. Used for SLA compliance monitoring, missed pickup detection, and customer service inquiries.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the service address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch for route optimization, GPS-based driver navigation, and geospatial analytics.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the service address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch for route optimization, GPS-based driver navigation, and geospatial analytics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service address record was most recently updated, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']
  municipality_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the municipality or local enforcement agency (LEA) jurisdiction for this service address. Used for franchise agreement billing, regulatory reporting to State Environmental Quality Departments, and SWIS reporting.']
  municipality_name varchar [note: 'Name of the local municipality or jurisdiction governing waste collection services at this address (e.g., \'City of Houston\', \'Harris County\'). Determines applicable franchise agreements, tipping fee structures, and regulatory reporting obligations to Local Solid Waste Authorities.']
  placement_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text operational instructions for drivers and collection crews regarding where containers should be placed or retrieved at this service address (e.g., \'Place cart at end of driveway\', \'Containers behind gate on north side\', \'Use rear alley access\'). Sourced from Waste Logics and AMCS Platform driver notes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the service location. Used for route zone assignment, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic analytics.']
  primary_container_size_yards decimal(5,2) [note: 'Size of the primary waste container at this service address measured in cubic yards (e.g., 2.0, 4.0, 6.0, 8.0, 96-gallon cart = 0.42 yards). Used for capacity planning, tipping fee estimation, and route load calculations. Sourced from Waste Logics container assignment.']
  recycling_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of recycling collection program active at this service address. \'single_stream\' = all recyclables commingled; \'dual_stream\' = paper separated from containers; \'source_separated\' = individual material streams. Determines MRF (Materials Recovery Facility) processing requirements and diversion rate calculations.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date on which waste collection or environmental services were terminated or are scheduled to end at this service address. Null for currently active addresses. Used for churn analysis, final billing, and container retrieval scheduling.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which waste collection or environmental services are performed at this address (e.g., weekly residential pickup, daily commercial collection). Drives route scheduling, container sizing recommendations, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  service_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for the service address indicating the customer type served at this location. Aligns with B2C residential, B2B commercial, industrial, and government/municipal contract categories. Used for pricing, routing, and regulatory reporting.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date on which waste collection or environmental services first commenced at this service address. Used for customer tenure analysis, contract billing start, and service history reporting.']
  special_handling_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special operational, safety, or regulatory handling requirements for this service address beyond standard placement instructions (e.g., \'Requires PPE due to chemical storage on site\', \'OSHA confined space entry required\', \'Coordinate with site safety officer before entry\').']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or Canadian province code for the service location (e.g., \'TX\', \'CA\'). Used for state-level environmental compliance reporting to State Environmental Quality Departments and regulatory fee calculations.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction identifier for this service address used by Oracle Revenue Management to apply correct state, county, and municipal tax rates to service charges. Determined by address geocoding and jurisdiction boundary mapping.']
  vehicle_type_restriction varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle type restrictions applicable to this service address (e.g., \'No rear-loaders\', \'ASL only\', \'No vehicles over 26,000 lbs GVW\'). Used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch to assign compatible collection vehicles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: asl_only|fel_only|rel_only|no_rear_loader|no_front_loader|no_side_loader|small_vehicle_only — promote to reference product]']
  waste_stream_types varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of waste stream types collected at this service address (e.g., \'MSW,recycling,organics\', \'C&D,hazardous\'). MSW = Municipal Solid Waste; C&D = Construction and Demolition Waste. Drives container assignment, route type, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msw|recycling|organics|c_and_d|hazardous|electronic|bulky|yard_waste — promote to reference product]']
  weight_restriction_tons decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable vehicle weight in tons for vehicles accessing this service address, as posted by local road authority or property owner. Constrains which collection vehicle types (ASL, FEL, REL) can service this location. Critical for fleet dispatch and DOT compliance.']
  zoning_classification varchar [note: 'Land use zoning classification of the service address as defined by local municipal zoning ordinances. Determines applicable service types, container sizes, collection frequency, and regulatory requirements. Drives segmentation between B2C residential, B2B commercial, and government accounts.']

  Note: 'Physical locations where waste collection, recycling, or other environmental services are rendered. Each address is a distinct service point with its own geolocation (latitude/longitude), route zone assignment, municipality jurisdiction, zoning classification (residential, commercial, industrial), accessibility notes (gate codes, weight restrictions, placement instructions), and address validation status. A single account may have multiple service addresses (e.g., a commercial chain with multiple sites). Distinct from billing address stored on the account.'
}

Table customer.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account hierarchy relationship record in the Waste Management customer account structure. Primary key for this entity.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account to which consolidated invoices are directed when consolidated_billing_flag is true. May reference the parent account or a designated payer account within the hierarchy.']
  child_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child (subordinate) account in this hierarchy relationship. Represents the site, subsidiary, or district that rolls up to the parent account for consolidated reporting and billing.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master service agreement or umbrella contract governing this hierarchy relationship. Links the hierarchy to the contractual terms, SLA commitments, and pricing schedules applicable across all child sites.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management account manager or national account executive responsible for managing this hierarchy relationship. Supports CRM ownership, escalation routing, and account performance tracking.']
  parent_epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Corporate hierarchies with multiple EPA IDs require tracking parent-subsidiary generator relationships for consolidated biennial reporting, liability allocation, and multi-site permit management. Supp']
  primary_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in this hierarchy relationship. References the customer account that sits above the child in the corporate, franchise, or municipal umbrella structure.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference to customer_segment table. The segment classification should be retrieved via JOIN to the authoritative customer_segment reference ']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for this hierarchy relationship record. New or modified hierarchy relationships require approval before becoming operationally active. Supports governance and audit trail requirements for account structure changes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the Waste Management manager or executive who approved this hierarchy relationship. Required for SOX compliance and internal audit trails for account structure governance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the designated authority. Supports SOX compliance audit trails and account governance reporting.']
  consolidated_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices for the child account are consolidated and issued at the parent account level rather than individually at the child site level. True enables enterprise-level consolidated billing for national accounts and municipal umbrella contracts.']
  consolidated_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operational and environmental compliance reports (e.g., diversion rates, tonnage, GHG emissions) for the child account are aggregated and reported at the parent account level. Supports enterprise sustainability reporting and national account KPI dashboards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and SOX compliance for account structure governance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this parent-child account hierarchy relationship expires or is terminated. Null for open-ended relationships with no planned end date. Supports historical hierarchy reporting and contract lifecycle management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this parent-child account hierarchy relationship became or becomes effective and binding. Used for time-based reporting and to determine which hierarchy was active during a given service period.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy relationship, used in cross-system references and national account management communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account hierarchy records.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child account within the hierarchy tree, where 1 represents a direct child of the ultimate parent. Supports tree traversal queries and enterprise reporting roll-ups for national account management.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Designates the positional level of the child account within the hierarchy structure. \'ultimate_parent\' is the root enterprise account, \'intermediate_parent\' is a mid-tier grouping (e.g., regional division), and \'child_site\' is the leaf-level service location.']
  hierarchy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for this hierarchy relationship or grouping, typically reflecting the corporate group, franchise network, or municipal contract name (e.g., \'Acme Corp National Account\', \'City of Houston Municipal District\'). Used in reporting and account management interfaces.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from the ultimate parent to the child account (e.g., \'/10001/10045/10089\'). Enables efficient subtree queries and hierarchy traversal without recursive joins in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this hierarchy relationship. \'active\' indicates the relationship is in force, \'pending\' indicates awaiting approval or activation, \'suspended\' indicates temporarily paused, \'terminated\' indicates the relationship has ended, and \'inactive\' indicates administratively deactivated.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the structural purpose of this hierarchy. \'corporate\' reflects legal ownership structures, \'franchise\' reflects franchised operator networks, \'municipal\' reflects government contract groupings, \'geographic\' reflects territory-based groupings, and \'billing\' reflects consolidated billing arrangements independent of ownership.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account hierarchy relationship record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing in the Databricks Silver layer and for audit trail compliance.']
  national_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy relationship is part of a national account program, where a single corporate parent has service agreements spanning multiple geographic markets. Drives national account management workflows and pricing tier eligibility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for account managers and data stewards to capture contextual notes about this hierarchy relationship, such as special contractual arrangements, pending restructuring events, or franchise agreement nuances not captured in structured fields.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification assigned to this hierarchy relationship, reflecting volume-based or contract-negotiated rate schedules applicable across all child sites. Confidential commercial information used by revenue management and sales teams.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship. \'corporate_subsidiary\' applies to owned business entities, \'franchise\' to franchised operators, \'municipal_district\' to government umbrella contracts, \'multi_site_commercial\' to commercial customers with multiple service locations, and \'joint_venture\' to shared-ownership arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate_subsidiary|franchise|municipal_district|multi_site_commercial|joint_venture|other — promote to reference product]']
  service_region_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the Waste Management operational service region or district in which this hierarchy relationship is primarily managed. Supports regional performance reporting and operational territory alignment.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement tier applicable to this hierarchy relationship, determining response time commitments, service frequency guarantees, and escalation priorities across all child sites. \'enterprise\' and \'government\' tiers carry enhanced SLA obligations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when hierarchy data is ingested from multiple upstream systems into the Databricks Silver layer.']
  source_system_hierarchy_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this hierarchy relationship in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Account Hierarchy ID, SAP Customer Hierarchy Node ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  total_child_site_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active child service sites or accounts directly or indirectly subordinate to the parent account in this hierarchy. Maintained as a denormalized count to support national account management dashboards and contract scope validation without recursive queries.']

  Note: 'Defines parent-child relationships between customer accounts to support corporate hierarchies, franchise groups, municipal umbrella contracts, and multi-site commercial customers. Captures hierarchy level (ultimate parent, intermediate parent, child site), relationship type (corporate subsidiary, franchise, municipal district), effective dates, and consolidated billing flag. Enables enterprise-level reporting across all sites under a parent account and supports national account management for large commercial clients.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer segment record in the Waste Management classification taxonomy. Primary key for the customer_segment reference product. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a reference classification taxonomy (code list / type-table) defining market segments; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt, but all meaningful business attributes are still included for completeness.]']
  parent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on segment (parent_segment_id)']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the business owner or data steward who approved this customer segment definition for production use. Required for governance compliance and SOX audit trail on reference data changes.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment definition was formally approved by the designated business owner or data steward. Required for governance compliance and SOX audit trail on reference data changes.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Default billing cycle frequency for customers in this segment as configured in Oracle Revenue Management. Residential segments are typically billed monthly or quarterly; commercial segments monthly; government contracts may be quarterly or annual.']
  contract_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment must have a formal service contract (as opposed to month-to-month or on-demand service). Commercial, Industrial, and Government segments typically require contracts; standard residential may be month-to-month.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change history tracking per Waste Management data governance policy.']
  credit_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit check is required during customer onboarding for this segment. Typically required for commercial and industrial segments; waived for government/municipal accounts and standard residential prepay accounts.']
  crm_segment_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding segment identifier code as configured in Salesforce CRM account records. Used for bi-directional synchronization between the Databricks Silver Layer and Salesforce CRM to ensure consistent segment classification across systems.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required from customers in this segment at account activation. Deposit requirements are managed in Oracle Revenue Management and SAP S/4HANA FI module.']
  diversion_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target waste diversion rate percentage (waste diverted from landfill through recycling, composting, or WTE) for customers in this segment. Used for sustainability program goal-setting, MRF throughput planning, and EPA/state environmental compliance reporting. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).']
  dot_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waste collection services for customers in this segment are subject to DOT hazardous materials transportation regulations (49 CFR). Applies to segments generating regulated hazardous waste requiring special vehicle placarding and CDL driver certification.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this customer segment definition is effective and available for customer assignment. Supports temporal validity tracking for segment taxonomy changes and ensures historical accuracy in customer lifecycle reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this customer segment definition is no longer active for new customer assignments. Null indicates the segment is open-ended with no planned expiration. Deprecated segments retain this date for historical reference.']
  epa_generator_class varchar [note: 'EPA hazardous waste generator classification applicable to customers in this segment under RCRA regulations: VSQG (Very Small Quantity Generator), SQG (Small Quantity Generator), LQG (Large Quantity Generator), or Not Applicable for non-hazardous segments. Drives compliance reporting requirements in Enviance EHS.']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment require Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reporting as part of their service agreement or regulatory obligation. Applies to large commercial, industrial, and government segments subject to EPA GHG Reporting Program (40 CFR Part 98). Managed in Enviance EHS.']
  is_default_segment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is the system default assigned to new customer accounts when no explicit segment classification has been determined during onboarding. Only one segment should carry this flag as True at any given time.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment record was most recently updated in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the customer segment definition within the classification taxonomy. \'active\' segments are available for customer assignment; \'deprecated\' segments are retained for historical reference only; \'draft\' segments are pending approval before activation.']
  manifest_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest (EPA Form 8700-22) is required for waste pickups from customers in this segment. Mandatory for LQG and SQG hazardous waste generators under RCRA. Tracked in Enviance EHS and SAP EHS module.']
  market_category varchar [note: 'High-level market category grouping for the segment: B2C (Business-to-Consumer residential), B2B (Business-to-Business commercial), Government, Industrial, or Non-Profit. Drives sales channel assignment, pricing strategy, and regulatory reporting classification.']
  max_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual revenue threshold in US dollars for customer accounts in this segment. Accounts exceeding this threshold are eligible for promotion to a higher segment. Null indicates no upper revenue cap for the segment.']
  min_annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annual revenue threshold in US dollars that a customer account must meet to qualify for this segment. Used in Salesforce CRM for automated segment assignment and in Oracle Revenue Management for rate eligibility validation. Null indicates no minimum revenue requirement.']
  min_contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract duration in months required for customers in this segment. Null or zero indicates no minimum contract term (e.g., residential month-to-month). Enterprise and National Account segments typically require 12–60 month minimum terms.']
  nps_benchmark_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) benchmark for customers in this segment, used to set performance expectations for customer satisfaction programs. NPS ranges from -100 to +100. Residential segments typically target 40+; National Account segments target 60+.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from invoice date within which payment is due for customers in this segment (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Configured in Oracle Revenue Management and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Government segments may have Net 45 or Net 60 terms.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier eligibility associated with this customer segment, used by Oracle Revenue Management to apply the correct rate schedule. Premium applies to national accounts and enterprise commercial; Government applies to municipal and government contracts with negotiated rates; Economy applies to standard residential.']
  routing_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank (1 = highest priority) used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch to sequence collection routes when capacity constraints require prioritization. Government and medical waste segments typically carry the highest routing priority.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which customers in this segment are acquired and managed. Drives Salesforce CRM lead routing and opportunity assignment rules. Municipal Bid applies to government/municipal contracts won through competitive procurement.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short, externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the customer segment (e.g., RES_SFH, COM_SMB, GOV_MUN). Used as the business key in Salesforce CRM account records, AMCS routing configurations, and Oracle Revenue Management rate schedules.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the customer segment, including the types of customers it encompasses, qualifying criteria, and intended use in service delivery and sales targeting.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer segment (e.g., \'Residential Single-Family\', \'Commercial Small Business\', \'Government/Municipal\'). Displayed in Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics customer portal, and all operational and executive reporting.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the segment type used for service eligibility rules, route planning in AMCS, and container management in Waste Logics. Residential covers single-family and multi-family; Commercial covers small business through national accounts; Industrial covers large-scale waste generators.']
  service_eligibility_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible for commercial waste collection services (dumpster/container service, compactor service, FEL/REL/ASL truck service). Used by AMCS Platform for service configuration.']
  service_eligibility_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible for hazardous waste handling and disposal services under RCRA regulations. Typically restricted to Industrial, Commercial, and Government segments with appropriate EPA generator registration.']
  service_eligibility_recycling boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible for recycling collection and Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) processing services. Drives diversion rate reporting and sustainability program enrollment.']
  service_eligibility_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible for residential waste collection services (curbside pickup, recycling bins, yard waste). Used by Waste Logics to filter available service offerings during customer onboarding.']
  service_eligibility_wte boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible for Waste-to-Energy (WTE) conversion services. Eligibility is typically limited to large commercial, industrial, and municipal segments generating sufficient MSW volume to support WTE processing economics.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier assigned to this customer segment, defining the contractual service response and resolution time commitments. Tier-1 represents the highest service priority (e.g., National Accounts, Government contracts); Tier-4 represents standard residential service. Drives operational prioritization in Locus Dispatch and AMCS.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying customer segments in UI dropdowns, reports, and dashboards across Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics portal, and internal analytics tools. Lower numbers appear first.']
  sub_segment_type varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the segment type (e.g., Single-Family, Multi-Family under Residential; Small Business, Mid-Market, Enterprise, National Account under Commercial). Used for targeted service offerings and NPS benchmark differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Single-Family|Multi-Family|Small Business|Mid-Market|Enterprise|National Account|Municipal|County|State Agency|Federal Agency|Light Industrial|Heavy Industrial|Non-Profit — promote to reference product]']
  sustainability_program_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible to enroll in Waste Management sustainability programs (e.g., zero-waste consulting, RNG offset credits, SRF programs, carbon footprint reporting). Typically available to commercial, industrial, and government segments.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number tracking revisions to the customer segment definition. Incremented each time segment attributes (SLA tier, pricing tier, eligibility rules) are modified. Supports audit trail and rollback capabilities.']
  waste_logics_segment_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding segment identifier code as configured in the Waste Logics customer portal and service scheduling system. Enables accurate service eligibility filtering and container management rules for customers accessing the self-service portal.']

  Note: 'Reference classification taxonomy defining the market segments used to categorize Waste Management customers. Segments include B2C Residential (single-family, multi-family), B2B Commercial (small business, mid-market, enterprise, national account), Industrial, Government/Municipal, and Non-Profit. Each segment carries SLA tiers, pricing tier eligibility, service eligibility rules, sales channel assignments, and NPS benchmark targets. Used for targeted service offerings, rate scheduling, and operational routing prioritization.'
}

Table customer.account_status_history {
  account_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account status history record. Serves as the primary key for this immutable audit trail table.']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Salesforce CRM service case or customer service ticket that initiated or is associated with this status transition (e.g., a cancellation request case, a dispute case leading to suspension). Nullable when the transition is system-initiated.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract associated with this account at the time of the status transition. Relevant for contract-driven closures, expirations, or renewals that trigger status changes.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account whose lifecycle status changed. Links this history record to the master customer account entity in Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics.']
  final_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the final invoice generated upon account closure or suspension. Links to the Oracle Revenue Management billing system for final billing reconciliation, credit memo processing, and AR write-off procedures.']
  primary_account_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (employee or system service account) who initiated or executed this status transition. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements under SOX compliance.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference. Account status history should capture segment at time of transition via FK rather than string value for accurate historical segmen']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary service address associated with the account at the time of this status transition. Relevant for relocation-driven closures and reactivations at new addresses. Supports geographic churn analysis.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Work Order (WO) associated with this status transition, such as a final service pickup work order generated upon account closure or a reactivation service order. Nullable when no WO is linked.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which supervisory approval was granted for this status transition. Distinct from effective_timestamp (when the change took effect) and created_timestamp (when the record was written). Supports SOX audit trail completeness.']
  ar_balance_at_transition decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding Accounts Receivable (AR) balance on the customer account at the exact moment of this status transition. Critical for non-payment suspension and closure analysis, collections prioritization, and bad debt provisioning under GAAP.']
  churn_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of account churn when the transition represents a closure or deactivation: voluntary (customer-initiated cancellation), involuntary (non-payment or collections-driven), regulatory (compliance-mandated), or seasonal (temporary suspension with planned reactivation). Supports churn analytics and retention strategy development.']
  collections_referral_date date [note: 'The date on which the account was referred to the collections process following a non-payment suspension or closure. Applicable only for financially-driven status transitions. Supports AR aging analysis and bad debt provisioning under GAAP.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes entered by the initiating user or system regarding this status transition. May include escalation notes, customer communication summaries, or operational context not captured in structured fields.']
  container_retrieval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Waste Management-owned containers (bins, dumpsters, roll-off containers) must be retrieved from the service address following this status transition. Triggers container retrieval work order creation in AMCS Platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this account status history record was first written to the data platform. Represents the audit record creation time, distinct from effective_timestamp (when the business event occurred). Supports data lineage, ETL audit trails, and SOX IT general controls.']
  days_in_prior_status varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days the account remained in the prior status before this transition occurred. Supports customer lifecycle duration analysis, churn prediction modeling, and SLA compliance measurement.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the new account status became operationally effective. This is the business event timestamp — distinct from the record audit timestamp — and is the authoritative time used for churn analysis, SLA measurement, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  final_service_date date [note: 'The date of the last scheduled or completed waste collection service for the account, captured at the time of closure or suspension. Used for final billing reconciliation, container retrieval scheduling, and service completion verification.']
  hazardous_waste_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account involves hazardous waste services subject to RCRA, DOT, and EPA regulatory requirements. Status transitions for hazardous waste accounts require additional regulatory documentation and notification procedures.']
  initiating_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated or triggered this status transition event. Supports data lineage, integration audit trails, and root-cause analysis for unexpected status changes.']
  manifest_closure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a hazardous waste Manifest (the regulatory tracking document) must be formally closed or reconciled as part of this account status transition. Applicable only for hazardous waste accounts. Supports RCRA compliance and EPA enforcement documentation.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The account lifecycle status immediately after this transition. Captures the \'to\' state in the status change event. Valid values mirror prior_status enumeration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|active|suspended|inactive|closed|pending_activation|pending_closure — promote to reference product]']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of this status transition. Supports multi-channel communication audit trails and regulatory compliance with required notification method standards.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (email, letter, or portal message) was sent to the customer regarding this status transition. Supports compliance with state consumer protection notification requirements and customer communication audit trails.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the customer notification for this status transition was sent. Supports regulatory compliance documentation for required advance notice periods (e.g., service termination notice requirements under state utility regulations).']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The account lifecycle status immediately before this transition occurred. Captures the \'from\' state in the status change event. Valid values: prospect (pre-service lead), active (receiving service), suspended (temporarily halted), inactive (service ended but record retained), closed (permanently terminated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|active|suspended|inactive|closed|pending_activation|pending_closure — promote to reference product]']
  prior_status_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the prior status originally became effective. Combined with effective_timestamp, enables precise calculation of duration in prior status for lifecycle analytics and SLA compliance measurement.']
  reactivation_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is eligible for reactivation following this status transition. Set to False for deceased customers, regulatory closures, or fraud-related closures. Supports win-back campaign targeting and collections recovery workflows.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the account status transition occurred. Supports churn analysis, regulatory reporting, and customer lifecycle management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_payment|service_cancellation|relocation|deceased|business_closure|reactivation|contract_expiry|regulatory_action|merger_acquisition|seasonal_suspension — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or detail about the reason for the status transition, supplementing the structured reason_code. May include customer-stated reasons, agent notes, or system-generated explanations.']
  regulatory_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for any regulatory enforcement action, permit violation notice, or compliance order that directly caused this status transition. Applicable for regulatory-driven account closures or suspensions. Supports RCRA, EPA, and state environmental agency compliance documentation.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'The Waste Management service territory or district code associated with the account at the time of this status transition. Supports geographic churn analysis, route optimization impact assessment, and regional regulatory compliance reporting.']
  sla_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition occurred in breach of a contractual Service Level Agreement (SLA), such as a failure to reactivate service within the committed timeframe or a suspension applied without required advance notice. Supports SLA compliance monitoring and contract penalty calculations.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating system of record (e.g., Salesforce CRM Account History ID, Oracle Revenue Management event ID, Waste Logics status log ID). Supports data lineage tracing, reconciliation between source systems and the lakehouse, and duplicate detection.']
  suspension_reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level category for suspension-specific transitions, providing a structured classification above the detailed reason_code. Applicable only when new_status is \'suspended\'. Supports regulatory reporting and collections workflow routing.']
  transition_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes how the status transition was initiated: manual (agent or user action), system_automated (triggered by billing engine, collections workflow, or scheduled job), bulk_update (mass data migration or batch operation), or regulatory (mandated by a regulatory authority or compliance requirement).']
  win_back_eligible_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which a closed or inactive account becomes eligible for win-back outreach campaigns. May be set based on cooling-off periods, debt clearance timelines, or business rules. Supports Salesforce CRM marketing campaign targeting.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of outstanding AR balance written off as uncollectable at the time of account closure. Applicable only for closure transitions with unrecoverable debt. Supports GAAP bad debt expense recognition and SOX financial controls.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of all account lifecycle status transitions — from prospect creation through active service, suspension, reactivation, and final closure. Each record captures the prior status, new status, effective timestamp, reason code (non-payment, service cancellation, relocation, deceased, business closure), the initiating user or system, and any associated case or work order reference. Critical for churn analysis, regulatory compliance, and customer lifecycle management.'
}

Table customer.service_enrollment {
  service_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each service enrollment record in the Waste Management lakehouse. Primary key for the service_enrollment data product.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific physical container asset assigned to this enrollment, identified by the Container Identification (CID) number tracked in AMCS Platform and Infor EAM. Nullable when a specific container has not yet been assigned or for bag-based services.']
  collection_stop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.stop. Business justification: Service enrollments define ongoing service commitments that map to specific recurring stops on routes. This link enables operational execution, capacity planning, and route optimization by connecting ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating service contract or service agreement that governs the terms of this enrollment. Nullable for one-time or on-call service requests not tied to a formal contract.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) enrolled in this service. Links to the customer master product in the Customer domain.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal user (customer service representative, sales agent, or system process) who created or processed this service enrollment. Used for audit trail, performance tracking, and enrollment quality review.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Customer service enrollments should link directly to the service catalog offering to ensure customers are enrolled in valid, current service offerings. This provides a single source of truth for servi']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Hazardous waste service enrollments must reference the customer\'s treatment/storage/disposal permit to verify authorized waste streams, quantity limits, and handling requirements. Waste acceptance dec']
  recycling_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.recycling_program. Business justification: Commercial recycling service enrollments are tied to specific municipal or regional recycling programs that define accepted materials, collection frequency, and contamination standards. Essential for ']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route to which this service enrollment is assigned for scheduled pickup. Managed in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch. Drives driver dispatch, vehicle assignment, and stop sequencing.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference. Service enrollments should reference the authoritative segment classification via FK rather than storing segment as a string.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service address where waste collection or related service is performed. A customer may have multiple service addresses, each with independent enrollments.']
  service_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service type catalog entry defining the nature of the waste management service enrolled (e.g., residential MSW cart pickup, commercial FEL dumpster, roll-off container rental, curbside recycling). Links to the service type reference product.']
  servicing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Service enrollments (recurring waste collection, recycling programs, roll-off services) are fulfilled by specific operational units (collection districts, recycling facilities). Enables route planning']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Specialized service enrollments (hazardous waste, large commercial accounts, medical waste) may require dedicated vehicles with specific certifications, equipment, or regulatory compliance. Tracks veh']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Recurring hazmat service enrollments specify approved waste profiles defining authorized waste codes, treatment requirements, TSDF destinations, and handling procedures. Essential for service authoriz']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Commercial/industrial service enrollments designate waste-to-energy facilities for material diversion.
