  bill_payment_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portal account has been granted access to the online bill payment feature, enabling customers to view invoices and submit payments through the Waste Logics portal. May be restricted for accounts under collections or with disputed balances.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the portal account was permanently closed and deactivated. Null for accounts that are not closed. Triggers data retention and deletion workflows per privacy regulations. Distinct from suspension which is temporary.']
  container_request_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portal account has been granted access to submit container delivery, swap, or removal requests through the self-service portal. Container ID (CID) management requests are routed to the AMCS Platform for fulfillment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this portal account record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Serves as the system-of-record audit creation timestamp for data lineage, compliance, and change tracking purposes.']
  document_download_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portal account has been granted access to download documents such as invoices, service agreements, waste manifests, and compliance certificates through the self-service portal.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address registered to the portal account. Used for login (when email-based authentication is configured), password reset communications, notification delivery, and billing alerts. Must be unique across all active portal accounts.']
  email_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the registered email address for this portal account has been confirmed via an email verification workflow (e.g., click-through confirmation link). True = verified; False = unverified. Unverified accounts may have restricted portal feature access.']
  email_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the portal account email address was confirmed via the verification workflow. Null if email has not yet been verified. Provides audit trail for consent and identity verification compliance.']
  failed_login_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of consecutive failed login attempts since the last successful login or since the counter was last reset. Used to trigger account lockout policies per security standards. Reset to zero upon successful authentication.']
  inactivity_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive days without a successful login after which this portal account is flagged as inactive and subject to dormancy policies (e.g., notification, auto-suspension). May be set at the account level to override the system default for specific customer segments.']
  last_login_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the most recent successful login was performed. Used for security anomaly detection, geographic access monitoring, and fraud investigation. May be considered PII under GDPR and CCPA in some jurisdictions.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent successful authenticated login to the Waste Logics customer portal for this account. Used for inactivity detection, dormant account management, security monitoring, and customer engagement analytics.']
  linked_account_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct customer service accounts (e.g., multiple service addresses or business locations) currently linked to this portal account. Supports multi-account management use cases common in commercial and municipal customers managing multiple service sites.']
  mfa_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is currently active for this portal account. True = MFA is enforced at login; False = single-factor authentication only. Supports security compliance reporting and risk-based access control policies.']
  mfa_method varchar [note: 'The specific second-factor mechanism enrolled for this portal account when MFA is enabled. \'totp\' — Time-based One-Time Password (e.g., Google Authenticator); \'sms\' — SMS text message code; \'email\' — email-delivered OTP; \'authenticator_app\' — push notification via authenticator app; \'none\' — MFA not configured.']
  notification_email_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive email notifications from the portal (e.g., service reminders, billing alerts, collection schedule changes). True = subscribed; False = unsubscribed. Governs outbound email communication consent.']
  notification_push_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive mobile push notifications via the Waste Logics customer portal mobile application. True = subscribed; False = unsubscribed. Supports real-time service alerts and collection reminders.']
  notification_sms_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive SMS/text message notifications from the portal (e.g., collection day reminders, service disruption alerts). True = subscribed; False = unsubscribed. Governs outbound SMS communication consent.']
  paperless_billing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has enrolled in paperless (electronic) billing through the portal, opting to receive invoices digitally rather than by postal mail. True = paperless billing active; False = paper billing. Supports sustainability reporting on paper reduction and digital adoption KPIs.']
  password_last_changed_date date [note: 'Date on which the portal account password was most recently changed or reset. Used to enforce password expiration policies, prompt users to update credentials, and support security compliance audits. Null for SSO-only accounts.']
  portal_account_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier assigned to the portal account by the Waste Logics customer portal system. Used in customer communications, support tickets, and portal UI display. Distinct from the internal surrogate key.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the customer\'s preferred language for portal UI display and communications (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'en-US\', \'es-MX\'). Supports multilingual portal experiences for diverse residential and commercial customer bases.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer completed portal account registration in the Waste Logics customer portal. Used for cohort analysis, onboarding funnel reporting, and customer digital adoption tracking.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the portal account registration was completed and confirmed. Provides audit-grade precision for security forensics, compliance logging, and SLA measurement of onboarding workflows.']
  service_history_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portal account has been granted access to view historical service records, including past collection events, work orders (WO), and service requests, through the self-service portal.']
  service_scheduling_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this portal account has been granted access to the self-service scheduling feature, allowing customers to request, modify, or cancel waste collection service appointments online. Controlled by account type and service agreement terms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this portal account record originated. \'waste_logics\' — Waste Logics customer portal (primary source); \'salesforce\' — Salesforce CRM (for accounts provisioned via CRM workflow); \'manual\' — manually created by operations staff.']
  sso_external_reference varchar [note: 'The unique subject identifier (sub claim or NameID) issued by the external SSO identity provider for this portal account. Used to correlate the portal account with the customer\'s federated identity. Null for non-SSO accounts.']
  sso_provider varchar [note: 'Identity provider (IdP) used for Single Sign-On (SSO) authentication when auth_method is \'sso\' or \'oauth2\'. Identifies the federated identity source. \'none\' for accounts using native password authentication.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the current or most recent suspension of the portal account. Populated when account_status is \'suspended\' or \'locked\'. \'none\' when account is active. Supports customer service investigation and reinstatement workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: security_violation|non_payment|customer_request|admin_review|fraud_investigation|none — promote to reference product]']
  suspension_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the portal account was most recently placed in a suspended or locked state. Null if the account has never been suspended. Used for SLA tracking on account reinstatement and security incident response timelines.']
  terms_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has accepted the Waste Management portal terms of service and privacy policy during registration or upon a terms update. True = accepted; False = not yet accepted. Accounts with False may have restricted access pending acceptance.']
  terms_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the customer most recently accepted the portal terms of service and privacy policy. Provides legally defensible consent audit trail. Updated each time terms are re-accepted following a policy revision.']
  terms_version_accepted varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the portal terms of service and privacy policy that the customer most recently accepted (e.g., \'v2.1\', \'v3.0\'). Enables compliance teams to identify customers who have not yet accepted the current version following a policy update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this portal account record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance.']
  username varchar [note: 'The unique login username chosen by or assigned to the customer for authenticating into the Waste Logics self-service portal. May be an email address or a custom alphanumeric handle. Used as the primary login credential identifier.']

  Note: 'Manages customer self-service portal accounts on the Waste Logics customer portal. Stores portal username, authentication method (password, SSO, MFA status), portal registration date, last login timestamp, linked customer account(s), portal feature access permissions, notification subscription settings, and portal account status (active, locked, suspended). Enables digital self-service for service scheduling, bill payment, container requests, and service history review. Distinct from the core customer account master.'
}

Table customer.lead {
  lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the lead record in the Waste Management customer acquisition pipeline. Primary key for the lead data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account created in Salesforce CRM or Waste Logics upon successful lead conversion. Populated only when lead_status = \'converted\'. Enables traceability from lead origin to active customer lifecycle.']
  lead_assigned_sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management sales representative assigned to own and progress this lead. References the employee record in Workday HCM. Used for workload balancing, performance tracking, and commission attribution.']
  recycling_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.recycling_program. Business justification: Sales leads for commercial recycling services are qualified against available recycling programs to verify service availability, accepted materials, and pricing. Required for accurate sales qualificat']
  budget_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prospect has confirmed they have an approved budget allocated for waste management services. A key qualification criterion in the sales process; confirmed budget increases conversion probability.']
  collection_frequency_requested varchar [note: 'Requested frequency of waste collection service as expressed by the prospect. Used for route planning feasibility and SLA (Service Level Agreement) design during the sales process.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the prospective commercial, industrial, or municipal organization. Null for residential (B2C) leads where individual name fields apply.']
  competitor_incumbent varchar [note: 'Name of the current waste service provider (competitor) serving the prospect, if known. Used for competitive intelligence, win/loss analysis, and tailoring the sales pitch to differentiate Waste Management\'s service offering.']
  container_type_requested varchar [note: 'Type of waste container the prospect has requested or that is appropriate for their service needs. Informs equipment provisioning and CID (Container Identification) assignment upon conversion. Values: cart (residential/small commercial), dumpster (FEL/REL front-end or rear-end loader), roll-off (C&D or large volume), compactor (commercial high-density), tote (recycling), none (service-only).']
  contract_end_date_incumbent date [note: 'Date on which the prospect\'s current service contract with an incumbent provider expires. Critical for timing outreach and proposal submission to align with the prospect\'s procurement window.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead was formally converted to an active customer account. Populated when lead_status transitions to \'converted\'. Used for sales cycle duration analysis and conversion rate KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lead record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Represents the start of the lead lifecycle and is used for lead age calculation, SLA compliance monitoring, and pipeline velocity reporting.']
  crm_lead_reference varchar [note: 'External lead identifier sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object. Used to trace the record back to the originating system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  decision_maker_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sales representative has confirmed contact with the actual decision-maker (budget authority) at the prospect organization. Critical qualification criterion; unconfirmed decision-maker contact reduces forecast reliability.']
  decision_timeline varchar [note: 'Prospect\'s anticipated timeframe for making a purchasing decision. Used for pipeline stage management, sales forecasting, and prioritizing follow-up activities by the assigned sales representative.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates that the lead contact has requested no further outreach from Waste Management. Enforces compliance with TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) and CAN-SPAM Act opt-out requirements. When true, all outbound sales and marketing communications must cease.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the lead contact used for sales outreach, marketing communications, and follow-up correspondence. Sourced from Salesforce CRM.']
  estimated_annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual revenue (USD) expected from this lead if converted to an active account. Used for sales pipeline valuation, quota tracking, and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) forecasting. Calculated by sales representative based on service scope and applicable rate schedules.']
  estimated_monthly_volume_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated monthly waste generation volume in tons (short tons) for the prospective customer. Key input for service sizing, container selection, collection frequency determination, and revenue forecasting. Expressed in TPD (Tons Per Day) equivalent basis for commercial and industrial prospects.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the primary contact person associated with the lead. Applies to residential (B2C) leads and the key contact for commercial or municipal prospects.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry classification of the prospective commercial or industrial customer (e.g., healthcare, retail, construction, manufacturing, hospitality, government). Used for targeted sales strategies, regulatory compliance pre-assessment (RCRA, CERCLA), and service customization. Applies to B2B and municipal leads.']
  is_hazardous_waste_generator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prospective customer generates hazardous waste requiring RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) compliant handling, manifesting, and disposal through a licensed TSDF (Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility). Triggers specialized sales routing and compliance review.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which a Waste Management sales representative made contact with the lead (call, email, meeting, or site visit). Used to identify stale leads requiring re-engagement and to enforce follow-up SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the primary contact person associated with the lead. Used in conjunction with first_name to form the full contact identity.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the lead was originally generated. Supports marketing attribution analysis and campaign ROI (Return on Investment) measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_form|referral|cold_outreach|trade_show|municipal_rfp|partner|inbound_call|digital_ad — promote to reference product]']
  lead_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the lead within the Waste Management customer acquisition pipeline. Tracks progression from initial capture through qualification to conversion or disqualification. Values: new (just captured), contacted (outreach made), qualified (meets service criteria), converted (became active account), lost (disqualified or chose competitor).']
  lost_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the lead was marked as lost and did not convert to an active account. Used for win/loss analysis, competitive intelligence, and sales process improvement. Populated only when lead_status = \'lost\'.']
  next_follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next sales follow-up action with the lead. Set by the assigned sales representative to manage pipeline cadence and ensure timely progression through the qualification stages.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for sales representative notes capturing qualitative context about the lead, including site visit observations, special service requirements, regulatory considerations (e.g., RCRA generator status), or competitive intelligence gathered during outreach.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the lead contact. Used by sales representatives for initial outreach and qualification calls. Formatted per E.164 international standard.']
  prospect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prospective customer by service segment. Residential (B2C household), commercial (B2B business), industrial (large-scale waste generator), or municipal (government/public sector contract). Drives service offering, pricing tier, and sales routing.']
  qualification_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) assigned to the lead based on qualification criteria including service volume, budget authority, decision timeline, and fit with Waste Management service offerings. Higher scores indicate higher conversion probability. Supports sales prioritization and pipeline management.']
  rfp_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal Request for Proposal issued by a municipal or government prospect. Applicable primarily to municipal contract leads. Used to track competitive bid processes and link to proposal documentation.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region to which this lead is assigned within Waste Management\'s organizational territory structure (e.g., Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West). Used for regional performance reporting and territory management.']
  service_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the prospective service location. Used for route planning feasibility assessment, service zone validation, and eventual account setup upon conversion.']
  service_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the prospective service location (suite, unit, floor, building identifier). Supplements service_address_line1 for precise location identification.']
  service_city varchar [note: 'City of the prospective service location. Used for geographic market analysis, service territory assignment, and operational capacity planning.']
  service_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the prospective service location (e.g., USA). Supports international operations and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  service_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the prospective service location. Used for service zone validation, route feasibility, and market density analysis.']
  service_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the prospective service location. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, state environmental compliance requirements, and regional sales territory assignment.']
  service_type_requested varchar [note: 'Primary waste management service type the prospect is interested in. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) collection, recycling, C&D (Construction and Demolition) waste, hazardous waste handling, WTE (Waste-to-Energy), landfill disposal, roll-off container, or portable sanitation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msw_collection|recycling|c_and_d|hazardous_waste|wte|landfill|roll_off|portable_toilet — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the lead record in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Prospective customers who have expressed interest in Waste Management services but have not yet converted to active accounts. Captures lead source (web form, referral, cold outreach, trade show, municipal RFP), prospect type (residential, commercial, industrial, municipal), estimated service volume, assigned sales representative, lead status (new, contacted, qualified, converted, lost), qualification score, and conversion date. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Lead object. Supports the customer acquisition lifecycle from prospect to active account.'
}

Table customer.account_note {
  account_note_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each account note record. Primary key for the account_note data product.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Container Identification (CID) number of the specific waste container (dumpster, cart, roll-off, compactor) to which this note applies. Enables container-level instructions such as overweight warnings, damaged container flags, or special placement requirements.']
  collection_stop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.stop. Business justification: Account notes often contain stop-specific operational instructions (access codes, gate combinations, placement details, safety warnings, dog warnings) that drivers and operations teams need when servi']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Account notes document contract-specific exceptions (rate holds, SLA waivers, special handling terms). Operations and billing teams need agreement_id to associate notes with active contracts for servi']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account to which this note is attached. Links the note to the parent customer account in the Customer domain.']
  emergency_response_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.emergency_response_incident. Business justification: Account notes document site-specific safety concerns, access restrictions, and incident history related to emergency response events. Critical for driver safety briefings, future service planning, and']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Account notes frequently contain facility-specific instructions (gate access codes, disposal restrictions, delivery procedures for transfer stations/MRFs/landfills). Operations and drivers need facili']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Account notes document known hazards at customer sites (aggressive dogs, overhead wires, unstable surfaces). Business need: Hazard communication, safety briefing preparation, risk mitigation. Critical']
  inbound_load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.inbound_load. Business justification: Operational notes about customer accounts reference specific problematic inbound loads for contamination issues, access problems, or material quality concerns. Essential for driver safety alerts and s']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Account notes document safety-critical information related to past incidents at customer sites (prior injuries, hazmat spills, access issues). Business need: Historical incident context, repeat incide']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Account notes reference site-specific JHAs or safety procedures required at customer locations. Business need: Operational safety documentation, crew access to site-specific procedures, compliance ver']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Operations teams document site-specific access restrictions, special handling requirements, and regulatory constraints for customers using specific landfill facilities. Critical for route planning and']
  primary_account_author_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee (customer service representative, field operations staff, sales personnel, or system user) who created this note. Used for accountability, audit trails, and note ownership tracking.']
  related_note_account_note_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a parent or predecessor account note that this note supersedes, amends, or is related to. Enables note versioning and chaining of related instructions (e.g., an updated gate code note referencing the original).']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route to which this note is relevant, when the note contains route-specific instructions (e.g., weight-restricted road on route segment, seasonal access changes). Used by AMCS and Locus Dispatch for route-level note surfacing.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific service address associated with this note, when the note applies to a particular service location rather than the entire account. Null if the note applies account-wide.']
  tertiary_account_acknowledged_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field employee (driver or crew member) who most recently acknowledged this note prior to service execution. Used for safety compliance documentation.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Safety-critical and operational notes frequently reference specific collection vehicles: access restrictions for large vehicles, gate clearance issues, vehicle damage incidents, driver safety concerns']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Work Order (WO) associated with this note, if the note was created in the context of a specific service work order. Null if not linked to a WO.']
  access_code varchar [note: 'Gate code, keypad combination, or access credential required to enter the service location. Stored as a structured field separate from note_text to enable targeted display in driver mobile apps and automated gate systems. Classified as confidential due to security sensitivity.']
  applies_to_all_services boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note applies to all service types and lines at the associated account or address (True), or is scoped to a specific service line, container, or route as indicated by other fields (False). Drives note display logic in AMCS and Locus Dispatch.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the employee or system process that authored the note, captured at time of creation for audit and display purposes. Retained even if the employee record is later modified.']
  author_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the note author at the time of creation. Supports filtering notes by originating function and enables role-based visibility rules. automated_system indicates notes generated by integration processes (e.g., SAP, AMCS, Enviance).']
  billing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice, billing dispute, or accounts receivable (AR) transaction in Oracle Revenue Management associated with this note, when note_type is billing_note. Enables traceability between billing annotations and financial transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this account note was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Serves as the primary audit creation timestamp and is used for note ordering and SLA tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this note becomes active and should be applied during service operations. Allows notes to be pre-created for future service events (e.g., a gate code change effective next Monday).']
  escalation_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customer escalation case in Salesforce CRM associated with this note, when note_type is escalation_note. Enables cross-referencing between account notes and formal complaint or escalation case management workflows.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which this note is no longer active and should not be displayed to field crews or customer service representatives. Null indicates the note has no expiration and remains active indefinitely. Critical for time-limited instructions such as temporary access codes or seasonal hazard warnings.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of safety hazard documented in this note, when note_type is safety_alert. Enables targeted safety briefings and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) requirements for field crews. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aggressive_animal|biohazard|chemical_exposure|weight_restriction|overhead_obstruction|unstable_ground|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_pinned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note is pinned to the top of the account note list, ensuring it is always prominently displayed to customer service representatives and field crews regardless of creation date. True = pinned; False = standard ordering.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this note contains a safety-critical instruction or warning (e.g., aggressive dog, hazardous material on premises, weight-restricted access road, biohazard). Safety-critical notes trigger mandatory acknowledgment workflows in Locus Dispatch and AMCS before service execution.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the note text content (e.g., en, es, fr). Supports multilingual customer service operations in diverse service territories where notes may be authored in Spanish, English, or other languages.']
  last_acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this note was most recently acknowledged by a field crew member or driver via the Locus Dispatch or AMCS mobile application. Relevant for notes where requires_acknowledgment is True.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this account note was most recently updated, including edits to note text, status changes, or visibility modifications. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when this note was last reviewed by a supervisor or quality assurance process to confirm its accuracy and continued relevance. Supports note hygiene programs to prevent stale instructions from persisting in operational systems.']
  note_category varchar [note: 'Broad functional category of the note indicating which business function originated or owns the note. Supports filtering and reporting by department. Values: operational (field ops), customer_service (CSR-generated), compliance (regulatory or EHS-related), sales (sales team annotation), field_operations (driver or field staff note), automated (system-generated note).']
  note_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for the account note, used in customer service correspondence and operational communications (e.g., NOTE-202400012345).']
  note_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account note. Active notes are visible to applicable audiences; expired notes have passed their expiration date; archived notes have been manually retired; superseded notes have been replaced by a newer version.']
  note_subject varchar [note: 'Short summary or title of the account note, providing a quick-scan description of the note content without requiring the full note text to be read. Used in list views and operational dashboards.']
  note_text varchar [note: 'Full free-form or structured text body of the account note. Contains the primary informational content such as special handling instructions, safety warnings, access codes, billing annotations, or escalation details. May contain sensitive operational information.']
  note_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account note by its operational purpose. Drives routing, visibility, and urgency handling. Values: service_instruction (special handling for collection crews), billing_note (billing or payment-related annotation), safety_alert (hazard or safety warning such as aggressive dog or biohazard), access_instruction (gate codes, locked access, weight-restricted roads), escalation_note (customer complaint or escalated issue), general (unclassified free-form note).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to the note, used to surface critical safety alerts or escalation notes to operational teams. Critical notes (e.g., biohazard warnings, aggressive animal alerts) are highlighted in driver mobile apps and dispatch systems.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Defines whether this note recurs on a schedule, enabling time-limited instructions to automatically reactivate (e.g., a seasonal road closure note that applies every winter). none = one-time note; other values indicate recurring activation aligned to the effective_date and expiration_date cycle.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory requirement, permit condition, or compliance obligation that this note documents or supports (e.g., RCRA permit number, EPA inspection finding reference, OSHA citation number). Relevant for compliance and EHS-category notes.']
  requires_acknowledgment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether field staff or drivers must explicitly acknowledge this note before performing service at the associated address. Used for safety alerts, access instructions, and compliance-related notes to ensure crew awareness.']
  service_line_type varchar [note: 'The type of service line to which this note applies, enabling targeted display to relevant operational crews. Values align with Waste Management\'s core service lines: residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, hazardous_waste (RCRA-regulated), recycling (MRF-bound), wte (Waste-to-Energy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|municipal|hazardous_waste|recycling|wte — promote to reference product]']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this note record in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Note ID, Waste Logics Note ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this note originated. Supports data lineage tracking and integration reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|waste_logics|amcs|locus_dispatch|sap_s4hana|enviance_ehs|oracle_revenue_mgmt|manual — promote to reference product]']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version counter for this note, incremented each time the note text or key attributes are materially updated. Supports note versioning and audit trail requirements, enabling reconstruction of note history.']
  visibility_level varchar [note: 'Controls which audiences can view this note. internal_only: visible only to back-office staff; field_visible: visible to field operations crews and drivers via mobile dispatch systems (Locus Dispatch, AMCS); customer_visible: visible to the customer via the Waste Logics customer portal.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'The specific waste stream category relevant to this note, when the note pertains to a particular material type. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste), C&D (Construction and Demolition), hazardous (RCRA-regulated), organic (food/yard waste), bulky (large item), e_waste (electronic waste). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msw|recycling|c_and_d|hazardous|organic|bulky|e_waste — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Free-form and structured notes attached to customer accounts by customer service representatives, field operations staff, sales personnel, and automated systems. Captures note type (service instruction, billing note, safety alert, access instruction, escalation note), note text, visibility level (internal only, field-visible, customer-visible), author, creation timestamp, and expiration date. Critical for communicating special handling instructions (e.g., locked gate codes, aggressive dog warnings, weight-restricted access) across operational teams.'
}

Table customer.service_suspension {
  service_suspension_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each service suspension event record. Primary key for the service_suspension data product.']
  contamination_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.contamination_event. Business justification: Service suspensions due to repeated contamination violations reference specific contamination events that triggered the suspension. Required for regulatory compliance, customer notification, and reins']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Service suspensions trigger contract billing holds, term extensions, and SLA adjustments. Agreement reference required for automated billing system integration and contract lifecycle event tracking pe']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) whose service is being suspended. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account record or Waste Logics customer profile.']
  primary_service_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or system user who created or triggered the suspension record. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and internal governance policies.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.route. Business justification: Service suspensions must communicate route exclusions to operations teams. Route managers need visibility into which stops are temporarily inactive for route optimization, driver instructions, and cap']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service location address where waste collection or other service is being suspended. Enables route planning exclusions during the suspension period.']
  service_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service enrollment (subscription/agreement) that is being suspended. Links this suspension event to the specific recurring service agreement in Waste Logics or SAP SD.']
  actual_resume_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which service was restored and the suspension was lifted. Null if the suspension is still active. Compared against expected_resume_date to measure suspension duration accuracy and SLA compliance.']
  auto_resume_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system should automatically reinstate service on the expected_resume_date without requiring manual intervention. When True, AMCS Platform and Oracle Revenue Management are triggered automatically on the resume date.']
  billing_adjustment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a billing credit or adjustment must be applied to the customer account for the duration of the suspension. True for voluntary holds and operational interruptions; typically False for non-payment suspensions. Triggers workflow in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  billing_credit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary credit amount (in USD) to be applied to the customer\'s account for the suspension period. Calculated based on the prorated service rate and suspension duration. Populated by Oracle Revenue Management upon suspension approval.']
  billing_hold_end_date date [note: 'The date on which billing charges resume for the customer account following the suspension. May differ from actual_resume_date due to billing cycle alignment. Used by Oracle Revenue Management.']
  billing_hold_start_date date [note: 'The date from which billing charges are paused or adjusted for the customer account. May differ from suspension_start_date if billing cycles do not align with service dates. Used by Oracle Revenue Management for invoice generation.']
  container_retrieval_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual date on which containers are retrieved from the service address during the suspension. Null if container_retrieval_required is False. Tracked in AMCS Platform container management.']
  container_retrieval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waste containers (bins, dumpsters, roll-offs) at the service address must be physically retrieved during the suspension period. Relevant for long-term or permanent suspensions. Triggers a container retrieval work order in AMCS or Waste Logics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the suspension record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  crm_case_reference varchar [note: 'The case or ticket identifier in Salesforce CRM associated with this suspension event. Enables cross-system traceability between the operational suspension record and the customer service case for communication history and resolution tracking.']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the customer has been notified of the suspension event. \'not_sent\' = notification not yet dispatched; \'sent\' = notification dispatched via configured channel; \'acknowledged\' = customer confirmed receipt; \'failed\' = notification delivery failed. Supports SLA compliance and customer communication obligations.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this suspension has been escalated to a higher authority (e.g., collections manager, compliance officer, legal team) due to non-resolution, regulatory severity, or repeat offense. Triggers escalation workflow in Salesforce CRM.']
  expected_resume_date date [note: 'The anticipated calendar date on which service is expected to resume. Used for proactive route re-inclusion planning, customer communication scheduling, and billing adjustment calculations. May differ from actual_resume_date.']
  initiating_department varchar [note: 'The internal department that initiated or authorized the suspension event. Supports accountability tracking and root-cause analysis of suspension volumes by department.']
  is_permanent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this suspension is permanent (i.e., service will not resume). True for account closures or permanent service terminations processed as suspensions. When True, expected_resume_date and actual_resume_date are expected to be null.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, internal comments, or follow-up actions related to the suspension event. Captured by customer service representatives or operations staff.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of the suspension event (e.g., email, SMS, phone call, customer portal message, physical mail). Sourced from Salesforce CRM communication preferences.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer notification for this suspension was dispatched. Null if notification has not been sent. Used to measure notification lead time relative to suspension_start_date.']
  prior_suspension_count varchar [note: 'The number of previous suspension events recorded for this customer enrollment prior to the current suspension. Used for customer risk scoring, collections escalation, and identifying repeat non-payment patterns.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory body that mandated or is associated with the suspension event. Applicable only when suspension_type is \'regulatory\'. Values: EPA (U.S. Environmental Protection Agency), OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration), DOT (Department of Transportation), STATE_ENV (State Environmental Quality Department), LOCAL_SWA (Local Solid Waste Authority), OTHER.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'The official reference number from a regulatory agency (EPA, OSHA, DOT, State Environmental Quality Department) associated with a regulatory-mandated suspension. Null for non-regulatory suspensions. Required for RCRA, CERCLA, and Clean Water Act compliance documentation.']
  resume_condition varchar [note: 'The condition that must be met before service can be resumed. \'date_based\' = resumes on expected_resume_date; \'payment_received\' = requires outstanding balance cleared; \'inspection_passed\' = requires safety or compliance inspection; \'regulatory_clearance\' = requires regulatory agency sign-off; \'manual_review\' = requires supervisor approval.']
  route_exclusion_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route exclusion has been confirmed and applied in the AMCS Platform or Locus Dispatch system. Supports operational verification that the address will not receive service during the suspension.']
  route_exclusion_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service address must be excluded from active collection routes during the suspension period. When True, triggers route update in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch to prevent unnecessary truck dispatches.']
  safety_incident_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking this suspension to a safety incident record in Enviance EHS (e.g., OSHA recordable incident, PPE violation, hazardous material spill). Null if suspension is not safety-related. Supports OSHA 300 log and incident management reporting.']
  sap_hold_code varchar [note: 'The delivery or billing block code applied in SAP S/4HANA SD or FI module corresponding to this suspension. Used to synchronize the suspension state with SAP order management and accounts receivable processes.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service being suspended based on customer segment. \'residential\' = household waste collection (B2C); \'commercial\' = business waste collection (B2B); \'industrial\' = large-scale industrial waste; \'municipal\' = government/municipal contract services.']
  suspension_duration_days varchar [note: 'The planned number of calendar days for the suspension, calculated as the difference between expected_resume_date and suspension_start_date at the time of creation. Used for billing credit calculations and route planning horizon.']
  suspension_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the suspension event, used in customer communications, billing adjustments, and cross-system references (e.g., Salesforce CRM case number, Oracle Revenue Management hold reference). Format: SUSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  suspension_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code identifying the specific cause of the suspension (e.g., NON_PAY, SEASONAL_HOLD, VACATION_HOLD, SAFETY_VIOLATION, WEATHER_EVENT, ROUTE_DISRUPTION, REGULATORY_HOLD, CUSTOMER_REQUEST, HAZMAT_INCIDENT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NON_PAY|SEASONAL_HOLD|VACATION_HOLD|SAFETY_VIOLATION|WEATHER_EVENT|ROUTE_DISRUPTION|REGULATORY_HOLD|CUSTOMER_REQUEST|HAZMAT_INCIDENT — promote to reference product]']
  suspension_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or detail about the specific reason for the suspension, supplementing the structured reason code. Captured by the initiating department representative.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the service suspension becomes effective and collection/service activities are halted for the customer. Used by AMCS route optimization to exclude the address from active routes.']
  suspension_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the suspension event. \'pending\' = approved but not yet started; \'active\' = suspension is currently in effect; \'resumed\' = service has been restored; \'cancelled\' = suspension was voided before taking effect; \'expired\' = suspension end date passed without explicit resume action.']
  suspension_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the suspension event. \'voluntary\' = customer-initiated hold (seasonal, vacation); \'involuntary\' = company-initiated for non-payment or policy violation; \'regulatory\' = mandated by EPA, OSHA, DOT, or other governing body; \'operational\' = service interruption due to weather, route disruption, or fleet issue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the suspension record was last modified. Updated on any status change, date revision, or field update. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements under SOX.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'The type of waste stream associated with the suspended service. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste), recycling, C&D (Construction and Demolition Waste), hazardous, organics, or bulk. Determines which operational teams and regulatory requirements are affected by the suspension.']

  Note: 'Records temporary or permanent suspension events on customer service enrollments — including voluntary holds (seasonal, vacation), involuntary suspensions (non-payment, regulatory hold, safety violation), and service interruptions (weather, route disruption). Captures suspension type, start date, expected resume date, actual resume date, suspension reason code, initiating department, and customer notification status. Enables accurate billing adjustments, route planning exclusions, and customer communication during service interruptions.'
}

Table customer.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each customer referral event record in the Waste Management referral tracking system.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management customer service representative or sales agent who assisted in capturing or processing the referral, particularly for phone and written channel referrals. Null for self-service portal submissions. Used for agent performance attribution.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the referral incentive program under which this referral was submitted. Links to the referral program configuration defining reward rules, eligibility, and expiration terms.']
  referral_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the existing Waste Management customer account that initiated the referral. Links to the customer master record in Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics.']
  referral_referred_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prospect or newly converted customer account that was referred. Populated upon conversion from prospect to active customer. Null if the referral has not yet converted.']
  salesforce_lead_id varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM Lead or Opportunity record ID associated with the referred prospect. Populated when the referral generates a lead in Salesforce. Used for CRM-to-referral reconciliation and sales pipeline attribution.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference. Referrals should reference segment classification via FK to enable segment-based referral program analysis.']
  acquisition_cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cost attributed to acquiring the referred customer through this referral event, expressed in USD. Includes the reward amount plus any administrative processing costs allocated to this referral. Used for customer acquisition cost (CAC) analysis and ROI measurement of the referral program.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the referral was submitted by the existing customer. Supports multi-channel acquisition analytics and channel effectiveness reporting. Values include portal (Waste Logics self-service), phone (customer service center), written (mail or in-person form), mobile_app, and email.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'The date on which the referred prospect successfully converted to an active Waste Management customer account. Null if the referral has not yet converted. Used to calculate time-to-conversion and referral program effectiveness metrics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the referral record was first created in the Waste Management data platform Silver layer. Represents the system ingestion time and serves as the audit trail creation marker per data governance policy.']
  duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral has been identified as a duplicate of an existing referral record for the same prospect. True when a duplicate is detected; False otherwise. Duplicate referrals are ineligible for reward fulfillment per program policy.']
  eligibility_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referral has been validated against program eligibility rules, including service area coverage, account standing of the referring customer, and prospect uniqueness checks. True when eligibility has been confirmed; False when pending or failed validation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which the referral is no longer eligible for reward fulfillment if the prospect has not converted. Calculated from referral_date based on the program\'s validity window. Used to trigger status transitions to \'expired\'.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to the referral record in the Silver layer. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance. Updated on every status transition, reward fulfillment event, or data correction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context about the referral event, such as special circumstances, manual override justifications, or customer service agent observations recorded during phone or written channel referral intake.']
  prospect_city varchar [note: 'City component of the referred prospect\'s service address. Used for service area eligibility validation and geographic reporting of referral acquisition activity.']
  prospect_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the referred prospect provided by the referring customer. Used for outreach communications, lead tracking in Salesforce CRM, and identity matching upon conversion to an active account.']
  prospect_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the referred prospect as provided at the time of referral submission. Captured before the prospect converts to a full customer account. Used for outreach and identity matching during the conversion process.']
  prospect_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the referred prospect as provided at the time of referral submission. Used in conjunction with prospect_first_name for identity resolution and CRM lead matching in Salesforce.']
  prospect_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the referred prospect as provided at referral submission. Used for outbound sales contact and identity verification during the conversion process.']
  prospect_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the referred prospect\'s service address. Used for service area eligibility checks, route assignment, and geographic segmentation of referral acquisition data.']
  prospect_service_address varchar [note: 'The service address provided by the referring customer for the prospect. Used to validate service area eligibility, assign the prospect to the correct route and district, and support conversion to an active service account.']
  prospect_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the referred prospect\'s service address. Used for service territory validation, state-level regulatory compliance, and geographic referral performance reporting.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric code assigned to this referral event, used by the referring customer to share with prospects via the Waste Logics customer portal, phone, or written channels. Serves as the business-facing identifier for tracking and attribution.']
  referral_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the referring customer submitted the referral. This is the principal business event date used for program eligibility, expiration window calculation, and acquisition cost attribution reporting.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the referral event. \'Pending\' indicates the prospect has not yet signed up; \'converted\' means the prospect became an active customer; \'expired\' means the referral window lapsed without conversion; \'rewarded\' means the reward has been fulfilled; \'cancelled\' means the referral was voided.']
  reward_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the reward to be issued to the referring customer upon successful referral conversion, expressed in USD. For bill credits, this is the credit amount applied to the account. For gift cards, this is the face value. Zero if reward_type is \'none\'.']
  reward_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the reward amount. Defaults to USD for domestic US operations. Supports multi-currency reward programs for international or cross-border service territories.']
  reward_fulfillment_date date [note: 'The date on which the referral reward was actually issued to the referring customer (e.g., bill credit applied, gift card sent). Null until the reward has been fulfilled. Used to track reward liability, fulfillment SLA compliance, and loyalty program cost accounting.']
  reward_fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reward fulfillment for this referral. \'Pending\' indicates the reward is approved but not yet issued; \'issued\' means the reward has been delivered; \'failed\' indicates a processing error; \'voided\' means the reward was cancelled after approval.']
  reward_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the issued reward, such as the bill credit transaction ID in Oracle Revenue Management or the gift card authorization number. Used for reconciliation between the referral record and the billing or fulfillment system.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'The type of incentive reward offered to the referring customer upon successful conversion of the referred prospect. Supports bill credit applied to the referring account, physical or digital gift card, or a service discount. \'None\' indicates no reward is associated with this referral.']
  self_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referring customer and the referred prospect have been identified as the same individual or household, which would constitute a fraudulent self-referral. True when self-referral is detected; False otherwise. Self-referrals are ineligible for rewards.']
  service_district_code varchar [note: 'The Waste Management operational service district code associated with the prospect\'s service address. Used to assign the referral to the correct regional operations team and to measure referral volume and conversion rates by district.']
  service_type_requested varchar [note: 'The type of Waste Management service the referred prospect is interested in, as indicated at referral submission. Used to route the prospect to the appropriate sales team and to measure referral conversion rates by service line.']
  waste_logics_referral_reference varchar [note: 'The source system referral identifier from the Waste Logics customer portal, where the majority of self-service referrals are submitted. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the Silver layer and the operational source system.']

  Note: 'Tracks customer referral events where existing Waste Management customers refer new prospects. Captures referring account, referred prospect identity, referral date, referral channel (portal, phone, written), referral program code, referral status (pending, converted, expired, rewarded), reward type (bill credit, gift card), reward amount, and reward fulfillment date. Supports customer loyalty programs, referral incentive management, and acquisition cost attribution for residential and commercial segments.'
}

Table customer.account_document {
  account_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each account document record in the Waste Management customer domain. Primary key for the account_document data product.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific contact person associated with this document (e.g., the signatory on a service agreement or the authorized representative who submitted a tax exemption certificate). Nullable when the document is account-level rather than contact-specific.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Permits, insurance certificates, tax exemptions, and hazmat authorizations are contract-required documents per compliance_obligation and billing_term clauses. Linking enables automated contract compli']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) to which this document belongs. Links the document to its owning account in the customer master.']
  epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Permits, EPA notifications, certifications, and regulatory correspondence stored as account documents must link to specific EPA ID registrations for compliance audit trails, renewal tracking, and regu']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Regulatory documents (hazardous waste permits, air quality certificates, operating licenses) are facility-specific and must be tracked per facility for EPA, state, and local compliance audits. Documen']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Customers hold site-specific disposal permits, waste profile approvals, and special waste authorizations tied to individual landfill facilities. Regulatory compliance and gate operations require track']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit copies, certifications, insurance certificates, and regulatory approvals are stored as account documents. Compliance audits, service authorization workflows, and regulatory inspections require ']
  superseded_by_document_account_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer account_document record that replaced this document when document_status is \'superseded\'. Enables navigation of the document version chain for audit and compliance purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when this account_document record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  days_until_expiration varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days remaining until the document expires, calculated at the time of last refresh. Used by compliance monitoring dashboards and automated renewal notification workflows to prioritize documents requiring action. Negative values indicate the document is already expired.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'Broad grouping of the document for reporting and retention management. Compliance documents are subject to EPA/RCRA/DOT retention mandates; contractual documents are governed by SOX and GAAP; financial documents are subject to IFRS/GAAP audit requirements.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the document, such as a contract number, permit number, manifest number, or certificate reference. Used for cross-referencing with source systems (SAP SD, Salesforce CRM, Enviance EHS) and regulatory submissions.']
  document_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which the document was received or uploaded (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics customer portal, SAP S/4HANA, Enviance EHS, email submission, physical mail). Used for intake workflow routing and source system traceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|waste_logics|sap_s4hana|enviance_ehs|email|mail|fax|in_person — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document. \'active\' indicates the document is valid and in use; \'expired\' indicates the document has passed its expiration date; \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced it; \'pending_review\' indicates the document is awaiting internal approval; \'rejected\' indicates the document failed validation; \'archived\' indicates the document is retained for compliance but no longer operationally active.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the document as it appears on the file (e.g., \'Commercial Waste Service Agreement – Acme Corp\', \'EPA Hazardous Waste Generator Registration\', \'Tax Exemption Certificate – City of Houston\'). Used for display and search in the customer portal and CRM.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document by its business purpose. Drives workflow routing, retention rules, and compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: service_agreement|tax_exemption_certificate|proof_of_identity|municipal_franchise|hazardous_waste_generator_registration|correspondence|permit|bill_of_lading|manifest|rate_schedule|insurance_certificate|power_of_attorney — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the document becomes legally or operationally effective (e.g., the start date of a service agreement, the issue date of a permit or certificate). Distinct from upload_date; represents the real-world binding date of the document.']
  epa_id_number varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned identification number for hazardous waste generator registrations (format: 3-letter state code + 9 digits, e.g., \'TXD123456789\'). Required for RCRA compliance and hazardous waste manifest tracking. Nullable for non-hazardous waste documents.']
  esignature_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the electronic signature platform used to execute the document (e.g., DocuSign, Adobe Sign). Nullable when is_electronic_signature is False. Used for audit trail and legal enforceability documentation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the document expires and is no longer valid (e.g., tax exemption certificate expiry, permit renewal deadline, insurance certificate expiry). Nullable for documents with no defined expiration. Drives automated expiration alerts and compliance monitoring workflows.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Format or MIME type of the stored document file. Used to determine rendering capability in the customer portal and to enforce format compliance policies (e.g., regulatory submissions may require PDF/A). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|JPEG|PNG|TIFF|XML — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the uploaded document including extension (e.g., \'acme_corp_service_agreement_2024.pdf\'). Preserved for audit trail and user-facing display in the Waste Logics customer portal.']
  file_size_kb varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in kilobytes at the time of upload. Used for storage capacity planning and to enforce maximum file size policies in the customer portal and document management system.']
  file_uri varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI pointing to the physical document file in the enterprise content management system or cloud object store (e.g., Azure Data Lake Storage, S3 bucket path, SharePoint URL). Enables retrieval of the actual document for review, compliance audit, or customer portal display.']
  is_electronic_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document was executed via electronic signature (True) or wet ink/physical signature (False). Relevant for legal enforceability under the ESIGN Act and UETA, and for audit trail completeness.']
  is_latest_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document record represents the most current version of the document for the account. True when this is the active version; False when superseded by a newer version. Supports efficient querying for current documents without filtering on version_number.']
  is_regulatory_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is mandated by a regulatory authority (True) or is a voluntary/operational document (False). Regulatory-required documents (e.g., RCRA generator registrations, EPA permits, DOT hazmat certifications) trigger compliance alerts if missing or expired.']
  is_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document has been formally signed or executed (True) or is still in draft/unsigned state (False). Critical for service agreement activation workflows and onboarding completion checks in Salesforce CRM.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental agency, regulatory body, or organization that issued or authorized the document (e.g., \'U.S. EPA Region 6\', \'Texas Commission on Environmental Quality\', \'City of Houston Public Works\', \'IRS\'). Critical for regulatory compliance tracking and permit management.']
  issuing_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction under which the document was issued (e.g., state code \'TX\', \'CA\', or federal \'US-FED\'). Used for multi-state compliance reporting and to route documents to the appropriate regulatory compliance team.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when this account_document record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, annotations, or remarks about the document (e.g., \'Renewal pending state approval\', \'Superseded due to contract amendment\', \'Original hard copy on file at Houston district office\'). Used by account managers and compliance staff.']
  purge_eligible_date date [note: 'Date on or after which the document may be legally purged or destroyed per the applicable retention policy. Calculated from the document\'s effective or expiration date plus the retention_period_years. Prevents premature destruction of compliance-critical records.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework under which this document is required or governed (e.g., RCRA for hazardous waste generator registration, CAA for air permit, CWA for stormwater permit, DOT for hazardous materials transport). Drives compliance reporting and retention schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RCRA|CERCLA|CAA|CWA|DOT|OSHA|EPA|state|local|none — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_lead_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before expiration_date at which the renewal notification and workflow should be triggered. Configurable per document type (e.g., 90 days for municipal franchise agreements, 30 days for tax exemption certificates). Used by the compliance calendar in Enviance EHS.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document requires proactive renewal before expiration (True) or is a one-time document with no renewal obligation (False). Drives renewal workflow initiation in Enviance EHS and Salesforce CRM task management.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per applicable regulatory or business policy requirements (e.g., RCRA requires 3 years for hazardous waste manifests, SOX requires 7 years for financial records). Drives automated archival and purge scheduling.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was reviewed and validated by Waste Management staff. Used for compliance audit trails and to track the timeliness of document review in onboarding workflows.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Waste Management staff member who reviewed and validated the document for completeness and compliance. Used for internal audit trail and accountability tracking.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who signed or authorized the document on behalf of the customer account (e.g., the authorized representative who signed the service agreement or franchise document). Used for legal validation and audit purposes.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual who signed the document (e.g., \'Chief Financial Officer\', \'Facility Manager\', \'Authorized Environmental Representative\'). Validates the signatory\'s authority to bind the account.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was signed or formally executed by the authorized signatory. May differ from effective_date (e.g., a contract signed on Jan 5 but effective Jan 1). Used for legal enforceability and audit trail.']
  source_system_document_reference varchar [note: 'Original document identifier from the upstream source system (e.g., Salesforce CRM ContentDocument ID, SAP DMS document key, Enviance EHS permit record ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing.']
  tax_exemption_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax exemption certificate number for accounts exempt from sales tax on waste services (e.g., municipal governments, non-profit organizations). Nullable for non-exempt accounts. Used by Oracle Revenue Management for tax calculation and billing.']
  tax_exemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax exemption basis (e.g., federal government, state government, municipal, non-profit). Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle Revenue Management and determines applicable billing rates.']
  upload_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the document was uploaded or ingested into the document management system. Distinct from the document\'s own effective date. Used for onboarding workflow tracking and SLA compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document following major.minor convention (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Enables tracking of document revisions over the account lifecycle, particularly for service agreements that are amended or renewed.']

  Note: 'Stores metadata and references to documents associated with customer accounts — signed service agreements, proof of identity, tax exemption certificates, municipal franchise documents, hazardous waste generator registrations, and correspondence records. Captures document type, document title, file reference (storage path/URI), upload date, expiration date, document status (active, expired, superseded), and the associated account and contact. Supports compliance documentation requirements and customer onboarding workflows.'
}

Table customer.waste_generator_profile {
  waste_generator_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the waste generator regulatory profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Hazardous waste generator profiles require assigned compliance officers for RCRA inspections, manifest reviews, permit renewals, and regulatory correspondence. EPA and state regulations mandate docume']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds this waste generator regulatory profile. Links to the customer master record in the Customer domain.']
  epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Generator profiles require formal EPA ID registration for manifest signing authority, RCRAInfo synchronization, biennial reporting, and handler classification. Replaces denormalized epa_generator_id w']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Hazardous waste generator sites require specific JHAs for waste handling operations (chemical exposure, PPE requirements, emergency procedures). Business need: Hazmat crew safety, regulatory complianc']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: EPA generator profiles directly reference RCRA operating permits, state hazardous waste permits, and treatment/storage/disposal authorizations. Manifest generation, waste acceptance decisions, and reg']
  primary_waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Waste generator profiles classify customers by primary waste stream for EPA/RCRA regulatory compliance, manifest generation, biennial reporting, service eligibility, and disposal pathway determination']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Generator category (Large Quantity Generator, Small Quantity Generator, CESQG) determines applicable RCRA requirements for manifesting, accumulation time limits, training, and contingency planning. Se']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Waste generator profiles determine applicable safety programs (RCRA training, DOT hazmat, emergency response). Business need: Regulatory compliance, program enrollment, training requirements. Standard']
  transfer_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.transfer_station. Business justification: Hazardous waste generators must be associated with permitted transfer stations for regulatory compliance, EPA manifest routing, RCRA reporting, and TSDF facility coordination. This link enables proper']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary designated Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF) used by this generator for hazardous waste disposal. The TSDF must hold a valid RCRA permit. Links to the facility/asset master in the Asset Management domain.']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Hazardous waste generators with energy recovery options require designated WTE facility assignment for manifest routing, RCRA compliance tracking, and EPA reporting of waste-to-energy diversion vs TSD']
