  accumulation_area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the on-site hazardous waste accumulation area based on RCRA generator category and applicable time limits. LQGs may accumulate up to 90 days; SQGs up to 180 days (or 270 days in rural areas); VSQGs have no time limit but volume limits apply. Satellite areas are small accumulation points feeding a central accumulation area.']
  biennial_report_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the next EPA Biennial Hazardous Waste Report submission. Required for LQGs in even-numbered years under RCRA 40 CFR Part 262.41. Tracks compliance calendar obligations for large quantity generators.']
  contingency_plan_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a RCRA-compliant emergency contingency plan is on file for this generator. LQGs are required to maintain a formal contingency plan under 40 CFR Part 262.261; SQGs must have an emergency plan. Critical for compliance verification and emergency response readiness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this waste generator profile record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping requirements under RCRA and SOX.']
  e_manifest_registered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is registered in the EPA\'s national e-Manifest system for electronic hazardous waste tracking. The e-Manifest system replaced paper manifests and is operated by the EPA under RCRA authority.']
  emergency_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated emergency coordinator responsible for implementing the contingency plan and coordinating emergency response at the generator site. Required to be listed in the contingency plan and available to local emergency responders under RCRA.']
  emergency_coordinator_phone varchar [note: '24-hour contact phone number for the designated emergency coordinator at the generator site. Must be posted at the facility and provided to local emergency response agencies as required under RCRA contingency plan regulations.']
  estimated_hazardous_volume_kg_per_month decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated monthly generation quantity of RCRA-regulated hazardous waste in kilograms. This is the primary metric used to classify the generator into VSQG (≤100 kg), SQG (100–999 kg), or LQG (≥1,000 kg) categories and must be re-evaluated whenever operations change.']
  estimated_monthly_volume_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated total waste generation volume per month expressed in short tons. Used to determine and validate RCRA generator category (VSQG/SQG/LQG), plan collection frequency, and support TPD (Tons Per Day) capacity planning for routing and disposal.']
  generator_category varchar [note: 'RCRA regulatory classification of the waste generator based on monthly hazardous waste generation quantity. LQG (Large Quantity Generator) generates ≥1,000 kg/month; SQG (Small Quantity Generator) generates 100–999 kg/month; VSQG (Very Small Quantity Generator) generates ≤100 kg/month. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, accumulation time limits, and manifest obligations.']
  generator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waste generator regulatory profile. Active indicates the generator is currently producing regulated waste; inactive indicates operations have ceased; pending indicates initial registration is in progress; suspended indicates regulatory hold; closed indicates the site has been formally decommissioned.']
  hazardous_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of EPA hazardous waste codes (e.g., D001, F003, U080) applicable to the waste streams generated at this site as determined by TCLP (Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure) testing or listed waste classification. Required for manifest preparation and RCRA compliance reporting.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent regulatory inspection at this generator site. Significant violations may trigger enforcement actions, fines, or mandatory corrective measures under RCRA or CERCLA authority.']
  last_biennial_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent EPA Biennial Hazardous Waste Report was submitted for this generator. Used to verify compliance with LQG biennial reporting obligations and to support audit documentation.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance inspection conducted at this generator site by the EPA, state environmental agency, or Local Enforcement Agency (LEA). Used to track inspection history and assess compliance standing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this waste generator profile record in the data platform. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  last_notification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent RCRA Site ID notification update submitted to the EPA or authorized state agency. Generators must re-notify when their generator category changes, when they begin generating new hazardous waste streams, or when site information changes.']
  manifest_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is required to use a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest (EPA Form 8700-22) for off-site shipments of hazardous waste. Mandatory for SQGs and LQGs; VSQGs are exempt from manifest requirements under 40 CFR Part 262.']
  max_accumulation_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days this generator is permitted to accumulate hazardous waste on-site before it must be shipped to a TSDF, as determined by generator category and applicable RCRA regulations. Exceeding this limit triggers LQG-level requirements or potential RCRA violation.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code representing the primary industry classification of the waste-generating facility. Used by EPA and state agencies to categorize generator type, assess typical waste streams, and benchmark regulatory compliance requirements.']
  next_notification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the next required RCRA notification or re-notification. LQGs must notify every four years under the 2016 Generator Improvements Rule. Used by compliance teams to proactively manage regulatory deadlines.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers and environmental managers to record supplementary information about the generator\'s regulatory status, special handling requirements, enforcement history, or operational context not captured in structured fields.']
  open_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator currently has one or more open, unresolved regulatory violations on record with the EPA or state environmental agency. Triggers enhanced compliance monitoring and may affect service agreements and manifest acceptance.']
  personnel_training_current_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all relevant facility personnel have completed current RCRA hazardous waste management training as required for the generator\'s category. LQGs must provide formal classroom or on-the-job training; SQGs must ensure familiarity with emergency procedures.']
  primary_waste_stream varchar [note: 'The predominant category of waste generated at this site. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) covers general commercial/residential refuse; C&D (Construction and Demolition Waste) covers building debris; hazardous covers RCRA-regulated hazardous waste; universal waste covers batteries, lamps, and pesticides; mixed indicates multiple co-dominant streams.']
  profile_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this waste generator regulatory profile is considered valid and active. Represents the start of the current regulatory classification period, which may be triggered by initial registration, a generator category change, or a site re-notification.']
  profile_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this waste generator regulatory profile expires or must be renewed. Applicable when state permits or generator registrations have fixed validity periods. Null for open-ended federal EPA registrations.']
  rcra_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the generator submitted the initial RCRA Site Identification (Site ID) notification to the EPA or authorized state agency, establishing the generator\'s regulatory identity and obligations. Required under 40 CFR Part 262.18.']
  site_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the regulated waste-generating facility as registered with the EPA and state environmental authority. Used for regulatory correspondence, manifest routing, and compliance inspections.']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City of the regulated waste-generating facility as registered with the EPA and state environmental authority.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Legal or operating name of the waste-generating facility or site as registered with the EPA and state environmental agency. May differ from the customer account name if the customer operates multiple sites.']
  site_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the regulated waste-generating facility. Used for geographic routing, regulatory jurisdiction mapping, and compliance reporting.']
  site_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the regulated waste-generating facility. Determines which state environmental agency has jurisdiction and which state-specific RCRA regulations apply.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this waste generator profile was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Enviance EHS, SAP S/4HANA EHS module, EPA RCRAInfo, Salesforce CRM, and Waste Logics.']
  source_system_profile_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this waste generator profile record in the originating operational system (e.g., Enviance EHS record ID, SAP EHS business partner number, or RCRAInfo handler ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  state_permit_number varchar [note: 'State-issued environmental permit or registration number for the waste-generating facility, assigned by the applicable State Environmental Quality Department. Required for state-level RCRA-authorized program compliance and may differ from the federal EPA Generator ID.']
  state_program_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code identifying the RCRA-authorized state program under which this generator is regulated. In RCRA-authorized states, the state program operates in lieu of the federal program; in non-authorized states, the EPA directly administers RCRA.']
  universal_waste_handler_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is registered as a universal waste handler under 40 CFR Part 273, managing batteries, pesticides, mercury-containing equipment, or lamps under the streamlined universal waste rules rather than full RCRA hazardous waste requirements.']

  Note: 'Regulatory classification profile for commercial, industrial, and hazardous waste generating customers as required under RCRA and state environmental regulations. Captures EPA Generator ID, generator category (Very Small Quantity Generator VSQG, Small Quantity Generator SQG, Large Quantity Generator LQG), primary waste streams generated (MSW, C&D, hazardous, universal waste), estimated monthly waste volume (TPD), state permit numbers, and regulatory notification dates. Required for RCRA compliance and hazardous waste manifest management.'
}

Table customer.sla_assignment {
  sla_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each SLA assignment record linking a customer account or service enrollment to a specific SLA tier.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service agreement or contract enrollment under which this SLA assignment is operative. Enables SLA tracking at the service-agreement level for multi-service accounts.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) to which this SLA assignment applies. Links to the customer_account master record.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference. SLA assignments should reference segment classification via FK to ensure SLA tiers align with authoritative segment definitions.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Waste Management representative who authorized and approved this SLA assignment. Required for municipal and hazardous waste contracts per SOX internal controls.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this SLA assignment was formally approved by the authorized Waste Management representative. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance documentation.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number for this SLA assignment, used in customer communications, contract documentation, and operational reporting. Sourced from Salesforce CRM or Oracle Revenue Management.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA assignment. Active indicates the SLA is in force; pending indicates awaiting approval or activation; suspended indicates temporarily paused; expired indicates the effective period has lapsed; terminated indicates the assignment was ended before expiry.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA assignment automatically renews upon reaching the effective end date without requiring a new approval workflow. Relevant for recurring residential and commercial service agreements.']
  billing_dispute_resolution_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which a billing dispute raised by the customer must be investigated and resolved under this SLA tier. Tracked in Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM for Accounts Receivable (AR) compliance.']
  complaint_resolution_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which a customer complaint must be resolved and closed under this SLA tier. Applies to customer service interactions logged in Salesforce CRM and tracked for NPS/CSAT impact.']
  compliance_tracking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether active SLA compliance monitoring and reporting is enabled for this assignment. When true, operational systems (AMCS, Locus Dispatch, Salesforce CRM) feed compliance metrics against this SLA\'s targets for KPI dashboards and regulatory reporting.']
  container_delivery_lead_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days within which a new or replacement container must be delivered to the customer site after a confirmed service request, as committed under this SLA tier. Tracked in Waste Logics and AMCS Platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this SLA assignment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  diversion_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum waste diversion rate (percentage of waste diverted from landfill through recycling or recovery) committed under this SLA for applicable recycling or sustainability-focused service agreements. Null if not applicable to this service type.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this SLA assignment expires or is terminated. Null indicates an open-ended assignment with no scheduled end. Used for contract renewal tracking and compliance audits.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this SLA assignment becomes binding and operational. Used to determine whether the SLA applies to a given service event or complaint.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The organizational escalation tier to which unresolved issues are routed when the escalation threshold is breached under this SLA (L1 = frontline supervisor, L2 = operations manager, L3 = regional director, executive = VP/C-suite for municipal contracts).']
  escalation_threshold_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours after which an unresolved service request or complaint is automatically escalated to a higher support tier or management under this SLA assignment. Drives automated escalation workflows in Salesforce CRM.']
  hazardous_waste_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA assignment covers hazardous waste handling services subject to RCRA, DOT, and EPA manifest requirements. When true, stricter response time targets and regulatory compliance tracking apply.']
  initial_response_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which the first acknowledgment or response to a customer service request or complaint must be delivered under this SLA tier. Distinct from full resolution time.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this SLA assignment record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  missed_pickup_resolution_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which a missed waste pickup must be resolved and rescheduled under this SLA tier. Core operational commitment tracked against actual resolution times in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch.']
  municipal_contract_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA assignment is associated with a government or municipal contract subject to Local Solid Waste Authority regulations, public reporting requirements, and enhanced penalty clauses.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date for the next formal review of this SLA assignment. Calculated from the last review date plus the review cycle. Triggers account manager notifications in Salesforce CRM.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, negotiated exceptions, or operational instructions associated with this SLA assignment. Used by account managers and operations teams.']
  penalty_amount_per_breach decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary penalty amount (in USD) assessed per SLA breach event when penalty clauses are applicable. Used in Oracle Revenue Management for credit memo generation and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS contract liability standards.']
  penalty_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative monetary penalty (in USD) that can be assessed against Waste Management under this SLA assignment within a defined period, as stipulated in the contract. Prevents unbounded financial exposure on municipal contracts.']
  penalty_cap_period varchar [note: 'The time period over which the penalty cap amount is measured and reset (monthly, quarterly, or annual). Aligns with contract billing cycles in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  penalty_clause_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial penalty clauses are contractually applicable when SLA targets are breached under this assignment. Typically true for municipal government contracts governed by Local Solid Waste Authority agreements.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which SLA compliance performance reports are generated and delivered to the customer or regulatory authority under this assignment. Governs automated report scheduling in Oracle Revenue Management and Enviance EHS.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled reviews of this SLA assignment to assess performance, renegotiate targets, or update tier classification. Supports proactive contract management and customer retention workflows.']
  service_availability_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of scheduled service events (pickups, deliveries) that must be completed on time within the measurement period under this SLA tier. For example, 98.50 means 98.5% on-time service completion is required.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The category of waste management service to which this SLA assignment applies. Determines which operational teams and response time targets are activated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential_collection|commercial_collection|recycling|landfill|hazardous_waste|waste_to_energy|container_delivery|bulk_pickup — promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the SLA tier, used in system integrations, route management, and operational dispatching (e.g., STD, PREM, MUN, HAZ, EMG). Sourced from AMCS Platform and Salesforce CRM.']
  sla_tier_name varchar [note: 'Named classification of the SLA tier assigned to this customer or service enrollment (e.g., Standard, Premium, Municipal, Hazardous, Emergency, Custom). Drives response time targets and escalation thresholds across collection, billing, and customer service operations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this SLA assignment record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM for commercial contracts, SAP S/4HANA SD for billing-linked SLAs, Oracle Revenue Management for rate-linked SLAs, Waste Logics for portal-initiated assignments).']
  source_system_sla_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this SLA assignment record in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Contract ID, SAP SD Agreement Number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing in the Silver Layer.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate this SLA assignment before the effective end date. Contractually binding for municipal and commercial accounts.']

  Note: 'Associates specific Service Level Agreement (SLA) tiers to customer accounts and service enrollments, defining response time commitments, missed pickup resolution windows, complaint resolution targets, and container delivery lead times. Captures SLA tier name, applicable service types, response time targets (hours), escalation thresholds, penalty clauses for municipal contracts, effective date range, and SLA compliance tracking flag. Bridges customer commitments with operational execution standards across collection, billing, and customer service teams.'
}

Table customer.complaint {
  complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each formal customer complaint record in the Waste Management complaint management system.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific waste container (identified by CID) involved in the complaint, applicable for container damage, missed pickup, or contamination complaints.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Customer complaints often trigger unscheduled compliance inspections by EPA, state environmental agencies, or local health departments. Linking complaints to resulting inspections enables response tra']
  contamination_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.contamination_event. Business justification: Customer complaints about rejected loads or contamination surcharges directly reference documented contamination events at MRF. Required for billing dispute resolution and customer education on materi']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Complaints frequently cite SLA breaches, service level violations, or billing disputes requiring contract term verification. Resolution workflows need agreement_id to validate customer entitlements, p']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that submitted or is associated with this complaint. Links to the customer_account master record.']
  emergency_response_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.emergency_response_incident. Business justification: Customer complaints about spills, releases, or hazmat incidents must link to formal emergency response records for NRC reporting, regulatory investigation tracking, liability management, and correctiv']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent assigned as the primary responsible party for resolving this complaint. Drives accountability tracking, workload balancing, and resolution performance metrics.']
  enviance_incident_id varchar [note: 'External system identifier for complaints that generated an environmental health and safety incident record in Enviance EHS, applicable for environmental concern and hazardous waste complaints requiring regulatory tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Complaints about odor, noise, dust, or environmental violations are often facility-specific (e.g., landfill odor complaints, MRF noise complaints). Regulatory agencies require facility attribution for']
  interaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating customer interaction (call, chat, portal submission) that generated this formal complaint, enabling traceability from first contact.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Customers file complaints about specific landfill sites (odor, dust, traffic, environmental concerns). Regulatory agencies require complaint tracking by facility for permit compliance and community re']
  pickup_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.pickup_event. Business justification: Complaints about specific service events (contamination, property damage, missed lift, spillage) need to reference the exact pickup event for proof-of-service verification, RFID scan validation, photo']
  prior_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous complaint record when repeat_complaint_flag is true, enabling complaint recurrence chain analysis and chronic service failure identification.']
  resolution_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Complaints (missed pickups, property damage, billing disputes) are routed to and resolved by specific operational units. Enables unit-level performance metrics, regulatory reporting to municipal autho']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route associated with the complaint incident, enabling route-level quality analysis, driver accountability, and operational root cause investigation.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service address where the complaint-triggering event occurred, enabling geographic and route-level analysis.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Complaints about driver behavior, property damage, noise violations, or service quality are tied to specific vehicles reported by customers (license plate, unit number). Critical for fleet performance']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Customer complaints about environmental impacts (odor, noise, spills, illegal dumping) can trigger regulatory investigations resulting in violation notices. Tracking complaint-to-violation linkage is ']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the remediation or service recovery Work Order (WO) created to resolve the complaint, such as a re-collection dispatch or container replacement order.']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Community complaints about odor, emissions, noise, or environmental impacts from WTE facilities require facility linkage for regulatory agency response, permit compliance tracking, and community relat']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Functional team responsible for complaint resolution. Environmental compliance team handles EPA/OSHA-reportable complaints; legal team handles complaints with litigation potential.']
  billing_dispute_amount_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount in dispute for billing-related complaints. Populated only when complaint_type is billing_dispute. Used for accounts receivable (AR) adjustment tracking and revenue impact analysis.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was formally closed after customer confirmation or timeout period. Distinct from resolved_timestamp as closure may follow resolution after a customer satisfaction confirmation window.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the complaint in detail as reported by the customer or regulatory agency. Captures the full context of the issue including specific circumstances, locations, and impacts.']
  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable complaint reference number communicated to the customer and used in regulatory correspondence. Format: CMP-YYYY-NNNNNNN.']
  complaint_source varchar [note: 'Channel or origination point through which the complaint was received. Regulatory agency source indicates complaints filed directly by EPA, OSHA, DOT, or state environmental bodies requiring mandatory response timelines.']
  complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the complaint from initial receipt through resolution and closure. Drives SLA tracking and operational dashboards.']
  complaint_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the complaint subject matter. Missed pickup covers scheduled collection failures; billing dispute covers invoice or charge disagreements; driver behavior covers conduct or safety concerns; odor/noise covers nuisance impacts; environmental concern covers spills, leachate, or regulatory issues; container damage covers physical damage to customer containers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the complaint record was first created in the data platform. May differ from received_timestamp if there was a data entry lag.']
  credit_amount_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar value of the billing or service credit issued to the customer as resolution for the complaint. Populated only when credit_issued_flag is true. Feeds into revenue management and EBITDA impact reporting.']
  credit_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a billing credit or service credit was issued to the customer as part of the complaint resolution. Supports accounts receivable (AR) adjustment tracking and revenue impact reporting.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction score on a 1-5 scale collected via follow-up survey. Feeds into NPS (Net Promoter Score) and customer retention analytics. Null if survey not completed.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint was escalated internally beyond the initial assigned team (e.g., from customer service to operations management or legal). Distinct from regulatory_escalation_flag which tracks external regulatory body involvement.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the complaint was escalated, capturing the business justification for escalation such as SLA breach, repeat complaint, high-value customer, or regulatory risk.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer received the first substantive response to their complaint. Used to calculate first response time against SLA targets and regulatory response obligations.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the underlying service failure or incident that prompted the complaint actually occurred. May differ from received_timestamp if the customer reported the issue after the fact.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice or billing document number referenced in a billing dispute complaint. Enables direct linkage to the Oracle Revenue Management billing record for dispute resolution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording the most recent update to the complaint record, supporting change tracking and data lineage for regulatory audit purposes.']
  legal_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint record is subject to a legal hold, preventing deletion or modification of associated records. Set when litigation is threatened or initiated.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was formally received and logged in the system. This is the principal business event timestamp used as the SLA clock start for all response time calculations.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific regulatory body involved when regulatory_escalation_flag is true. Determines applicable response timelines, reporting formats, and compliance obligations. LEA (Local Enforcement Agency) complaints are captured under local_enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|DOT|state_env_agency|local_enforcement|CERCLA|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint requires mandatory reporting or response to a regulatory body (EPA, OSHA, DOT, state environmental agency). When true, triggers regulatory response workflow with statutory deadlines.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'External case or docket number assigned by the regulatory agency (EPA, OSHA, DOT) for tracking the complaint in their system. Required for regulatory correspondence and compliance documentation.']
  repeat_complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint is a recurrence of a previously filed complaint for the same customer and same issue type within a rolling 90-day window. Repeat complaints trigger enhanced resolution workflows and root cause analysis requirements.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken to resolve the complaint, such as re-collection dispatched, billing credit issued, driver counseling completed, or environmental remediation performed.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the complaint resolution. Resolved_accepted means customer confirmed satisfaction; resolved_rejected means customer disputed the resolution; referred_to_regulatory means the complaint was escalated to an external regulatory body for adjudication.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was marked as resolved, meaning the root cause was addressed and corrective action completed. Used to calculate total resolution time against SLA targets.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the underlying operational or systemic cause of the complaint (e.g., driver_error, route_planning_failure, equipment_malfunction, billing_system_error, weather_delay, container_overfill). Used for operational quality improvement and recurring issue analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: driver_error|route_planning_failure|equipment_malfunction|billing_system_error|weather_delay|container_overfill|access_obstruction|contamination|permit_violation|staffing_shortage — promote to reference product]']
  salesforce_case_number varchar [note: 'External system identifier for this complaint record in Salesforce CRM, enabling bidirectional synchronization and audit traceability between the data lakehouse and the operational CRM system.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of waste collection or environmental service associated with the complaint. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) residential and commercial are the most common; hazardous waste complaints trigger RCRA and TSDF compliance workflows. C&D refers to Construction and Demolition Waste. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential_msw|commercial_msw|recycling|hazardous_waste|c_and_d|bulk_item|rolloff — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Operational severity rating assigned to the complaint indicating urgency and resource prioritization. Critical severity triggers immediate escalation workflows and may require regulatory notification.']
  sla_resolution_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Calculated deadline by which the complaint must be fully resolved and closed based on severity level and SLA tier. Distinct from response due timestamp which only covers initial acknowledgment.']
  sla_response_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Calculated deadline by which the complaint must receive an initial response based on severity level and SLA tier. Regulatory complaints have statutory response deadlines that override standard SLA tiers.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular detail within the complaint type (e.g., under missed_pickup: residential_route_skip, commercial_container_not_serviced; under billing_dispute: incorrect_tipping_fee, unauthorized_surcharge). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: route_skip|container_not_serviced|incorrect_tipping_fee|unauthorized_surcharge|spill_incident|property_damage|aggressive_driving|noise_violation|odor_complaint|hazmat_concern — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Formal customer complaints requiring structured resolution workflows, distinct from general service requests. Captures complaint type (missed pickup, billing dispute, driver behavior, odor/noise, environmental concern, container damage), severity level, regulatory escalation flag (EPA/OSHA reportable), complaint source (phone, portal, social media, regulatory agency, legal), assigned resolution owner, resolution status, root cause code, and resolution timestamp. Supports regulatory response obligations, operational quality improvement, and customer retention management.'
}

Table customer.customer_program_enrollment {
  customer_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_program_enrollment']
  circular_economy_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the circular economy program in which the customer is enrolled']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account participating in the circular economy program']
  program_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this enrollment record. Primary key for the association.']
  customer_program_enrollment_date date [note: 'The date when the customer account was enrolled in the circular economy program. Tracks when participation commenced.']
  customer_program_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment. Active = currently participating, Suspended = temporarily paused, Completed = program term ended successfully, Cancelled = customer withdrew.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this enrollment ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing enrollments. Populated when status changes to Completed or Cancelled.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this enrollment became effective and services commenced. May differ from enrollment_date if there was a future-dated enrollment.']
  estimated_annual_diversion_volume_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated annual volume of material in tons that this specific customer is expected to divert from landfill through this program. Used for program planning and customer performance tracking.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'The type of incentive offered to this customer for participation in the program. Varies by customer tier and program type.']
  incentive_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value in USD of the incentive provided to this customer for program participation. May be annual rebate, per-ton credit, or percentage discount depending on incentive_type.']
  material_streams_included varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific material types this customer is participating with in this program (e.g., food_waste, electronics, packaging). Subset of the program\'s target_material_streams tailored to this customer.']
  participation_tier varchar [note: 'The service tier level for this customer\'s participation in the program, determining service frequency, reporting detail, and incentive eligibility.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between customer_account and circular_economy_program. It captures the participation terms, material streams, performance targets, and incentive structures for each customer\'s participation in a circular economy program. Each record links one customer_account to one circular_economy_program with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management\'s circular economy operations, customer accounts can simultaneously enroll in multiple programs (e.g., a commercial customer participating in both food waste composting and electronics recycling programs), and each program serves multiple customers across different segments. The enrollment relationship is actively managed by sustainability program managers who track participation terms, material streams, diversion targets, and incentive structures specific to each customer-program combination.'
}

Table customer.service_event {
  service_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each service event record. Primary key.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who performed this service event. Critical for safety tracking, performance management, and operational accountability. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the route on which this service event occurred. Links the event to the planned route for variance analysis and route optimization. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service address where this collection event occurred']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vehicle that performed this collection service']
  collection_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of collection for this service event (Daily, Weekly, Biweekly, Monthly, On-Demand). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  container_count_serviced varchar [note: 'Number of waste containers (bins, carts, dumpsters) actually serviced during this event. May differ from the total container count at the address. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this service event record was created in the operational system.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any service exception or issue encountered during this event (e.g., ACCESS_BLOCKED, CONTAMINATION, OVERWEIGHT, WEATHER). Null if service completed without issues.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the driver or dispatcher describing any service exceptions, access issues, or operational notes for this event.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate captured by the vehicle telematics system at the time of service. Used to validate service location and route adherence.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate captured by the vehicle telematics system at the time of service. Used to validate service location and route adherence.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of this service event. Used for vehicle maintenance scheduling and mileage tracking.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the waste collection or environmental service was performed at this service address by this vehicle. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent at this service address during this event measured in minutes, from arrival to departure. Used for route optimization and productivity analysis. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle completed service and departed the service address. Used for precise route timing and productivity calculation.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle arrived at the service address and began service operations. Used for precise route timing and GPS validation.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of this service event (Completed, Missed, Partial, Blocked, Rescheduled). Used for service quality tracking and customer communication.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of waste collection service performed during this event (e.g., MSW, Recycling, Yard Waste, Bulk, Hazardous, Roll-off). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  tonnage_collected decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of waste collected during this service event measured in tons. Critical operational metric for vehicle payload tracking and billing. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this service event record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational service event between a service address and a vehicle. It captures each instance of waste collection, recycling pickup, or environmental service delivery performed by a specific vehicle at a specific service address. Each record represents one service transaction with operational details including service date, waste tonnage collected, service duration, driver assignment, and route context. This is the core transactional record of waste collection operations.. Existence Justification: In waste collection operations, service addresses are serviced by multiple vehicles over time (different days, backup vehicles, seasonal equipment changes, specialized waste streams), and each vehicle services hundreds of different addresses daily across its assigned routes. The business actively manages and tracks each service event as a distinct operational transaction with specific metrics (tonnage, duration, driver, exceptions) that belong to neither the address nor the vehicle alone, but to the service interaction itself.'
}

Table customer.transporter_authorization {
  transporter_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this customer-transporter authorization record. Primary key for the association.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that has authorized this transporter for hazardous waste transportation services.']
  transporter_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the EPA-registered transporter that has been authorized by this customer account.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this customer approved this transporter for use on hazardous waste manifests. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this customer-specific transporter authorization. Pending = under review, Approved = active for use on manifests, Suspended = temporarily inactive, Revoked = permanently removed, Expired = approval period ended.']
  authorized_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Waste Management employee or customer representative who approved this transporter authorization. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  authorized_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D, F, K, P, U series) that this customer has authorized this specific transporter to handle. May be a subset of the transporter\'s full authorization list based on customer-specific risk assessment and insurance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this authorization record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this customer-specific authorization expires and must be renewed. Null for indefinite authorizations. Customers may set expiration dates for periodic compliance reviews.']
  insurance_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the customer last verified that this transporter\'s liability insurance meets the customer\'s requirements for this authorization. Customers may have insurance requirements beyond EPA minimums.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this authorization record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing customer-specific requirements, restrictions, or special handling instructions for this transporter authorization. May include geographic restrictions, time-of-day constraints, or facility-specific requirements.']
  preferred_transporter_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transporter is a preferred carrier for this customer account. Preferred transporters receive priority consideration during manifest generation and route optimization. Each customer may have multiple preferred transporters for different waste types or geographic regions.']
  rate_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the negotiated rate agreement or contract between this customer and this transporter. Links to contract management system or pricing tables for customer-specific transportation rates.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between customer_account and transporter_registration. It captures customer-specific approvals for EPA-registered hazardous waste transporters to transport waste on behalf of the customer. Each record links one customer_account to one transporter_registration with attributes that govern the authorization terms, approved waste codes, insurance verification, and preferred carrier status for manifest generation and regulatory compliance.. Existence Justification: In hazardous waste operations, customer accounts authorize multiple EPA-registered transporters to handle their waste shipments, and each transporter serves multiple customer accounts. The authorization is an active business process managed by hazmat compliance teams: customers conduct due diligence on transporters, verify insurance, approve specific waste codes, negotiate rates, and designate preferred carriers. Each authorization is a regulatory compliance record with customer-specific terms that differ from the transporter\'s general registration.'
}

Table customer.subscription {
  subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this service subscription record. Primary key.']
  account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that holds this service subscription']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to customer_account. Links this subscription to the customer account.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to offering. Links this subscription to the waste management service offering.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to service_address if this subscription is tied to a specific service location. Critical for route planning and collection operations. Null for account-level services.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific subscription is set to automatically renew at contract expiration. Customer may opt in or out regardless of the offerings default setting. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when this subscription was cancelled or is scheduled to be cancelled. Null for active subscriptions. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for subscription cancellation. Null for active subscriptions. Used for churn analysis and retention strategy.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the contract term for this subscription, calculated from contract_start_date plus contract_term_months. May be extended if auto_renewal_flag is true.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the contract term for this subscription, which may differ from enrollment_date if there was a delayed start or pre-enrollment period.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the contract term for this specific subscription. May differ from the offerings default minimum term due to negotiated agreements. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscription record was created in the data platform.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the customer account enrolled in or activated this specific service offering. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  monthly_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted monthly rate for this customer-offering subscription, which may differ from the base price due to negotiated discounts, volume pricing, or promotional rates. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  pricing_tier_override varchar [note: 'Optional pricing tier override for this specific subscription if the customer negotiated special pricing that differs from their account-level tier.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this specific subscription. Active = service is being delivered, Suspended = temporarily halted, Pending_Cancellation = notice given, Cancelled = terminated, Expired = contract ended. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscription record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual subscription relationship between a customer account and a waste management service offering. It captures the enrollment, pricing, contract terms, and lifecycle status of each customer-service combination. Each record links one customer_account to one offering with attributes that exist only in the context of this subscription relationship, including enrollment dates, monthly rates, contract duration, auto-renewal settings, and cancellation tracking.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, customer accounts routinely subscribe to multiple service offerings simultaneously (e.g., a commercial customer may have weekly trash collection, bi-weekly recycling, monthly hazmat pickup, and on-demand roll-off rentals). Conversely, each service offering serves thousands of customer accounts. The business actively manages these subscriptions as distinct operational entities with enrollment dates, negotiated pricing, contract terms, and lifecycle status tracking.'
}

Table customer.customer_buyer_contract {
  customer_buyer_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_buyer_contract']
  buyer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this buyer contract record. Primary key for the association.']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the commodity buyer purchasing recovered materials under this contract. Buyers typically establish contracts with multiple customer accounts to diversify their supply sources and ensure consistent material flow.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that is selling recovered materials through this contract. Large commercial and industrial customers with recycling programs may have multiple contracts with different buyers to diversify their commodity sales channels.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides termination notice.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity sales contract expires or terminates. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable contract reference number used in business communications and legal documentation for this specific buyer-customer agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity sales contract becomes effective. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this buyer contract. Active contracts govern current commodity sales transactions.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this buyer contract record was created in the system.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this buyer contract record was last updated.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms specific to this buyer-customer contract, which may differ from the buyer\'s standard payment terms based on negotiation. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing structure tier applied to this specific buyer-customer contract. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data. Different buyers may receive different pricing tiers from the same customer based on volume, payment terms, and relationship strength.']
  quality_grade_requirement varchar [note: 'Quality specification or grade standard required by this buyer for materials supplied under this contract (e.g., ISRI specs, contamination thresholds). Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  volume_commitment_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum tonnage of recovered materials the customer commits to supply to this buyer over the contract period. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual agreement between a customer account and a commodity buyer for the sale of recovered materials from recycling operations. It captures the commercial terms, pricing, volume commitments, and quality requirements that govern each buyer-customer relationship. Each record links one customer_account to one commodity_buyer with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific supply contract.. Existence Justification: In waste management recycling operations, large commercial and industrial customer accounts with recycling programs actively diversify their commodity sales by establishing contracts with multiple buyers to optimize pricing and reduce market risk. Simultaneously, commodity buyers (mills, brokers, reprocessors) establish supply contracts with multiple customer accounts to ensure consistent material flow and supply chain resilience. The detection phase explicitly identified that a buyer_contract table already exists modeling this M:N relationship with contract-specific terms, pricing, and volume commitments.'
}

Table customer.customer_service_commitment {
  customer_service_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_service_commitment']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the master service agreement governing this service commitment']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical service location where contracted services are rendered']
  service_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each service commitment record linking a service address to a contract']
  billing_account_override varchar [note: 'Optional override billing account for this specific service commitment. Used when a multi-site contract requires individual addresses to bill to different cost centers or subsidiaries. Nullable if contract customer_account_id applies.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this service commitment. Active indicates services are being rendered. Suspended indicates temporary service hold. Terminated indicates services have ceased. Pending_activation indicates contract signed but services not yet commenced.']
  container_allocation varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of container types and quantities allocated to this address under this specific contract (e.g., \'2x96gal-carts,1x8yd-dumpster\'). Tracks which containers are deployed under which contractual agreement when an address has multiple concurrent contracts for different waste streams.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this service commitment record was created in the system']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this service commitment record was last updated']
  rate_override decimal(15,2) [note: 'Location-specific service rate that overrides the contract base_service_rate. Used when individual addresses within a master contract have negotiated different pricing based on volume, accessibility, or service complexity. Nullable if contract base rate applies.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date when services ceased at this address under this contract. Nullable for active commitments. May differ from contract expiration_date when individual addresses are removed from multi-site agreements.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'Collection frequency specific to this address-contract combination. May override the address default service_frequency when contract terms specify different pickup schedules (e.g., commercial contract requiring daily pickup vs. standard weekly).']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date when waste collection or environmental services commenced at this specific address under this contract. May differ from contract effective_date when addresses are added mid-term to existing master agreements.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier applicable to this specific address-contract combination. Defines response time commitments, service guarantees, and escalation procedures. May vary by address within a master contract based on site criticality or negotiated terms.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'Specific waste stream covered by this service commitment. Critical for addresses with multiple concurrent contracts - one contract may cover MSW collection while another covers recycling, and a third covers hazardous waste handling.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between service_address and contract. It captures the binding of a specific physical service location to a master service agreement with location-specific service parameters, rate overrides, and operational commitments. Each record links one service address to one contract with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, enabling multi-site commercial accounts with master contracts covering multiple locations, and individual addresses with multiple concurrent contracts for different waste streams.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, multi-site commercial customers (e.g., national retail chains, hospital systems) execute master service agreements that cover multiple physical service locations, while individual service addresses frequently require multiple concurrent contracts to handle different waste streams (MSW collection, recycling programs, hazardous waste handling). The service commitment represents the operational binding of a specific address to a specific contract with location-specific service parameters, rate overrides, and waste stream designations that exist only in the context of that address-contract pairing.'
}

Table customer.customer_stop {
  customer_stop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for customer_stop']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the master collection route definition']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical service address where waste collection is performed']
  stop_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this route stop assignment. Primary key.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this stop assignment ended or will end on this route. Null indicates currently active. Supports historical tracking of route optimization and service changes. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this stop assignment became active on this route. Supports temporal tracking of route changes and address reassignments. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  estimated_service_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes to complete service at this stop, used for route duration planning and optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Operational notes specific to this stop on this route (e.g., \'skip if gate locked\', \'coordinate with site manager\').']
  route_zone_code varchar [note: 'Operational route zone identifier assigned to this service address by the AMCS Platform or Locus Dispatch system. Determines which collection route and crew services this location. Critical for route optimization, driver assignment, and service scheduling. [Moved from service_address: This attribute currently exists on service_address but represents the operational assignment to a route zone, which is a collection planning concept. It should be derived from the stop-route relationship rather than stored redundantly on the address.]']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The sequential order of this stop within the route execution. Used by drivers to navigate the route efficiently. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_day varchar [note: 'Day of the week on which waste collection or environmental services are regularly scheduled at this address. Used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch for route planning and customer communication. [Moved from service_address: This attribute currently exists on service_address but represents when collection occurs, which depends on which route services the address. Since an address can be serviced by multiple routes (different waste streams, seasonal routes), this belongs on the stop association as service_day_of_week.] [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_day_of_week varchar [note: 'The specific day of the week this stop is serviced on this route. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this specific stop is serviced on this route (may differ from route default frequency). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  stop_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this stop on this route. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of a service address to a collection route. It captures the specific stop sequence, service schedule, and temporal validity of each address-route pairing. Each record links one service address to one route with attributes that exist only in the context of this scheduled collection relationship. This is the operational entity that route planners and dispatchers actively manage when building, optimizing, and executing daily collection routes.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, a service address can legitimately be serviced by multiple routes simultaneously (e.g., one route for municipal solid waste on Tuesdays, a different route for recycling on Fridays, a bulk waste route on-demand). Conversely, each route services hundreds of addresses. Route planners actively manage the stop assignment as an operational entity, optimizing stop sequences, adjusting service frequencies, and tracking temporal validity when addresses are reassigned between routes due to route optimization or service changes.'
}

Table customer.contact_contract_role {
  contact_contract_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each contact-contract role assignment record']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contact record representing the individual person assigned to this contract role']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contract record for which this contact role assignment applies']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management user who created this contact-contract role assignment. Used for audit and accountability']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this role assignment record was created in the system. Used for audit trails']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Functional role this contact plays for this specific contract. Determines routing of notifications, approval authority, and communication workflows. Values: billing (receives invoices), service (operational issues), signatory (contract execution authority), emergency (incident notifications), legal (compliance/disputes), operations (day-to-day coordination), primary (main point of contact)']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this contact began serving in this role for this contract. Used for audit trails and historical role tracking']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact ceased serving in this role for this contract. Nullable for active role assignments. Supports role transition tracking']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to this role assignment record. Used for change tracking']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel for contract-specific notifications to this contact in this role. Overrides the contacts global preferred_contact_method for this contract context. Values: email, sms, phone, portal, none']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for this specific contract. Used for escalation routing and default communication recipient. Only one contact per contract should have this flag set to true at any time']
  role_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this role assignment. Active assignments receive notifications and appear in operational workflows. Inactive assignments are historical. Suspended assignments are temporarily disabled. Values: active, inactive, suspended']
  signature_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has legal authority to execute amendments, renewals, or terminations for this contract. Used in approval workflows and contract change management processes']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Role relationship between contact and contract. It captures the specific functional roles that individual contacts play in the lifecycle and execution of service agreements. Each record links one contact to one contract with role-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, enabling proper routing of notifications, approval workflows, and communication based on contact responsibilities.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management operations, a single contact can serve in multiple roles across different contracts (e.g., Jane Smith is the billing contact for the municipal MSW contract, service contact for the commercial hauling agreement, and signatory for the hazardous waste disposal contract). Conversely, each contract requires multiple designated contacts serving distinct functional roles (billing recipient, operational coordinator, emergency contact, legal signatory, primary account manager). The business actively manages these role assignments through contract setup, amendment, and renewal workflows, routing notifications and approvals based on contact-contract-role combinations.'
}

Table customer.facility_allocation_agreement {
  facility_allocation_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the facility allocation agreement record. Primary key for the association.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that has contracted for waste disposal capacity at the WTE facility']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Waste-to-Energy facility providing waste disposal and energy conversion services']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the facility allocation agreement. Active = currently routing waste; Suspended = temporarily paused; Expired = past expiration_date; Terminated = cancelled before expiration; Pending = signed but not yet effective.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews upon expiration. True = agreement renews for another term unless cancelled; False = agreement expires without action.']
  contracted_annual_tonnage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tonnage of waste the customer has committed to deliver to this WTE facility annually under the agreement terms. Used for capacity planning and minimum volume commitments.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility allocation agreement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the facility allocation agreement becomes active and waste routing begins under the contracted terms.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the facility allocation agreement terminates. May be null for evergreen agreements or those with automatic renewal clauses.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility allocation agreement record was last updated.']
  minimum_monthly_tonnage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum tonnage the customer must deliver monthly to avoid contract penalties or minimum billing charges. Null if no minimum commitment exists.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Routing priority rank for this facility when the customer has multiple facility allocation agreements. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for waste routing decisions.']
  tipping_fee_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contracted price per ton the customer pays for waste disposal at this WTE facility. May vary by customer tier, volume commitments, and contract negotiation.']
  waste_stream_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the customer\'s total waste stream committed to this specific WTE facility under the agreement. Used for routing decisions when customer has agreements with multiple facilities.']
  waste_type_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of waste stream types permitted or prohibited under this agreement based on facility capabilities and permit restrictions (e.g., \'MSW only, no hazardous\', \'Commercial waste, no medical\').']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual agreement between a customer account and a Waste-to-Energy facility for waste disposal capacity allocation. It captures the committed tonnage, waste stream allocation percentages, contract terms, and priority routing rules that govern how customer waste is directed to specific WTE facilities. Each record links one customer account to one WTE facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this contractual relationship.. Existence Justification: Large commercial and municipal customer accounts contract with multiple WTE facilities to ensure waste disposal capacity, geographic coverage, and backup routing options. Each WTE facility serves multiple customer accounts under individual contractual terms. The business actively manages these facility allocation agreements as operational contracts with specific tonnage commitments, pricing, allocation percentages, and priority routing rules that vary by customer-facility combination.'
}

Table customer.survey {
  survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for survey']
  revised_survey_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on survey (revised_survey_id)']
  actual_response_count varchar [note: 'Current count of completed survey responses received. Updated as responses are submitted.']
  anonymous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether respondent identity is captured (false) or kept anonymous (true). Anonymous surveys may improve response rates for sensitive topics.']
  consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit consent must be obtained from respondents before they can participate in the survey, typically required for surveys collecting personal data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that survey response data will be retained before archival or deletion, in accordance with data governance and privacy policies.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the survey is distributed to respondents: email, SMS text message, customer web portal, mobile app, interactive voice response (IVR), paper mail, or in-person interview.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the survey closes and no longer accepts new responses. Null for surveys with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the survey becomes active and available for respondents to complete.']
  estimated_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes for a respondent to complete the survey, used to set respondent expectations and improve response rates.']
  external_survey_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to this survey by the external survey platform or vendor system. Used for integration and reconciliation.']
  incentive_description varchar [note: 'Description of the incentive offered to survey respondents (e.g., \'$10 account credit\', \'Entry into monthly prize draw\', \'10% discount on next service\'). Null if no incentive is offered.']
  incentive_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether respondents are offered an incentive (e.g., discount, gift card, prize entry) for completing the survey.']
  invitation_sent_count varchar [note: 'Total number of survey invitations distributed to potential respondents.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that most recently modified the survey record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the survey record was most recently updated.']
  max_reminder_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of reminder messages that will be sent to a non-respondent before ceasing outreach. Null if reminders are not enabled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or operational notes about the survey.']
  privacy_notice_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether respondents are presented with a privacy notice explaining how their survey data will be used, stored, and protected.']
  question_count varchar [note: 'Total number of questions included in the survey instrument.']
  reminder_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated reminder messages are sent to non-respondents to encourage survey completion.']
  reminder_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between automated reminder messages sent to non-respondents. Null if reminders are not enabled.']
  response_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of survey invitations that resulted in completed responses, calculated as (actual_response_count / invitation_sent_count) * 100.']
  response_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all questions in the survey require a response (true) or if some questions are optional (false).']
  survey_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the survey by its timing and trigger: transactional (post-service), relationship (periodic customer health check), event-triggered (after specific incident), periodic (scheduled intervals), or ad-hoc (one-time special purpose).']
  survey_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the survey in operational systems and reports (e.g., \'CSAT-Q1-2024\', \'NPS-ANN\').']
  survey_language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the survey is presented, using ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish, FRA for French).']
  survey_name varchar [note: 'The business name or title of the survey (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Customer Satisfaction Survey\', \'Annual NPS Survey\').']
  survey_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit, department, or individual responsible for designing, deploying, and analyzing this survey (e.g., \'Customer Experience Team\', \'Operations Quality\', \'Regional Manager - Northeast\').']
  survey_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the technology platform or tool used to administer the survey (e.g., \'Qualtrics\', \'SurveyMonkey\', \'Medallia\', \'Salesforce Feedback Management\', \'Custom Portal\').']
