  survey_purpose varchar [note: 'Detailed business purpose and objectives of the survey, explaining what insights or decisions it is intended to support.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey: draft (being designed), active (accepting responses), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (closed to responses), archived (historical record), or cancelled (discontinued).']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey by its primary purpose: customer satisfaction (CSAT), net promoter score (NPS), service quality assessment, driver performance feedback, facility feedback, or complaint resolution follow-up.']
  survey_url varchar [note: 'Web address (URL) where respondents can access and complete the survey online.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the survey instrument, incremented when questions or structure are modified (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Enables tracking of survey changes over time.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'The intended respondent population for this survey: residential customers, commercial accounts, industrial clients, municipal contracts, all customer segments, or internal staff.']
  target_response_count varchar [note: 'Desired number of completed survey responses needed to achieve statistical significance or business objectives.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the survey record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for survey. Referenced by survey_id.'
}

Table customer.referral_program {
  referral_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for referral_program']
  parent_referral_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on referral_program (parent_referral_program_id)']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new referrals under this program require management approval before rewards are issued.']
  auto_approval_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether referrals under this program are automatically approved when qualification criteria are met, or require manual review.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for customer inquiries and support related to this referral program.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for customer inquiries and support related to this referral program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this referral program record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the referral program expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the referral program becomes active and available for customer participation.']
  eligible_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of states, regions, or service areas where this referral program is active and customers can participate.']
  eligible_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of waste management service types eligible for this referral program, such as residential collection, commercial dumpster, recycling, or hazardous waste services.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage level of the referral program indicating where the program is available to customers.']
  integration_system varchar [note: 'Name of the external system integrated with this referral program, such as Salesforce CRM or Waste Logics customer portal.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral program can be combined with other promotions, discounts, or referral programs.']
  marketing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channels used to promote this referral program, such as email, social media, customer portal, direct mail, or in-person.']
  maximum_referrals_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of successful referrals a single customer can make within the program period. Null indicates no limit.']
  minimum_service_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the referred customer must maintain active service before the referral qualifies for rewards.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this referral program record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this referral program record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments about the referral program for operational reference and program management.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to determine which referral program applies when a customer is eligible for multiple programs. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  program_budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the referral program including all rewards and administrative costs.']
  program_budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the program budget amount.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the referral program used in external communications and customer-facing materials.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the referral program including terms, conditions, and benefits for both referrer and referee.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the referral program displayed to customers and used in promotional materials.']
  program_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit, department, or individual responsible for managing this referral program.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral program indicating whether it is available for customer participation.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the referral program by target customer segment: residential households, commercial businesses, industrial clients, municipal contracts, or mixed segments.']
  qualification_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules and conditions that must be met for a referral to qualify for rewards, such as minimum service duration, payment requirements, or service type restrictions.']
  referee_reward_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value or quantity of the reward provided to the referee upon successful enrollment and qualification.']
  referee_reward_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the referee reward amount. Waste Management primarily operates in USD.']
  referee_reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive or reward provided to the new customer who was referred (the referee).']
  referrer_reward_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value or quantity of the reward provided to the referrer upon successful referral completion.']
  referrer_reward_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the referrer reward amount. Waste Management primarily operates in USD.']
  referrer_reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive or reward provided to the customer who makes the referral (the referrer).']
  reward_fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which rewards are delivered to customers: automatically through billing system, manually by customer service, or via third-party vendor.']
  reward_fulfillment_timing_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after qualification that rewards are delivered to customers.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web address linking to the full legal terms and conditions document for the referral program.']
  tracking_code_prefix varchar [note: 'Standardized prefix used in referral tracking codes to identify referrals associated with this program.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this referral program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for referral_program. Referenced by program_id.'
}

Table collection.route {
  route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the waste collection route. Primary key.']
  carbon_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_initiative. Business justification: Route optimization and electrification initiatives directly target collection route emissions. Tracking which routes participate in carbon reduction programs enables initiative performance measurement']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Routes execute service commitments defined in customer contracts. Route planning, pricing validation, and SLA compliance reporting require linking routes to their governing agreements. Essential for c']
  disposal_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary disposal facility (landfill, transfer station, Materials Recovery Facility (MRF), or Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plant) where collected waste is delivered.']
  district_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational district or region to which this route is assigned.']
  fuel_purchase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_purchase. Business justification: Routes plan fuel consumption; operations reconcile planned vs. actual fuel purchases for cost allocation and efficiency analysis. Fleet managers use this to validate fuel budgets per route and detect ']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_line. Business justification: Routes are organized by service line (residential, commercial, industrial) for operational planning, fleet assignment, and pricing model selection. Essential for route optimization and resource alloca']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Collection routes operate under facility operating permits defining service areas, waste types, and operational constraints. Routes must comply with permitted activities and capacity limits. Essential']
  primary_driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver typically assigned to this route. Actual daily assignments may vary and are tracked in route execution records.']
  capacity_constraint_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum tonnage capacity for this route based on truck payload limits and disposal facility constraints.']
  container_type_primary varchar [note: 'Primary type of waste container serviced on this route: cart (residential curbside), bin (small commercial), dumpster (large commercial), compactor, or roll-off container.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer segment served by this route: residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal accounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration is no longer effective. Null indicates the route is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this route configuration became or will become effective for scheduling and execution.']
  end_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the route end location (typically a disposal facility, transfer station, or return depot).']
  end_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the route end location (typically a disposal facility, transfer station, or return depot).']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes to complete the entire route under normal conditions, including drive time and service time.']
  estimated_fuel_consumption_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated fuel consumption in gallons for a typical execution of this route, used for budgeting and environmental reporting.']
  estimated_ghg_emissions_co2e_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated greenhouse gas emissions in kilograms of Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) for a typical execution of this route, supporting sustainability and EPA reporting.']
  estimated_tonnage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Expected total tonnage of waste to be collected on a typical execution of this route, used for capacity planning.']
  fuel_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of fuel required for vehicles assigned to this route: diesel, Compressed Natural Gas (CNG), Renewable Natural Gas (RNG), electric, or hybrid.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was last updated, reflecting any changes to route configuration, status, or attributes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes for drivers and dispatchers, including special instructions, access restrictions, or customer service considerations.']
  optimization_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Efficiency score (0-100) assigned by route optimization algorithms, reflecting factors such as mileage efficiency, stop density, and time utilization.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for this route, used in dispatch and resource allocation decisions: critical (must-run, high-value customers), high, normal, or low.']
  requires_cdl boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Commercial Driver License (CDL) is required to operate the vehicle type assigned to this route.']
  requires_hazmat_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous materials handling certification is required for drivers on this route due to the nature of waste collected.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the route, used in operational systems and driver communications. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by dispatch.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the route, often including geographic area or customer segment (e.g., \'Downtown Commercial MSW Monday\').']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route: active (in regular operation), inactive (temporarily not scheduled), suspended (on hold), planned (designed but not yet operational), or retired (permanently discontinued).']
  scheduled_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Primary day of the week this route is scheduled to run (for weekly/biweekly routes). Multiple days may result in multiple route records or a separate schedule table.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this route is executed: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or on-demand.']
  sla_on_time_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of stops that must be serviced within the scheduled time window to meet Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments.']
  start_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the route start location (typically a depot or transfer station).']
  start_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the route start location (typically a depot or transfer station).']
  total_mileage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned mileage for the route in miles, including travel to first stop, between stops, and return to facility.']
  total_stops varchar [note: 'Total number of scheduled service stops (customer pickup points) on this route.']
  truck_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of collection vehicle required for this route: Automated Side Loader (ASL), Front End Loader (FEL), Rear End Loader (REL), roll-off truck, or compactor truck.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Sub-district zone code for finer geographic segmentation within a district.']

  Note: 'Master record for a defined waste collection route, representing a named geographic sequence of stops assigned to a specific truck type (ASL, FEL, REL) and service line (MSW, C&D, bulk). Captures route code, district, zone, day-of-week schedule, estimated duration, total stops, total mileage, truck type requirement, service frequency, capacity constraints, and active status. Sourced from AMCS Platform route master. This is the canonical route definition — the template from which daily route executions are generated. One route may produce multiple executions per week depending on service frequency.'
}

Table collection.route_execution {
  route_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for a single day\'s execution of a route. Primary key representing the operational instance of a route on a specific service date.']
  air_emission_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.air_emission_event. Business justification: Route executions generate emissions events when vehicles experience mechanical failures, fuel spills, or exhaust system malfunctions requiring regulatory notification and emissions reporting.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_audit. Business justification: Safety audits are conducted on active route executions to verify driver behavior, vehicle condition, and operational compliance. Real-world safety programs audit routes in progress for behavioral obse']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Regulatory inspectors observe route executions during field inspections to verify collection protocols, safety standards, and permit compliance. Direct inspection-to-operation link for enforcement and']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Route executions fulfill contractual service obligations. Performance obligation satisfaction, revenue recognition, and SLA compliance measurement require linking executions to contracts. Critical for']
  disposal_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill, transfer station, or disposal facility where collected waste was delivered.']
  disposal_landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Route executions disposing directly at landfills (vs. transfer stations) need landfill site FK for operational tracking, capacity planning, waste flow analysis, and route-to-disposal reporting. Real d']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver assigned to execute this route on the service date.']
  fuel_purchase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_purchase. Business justification: Route executions consume fuel tracked in fuel purchases. Finance reconciles fuel_consumed_gallons against fuel_purchase records for cost accounting, variance analysis, and GL posting. Core operational']
  inbound_load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.inbound_load. Business justification: Collection route executions delivering recyclables directly to MRFs need linkage to the resulting inbound load for operational reconciliation, driver accountability, and diversion reporting. This supp']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Route executions transporting hazardous waste generate manifests for RCRA compliance. Essential for regulatory tracking of hazmat collection routes and proof-of-transport documentation required by EPA']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master route definition that was executed. Links to the planned route template.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Route supervisors monitor daily execution performance, approve route deviations, handle driver escalations, and ensure SLA compliance—core operational oversight in waste collection operations.']
  transfer_station_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transfer station used for waste consolidation during this route execution, if applicable.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection truck assigned to this route execution.']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Collection routes deliver MSW to WTE facilities for energy recovery. Route execution tracks actual disposal destination. Enables tonnage-to-energy correlation analysis, route planning optimization to ']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the route execution was completed or terminated. Captured from GPS or driver mobile device.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the driver began executing the route. Captured from GPS or driver mobile device.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the route execution was cancelled, if applicable. Null for completed routes.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of planned stops that were successfully completed. Calculated as (total_stops_completed / total_stops_planned) * 100.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route execution record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  customer_complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any customer complaints were logged against this route execution. True if service quality issues were reported.']
  dispatch_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from dispatch regarding special instructions, route changes, or operational considerations for this execution.']
  distance_traveled_miles decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled during route execution measured in miles. Captured from GPS telematics.']
  driver_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the driver regarding route conditions, exceptions, or issues encountered during execution.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route execution. Tracks progression from scheduled through completion or cancellation.']
  fuel_consumed_gallons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total fuel consumed by the vehicle during this route execution measured in gallons. Sourced from telematics or fuel card data.']
  helper_assigned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a helper or second crew member was assigned to assist with this route execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route execution record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  number_of_dumps varchar [note: 'Count of times the vehicle dumped its load at a disposal or transfer facility during this route execution.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route execution resulted in driver overtime hours. True if actual duration exceeded standard shift length.']
  planned_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled completion time for the route execution as defined in the dispatch plan.']
  planned_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for the route execution as defined in the dispatch plan.']
  route_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time from actual start to actual end of route execution measured in minutes.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route based on customer segment and waste stream. Determines service characteristics and equipment requirements.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident occurred during this route execution. True if any OSHA-reportable or internal safety event was logged.']
  service_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this route execution occurred. Represents the business event date for collection operations.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of service for this route. Defines the recurring collection pattern.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system that originated this route execution record. Primary sources are AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch.']
  total_stops_completed varchar [note: 'Number of collection stops successfully serviced during this route execution.']
  total_stops_missed varchar [note: 'Number of planned stops that were not serviced during this route execution. Includes skipped, inaccessible, and cancelled stops.']
  total_stops_planned varchar [note: 'Number of collection stops scheduled for this route execution based on the route definition and service calendar.']
  total_tonnage_collected decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of waste collected during this route execution measured in tons. Key operational metric for capacity planning and billing.']
  total_volume_collected_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total volume of waste collected during this route execution measured in cubic yards. Used for capacity utilization analysis.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition during route execution. Impacts safety, efficiency, and completion rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|ice|fog|wind|extreme_heat — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a single day\'s actual execution of a route. Represents the operational instance of a route on a specific date, including assigned driver, assigned truck, planned vs. actual start/end times, total stops completed, total stops missed, total tonnage collected (TPD), route completion percentage, fuel consumed, and execution status (in-progress, completed, incomplete, cancelled). Sourced from AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch. One record per route per service date.'
}

Table collection.collection_stop {
  collection_stop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service stop. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Stops serve customer locations under specific service agreements. Billing validation, service commitment verification, and contract scope audits require linking stops to their governing contracts. Fun']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account being serviced at this stop.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver assigned to service this stop.']
  generator_epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Stops at hazmat generator sites reference EPA ID for regulatory tracking and service authorization. Required for verifying generator permit status at point of service.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Each stop represents delivery of a specific service offering (weekly MSW, bi-weekly recycling, bulk pickup). Critical for service fulfillment tracking, billing validation, SLA compliance monitoring, a']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Service stops must comply with permit conditions regarding acceptable waste types, service frequencies, and operational hours. Permit violations at stops trigger compliance actions and service restric']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the route this stop belongs to. Links stop to its parent collection route.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service address where collection occurs.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Stops requiring special handling (restricted access, hazmat, customer escalation) need supervisor assignment for field resolution authority and customer communication during service delivery.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection vehicle assigned to service this stop.']
  access_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for accessing the service location, such as gate codes, key locations, or entry procedures.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the collection vehicle arrived at this stop, captured via GPS telematics or driver input.']
  actual_service_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when service activity was completed at this stop.']
  actual_service_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when service activity began at this stop.']
  cid_tag_identifiers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CID or RFID tag identifiers for containers at this stop, used for automated container tracking.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers serviced at this stop during the visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stop record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this stop was deactivated or service was terminated. Null for currently active stops.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this stop became active and eligible for service.']
  estimated_service_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes required to complete service at this stop, used for route time calculations.']
  geofence_radius_meters varchar [note: 'Radius in meters defining the geofence boundary around the stop location, used to trigger automated arrival/departure events.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stop is currently active and scheduled for service.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stop record was last updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the stop location in decimal degrees, used for GPS navigation and route optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the stop location in decimal degrees, used for GPS navigation and route optimization.']
  planned_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp when the collection vehicle is expected to arrive at this stop, based on route optimization.']
  planned_service_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the time window during which service is scheduled to occur.']
  planned_service_window_start timestamp [note: 'Beginning of the time window during which service is scheduled to occur, often used for commercial SLA commitments.']
  requires_backup boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the collection vehicle must back up to access this stop, relevant for safety and route planning.']
  requires_ppe boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether specialized personal protective equipment is required for service at this stop due to hazardous materials or safety concerns.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this stop within the route. Determines the order in which the driver visits stops.']
  service_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Designated day of the week when this stop is scheduled for service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'Recurring schedule pattern for service at this stop.']
  skip_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why service was not completed at this stop, if applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_access|blocked|weather|customer_request|equipment_failure|safety_concern|no_waste_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  skip_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for why service was skipped.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system from which this stop record originated.']
  special_handling_notes varchar [note: 'Additional instructions for service delivery, such as backup required, hazardous materials present, or customer preferences.']
  stop_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the stop, often used in driver manifests and dispatch communications.']
  stop_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stop within its service lifecycle.']
  stop_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stop based on customer segment and service characteristics.']
  volume_collected_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated volume in cubic yards of waste collected at this stop.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the waste material collected at this stop. MSW = Municipal Solid Waste, C&D = Construction and Demolition Waste.']
  weight_collected_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight in pounds of waste collected at this stop, captured via onboard scales or estimated.']

  Note: 'Master record for an individual service stop on a route, representing a physical service location (residential address or commercial site) with a defined sequence position. Captures stop sequence number, planned arrival window, estimated service duration, service address, customer account reference, container count, service type (curbside, alley, drive-in), access notes, CID/RFID tag identifiers for containers at this stop, special instructions (gate codes, back-up required), and stop status. Sourced from AMCS Platform and Waste Logics. SSOT for the stop-level service assignment and sequencing within a route.'
}

Table collection.pickup_event {
  pickup_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pickup event. Primary key for the pickup_event data product.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary container serviced during this pickup event. Container Identification (CID) is the unique identifier assigned to each waste container for tracking and service verification.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Inspectors witness individual pickup events to verify proper handling procedures, contamination controls, and safety compliance. Direct observation relationship for regulatory verification and enforce']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Pickup events are billable service transactions governed by contracts. Invoice generation, overage charge calculation, and proof-of-service documentation require linking events to contracts. Essential']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account associated with this pickup event.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who performed the pickup service.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Incidents during pickup (container tip-over, spill, injury) must link to the specific pickup event for root cause analysis, liability determination, workers compensation claims, and service quality tr']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Individual hazmat pickup events require manifest documentation per RCRA regulations. Links proof-of-service at container level to regulatory chain-of-custody for hazardous waste generators.']
  route_execution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this pickup event to the parent route execution instance during which the pickup occurred.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service address where the pickup occurred.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Exception pickups (contamination flags, safety incidents, customer disputes) require supervisor review and approval in real-time for service recovery and billing adjustments.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection vehicle used to perform the pickup.']
  arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collection vehicle arrived at the service stop, captured from GPS telemetry.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pickup event is billable to the customer account, used for revenue recognition and invoicing.']
  compaction_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volume reduction ratio achieved by the compaction mechanism during this pickup, calculated as original volume divided by compacted volume.']
  contamination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether contamination was detected in the waste stream, requiring special handling or customer notification.']
  contamination_type varchar [note: 'Type of contamination detected in the waste stream, if any, used for customer education and compliance tracking.']
  customer_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a customer signature was captured as proof of service delivery, typically for special waste or high-value services.']
  driver_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the driver regarding service conditions, customer interactions, or issues encountered during pickup.']
  estimated_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated weight of waste collected in pounds, derived from onboard scale sensors or volumetric calculations.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for service exception or non-completion, used for operational reporting and customer communication.']
  gps_accuracy_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Accuracy of the GPS reading in meters, indicating the precision of the location capture.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the vehicle location at the time of service completion, used for proof of service and route verification.']
  gps_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the vehicle location at the time of service completion, used for proof of service and route verification.']
  lift_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the Power Take-Off (PTO) sensor confirmed that the container was physically lifted and emptied.']
  lift_count varchar [note: 'Number of container lifts performed during this pickup event. Typically 1, but may be higher for multiple containers at a single stop.']
  overage_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional charge amount in USD for overage conditions such as excess weight or extra containers, applied per contract terms.']
  photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a photo was captured during service for proof of service or documentation of service issues.']
  photo_storage_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the stored photo image in the document management system, if a photo was captured.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup event record was first created in the data platform.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pickup event record was last updated in the data platform.']
  rfid_scan_status varchar [note: 'Status of the RFID scan attempt indicating whether the container tag was successfully read, failed to read, or encountered other issues.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'The Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tag number scanned from the container at time of service, providing automated proof of service.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a safety incident occurred during this pickup event, requiring incident reporting and investigation.']
  scheduled_pickup_time timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled time for this pickup as planned in the route optimization system.']
  service_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary charge amount for this pickup service in USD, used for billing and revenue tracking.']
  service_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pickup service was completed and the vehicle departed the stop.']
  service_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds spent at the stop from arrival to departure, used for route efficiency analysis.']
  service_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the pickup attempt indicating whether service was completed successfully or the reason for non-completion.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this pickup event data, used for data lineage and quality tracking.']
  stop_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this stop within the route execution, used for route optimization and performance analysis.']
  truck_loader_type varchar [note: 'Type of loading mechanism on the collection vehicle. ASL (Automated Side Loader), FEL (Front End Loader), REL (Rear End Loader), Roll-Off, or Side-Load.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'Type of waste stream collected during this pickup. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste), Recycling, C&D (Construction and Demolition Waste), Bulk, Yard Waste, or Hazardous.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of service, captured for safety analysis and route performance evaluation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the actual execution of a single waste pickup at a stop during a route execution, including container-level proof of service via RFID. Records timestamp of arrival, timestamp of service completion, driver ID, truck ID, container(s) serviced (by CID/RFID), RFID scan result (successful read, no read, damaged tag), lift confirmation from PTO sensor, waste stream type (MSW, recycling, C&D, bulk), estimated weight or lift count, GPS coordinates at time of service, compaction ratio recorded, and service outcome (completed, skipped, contaminated). Sourced from Locus Dispatch, Geotab telemetry, and AMCS Platform RFID readers. Core operational event of the collection domain — SSOT for both service execution and container-level proof-of-service.'
}

Table collection.service_exception {
  service_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service exception record. Primary key.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific container involved in the exception event, tracked via Container Identification (CID) system.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Service exceptions may trigger SLA penalties, credits, or breach notifications defined in contracts. Exception resolution, penalty calculation, and contract compliance reporting require linking except']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account affected by this service exception.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who reported or encountered the service exception.']
  emergency_response_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.emergency_response_incident. Business justification: Service exceptions involving hazmat spills or releases trigger emergency response incidents requiring NRC notification. Links operational exception tracking to regulatory incident reporting and remedi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the system user or employee who resolved and closed the exception.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Service exceptions (access blocked, contamination, equipment failure) can escalate to safety incidents. Tracking this relationship is essential for identifying systemic hazards, preventing recurrence,']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Service exceptions (container damage, equipment failure at stop, access issues requiring physical changes) often trigger maintenance work orders for corrective action. Tracking the resulting WO enable']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Service exceptions are tracked against specific offerings for SLA breach analysis, credit issuance, and service quality monitoring. Critical for customer satisfaction management, operational performan']
  primary_service_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route where the exception occurred.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service address where the exception occurred.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating service request that this exception is associated with.']

Business justification: Service exceptions involving spills or releases of hazardous materials trigger spill_release incident reporting and regulatory notification to environmental agencies per CERCLA and state requirements.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection vehicle involved in the exception event.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Service exceptions may be caused by vendor performance issues (vendor-operated equipment failure, vendor driver issues). Operations tracks exception root causes to vendors for contract compliance and ']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice.   contamination_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of contamination requiring different handling or customer education responses.']
  contamination_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of contamination found in the container when exception type is contamination-related.   created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service exception record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of credit issued to customer account due to service exception, in USD.']
  credit_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a billing credit was issued to the customer as a result of this exception.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer was notified about the service exception.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified about the service exception.']
  estimated_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Driver-estimated or system-calculated weight of material involved in the exception, measured in pounds.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the service exception event, circumstances, and any relevant observations from the field.']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique exception tracking number displayed to customers and service personnel.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service exception indicating resolution progress.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service exception was encountered or reported by the driver in the field.']
  exception_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the type of service exception encountered during collection operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MISSED_PICKUP|CONTAMINATION|BLOCKED_ACCESS|OVERWEIGHT|OVERFILLED|WRONG_MATERIAL|NO_CONTAINER|DAMAGED_CONTAINER|SAFETY_HAZARD|WEATHER_DELAY|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|CUSTOMER_NOT_READY — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  follow_up_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up service or resolution action related to this exception.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate where the exception occurred, captured from vehicle GPS system.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate where the exception occurred, captured from vehicle GPS system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service exception record was last updated or modified.']
  photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic evidence of the exception was captured by the driver or system.']
  photo_storage_path varchar [note: 'File system or cloud storage path reference to exception documentation photos.']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception was preventable through operational improvements or customer education.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for exception resolution based on customer impact and service requirements.']
  repeat_exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a recurring exception at the same service address or for the same customer.']
  reported_by_source varchar [note: 'Indicates the source or channel through which the exception was initially reported.']
  requires_follow_up_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception requires additional follow-up action or customer contact.']
  reschedule_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the missed or incomplete service requires rescheduling on a future route.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of actions taken to resolve the exception and any follow-up required.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service exception was marked as resolved or closed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the underlying root cause of the service exception for trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_ERROR|DRIVER_ERROR|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|ROUTE_PLANNING|WEATHER|ACCESS_ISSUE|CONTAMINATION|OTHER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception resulted in a breach of the customer Service Level Agreement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this service exception record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for any non-standard service event during collection operations including missed pickups, contamination events, blocked access, overweight containers, and other exceptions requiring documentation and potential customer follow-up'
}

Table collection.container_placement {
  container_placement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the container placement record. Primary key for tracking the assignment of a container to a specific service location.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.container. Business justification: Container placements track deployment/retrieval of specific container assets. Link to container master required for asset ownership verification, depreciation calculation, condition assessment, and ca']
  container_driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who performed the container placement. Links to the workforce employee record.']
  container_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle used to deliver and place the container. Links to the fleet asset master record.']
  container_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that authorized and tracked the container placement activity. Links to the work order management system.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Container placements fulfill service commitments in customer contracts. Equipment deployment tracking, billable placement charges, and contract scope verification require linking placements to agreeme']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that is assigned this container placement. Links the container to the billing and service account.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Container placements fulfill specific service offerings (temporary dumpster rental, permanent cart deployment). Essential for asset deployment tracking, service initiation billing, container inventory']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Container placements must comply with permit conditions regarding container types, waste streams, and placement locations. Permit restrictions govern placement activities and container management prog']
  primary_container_removal_driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who performed the container removal. Links to the workforce employee record.']
  primary_container_removal_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle used to remove the container. Links to the fleet asset master record.']
  primary_container_removal_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that authorized and tracked the container removal activity. Links to the work order management system.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Container placements may be fulfilled via purchase orders for container rental or delivery services from third-party vendors. Operations reconciles placement_work_order_id against POs for billing vali']
  rfid_tag_id varchar [note: 'Unique RFID tag identifier embedded in or attached to the container for automated tracking and identification during collection operations.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route assigned to service this container placement. Links to the route master record for dispatch and optimization.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service location where the container is physically placed. Links to the service address master record.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Container placements/removals requiring site assessment, customer coordination, or permit verification need supervisor authorization for asset accountability and customer billing accuracy.']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Hazmat container placements must match approved waste profiles to ensure proper container type, labeling, and compatibility. Prevents unauthorized waste accumulation and ensures RCRA compliance.']
  actual_removal_date date [note: 'Actual date when the container was physically removed from the service location. Marks the end of the container assignment lifecycle.']
  cid varchar [note: 'Unique Container Identification number assigned to the physical container. Industry-standard identifier used for tracking and asset management across the waste collection lifecycle.']
  container_condition_at_placement varchar [note: 'Physical condition assessment of the container at the time of placement. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and maintenance planning.']
  container_condition_at_removal varchar [note: 'Physical condition assessment of the container at the time of removal. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, maintenance planning, and loss tracking.']
  container_size varchar [note: 'Physical capacity of the container expressed with unit of measure (e.g., 2-yd, 4-yd, 6-yd, 8-yd, 96-gal, 10-cu-yd, 20-cu-yd, 30-cu-yd, 40-cu-yd). Critical for route planning and capacity management.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the container by form factor and collection mechanism (e.g., dumpster, cart, roll-off box, compactor, toter, bin).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this container placement record was first created in the source system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this container placement generates billable charges to the customer account. Some placements may be promotional, warranty replacements, or non-revenue generating.']
  is_temporary_placement boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a temporary container placement (e.g., roll-off for construction project, event-based service) versus a permanent ongoing service.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this container placement record was last updated in the source system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  placement_accuracy_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'GPS accuracy radius in meters for the placement coordinates. Indicates the precision of the recorded location.']
  placement_date date [note: 'Date when the container was physically placed at the service location. Marks the start of the container assignment lifecycle.']
  placement_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the container placement location. Used for route optimization, asset tracking, and geospatial analytics.']
  placement_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the container placement location. Used for route optimization, asset tracking, and geospatial analytics.']
  placement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the driver or dispatcher at the time of placement. Captures special instructions, access issues, or site-specific details.']
  placement_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the container placement. Indicates whether this is a new service, replacement, additional container, temporary placement, seasonal service, or event-based service.']
  placement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the container placement. Tracks whether the container is actively in service, pending placement, pending removal, removed, relocated, damaged, or under repair. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending-placement|pending-removal|removed|relocated|damaged|under-repair — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  placement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the container was placed at the service location. Captured from GPS-enabled fleet telematics or driver mobile application.']
  removal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the driver or dispatcher at the time of removal. Captures condition issues, access problems, or other relevant observations.']
  removal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the container removal. Indicates whether this is due to service termination, replacement, relocation, end of temporary service, seasonal end, customer request, non-payment, or damage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: service-termination|replacement|relocation|temporary-end|seasonal-end|customer-request|non-payment|damaged — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  removal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the container was removed from the service location. Captured from GPS-enabled fleet telematics or driver mobile application.']
  scheduled_removal_date date [note: 'Planned date for container removal from the service location. Used for temporary placements such as roll-off boxes or event-based service.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled collection frequency for this container placement. Determines routing and service planning.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originated this container placement record. Typically AMCS Platform or Waste Logics customer portal, but may include Salesforce CRM or manual entry.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this container placement record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking the physical placement and assignment of a container (dumpster, cart, roll-off box) at a customer service location. Captures container CID (Container Identification number), RFID tag ID, container type (2-yd dumpster, 4-yd, 6-yd, 8-yd, 96-gal cart, roll-off), size, material stream (MSW, recycling, organics, C&D), placement date, removal date, service address, customer account reference, placement status (active, pending removal, removed), and GPS coordinates of placement. Sourced from AMCS Platform and Waste Logics. SSOT for container-to-location assignment.'
}

Table collection.driver_assignment {
  driver_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the driver assignment record. Primary key for this transactional entity capturing the assignment of a CDL-licensed driver to a specific route execution.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operations depot (facility) from which the driver and truck are dispatched for this route execution. Links to the facility master record.']
  hazwoper_training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazwoper_training. Business justification: Drivers assigned to hazmat collection routes must have current HAZWOPER certification per OSHA 1910.120. Links assignment to training verification for regulatory compliance and safety qualification.']
  observation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_observation. Business justification: Safety observations are made on specific driver assignments during their shift. Observers need to link observations to the assignment context (route type, vehicle, shift conditions) for meaningful beh']
  primary_driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the helper (second crew member) assigned to assist the driver on this route execution. Links to the workforce employee master record. Null if no helper assigned.']
  primary_driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Commercial Driver License (CDL) licensed driver assigned to execute the route. Links to the driver master record in Workday HCM.']
  route_execution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific route execution instance that the driver is assigned to perform. Links to the route execution transactional record.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection vehicle (truck) assigned to the driver for this route execution. Links to the fleet asset master record.']
  absence_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for driver absence if assignment status is absent. Sick for illness, vacation for planned time off, personal for personal leave, injury for work-related injury, no_show for unexcused absence, other for miscellaneous reasons. Null if driver reported for duty.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the driver completed their shift and returned to the depot. Captured from Geotab Fleet Telematics GPS tracking or driver mobile app check-out. Used for performance analysis and variance reporting against scheduled end time.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the driver began their shift and commenced route execution. Captured from Geotab Fleet Telematics GPS tracking or driver mobile app check-in. Used for performance analysis and variance reporting against scheduled start time.']
  assignment_created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the dispatcher or system user who created this driver assignment record. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  assignment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this driver assignment record was first created in the Locus Dispatch system. Used for audit trail and assignment lead time analysis.']
  assignment_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the dispatcher or system user who last modified this driver assignment record. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  assignment_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this driver assignment record was last modified in the Locus Dispatch system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and special instructions for the driver assignment. May include route-specific guidance, customer alerts, safety warnings, or equipment requirements.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the driver assignment, formatted as DA- followed by 10 digits. Used for operational tracking and communication.']
  assignment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the driver assignment indicating urgency and importance. Critical for time-sensitive routes or service level agreement (SLA) commitments, high for premium customers, normal for standard service, low for flexible/deferred routes.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the driver assignment. Scheduled indicates future planned assignment, active indicates driver is currently executing the route, completed indicates route execution finished, absent indicates driver did not report, cancelled indicates assignment was voided before execution.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the driver assignment indicating the role and responsibility level. Primary indicates regular assigned driver, relief indicates substitute coverage, trainee indicates driver under training supervision, backup indicates standby assignment, temporary indicates short-term fill-in.']
  cdl_class_required varchar [note: 'The minimum Commercial Driver License (CDL) class required to operate the assigned truck and execute the route. Class A for combination vehicles over 26,001 lbs, Class B for single vehicles over 26,001 lbs, Class C for vehicles designed to transport 16+ passengers or hazardous materials.']
  cdl_endorsements_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CDL endorsements required for the assignment. Common endorsements include H (Hazardous Materials), N (Tank Vehicle), P (Passenger), S (School Bus), T (Double/Triple Trailers). Empty if no special endorsements required.']
  dispatch_sequence varchar [note: 'The sequential order in which this driver assignment was dispatched from the depot on the service date. Used for operational tracking and first-out/last-in analysis.']
  dot_hos_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the driver assignment complies with DOT hours-of-service regulations at the time of assignment. True indicates the driver has sufficient available hours and rest periods to legally execute the route, false indicates a compliance violation or risk.']
  estimated_route_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated total duration in hours required to complete the assigned route execution, including driving time, service stops, and breaks. Used for scheduling and DOT hours-of-service compliance planning.']
  helper_assigned_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a helper (second crew member) is assigned to assist the driver on this route execution. True indicates a two-person crew, false indicates solo driver operation. Common for residential rear-loader routes and heavy commercial pickups.']
  hos_available_drive_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of driving hours available to the driver under DOT hours-of-service regulations at the time of assignment. Calculated from the drivers electronic logging device (ELD) data and used to ensure compliance with the 11-hour driving limit.']
  hos_available_duty_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The number of on-duty hours available to the driver under DOT hours-of-service regulations at the time of assignment. Calculated from the drivers electronic logging device (ELD) data and used to ensure compliance with the 14-hour on-duty limit.']
  pre_trip_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the mandatory pre-trip vehicle inspection conducted by the driver before route execution. Passed indicates inspection completed with no defects, failed indicates defects found requiring maintenance, pending indicates inspection not yet completed, not_required indicates inspection waived for specific assignment type, waived indicates management override.']
  pre_trip_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the pre-trip vehicle inspection was completed and recorded by the driver. Used for DOT compliance documentation and audit trail.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route type that the driver is assigned to execute. Residential for curbside household collection, commercial for business waste pickup, roll_off for large container haul, recycling for materials recovery collection, hazardous for special waste handling.']
  service_area_code varchar [note: 'Geographic service area code where the route is executed. Formatted as 2-3 letter region code followed by 3-digit zone number (e.g., NE-001, SW-042). Used for territory management and performance reporting.']
  service_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the driver is assigned to execute the route. This is the business event date for the assignment and the primary date dimension for operational reporting.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled timestamp when the driver is expected to complete their shift and return to the depot. Used for workforce scheduling and DOT hours-of-service compliance tracking.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The scheduled timestamp when the driver is expected to begin their shift and commence route execution. Used for workforce scheduling and Department of Transportation (DOT) hours-of-service compliance tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this driver assignment record. Locus_dispatch for automated dispatch assignments, workday_hcm for workforce management initiated assignments, manual_entry for dispatcher-created assignments.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique record identifier from the source operational system (Locus Dispatch or Workday HCM). Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation with source systems.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the assignment of a CDL-licensed driver to a specific route execution on a given service date. Records driver ID, route execution reference, truck ID, assignment type (primary, relief, trainee), shift start time, shift end time, pre-trip inspection status, DOT hours-of-service compliance flag, and assignment status (scheduled, active, completed, absent). Sourced from Locus Dispatch and Workday HCM. Enables driver performance tracking, DOT compliance, and route coverage management.'
}

Table collection.truck_assignment {
  truck_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the truck assignment record. Primary key for this transactional entity capturing the assignment of a collection vehicle to a route execution on a specific service date.']
  fuel_purchase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_purchase. Business justification: Truck assignments determine which vehicle consumed fuel on which date. Fleet managers reconcile fuel purchases against assignments to detect unauthorized usage, validate fuel efficiency by vehicle, an']
  primary_truck_dispatch_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or yard from which the truck was dispatched for this route execution. Typically a collection operations center, transfer station, or regional depot.']
  route_execution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific route execution instance that this truck is assigned to perform. Links to the route_execution transactional record.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection vehicle (truck) assigned to this route execution. Links to the fleet vehicle master record.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the truck completed the route execution and returned to the facility or disposal site. Captured from Geotab GPS tracking or driver mobile app check-out.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the truck departed the facility or yard to begin route execution. Captured from Geotab GPS tracking or driver mobile app check-in.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for this truck assignment, typically formatted as a combination of date, route, and sequence number for operational tracking and dispatch communication.']
  assignment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this truck assignment. Emergency assignments for service failures or complaints; special event for large-scale temporary collections; routine for standard scheduled service.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the truck assignment. Tracks progression from initial scheduling through dispatch, execution, and completion or cancellation.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the truck was assigned to the route execution. Represents the business event time when dispatch confirmed the vehicle-to-route pairing.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the truck assignment was cancelled before or during execution. Captures operational context for weather delays, vehicle failures, route cancellations, or customer service suspensions.']
  capacity_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated capacity of the assigned truck in cubic yards. Represents the maximum volume of waste the vehicle can carry per load, used for route optimization and load planning.']
  capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated weight capacity of the assigned truck in tons. Represents the maximum weight the vehicle can legally and safely carry, constrained by DOT regulations and vehicle GVWR.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this truck assignment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation, distinct from the business assignment timestamp.']
  environmental_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an environmental incident occurred during this truck assignment. True if a spill, leak, unauthorized discharge, or EPA-reportable event was logged during route execution.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel or energy source for the assigned vehicle. CNG (Compressed Natural Gas), RNG (Renewable Natural Gas), diesel, electric, or hybrid powertrain. Critical for sustainability reporting and GHG emissions tracking.']
  gps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time GPS tracking was active for this truck assignment. True if Geotab telematics was operational and transmitting location data during route execution.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this truck assignment record was last modified. Audit trail for tracking updates to assignment status, times, or other attributes throughout the assignment lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments related to this truck assignment. Captures dispatcher instructions, driver feedback, route conditions, or any contextual information relevant to the assignment execution.']
  odometer_end decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer reading in miles at the time the truck completed the route execution and returned. Used to calculate route mileage and update vehicle lifecycle metrics.']
  odometer_start decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer reading in miles at the time the truck was assigned and departed for route execution. Used for mileage tracking, fuel efficiency analysis, and preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  post_trip_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the post-trip vehicle inspection performed after route completion. Identifies any defects or maintenance needs discovered during route execution.']
  post_trip_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the post-trip inspection was completed. Used for maintenance work order generation and fleet safety tracking.']
  pre_trip_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the mandatory DOT pre-trip vehicle inspection performed before route execution. Pass indicates vehicle is safe for operation; fail or conditional requires maintenance intervention before dispatch.']
  pre_trip_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pre-trip inspection was completed. Required for DOT compliance documentation and OSHA safety audit trails.']
  reassignment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the truck was reassigned during route execution. Captures operational context for vehicle breakdowns, driver changes, route adjustments, or emergency redeployment.']
  route_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the truck deviated from the planned route during execution. True if GPS tracking detected significant off-route travel, triggering operational review or customer service follow-up.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident occurred during this truck assignment. True if an accident, injury, near-miss, or OSHA-reportable event was logged during route execution.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the truck is scheduled to begin the route execution. Used for dispatch planning and driver coordination.']
  truck_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the collection vehicle by body configuration and loading mechanism. ASL (Automated Side Loader), FEL (Front End Loader), REL (Rear End Loader), roll-off for container hauling, compactor for transfer operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASL|FEL|REL|roll_off|compactor|hook_lift|grapple|flatbed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the assignment of a specific collection vehicle (ASL, FEL, REL, roll-off, CNG/RNG unit) to a route execution on a given service date. Records vehicle ID, route execution reference, truck type, fuel type (diesel, CNG, RNG), odometer at assignment, pre-trip inspection pass/fail, assigned capacity (cubic yards), and assignment status. Sourced from AMCS Platform and Geotab Fleet Telematics. Distinct from driver_assignment — a truck and driver are assigned independently and may change mid-route.'
}

Table collection.service_schedule {
  service_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service schedule record. Primary key.']
  collection_stop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection stop associated with this schedule. Links to the stop master record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service schedules track which employee created them for audit trail, training assessment, and accountability in customer service setup—complements modified_by_user for change tracking.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that owns this service schedule. Links to the customer account master record.']
  frequency_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.frequency_plan. Business justification: Schedules reference standardized frequency plans (weekly, bi-weekly, on-demand) for consistent service delivery. Critical for route optimization, capacity planning, customer contract fulfillment, and ']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Service schedules define recurring delivery of specific offerings to customers. Essential for subscription management, automated billing, contract fulfillment tracking, and service level agreement enf']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service address where collection occurs. Links to the service address master record.']
  sla_term_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA governing this service schedule. Defines performance commitments, response times, and service guarantees.']
  access_requirements varchar [note: 'Special access conditions required to service this stop. Used for driver preparation and route planning.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule automatically renews at the end of its term. True if the schedule continues indefinitely unless cancelled.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the customer is billed for this scheduled service. May differ from service frequency.']
  container_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of containers serviced at this stop per scheduled pickup. Used for capacity planning and billing calculations.']
  container_size decimal(10,2) [note: 'Capacity of the container in cubic yards or gallons, depending on container type. Used for capacity planning and billing.']
  container_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the container size. Standardizes capacity reporting across different container types.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of waste container used for this scheduled service. Determines collection equipment and handling procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cart|bin|dumpster|compactor|roll_off|bag|toter — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service schedule expires or is terminated. Nullable for open-ended schedules. Marks the end of the schedule validity period.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this service schedule becomes active and service delivery begins. Marks the beginning of the schedule validity period.']
  external_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.']
  friday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether service is scheduled on Fridays. True if Friday is a scheduled service day.']
  holiday_handling_rule varchar [note: 'Defines how service is adjusted when a scheduled day falls on a recognized holiday. Determines whether service is skipped, rescheduled, or delivered as normal.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service schedule record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  monday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether service is scheduled on Mondays. True if Monday is a scheduled service day.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for service delivery. High and critical priorities receive preferential routing and must-complete status.']
  route_assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign this schedule to collection routes. Fixed schedules are always on the same route; dynamic schedules are optimized daily.']
