  gvwr varchar [note: 'Maximum operating weight of the vehicle including chassis, body, engine, fuel, driver, passengers, and cargo, measured in pounds.']
  home_yard_location varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the primary yard or facility where the vehicle is based for daily operations.']
  hydraulic_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydraulic system used for compaction and lifting operations (applicable to collection vehicles).']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the insurance coverage for this vehicle.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent DOT or safety inspection performed on the vehicle.']
  license_plate varchar [note: 'Current license plate number registered with the Department of Transportation (DOT) for this vehicle.']
  lift_mechanism varchar [note: 'Type of lifting mechanism used to pick up and empty containers or bins. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Automated Arm|Manual|Front Forks|Rear Forks|Hook Lift|Cable Hoist|N/A — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer of the vehicle (e.g., Mack, Peterbilt, Freightliner, International).']
  model varchar [note: 'Specific model designation of the vehicle as assigned by the manufacturer.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Year the vehicle was manufactured as designated by the manufacturer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle record was last modified in the system.']
  next_pm_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance service based on time or usage intervals.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the vehicle in the fleet lifecycle.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is owned outright, leased, or rented by Waste Management.']
  payload_capacity_lbs varchar [note: 'Maximum weight of waste or cargo the vehicle can legally carry, measured in pounds.']
  registration_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current DOT vehicle registration expires and must be renewed.']
  telematics_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the GPS/telematics device installed on the vehicle for tracking and monitoring (e.g., Geotab device ID).']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission installed in the vehicle.']
  unit_number varchar [note: 'Fleet-assigned unique unit identifier displayed on the vehicle for operational tracking and dispatch purposes.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned by the manufacturer. Used for registration, insurance, and warranty tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for every mobile asset in the Waste Management fleet including ASL (Automated Side Loader), FEL (Front End Loader), REL (Rear End Loader), roll-off trucks, transfer tractors, CNG/RNG-fueled units, and support vehicles. Captures vehicle identification (VIN, unit number, license plate), make, model, year, body type, fuel type, GVWR, axle configuration, DOT registration number, acquisition date and cost, current odometer and engine hours, operational status, assigned district/division, home yard, and detailed technical specifications (engine, transmission, hydraulic system, compaction type, lift mechanism, body capacity, payload capacity, fuel tank capacity, emissions certification). This is the SSOT for vehicle identity and specifications in the fleet domain.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_registration {
  vehicle_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle registration record. Primary key.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: IFTA, IRP, and UCR registrations are regulatory permits/authorizations required for interstate commerce. Fleet compliance managers track registration permits alongside facility operating permits for u']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle unit that this registration record applies to.']
  annual_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the vehicle must complete its annual DOT safety inspection to maintain compliance. Failure to inspect by this date results in out-of-service status.']
  cab_card_storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the IRP cab card (registration document) is stored. Cab cards must be carried in the vehicle at all times for roadside inspection readiness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle registration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) to commercial motor carriers. Required for interstate commerce and displayed on vehicle.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle registration becomes valid and the vehicle is legally authorized to operate on public roads.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle registration expires and must be renewed to maintain legal operation. Critical for compliance tracking and renewal scheduling.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel type for the vehicle as recorded on the registration. CNG = Compressed Natural Gas, RNG = Renewable Natural Gas. Impacts IFTA reporting and environmental compliance.']
  gross_vehicle_weight varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable weight of the vehicle including cargo, as registered with the state DMV. Measured in pounds. Determines registration class and fee structure.']
  ifta_decal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the IFTA decal affixed to the vehicle. IFTA simplifies fuel tax reporting for commercial vehicles operating across multiple jurisdictions.']
  ifta_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the IFTA decal expires and must be renewed. Typically aligns with calendar year end.']
  irp_apportioned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle is registered under the International Registration Plan (IRP), which allows commercial vehicles to travel across multiple jurisdictions with a single registration and apportioned fees.']
  irp_base_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code representing the base jurisdiction for IRP apportionment. This is where the carrier maintains its principal place of business.']
  irp_fleet_number varchar [note: 'Unique fleet identifier assigned under the IRP program. Used for tracking and reporting across multiple jurisdictions.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the vehicle registration was issued (e.g., TX, CA, IL).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle last completed a DOT annual safety inspection. Used to calculate next inspection due date.']
  lien_holder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or entity holding a lien on the vehicle, if applicable. Recorded on the title and registration documents.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Operating authority number issued by FMCSA for carriers transporting regulated commodities in interstate commerce. Not all vehicles require an MC number.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or remarks related to the vehicle registration (e.g., temporary permits, exemptions, pending renewals).']
  registered_owner_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the registered owner as recorded on the vehicle registration document. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  registered_owner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or individual listed as the registered owner on the vehicle registration. Typically the company name for fleet vehicles.']
  registration_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle registration as defined by state DMV regulations. Determines fee structure and operational restrictions.']
  registration_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the vehicle registration document. Used for electronic record-keeping and roadside inspection readiness.']
  registration_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fee paid for the vehicle registration, including base registration, IRP apportionment fees, and any applicable surcharges. Denominated in USD.']
  registration_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the registration fee. Typically USD for U.S. operations.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'The official license plate or registration number issued by the state Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV). This is the externally-known identifier displayed on the vehicle.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vehicle registration. Active registrations are compliant for road operation; expired or suspended registrations require immediate attention to maintain DOT compliance.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the registration was last renewed. Used to track renewal history and calculate next renewal cycle.']
  title_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle title document issued by the state. Links registration to proof of ownership.']
  ucr_registration_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the UCR registration is valid. UCR must be renewed annually.']
  ucr_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Unified Carrier Registration for this vehicle. UCR is a federally-mandated registration system for commercial motor carriers operating in interstate commerce.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle registration record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle type as recorded on the registration (e.g., ASL = Automated Side Loader, FEL = Front End Loader, REL = Rear End Loader, roll-off truck, transfer vehicle).']
  vehicle_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle as recorded on the registration document.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character alphanumeric Vehicle Identification Number assigned by the manufacturer. Unique global identifier for the vehicle, recorded on registration documents.']

  Note: 'DOT and state motor vehicle registration records for each fleet unit. Tracks registration number, issuing state, registration class, expiration date, renewal date, IRP (International Registration Plan) apportionment details, IFTA (International Fuel Tax Agreement) decal number, UCR (Unified Carrier Registration) status, DOT number, MC number (if applicable), annual inspection due date, and cab card storage location. Critical for DOT/FMCSA regulatory compliance and roadside inspection readiness.'
}

Table fleet.driver {
  driver_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the driver record. Primary key for the driver entity within the fleet domain.']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_area. Business justification: Drivers are assigned to service territories for route planning, CDL compliance by jurisdiction, labor deployment, and territory-based performance management. Critical for workforce planning and regula']
  cdl_training_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_certification. Business justification: CDL licenses, hazmat endorsements, and DOT medical certifications are regulatory training certifications. Driver qualification files require linking to certification records for FMCSA compliance audit']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record in the workforce domain. Links driver to their HR profile in Workday HCM.']
  home_district_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary operational district where the driver is based. Used for route assignment and workforce planning.']
  cdl_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the Commercial Driver License indicating the types of vehicles the driver is authorized to operate. Class A for combination vehicles, Class B for heavy straight vehicles, Class C for smaller vehicles requiring placards or designed to transport 16+ passengers.']
  cdl_endorsements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CDL endorsements held by the driver (e.g., H for Hazmat, N for Tanker, T for Doubles/Triples, P for Passenger). Determines specialized vehicle and cargo authorization.']
  cdl_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the Commercial Driver License expires and must be renewed. Critical for DOT compliance monitoring.']
  cdl_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the current Commercial Driver License was issued by the state authority.']
  cdl_number varchar [note: 'Unique Commercial Driver License number issued by the state licensing authority. Required for all drivers operating commercial motor vehicles.']
  cdl_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the Commercial Driver License was issued (e.g., CA, TX, NY).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the driver record was first created in the fleet management system.']
  dot_physical_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the DOT medical examiner certificate expires. Drivers must maintain current medical certification to operate commercial vehicles.']
  dot_physical_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the current DOT medical examiner certificate was issued.']
  drug_alcohol_program_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the driver was enrolled in the DOT drug and alcohol testing program.']
  drug_alcohol_program_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver is enrolled in the DOT-mandated drug and alcohol testing program. Required for all CDL drivers.']
  hazmat_clearance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the driver\'s TSA security threat assessment clearance required for Hazmat endorsement. Must be renewed every five years.']
  hazmat_endorsement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver holds a valid Hazmat endorsement on their CDL, required for transporting hazardous waste materials subject to RCRA regulations.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date when the driver was hired by Waste Management. Used for seniority, tenure analysis, and route assignment priority.']
  last_alcohol_test_date date [note: 'Date of the driver\'s most recent alcohol test (pre-employment, random, post-accident, reasonable suspicion, or return-to-duty).']
  last_alcohol_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the driver\'s most recent alcohol test. Positive results (BAC >= 0.04) or refusals trigger disqualification.']
  last_drug_test_date date [note: 'Date of the driver\'s most recent drug test (pre-employment, random, post-accident, reasonable suspicion, or return-to-duty).']
  last_drug_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the driver\'s most recent drug test. Positive results or refusals trigger disqualification and return-to-duty protocols.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the driver record was last updated. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  last_qualification_review_date date [note: 'Date when the driver\'s qualification file was last reviewed for completeness and compliance with DOT requirements.']
  medical_examiner_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the driver\'s DOT medical examiner certificate. Required to demonstrate physical fitness to operate commercial motor vehicles.']
  medical_examiner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certified medical examiner who issued the DOT physical certificate.']
  mvr_last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the driver\'s Motor Vehicle Record was last reviewed by the fleet safety department. DOT requires annual MVR reviews.']
  mvr_next_review_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next Motor Vehicle Record review is due. Used for compliance tracking and proactive safety management.']
  mvr_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the driver\'s Motor Vehicle Record based on the most recent review. Indicates driving history and violation status for risk assessment and DOT compliance.']
  next_qualification_review_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next driver qualification file review is due. Used for proactive compliance management.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the driver within the fleet. Indicates availability for route assignments and vehicle operations.']
  primary_route_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of collection route the driver is assigned to. Determines vehicle type and operational requirements.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the driver based on DOT requirements, medical certification, license validity, and company safety standards. Determines whether the driver is authorized to operate fleet vehicles.']
  safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite safety score based on driving behavior, incident history, and compliance metrics captured from Geotab Fleet Telematics. Scale 0-100, higher is better.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the driver\'s employment was terminated. Null for active drivers.']
  total_miles_driven decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total miles driven by the driver across all fleet vehicles since hire date. Used for experience assessment and performance analysis.']

  Note: 'Master record for all CDL (Commercial Driver License) holders and non-CDL operators assigned to fleet vehicles. Captures driver employee ID (FK to workforce domain), CDL number, class (A/B/C), state of issuance, expiration date, endorsements (Hazmat, Tanker, Doubles/Triples), medical examiner certificate details, DOT physical due date, MVR status, HazMat clearance, drug/alcohol program enrollment, current qualification status, last qualification review date, next review due date, and operational status. SSOT for driver identity and qualification records within the fleet domain.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_assignment {
  vehicle_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle assignment record. Primary key for the vehicle assignment entity.']
  delivery_wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Daily vehicle assignments include deliveries to WTE facilities. Dispatch and operations need to track which vehicle/driver delivered waste to which energy facility on each shift for load reconciliatio']
  dispatcher_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dispatchers assign vehicles to routes and drivers daily, critical for operational accountability, shift planning, HOS compliance verification, and route optimization decisions.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver assigned to operate the vehicle during this shift. References the driver master record.']
  pre_post_trip_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.pre_post_trip_inspection. Business justification: Vehicle assignments require pre-trip inspections per FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.11. The vehicle_assignment table currently has pre_trip_inspection_completed BOOLEAN and pre_trip_inspection_timestamp, but n']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route or run assigned to this driver-vehicle combination for the shift. May be null for non-route assignments such as maintenance runs or standby.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle unit assigned for this operational shift. References the fleet vehicle master record.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the driver completed the shift and returned the vehicle, as recorded by telematics or dispatch system. May differ from scheduled shift end time.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual timestamp when the driver began the shift and took control of the vehicle, as recorded by telematics or dispatch system. May differ from scheduled shift start time.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which this vehicle assignment is effective. Represents the operational day of the assignment.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vehicle assignment. Tracks progression from scheduled through active operation to completion or cancellation.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment purpose. Indicates whether the vehicle is assigned for route collection, transfer haul, roll-off service, maintenance run, standby duty, or emergency response.']
  cdl_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the drivers Commercial Driver License (CDL) was verified as valid and appropriate for the vehicle class at the time of assignment. Required for DOT compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vehicle assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any vehicle defects or maintenance issues identified during the assignment. Used to generate work orders for corrective maintenance.']
  defects_reported boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any vehicle defects or maintenance issues were reported during the pre-trip or post-trip inspection for this assignment.']
  dispatch_location varchar [note: 'The facility or yard location from which the vehicle was dispatched for this assignment. Typically the operating base or depot.']
  engine_hours_end decimal(10,1) [note: 'The cumulative engine operating hours at the end of the assignment. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and asset lifecycle management.']
  engine_hours_start decimal(10,1) [note: 'The cumulative engine operating hours at the beginning of the assignment. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and asset lifecycle management.']
  fuel_level_end_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fuel tank level as a percentage at the end of the assignment. Used for fuel consumption tracking and refueling planning.']
  fuel_level_start_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fuel tank level as a percentage at the beginning of the assignment. Used for fuel consumption tracking and refueling planning.']
  hos_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver was in compliance with Hours of Service (HOS) regulations at the time of assignment. Ensures driver has adequate rest and is within allowable driving hours.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vehicle assignment record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the vehicle assignment. May include special instructions, operational observations, or other relevant information.']
  odometer_end decimal(10,1) [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the end of the assignment. Used to calculate distance traveled during the shift for utilization and maintenance tracking.']
  odometer_start decimal(10,1) [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the beginning of the assignment. Used to calculate distance traveled during the shift for utilization and maintenance tracking.']
  post_trip_inspection_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver completed the mandatory post-trip vehicle inspection after completing the shift. Required for DOT compliance and vehicle maintenance tracking.']
  post_trip_inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the post-trip inspection was completed and recorded. Used for compliance audit trail.']
  pre_trip_inspection_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver completed the mandatory pre-trip vehicle inspection before beginning the shift. Required for DOT compliance.']
  return_location varchar [note: 'The facility or yard location to which the vehicle was returned at the end of the assignment. May differ from dispatch location for transfer operations.']
  safety_incident_occurred boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any safety incident, accident, or near-miss event occurred during this vehicle assignment. Triggers incident investigation and reporting workflow.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the driver is scheduled to complete the shift and return the vehicle. Used for Hours of Service (HOS) tracking and shift duration calculation.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the driver is scheduled to begin the shift and take control of the vehicle. Used for Hours of Service (HOS) tracking and shift duration calculation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of driver-to-vehicle assignments for each operational shift or day. Captures assigned driver, assigned vehicle unit, assignment date, shift start time, shift end time, route or run assignment reference, pre-trip inspection completion flag, post-trip inspection completion flag, odometer at assignment start, odometer at assignment end, engine hours at start, engine hours at end, and assignment status. Supports HOS (Hours of Service) tracking, vehicle utilization reporting, and driver accountability.'
}

Table fleet.telematics_event {
  telematics_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each telematics event record captured from the vehicle tracking system.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver assigned to the vehicle at the time of the event.']
  geofence_zone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the geographic boundary zone the vehicle was within at the time of the event.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planned collection route the vehicle was assigned to at the time of the event.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet vehicle that generated this telematics event.']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Telematics systems use geofencing to track vehicle arrival, dwell time, and departure at WTE facilities. Operations need this link for facility queue management, driver behavior monitoring at tipping ']
  altitude_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the vehicle above sea level measured in feet at the time of the event.']
  battery_voltage decimal(4,2) [note: 'Vehicle battery voltage measured in volts at the time of the event.']
  coolant_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engine coolant temperature measured in degrees Fahrenheit at the time of the event.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the telematics platform or system that captured and transmitted this event record.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the telematics tracking device installed in the vehicle.']
  engine_load_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of maximum engine load being utilized at the time of the event.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine speed measured in revolutions per minute at the time of the event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the telematics event was captured by the vehicle tracking device.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the telematics event indicating the trigger or reason for the event record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gps_ping|ignition_on|ignition_off|harsh_event|idle_alert|geofence_entry|geofence_exit — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fuel_consumption_gallons decimal(8,3) [note: 'Cumulative fuel consumed by the vehicle measured in gallons since the last reset or event.']
  fuel_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of fuel remaining in the vehicle tank at the time of the event.']
  gps_accuracy_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the GPS coordinates measured in meters indicating the precision of the location data.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the vehicle at the time of the event in decimal degrees.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the vehicle at the time of the event in decimal degrees.']
  harsh_acceleration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether a harsh acceleration event occurred based on acceleration thresholds.']
  harsh_braking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether a harsh braking event occurred based on deceleration thresholds.']
  heading_degrees varchar [note: 'Compass direction the vehicle is traveling measured in degrees from 0 to 359 where 0 is north.']
  idle_duration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the vehicle was idling for an extended period at the time of the event.']
  idle_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration of time the vehicle engine was running while stationary measured in minutes.']
  ignition_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the vehicle ignition indicating whether the engine is running or off.']
  odometer_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cumulative distance traveled by the vehicle in miles as recorded at the time of the event.']
  posted_speed_limit_mph varchar [note: 'Legal speed limit at the vehicles location at the time of the event in miles per hour.']
  pto_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the power take-off mechanism indicating whether it is engaged for auxiliary equipment operation.']
  satellite_count varchar [note: 'Number of GPS satellites used to determine the vehicles location at the time of the event.']
  seatbelt_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the driver seatbelt indicating whether it is fastened or unfastened.']
  speeding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the vehicle was exceeding the posted speed limit at the time of the event.']
  vehicle_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Instantaneous speed of the vehicle at the time of the event measured in miles per hour.']

  Note: 'High-frequency GPS and telematics event records sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics for each vehicle. Captures event timestamp, vehicle unit, GPS latitude, GPS longitude, vehicle speed, posted speed limit, heading, ignition status, engine RPM, engine load percentage, coolant temperature, battery voltage, odometer reading at event, fuel level, idle duration flag, harsh braking event flag, harsh acceleration event flag, seatbelt status, PTO (Power Take-Off) status, and geofence zone identifier. Foundational for route compliance, driver behavior scoring, and fuel consumption analysis.'
}

Table fleet.fuel_transaction {
  fuel_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel transaction record. Primary key.']
  air_emission_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.air_emission_event. Business justification: Fuel dispensing events can trigger vapor recovery system failures, spills, or overfill events reportable under air quality permits. Environmental compliance officers link fuel transactions to emission']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who performed the fueling transaction. Links to driver master record.']
  fuel_purchase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_purchase. Business justification: Individual vehicle fuel transactions must reconcile against bulk fuel purchases for inventory management, cost allocation, and variance analysis. Essential for fuel accounting and detecting shrinkage/']
  fueling_station_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fueling location. May be internal yard pump ID or external commercial station identifier from fuel card network.']
  fueling_wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Many WTE facilities have on-site fueling stations for fleet vehicles. Fuel transactions need to link to the WTE facility where fueling occurred for cost allocation, facility-specific fuel inventory ma']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route the vehicle was assigned to at the time of fueling. Links fuel consumption to operational route performance.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the fueling occurred. Supports labor cost allocation and shift-based fuel consumption analysis.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet vehicle that was fueled in this transaction. Links to the vehicle master record.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization or approval code from the fuel card processor or yard pump system. Used for transaction reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP cost center to which this fuel transaction cost is allocated. Supports financial reporting and budget tracking by operational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel transaction record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the transaction cost (e.g., USD).']
  def_quantity_dispensed decimal(8,2) [note: 'Volume of DEF (Diesel Exhaust Fluid) dispensed in gallons during this transaction. Required for diesel vehicles with SCR emissions systems to meet EPA standards.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Cumulative engine operating hours at time of fueling. Critical for maintenance scheduling and fuel efficiency analysis for heavy equipment.']
  fuel_card_number varchar [note: 'Fleet fuel card number used for the transaction. Masked or tokenized for security. Used to reconcile with fuel card provider statements.']
  fuel_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or specification of the fuel dispensed. ULSD = Ultra-Low Sulfur Diesel; B5/B20 = biodiesel blend percentages. Ensures compliance with vehicle manufacturer specifications.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of fuel dispensed in this transaction. CNG = Compressed Natural Gas, RNG = Renewable Natural Gas.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for fuel expense posting in SAP FI. Varies by fuel type and vehicle class.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the fueling location. Captured from vehicle telematics or fuel card network. Used to validate station location and detect anomalies.']
  gps_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the fueling location. Captured from vehicle telematics or fuel card network. Used to validate station location and detect anomalies.']
  ifta_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two-letter state/province code where fuel was purchased. Required for IFTA quarterly fuel tax reporting for interstate commercial vehicles.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice or receipt number for this fuel transaction. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and audit trail.']
  is_emergency_fuel boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this was an unplanned emergency fueling event (e.g., vehicle ran low on route). Used to identify route planning issues and driver behavior patterns.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel transaction record was last modified. Tracks updates for reconciliation, corrections, or status changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the fuel transaction. May include driver remarks, exception explanations, or reconciliation notes.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time of fueling. Used to calculate fuel economy (MPG or MPGe) and validate vehicle utilization.']
  pre_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Transaction cost before fuel taxes. Calculated as total_cost minus tax_amount. Used for cost analysis and vendor invoice reconciliation.']
  pump_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific fuel pump or dispenser used at the fueling station. Supports pump-level performance tracking and maintenance.']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume or energy-equivalent quantity of fuel dispensed. For liquid fuels measured in gallons; for CNG/RNG measured in GGE (Gasoline Gallon Equivalent).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this transaction record (e.g., Geotab, WEX FleetCard, internal yard pump system). Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Original transaction identifier from the source system. Maintains traceability to upstream system for reconciliation and issue resolution.']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fueling station. Company yard = internal fleet facility; public station = commercial retail; CNG station = specialized compressed natural gas facility.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel tax included in the transaction cost. Includes federal, state, and local fuel excise taxes. Used for IFTA reporting and tax reconciliation.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the fuel transaction (quantity × unit cost). Used for cost allocation and budget tracking.']
  transaction_source varchar [note: 'System or method that captured this fuel transaction. Fleet card = commercial fuel card network; yard pump = internal automated pump; telematics = vehicle onboard system; manual entry = driver-reported.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the fuel transaction. Approved = validated and posted; pending = awaiting review; disputed = flagged for investigation; reconciled = matched to invoice.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel dispensing transaction occurred. Captured from fuel card system or yard pump controller.']
  unit_cost decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of fuel at the time of transaction (price per gallon, GGE, or kWh).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity dispensed. GGE = Gasoline Gallon Equivalent used for CNG/RNG; kWh for electric charging.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the fuel vendor or station operator (e.g., Shell, BP, internal fleet services). Used for vendor performance analysis and contract compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual fuel dispensing transaction records for all fleet vehicles covering diesel, CNG (Compressed Natural Gas), and RNG (Renewable Natural Gas) fueling events. Captures transaction date and time, vehicle unit, driver, fuel type, quantity dispensed (gallons or GGE), unit cost, total cost, fuel card number, fueling station identifier (company yard or public station), odometer at fueling, engine hours at fueling, DEF quantity dispensed, and transaction source (fleet card, yard pump, CNG station). Integrates with SAP MM for cost allocation and supports IFTA fuel tax reporting.'
}

Table fleet.fleet_dot_inspection {
  fleet_dot_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DOT inspection record. Primary key.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.corrective_action. Business justification: DOT inspection violations generate corrective actions tracked in safety system for closure verification, compliance tracking, and CSA score management. Critical for FMCSA compliance.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who was operating the vehicle at the time of inspection.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: DOT inspections assess asset condition and may trigger impairment or retirement decisions. Results must be recorded in the asset condition history for regulatory compliance, valuation support, and aud']
  inspection_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: DOT inspections performed by third-party certified inspection vendors require vendor tracking for certification validation, performance management, and payment processing. Role-prefixed to distinguish']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: DOT roadside inspections of hazmat loads must reference the manifest being transported for compliance verification per 49 CFR 177.817. Critical for documenting placarding violations, shipping paper ac']
  reviewing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal review of DOT roadside inspections by safety managers is required for CSA score management, corrective action tracking, and FMCSA compliance audits.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle that was inspected.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: DOT roadside inspections that result in out-of-service orders or citations generate formal violation notices. Safety directors link inspection reports to notices for penalty tracking and corrective ac']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order created to address inspection violations.']
  basic_category varchar [note: 'CSA BASIC category that the violations fall under (e.g., Unsafe Driving, Hours of Service Compliance, Vehicle Maintenance, Controlled Substances/Alcohol, Driver Fitness, Hazardous Materials Compliance, Crash Indicator). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Unsafe Driving|Hours of Service|Vehicle Maintenance|Controlled Substances|Driver Fitness|Hazardous Materials|Crash Indicator — promote to reference product]']
  carrier_dot_number varchar [note: 'USDOT number of the carrier at the time of inspection. Seven-digit identifier assigned by FMCSA.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Date when all required corrective actions were completed and verified.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective actions required to address the violations.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective action is required to address violations found during the inspection.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corrective action process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the system.']
  csa_score_impact decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated impact of this inspection on the carrier\'s CSA score.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'CDL number of the driver at the time of inspection. Recorded for compliance tracking.']
  driver_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the issuing state for the driver\'s CDL.']
  driver_oos_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver specifically was placed out of service.']
  inspection_category varchar [note: 'Category or context of the inspection event (roadside stop, scheduled annual inspection, state patrol enforcement, terminal audit, compliance review, or random selection).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the DOT inspection was performed.']
  inspection_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the scanned or digital copy of the official inspection report.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the inspection in minutes.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the inspection was conducted (address, weigh station name, or facility identifier).']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments recorded by the inspector during the inspection.']
  inspection_report_number varchar [note: 'Official inspection report number issued by the inspector or enforcement agency. Unique identifier for the inspection event in DOT/CVSA systems.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall result of the DOT inspection. Pass indicates no violations; Out-of-Service indicates vehicle or driver was placed out of service; Warning Issued indicates minor issues noted; Violation Cited indicates formal citation; Conditional Pass indicates pass with follow-up required.']
  inspection_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the inspection occurred.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the DOT inspection was initiated.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of CVSA inspection performed. Level I (North American Standard Inspection - full vehicle and driver), Level II (Walk-Around Driver/Vehicle Inspection), Level III (Driver-Only Inspection), Level IV (Special Inspections), Level V (Vehicle-Only Inspection), Level VI (Enhanced NAS Inspection for Radioactive Shipments).']
  inspector_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the enforcement agency or organization that conducted the inspection (e.g., State Highway Patrol, DOT, CVSA).']
  inspector_badge_number varchar [note: 'Official badge or identification number of the inspector.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector who performed the DOT inspection.']
  oos_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of violations that met FMCSA out-of-service criteria.']
  out_of_service_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the vehicle or driver was placed out of service as a result of the inspection. True if OOS order was issued.']
  severity_weight varchar [note: 'Severity weight assigned to the violations for CSA scoring purposes (1-10 scale, with 10 being most severe).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was last updated in the system.']
  vehicle_odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Odometer reading of the vehicle at the time of inspection, in miles.']
  vehicle_oos_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the vehicle specifically was placed out of service.']
  violation_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of FMCSA violation codes cited during the inspection (e.g., 393.75B, 396.3A1).']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of violations cited during the inspection.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the violations found during the inspection, including inspector notes.']

  Note: 'Records of DOT roadside inspections and annual vehicle inspections (CVSA — Commercial Vehicle Safety Alliance) for fleet units. Captures inspection date, inspection type (Level I–VI CVSA, Annual DOT, State Patrol), inspection location, inspector name and badge number, inspection result (pass, out-of-service, warning), number of violations cited, violation codes (FMCSA OOS criteria), corrective action required flag, corrective action completion date, and inspection report number. Critical for FMCSA compliance tracking and CSA (Compliance Safety Accountability) score management.'
}

Table fleet.pre_post_trip_inspection {
  pre_post_trip_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pre-trip or post-trip vehicle inspection record.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who performed the inspection.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the mechanic who reviewed and acknowledged the inspection report and any reported defects.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route associated with this inspection, linking the inspection to the planned or completed route.']
  trailer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trailer unit attached to the vehicle during inspection, if applicable.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle (collection truck, transfer vehicle, roll-off truck, or support vehicle) that was inspected.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order generated from reported defects in this inspection, linking inspection to corrective action.']
  body_frame_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the vehicle body, frame, and structural integrity including container body, hydraulic systems, and visible damage.']
  brakes_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the vehicle braking system including service brakes, parking brake, and emergency brake.']
  cdl_number varchar [note: 'The Commercial Driver License number of the driver performing the inspection, required for DOT compliance verification.']
  coupling_devices_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for fifth wheel, pintle hook, or other coupling devices used to attach trailers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inspection record was first created in the system.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any defects, safety concerns, or maintenance issues identified during the inspection.']
  defect_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any defects or safety concerns were identified during the inspection that require maintenance attention.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect severity: critical (vehicle out-of-service), major (requires immediate attention), or minor (can be scheduled for repair).']
  driver_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the driver electronically signed or certified the inspection report.']
  emergency_equipment_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for emergency equipment including fire extinguisher, warning triangles, first aid kit, and spill kit.']
  exhaust_system_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the exhaust system including muffler, pipes, and emissions control components.']
  fuel_system_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the fuel system including tank, lines, and connections for leaks or damage. Applies to diesel, CNG, and RNG fuel systems.']
  horn_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the vehicle horn functionality.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'The facility, depot, or geographic location where the inspection was performed.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique inspection number or reference code assigned to this inspection event for tracking and audit purposes.']
  inspection_source varchar [note: 'The method or system used to capture the inspection data: mobile application, paper form (later digitized), or telematics system.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the inspection was completed by the driver.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is a pre-trip inspection (conducted before route departure) or post-trip inspection (conducted after route completion).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inspection record was last updated or modified.']
  lights_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for all vehicle lighting including headlights, taillights, brake lights, turn signals, and hazard lights.']
  mechanic_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the mechanic reviewed and acknowledged the inspection report, required for regulatory compliance.']
  mirrors_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for all vehicle mirrors including side mirrors and rearview mirrors for proper adjustment and condition.']
  odometer_reading decimal(10,1) [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time of inspection, used for maintenance scheduling and utilization tracking.']
  out_of_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was placed out of service due to critical safety defects that must be repaired before the vehicle can be operated.']
  steering_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the steering system including steering wheel, linkage, and power steering operation.']
  suspension_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for the vehicle suspension system including springs, shocks, and air suspension components.']
  tires_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for all vehicle tires including tread depth, inflation, and visible damage.']
  trailer_unit_number varchar [note: 'The fleet unit number or asset tag of the trailer inspected, if applicable.']
  wipers_status varchar [note: 'Inspection result for windshield wipers and washer system functionality.']

  Note: 'Driver-completed pre-trip and post-trip vehicle inspection records as required by FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.11. Captures inspection date, inspection type (pre-trip or post-trip), driver, vehicle unit, trailer unit (if applicable), odometer reading, each inspection item result (brakes, lights, tires, mirrors, horn, wipers, coupling devices, emergency equipment, body/frame), defect reported flag, defect description, defect severity, driver signature timestamp, and mechanic acknowledgment timestamp. Feeds directly into maintenance work order generation for reported defects.'
}

Table fleet.hos_log {
  hos_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Hours of Service log record. Primary key for the HOS log entry.']
  primary_hos_driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Commercial Driver\'s License (CDL) driver who generated this HOS log entry.']
  reviewing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Safety managers review HOS logs for FMCSA compliance violations, ELD audit trails, driver qualification file maintenance, and hours-of-service enforcement per DOT regulations.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle unit (truck) operated during this duty status period.']
  break_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the driver has taken the required 30-minute break after 8 hours of driving time, as mandated by FMCSA.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'The certification status of this HOS log entry: certified by the driver, uncertified (pending driver review), or rejected by the driver.']
  certification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the driver certified this HOS log entry, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  cumulative_driving_hours_8day decimal(5,2) [note: 'The cumulative driving hours accumulated by the driver in the rolling 8-day window at the time of this log entry, used to enforce the 60/70-hour rule.']
  cumulative_on_duty_hours_8day decimal(5,2) [note: 'The cumulative on-duty hours (driving plus on-duty not driving) accumulated by the driver in the rolling 8-day window at the time of this log entry.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source of this HOS log entry: automatically captured by ELD, manually entered by driver, or edited by carrier.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total duration in minutes that the driver remained in this duty status.']
  duty_status varchar [note: 'The duty status category as defined by FMCSA: off duty, sleeper berth, driving, or on-duty not driving.']
  duty_status_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the driver exited this duty status, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Null if the status is still active.']
  duty_status_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the driver entered this duty status, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  edit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this HOS log entry has been manually edited by the driver or carrier after initial creation.']
  edit_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided for any manual edit made to this HOS log entry, as required by FMCSA regulations.']
  eld_device_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Electronic Logging Device (ELD) that recorded this HOS log entry.']
  eld_serial_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer serial number of the ELD device used to capture this log entry.']
  gps_latitude_end decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS latitude coordinate at the end of this duty status period, captured by the ELD device.']
  gps_latitude_start decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS latitude coordinate at the start of this duty status period, captured by the ELD device.']
  gps_longitude_end decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS longitude coordinate at the end of this duty status period, captured by the ELD device.']
  gps_longitude_start decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS longitude coordinate at the start of this duty status period, captured by the ELD device.']
  hos_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this log entry resulted in an HOS violation (exceeding driving hours, on-duty hours, or missing required breaks).']
  location_end varchar [note: 'The geographic location (city, state) where the driver ended this duty status period.']
  location_start varchar [note: 'The geographic location (city, state) where the driver began this duty status period.']
  log_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which this HOS log entry applies, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  odometer_end varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the end of this duty status period.']
  odometer_start varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the start of this duty status period.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this HOS log record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this HOS log record was last updated in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or annotations entered by the driver to provide additional context for this duty status period.']
  shipping_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the shipping document or Bill of Lading (BOL) associated with the load being transported during this duty period.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'The identification number of the trailer being hauled during this duty status period, if applicable.']
  violation_type_code varchar [note: 'The specific type of HOS violation detected: 11-hour driving limit, 14-hour on-duty limit, 30-minute break requirement, 60/70-hour limit, or none if no violation occurred.']

  Note: 'Hours of Service (HOS) log records for CDL drivers as mandated by FMCSA ELD (Electronic Logging Device) rules under 49 CFR Part 395. Captures log date, driver, vehicle unit, duty status (off duty, sleeper berth, driving, on-duty not driving), duty status start timestamp, duty status end timestamp, duration in status, cumulative driving hours in 8-day window, cumulative on-duty hours in 8-day window, 30-minute break compliance flag, ELD device identifier, co-driver identifier, shipping document reference, and HOS violation flag with violation type code. Supports FMCSA compliance and driver safety management.'
}

Table fleet.accident_report {
  accident_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accident report record. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Accidents occurring while servicing specific customer contracts must be tracked for liability determination, insurance claims, customer notification requirements, and contract performance impact analy']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.corrective_action. Business justification: Accident investigations generate corrective actions (driver retraining, vehicle modifications, policy changes) tracked in safety system for closure verification and preventive measure effectiveness.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver operating the vehicle at the time of the accident. Links to driver master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the safety manager or investigator assigned to conduct the accident investigation.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Accidents with material property damage trigger asset impairment testing per IAS 36, insurance claim processing against the fixed asset record, and condition reassessment. This link enables finance to']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Fleet accidents are also safety incidents requiring unified case management for investigation, workers comp, OSHA recordability determination, and integrated safety performance reporting.']
  incident_wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Accidents occurring at WTE facility locations (tipping floor, access roads, queuing areas) must be linked to the facility for safety reporting, facility-specific incident rate tracking, insurance clai']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Accidents involving hazmat shipments require manifest reference for NRC reporting (40 CFR 302), spill response coordination, insurance claims, and determining if DOT reportable quantities were release']
  repair_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Accident repairs performed by third-party body shops/collision repair vendors. Essential for repair cost tracking, vendor performance evaluation, insurance claim documentation, and payment processing.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet vehicle involved in the accident. Links to the fleet vehicle master record.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Accidents with moving violations, hazmat releases, or DOT-reportable incidents generate violation notices from regulatory agencies. Risk managers link accident reports to notices for claims defense an']
  accident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of how the accident occurred, including sequence of events, contributing factors, and circumstances.']
  citation_description varchar [note: 'Description of the traffic violation or citation issued, including violation code and description if applicable.']
  citation_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a traffic citation or ticket was issued to the driver as a result of the accident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accident report record was first created in the system.']
  csa_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident is reportable under FMCSA CSA program and will impact the carriers safety score.']
  dot_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident meets DOT reportability criteria (fatality, injury requiring immediate medical treatment away from scene, or disabling damage requiring tow-away).']
  driver_at_fault_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company driver was determined to be at fault for the accident based on investigation findings.']
  estimated_property_damage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total dollar amount of property damage resulting from the accident, including damage to company vehicle, other vehicles, and third-party property.']
  incident_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the accident occurred.']
  incident_severity varchar [note: 'Overall severity classification of the accident based on injuries, fatalities, and property damage.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the accident occurred, including timezone information.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of accident that occurred (collision with another vehicle, rollover, backing incident, pedestrian involvement, property damage only, single vehicle incident).']
  insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company handling the claim for this accident.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number assigned by the insurance carrier for processing the accident claim.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the internal accident investigation was completed and findings documented.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal accident investigation process.']
  light_conditions varchar [note: 'Lighting conditions at the time of the accident.']
  location_address varchar [note: 'Street address or location description where the accident occurred.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the accident occurred.']
  location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the accident location, captured from telematics or manually entered.']
  location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the accident location, captured from telematics or manually entered.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the accident occurred.']
  location_zip_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code where the accident occurred.']
  number_of_fatalities varchar [note: 'Total count of fatalities resulting from the accident.']
  number_of_injuries varchar [note: 'Total count of individuals injured in the accident, including driver, passengers, pedestrians, and occupants of other vehicles.']
  police_department varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency that responded to and documented the accident.']
  police_report_number varchar [note: 'Official police report or case number assigned by law enforcement agency responding to the accident scene.']
  post_accident_test_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required post-accident drug and alcohol test was completed within the regulatory timeframe.']
  post_accident_test_date date [note: 'Date when the post-accident drug and alcohol test was administered.']
  post_accident_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-accident drug and alcohol test is required per FMCSA regulations (49 CFR Part 382) based on accident severity and circumstances.']
  post_accident_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the post-accident drug and alcohol test (negative, positive, refused, or not completed).']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident was determined to be preventable by the driver through proper defensive driving techniques, based on investigation findings.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date when the accident was first reported to the company by the driver or other party.']
  road_conditions varchar [note: 'Road surface conditions at the time and location of the accident.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accident report record was last modified in the system.']
  vehicle_speed_mph varchar [note: 'Estimated or recorded speed of the company vehicle in miles per hour at the time of the accident, captured from telematics or driver report.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time and location of the accident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|ice|wind|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'DOT-reportable and non-reportable vehicle accident and incident records for fleet units. Captures incident date and time, vehicle unit, driver, incident location (address, GPS coordinates), incident type (collision, rollover, backing incident, pedestrian, property damage), DOT reportability flag (fatality, injury, tow-away), number of injuries, number of fatalities, estimated property damage amount, police report number, citation issued flag, citation description, drug and alcohol post-accident test required flag, test completion status, insurance claim number, and incident investigation status. Required for FMCSA accident register (49 CFR 390.15) and CSA score management.'
}

Table fleet.driver_behavior_event {
  driver_behavior_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for driver_behavior_event']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who triggered the behavior event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor or fleet manager who reviewed the driver behavior event.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route being executed when the behavior event occurred.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver shift during which the behavior event occurred.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle unit in which the event occurred.']
  actual_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual vehicle speed in miles per hour at the time of the speeding event, captured from telematics.']
  address_text varchar [note: 'Human-readable street address or location description where the driver behavior event occurred, reverse-geocoded from GPS (Global Positioning System) coordinates.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the driver behavior event occurred, derived from GPS (Global Positioning System) coordinates.']
  coaching_completed_date date [note: 'Date when driver coaching or corrective action was completed for this behavior event.']
  coaching_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required driver coaching or corrective action has been completed following the behavior event.']
  coaching_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether driver coaching or corrective action is required based on the severity and frequency of the behavior event.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the driver behavior event occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the driver behavior event record was first created in the system.']
  dot_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this driver behavior event meets DOT (Department of Transportation) reporting thresholds for regulatory compliance.']
  driver_score_impact decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric impact of this behavior event on the driver\'s overall safety score, used for performance evaluation and incentive programs.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative engine hours at the time of the behavior event, captured from telematics.']
  event_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of the behavior event in seconds, applicable to events such as excessive idle or distracted driving.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the driver behavior event, including driver explanations or supervisor observations.']
  event_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the driver behavior event based on risk assessment algorithms.']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the driver behavior event record, typically Geotab Fleet Telematics.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the driver behavior event occurred, captured from telematics system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the driver behavior event captured by telematics system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: harsh_braking|harsh_acceleration|harsh_cornering|speeding|excessive_idle|seatbelt_violation|distracted_driving|after_hours_operation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fuel_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel level as a percentage of tank capacity at the time of the behavior event, captured from telematics.']
  g_force_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Gravitational force measurement in g-units for harsh braking, acceleration, or cornering events, indicating severity of the maneuver.']
  idle_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of excessive idle time in minutes for idle events, used to track fuel efficiency and driver behavior.']
  incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this driver behavior event resulted in or contributed to a safety incident or accident.']
  insurance_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this driver behavior event resulted in or contributed to an insurance claim.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate where the driver behavior event occurred, captured from GPS (Global Positioning System).']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate where the driver behavior event occurred, captured from GPS (Global Positioning System).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code where the driver behavior event occurred, derived from GPS (Global Positioning System) coordinates.']
  posted_speed_limit_mph varchar [note: 'Posted speed limit in miles per hour at the location where the speeding event occurred, derived from map data.']
  reviewed_by_supervisor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver behavior event has been reviewed by a supervisor or fleet manager.']
  reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the driver behavior event was reviewed by a supervisor or fleet manager.']
  road_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the road type where the driver behavior event occurred, used for contextual risk analysis.']
  speed_over_limit_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Amount by which the actual speed exceeded the posted speed limit in miles per hour, calculated for speeding events.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the driver behavior event occurred, derived from GPS (Global Positioning System) coordinates.']
  telematics_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Geotab telematics device that captured the behavior event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the driver behavior event record was last updated in the system.']
  vehicle_odometer_miles decimal(12,2) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time of the behavior event, captured from telematics.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time and location of the driver behavior event, if available from integrated weather data sources.']

  Note: 'Scored driver behavior events derived from Geotab telematics data capturing individual unsafe or inefficient driving behaviors. Captures event timestamp, driver, vehicle unit, event type (harsh braking, harsh acceleration, harsh cornering, speeding, excessive idle, seatbelt violation, distracted driving, after-hours operation), event severity (low, medium, high), event duration (seconds), GPS location at event, posted speed limit (for speeding events), actual speed (for speeding events), idle duration minutes (for idle events), and coaching required flag. Supports driver safety programs, insurance risk management, and fuel efficiency initiatives.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_utilization {
  vehicle_utilization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the daily vehicle utilization summary record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Measuring vehicle productivity and utilization rates by customer contract enables performance monitoring against SLA commitments, validates pricing models, and supports contract renewal negotiations. ']
  district_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational district or service area where the vehicle operated.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Commercial Driver License (CDL) driver assigned to operate the vehicle on this date.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the depot or facility from which the vehicle was dispatched.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Primary route assignment for the vehicle on this operational day.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Shift supervisors are accountable for daily vehicle utilization metrics, route completion status, operational efficiency reporting, and driver performance management at district level.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle unit for which utilization is being tracked.']
  assigned_route_count varchar [note: 'Total number of routes assigned to the vehicle during the operational day.']
  available_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours the vehicle was available for operational use during the scheduled shift.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle utilization record was first created in the system.']
  deadhead_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Non-revenue miles driven, including travel to/from depot, between routes, or repositioning.']
  dot_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the vehicle operation with DOT and FMCSA regulations for this operational day.']
  driving_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the vehicle was actively in motion during the operational day.']
  fuel_consumed_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel consumed during the operational day, measured in gallons for diesel or Gasoline Gallon Equivalent (GGE) for Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) or Renewable Natural Gas (RNG).']
  fuel_efficiency_mpg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Calculated fuel efficiency in miles per gallon (MPG) or miles per gasoline gallon equivalent (MPGe) for alternative fuel vehicles.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of fuel used by the vehicle: diesel, Compressed Natural Gas (CNG), Renewable Natural Gas (RNG), gasoline, electric, or hybrid.']
  gps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GPS tracking was active and operational for the vehicle during this utilization period.']
  idle_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the vehicle engine was running while stationary, excluding Power Take-Off (PTO) operations.']
  lift_count varchar [note: 'Total number of container lifts or pickups performed by the vehicle during the operational day.']
  maintenance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was flagged for maintenance or experienced a maintenance event during this operational day.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding vehicle utilization, operational issues, or special circumstances for this operational day.']
  odometer_end decimal(10,1) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the end of the operational day, measured in miles.']
  odometer_start decimal(10,1) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the start of the operational day, measured in miles.']
  payload_tons_collected decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of waste collected during the operational day, measured in tons, captured from onboard scales where available.']
  pto_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hours the vehicle Power Take-Off system was engaged for collection operations (compactor, hydraulic lifts).']
  revenue_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Miles driven while performing revenue-generating collection or hauling activities.']
  route_completion_status varchar [note: 'Status of route completion for the assigned routes: completed, partial, cancelled, or rescheduled.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident or violation was recorded for the vehicle during this operational day.']
  service_stop_count varchar [note: 'Total number of service stops completed during the operational day.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle operational shift ended.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle operational shift began.']
  telematics_data_quality varchar [note: 'Quality assessment of telematics data captured for this utilization record: complete, partial, missing, or error.']
  total_engine_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the vehicle engine was running during the operational day.']
  total_miles_driven decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled by the vehicle during the operational day, measured in miles.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle utilization record was last modified.']
  utilization_date date [note: 'The operational date for which vehicle utilization metrics are captured.']
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available hours that the vehicle was actively utilized, calculated as (total_engine_hours / available_hours) * 100.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle unit: Automated Side Loader (ASL), Front End Loader (FEL), Rear End Loader (REL), roll-off truck, transfer vehicle, or support vehicle.']

  Note: 'Daily vehicle utilization summary records capturing operational performance metrics for each fleet unit per operational day. Captures utilization date, vehicle unit, assigned route count, total miles driven, revenue miles, deadhead miles, total engine hours, driving hours, idle hours, PTO hours, fuel consumed (gallons or GGE), fuel efficiency (MPG or MPGe), payload tons collected (where available from onboard scales), number of lifts/pickups, number of service stops, and utilization rate percentage (actual hours vs. available hours). Supports fleet right-sizing, route efficiency analysis, and capital planning.'
}

Table fleet.drug_alcohol_test {
  drug_alcohol_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the drug and alcohol test record. Primary key.']
  accident_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accident record if this test was conducted as a post-accident test. Null if not accident-related.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Commercial Driver\'s License (CDL) driver who was tested.']
