  reviewing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: HR/safety managers review test results for FMCSA compliance, SAP referrals, return-to-duty clearances, and FMCSA Drug & Alcohol Clearinghouse reporting requirements.']
  testing_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Drug/alcohol testing performed by contracted laboratories and collection sites. Essential for vendor certification tracking, FMCSA compliance, payment processing. Role-prefixed; removes denormalized c']
  training_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_certification. Business justification: DOT drug/alcohol clearances and SAP return-to-duty certifications are regulatory certifications tracked in driver qualification files. HR and safety directors link test results to certification record']
  accident_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test was conducted as a post-accident test following a DOT-reportable accident involving fatality, injury requiring immediate medical treatment away from the scene, or disabling damage to any vehicle requiring tow-away.']
  alcohol_concentration decimal(5,3) [note: 'The measured blood alcohol concentration (BAC) level if an alcohol test was performed, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.040 for 0.04%). Null if drug-only test.']
  chain_of_custody_number varchar [note: 'The Federal CCF (Custody and Control Form) number used to track the specimen from collection through laboratory analysis.']
  clearinghouse_report_date date [note: 'The date the test result was reported to the FMCSA Drug and Alcohol Clearinghouse. Null if not yet reported or not reportable.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The specimen type or method used for collection: urine (drug test), breath (alcohol), oral fluid (drug or alcohol), or blood (rare, post-accident).']
  collection_site_address varchar [note: 'The physical address of the collection site, including street, city, state, and postal code.']
  collector_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the certified specimen collector who performed the collection procedure.']
  confirmation_test_date date [note: 'The date the confirmation test was conducted, if applicable. Null if no confirmation test was required.']
  confirmation_test_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a confirmation test (GC/MS for drugs, evidential breath test for alcohol) was performed following a non-negative screening result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug and alcohol test record was first created in the system.']
  driver_notification_date date [note: 'The date the driver was notified of the test result by the employer or MRO.']
  fmcsa_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test result has been reported to the FMCSA Drug and Alcohol Clearinghouse as required for positive, refused, adulterated, or substituted results.']
  follow_up_test_count_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of follow-up tests prescribed by the SAP (typically 6 tests in the first 12 months). Null if no follow-up testing is required.']
  follow_up_testing_end_date date [note: 'The date when the follow-up testing period prescribed by the SAP is scheduled to end (12 to 60 months from return-to-duty). Null if not applicable.']
  follow_up_testing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver is subject to unannounced follow-up testing for a minimum of 12 months (up to 60 months) as prescribed by the SAP.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'The SAMHSA certification or accreditation number of the testing laboratory.']
  mro_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the licensed physician (Medical Review Officer) who reviewed and verified the drug test result.']
  mro_phone varchar [note: 'The contact phone number for the Medical Review Officer who verified the test result.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special circumstances related to the test (e.g., shy bladder, split specimen requested, driver comments).']
  positive_substance_identified varchar [note: 'The specific controlled substance(s) detected if the test result was positive (e.g., marijuana, cocaine, opiates, amphetamines, PCP). Null if negative or alcohol-only test.']
  reasonable_suspicion_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a trained supervisor documented specific, contemporaneous observations of driver behavior, appearance, or conduct that led to a reasonable suspicion test.']
  record_retention_date date [note: 'The date through which this test record must be retained per DOT regulations (typically 5 years from test date for negative results, longer for positive results).']
  return_to_duty_clearance_date date [note: 'The date the driver was cleared by the SAP and passed a return-to-duty test, allowing them to resume safety-sensitive functions. Null if not applicable.']
  sap_evaluation_date date [note: 'The date the driver completed the initial SAP evaluation, if required. Null if no SAP referral was made.']
  sap_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the Substance Abuse Professional assigned to evaluate and recommend treatment for the driver, if applicable.']
  sap_referral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver must be referred to a Substance Abuse Professional (SAP) for evaluation and treatment following a positive, adulterated, substituted, or refused test.']
  specimen_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to the specimen (urine, breath, oral fluid) for chain-of-custody tracking.']
  substance_tested varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the test screened for drugs (5-panel or 10-panel), alcohol (breath or saliva), or both substances.']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the trained supervisor who made the reasonable suspicion determination, if applicable. Null if not a reasonable suspicion test.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug or alcohol test was conducted.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'The final outcome of the test as determined by the MRO or Breath Alcohol Technician (BAT): negative (no substances detected), positive (substance detected above threshold), dilute (specimen too diluted), substituted (not human urine), adulterated (specimen tampered), cancelled (invalid test), or refused (driver refused to test). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: negative|positive|dilute|substituted|adulterated|cancelled|refused — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_status varchar [note: 'The current administrative status of the test record: scheduled (test appointment set), completed (result finalized), cancelled (test invalidated), refused (driver refused to test), or pending review (awaiting MRO verification).']
  test_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the specimen was collected or breath test was administered.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'The category of DOT-mandated test: pre-employment (before hire), random (unannounced selection), post-accident (after qualifying incident), reasonable suspicion (supervisor-initiated), return-to-duty (after violation), or follow-up (ongoing monitoring).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this drug and alcohol test record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'DOT-mandated drug and alcohol testing records for CDL drivers under 49 CFR Part 382 (FMCSA) and Part 40 (DOT). Captures test date, driver, test type (pre-employment, random, post-accident, reasonable suspicion, return-to-duty, follow-up), substance tested (drug panel, alcohol, or both), collection site, collection method (urine, breath, oral fluid), MRO (Medical Review Officer) name, test result (negative, positive, dilute, substituted, adulterated, cancelled), alcohol concentration (BAC) if applicable, positive substance identified (if applicable), SAP (Substance Abuse Professional) referral flag, and return-to-duty clearance date. Critical for FMCSA compliance and driver qualification file maintenance.'
}

Table fleet.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cost_allocation']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Fundamental business process: allocating fleet operating costs (fuel, maintenance, depreciation) to customer contracts for profitability analysis, pricing validation, and contract renewal decisions. E']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this cost allocation for posting, for audit and accountability.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Fleet costs are allocated to specific service offerings for profitability analysis, rate setting, cost-to-serve calculations, and offering-level P&L reporting. Essential for financial management and p']
  original_allocation_cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original cost allocation record if this record is a reversal or correction.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific collection route to which the cost is allocated, if applicable.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle unit for which costs are being allocated.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with this cost allocation, if the cost is maintenance-related.']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Fleet operating costs are allocated to WTE facilities for internal cost accounting and profitability analysis. Finance needs to attribute vehicle costs (fuel, maintenance, depreciation) to the specifi']
  allocation_basis_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the allocation basis used (miles driven, hours operated, tons hauled) for the allocation calculation.']
  allocation_basis_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the allocation basis quantity (miles, kilometers, hours, tons, trips, percentage).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate the cost (actual, standard rate, mileage-based, hours-based, tonnage-based, equal distribution).']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments explaining special circumstances, adjustments, or rationale for this cost allocation.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total cost allocated to this cost center or service line when using percentage-based allocation (0.00 to 100.00).']
  allocation_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the period for which costs are being allocated (typically month or year end).']
  allocation_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the period for which costs are being allocated (typically month or year start).']
  allocation_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The rate applied per unit of allocation basis (e.g., cost per mile, cost per hour) used to calculate the allocated amount.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'The current workflow status of the cost allocation record (draft, pending approval, approved, posted, reversed, cancelled).']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost allocation was approved for posting.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of the cost being allocated for this category and period, in USD.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'The category of fleet operating cost being allocated (fuel, preventive maintenance, corrective repair, tires, insurance, registration, depreciation, lease, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fuel|preventive_maintenance|corrective_repair|tires|insurance|registration|depreciation|lease|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The SAP cost center code to which the fleet cost is allocated (operational cost center, route, district).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost allocation record was first created in the system.']
  district_code varchar [note: 'The operational district code to which the cost is allocated for geographic reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this cost allocation belongs (1-12).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this cost allocation belongs for annual financial reporting.']
  fuel_consumed_gallons decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total fuel consumed in gallons during the allocation period, sourced from Geotab telematics or fuel card transactions.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'The fuel type of the vehicle (diesel, CNG - Compressed Natural Gas, RNG - Renewable Natural Gas, gasoline, electric, hybrid) for sustainability and cost analysis.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP General Ledger account code to which this cost is posted for financial statement reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cost allocation record was last updated or modified.']
  mileage_driven decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total miles driven by the vehicle during the allocation period, sourced from Geotab telematics.']
  operating_hours decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total operating hours logged by the vehicle during the allocation period, sourced from Geotab telematics.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The SAP profit center code for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for reversing or cancelling this cost allocation, if applicable (e.g., data error, period adjustment, reallocation).']
  sap_cost_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI/CO document number that records this cost allocation transaction for financial reporting and audit trail.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the cost allocation was posted in SAP FI/CO for financial period assignment.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'The business service line to which the cost is allocated (residential, commercial, roll-off, recycling, hazmat, transfer) for COGS and EBITDA analysis.']
  tonnage_hauled decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tonnage hauled by the vehicle during the allocation period, measured in tons, for tonnage-based cost allocation.']
  trips_completed varchar [note: 'The total number of trips or runs completed by the vehicle during the allocation period.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'The type of vehicle for which costs are allocated (ASL - Automated Side Loader, FEL - Front End Loader, REL - Rear End Loader, roll-off, transfer, support).']

  Note: 'Periodic cost allocation records distributing fleet operating costs (fuel, maintenance, depreciation, insurance, licensing) to operational cost centers, routes, districts, and service lines. Captures allocation period (month/year), vehicle unit, cost category (fuel, preventive maintenance, corrective repair, tires, insurance, registration, depreciation), cost amount, cost center code, district code, service line (residential, commercial, roll-off), allocation method (actual, standard rate, mileage-based), SAP cost document reference, and allocation status. Integrates with SAP FI/CO for financial reporting and supports COGS and EBITDA analysis by service line.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_lifecycle_event {
  vehicle_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle lifecycle event record. Primary key.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Vehicle commissioning/decommissioning events trigger emission source inventory updates. When vehicles enter/exit fleet, corresponding emission_source records must be created/retired for accurate GHG i']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor who authorized or approved this lifecycle event. Provides accountability and audit trail.']
  primary_vehicle_district_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational district or service area associated with this lifecycle event. Relevant for transfer, assignment, and deployment events.']
  primary_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle unit that this lifecycle event pertains to. Links to the vehicle master record.']
  tertiary_vehicle_transfer_to_district_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination district for vehicle transfer events. Null for non-transfer events.']
  yard_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical yard, depot, or maintenance facility where the vehicle was located at the time of this event.']
  accident_claim_number varchar [note: 'The insurance claim number associated with accident damage events. Links to insurance and risk management systems.']
  actual_return_to_service_date date [note: 'The actual date when the vehicle was returned to operational service. Enables comparison against estimated date for performance tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source operational system from which this lifecycle event record originated (e.g., Infor EAM, Geotab, SAP MM).']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the vehicle was disposed of at end-of-life. Applicable only for decommissioned, sold, scrapped, or totaled event types.']
  disposal_vendor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the third-party vendor, buyer, or salvage company involved in the vehicle disposal transaction.']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours the vehicle was unavailable for service due to this lifecycle event. Used for fleet availability and utilization KPI reporting.']
  engine_hours varchar [note: 'The cumulative engine operating hours at the time of the lifecycle event. Critical for maintenance scheduling and asset valuation.']
  estimated_return_to_service_date date [note: 'The projected date when the vehicle is expected to return to operational service. Used for fleet availability planning and resource allocation.']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the lifecycle event occurred or was recorded. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the lifecycle event, including context, circumstances, and any relevant operational notes.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or observations related to the lifecycle event. Captures supplementary information not covered by structured fields.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of lifecycle milestone event. Categorizes the nature of the vehicle status transition or operational change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ordered|received|commissioned|placed_in_service|out_of_service|returned_to_service|transferred|refurbished|decommissioned|sold|scrapped|totaled — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The vehicle operational status resulting from this lifecycle event. Represents the current state after the event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ordered|in_transit|commissioned|in_service|out_of_service|transferred|refurbished|decommissioned|disposed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  odometer_reading_miles varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time of the lifecycle event. Provides mileage-based lifecycle tracking.']
  oos_reason_category varchar [note: 'The primary reason category for an out-of-service event. Applicable only when event_type is out_of_service. Supports DOT and FMCSA compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dot_order|mechanical_failure|scheduled_pm|accident_damage|awaiting_parts|regulatory_hold|safety_inspection|body_repair|engine_overhaul|transmission_repair — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  oos_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the specific reason for the out-of-service status, including technical diagnosis, failure mode, or regulatory citation.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The vehicle operational status immediately before this lifecycle event occurred. Provides audit trail of status transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ordered|in_transit|commissioned|in_service|out_of_service|transferred|refurbished|decommissioned|disposed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number associated with vehicle acquisition or major refurbishment events. Links to SAP MM procurement records.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this lifecycle event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this lifecycle event record was last modified. Audit trail for data governance.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory code, standard, or citation that triggered or governs this lifecycle event. Applicable for compliance-driven events.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event was triggered by or related to regulatory compliance requirements (DOT, EPA, OSHA). True if compliance-related, False otherwise.']
  replacement_vehicle_assigned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a replacement vehicle was assigned to cover the operational capacity during an out-of-service period. True if replacement assigned, False otherwise.']
  transaction_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value associated with the lifecycle event in US Dollars. Applicable for acquisition, sale, disposal, or refurbishment events. Null for non-financial events.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'The maintenance or repair work order number associated with this lifecycle event. Links to Infor EAM work order records for maintenance-related events.']

  Note: 'Milestone lifecycle event records tracking the full cradle-to-grave history of each fleet vehicle from acquisition through disposal, including out-of-service periods. Captures event date, vehicle unit, event type (ordered, received, commissioned, placed-in-service, out-of-service, returned-to-service, transferred, refurbished, decommissioned, sold, scrapped, totaled), event description, prior and new status, associated district/yard, OOS reason category (DOT order, mechanical failure, scheduled PM, accident damage, awaiting parts, regulatory hold), estimated and actual return-to-service dates, replacement vehicle assigned flag, transaction value, disposal method, and authorizing manager. Provides complete vehicle lifecycle and availability audit trail.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_class {
  vehicle_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle class reference record. Primary key.']
  emission_factor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_factor. Business justification: Vehicle class characteristics (fuel type, engine specs, body type) determine applicable EPA emission factors for mobile source calculations. Business process: emission factor assignment for fleet GHG ']
  fleet_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fleet managers define vehicle class specifications, PM intervals, lifecycle policies, and procurement standards for fleet standardization, total cost of ownership analysis, and capital planning.']
  parent_vehicle_class_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vehicle_class (parent_vehicle_class_id)']
  annual_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles in this class require annual DOT safety inspections. True if required, False if not.']
  average_acquisition_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average acquisition cost for a new vehicle in this class, measured in US dollars. Used for capital planning and budgeting.']
  average_annual_operating_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average annual operating cost for vehicles in this class including fuel, maintenance, insurance, and depreciation, measured in US dollars.']
  average_stops_per_shift varchar [note: 'Typical number of service stops this vehicle class can complete in a standard shift. Used for route planning and capacity modeling.']
  average_tons_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average tonnage of waste this vehicle class typically collects per operating day. TPD = Tons Per Day.']
  axle_configuration varchar [note: 'Axle configuration of the vehicle class (e.g., 4x2, 6x4, 8x4). Format is drive_axles x total_axles.']
  backup_camera_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backup cameras are required safety equipment for this vehicle class. True if required, False if not.']
  body_capacity_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard body capacity of the vehicle class measured in cubic yards. Represents the volume of waste the vehicle can hold.']
  body_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Typical or preferred body manufacturer for vehicles in this class (e.g., McNeilus, Heil, Labrie, New Way).']
  body_type varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle body configuration. ASL = Automated Side Loader, FEL = Front End Loader, REL = Rear End Loader. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASL|FEL|REL|roll-off|transfer|hook-lift|grapple|compactor|flatbed|service — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cdl_class_required varchar [note: 'Minimum CDL class required to operate vehicles in this class (A, B, C, or none if no CDL required).']
  cdl_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Commercial Driver License is required to operate vehicles in this class. True if CDL is required, False if not.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the vehicle class (e.g., ASL-20, FEL-30, REL-25, ROLLOFF-40). Used as the business identifier for classification.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the vehicle class (e.g., Automated Side Loader 20-Yard, Front End Loader 30-Yard, Rear End Loader 25-Yard, Roll-Off Truck 40-Yard).']
  compaction_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical compaction ratio achieved by this vehicle class (e.g., 3.5:1 means 3.5 cubic yards of loose waste compacts to 1 cubic yard). Null for non-compacting vehicles.']
  compaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of compaction system used in the vehicle body (packer for rear loaders, compactor for side loaders, non-compacting for roll-offs, stationary for transfer vehicles).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle class record was first created in the system.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Standard crew size for vehicles in this class (e.g., 1 for automated side loaders, 2-3 for rear loaders with helpers).']
  dot_vehicle_class varchar [note: 'DOT vehicle classification number (1-8) based on GVWR. Class 1-2: light duty, 3-6: medium duty, 7-8: heavy duty.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this vehicle class definition became effective and available for fleet planning and procurement.']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'EPA emissions standard certification level for this vehicle class (e.g., EPA 2010, EPA 2021, CARB Ultra-Low NOx).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this vehicle class was deprecated or discontinued. Null for active classes.']
  endorsements_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CDL endorsements required for this vehicle class (e.g., H for hazmat, N for tank, X for hazmat+tank). Null if no endorsements required.']
  expected_useful_life_miles varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of vehicles in this class measured in miles before replacement is typically required.']
  expected_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of vehicles in this class measured in years before replacement is typically required.']
  fuel_tank_capacity_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard fuel tank capacity for this vehicle class measured in gallons. For CNG vehicles, this represents gasoline gallon equivalent (GGE).']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel type for this vehicle class. CNG = Compressed Natural Gas, RNG = Renewable Natural Gas.']
  gvwr_lbs varchar [note: 'Gross Vehicle Weight Rating in pounds. Maximum total weight of the vehicle including chassis, body, fuel, and payload as specified by the manufacturer.']
  hydraulic_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydraulic system used for lifting and compaction operations (single-stage, two-stage, or none for non-hydraulic vehicles).']
  lift_mechanism varchar [note: 'Type of lifting mechanism used by this vehicle class to handle containers or waste (automated arm for ASL, front forks for FEL, rear forks for REL, hook/cable for roll-off, grapple for transfer, none for non-collection vehicles). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automated_arm|front_forks|rear_forks|hook|cable|grapple|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manufacturer_make varchar [note: 'Typical or preferred manufacturer make for vehicles in this class (e.g., Peterbilt, Mack, Freightliner, Autocar).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, specifications, or operational guidance for this vehicle class.']
  payload_capacity_lbs varchar [note: 'Maximum payload weight capacity in pounds that the vehicle class is rated to carry safely.']
  pm_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard preventive maintenance interval in calendar days for vehicles in this class. PM = Preventive Maintenance.']
  pm_interval_miles varchar [note: 'Standard preventive maintenance interval in miles for vehicles in this class. PM = Preventive Maintenance.']
  pto_equipped_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles in this class are equipped with Power Take-Off systems for hydraulic operations. True if PTO-equipped, False if not.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'Primary service line this vehicle class is designed to support (residential, commercial, industrial, recycling, hazardous waste, landfill operations).']
  telematics_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telematics devices are required for vehicles in this class. True if required, False if optional.']
  typical_route_type varchar [note: 'Typical route type this vehicle class is optimized for (residential collection, commercial collection, industrial, transfer haul, landfill operations, or mixed use).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle class record was last modified.']
  vehicle_category varchar [note: 'High-level operational category of the vehicle class (collection, transfer, roll-off, support, administrative).']
  vehicle_class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this vehicle class. Active = currently in use, Inactive = no longer procuring but existing vehicles still operational, Deprecated = phased out, Planned = future class not yet deployed.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of fleet vehicle types and body configurations (ASL, FEL, REL, roll-off, CNG, RNG, electric) with capacity specs, weight ratings, and operational parameters'
}

Table fleet.maintenance_schedule {
  maintenance_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule entry. Primary key.']
  assigned_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility or yard where this preventive maintenance is scheduled to be performed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mechanic or technician assigned to perform this preventive maintenance.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules are asset maintenance strategies that drive condition-based maintenance planning, asset reliability analysis, and maintenance cost capitalization decisions per IAS 16.']
  service_checklist_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized service checklist template that defines the tasks and inspections to be performed during this preventive maintenance.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Preventive maintenance services (oil changes, tire rotations, hydraulic service) are often executed under master service agreements. Links scheduled maintenance to contracted service terms for complia']
  superseded_maintenance_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_schedule (superseded_maintenance_schedule_id)']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle assigned to this maintenance schedule.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual duration (in hours) taken to complete this preventive maintenance service.']
  calendar_interval_days varchar [note: 'The calendar-based interval (in days) at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The certification or inspection number issued upon successful completion of this preventive maintenance, particularly for DOT or emissions inspections.']
  completion_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The vehicle engine hours at the time this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  completion_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading (in miles) at the time this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance schedule record was first created in the system.']
  defects_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any defects or issues identified during this preventive maintenance service.']
  defects_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any defects or issues were identified during this preventive maintenance service.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance includes a DOT annual inspection requirement.']
  emissions_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance includes an emissions inspection requirement per EPA and state regulations.']
  engine_hours_interval varchar [note: 'The engine hours interval at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated duration (in hours) required to complete this preventive maintenance service.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated labor cost for this preventive maintenance service.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated cost of parts required for this preventive maintenance service.']
  fmcsa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance is required by FMCSA regulations for DOT compliance.']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'The date when this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_completed_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The vehicle engine hours at the time this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_completed_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading (in miles) at the time this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance schedule record was last modified.']
  mileage_interval varchar [note: 'The mileage interval (in miles) at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The calculated date when this preventive maintenance is next due based on calendar interval.']
  next_due_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The calculated engine hours at which this preventive maintenance is next due based on engine hours interval.']
  next_due_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The calculated odometer reading (in miles) at which this preventive maintenance is next due based on mileage interval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to this preventive maintenance schedule entry.']
  out_of_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was placed out of service as a result of findings during this preventive maintenance.']
  pm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the preventive maintenance service level (A-service for minor, B-service for intermediate, C-service for major overhaul, annual inspection, DOT inspection, emissions inspection).']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this preventive maintenance schedule entry.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule, used for tracking and reference in Infor EAM.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance schedule entry.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to be performed.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this preventive maintenance is scheduled to begin.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'The basis for triggering this preventive maintenance event: mileage-based, engine hours-based, calendar-based, or combined criteria per FMCSA requirements.']

  Note: 'Fleet-specific preventive maintenance schedule linking vehicles to PM intervals based on mileage, engine hours, and calendar triggers per FMCSA requirements'
}

Table fleet.fleet_insurance {
  fleet_insurance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fleet insurance policy record. Primary key.']
  carrier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Insurance carriers are vendors providing insurance services. Critical for vendor management, policy renewal tracking, claims processing, payment management. Role-prefixed to distinguish from other ven']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Fleet insurance policies cover specific fixed assets and must reconcile insured value vs. book value for risk management and certificate of insurance generation. This link enables finance to identify ']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: DOT insurance filings (MCS-90, BMC-91X) and certificates of insurance are regulatory permits/authorizations. Compliance officers track insurance permits alongside operating permits for unified DOT/FMC']
  renewed_fleet_insurance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fleet_insurance (renewed_fleet_insurance_id)']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle covered by this insurance policy.']
  additional_insured_parties varchar [note: 'Names of any additional parties covered under this insurance policy.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy is set to automatically renew at expiration.']
  broker_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the insurance broker.']
  broker_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for contacting the insurance broker.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance broker or agent who facilitated this policy.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was cancelled before its natural expiration.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the insurance policy was cancelled.']
  cargo_coverage_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the insurer will pay for damage to cargo being transported.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance carrier\'s internal identifier for this policy.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the certificate of insurance was issued.']
  certificate_of_insurance_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the certificate of insurance document issued for this policy.']
  claims_phone_number varchar [note: 'Phone number to contact the insurance carrier for filing claims.']
  coverage_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic territories where the insurance coverage is valid (e.g., USA, Canada, Mexico).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this insurance policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this policy.']
  deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount the insured must pay out-of-pocket before insurance coverage applies.']
  dot_filing_type varchar [note: 'Type of DOT insurance filing form submitted to demonstrate financial responsibility.']
  dot_minimum_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy meets DOT minimum financial responsibility requirements for commercial motor vehicles.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage becomes active and binding.']
  excluded_drivers varchar [note: 'List of drivers explicitly excluded from coverage under this policy.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage terminates unless renewed.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent insurance audit conducted for premium adjustment purposes.']
  liability_coverage_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the insurer will pay for liability claims per occurrence.']
  loss_payee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party (typically a lender or lessor) designated to receive insurance claim payments.']
  named_insured varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or person listed as the insured party on the policy.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next insurance audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this insurance policy.']
  physical_damage_coverage_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the insurer will pay for physical damage to the insured vehicle.']
  policy_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the insurance policy document.']
  policy_endorsements varchar [note: 'Additional endorsements or riders attached to the base policy that modify coverage terms.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique policy number assigned by the insurance carrier for this commercial auto insurance policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the insurance policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Type of commercial auto insurance coverage provided by this policy.']
  premium_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total premium amount charged for this insurance policy coverage period.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which insurance premium payments are due.']
  radius_of_operation varchar [note: 'Geographic operating radius covered by the policy (local, regional, or nationwide).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for policy renewal evaluation and processing.']
  uninsured_motorist_coverage_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the insurer will pay for damages caused by uninsured or underinsured motorists.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this insurance policy record was last modified in the system.']
  vehicle_use_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of how the insured vehicle is used, affecting coverage terms and premium.']

  Note: 'Commercial auto insurance policy records for fleet vehicles including liability, physical damage, cargo coverage, and DOT minimum financial responsibility compliance'
}

Table fleet.driver_initiative_participation {
  driver_initiative_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this driver-initiative participation record. Primary key for the association.']
  carbon_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carbon reduction initiative in which the driver is enrolled']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the driver participating in the carbon initiative']
  behavior_score_baseline decimal(5,2) [note: 'Driver\'s baseline eco-driving behavior score measured before participation in this initiative. Used to calculate improvement and initiative effectiveness. Scale typically 0-100.']
  behavior_score_current decimal(5,2) [note: 'Driver\'s current eco-driving behavior score while participating in this initiative. Updated periodically based on telematics data. Scale typically 0-100.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the driver achieved certification for this eco-driving or sustainability initiative. Null if not yet certified.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the driver\'s certification for this initiative expires and requires renewal. Null if certification does not expire or driver is not certified.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the driver for this specific initiative: not_certified (no certification obtained), in_progress (working toward certification), certified (holds valid certification), expired (certification lapsed), revoked (certification removed).']
  co2e_reduction_contribution_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated metric tons of CO2 equivalent emissions reduced by this specific driver\'s participation in this initiative. Aggregated across all drivers to measure total initiative impact.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the driver was enrolled in this specific carbon initiative or eco-driving program.']
  fuel_efficiency_improvement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage improvement in fuel efficiency achieved by this driver through participation in this initiative, calculated as (current_mpg - baseline_mpg) / baseline_mpg * 100. Used for ROI calculation and driver performance tracking.']
  idle_time_reduction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction in vehicle idle time achieved by this driver through participation in idle reduction initiatives. Calculated from telematics data comparing baseline to current period.']
  incentive_earned_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total monetary incentive or bonus earned by this driver for achieving performance targets in this carbon initiative. Used for driver engagement and program ROI tracking.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date when this driver\'s performance in this initiative was last reviewed by the program manager or sustainability team.']
  next_performance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review of this driver\'s participation in this initiative.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this driver\'s participation in this initiative, including special circumstances, accommodations, or performance observations.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the driver\'s participation in this initiative: enrolled (registered but not started), active (currently participating), completed (finished program), withdrawn (voluntarily left), suspended (temporarily inactive).']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the driver completed the required training for this carbon initiative. Null if training is not yet complete or not required.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between drivers and carbon reduction initiatives. It captures driver enrollment in eco-driving programs, idle reduction training, and other sustainability initiatives. Each record links one driver to one carbon initiative with training completion status, certification details, and performance metrics that exist only in the context of this participation.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, drivers participate in multiple concurrent carbon reduction initiatives (idle reduction, eco-driving training, speed management, route efficiency programs), and each initiative enrolls hundreds of drivers across the fleet. The business actively manages these participations as operational records with training completion, certification status, performance metrics, and incentive tracking. This is not a derived correlation but an operational enrollment process managed by sustainability and fleet safety teams.'
}

Table fleet.fueling_station {
  fueling_station_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fueling_station']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent facility or operational site where this fueling station is located.']
  parent_fueling_station_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fueling_station (parent_fueling_station_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the fueling station location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, building, or unit number.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the fueling station is located.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for fueling station communications and operational coordination.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the fueling station operations or management.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the fueling station contract or service agreement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the contract or agreement governing the use of this fueling station.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the fueling station contract or service agreement.']
  cost_per_gallon_cng decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current negotiated or market cost per gallon equivalent for CNG fuel at this station.']
  cost_per_gallon_diesel decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current negotiated or market cost per gallon for diesel fuel at this station, used for fleet cost analysis.']
  cost_per_kwh_electric decimal(8,4) [note: 'Current cost per kilowatt-hour for electric vehicle charging at this station.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the fueling station location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fueling station record was first created in the system.']
  daily_throughput_capacity_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum daily fuel dispensing capacity in gallons, used for fleet scheduling and station utilization analysis.']
  decommissioned_date date [note: 'Date when the fueling station was permanently taken out of service, if applicable.']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental certifications or compliance standards met by the fueling station (e.g., EPA Tier 3, CARB, LEED).']
  fuel_types_available varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fuel types available at this station (e.g., diesel, CNG, RNG, gasoline, electric). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: diesel|cng|rng|gasoline|electric|biodiesel|lng|hydrogen — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_permit_number varchar [note: 'Permit number for handling and storing hazardous materials at the fueling station, required for compliance.']
  is_24_hour_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fueling station provides round-the-clock access for fleet vehicles.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fueling station record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the fueling station for GPS routing and telematics integration.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the fueling station for GPS routing and telematics integration.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required safety or regulatory inspection of the fueling station.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or station-specific information.']
  number_of_cng_dispensers varchar [note: 'Count of CNG-specific dispensers at the station, critical for CNG fleet capacity planning.']
  number_of_electric_chargers varchar [note: 'Count of electric vehicle charging stations available for electric fleet vehicles.']
  number_of_pumps varchar [note: 'Total count of fueling pumps or dispensers available at the station.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the fueling station (e.g., 24/7, Mon-Fri 6am-10pm), used for fleet scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fueling station indicating its availability for fleet operations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the fueling station address.']
  primary_fuel_type varchar [note: 'The predominant fuel type dispensed at this station, used for fleet routing and capacity planning.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Business region or district code for organizational reporting and fleet management hierarchy.']
  safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety inspection for the fueling station, required for regulatory compliance.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the fueling station location.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the fueling station, used for operational reference and reporting.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fueling station for identification and display purposes.']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fueling station based on ownership and access model.']
  storage_capacity_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total fuel storage capacity at the station measured in gallons, used for inventory and supply chain planning.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party vendor or operator managing the fueling station, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fueling_station. Referenced by fueling_station_id.'
}

Table fleet.geofence_zone {
  geofence_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for geofence_zone']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility associated with this geofence zone (e.g., landfill, transfer station, depot).']
  parent_geofence_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on geofence_zone (parent_geofence_zone_id)']
  service_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service area that this geofence zone belongs to or supports.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address or location descriptor for the geofence zone (organizational contact data).']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, building, or unit number for the geofence zone (organizational contact data).']
  alert_on_entry boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an alert should be triggered when a vehicle enters this geofence zone.']
  alert_on_exit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an alert should be triggered when a vehicle exits this geofence zone.']
  allowed_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of vehicle types permitted to operate within this geofence zone (e.g., ASL, FEL, REL, roll-off, CNG, RNG).']
  area_square_meters decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total area covered by the geofence zone in square meters, calculated from the defined geometry.']
  boundary_coordinates varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the geofence boundary coordinates (e.g., WKT, GeoJSON string) defining the polygon or custom shape.']
  center_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the geofence zone center point in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  center_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the geofence zone center point in decimal degrees (WGS84).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the geofence zone is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the geofence zone is located (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this geofence zone record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geofence zone record was first created in the system.']
  dot_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operations within this geofence zone are subject to DOT/FMCSA compliance monitoring and reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this geofence zone is deactivated or retired. Null for zones with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this geofence zone becomes active and begins being monitored by telematics systems.']
  external_zone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this geofence zone in the external telematics or fleet management system (e.g., Geotab zone ID).']
  geometry_type varchar [note: 'The geometric shape type used to define the geofence zone boundaries (e.g., circle, polygon, rectangle, custom).']
  hazmat_zone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this geofence zone is designated for hazardous waste handling and requires special compliance monitoring.']
  idle_time_threshold_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed idle time in minutes for vehicles within this geofence zone before triggering an alert or compliance violation.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this geofence zone record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this geofence zone record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or operational instructions related to this geofence zone.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the geofence zone location (organizational contact data).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for monitoring and alerting when vehicles enter or exit this geofence zone.']
  radius_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Radius of the geofence zone in meters, applicable when geometry_type is circle. Null for non-circular zones.']
  requires_cdl boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drivers operating within this geofence zone must hold a valid Commercial Driver License (CDL).']
  restricted_hours varchar [note: 'Time ranges during which access to or operations within the geofence zone are restricted (e.g., \'22:00-06:00\' for noise ordinances).']
  speed_limit_mph varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed speed in miles per hour for vehicles operating within this geofence zone. Used for compliance monitoring.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the geofence zone is located.']
  telematics_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the telematics system provider that monitors this geofence zone (e.g., Geotab Fleet Telematics).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the geofence zone (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago) used for timestamp interpretation.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the geofence zone used in operational systems and reporting.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the geofence zone, including its purpose, boundaries, and operational context.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the geofence zone (e.g., \'Downtown Collection District\', \'Landfill Perimeter\', \'Customer Service Area 5\').']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the geofence zone indicating whether it is actively monitored and enforced.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the geofence zone based on its operational purpose (e.g., service area, facility perimeter, restricted zone, customer site, depot, transfer station).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for geofence_zone. Referenced by geofence_zone_id.'
}

Table maintenance.work_order {

  air_pollution_control_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.air_pollution_control_unit. Business justification: APC units require frequent maintenance work orders for baghouse filter replacements, scrubber maintenance, reagent injection system repairs, and CEMS calibration. Essential for emissions compliance an']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: WTE facility boiler units require maintenance work orders for repairs, inspections, tube cleaning, refractory work, and regulatory compliance. Essential for tracking boiler-specific maintenance histor']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Fleet maintenance work orders are frequently performed under third-party service contracts, warranty agreements, or vendor maintenance contracts. Real business process: contract compliance tracking, w']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Work orders are performed ON emission sources (LFG collection systems, flares, compressors, vehicles). Linking work orders to emission sources enables compliance documentation, uptime reporting, and m']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or depot where the maintenance work is performed (e.g., vehicle maintenance shop, landfill facility, transfer station). Used for geographic and operational reporting.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset against which this work order is executed. Links to the asset master in the Asset Management domain (e.g., collection truck, compactor, landfill equipment, transfer station conveyor).']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: Maintenance work at EPA-registered generator facilities requires tracking the generator for regulatory compliance, DOT inspection reporting, and cost allocation. Essential for RCRA compliance document']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Customer-billable work orders (container repairs, equipment maintenance at customer sites) must link to invoices for revenue recognition. Enables traceability from service delivery to billing, require']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Complex or hazardous work orders require Job Hazard Analysis before execution. Real business process: pre-work safety assessment for high-risk maintenance (confined space, elevated work, energized equ']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Maintenance work orders for landfill equipment (compactors, gas collection systems, leachate pumps) must be tracked by site for environmental compliance reporting, regulatory audits, and site-specific']
  lfg_collection_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.lfg_collection_system. Business justification: LFG collection systems require maintenance work orders for well field optimization, blower repairs, header pipe maintenance, and flare station service. Essential for RNG facility operations and methan']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance plan or PM cycle that generated this work order, if applicable. Null for corrective or emergency work orders not originating from a scheduled PM cycle.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP FI/CO cost center responsible for absorbing the maintenance costs associated with this work order. Enables financial allocation, EBITDA reporting, and maintenance cost analysis by business unit.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Certain maintenance activities are mandated by regulations (DOT inspections, EPA equipment standards, OSHA machinery safeguards). Work orders must reference the driving regulatory requirement for comp']
  rng_processing_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.rng_processing_unit. Business justification: RNG processing units require specialized maintenance work orders for membrane replacement, compressor service, siloxane removal system maintenance, and gas quality monitoring equipment. Critical for R']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Work orders implement safety program requirements (OSHA inspections, EPA compliance, LOTO programs). Waste Management tracks which safety program mandates triggered maintenance work for regulatory aud']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Work orders for service equipment (compactors, balers, containers) are tracked against service offerings for cost allocation, warranty claims, and SLA compliance. Real WM operations require linking ma']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Turbine generators require dedicated maintenance work orders for bearing inspections, oil changes, vibration analysis, and generator testing. Critical for tracking generator-specific maintenance and c']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: Work orders should be classified by standardized maintenance type for consistent categorization, cost allocation, and regulatory reporting. Currently has wo_type (STRING) which should reference mainte']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Work orders generating hazardous waste (parts cleaning, fluid changes, contaminated materials) must reference approved waste profiles for proper characterization, manifest preparation, and disposal co']
  actual_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred for the work order upon completion, including actual labor, parts consumed, and external contractor charges in USD. Posted to the SAP FI/CO cost center. Used for EBITDA reporting, maintenance budget variance, and asset lifecycle cost analysis.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the maintenance work was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual labor duration, downtime, and mean time to repair (MTTR) metrics.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours expended by technicians to complete the work order. Captured from time confirmations in SAP PM or Infor EAM. Used for labor cost calculation, productivity analysis, and maintenance KPI reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the technician began executing the work order. Used to calculate schedule adherence, labor hours, and downtime duration. Compared against scheduled_start_date for KPI reporting.']
  asset_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hours reading of the asset at the time the work order was executed. Used to track PM intervals (e.g., oil change every 5,000 miles), calculate mean time between failures (MTBF), and validate DOT inspection compliance.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was formally closed in the originating system, confirming all costs have been settled and the asset has been returned to service. Used for cycle time analysis, cost settlement confirmation, and regulatory record completion.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the repair, adjustment, or corrective action performed by the technician to resolve the failure. Captured at work order completion. Required for DOT inspection records and OSHA incident documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was first created in the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Used for aging analysis, SLA measurement, and audit trails.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is associated with or fulfills a DOT vehicle inspection requirement under 49 CFR Part 396. When True, the work order record serves as part of the DOT inspection documentation and must be retained per regulatory retention schedules.']
  dot_inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of DOT inspection associated with this work order, if applicable. Annual = federally mandated annual vehicle inspection; Pre_Trip = driver pre-trip inspection defect repair; Post_Trip = post-trip defect repair; Roadside = repair following roadside enforcement inspection; None = not a DOT inspection work order.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset was returned to operational service following maintenance completion. Used with downtime_start_timestamp to calculate total asset downtime duration for fleet availability reporting.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset was taken out of service due to the maintenance event. May precede actual_start_timestamp if the asset was grounded before a technician was assigned. Critical for fleet availability and route impact analysis.']
  estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost of the work order at time of creation, including planned labor, parts, and external services in USD. Used for budget planning, purchase order approval thresholds, and maintenance cost forecasting.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned number of labor hours required to complete the work order, as estimated at time of creation. Used for workforce scheduling, capacity planning, and cost estimation. Compared against actual_labor_hours for variance analysis.']
  external_service_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cost of external contractor or vendor services engaged to complete or support the work order in USD. Linked to purchase orders (PO) in SAP MM. Relevant for outsourced repairs, specialized inspections, and warranty work.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of failure or defect (e.g., brake failure, hydraulic leak, engine fault). Sourced from the SAP PM or Infor EAM failure catalog. Enables structured failure mode analysis and fleet reliability reporting.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the observed failure, defect, or maintenance need that triggered the work order. Captured by the requestor or technician. Used for root cause analysis, failure mode trending, and regulatory incident documentation.']
  loto_cleared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required LOTO energy isolation procedure has been completed and verified prior to work commencement. Must be True before work begins on LOTO-required work orders. Supports OSHA compliance audit trails.']
  loto_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required before executing this work order. Mandatory safety clearance flag per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for work on equipment with hazardous energy sources (hydraulics, electrical, pneumatic).']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the asset_meter_reading field. Miles or kilometers for vehicle odometers; hours for engine hour meters; cycles for equipment with cycle-based PM intervals.']
  originating_system varchar [note: 'Source system that created and owns this work order record. Determines the authoritative system of record for reconciliation. SAP_PM indicates SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance module; INFOR_EAM indicates Infor Enterprise Asset Management.']
  parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Actual cost of spare parts and materials consumed during work order execution in USD. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue or Infor EAM parts transactions. Enables parts vs. labor cost split analysis for maintenance optimization.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number from SAP MM associated with external services or parts procurement for this work order. Links maintenance execution to the procurement process for AP matching and spend management.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the work order, driving scheduling urgency and resource allocation. Critical = immediate safety or regulatory risk (e.g., brake failure, DOT out-of-service condition); High = significant operational impact; Medium = moderate impact; Low = minor issue; Routine = standard PM cycle.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is required to satisfy a regulatory compliance obligation beyond DOT (e.g., EPA equipment inspection, OSHA mandated maintenance, RCRA hazardous waste equipment certification, Clean Air Act emission control maintenance). Triggers enhanced documentation and retention requirements.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The business event date on which the maintenance need was identified and the work order was formally requested. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category. Distinct from created_timestamp (system entry time) — captures the real-world trigger date for SLA and compliance measurement.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Technician-documented root cause of the failure or maintenance need, captured upon work order completion. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis, repeat failure prevention, and regulatory reporting for DOT and OSHA incidents.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the maintenance work is expected to be completed. Used for fleet availability commitments, route planning coordination, and maintenance window management.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for workforce scheduling, parts pre-staging, and fleet availability planning. May differ from actual_start_date if work is rescheduled.']
  source_system_wo_reference varchar [note: 'Native primary key or internal identifier of this work order in the originating system (SAP PM order number or Infor EAM internal WO ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the work order record in the originating system. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit compliance.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair or parts replacement on this work order is eligible for or has been submitted as a warranty claim to the OEM or parts supplier. When True, actual_cost may be partially or fully recovered. Supports warranty recovery tracking and vendor management.']
  wo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable work order number assigned by the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Used for cross-system reference, technician communication, and regulatory documentation such as DOT inspection records.']
  wo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order. Open = created but not started; In_Progress = technician actively working; On_Hold = paused pending approval or resources; Pending_Parts = awaiting parts from inventory; Completed = work finished, pending closure review; Closed = fully closed and costed; Cancelled = voided without execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|on_hold|pending_parts|completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a maintenance work order (WO) for fleet vehicles or facility assets. Captures WO type (preventive/corrective/emergency), priority, status lifecycle (open, in-progress, on-hold, closed), originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM), estimated vs. actual labor hours, estimated vs. actual cost, downtime start/end, failure description, root cause, corrective action taken, safety clearance flags (LOTO), DOT inspection linkage, and regulatory compliance indicators. SSOT for all maintenance execution records.'
}

Business justification: PM schedules are designed FOR emission sources (quarterly flare inspections, monthly blower maintenance, annual LFG system certifications). EPA 40 CFR Part 60 and state permits mandate documented prev']

  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset for which this PM schedule is defined. Links to the asset master record in the asset management domain.']

Business justification: LFG systems require scheduled PM for well field optimization, quarterly flow monitoring, blower maintenance, and condensate removal. Critical for maintaining methane capture rates and EPA NSPS Subpart']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance crew or work center assigned as the default responsible party for executing this PM schedule. Used for capacity planning and work order auto-assignment. Sourced from Infor EAM work center or SAP PM work center.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules are often driven by permit conditions (quarterly leak detection, annual emissions testing, monthly stormwater inspections). PM systems must track permit-driven schedul']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: PM schedules implement regulatory requirements (DOT annual inspections, EPA RCRA container inspections, OSHA equipment certifications). Direct link needed for compliance reporting, demonstrating regul']
  rng_processing_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.rng_processing_unit. Business justification: RNG processing units require scheduled maintenance for membrane replacement, compressor service, gas quality equipment calibration, and siloxane monitoring. Essential for maintaining pipeline injectio']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: PM schedules driven by safety program mandates (OSHA-required inspections, EPA equipment checks, LOTO verification schedules). Waste Management must demonstrate PM compliance with specific safety prog']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: PM schedules for service equipment (compactor maintenance every 90 days, container inspections) are dictated by service contracts. Service offerings define PM frequency, scope, and cost allocation for']
  task_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized task list (checklist of operations) associated with this PM schedule. The task list defines the specific maintenance operations, inspection steps, and parts required. Sourced from Infor EAM task list master or SAP PM task list.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Turbine generators have manufacturer-specified PM schedules for bearing inspections, oil analysis, vibration monitoring, and performance testing. Essential for maximizing generator availability and me']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: PM schedules should reference standardized maintenance types for consistent preventive maintenance classification and planning. Currently has plan_type (STRING) and maintenance_category (STRING) which']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: PM schedules are vehicle-specific (oil changes, brake inspections, DOT annuals). Fleet operations require direct vehicle→PM schedule linkage for compliance tracking and maintenance planning. Asset_id ']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the asset covered by this PM schedule. Determines applicable regulatory inspection requirements (e.g., DOT for fleet vehicles, EPA for landfill equipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fleet_vehicle|facility_equipment|container|transfer_station|landfill_equipment|mrf_equipment — promote to reference product]']
  auto_wo_generation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders are automatically generated by the CMMS (Infor EAM or SAP PM) when the PM due date or meter threshold is reached. When false, a planner must manually release the work order. Supports both automated and manual scheduling workflows.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which maintenance costs for this PM schedule are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by business unit, region, or asset class. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PM schedule record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Populated by the ingestion pipeline. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data model conventions.']
