  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this PM schedule becomes active and begins generating work orders. Allows future-dated PM plans to be configured in advance (e.g., when a new asset is commissioned or a regulatory requirement takes effect).']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the PM task. Used for technician scheduling, crew capacity planning, and asset downtime forecasting. Sourced from the task list standard times in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated labor cost to complete one cycle of this PM task, in US dollars. Calculated from estimated_duration_hours multiplied by the applicable technician labor rate. Used for maintenance budget planning and COGS allocation.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of parts and materials required to complete one cycle of this PM task, in US dollars. Sourced from the Bill of Materials (BOM) associated with the task list. Used for maintenance budget planning and cost forecasting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this PM schedule is no longer active and will not generate new work orders. Applicable for temporary PM plans, asset disposal schedules, or time-limited regulatory inspection programs. Null for open-ended schedules.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing how often the PM task recurs, interpreted in conjunction with frequency_unit. For example, a value of 90 with frequency_unit of \'days\' means every 90 days. For meter-based plans, this is the meter increment (e.g., 5000 miles).']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the frequency_interval. Defines whether the PM cycle is driven by elapsed time (days, weeks, months) or asset utilization meters (miles, engine_hours, operating_hours). Critical for calculating next due date or meter reading.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM or Infor EAM functional location code identifying the physical installation point or system hierarchy node where the asset is installed (e.g., fleet depot bay, landfill pump station). Supports hierarchical maintenance reporting.']
  is_dot_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule represents a federally mandated Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection cycle (e.g., annual inspection per 49 CFR Part 396.17). When true, completion records are subject to DOT recordkeeping requirements and regulatory audit.']
  is_epa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by EPA environmental regulations (e.g., landfill gas (LFG) collection system inspections, leachate system checks, or CNG fueling station inspections). When true, records are subject to EPA reporting requirements.']
  is_osha_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by OSHA regulations (e.g., crane inspections per 29 CFR 1910.179, powered industrial truck inspections per 29 CFR 1910.178). When true, records must be retained per OSHA recordkeeping requirements.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the most recent PM task was completed for this schedule. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due date for time-based plans. Populated from work order completion records in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  last_performed_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hour meter reading recorded at the time the most recent PM was completed. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due meter reading for meter-based plans. Sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics or Infor EAM meter readings.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the next_due_date that a work order should be automatically generated to allow for parts procurement, technician scheduling, and asset availability planning. Ensures PM tasks are not missed due to resource constraints.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated calendar date by which the next PM task must be completed. For time-based plans, derived from last_performed_date plus frequency_interval. Drives work order generation and maintenance scheduling. Critical for DOT compliance tracking.']
  next_due_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated odometer or engine hour meter value at which the next PM task must be performed. For meter-based plans, derived from last_performed_meter_reading plus frequency_interval. Null for time-based plans.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for maintenance planners to record special instructions, regulatory references, seasonal considerations, or asset-specific requirements associated with this PM schedule. Not used for structured data.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the PM schedule plan (e.g., \'Annual DOT Brake Inspection – Class 8 Trucks\', \'Monthly Compactor Hydraulic Service\'). Used for display and reporting.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the PM plan as assigned in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Format: PM- followed by 4-20 alphanumeric characters.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this PM schedule, used for work order queue management and resource allocation when multiple PM tasks compete for the same technician or asset downtime window. Critical priority is reserved for safety-critical and regulatory-mandated inspections.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent revision to this PM schedule was approved and applied. Used in conjunction with revision_number for change history tracking and regulatory audit documentation.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision counter incremented each time the PM schedule definition is modified (e.g., frequency change, task list update, regulatory requirement change). Supports change management and audit trail for compliance purposes.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PM schedule record. Active schedules generate work orders on due dates. Suspended schedules are temporarily paused (e.g., asset out of service). Archived schedules are retained for historical reference.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this PM schedule record was sourced: Infor EAM PM module, SAP PM maintenance plans, or manually entered. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  source_system_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this PM plan record in the originating source system (Infor EAM plan number or SAP PM maintenance plan number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  technician_role varchar [note: 'Skill or trade classification required to perform this PM task. Used for workforce planning and technician qualification matching. Examples include mechanic for engine service, electrician for CNG system checks, or inspector for DOT annual inspections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanic|electrician|hydraulic_tech|tire_tech|inspector|welder|general_tech — promote to reference product]']
  tolerance_days varchar [note: 'Allowable number of days before or after the next_due_date within which the PM task can be completed without being flagged as overdue. Provides scheduling flexibility while maintaining compliance. Negative values indicate early completion tolerance.']
  tolerance_meter_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Allowable meter reading variance (miles or engine hours) above or below the next_due_meter_reading within which the PM task can be completed without being flagged as overdue. Applicable only to meter-based PM plans. Null for time-based plans.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PM schedule record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Updated by the ingestion pipeline on each delta load. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data model conventions.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Type of work order that will be generated when this PM schedule triggers. Aligns with SAP PM order types and Infor EAM work order categories. PM is standard preventive maintenance, inspection is a check-only task, lubrication is a fluid service, calibration is for measurement equipment, and overhaul is a major rebuild.']

  Note: 'Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule master defining recurring maintenance cycles for fleet and facility assets. Stores PM plan type (time-based, meter-based, condition-based), frequency interval (days, miles, engine hours), last performed date/meter reading, next due date/meter reading, estimated duration, assigned crew or technician role, associated task list, DOT inspection cycle flags, and active/inactive status. Sourced from Infor EAM PM module and SAP PM maintenance plans.'
}

Table maintenance.wo_task {
  wo_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for an individual task line within a work order. Serves as the primary key for the wo_task entity in the Infor EAM / SAP PM integrated maintenance execution workflow.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fleet vehicle or facility asset on which this task is performed. Enables asset-level maintenance history, reliability analysis, and lifecycle cost tracking in Infor EAM.']
  lockout_tagout_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.lockout_tagout_procedure. Business justification: Tasks requiring energy isolation must reference specific LOTO procedures per OSHA 1910.147. Real business process: technicians must follow documented LOTO procedures for equipment servicing. Critical ']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that generated this task, if applicable. Links corrective and preventive tasks to their originating maintenance plan for PM compliance tracking and schedule adherence reporting.']
  primary_wo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce employee record of the technician assigned to execute this task. Used for labor tracking, productivity analysis, and certification compliance verification via Workday HCM integration.']
  primary_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order header that this task belongs to. Establishes the header-detail relationship between the work order and its constituent task steps.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center or shop (e.g., Fleet Garage Bay 3, Transfer Station Workshop) where this task is to be performed. Used for capacity planning, resource allocation, and facility utilization reporting.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician marked this task as complete. Triggers downstream WO completion logic and asset return-to-service workflows. Critical for downtime duration and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) calculations.']
  actual_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost incurred for this task based on actual hours worked and the technician\'s labor rate. Feeds into work order total cost and asset maintenance cost reporting in SAP FI/CO.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours recorded by the technician upon task completion. Compared against estimated hours for variance analysis, technician productivity measurement, and future labor estimate refinement.']
  actual_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual cost of parts and materials consumed during task execution, recorded upon parts issuance from inventory. Used for work order cost variance analysis and asset lifecycle cost tracking.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician actually began executing this task. Captured via CMMS mobile app or time-clock entry. Used for schedule adherence analysis and downtime duration calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this task record was first created in the CMMS system, either by the work order planner or automatically generated from a PM schedule. Supports audit trail requirements and SOX financial controls for maintenance cost records.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any defect, fault, or abnormal condition discovered during task execution. Populated by the technician and used to initiate corrective work orders, parts procurement, and safety incident reporting.']
  defect_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a defect, fault, or out-of-tolerance condition was identified during execution of this task. Triggers corrective action workflow and defect documentation in Infor EAM and Enviance EHS.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task constitutes or contributes to a federally mandated Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection under 49 CFR Part 396. Triggers regulatory documentation requirements and inspection record retention obligations.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes the asset was taken out of service specifically for this task. Contributes to overall work order downtime and feeds fleet availability and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) calculations. Distinct from labor hours as it includes wait time for parts or approvals.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned labor cost for this task calculated from estimated labor hours multiplied by the technician\'s standard labor rate. Used for work order cost budgeting and maintenance financial planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned labor hours required to complete this task, derived from the standard task template or planner estimate. Used for work order scheduling, technician capacity planning, and maintenance cost budgeting.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned cost of parts and materials required for this task, derived from the Bill of Materials (BOM) and current inventory pricing. Used for work order cost estimation and maintenance budget planning.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of an inspection task, indicating whether the inspected component passed, failed, or passed with conditions. Required for DOT inspection records and regulatory compliance reporting. Null for non-inspection task types.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this task record was most recently modified, including status changes, labor hour updates, or technician note additions. Supports data lineage tracking and change audit requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required before performing this task, per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147. Enforces hazardous energy control compliance for tasks involving electrical, hydraulic, or pneumatic systems.']
  meter_reading_at_task decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hours reading recorded at the time this task was performed. Critical for mileage-based and hour-based PM interval tracking, DOT inspection records, and asset lifecycle management in Infor EAM.']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the meter reading recorded at task execution (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for engine hour meter). Required to correctly interpret meter_reading_at_task for PM interval calculations.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task requires one or more parts or materials from inventory to be completed. Drives parts reservation workflow in Infor EAM and SAP MM materials management integration.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) items for this task (e.g., \'safety_glasses,gloves,steel_toe_boots,hearing_protection\'). Sourced from the EHS system and enforced per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 standards.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this task, which may differ from the parent work order priority if specific steps are more time-critical (e.g., a safety-critical brake inspection step within a broader PM work order). Drives technician scheduling and dispatch sequencing.']
  required_certification varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or industry certification required to perform this task (e.g., CDL for vehicle brake inspection, ASE for engine diagnostics, DOT_INSPECTOR for federal vehicle inspection). Enforces compliance with DOT 49 CFR Part 396 and OSHA standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDL|ASE|DOT_INSPECTOR|HAZMAT|OSHA_30|FORKLIFT|NONE|EPA_608|RCRA_HAZWASTE — promote to reference product]']
  required_skill_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the minimum skill or trade qualification required to perform this task (e.g., \'MECH-DIESEL\', \'ELEC-HV\', \'HVAC-CERT\'). Used for technician assignment validation and workforce capability gap analysis.']
  safety_instructions varchar [note: 'Mandatory safety procedures, lockout/tagout (LOTO) requirements, Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) specifications, and hazard warnings that must be followed before and during task execution. Sourced from EHS system and linked to OSHA compliance requirements.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the technician is scheduled to begin this task. Used for maintenance scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime window management.']
  skip_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a task was skipped or bypassed in the work order execution. Required when task_status is \'skipped\' to maintain audit trail integrity and support PM compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_applicable|deferred|parts_unavailable|asset_unavailable|safety_hold|supervisor_override|weather|other — promote to reference product]']
  standard_task_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this task to a standardized maintenance task template in the CMMS library (e.g., \'HYD-INSP-001\' for hydraulic inspection). Enables consistent task definition, labor estimation benchmarking, and cross-fleet maintenance analytics.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task to be performed, including the specific action, component, and expected outcome (e.g., \'Inspect and replace hydraulic hose on compactor lift arm\'). Sourced from standard task library or entered by planner.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the task, typically formatted as the parent WO number suffixed with a task sequence (e.g., WO-2024-00123-T01). Used in technician communications, printed work packets, and audit trails.']
  task_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this task within the parent work order, defining the execution sequence of multi-step maintenance procedures (e.g., 10, 20, 30 in SAP PM operation numbering convention). Enables dependency management and step-by-step workflow tracking.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance task within the work order execution workflow. Drives technician dashboard visibility, WO completion logic, and downtime reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|complete|skipped|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance task by the nature of the work performed. Used for labor planning, parts forecasting, and maintenance cost categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection|repair|replacement|lubrication|calibration|cleaning|adjustment|testing|documentation — promote to reference product]']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the assigned technician to record observations, findings, deviations from standard procedure, or recommendations during task execution. Captured via Infor EAM mobile app and retained for maintenance history and knowledge management.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supervisor or quality inspector verified and approved the completed task. Establishes the regulatory sign-off timestamp for DOT inspection records and OSHA compliance documentation.']

  Note: 'Individual task or operation line within a work order, representing a discrete step in the maintenance execution workflow. Captures task sequence number, task description, standard task code, estimated labor hours, actual labor hours, assigned technician, task status (pending, in-progress, complete, skipped), required skill/certification (e.g., CDL, ASE), safety instructions, and completion timestamp. Enables granular tracking of multi-step maintenance procedures and technician productivity.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_usage {
  parts_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parts usage transaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional line entity.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Parts consumption for boiler repairs must be tracked at the unit level for maintenance cost allocation, spare parts inventory optimization, and identifying high-wear components requiring design improv']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance technician or mechanic who requested or received the parts issuance. Supports labor-parts correlation and technician productivity analysis.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset for which the parts were consumed. Enables asset-level parts cost tracking and lifecycle analysis. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.']
  generated_waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Hazardous parts replacement transactions must reference waste profiles for disposal tracking, manifest preparation, and cost allocation. Critical for tracking waste generation at the transaction level']
  ghg_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_emission. Business justification: Specific parts (refrigerants, hydraulic fluids, solvents) have direct GHG impact when installed or disposed. EPA Section 608 requires tracking refrigerant quantities by work order. Hazmat parts flagge']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Parts consumed during customer-billable repairs (container parts, compactor components) must map to specific invoice line items. Enables parts cost pass-through billing and reconciliation between main']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Parts used in landfill equipment maintenance must be tracked by site for environmental compliance reporting, cost allocation to specific landfill operations, and regulatory audits. Landfill sites have']
  parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parts catalog master record identifying the specific part or material consumed. Links to Bill of Materials (BOM) for traceability and reorder analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase Order number associated with the procurement of this part if it was a direct purchase rather than issued from storeroom stock. Links parts usage to procurement spend for total cost of ownership analysis.']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Parts consumed during service-related maintenance (container parts, compactor components) are tracked against service offerings for cost recovery, warranty claims, and profitability analysis. Real WM ']
  storeroom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing warehouse or storeroom from which the part was physically dispensed. Supports inventory location tracking and storeroom replenishment analysis.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator parts usage tracking enables unit-level cost analysis, critical spares planning, and warranty parts tracking for OEM reimbursement claims on defective components.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Parts usage tracks component consumption by vehicle for cost allocation and inventory planning. Fleet financial analysts need vehicle→parts usage linkage for TCO analysis and parts demand forecasting.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order against which parts were issued. Links parts consumption to the specific maintenance event (corrective or preventive). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset category for which parts were consumed. Distinguishes fleet vehicles (trucks, loaders) from facility assets (compactors, conveyors) and containers for parts spend segmentation.']
  bom_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) component line from which this parts usage was derived. Enables traceability from actual consumption back to the planned maintenance BOM for variance analysis.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center to which the parts cost is allocated. Enables departmental cost tracking, budget variance analysis, and financial reporting by organizational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts usage record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international or cross-border maintenance activities.']
  dot_inspection_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parts usage was associated with a DOT-required vehicle inspection or compliance repair. Supports regulatory reporting for DOT out-of-service violations and fleet compliance audits.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code used to post the parts cost in SAP FI. Determines whether the expense is capitalized (asset improvement) or expensed (maintenance COGS) per accounting policy.']
  goods_issue_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM goods issue document number generated at the time of physical parts issuance from inventory. Provides cross-system traceability between Infor EAM work order and SAP MM inventory movement.']
  hazmat_part_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part or material is classified as hazardous (e.g., certain lubricants, solvents, refrigerants). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal requirements per EPA and OSHA regulations.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the parts were physically issued from the storeroom to the work order. Used for inventory aging, consumption trend analysis, and maintenance scheduling alignment. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT date dimension.']
  issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parts issuance transaction. Tracks progression from initial request through reservation, physical issuance, and potential return. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT lifecycle state.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the parts were physically issued from the storeroom. Enables time-of-day analysis for storeroom operations and supports audit trail requirements. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number ordering this parts usage record within the parent work order. Enables multi-part work orders to be reconstructed in original sequence. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or parts manufacturer. Used for vendor performance analysis, warranty tracking, and preferred supplier compliance.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the manufacturer. Enables cross-referencing between internal catalog numbers and OEM catalogs for procurement and warranty claims.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this parts usage record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician or storeroom clerk notes regarding the parts issuance (e.g., substitution reason, special handling instructions, warranty claim details). Supports operational context and audit documentation.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the part type used for spend analysis and inventory segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|pneumatic|body|consumable|lubricant|tire|safety|other — promote to reference product]']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the part or material consumed (e.g., \'Air Filter - Heavy Duty Diesel\', \'Hydraulic Pump Seal Kit\'). Supports reporting and technician communication.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or internal catalog part number uniquely identifying the part or material. Used for procurement, cross-referencing with vendor catalogs, and BOM traceability.']
  quantity_issued decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual number of units physically dispensed from the storeroom to the work order. May differ from quantity requested due to partial availability or over-issuance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units of the part originally requested by the technician or planner for the work order. Compared against quantity issued to identify fulfillment gaps and partial issuances.']
  quantity_returned decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units returned to the storeroom after work order completion because they were not consumed. Used to calculate net consumption and adjust inventory balances.']
  reorder_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parts issuance triggered an automatic reorder request because inventory fell below the minimum reorder point. Supports proactive inventory replenishment and prevents stockout-driven maintenance delays.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Date on which the parts were originally requested or reserved for the work order. Compared against issue date to measure storeroom fulfillment lead time and parts availability performance.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known reservation or pick ticket number generated when parts are reserved against a work order prior to physical issuance. Enables pre-staging and kitting of parts for planned maintenance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date on which unused parts were returned to the storeroom after work order completion. Null if no parts were returned. Used to calculate net consumption and storeroom restocking timelines.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this parts usage record originated. Infor EAM for CMMS-driven issuances, SAP MM for goods issue transactions, or manual for paper-based entries. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Native transaction identifier from the originating source system (Infor EAM parts issuance ID or SAP MM material document number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of parts issued calculated as quantity issued minus quantity returned multiplied by unit cost. Represents the net parts spend charged to the work order for COGS allocation and maintenance budget tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT monetary category.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parts movement: issue (parts dispensed to work order), return (unused parts returned to storeroom), transfer (moved between storerooms), or adjustment (inventory correction). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT discriminator.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of the part at the time of issuance, sourced from SAP MM material valuation. Used for COGS allocation and maintenance cost reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the part quantity (e.g., EA=Each, QT=Quart, GL=Gallon, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram, FT=Feet, L=Liter). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure for inventory valuation consistency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|QT|GL|LB|KG|FT|M|L|BOX|SET|KIT|PR — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parts usage is associated with a warranty claim against the OEM or supplier. Enables warranty cost recovery tracking and vendor chargeback processing.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the parent work order type indicating whether parts were consumed for planned preventive maintenance (PM), unplanned corrective repair, scheduled inspection, major overhaul, or emergency breakdown response.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of parts and materials consumed against a work order. Captures part number, part description, quantity requested, quantity issued, unit of measure, unit cost, total cost, issuing warehouse/storeroom, reservation number, actual issue date, and return quantity. Links to BOM and parts catalog for traceability. Supports COGS allocation, parts consumption analysis, and reorder triggering. Sourced from Infor EAM parts issuance and SAP MM goods issue.'
}

Table maintenance.repair_history {
  repair_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each closed/archived repair or maintenance event record in the repair history. Primary key for this data product.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Boiler repair history must be tracked at the unit level for reliability analysis, MTBF calculations, warranty claim documentation, and regulatory compliance reporting to state boiler inspectors.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Repair history on emission sources (LFG wells, flares, compressors) is required for EPA annual compliance reports and state air permit renewals. Regulators audit repair frequency, downtime duration, a']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary technician or mechanic who performed and closed the repair. Used for workforce productivity analysis and skill-based routing of future work orders.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility, shop, or service location where the repair was performed (e.g., fleet maintenance yard, landfill workshop, transfer station). Enables geographic and facility-level maintenance cost and performance analysis.']
  failure_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Repair history records should link to standardized failure mode catalog for consistent failure tracking and trend analysis. Currently has failure_mode (STRING) and failure_code (STRING) which duplicat']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle, facility equipment, or infrastructure asset on which the repair or maintenance event was performed. Enables longitudinal asset health and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Repair history for landfill equipment must be maintained by site for regulatory inspections, environmental compliance audits, and demonstrating proper maintenance of pollution control equipment. Requi']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or maintenance plan that triggered this work order, if applicable. Populated for planned/preventive repairs; null for corrective or emergency repairs. Enables PM compliance rate analysis.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator repair history is essential for performance trending, warranty management, major overhaul planning, and demonstrating equipment reliability to utility offtake partners and insurance underwri']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Repair history tracks vehicle-specific failure patterns, warranty claims, and cost trends. Fleet managers analyze repair history by vehicle for replacement decisions and reliability analysis. Asset_id']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Work Order (WO) that was executed and subsequently closed to produce this repair history record. Links the archived record back to the active WO lifecycle.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known asset tag or equipment number (e.g., fleet unit number, facility equipment tag) as assigned in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Enables direct lookup without requiring a join to the asset master.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Work Order (WO) was formally closed and archived in the CMMS, creating this repair history record. May differ from completion_timestamp if administrative review or cost settlement occurs after physical completion.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair or maintenance event was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual repair duration and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR). Distinct from the WO close date which may occur later during administrative closure.']
  component_repaired varchar [note: 'Name or description of the specific component, sub-assembly, or system on the asset that was repaired or replaced (e.g., rear axle, hydraulic pump, compactor blade, engine air filter, brake caliper). Supports Bill of Materials (BOM) analysis and component-level MTBF tracking.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) cost center code to which the repair costs are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by business unit, region, or operational division for EBITDA and COGS analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this repair history record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  dot_inspection_number varchar [note: 'Official DOT inspection report number assigned by the inspecting authority for DOT-mandated inspections. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and FMCSA audit response. Populated only when is_dot_inspection is True.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was out of service (unavailable for operations) due to this repair event, from the time it was taken out of service to the time it was returned to active duty. Key input for fleet availability rate and operational impact assessment.']
  engine_hours_at_repair decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine hour meter reading recorded at the time of repair for hour-metered assets (compactors, generators, heavy equipment, Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plant equipment). Used for hour-based PM interval tracking and MTBF analysis where mileage is not applicable.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) account code used to post the repair costs in the financial system. Distinguishes between capital expenditure (CapEx) and operating expenditure (OpEx) maintenance costs for GAAP/IFRS financial reporting.']
  is_dot_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repair event was associated with or triggered by a Department of Transportation (DOT) mandated vehicle inspection (e.g., annual inspection per 49 CFR Part 396.17, roadside inspection, or out-of-service order). Critical for DOT compliance audit trail.']
  is_warranty_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair costs are recoverable under an OEM or vendor warranty. When True, triggers the warranty recovery process and cost reclassification from maintenance expense to warranty receivable.']
  labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total labor cost in US dollars for this repair event, calculated from technician hours and applicable labor rates. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis. Confidential as it reflects internal labor rate structures.']
  labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total actual labor hours expended by all technicians on this repair event, as recorded in the CMMS time entry. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation, technician productivity analysis, and labor cost allocation.']
  odometer_at_repair varchar [note: 'Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time of repair for wheeled fleet assets (trucks, ASL, FEL, REL vehicles). Used to calculate mileage-based Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), establish PM trigger intervals, and support DOT inspection compliance records.']
  parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost in US dollars of all parts and materials consumed during this repair event, sourced from the Bill of Materials (BOM) and parts inventory. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the work order at the time of execution, indicating urgency and impact on fleet availability or regulatory compliance (e.g., critical for DOT out-of-service violations, routine for scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM)).']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP Purchase Order (PO) number associated with external parts procurement or subcontractor services for this repair. Links the maintenance record to the Accounts Payable (AP) process for cost reconciliation.']
  repair_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the work performed, findings, corrective actions taken, and any relevant observations by the technician. Provides the detailed maintenance record for audit, warranty claims, and future diagnostic reference.']
  repair_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the repair history record. Completed indicates work was finished and verified; closed indicates the WO was formally archived; cancelled indicates the WO was voided; deferred indicates work was postponed and re-scheduled.']
  repair_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance event: corrective (reactive repair after failure), preventive (scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM) cycle), predictive (condition-based), emergency (unplanned critical failure), or inspection (DOT/regulatory inspection). Drives MTBF and MTTR segmentation.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date the failure, defect, or maintenance need was first reported or identified, either by a driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR), operator, or automated telematics alert. Establishes the start of the repair lifecycle for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Technician or engineer analysis of the underlying root cause of the failure or maintenance need (e.g., deferred maintenance, operator misuse, manufacturing defect, environmental exposure). Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement programs.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date the maintenance or repair event was scheduled to be performed, as planned in the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or work order dispatch. Used to measure schedule adherence and PM compliance rate.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this repair history record was sourced (Infor EAM for asset-centric maintenance, SAP PM for work order execution, or Manual for paper-based records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician physically began work on the asset. Used as the primary business event timestamp for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation and technician utilization analysis.']
  subcontractor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cost in US dollars paid to external vendors or subcontractors for work performed on this repair event (e.g., specialized engine rebuilds, body shop work, OEM warranty repairs). Tracked separately from internal labor cost for make-vs-buy analysis.']
  supervisor_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the maintenance supervisor or fleet manager who reviewed and approved the completed repair before work order closure. Required for DOT annual inspection certification and internal quality control.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary technician who performed the repair, as recorded at the time of work order closure. Retained in the historical record for audit and accountability purposes even if the technician record changes. Classified confidential as it is employee data.']
  total_repair_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost in US dollars for the complete repair event, encompassing labor, parts, subcontractor, and any other direct costs. Primary financial metric for asset lifecycle cost analysis, EBITDA impact assessment, and repair vs. replace decision support.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this repair history record in the source system. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail requirements.']
  vmrs_system_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards (VMRS) system code identifying the major vehicle system affected by the repair (e.g., 011 = Air Brakes, 013 = Cooling System, 032 = Engine). Industry-standard classification for fleet maintenance benchmarking and DOT compliance reporting.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'OEM or vendor-assigned warranty claim reference number for repairs covered under warranty. Populated only when is_warranty_claim is True. Used to track warranty recovery status and receivable amounts.']
  wo_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known Work Order (WO) number assigned by the CMMS (Infor EAM or SAP PM) at the time the work order was created. Serves as the primary business identifier for cross-system traceability and audit.']

  Note: 'Historical record of completed maintenance and repair events for each asset, providing a longitudinal view of asset health and reliability. Captures asset identifier, WO reference, repair type, failure mode, failure code, component repaired, repair description, labor hours, parts cost, total repair cost, technician, completion date, and mileage/hours at repair. Enables MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time To Repair), and lifecycle cost analysis. Distinct from the active work_order as it represents the closed/archived maintenance record.'
}

Table maintenance.downtime_event {
  downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each discrete asset downtime event record in the maintenance domain.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Boiler failures cause operational downtime events that must be tracked at the unit level for availability factor calculations, lost generation reporting, and power purchase agreement performance metri']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.cell. Business justification: Equipment downtime in specific landfill cells affects airspace consumption rates, daily cover operations, and cell-specific operational planning. Operations track which cell was impacted to adjust dai']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility, depot, or garage where the asset is being serviced during the downtime event.']
  failure_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Downtime events are caused by specific failure modes. Currently has failure_mode (STRING) and failure_cause (STRING) which should reference the standardized failure_mode catalog for consistent root ca']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset that experienced the downtime event. Links to the asset master record in the asset management domain.']
  ghg_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_emission. Business justification: Emission control equipment downtime (flare outages, LFG system failures) triggers quantifiable fugitive methane emissions that must be calculated and reported to EPA under GHGRP. Each downtime event g']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Equipment downtime events caused by or resulting in safety incidents. Real business need: incident investigation includes equipment failure analysis; downtime cost attribution to incidents for workers']
  near_miss_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.near_miss. Business justification: Downtime events may be preceded by near-miss incidents (equipment malfunction almost causing injury). Real business process: leading indicator tracking for predictive safety management. Waste Manageme']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that triggered this planned downtime event. Populated only for planned_pm downtime category. Links to the PM schedule master in the maintenance domain.']
  primary_downtime_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance technician or mechanic assigned to resolve the downtime event. Links to the workforce/employee record.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route that was impacted or cancelled due to this downtime event. Used to assess operational impact on service delivery.']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Service equipment downtime (compactor failure, container damage) is tracked against service offerings for SLA compliance, customer credits, and service quality metrics. Real WM operations require link']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator downtime directly impacts energy production revenue and must be tracked at the unit level for capacity factor reporting, utility settlement reconciliation, and maintenance effectiveness anal']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Downtime events track vehicle unavailability impacting route operations. Fleet dispatchers need vehicle→downtime linkage for replacement vehicle assignment and utilization reporting. Asset_id exists b']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance Work Order (WO) associated with this downtime event, whether corrective or preventive. Links to the work order record in the maintenance domain.']
  asset_identifier varchar [note: 'Human-readable asset tag, vehicle unit number, or equipment identifier as registered in the fleet management system (e.g., truck unit number, equipment tag). Used for operational cross-referencing with Geotab telematics and AMCS dispatch.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset that experienced downtime: collection_vehicle (residential/commercial waste collection trucks including ASL, FEL, REL), transfer_truck (long-haul transfer vehicles), support_vehicle (non-collection fleet), facility_equipment (MRF, landfill, or WTE facility machinery), container (roll-off or compactor containers).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this downtime event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  dot_inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the DOT inspection was completed for this downtime event. Populated only when is_dot_inspection_required is True. Required for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  dot_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the DOT inspection conducted during or following the downtime event: passed (vehicle cleared for service), failed (vehicle requires additional repairs before service), conditional_pass (vehicle approved with restrictions), not_applicable (no DOT inspection required).']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the downtime event type: planned_pm (scheduled Preventive Maintenance cycle), unplanned_breakdown (unexpected mechanical or operational failure), awaiting_parts (asset idle pending parts availability from BOM/inventory), awaiting_technician (asset idle pending technician assignment), dot_hold (asset placed out-of-service pending Department of Transportation (DOT) inspection or compliance resolution).']
  downtime_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours that the asset was unavailable for service, calculated as the difference between downtime_end_timestamp and downtime_start_timestamp. Populated upon event closure. Critical for fleet availability KPI and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) calculations.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the asset was returned to service and the downtime event was closed. Null if the asset is still unavailable.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the asset became unavailable for service, marking the beginning of the downtime event. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculations and KPI reporting.']
  downtime_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime event: open (event recorded, work not yet started), in_progress (maintenance actively underway), resolved (repair complete, asset returned to service), closed (event fully documented and closed in CMMS), cancelled (event voided, e.g., duplicate entry).']
  engine_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Total engine operating hours recorded at the time the downtime event began. Used for hour-based PM scheduling and asset lifecycle management, particularly for heavy equipment and facility machinery where engine hours are the primary maintenance interval metric.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the downtime event, formatted as DT-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Used for cross-system reference, reporting, and communication with operations teams.']
  is_dot_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Department of Transportation (DOT) inspection is required before the asset can be returned to service. True when downtime_category is dot_hold or when a safety-critical failure is identified. Supports DOT FMCSR compliance tracking.']
  is_repeat_failure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event represents a recurrence of the same failure mode on the same asset within a defined lookback period (typically 90 days). True signals a chronic reliability issue requiring escalated investigation or asset replacement consideration.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost in USD incurred for technician time associated with this downtime event, including regular and overtime rates. Sourced from Infor EAM labor records and Workday HCM payroll rates.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the asset location at the time of the downtime event. Sourced from Geotab telematics. Used for geographic analysis of breakdown hotspots and dispatch optimization.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geographic location where the asset breakdown or failure occurred (e.g., route name, street address, facility name, depot). Relevant for unplanned breakdowns occurring in the field.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the asset location at the time of the downtime event. Sourced from Geotab telematics. Used for geographic analysis of breakdown hotspots and dispatch optimization.']
  lost_revenue_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue in USD lost due to service disruptions caused by this downtime event, based on affected stops and applicable service rates. Used for operational impact financial assessment and ROI analysis of maintenance investments.']
  odometer_reading_miles decimal(10,1) [note: 'Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time the downtime event began. Used for mileage-based maintenance interval tracking and asset lifecycle analysis. Sourced from Geotab telematics or driver log.']
  operational_impact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational impact caused by the downtime event on waste collection or facility operations: route_cancellation (entire route not serviced), route_delay (route completed late), service_disruption (partial or degraded service), partial_service (some stops serviced by alternate vehicle), no_impact (downtime absorbed without service effect).']
  parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost in USD of parts and materials consumed during the repair or maintenance activity associated with this downtime event. Sourced from Infor EAM parts inventory and SAP MM purchase orders.']
  parts_wait_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was idle specifically waiting for parts or materials to become available from inventory or procurement. Supports parts inventory optimization and supply chain analysis.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the downtime event to guide maintenance resource allocation: critical (immediate safety or regulatory risk, asset must be repaired within hours), high (significant operational impact, same-day resolution required), medium (moderate impact, next-day resolution), low (minimal impact, scheduled resolution acceptable).']
  repair_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual hands-on repair or maintenance labor time in hours, excluding waiting time for parts or technician. Used to calculate Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) and labor efficiency metrics.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the downtime event was first reported to the maintenance team or entered into the CMMS system. May differ from downtime_start_timestamp if there was a reporting lag.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text technician notes describing the repair actions taken, parts replaced, and any recommendations for follow-up maintenance or monitoring. Captured in Infor EAM upon work order completion.']
  response_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in hours between the reported_timestamp and the time a technician began active work on the asset. Key KPI for maintenance responsiveness and SLA compliance measurement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this downtime event record originated: infor_eam (Infor EAM CMMS), sap_pm (SAP Plant Maintenance module), geotab (Geotab telematics automated alert), amcs (AMCS Platform dispatch-triggered event), manual (manually entered by dispatcher or supervisor).']
  stops_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer service stops that were not completed or were disrupted as a direct result of this downtime event. Used to quantify customer impact and SLA compliance reporting.']
  total_downtime_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total financial cost in USD attributable to this downtime event, encompassing labor cost, parts cost, and any additional costs such as rental equipment or contractor fees. Used for cost of downtime analysis and EBITDA impact assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this downtime event record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, resolution notes, cost fields, or inspection results throughout the event lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Captures discrete asset downtime events resulting from maintenance activities or unplanned failures. Records asset identifier, downtime start timestamp, downtime end timestamp, total downtime duration (hours), downtime category (planned PM, unplanned breakdown, awaiting parts, awaiting technician, DOT hold), impact on operations (route cancellation, service disruption), associated work order, and cost of downtime. Critical for fleet availability KPI tracking and operational impact assessment.'
}

Table maintenance.technician_assignment {
  technician_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each technician assignment record linking a maintenance technician to a specific work order and task in the Infor EAM / SAP PM system.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Technician work assignments for boiler maintenance must specify the unit for labor tracking, skill matching with boiler operator certifications, and calculating unit-level labor costs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility, garage, or service location where the technician assignment is being executed. Used for geographic labor distribution analysis and facility capacity planning.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset on which maintenance work is being performed under this assignment. Enables technician-to-asset utilization analysis and asset maintenance history by technician.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance crew or team to which this technician belongs for this assignment. Supports crew-level scheduling, multi-technician WO coordination, and crew productivity analysis.']
  primary_technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance technician\'s employee record in Workday HCM. Used for labor cost allocation, utilization tracking, and payroll integration. Distinct from the workforce employee master — this domain owns the maintenance-specific assignment record.']
  required_hazwoper_training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazwoper_training. Business justification: Technicians assigned to work involving hazardous materials must have current HAZWOPER certification verified before assignment. Mandatory for worker safety, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.120 compliance, and qualif']
  safety_training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_training_record. Business justification: Technicians must have required safety certifications for assigned work (LOTO, confined space, hazmat). Real business process: competency verification before hazardous work assignment. OSHA requires do']
  tertiary_technician_confirmed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or authorized personnel who confirmed and approved the labor hours for this assignment in SAP PM or Infor EAM. Supports approval audit trail and SOX compliance for labor cost postings.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator maintenance assignments require unit-level tracking for certified technician requirements, labor costing, and ensuring qualified personnel perform warranty-covered work per OEM specification']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Technician assignments track labor hours by vehicle for cost allocation and productivity analysis. Fleet maintenance managers need vehicle→labor linkage for shop efficiency reporting. Asset_id exists ']
  wo_task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific maintenance task or operation within the work order to which this technician is assigned. Supports multi-task WO decomposition and individual task-level labor tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent maintenance work order (WO) to which this technician is assigned. Establishes the primary header relationship for labor cost allocation and WO execution tracking.']
  actual_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the technician completed work on the assigned task, as confirmed in Infor EAM. Used to calculate actual labor hours, measure WO cycle time, and support payroll confirmation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of regular labor hours logged by the technician for this assignment, as confirmed in Infor EAM or SAP PM time confirmation. Used for labor cost allocation, technician utilization reporting, and payroll processing.']
  actual_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the technician began work on the assigned task, as recorded in Infor EAM or via mobile confirmation. Compared against scheduled start to measure schedule adherence and response time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Category of the asset being maintained: vehicle (fleet trucks including ASL, FEL, REL), equipment (non-vehicle machinery), facility (building or infrastructure), container (waste containers), or other. Denormalized to support assignment-level filtering without asset master join.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician or supervisor notes specific to this assignment, including observations, deviations from standard procedure, safety concerns, or follow-up actions required. Captured in Infor EAM mobile or SAP PM confirmation.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this technician assignment record, used in Infor EAM and SAP PM for cross-system reference, labor confirmation, and audit trail. Format: \'ASGN-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN\'.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the technician assignment: scheduled (planned but not started), in_progress (technician actively working), completed (labor confirmed and closed), cancelled (assignment voided), or on_hold (paused pending parts or approval).']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the technician\'s role on the work order: primary (lead technician responsible for execution), secondary (supporting technician), supervisor (oversight role), trainee (under supervision), or contractor (external labor). Drives crew scheduling and cost rate selection.']
  certification_used varchar [note: 'Specific certification or license applied by the technician for this assignment (e.g., CDL, DOT Inspector, RCRA Hazardous Waste Handler, ASE Certification). Ensures regulatory compliance and documents that qualified personnel performed the work. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDL|DOT_INSPECTOR|RCRA_HANDLER|ASE|OSHA_30|HAZWOPER|CDL_HAZMAT — promote to reference product]']
  completion_code varchar [note: 'Standardized outcome code recorded at assignment closure: completed_normal (fully executed as planned), completed_partial (partial work done, follow-up required), deferred (rescheduled), cancelled_no_fault (cancelled without technician fault), or cancelled_parts (cancelled due to parts unavailability).']
  confirmed_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the labor hours for this assignment were formally confirmed and approved in SAP PM or Infor EAM. Used for payroll cutoff processing, financial period allocation, and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which the labor cost for this technician assignment is allocated. Enables maintenance cost reporting by organizational unit, fleet division, or facility for EBITDA and COGS analysis.']
  craft_code varchar [note: 'Trade or craft classification code identifying the technician\'s skill category for this assignment (e.g., MECH for mechanic, ELEC for electrician, WELD for welder, HVAC for HVAC technician). Used in Infor EAM for labor planning and rate determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MECH|ELEC|WELD|HVAC|LUBE|DIAG|BODY|TIRE|INSP — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this technician assignment record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA response time measurement. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technician assignment involves a DOT-required vehicle inspection (True) or is a standard maintenance task (False). Ensures DOT-qualified inspectors are assigned and supports regulatory compliance documentation under 49 CFR Part 396.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of hours the asset was out of service (downtime) attributable to this technician assignment. Used to calculate asset availability, Mean Time to Repair (MTTR), and fleet operational readiness metrics.']
  hazmat_work_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment involves work on hazardous waste handling equipment or requires HAZMAT-certified personnel (True). Triggers PPE requirements, RCRA compliance documentation, and OSHA HAZWOPER certification verification.']
  labor_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'SAP PM or Infor EAM labor confirmation document number generated when the technician\'s actual hours are posted and confirmed against the work order. Serves as the audit reference linking this assignment to the financial posting in SAP FI/CO.']
  labor_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician\'s actual labor hours have been formally confirmed and posted in SAP PM or Infor EAM (True), triggering financial posting to the work order cost object. False indicates hours are still pending confirmation.']
  labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total labor cost charged to the work order for this technician assignment, calculated from actual labor hours and the technician\'s applicable labor rate. Used for maintenance cost allocation, COGS reporting, and asset lifecycle cost tracking.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to this technician assignment for cost calculation, sourced from the technician\'s craft/trade rate in SAP PM or Infor EAM. May reflect standard, overtime, or contractor rate depending on assignment type.']
  overtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours logged by the technician for this assignment beyond the standard shift, as tracked per FLSA and company policy. Drives overtime cost calculation and labor budget variance reporting.']
  ppe_required varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of required PPE items for this technician assignment (e.g., \'hard_hat,safety_vest,gloves,respirator\'). Derived from the task hazard assessment and OSHA requirements. Supports safety compliance verification and incident prevention.']
  scheduled_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time the technician is expected to complete work on the assigned task. Used for shift planning, resource availability, and work order duration estimation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  scheduled_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned number of labor hours allocated to this technician for the assigned task, as estimated during work order planning. Used for capacity planning, cost estimation, and variance analysis against actual hours.']
  scheduled_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time the technician is scheduled to begin work on the assigned task. Used for crew scheduling, resource capacity planning, and SLA compliance monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Work shift during which this technician assignment is scheduled or executed: day, evening, night, weekend, or on_call. Used for shift differential pay calculation, labor scheduling, and 24/7 maintenance coverage analysis.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Proficiency level of the technician for the craft applied in this assignment: apprentice (entry-level under supervision), journeyman (qualified independent), senior (experienced), master (highest craft level), or specialist (domain-specific expert). Used for crew composition validation and training gap analysis.']
  travel_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours the technician spent traveling to and from the maintenance site for this assignment. Captured separately from labor hours for accurate cost allocation, technician utilization analysis, and field service efficiency reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this technician assignment record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  work_order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the parent work order, denormalized to the assignment level to support technician dispatch sequencing and scheduling decisions: emergency (immediate response), urgent (same-day), high (next available), normal (scheduled), or low (deferred).']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the parent work order type: preventive (scheduled PM cycle), corrective (reactive repair), emergency (unplanned critical failure), inspection (DOT or regulatory inspection), or project (capital improvement). Denormalized here to support assignment-level analytics without joining to the WO header.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking maintenance technicians to work orders and tasks, capturing the labor execution record. Stores technician employee reference, work order reference, assigned task, assignment type (primary, secondary, supervisor), scheduled start/end datetime, actual start/end datetime, labor hours logged, overtime hours, skill/certification used, and assignment status. Enables labor cost allocation, technician utilization tracking, and crew scheduling. Distinct from workforce employee master — this domain owns the maintenance-specific assignment record.'
}

Table maintenance.maintenance_dot_inspection {
  maintenance_dot_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each DOT vehicle inspection record in the Waste Management fleet compliance system. Primary key for this entity.']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_area. Business justification: DOT inspections are jurisdiction-specific; service areas define regulatory requirements, permit numbers, and LEA agencies. Real WM operations track inspections by service area for compliance reporting']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee identifier for the CDL driver associated with this inspection. Used to cross-reference with Workday HCM driver records, Hours of Service logs, and AMCS dispatch assignments.']
  fleet_fuel_consumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.fleet_fuel_consumption. Business justification: DOT inspections verify emissions control systems (DPF, DEF, catalytic converters). Failed emissions components directly impact fuel efficiency and GHG emissions. Linking inspection results to fuel con']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.franchise_agreement. Business justification: DOT inspections are regulatory compliance requirements often mandated by municipal franchise agreements governing waste collection services. Real business process: franchise compliance reporting, regu']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: DOT inspections triggered by safety incidents (post-accident inspections) or identifying safety incidents (out-of-service violations). Real business process: FMCSA requires post-accident inspections; ']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: DOT inspections of vehicles operating at landfill sites should be tracked by site for operational oversight and site-specific fleet compliance management, especially for dedicated landfill equipment t']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: DOT inspections may be required by operating permits for commercial vehicle fleets in waste management. Permit conditions often mandate inspection frequency, scope, and recordkeeping. Link enables per']
  primary_maintenance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee ID or external badge/certification number of the inspector who performed the DOT inspection. Used to cross-reference with Workday HCM for internal technicians or external certification registries for third-party inspectors.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: DOT inspections are a specific type of maintenance activity and should be classified using the standardized maintenance_type taxonomy. Currently has inspection_type (STRING) which should reference mai']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: DOT inspections frequently generate corrective work orders. The inspection record should link to the resulting work order for full traceability. Currently has work_order_number (STRING) which should b']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: DOT inspections of collection vehicles operating at WTE facilities must reference the facility for regulatory reporting, fleet assignment tracking, and facility-level DOT compliance metrics required b']
  annual_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next mandatory annual DOT vehicle inspection must be completed per FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.17. Calculated as 12 months from the last annual inspection date. Used for proactive compliance scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  cdl_number varchar [note: 'Commercial Driver License (CDL) number of the driver operating the vehicle at the time of inspection. Required for FMCSA compliance documentation and driver qualification file maintenance per 49 CFR Part 391.']
  certifying_mechanic_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified mechanic or technician who certified that all corrective actions were completed and the vehicle is safe for operation. Required for FMCSA DVIR sign-off and annual inspection certification per 49 CFR Part 396.17.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which all required corrective actions were completed and the vehicle was certified as roadworthy. Used to calculate repair turnaround time, vehicle downtime duration, and FMCSA compliance closure timelines.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective actions taken or required to address defects identified during the DOT inspection. Documents repairs performed, parts replaced, and adjustments made. Required for FMCSA compliance documentation and DVIR sign-off.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action (repair or adjustment) is required as a result of defects identified during this inspection. True triggers a work order (WO) creation in Infor EAM or SAP PM for repair scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DOT inspection record was first created in the maintenance management system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record retention compliance under FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.21.']
  csa_basic_category varchar [note: 'FMCSA Compliance, Safety, Accountability (CSA) Behavior Analysis and Safety Improvement Category (BASIC) most relevant to the violations cited in this inspection. Used for CSA score impact analysis and targeted safety improvement programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unsafe_driving|hos_compliance|driver_fitness|controlled_substances|vehicle_maintenance|hazmat_compliance|crash_indicator — promote to reference product]']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct defect items identified during the DOT inspection. Used for fleet safety analytics, FMCSA CSA BASIC scoring, and maintenance prioritization. A value of zero indicates no defects found.']
