  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of all defect items identified during the inspection, including affected vehicle systems (brakes, lights, tires, steering, coupling devices, etc.). Captured from the DVIR or FMCSA inspection report. For structured defect line items, see the maintenance_dot_inspection_defect child product.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of defects identified during the inspection. Critical defects trigger an out-of-service order. Major defects require repair before next dispatch. Minor defects are noted but do not prohibit operation. None indicates a clean inspection.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the vehicle was out of service as a result of this DOT inspection finding, from the time of out-of-service order or inspection failure to vehicle return-to-service. Used for fleet availability KPI reporting and maintenance cost analysis.']
  driver_signature_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CDL driver\'s signature was obtained on the Driver Vehicle Inspection Report (DVIR) as required by FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.11. True confirms the driver acknowledged the inspection findings. Required for FMCSA audit compliance.']
  fmcsa_report_number varchar [note: 'Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) assigned report number for roadside inspections entered into the FMCSA Motor Carrier Management Information System (MCMIS). Used for DataQs challenge submissions and CSA score reconciliation.']
  gross_vehicle_weight_rating_lbs varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-specified Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) in pounds for the inspected vehicle. Determines applicable DOT inspection requirements, weight limit compliance, and bridge formula applicability under FHWA regulations.']
  hazmat_placard_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was required to display hazardous materials (HAZMAT) placards at the time of inspection, triggering additional DOT HAZMAT inspection requirements under 49 CFR Part 172. Relevant for Waste Management hazardous waste collection vehicles.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the DOT vehicle inspection was conducted. Principal business event date used for compliance scheduling, FMCSA audit readiness, and annual inspection interval tracking.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the DOT inspection was completed. Used to calculate inspection duration, assess thoroughness, and support operational scheduling.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the DOT inspection was conducted, such as a Waste Management maintenance facility address, a roadside checkpoint location, or a third-party inspection station. Used for geographic compliance tracking and facility-level reporting.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the inspection record, used for FMCSA audit trails, roadside inspection reports, and cross-referencing with Infor EAM work orders. Corresponds to the inspection report number on FMCSA Form MCS-63 or equivalent state form.']
  inspection_report_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or channel from which this inspection record was captured. Infor EAM for internally-scheduled inspections, SAP PM for work order-linked inspections, FMCSA DataQs for roadside inspection data feeds, manual entry for paper-based records, or roadside enforcement for law enforcement-issued reports.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the DOT inspection. Pass indicates the vehicle meets all applicable safety standards. Fail indicates defects requiring corrective action before operation. Out-of-service (OOS) indicates the vehicle is prohibited from operation per FMCSA OOS criteria. Conditional pass indicates minor defects noted but vehicle is permitted to operate with restrictions.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the DOT inspection commenced. Used for Hours of Service (HOS) compliance correlation, driver log verification, and operational scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  inspection_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation where the DOT inspection was conducted. Required for multi-state compliance tracking, state DOT reporting, and FMCSA SafeStat/CSA scoring by jurisdiction.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the DOT inspection record in the maintenance management system. Open indicates inspection initiated but not completed. In-progress indicates active inspection underway. Completed indicates inspection finalized and signed off. Voided indicates record cancelled or superseded. Pending review indicates awaiting supervisor or compliance officer sign-off.']
  inspector_certification_number varchar [note: 'FMCSA or state-issued certification number confirming the inspector is qualified to perform annual or periodic DOT vehicle inspections per 49 CFR Part 396.19. Required for annual inspection validity and FMCSA audit readiness.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the DOT inspection. May be a certified Waste Management fleet technician, a DOT/state enforcement officer, or a third-party FMCSA-certified inspector. Required for FMCSA audit documentation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this DOT inspection record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between Infor EAM and the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit trail maintenance.']
  license_plate_number varchar [note: 'State-issued license plate number of the commercial vehicle at the time of inspection. Required for roadside inspection documentation and DOT compliance verification.']
  license_plate_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation (or Canadian province code) where the commercial vehicle is registered. Used for multi-jurisdictional DOT compliance tracking.']
  odometer_reading_miles varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time of the DOT inspection. Used for mileage-based preventive maintenance (PM) scheduling, asset lifecycle tracking in Infor EAM, and FMCSA annual inspection interval verification.']
  out_of_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was placed out-of-service (OOS) as a result of this inspection. True means the vehicle is prohibited from commercial operation until defects are corrected and the vehicle is re-inspected. Critical for fleet availability tracking and FMCSA compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_citation_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of FMCSA or state regulatory citation codes associated with defects identified during the inspection (e.g., 393.75|396.3). Used for CSA BASIC scoring, violation tracking, and regulatory audit response. Sourced from FMCSA violation code tables.']
  vehicle_return_to_service_date date [note: 'Date on which the vehicle was officially cleared and returned to active service following an out-of-service order or corrective action completion. Used for fleet availability reporting and downtime cost analysis.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial vehicle subject to inspection. ASL (Automated Side Loader), FEL (Front End Loader), REL (Rear End Loader), roll-off, transfer trailer, support vehicle, or other. Used for fleet-type-specific compliance analysis and maintenance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: asl|fel|rel|roll_off|transfer_trailer|support_vehicle|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vehicle_unit_number varchar [note: 'Internal fleet unit number assigned to the commercial vehicle (truck, trailer, or auxiliary equipment) subject to this DOT inspection. Used to cross-reference with AMCS Platform container tracking and Geotab Fleet Telematics records.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character standardized Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) as assigned by the manufacturer. Required for FMCSA registration, DOT compliance records, and state motor vehicle authority filings.']

  Note: 'DOT (Department of Transportation) vehicle inspection records for fleet assets subject to federal and state commercial vehicle regulations. Captures inspection date, inspection type (pre-trip, post-trip, annual FMCSA, roadside), inspector name/ID, vehicle unit number, odometer reading, inspection result (pass, fail, out-of-service), defect items identified, defect severity, corrective action required, corrective action completed date, and regulatory citation codes. Mandatory for CDL fleet compliance and FMCSA audit readiness.'
}

Table maintenance.inspection_defect {
  inspection_defect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual defect line item recorded during a DOT or internal maintenance inspection. Primary key for this entity.']
  air_pollution_control_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.air_pollution_control_unit. Business justification: APC unit defects identified during inspections require unit-level tracking for emissions compliance, corrective action management, and demonstrating timely repairs to EPA and state air quality regulat']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Boiler inspection defects must be tracked at the unit level for regulatory compliance with state boiler codes, repair prioritization, and demonstrating corrective action to insurance inspectors.']
  cleanup_waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Defects involving hazardous material leaks or contamination must reference waste profiles for cleanup material disposal and spill response tracking. Essential for environmental compliance, waste chara']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility or yard where the inspection was performed and/or where the repair will be or was completed. Supports facility-level defect and repair workload analysis.']
  failure_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Inspection defects should map to standardized failure modes for consistent defect classification and trend analysis across DOT inspections and internal inspections. Currently has defect_code (STRING) ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset on which this defect was identified. Enables defect trend analysis at the asset level.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Inspection defects may cause or be discovered during safety incidents. Real business process: defect-to-incident correlation for root cause analysis, preventive action tracking, and OSHA recordkeeping']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (driver, technician, or certified DOT inspector) who identified and recorded this defect during the inspection. Used for accountability tracking and regulatory audit.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Defects found during inspections of landfill equipment should be tracked by site for site-specific safety programs, regulatory compliance, and operational risk management. Enables site managers to mon']
  maintenance_dot_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inspection event that produced this defect line item. Links the defect to its originating inspection header record.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Defects found during inspections may violate permit conditions (emissions equipment failure, safety system defects affecting permit compliance). Link enables tracking permit condition violations, trig']
  primary_inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance technician assigned to and responsible for completing the repair of this defect. Null if no technician has been assigned yet.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Inspection defects track vehicle-specific safety and compliance issues. Fleet safety managers need vehicle→defect linkage for DOT compliance, CSA scoring, and defect trend analysis. Asset_id exists bu']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective maintenance Work Order (WO) created to repair this defect. Null if no repair work order has been generated yet.']
  component_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific sub-component or part within the component system where the defect was found (e.g., left front brake drum, right rear tire). Aligns with Infor EAM Bill of Materials (BOM) component codes.']
  component_system varchar [note: 'The major vehicle or facility component system in which the defect was identified (e.g., brakes, tires, lights, engine, body). Aligns with DOT FMCSA inspection categories and Infor EAM component hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brakes|tires|lights|engine|body|steering|suspension|fuel_system|exhaust|coupling|hydraulics|electrical|frame|cab — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this defect record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific defect observed, including location, condition, and any relevant contextual details noted by the inspector or driver.']
  defect_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for this defect line item, used in regulatory reports, driver vehicle inspection reports (DVIR), and maintenance communications. Sourced from Infor EAM or SAP PM defect numbering sequence.']
  defect_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the defect: open (identified, no action taken), in_progress (repair work order active), repaired (repair completed, pending verification), verified (repair confirmed by inspector or supervisor), waived (formally accepted risk with documented justification), closed (fully resolved and documented).']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when this defect was identified and recorded during the inspection. Provides exact event time for regulatory audit trails and time-to-repair calculations.']
  dot_oos_category varchar [note: 'DOT FMCSA out-of-service category applicable to this defect: driver OOS (driver condition), vehicle OOS (mechanical condition), hazmat OOS (hazardous materials violation), or not_applicable for non-OOS defects.']
  dot_violation_code varchar [note: 'Official DOT FMCSA violation code associated with this defect when it constitutes a regulatory violation (e.g., 393.45 for brake hose and tubing). Null for internal defects that do not map to a DOT violation code.']
  downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours the asset was taken out of service due to this defect, from the time of detection (or OOS declaration) to the time the asset was returned to service. Key metric for fleet availability and operational impact analysis.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the inspection was conducted and this defect was identified. Used for regulatory compliance tracking, defect aging analysis, and trend reporting.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the inspection was conducted and this defect was identified (e.g., maintenance yard name, facility address, roadside inspection station). Supports geographic analysis of defect patterns.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection that produced this defect: DOT annual inspection (49 CFR Part 396.17), DOT roadside inspection, driver pre-trip inspection, driver post-trip inspection, internal preventive maintenance (PM) inspection, or internal unscheduled inspection.']
  is_out_of_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this defect triggers an out-of-service (OOS) condition requiring the vehicle or equipment to be immediately removed from operation until repaired, per DOT FMCSA or CVSA out-of-service criteria.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this defect has been identified on the same asset and component system in a prior inspection within a defined lookback period. Flags recurring failures for root cause analysis and potential design or maintenance process changes.']
  is_repair_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair for this defect has been completed and verified. Used to track open versus resolved defects and to confirm regulatory compliance before returning an asset to service.']
  is_repair_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a repair action is required to resolve this defect. True for all major and critical defects; may be false for minor cosmetic observations that are logged but do not require immediate corrective action.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of technician labor hours expended to repair this defect. Used for workforce productivity analysis, maintenance cost allocation, and work order efficiency reporting.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Materials (BOM) part number of the primary replacement part used to repair this defect. Supports parts consumption tracking, inventory management, and recurring failure analysis by part.']
  photo_reference varchar [note: 'File path or document management system reference to photographic evidence of the defect captured during inspection. Supports visual documentation for regulatory compliance, insurance claims, and repair verification.']
  recurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this same defect type (by defect_code and component_system) has been recorded on this asset within the trailing 12-month period. Supports recurring failure identification and escalation thresholds.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory code section or standard citation applicable to this defect (e.g., \'49 CFR 393.45\', \'OSHA 1910.178\'). Supports regulatory compliance reporting and enforcement response documentation.']
  repair_action_taken varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action performed to resolve this defect, including parts replaced, adjustments made, or procedures followed. Captured from the work order completion notes in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  repair_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the repair for this defect was completed. Used to calculate time-to-repair, assess SLA compliance, and confirm regulatory compliance before returning the asset to service.']
  repair_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to repair this defect, including labor, parts, and any subcontracted services. Expressed in USD. Used for maintenance cost analysis, asset lifecycle costing, and budget management.']
  repair_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the repair for this defect must be completed, based on severity level, regulatory requirements, and operational scheduling. Critical defects require same-day resolution; major defects may have a defined SLA window.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the defect was formally closed and verified as resolved, including any required sign-off by a certified mechanic or inspector. May differ from repair_completed_date if a verification step is required.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect\'s severity: minor (cosmetic or low-risk, no immediate action required), major (significant defect requiring prompt repair), or critical (out-of-service condition requiring immediate removal from service per DOT FMCSA criteria).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this defect record originated: Infor EAM (CMMS), SAP PM (work order execution), AMCS (fleet inspection module), Geotab (telematics-triggered alert), or manual entry.']
  source_system_defect_code varchar [note: 'The native defect or notification identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Infor EAM defect ID, SAP PM notification number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this defect record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vehicle_engine_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine hours recorded at the time of the inspection when this defect was identified. Used for hour-based defect trend analysis on equipment where engine hours are the primary usage metric (e.g., off-road equipment, compactors).']
  vehicle_odometer_miles decimal(10,1) [note: 'Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time of the inspection when this defect was identified. Used for mileage-based defect trend analysis and predictive maintenance modeling.']

  Note: 'Individual defect line items identified during DOT or internal maintenance inspections. Captures defect code, defect description, component system (brakes, tires, lights, engine, body), severity level (minor, major, critical/out-of-service), out-of-service indicator, repair required flag, repair completed flag, repair work order reference, and resolution date. Enables defect trend analysis, recurring failure identification, and regulatory compliance tracking at the component level.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_catalog {
  parts_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each part record in the master parts catalog. Primary key for the parts_catalog data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Parts procurement and inventory planning require knowing which asset classes each part applies to. The existing text field \'asset_class_applicability\' should be a proper FK for asset class-based parts']
  disposal_waste_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: Parts containing hazardous materials (batteries, mercury switches, PCB capacitors) must link to EPA waste codes for disposal planning, waste characterization, and regulatory compliance. Essential for ']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Hazardous parts (batteries, hydraulic fluids, chemicals, refrigerants) must be tracked in facility hazard registers. Real business need: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard compliance, EPA hazmat track']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material. Business justification: Parts catalog entries map to procurement material master for standardized purchasing. Enables consolidated sourcing, contract pricing application, hazmat compliance validation (RCRA codes, UN numbers)']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Certain parts are permit-specific (emissions control components, hazardous waste containment parts, EPA-certified equipment). Parts catalogs must track permit applicability to ensure only approved com']
  preferred_supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to procurement.vendor']
  required_ppe_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ppe_issuance. Business justification: Certain parts require specific PPE for handling (hazmat gloves for batteries, respirators for chemicals, cut-resistant gloves for sharp components). Real business need: worker protection during parts ']
  catalog_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this part record became effective and available for use in work orders and purchasing. Supports catalog version management and historical BOM accuracy.']
  core_charge_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Refundable deposit amount charged by the supplier for a remanufactured part\'s core. Credited back upon return of the used core. Expressed in USD. Applicable only when is_core_returnable is True.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Default SAP cost center code associated with the procurement and consumption of this part. Used for departmental cost allocation and maintenance budget tracking in SAP CO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this part record was first created in the parts catalog. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  dot_inspection_part boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part is a safety-critical component subject to Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection requirements (True). Examples include brake components, tires, lights, and steering parts. Ensures compliance tracking for DOT roadworthiness inspections.']
  expected_service_life_days varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or engineering-specified expected useful service life of the part in calendar days under normal operating conditions. Used for PM interval planning, lifecycle cost analysis, and asset reliability modeling.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code to which the cost of this part is posted upon consumption in a work order. Enables accurate cost allocation to maintenance expense accounts in SAP FI/CO. Examples: parts expense, consumables, capital spares.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous material classification code for the part when is_hazardous_material is True (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 8 Corrosive, Class 9 Miscellaneous). Required for compliant storage, handling, and disposal documentation.']
  is_core_returnable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a core (used/rebuildable) part must be returned to the supplier upon installation of a replacement (True). Common for remanufactured parts such as alternators, starters, and hydraulic pumps. Drives core charge tracking in procurement.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part or material is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and disposal procedures under DOT, EPA, and OSHA regulations (e.g., certain fluids, batteries, refrigerants). True = hazardous; False = non-hazardous.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual units of this part are tracked by unique serial number (True) or managed as bulk/lot inventory (False). Serialized parts (e.g., engines, transmissions, compactors) require individual asset tracking in Infor EAM.']
  last_cost_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard cost for this part was last reviewed and updated. Supports annual cost roll-up processes and ensures standard costs remain aligned with market pricing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this part record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of the part from the preferred supplier. Used for reorder point calculation and maintenance scheduling to ensure parts availability.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer or parts manufacturer (e.g., Cummins, Parker Hannifin, Donaldson, Heil). Used for warranty tracking, sourcing decisions, and quality management.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The part number assigned by the manufacturer (distinct from OEM number when the manufacturer is a third-party supplier rather than the equipment OEM). Enables direct ordering from manufacturer catalogs.']
  maximum_stock_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity to prevent overstocking and excess carrying costs. Used in min-max inventory planning. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  minimum_stock_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Safety stock level — the absolute minimum quantity that must be maintained on hand to prevent stockouts during lead time. Distinct from reorder_point which is the trigger level. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  obsolete_date date [note: 'Date on which this part was designated as obsolete or discontinued. After this date, the part is no longer available for new work orders or purchase orders. Null if the part is still active.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'The Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the vehicle or equipment manufacturer (e.g., Mack Trucks, Heil Environmental, McNeilus). Used for cross-referencing with manufacturer catalogs and sourcing OEM replacements.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the part by functional system or component group. Drives Bill of Materials (BOM) organization, work order planning, and inventory segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine|hydraulic|electrical|body|tire|filter|fluid|brake|transmission|exhaust|pneumatic|safety|chassis|cooling|fuel|waste_body|compaction|lift_system|cab — promote to reference product]']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the part including specifications, dimensions, material composition, and application context. Supports technician identification and procurement accuracy.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name or title of the part (e.g., \'Hydraulic Pump Assembly\', \'Air Filter Element\', \'Brake Caliper\'). Used as the primary identity label for display in work orders, purchase orders, and inventory reports.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Internal Waste Management part number used as the primary business identifier for the part across procurement, maintenance work orders, and inventory systems. Corresponds to the Material Number in SAP MM and the Part Number in Infor EAM.']
  part_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the part in the catalog. Active parts are available for use in work orders and purchasing. Obsolete parts are no longer procured. Superseded parts have been replaced by a newer part number. Pending_approval parts are under review before activation.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part by its maintenance disposition: consumable (single-use, e.g., filters, fluids), repairable (can be rebuilt), rotable (tracked serialized item returned to stock after repair), expendable (low-cost, not tracked), or capital (high-value tracked asset component).']
  pm_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part is used in scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM) cycles (True) versus only in corrective/unplanned repairs (False). Drives PM parts kit planning and stocking strategies.']
  reorder_point decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum on-hand inventory quantity threshold that triggers a replenishment purchase order. When stock falls to or below this level, procurement is initiated. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  reorder_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Standard quantity to order when a replenishment is triggered. Represents the economic order quantity (EOQ) or fixed lot size for the part. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  sds_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this part or material. Required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard for hazardous chemicals. Enables technicians to access handling, storage, and emergency response information.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this part record originated (e.g., INFOR_EAM for Infor EAM parts master, SAP_MM for SAP S/4HANA Material Master, MANUAL for manually entered records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of measure for the part as maintained in the cost accounting system. Used for work order cost estimation, budget planning, and variance analysis. Expressed in USD. Corresponds to the standard price in SAP MM Material Master costing view.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Default warehouse or parts room storage location code where this part is stocked (e.g., bin, shelf, or zone identifier). Used for inventory picking, cycle counting, and parts room organization in Infor EAM.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations (UN) four-digit identification number for hazardous materials (e.g., UN1993 for flammable liquids). Required on shipping documents, labels, and placards for hazardous parts per DOT regulations. Null for non-hazardous parts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the part used in inventory management, purchasing, and work order consumption (e.g., EA=Each, QT=Quart, GL=Gallon, LT=Liter, KG=Kilogram, SET=Set, PAIR=Pair). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|QT|GL|LT|KG|LB|FT|M|SET|BOX|CASE|ROLL|PAIR — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warranty_duration_days varchar [note: 'Standard warranty period in calendar days provided by the manufacturer or supplier for this part. Used to track warranty claims and determine eligibility for no-cost replacements on failed parts.']
  weight_lbs decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the part in pounds. Used for shipping cost estimation, DOT weight compliance on service vehicles, and ergonomic safety assessments for technician handling under OSHA guidelines.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all parts, components, and materials used in fleet and facility maintenance operations. Stores part number, part description, manufacturer, OEM part number, part category (engine, hydraulic, electrical, body, tire, filter, fluid), unit of measure, standard cost, reorder point, reorder quantity, lead time days, preferred supplier reference, hazardous material flag, and active/obsolete status. Serves as the BOM (Bill of Materials) reference for work order parts planning. Owned by maintenance domain; procurement domain references for purchasing.'
}

Table maintenance.storeroom {
  storeroom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the maintenance storeroom or parts warehouse record. Primary key for this entity.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or maintenance yard where this storeroom is physically located. Used to associate parts inventory with a specific operational site.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the individual responsible for managing this storeroom, including inventory oversight, replenishment authorization, and physical security.']
  parent_storeroom_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent storeroom in a hierarchical storeroom network (e.g., a satellite storeroom references its central hub storeroom). Enables multi-tier inventory replenishment and inter-storeroom transfer management.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the storeroom physical location. Used for logistics, delivery routing, and regulatory site identification.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail for the storeroom physical location (e.g., suite, building, bay number). Supplements address_line1 for precise delivery and site identification.']
  bin_location_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete bin, shelf, or rack locations within the storeroom. Drives bin-level inventory tracking configuration in the CMMS and determines the granularity of parts storage management.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the storeroom is physically located. Used for geographic reporting, regional inventory analysis, and logistics planning.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the storeroom physical location (e.g., USA, CAN). Supports multi-country operations and regulatory jurisdiction identification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storeroom master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which physical inventory cycle counts are performed in this storeroom. Cycle counts verify on-hand quantities against system records and are required for SOX-compliant inventory accuracy controls.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the storeroom was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from active inventory operations. Populated when storeroom_status transitions to decommissioned.']
  eam_storeroom_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this storeroom as recorded in the Infor EAM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CMMS source of record.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the storeroom used for purchase order confirmations, parts requisition notifications, and vendor communications.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date on which the storeroom was formally established and made operational. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, depreciation scheduling, and historical inventory network analysis.']
  fire_suppression_type varchar [note: 'Type of fire suppression system installed in the storeroom. Required for OSHA and local fire code compliance, particularly for storerooms holding flammable lubricants, solvents, or compressed gas cylinders.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code used for inventory valuation and parts issuance accounting entries associated with this storeroom. Supports financial reporting and SOX-compliant inventory controls.']
  hazmat_permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the hazardous materials storage permit for this storeroom. Triggers permit renewal workflows and compliance alerts to prevent lapse of regulatory authorization.']
  hazmat_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit number issued by the applicable authority (EPA, state environmental agency, or local fire marshal) authorizing hazardous materials storage at this storeroom. Required when is_hazmat_certified is true.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value parts inventory held in this storeroom. Standard cost uses predetermined prices; moving average recalculates cost with each receipt; FIFO and LIFO are queue-based methods. Determines how parts cost flows to work orders and financial statements.']
  is_bonded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storeroom operates as a bonded (secured, access-controlled) inventory location where parts are tracked under formal custody and issuance controls. Bonded storerooms require documented check-in/check-out procedures and are subject to periodic audits.']
  is_climate_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storeroom maintains controlled temperature and humidity conditions. Climate-controlled storerooms are required for storing temperature-sensitive parts such as electronic control modules, batteries, lubricants, and certain chemical supplies.']
  is_hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storeroom is certified to store hazardous materials such as solvents, lubricants, batteries, and other regulated substances. Certification requires compliance with EPA, DOT, and OSHA hazmat storage regulations.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory replenishment for this storeroom is managed by an external vendor under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement. VMI storerooms have supplier-controlled reorder triggers and consignment stock arrangements.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal storeroom audit covering inventory accuracy, hazmat compliance, storage conditions, and security controls. Supports regulatory compliance tracking and internal audit scheduling.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory cycle count was completed for this storeroom. Used to track compliance with cycle count schedules and identify storerooms overdue for physical verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storeroom master record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the storeroom in decimal degrees (WGS84). Enables GPS-based proximity analysis for parts sourcing, mobile storeroom dispatch, and fleet maintenance routing.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the storeroom in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for spatial analysis, proximity-based parts sourcing, and logistics optimization.']
  max_inventory_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Authorized maximum dollar value of parts inventory that may be held in this storeroom at any time. Used for capital budgeting, insurance coverage determination, and inventory investment controls. Expressed in US dollars.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, access directions, or other contextual information about the storeroom that does not fit structured fields. Used by storeroom managers and maintenance planners.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Descriptive text specifying the days and hours during which the storeroom is staffed and open for parts issuance and receipt (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 06:00-18:00, Sat 07:00-12:00\'). Used for work order planning and technician parts availability scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the storeroom. Used by technicians, procurement staff, and fleet managers to coordinate parts availability, emergency parts requests, and delivery scheduling.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the storeroom physical location. Supports geographic analysis, delivery routing, and regulatory site reporting.']
  primary_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost center code to which storeroom operating expenses (overhead, labor, utilities) are charged. Enables financial reporting of storeroom costs within the maintenance domain P&L.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'The inventory replenishment strategy applied to this storeroom. Min/max triggers reorder when stock falls below minimum level; reorder_point uses statistical demand forecasting; kanban uses visual pull signals; vmi delegates replenishment to the vendor; manual requires human-initiated purchase orders.']
  sap_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA MM storage location code corresponding to this storeroom. Used for purchase order goods receipt, material document posting, and inventory reconciliation between SAP and Infor EAM.']
  security_access_level varchar [note: 'Physical security access control level for the storeroom. Open storerooms have no access restriction; badge_access requires electronic credential; supervised requires attendant presence; locked requires key/combination access. Drives access control configuration and audit requirements.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or Canadian province code for the storeroom physical location. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping, DOT compliance, and regional inventory reporting.']
  storage_capacity_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical floor area of the storeroom in square feet. Used for capacity planning, storeroom utilization analysis, and determining maximum parts inventory volume that can be physically accommodated.']
  storeroom_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storeroom within the enterprise maintenance network. Used in work orders, purchase orders, and parts requisitions to reference the issuing storeroom. Aligns with Infor EAM storeroom code field.']
  storeroom_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the storeroom (e.g., \'Houston Central Fleet Parts Storeroom\', \'Dallas North Satellite Storeroom\'). Used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces.']
  storeroom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the storeroom record. Active storerooms are operational and available for parts issuance and receipt. Inactive storerooms are temporarily closed. Decommissioned storerooms are permanently closed and no longer used.']
  storeroom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storeroom by its operational role within the parts distribution network. Central storerooms serve as primary hubs; satellite storerooms serve regional yards; mobile storerooms are truck-mounted for field use; field_cache are small on-site bins; virtual storerooms represent consignment or vendor-managed inventory locations.']

  Note: 'Master record for maintenance storerooms and parts warehouses that hold spare parts inventory for fleet and facility maintenance. Captures storeroom code, storeroom name, location (facility/yard), storeroom type (central, satellite, mobile), responsible manager, physical address, storage capacity, climate-controlled flag, hazmat storage certified flag, and active status. Enables multi-location parts inventory management and storeroom replenishment planning across the fleet maintenance network.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_inventory {
  parts_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parts inventory record representing the on-hand stock position of a specific part at a specific storeroom and bin location. Primary key for the parts_inventory data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  primary_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the part master record in the parts catalog. Links this inventory position to the part definition including description, specifications, and classification. Sourced from Infor EAM parts catalog.']
  storeroom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance storeroom or warehouse location where this part inventory is physically held. Each storeroom is associated with a facility or depot serving fleet and facility maintenance operations.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred or primary supplier for replenishing this part at this storeroom. Used to auto-populate purchase requisitions and support supplier performance analysis. Sourced from SAP MM vendor master or Infor EAM supplier catalog.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC analysis classification of the part based on annual consumption value. \'A\' items represent high-value/high-usage parts requiring tight inventory control; \'B\' items are moderate; \'C\' items are low-value/low-usage parts. Used to prioritize cycle count frequency and inventory management effort.']
  annual_usage_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this part issued from this storeroom over the trailing 12-month period. Used for ABC classification, reorder point calculation, safety stock sizing, and inventory carrying cost analysis. Updated periodically from goods issue history.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, rack, or aisle location within the storeroom where the part is physically stored (e.g., \'A-12-3\', \'RACK-B-SHELF-4\'). Enables efficient parts picking during work order execution and physical inventory counts.']
  count_variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between last_count_quantity and the system quantity_on_hand at the time of the physical count. Positive values indicate more stock found than recorded; negative values indicate shrinkage or unrecorded usage. Used to trigger inventory adjustments and investigate discrepancies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts inventory record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP MM). Represents the date the part was first stocked at this storeroom location. Used for inventory aging analysis and audit trail.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the part\'s operational criticality based on the impact of a stockout on fleet or facility operations. \'critical\' parts (e.g., DOT-required safety components, CNG system parts) cause immediate vehicle out-of-service if unavailable. Used to prioritize reorder actions and safety stock levels.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for physical cycle counting of this part at this storeroom. Typically driven by ABC classification (A=monthly, B=quarterly, C=annual) and criticality level. Used to schedule cycle count tasks and ensure inventory accuracy for SOX compliance.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous materials classification code for the part when is_hazardous is True (e.g., \'3\' for flammable liquids, \'8\' for corrosives, \'9\' for miscellaneous). Required for compliant storage, handling, and disposal documentation. Null when is_hazardous is False.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this inventory record. \'active\' indicates the part is available for use; \'quarantine\' indicates the part is held pending quality inspection; \'obsolete\' indicates the part is no longer used but stock remains; \'pending_disposal\' indicates stock is being removed from service.']
  inventory_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the on-hand inventory for this part at this storeroom, calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Expressed in USD. Used for balance sheet inventory asset reporting, carrying cost analysis, and financial close processes per GAAP/IFRS.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part or material is classified as hazardous under DOT, EPA, or OSHA regulations (e.g., solvents, lubricants, refrigerants, battery acid). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal requirements. True = hazardous material requiring regulatory compliance controls.']
  is_lot_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part is managed by lot or batch number for traceability purposes (True). Lot-controlled parts (e.g., lubricants, chemicals, safety-critical components) require lot tracking for quality control, recall management, and regulatory compliance.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual units of this part are tracked by unique serial number (True) or managed as bulk/lot quantity (False). Serialized parts (e.g., major assemblies, electronic control units) require individual tracking through installation and removal from assets.']
  last_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count for this part at this storeroom. Compared against the system quantity_on_hand to identify and reconcile inventory discrepancies.']
  last_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent stock issue (goods issue to a work order or cost center) was posted for this part at this storeroom. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory and support inventory rationalization decisions.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was performed for this part at this storeroom. Used to assess inventory record accuracy, schedule cycle counts, and support audit compliance. Required for SOX internal controls over inventory.']
  last_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent stock receipt (goods receipt from a purchase order or transfer) was posted for this part at this storeroom. Used to assess replenishment frequency and identify slow-moving or dormant inventory.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this inventory record, including quantity changes from receipts, issues, reservations, or manual adjustments. Used to determine data freshness and support incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of the part at the storeroom. Used to calculate reorder timing, safety stock levels, and projected availability dates for work order planning.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the manufacturer. Used for cross-referencing with supplier catalogs, identifying approved substitutes, and ensuring correct part specification during procurement and installation.']
  max_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the part that should be held in the storeroom at any time. Used to prevent overstocking, control carrying costs, and optimize storeroom space utilization. Replenishment orders are sized to bring stock up to but not exceed this level.']
  min_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum reorder point quantity below which a replenishment requisition or purchase order should be triggered. Used by the CMMS to generate automatic reorder alerts and prevent stockouts that could cause fleet or facility downtime.']
  next_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next physical cycle count of this part at this storeroom. Calculated based on last_physical_count_date and cycle_count_frequency. Used by storeroom supervisors to plan and assign counting tasks.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the part type used for inventory reporting and spend analysis (e.g., \'Engine Components\', \'Hydraulic System\', \'Electrical\', \'Body & Frame\', \'Tires & Wheels\', \'Filters & Fluids\', \'Safety Equipment\'). Aligns with the parts catalog classification hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine_components|hydraulic_system|electrical|body_frame|tires_wheels|filters_fluids|safety_equipment|pneumatic|brake_system|other — promote to reference product]']
  quantity_available decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net quantity available for new work order allocation, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved. Represents the true available-to-promise stock for WO planning and parts availability checks. Stored as a business field to support real-time availability queries without recalculation.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the part available in the storeroom at the time of the last inventory update. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure (e.g., EA, GAL, FT). This is the gross stock before reservations are subtracted.']
  quantity_on_order decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the part currently on open Purchase Orders (POs) that have been issued to suppliers but not yet received into the storeroom. Used for replenishment planning and projected stock availability calculations.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the part currently allocated or reserved against open Work Orders (WOs) that have not yet been issued/picked. Reduces the effective available quantity for new WO planning. Sourced from Infor EAM or SAP PM reservation records.']
  reorder_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard order quantity used when a replenishment purchase order is generated for this part at this storeroom. Typically set to the economic order quantity (EOQ) or supplier minimum order quantity to optimize procurement costs.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained above the minimum stock level to protect against demand variability and supplier lead time uncertainty. Particularly important for critical fleet parts (e.g., DOT-required components) where stockouts cause vehicle out-of-service events.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days this part can be stored before it expires or degrades and must be disposed of (e.g., lubricants, adhesives, rubber seals, safety equipment with expiry dates). Null for parts with no shelf life restriction. Used to manage FIFO rotation and prevent use of expired materials.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this inventory record was sourced (e.g., \'infor_eam\' for CMMS-managed storerooms, \'sap_s4hana\' for SAP MM-managed locations, \'manual\' for manually entered records). Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or average cost per unit of the part used for inventory valuation. Expressed in USD. Used to calculate total inventory value, work order material costs, and COGS reporting. May reflect moving average cost or standard cost depending on valuation method.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure in which the part quantity is tracked (e.g., EA=Each, GAL=Gallon, LB=Pound, FT=Feet). All quantity fields (on_hand, reserved, on_order, min, max) are expressed in this unit. Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|GAL|QT|LB|FT|IN|M|L|KG|BOX|SET|PKG|ROLL|DRUM — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value this part\'s inventory (e.g., moving_average, standard_cost, FIFO, LIFO). Determines how unit_cost is calculated when stock is received at varying prices. Must align with the company\'s GAAP/IFRS inventory accounting policy.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand inventory levels for parts and materials across all maintenance storerooms. Captures part reference, storeroom reference, bin location, quantity on hand, quantity reserved (allocated to open WOs), quantity on order, minimum stock level, maximum stock level, last physical count date, last count quantity, and inventory valuation. Enables parts availability checks during WO planning, stockout prevention, and inventory carrying cost management. Distinct from parts_catalog (definition) — this is the live inventory position.'
}

Table maintenance.cost {
  cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each maintenance cost record at the work order level. Primary key for the maintenance_cost data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Maintenance costs must be allocated to specific boiler units for financial reporting, budget variance analysis, and calculating unit-level operating cost per ton of waste processed.']
  carbon_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_initiative. Business justification: Capital and operating costs for emission reduction projects (LFG system upgrades, fleet electrification, renewable energy installations) must be tracked against carbon initiatives for ROI analysis, pa']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle, equipment unit, or facility asset against which this maintenance cost is recorded. Supports asset lifecycle costing and total cost of ownership analysis.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Maintenance cost records for customer-billable work must map to invoice line items for cost-to-revenue reconciliation. Enables finance to verify billing accuracy, calculate service margins, and audit ']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP FI/CO cost center responsible for absorbing this maintenance expenditure. Used for departmental cost allocation and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that triggered this work order and its associated costs, if applicable. Null for corrective or emergency work orders. Supports PM cost tracking and schedule optimization.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'SAP Purchase Order (PO) number associated with subcontracted or externally procured maintenance services or parts for this work order. Enables three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) for accounts payable processing.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator maintenance costs require unit-level tracking for energy production profitability analysis, capital replacement planning, and calculating cost per MWh for power purchase agreement negotiatio']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Maintenance costs track total repair and PM spend by vehicle for financial reporting and replacement analysis. Fleet controllers need vehicle→cost linkage for budget variance analysis and lifecycle co']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order (WO) for which this cost record is captured. Links the cost record to the specific maintenance event in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost actually incurred for this cost category on the work order, as posted to SAP FI/CO upon WO completion. Expressed in USD. Core metric for fleet maintenance spend and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of this maintenance cost record: pending (awaiting review), approved (authorized for GL posting), rejected (denied, requires correction), or on_hold (suspended pending investigation). Supports financial controls and SOX compliance.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance cost record was approved or rejected by the authorized approver. Provides the audit timestamp for financial controls and SOX compliance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the manager or financial controller who approved this maintenance cost record for GL posting. Required for SOX audit trail and financial authorization controls.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset incurring the maintenance cost: fleet vehicle (ASL, FEL, REL, CNG trucks), container, facility (MRF, landfill, transfer station), equipment (compactor, conveyor), or other. Supports cost segmentation by asset class.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost type within the work order: labor (technician time), parts (materials/BOM components), subcontract (third-party vendor services), overhead (allocated indirect costs), fuel (vehicle/equipment fuel consumed during maintenance), or other. Drives cost breakdown reporting and EBITDA analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: labor|parts|subcontract|overhead|fuel|warranty|other — promote to reference product]']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a planned (budgeted) cost, actual (incurred) cost, variance (planned minus actual), or accrual (estimated but not yet invoiced). Supports plan-vs-actual financial analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance cost record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Provides the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and financial controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency reporting for international operations.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order and its associated costs are related to a mandatory Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection. True = DOT inspection-related cost. Used for regulatory compliance cost tracking and DOT audit support.']
  downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was out of service (downtime) due to the maintenance activity associated with this work order. Used to calculate the operational cost of downtime and support fleet availability KPI reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (1–12 or 1–13 for 4-4-5 calendars) within the fiscal year in which this cost is recognized in SAP FI/CO. Supports monthly financial close and period-over-period maintenance cost trending.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this maintenance cost is recognized for financial reporting purposes, as defined in SAP FI/CO. Used for annual budget tracking and EBITDA period analysis.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) account code to which this maintenance cost is posted. Enables financial reconciliation between maintenance operations and the corporate chart of accounts. Critical for SOX compliance and GAAP reporting.']
  labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total technician labor hours charged to this work order for the labor cost category. Sourced from Infor EAM time confirmations or SAP PM activity confirmations. Used to validate labor cost calculations and workforce productivity analysis.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Blended or standard hourly labor rate (USD/hour) applied to calculate the labor cost for this work order. May reflect straight-time, overtime, or contract rates. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of internal wage rate data.']
  note varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or justification provided by the maintenance planner, technician, or financial controller regarding this cost record. May include explanations for cost variances, warranty claim details, or approval conditions.']
  parts_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total quantity of parts or materials consumed on this work order for the parts cost category, as recorded in the Bill of Materials (BOM) confirmation in Infor EAM or SAP MM. Supports inventory reconciliation and parts usage analysis.']
  parts_unit_cost decimal(14,4) [note: 'Standard or actual unit cost per part or material item consumed on this work order, as sourced from SAP MM material master or Infor EAM parts catalog. Classified as confidential due to procurement pricing sensitivity.']
  planned_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted or estimated cost for this cost category on the work order, as established during WO creation or PM scheduling in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Expressed in USD. Used for budget variance analysis.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which this maintenance cost was posted to the SAP General Ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the authoritative date for financial reporting and audit purposes.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The originating document number from the source system (e.g., SAP accounting document number, Infor EAM transaction ID, or vendor invoice number) that generated this cost record. Enables end-to-end audit traceability from the Silver Layer back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this maintenance cost record originated: SAP_PM (SAP Plant Maintenance), Infor_EAM (Infor Enterprise Asset Management), manual (manually entered adjustment), or other. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  subcontract_vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or subcontractor providing services for this work order when cost_category is \'subcontract\'. Sourced from SAP MM vendor master. Used for vendor spend analysis and contract compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance cost record was last modified in the source system or updated in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail requirements.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between planned and actual cost (planned_cost_amount minus actual_cost_amount) for this cost category. A positive value indicates under-spend; negative indicates over-spend. Stored as a raw field to avoid recalculation overhead in reporting layers.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any portion of this maintenance cost is expected to be recovered through a manufacturer or vendor warranty claim. True = warranty claim initiated. Supports warranty cost recovery tracking and net cost reporting.']
  warranty_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount expected to be or already recovered from a manufacturer or vendor warranty claim against this maintenance cost. Reduces net maintenance cost for asset lifecycle costing. Expressed in USD.']
  wo_completion_date date [note: 'The date the associated work order was technically completed and costs were confirmed in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Represents the principal business event date for this cost record. Used to align maintenance activity with financial period.']
  wo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance work order driving this cost: preventive (PM cycle-based), corrective (repair after failure), emergency (unplanned breakdown), inspection (DOT or regulatory), or overhaul (major rebuild). Enables cost analysis by maintenance strategy.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP or Infor EAM work center code identifying the maintenance shop, garage, or facility where the work was performed. Used for cost allocation by operational location and capacity planning.']

  Note: 'Aggregated cost record for maintenance activities at the work order level, capturing the full cost breakdown for financial reporting and asset lifecycle costing. Stores work order reference, asset reference, cost category (labor, parts, subcontract, overhead), planned cost, actual cost, variance, cost center, GL account code, fiscal period, and approval status. Integrates with SAP FI/CO for cost posting and supports EBITDA impact analysis of fleet maintenance spend.'
}

Table maintenance.failure_mode {
  failure_mode_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each failure mode record in the reference catalog. Primary key for the failure_mode data product. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a standardized reference catalog of failure codes and classifications; it is a REFERENCE_LOOKUP/MASTER_RESOURCE hybrid used to classify asset breakdowns across work orders and repair history.]']
  applicable_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of vehicle or equipment types to which this failure mode applies (e.g., \'ASL, FEL, REL, CNG Truck, Roll-Off, Transfer Trailer\'). Restricts failure code availability in work order entry to relevant asset types, reducing misclassification. Null if applicable to all asset types.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of asset to which this failure mode applies. Determines which asset populations are eligible to use this failure code on work orders and PM schedules. Fleet covers collection vehicles (ASL, FEL, REL, CNG trucks); facility covers landfill, MRF, and WTE plant assets.']
  component_system varchar [note: 'The primary vehicle or facility component system affected by this failure mode (e.g., \'Engine\', \'Transmission\', \'Hydraulic Lift System\', \'Braking System\', \'Electrical System\', \'Compactor Body\', \'Chassis\'). Aligns with VMRS system codes for fleet assets and EAM component hierarchy for facility assets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure mode record was first created in the enterprise catalog. Supports audit trail requirements and catalog version management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Standard method by which this failure mode is typically detected. Informs PM schedule design and sensor/telematics alert configuration. Sensor alerts integrate with Geotab telematics; diagnostic codes align with OBD-II/J1939 fault code systems for fleet assets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual_inspection|sensor_alert|driver_report|pm_inspection|diagnostic_code|operator_complaint|third_party_inspection — promote to reference product]']
  dot_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure mode, when identified on a commercial motor vehicle, constitutes a DOT-reportable defect requiring out-of-service designation or driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR) notation. Drives compliance workflows in fleet maintenance and DOT inspection management.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this failure mode code is valid for use on work orders and maintenance records. Supports catalog versioning and ensures that historical work orders retain the failure classification that was active at the time of the repair.']
  epa_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure mode may result in an EPA-reportable environmental incident (e.g., hydraulic fluid spill, leachate containment breach, LFG emission exceedance). When true, triggers environmental incident notification workflows in Enviance EHS and CERCLA/RCRA compliance reporting.']
  estimated_downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard estimated number of hours an asset is expected to be out of service when this failure mode occurs, including diagnosis, repair, and return-to-service time. Used for fleet availability modeling, route coverage planning, and KPI reporting on asset utilization.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard estimated cost of parts and materials required to repair this failure mode, expressed in US dollars. Used for work order cost estimation, maintenance budget planning, and repair-vs-replace decision support. Sourced from SAP MM BOM pricing and Infor EAM parts catalog.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this failure mode code is no longer valid for assignment to new work orders. Null for codes with no planned expiration. Supports catalog lifecycle management and ensures deprecated codes are not used in active maintenance workflows.']
  failure_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping failure modes by the nature of the failure mechanism. Drives routing of work orders to the appropriate technician skill set and supports trend analysis by failure type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|structural|operator_induced|pneumatic|software — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the failure mode within the enterprise catalog. Used as the externally-known business identifier on work orders, repair records, and FMEA documentation. Sourced from Infor EAM failure code configuration.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the failure, observable symptoms, and conditions under which it typically occurs. Provides technicians and engineers with sufficient context to identify and classify the failure accurately in the field.']
  failure_mode_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of related failure modes for reporting and analysis purposes (e.g., \'Powertrain Failures\', \'Body and Compactor Failures\', \'Hydraulic System Failures\', \'Containment Failures\'). Supports roll-up reporting in maintenance dashboards and FMEA analysis by system group.']
  failure_mode_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failure mode record in the enterprise catalog. Active records are available for selection on work orders and PM schedules. Inactive or deprecated records are retained for historical reporting but cannot be assigned to new work orders.']
  failure_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name for the failure mode (e.g., \'Hydraulic Hose Burst\', \'Brake Pad Wear\', \'Electrical Short Circuit\'). Used as the identity label in work order dropdowns, dashboards, and maintenance reports.']
  fmea_detectability_score varchar [note: 'FMEA detectability rating on a 1-10 scale representing the ability of current inspection and monitoring controls to detect the failure before it causes asset breakdown. Score of 10 indicates the failure is virtually undetectable. Component of the RPN calculation.']
  fmea_occurrence_score varchar [note: 'FMEA occurrence rating on a 1-10 scale representing the likelihood of this failure mode occurring within a given operating period. Score of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence. Derived from historical work order frequency data and OEM reliability data. Component of the RPN calculation.']
  fmea_rpn varchar [note: 'Composite risk score calculated as the product of Severity, Occurrence, and Detectability ratings (each 1-10) per FMEA methodology. Range 1-1000. Higher RPN values indicate higher priority for preventive maintenance investment and engineering redesign. Maintained by reliability engineering and reviewed during PM optimization cycles.']
  fmea_severity_score varchar [note: 'FMEA severity rating on a 1-10 scale representing the seriousness of the failure effect on asset function, safety, or regulatory compliance. Score of 10 indicates catastrophic safety or environmental impact. Component of the RPN calculation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether repair of this failure mode involves handling, containment, or disposal of hazardous materials (e.g., hydraulic fluid, refrigerants, battery acid, leachate). When true, triggers hazardous waste handling procedures and DOT/EPA hazmat compliance workflows.']
  industry_standard_code varchar [note: 'External industry standard code that maps this failure mode to a recognized classification system beyond VMRS (e.g., ISO 14224 failure mechanism code, SWANA equipment failure taxonomy, or OEM-specific fault code). Enables cross-system benchmarking and regulatory reporting alignment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure mode record was most recently updated. Tracks catalog maintenance activity and supports change management auditing for regulated maintenance programs. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required when performing corrective maintenance for this failure mode. Drives automatic inclusion of LOTO safety instructions on generated work orders. Mandatory for compliance with OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147.']
  mean_time_between_failures decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operating hours between occurrences of this failure mode across the asset population, expressed in hours. Derived from historical work order data and used to calibrate PM interval recommendations and reliability forecasting models. Null for newly cataloged failure modes with insufficient history.']
  mean_time_to_repair decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average labor hours required to diagnose and repair this failure mode, expressed in hours. Used for maintenance scheduling, technician capacity planning, and downtime impact estimation on work orders. Derived from actual labor hours recorded in Infor EAM and SAP PM work order history.']
  obd_fault_code varchar [note: 'SAE J1939 or OBD-II diagnostic trouble code (DTC) associated with this failure mode for fleet assets. Enables automatic failure mode classification from telematics fault code alerts received via Geotab. Null for non-fleet or non-electronic failure modes.']
  pm_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detection of this failure mode should automatically trigger creation of a preventive maintenance work order or PM schedule review. Supports proactive maintenance strategy by escalating recurring failure patterns to PM program adjustments.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of required PPE items for technicians performing repairs associated with this failure mode (e.g., \'Safety glasses, Chemical-resistant gloves, Steel-toed boots, Face shield\'). Populated on work orders to ensure OSHA PPE compliance during maintenance activities.']
  recommended_corrective_action varchar [note: 'Standardized guidance on the preferred corrective maintenance action to resolve this failure mode (e.g., \'Replace hydraulic hose assembly\', \'Inspect and replace brake pads\', \'Perform electrical continuity test and rewire\'). Used to pre-populate work order task instructions and support technician decision-making.']
  recurrence_risk varchar [note: 'Assessed likelihood that this failure mode will recur on the same asset within 90 days of repair if only corrective action is taken without addressing the root cause. High recurrence risk failure modes are flagged for root cause analysis and PM program review.']
