  review_date date [note: 'Date on which this failure mode record was last reviewed by reliability engineering for accuracy, relevance, and FMEA score currency. Supports periodic catalog maintenance governance and ensures failure mode data quality for analytics and AI/ML applications.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the reliability engineer or maintenance manager who last reviewed and approved this failure mode record for catalog inclusion. Supports quality assurance and change management governance for the failure mode catalog.']
  safety_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure mode presents a direct safety risk to operators, technicians, or the public. When true, triggers mandatory safety review, LOTO (Lockout/Tagout) procedures, and potential DOT out-of-service designation on associated work orders. Supports OSHA and DOT compliance workflows.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Standardized severity rating indicating the potential impact of this failure mode on asset operability, safety, regulatory compliance, or service delivery. Critical failures trigger immediate out-of-service actions; high failures require same-day response; medium and low allow scheduled repair. Aligns with FMEA severity rankings.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system from which this failure mode record was created or imported. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and reconciliation between Infor EAM, SAP PM, and VMRS standard code imports.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native failure code identifier as it exists in the originating source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the enterprise catalog and source systems during ETL processing and audit reviews.']
  typical_root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the most common underlying root cause(s) associated with this failure mode (e.g., \'Lack of lubrication\', \'Fatigue cracking due to overloading\', \'Corrosion from leachate exposure\'). Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and FMEA documentation. Populated by maintenance engineering based on historical repair data.']
  vmrs_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard VMRS code mapping this failure mode to the American Trucking Associations Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards taxonomy. Enables benchmarking against industry fleet maintenance data, DOT compliance reporting, and interoperability with fleet telematics systems such as Geotab. Null for non-fleet (facility) failure modes.']
  warranty_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this failure mode may be covered under OEM or vendor warranty, triggering warranty claim initiation in SAP MM. When true, work orders associated with this failure mode are flagged for warranty review before parts are charged to the maintenance cost center.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of standardized failure modes and failure codes used to classify asset breakdowns and defects in work orders and repair history. Captures failure mode code, failure mode description, failure category (mechanical, electrical, hydraulic, structural, operator-induced), affected component system, typical root cause, recommended corrective action, and industry standard code mapping (e.g., VMRS codes for fleet). Enables consistent failure classification, trend analysis, and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) support.'
}

Table maintenance.type {
  type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each maintenance type record in the reference classification table. Primary key. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a small enumeration/code list providing standardized taxonomy for maintenance activity classification across fleet and facility assets; per-role minimums are exempt.]']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Maintenance planning requires defining which asset classes require specific maintenance types (PM schedules, DOT inspections, EPA compliance checks). The text field \'asset_class_applicability\' should ']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders of this maintenance type require supervisory or management approval before execution (True) or can be executed without prior approval (False). Drives approval workflow routing in SAP PM and Infor EAM for cost control and compliance.']
  approval_threshold_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum estimated cost in US dollars above which a work order of this maintenance type requires management approval. Supports tiered authorization controls for maintenance spend in compliance with SOX internal control requirements.']
  avg_labor_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard average labor cost for a maintenance activity of this type, expressed in US dollars. Derived from technician labor rates and estimated duration. Used for work order cost estimation, maintenance budget planning, and COGS reporting.']
  avg_parts_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard average cost of parts and materials consumed for a maintenance activity of this type, expressed in US dollars. Used for maintenance budget planning, work order cost estimation, and BOM cost benchmarking in SAP MM and Infor EAM.']
  cost_category_code varchar [note: 'Maps this maintenance type to a cost accounting category in SAP FI/CO for GL posting and COGS allocation (e.g., PREV_MAINT, CORR_MAINT, EMER_MAINT, DOT_INSP, WARRANTY). Enables financial reporting by maintenance type and supports EBITDA analysis for fleet operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance type record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change management tracking per SOX and internal data governance requirements.']
  default_priority_code varchar [note: 'Default work order priority assigned when a work order is created under this maintenance type. Drives scheduling urgency, technician dispatch sequencing, and SLA compliance tracking. Can be overridden at the individual work order level.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance type constitutes or includes a DOT-mandated vehicle inspection (True) or not (False). When True, triggers DOT inspection form completion, driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR) requirements, and out-of-service defect tracking per 49 CFR Part 396.']
  eam_type_code varchar [note: 'Native maintenance type code as defined in Infor EAM, used for system integration and data lineage tracing. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the Infor EAM CMMS source system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this maintenance type classification becomes valid and available for use in work order and PM schedule creation. Supports versioning and phased rollout of new maintenance type definitions aligned with regulatory or operational changes.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard estimated labor hours required to complete a maintenance activity of this type. Used for technician scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime forecasting. Serves as the default value populated on new work orders of this type in Infor EAM.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this maintenance type classification is no longer valid for new work order or PM schedule creation. Null indicates no expiration. Supports retirement of obsolete maintenance types while preserving historical records.']
  external_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type are typically performed by an external vendor or contractor (True) rather than internal technicians (False). When True, triggers purchase order (PO) creation workflow in SAP MM for external service procurement.']
  failure_mode_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure mode and root cause analysis is required for work orders of this maintenance type (True) or not (False). When True, technicians must document failure codes, cause codes, and remediation actions in Infor EAM for reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.']
  ghg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type generate reportable GHG emissions data (True) or not (False). Applicable for maintenance involving CNG/RNG fuel systems, LFG equipment, or WTE facility assets where EPA GHG reporting under the Clean Air Act is required.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger account code to which maintenance costs of this type are posted. Ensures accurate financial reporting, cost center allocation, and compliance with GAAP/IFRS standards for maintenance expense recognition.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required for maintenance activities of this type (True) or not (False). Mandatory safety control per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for hazardous energy control during maintenance on fleet and facility equipment.']
  max_allowable_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours an asset is permitted to remain out of service for maintenance of this type before escalation is triggered. Used to enforce SLA compliance for fleet availability and to drive escalation workflows in Infor EAM and AMCS dispatch.']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the recurrence_interval_meter field, defining the measurement basis for meter-triggered PM schedules (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for engine runtime, cycles for lift/compaction cycles, tons for throughput-based equipment).']
  planning_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days required to plan and prepare for a maintenance activity of this type before execution. Used by maintenance planners to schedule parts procurement, technician availability, and asset downtime windows. Sourced from Infor EAM PM cycle configuration.']
  pm_trigger_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the basis on which PM schedules of this maintenance type are triggered: time-based (fixed calendar intervals), meter-based (mileage, engine hours, cycles), condition-based (sensor thresholds), event-based (after specific operational events), or calendar-based (fixed dates). Drives PM cycle configuration in Infor EAM.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mandatory PPE items required for maintenance activities of this type (e.g., safety_glasses, gloves, hard_hat, hi_vis_vest, respirator). Populated on work orders to ensure technician safety compliance per OSHA PPE standards.']
  recurrence_interval_days varchar [note: 'Default calendar-based recurrence interval in days for PM schedules of this maintenance type. Used when pm_trigger_basis is \'time\' or \'calendar\'. Drives automatic PM work order generation in Infor EAM and SAP PM scheduling cycles.']
  recurrence_interval_meter decimal(10,2) [note: 'Default meter-based recurrence interval for PM schedules of this maintenance type (e.g., miles, engine hours, lift cycles). Used when pm_trigger_basis is \'meter\'. The unit of measure is defined by meter_unit. Drives meter-based PM work order generation in Infor EAM.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority that mandates or governs maintenance activities of this type. Used to route compliance documentation to the correct reporting workflow in Enviance EHS. Applicable values include DOT (Department of Transportation), EPA, OSHA, RCRA, STATE, LOCAL, or NONE for non-regulated types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DOT|EPA|OSHA|RCRA|STATE|LOCAL|NONE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be reported to a regulatory authority (True) or not (False). Triggers mandatory documentation workflows in Enviance EHS and SAP EHS for DOT inspections, EPA compliance events, and OSHA-required maintenance records.']
  required_certification_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific technician certification required to perform maintenance of this type (e.g., CDL_CLASS_A, ASE_MASTER, DOT_INSPECTOR, HAZMAT_HANDLER). Enforced during work order technician assignment in Infor EAM and Workday HCM. Applicable when requires_certified_technician is True.']
  requires_asset_shutdown boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance of this type requires the asset (vehicle, equipment, or facility) to be taken out of service (True) or can be performed while the asset remains operational (False). Drives downtime planning and route coverage adjustments in AMCS and Locus Dispatch.']
  requires_certified_technician boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be performed by a technician holding a specific certification (True) or can be performed by any qualified technician (False). When True, the required_certification_code field specifies the certification. Supports CDL, ASE, and DOT certification enforcement.']
  sap_order_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM order type code corresponding to this maintenance type (e.g., PM01, PM02, PM03). Used for GL posting, cost settlement, and integration with SAP FI/CO for maintenance cost reporting and EBITDA analysis.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of maintenance types in dropdown lists, reports, and scheduling interfaces. Lower values appear first. Enables business users to prioritize frequently used types for operational efficiency.']
  type_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the maintenance type indicating whether the activity is planned (scheduled in advance), unplanned (reactive/emergency), regulatory (DOT/EPA/OSHA mandated), or warranty (covered under manufacturer or service warranty). Supports cost allocation and KPI segmentation.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the maintenance type, used as a business key across work orders, PM schedules, and reporting systems (e.g., PREV, CORR, PRED, EMER, CBM, DOT, WARR, RBLD). Sourced from Infor EAM and SAP PM type classification.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and application of this maintenance type. Provides guidance to planners and technicians on when and how to apply this classification across fleet vehicles, containers, and facility assets.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the maintenance type as recognized across fleet and facility operations (e.g., Preventive, Corrective, Predictive, Emergency, Condition-Based, DOT Inspection, Warranty, Rebuild). Used in work order displays, PM schedule labels, and management reporting.']
  type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance type record indicating whether it is actively used in work order and PM schedule creation (active), temporarily disabled (inactive), or retired from use (deprecated). Controls availability in downstream scheduling and dispatch systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this maintenance type record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data synchronization between Infor EAM and the lakehouse silver layer, and audit compliance.']
  warranty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type may be covered under an OEM or service warranty (True) or are not warranty-eligible (False). When True, triggers warranty claim validation workflow in Infor EAM before cost posting to internal cost centers.']
  work_order_class varchar [note: 'SAP PM and Infor EAM work order class associated with this maintenance type. Determines the work order processing workflow, approval routing, and cost settlement rules. Maps directly to the order type configuration in SAP PM (e.g., PM01 Corrective, PM02 Preventive, PM03 Inspection).']

  Note: 'Reference classification of maintenance activity types used across work orders and PM schedules. Captures maintenance type code, maintenance type name (Preventive, Corrective, Predictive, Emergency, Condition-Based, DOT Inspection, Warranty, Rebuild), description, planning lead time standard, priority default, cost category mapping, and regulatory reporting flag. Provides standardized taxonomy for maintenance classification across fleet and facility assets.'
}

Table maintenance.service_bulletin {
  service_bulletin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the service bulletin record in the Waste Management lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  parts_kit_parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.parts_catalog. Business justification: Service bulletins often specify required parts kits for compliance. Currently has parts_kit_number (STRING) which should be replaced with FK to parts_catalog for proper inventory integration. Removes ']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: OEM service bulletins often implement regulatory changes (emissions recalls, safety modifications mandated by NHTSA/EPA, DOT safety requirements). Bulletins must reference the regulatory driver for pr']
  affected_asset_make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand of the fleet vehicle or equipment affected by this bulletin (e.g., Mack, Peterbilt, McNeilus, Heil, Leach). Used to filter the fleet asset register in Infor EAM to identify all affected units.']
  affected_asset_model varchar [note: 'Specific model designation of the fleet vehicle or equipment affected by this bulletin (e.g., LR64T, McNeilus Rear Loader, Heil Half/Pack). Combined with make and year range to precisely identify affected assets in the fleet.']
  affected_component varchar [note: 'Name or description of the specific vehicle or equipment component addressed by the bulletin (e.g., brake system, hydraulic lift cylinder, CNG fuel system, engine control module). Used to route the bulletin to the correct technician skill set and parts inventory.']
  affected_component_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the affected component per OEM or internal parts taxonomy. Enables automated BOM lookup and parts reservation in SAP MM and Infor EAM for bulletin-driven work orders.']
  affected_vin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of the range of vehicles affected by this bulletin. Null if the bulletin applies to all VINs from affected_vin_range_start onward or if VIN-range scoping is not applicable.']
  affected_vin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of the range of vehicles affected by this bulletin, as specified by the OEM or NHTSA. Used with affected_vin_range_end to precisely identify affected fleet units beyond make/model/year filtering.']
  affected_year_from varchar [note: 'Starting model year (inclusive) of the asset range affected by this bulletin. Combined with affected_year_to to define the model year band for fleet asset identification and compliance scoping.']
  affected_year_to varchar [note: 'Ending model year (inclusive) of the asset range affected by this bulletin. Null if the bulletin applies to all model years from affected_year_from onward. Used with affected_year_from to scope fleet compliance queries.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'High-level category of the asset affected by this bulletin. Collection vehicles include ASL, FEL, REL trucks; transfer vehicles include roll-off and transfer trailers; support equipment includes compactors and balers; facility equipment includes landfill and MRF machinery.']
  bulletin_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the bulletin including the problem statement, root cause, risk assessment, and corrective action required. Sourced from the OEM technical service notice or internal engineering documentation. Referenced by technicians during work order execution.']
  bulletin_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the issuing organization (OEM, internal engineering, or DOT) to uniquely identify this bulletin. Used for cross-referencing with manufacturer recall databases, DOT safety notices, and internal CMMS work order execution in SAP PM and Infor EAM.']
  bulletin_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service bulletin. Active bulletins require fleet action; superseded bulletins have been replaced by a newer revision; cancelled bulletins are withdrawn by the issuer; expired bulletins have passed their compliance deadline without regulatory consequence.']
  bulletin_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the service bulletin as issued by the originating organization. Provides a human-readable summary of the bulletin subject for fleet maintenance staff and compliance officers.']
  bulletin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bulletin by its nature and urgency. Safety recalls are mandated by NHTSA or OEM; mandatory repairs are required for continued operation; recommended repairs are advisory; informational bulletins provide technical guidance; regulatory notices originate from DOT/OSHA/EPA; field service actions are OEM-initiated campaigns.']
  completion_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total affected assets for which the bulletin corrective action has been completed (completed assets / total affected assets × 100). Provides a real-time compliance posture metric for fleet managers and regulatory reporting.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate completion status of the bulletin across all affected fleet assets in Waste Management\'s fleet. Tracks overall compliance posture for the bulletin. Detailed per-asset completion is tracked in the bulletin_asset_compliance association table.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which all affected fleet assets must have the bulletin action completed to remain in regulatory or OEM compliance. Null for informational bulletins with no mandatory deadline. Drives prioritization of PM work orders and DOT inspection readiness.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Specific repair, inspection, or modification procedure required to address the bulletin. Describes the technical steps technicians must perform, referencing OEM repair manuals or internal engineering procedures. Populates the task description in SAP PM work orders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service bulletin record was first created in the Waste Management lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this bulletin record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international fleet assets and OEM billing.']
  dot_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this bulletin requires a formal DOT (Department of Transportation) inspection or roadworthiness certification (True). Triggers DOT inspection workflow in SAP EHS and Enviance EHS upon work order completion.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'OEM or engineering-estimated number of labor hours required to complete the bulletin corrective action per affected asset. Used for maintenance scheduling, technician capacity planning, and cost estimation in SAP PM and Infor EAM.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'OEM or engineering-estimated cost of parts and materials required to complete the bulletin corrective action per affected asset, in USD. Used for maintenance budget planning and purchase order creation in SAP MM.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the bulletin was officially published or released by the issuing organization. Used to calculate compliance timelines and prioritize work order scheduling in SAP PM and Infor EAM.']
  issuing_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that issued the bulletin, such as the OEM (e.g., Mack Trucks, McNeilus, Heil), a regulatory body (DOT, OSHA, EPA), or Waste Management internal engineering. Determines the authority level and compliance obligation of the bulletin.']
  issuing_organization_type varchar [note: 'Category of the organization that issued the bulletin. Drives compliance workflow routing: OEM bulletins route to fleet maintenance; DOT/OSHA/EPA bulletins route to environmental compliance and safety teams; internal engineering bulletins route to fleet operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oem|internal_engineering|dot|osha|epa|state_agency|industry_association — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required when performing the bulletin corrective action (True). Mandatory for OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 compliance. Triggers LOTO permit workflow in Enviance EHS and SAP EHS.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this bulletin is mandatory (True) or optional/advisory (False). Mandatory bulletins trigger automatic work order creation in SAP PM and Infor EAM and may affect DOT roadworthiness certification.']
  nhtsa_recall_number varchar [note: 'National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) recall campaign number associated with this bulletin, in the format YYVnnnnnn (e.g., 23V123456). Populated for safety recalls mandated by NHTSA. Enables cross-referencing with the NHTSA public recall database for compliance verification.']
  oem_warranty_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OEM will cover the cost of parts and/or labor for this bulletin under an active warranty or recall program (True) or whether Waste Management bears the cost (False). Drives warranty claim processing in SAP SD and cost allocation in SAP FI/CO.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for technicians performing the bulletin corrective action (e.g., safety glasses, chemical-resistant gloves, respirator). Ensures OSHA compliance and technician safety during maintenance execution.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation, standard, or rule that mandates or relates to this bulletin (e.g., FMCSA 49 CFR 393.55, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147, EPA 40 CFR Part 63). Used for compliance reporting in Enviance EHS and regulatory audit documentation.']
  required_technician_certification varchar [note: 'Certification or qualification required for the technician performing the bulletin corrective action (e.g., ASE Master Technician, CNG Fuel System Certified, CDL Class B, OEM-specific certification). Used for technician assignment in SAP PM and Workday HCM workforce planning.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier of the bulletin as assigned by the issuing organization (e.g., Rev A, Rev 1.1). Tracks iterative updates to bulletin content, corrective action procedures, or affected asset scope.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bulletin addresses a safety-critical defect or condition (True) that could result in injury, accident, or regulatory violation if not addressed. Safety-critical bulletins trigger immediate fleet grounding review and OSHA/DOT notification workflows.']
  source_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system path to the original bulletin PDF or technical document as issued by the OEM or regulatory body. Stored in Infor EAM document management or SAP DMS for technician reference during work order execution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this bulletin record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., Infor EAM, SAP PM, Enviance EHS, OEM portal, or manual entry). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver layer reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  superseded_by_bulletin_number varchar [note: 'Bulletin number of the newer revision that replaces this bulletin when bulletin_status is \'superseded\'. Enables traceability of bulletin revision history and ensures technicians reference the most current corrective action procedures.']
  total_affected_assets varchar [note: 'Count of fleet assets within Waste Management\'s fleet that are identified as affected by this bulletin, as determined at the time of bulletin intake. Used for compliance planning, resource allocation, and executive reporting on recall exposure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this service bulletin record in the Waste Management lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the OEM for the warranty or recall reimbursement claim associated with this bulletin. Populated when oem_warranty_covered_flag is True. Used for accounts receivable tracking and OEM reimbursement reconciliation.']

  Note: 'OEM and internal service bulletins and technical service notices (TSBs) issued for fleet vehicles and equipment. Captures bulletin number, issuing organization (OEM, internal engineering, DOT), bulletin title, affected asset make/model/year range, affected component, bulletin type (safety recall, recommended repair, informational), issue date, compliance deadline, mandatory flag, estimated labor hours, estimated parts cost, and completion tracking status. Ensures fleet compliance with manufacturer recalls and regulatory safety notices.'
}

Table maintenance.bulletin_compliance {
  bulletin_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bulletin compliance tracking record in the Waste Management fleet and facility asset compliance system.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified technician who performed the bulletin compliance work. Must hold applicable Commercial Driver License (CDL) or DOT-required certifications for the asset type.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fleet vehicle or facility asset subject to the service bulletin or safety recall compliance requirement.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Bulletin compliance may be required by permits (emissions system updates, safety modifications required by operating permits). Link enables tracking permit-mandated bulletin completion, demonstrating ']
  service_bulletin_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.service_bulletin. Business justification: Each bulletin_compliance record tracks compliance status for a specific service bulletin. Currently has bulletin_number (STRING) and duplicates bulletin master data. Adding FK enables JOIN to service_']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Service bulletin compliance tracks recall and safety campaign completion by vehicle. Fleet compliance managers need vehicle→bulletin linkage for regulatory reporting and safety risk management. Vehicl']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Work Order (WO) created in SAP PM or Infor EAM to execute the corrective or preventive action required by the service bulletin or recall.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the bulletin compliance corrective action was physically completed and verified on the asset. Populated when compliance_status transitions to \'completed\'.']
  actual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to complete the bulletin compliance action for this asset, including labor, parts, and any external service costs. Feeds into SAP FI/CO for cost center reporting and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual number of technician labor hours expended to complete the bulletin compliance corrective action. Compared against estimated_labor_hours for maintenance efficiency analysis and future bulletin cost estimation.']
  affected_component varchar [note: 'The specific vehicle or equipment component addressed by the service bulletin or recall (e.g., \'Hydraulic Lift System\', \'CNG Fuel Line\', \'Brake Actuator\', \'Packer Body\'). Used for maintenance planning and parts procurement.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable asset identifier (e.g., vehicle unit number, equipment tag) as maintained in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Enables field technicians and fleet managers to identify the physical asset without requiring system ID lookups.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset subject to the bulletin compliance requirement. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (e.g., DOT for collection vehicles, EPA for facility assets). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collection_vehicle|transfer_vehicle|support_equipment|facility_asset|container — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the bulletin compliance work was marked complete in the CMMS system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Provides audit-grade precision beyond the completion date for regulatory reporting.']
  compliance_deadline_date date [note: 'The mandatory or recommended deadline by which the bulletin compliance action must be completed for this asset. Drives prioritization in fleet-wide recall compliance reporting and regulatory audit readiness.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of this asset against the applicable service bulletin or safety recall. \'pending\' = action required but not yet scheduled; \'scheduled\' = work order created with a planned date; \'in_progress\' = work order actively being executed; \'completed\' = corrective action verified complete; \'not_applicable\' = bulletin does not apply to this asset configuration; \'waived\' = compliance formally waived with documented justification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bulletin compliance tracking record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record creation and supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  dot_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this bulletin compliance action requires a formal DOT inspection or re-inspection of the asset before it can return to service. True = DOT inspection mandatory post-completion.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost (labor + parts + external services) to complete the bulletin compliance action for this asset. Used for maintenance budget planning and fleet-wide recall cost impact analysis.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of technician labor hours required to complete the bulletin compliance corrective action. Used for workforce planning, work order scheduling, and maintenance cost estimation.']
  make varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand of the asset (e.g., Mack, Peterbilt, Heil, McNeilus). Used to filter applicable bulletins by manufacturer and to cross-reference with OEM service bulletin databases.']
  model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the asset as specified by the manufacturer (e.g., LR Electric, Granite, Python 1000). Used in conjunction with make and model year to determine bulletin applicability.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year of the asset as assigned by the manufacturer (e.g., 2019, 2022). Critical for determining whether a specific service bulletin or recall applies to this asset\'s production run.']
  not_applicable_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason why the service bulletin or recall does not apply to this specific asset (e.g., \'Different engine configuration\', \'Component not installed\', \'Asset retired before bulletin issue date\'). Required when compliance_status is \'not_applicable\' for audit defensibility.']
  out_of_service_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset must be taken out of service immediately pending completion of the bulletin compliance action. True = asset must not be operated until compliance is achieved. Drives fleet availability and route planning impacts.']
  parts_availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the parts required to complete the bulletin compliance action. Drives scheduling decisions when parts_required_flag is True. \'backordered\' status may justify compliance deadline extension requests.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replacement parts are required to complete the bulletin compliance action. True = parts must be procured before work can be scheduled. Drives parts inventory and procurement planning in Infor EAM.']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity classification of the bulletin as assessed by the issuing authority or Waste Management\'s EHS team. \'critical\' = immediate safety hazard requiring out-of-service action; \'high\' = significant risk requiring urgent scheduling; \'medium\' = moderate risk; \'low\' = minor risk; \'informational\' = no immediate safety risk.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'The planned date on which the bulletin compliance work is scheduled to be performed for this asset. Populated when compliance_status transitions to \'scheduled\' or \'in_progress\'.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this bulletin compliance record originated (e.g., Infor EAM for CMMS-driven records, SAP PM for work order-driven records, Enviance EHS for regulatory directive-driven records, manual for paper-based entries).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (Infor EAM compliance record ID, SAP PM notification number, or Enviance EHS incident ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer data product and the system of record.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the technician during or after performing the bulletin compliance work. May include observations about asset condition, deviations from bulletin instructions, or additional defects found during the repair.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bulletin compliance tracking record was last modified. Supports incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer and provides audit trail for compliance status changes.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the completed bulletin compliance work was formally verified and signed off by the authorized verifier. Provides audit-grade timestamp for regulatory compliance records.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the supervisor, quality inspector, or fleet manager who verified and signed off on the completed bulletin compliance work. Required for DOT inspection records and regulatory audit readiness.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized manager or EHS officer who approved the compliance waiver. Required for regulatory audit trail when waiver_flag is True.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the compliance waiver expires, after which the asset must achieve compliance or obtain a renewed waiver. Enables proactive monitoring of expiring waivers in fleet compliance dashboards.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal waiver has been granted for this asset\'s compliance with the service bulletin or recall. True = waiver approved; waiver_justification must be populated. Waivers require documented approval per regulatory audit requirements.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Documented business or technical justification for waiving compliance with the service bulletin or recall for this asset (e.g., \'Asset scheduled for retirement within 30 days\', \'Component not installed on this configuration\'). Required when waiver_flag is True for regulatory audit readiness.']
  warranty_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bulletin compliance corrective action is covered under the manufacturer\'s warranty or recall program, meaning costs will be reimbursed by the OEM. True = OEM warranty/recall reimbursement applicable.']

  Note: 'Tracks the compliance status of individual assets against applicable service bulletins and safety recalls. Captures service bulletin reference, asset reference, compliance status (pending, scheduled, completed, not-applicable, waived), scheduled completion date, actual completion date, associated work order reference, technician who performed the work, and waiver justification if applicable. Enables fleet-wide recall compliance reporting and regulatory audit readiness.'
}

Table maintenance.subcontract_work {
  subcontract_work_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subcontracted maintenance work record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Major boiler overhauls, tube replacements, and refractory repairs are often subcontracted and must be tracked at the unit level for cost management, warranty tracking, and outage planning.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Subcontracted maintenance work is performed under master service agreements or hauling agreements with third-party vendors. Real business process: contract spend tracking against negotiated rates, ven']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Third-party maintenance on emission sources (annual flare stack testing, LFG system optimization, compressor overhauls) must be documented for permit compliance. Subcontractors perform specialized emi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee (maintenance manager or supervisor) who authorized the subcontract work engagement. Required for spend approval audit trail.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset on which the subcontracted maintenance work is being performed. Supports asset lifecycle and repair history tracking.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Subcontracted maintenance services (specialized repairs, hazmat handling) performed for customers must be invoiced. Links vendor costs to customer billing for markup and cost recovery tracking, essent']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Subcontractor work requires JHA review and approval per OSHA multi-employer citation doctrine. Real business need: Waste Management is responsible for contractor safety; must document JHA completion a']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Subcontracted maintenance work at landfill sites (specialized gas system repairs, leachate treatment equipment service) must be tracked by site for cost allocation, regulatory compliance documentation']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Subcontracted maintenance work often requires permit coverage (asbestos abatement, hazardous waste handling, air quality work). Subcontracts must reference applicable permits to ensure contractor comp']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement purchase order (PO) issued to the vendor authorizing the subcontracted maintenance work. Links to the AP/procurement process.']
  subcontractor_transporter_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.transporter_registration. Business justification: Subcontractors generating or transporting hazardous waste must have verified EPA transporter registration for compliance. Essential for contractor qualification verification, regulatory compliance, an']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Turbine major inspections, generator rewinds, and rotor balancing are typically subcontracted vendor work requiring unit-level tracking for cost control and performance guarantee verification.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: Subcontracted maintenance work should be classified by standardized maintenance type for consistent cost tracking and vendor performance analysis. Currently has work_type (STRING) which should referen']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Subcontracted work tracks external vendor services by vehicle (body repairs, specialized equipment). Fleet procurement managers need vehicle→subcontract linkage for vendor performance analysis and cos']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external vendor or specialty repair shop performing the subcontracted maintenance work. Used for vendor performance management and spend analysis.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent maintenance work order (WO) that triggered this subcontracted service engagement. Links subcontract activity back to the originating WO in the CMMS.']
  actual_invoiced_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount invoiced by the vendor upon completion of the subcontracted maintenance work. Compared against quoted cost to identify cost overruns and support three-way match in SAP AP.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the subcontract work authorization. Controls whether work may proceed and whether invoices may be processed. Values: pending, approved, rejected, cancelled.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset category on which subcontracted work is performed. Distinguishes fleet vehicles (ASL, FEL, REL trucks) from facility equipment, containers, or infrastructure assets.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which the subcontracted maintenance expense is charged. Enables departmental cost allocation, EBITDA reporting, and maintenance budget tracking.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual invoiced cost and quoted cost (actual_invoiced_cost minus quoted_cost). Positive value indicates cost overrun; negative indicates savings. Supports vendor performance management and budget control.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subcontract work record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports SOX compliance audit requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this subcontract work record (quoted cost, invoiced cost). Typically USD for domestic operations.']
  dot_inspection_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontracted work is related to a DOT vehicle inspection, safety defect correction, or regulatory compliance repair. True flags the record for DOT compliance reporting.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the asset was out of service due to the subcontracted maintenance work. Used for fleet availability KPI reporting, route impact analysis, and maintenance cost-of-downtime calculations.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of equipment failure or defect that necessitated the subcontracted repair. Supports failure mode analysis and reliability engineering.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code for posting the subcontracted maintenance expense in SAP FI/CO. Ensures correct financial classification of external maintenance spend.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date on the vendor\'s invoice for the subcontracted maintenance work. Used for AP payment terms calculation, aging analysis, and financial period accruals.']
  make_vs_buy_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the business reason for subcontracting this maintenance work rather than performing it in-house. Supports make-vs-buy analysis and strategic workforce planning. Values: specialty_skill, capacity_overflow, emergency, cost_efficiency, oem_required.']
  parts_supplied_by varchar [note: 'Indicates whether parts and materials for the subcontracted repair were supplied by the external vendor, by Waste Management from internal inventory, or shared between both parties. Affects cost allocation and BOM tracking.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'The externally communicated purchase order (PO) number issued to the vendor for this subcontracted work. Used for invoice matching, three-way match, and AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  promised_completion_date date [note: 'The date the vendor committed to completing the subcontracted work at time of engagement. Compared against actual completion date to measure vendor on-time delivery performance.']
  quality_acceptance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the completed subcontracted work passed internal quality inspection and was formally accepted. False triggers rework requests or dispute initiation.']
  quoted_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The vendor\'s quoted price for the subcontracted maintenance work prior to work commencement. Used as the baseline for budget approval, make-vs-buy analysis, and variance tracking against actual invoiced cost.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subcontracted work is required to meet a specific regulatory compliance obligation (EPA, OSHA, RCRA, DOT). True requires associated compliance documentation.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance work scope assigned to the external vendor. Includes specific repair tasks, components to be serviced, and expected deliverables. Sourced from Infor EAM service order description.']
  service_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the external vendor completed and delivered the subcontracted maintenance work. Used to calculate turnaround time, asset downtime duration, and vendor on-time performance.']
  service_location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates where the subcontracted maintenance work was physically performed. Distinguishes on-site repairs at Waste Management facilities from work performed at the vendor\'s shop, dealer, or via mobile service unit.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the external vendor commenced the subcontracted maintenance work at the site or shop. Used for scheduling, downtime tracking, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this subcontract work record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Infor EAM and SAP PM in the Silver Layer.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this subcontract work record in the originating operational system (Infor EAM service order ID or SAP PM service entry sheet number). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  subcontract_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for the subcontract work record. Used in vendor communications, PO references, and invoice matching. Format: SC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the internal maintenance technician or supervisor regarding the subcontracted work, including observations at handoff, acceptance inspection findings, or follow-up actions required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subcontract work record. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vendor_completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes provided by the external vendor upon completion of the subcontracted work, describing work performed, parts replaced, and any recommendations. Captured in Infor EAM service entry.']
  vendor_performance_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric rating (1.0 to 5.0 scale) assigned to the vendor for this subcontract work engagement, evaluating quality, timeliness, and cost adherence. Feeds vendor performance management and future sourcing decisions.']
  vendor_reference_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s own internal reference or job number for this subcontracted work engagement. Used for invoice reconciliation and vendor communication tracking.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a warranty claim has been filed against the vendor for this subcontracted repair work. True if a warranty claim is active or has been submitted.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor\'s repair warranty expires. Enables proactive warranty claim management before expiration and supports asset maintenance planning.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Description of the warranty terms provided by the vendor on the completed subcontracted repair work, including duration, coverage scope, and conditions. Supports warranty claim tracking and vendor accountability.']
  work_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subcontracted maintenance work execution. Tracks progression from open engagement through completion, invoicing, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|completed|invoiced|closed|disputed — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records maintenance work subcontracted to external vendors or specialty repair shops when in-house technician capacity or capability is insufficient. Captures work order reference, vendor/supplier reference, scope of work description, PO number, quoted cost, actual invoiced cost, service start date, service completion date, warranty terms on repair, vendor performance rating, and approval status. Supports make-vs-buy analysis and vendor performance management for outsourced maintenance.'
}

Table maintenance.warranty_claim {
  warranty_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the warranty claim record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.adjustment. Business justification: Approved warranty claims on customer equipment result in billing adjustments (credits) when original repair was invoiced. Enables automated customer credit processing when OEM reimburses warranty work']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Warranty claims reference the original equipment purchase contract or service agreement under which warranty coverage was provided. Real business process: warranty cost recovery tracking, contract-bas']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance technician or warranty administrator who submitted the claim to the OEM or supplier. Links to the workforce/employee master in Workday HCM.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset on which the failed component was installed. Links to the asset master in Infor EAM.']
  parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the failed component or part in the parts master catalog (Bill of Materials). Links to the parts inventory managed in Infor EAM and SAP MM.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Warranty claims on emissions/environmental equipment must track permit compliance (failed emissions controls, permit-required monitoring equipment). Link enables demonstrating equipment failures didn\'']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Warranty claims track manufacturer warranty recovery by vehicle. Fleet financial managers need vehicle→warranty linkage for cost recovery tracking and OEM performance analysis. Asset_number is denorma']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OEM manufacturer or parts supplier against whom the warranty claim is filed. Links to the vendor/supplier master in SAP MM.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order that identified the defective component or premature failure triggering this warranty claim. Links to maintenance.work_order.']
  appeal_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal appeal was submitted to the OEM or supplier for a denied or partially approved claim. Populated only when appeal_flag is True.']
  appeal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a denied or partially approved warranty claim has been formally appealed to the OEM or supplier. Enables tracking of appeal activity and outcomes for supplier quality management.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the formal appeal submitted to the OEM or supplier. Tracks whether the original denial was upheld, fully overturned, or partially overturned following appeal review.']
  approved_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary amount approved by the OEM or supplier for reimbursement. May differ from the claimed amount due to partial approvals, labor rate caps, or disputed items.']
  asset_meter_at_failure decimal(12,2) [note: 'The odometer or hour-meter reading of the asset at the time of component failure. Used to validate warranty coverage based on mileage or operating hour limits (e.g., 100,000-mile powertrain warranty).']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable claim reference number assigned at submission to the OEM manufacturer or parts supplier. Used for all correspondence and tracking with the counterparty. Sourced from Infor EAM warranty module.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the warranty claim. Tracks progression from initial submission through OEM/supplier review, approval or denial, and final payment or closure.']
  claimed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross monetary amount submitted to the OEM or supplier for reimbursement, including labor costs, parts costs, and any associated repair expenses. Represents the total recovery sought.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost center code to which the warranty claim recovery will be credited upon reimbursement. Enables financial tracking of warranty cost recovery by business unit or operational area.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warranty agreement under which the claim is filed. Distinguishes OEM vehicle warranty, OEM parts warranty, extended warranty contracts, supplier parts warranty, or service contracts. Drives claim routing and recovery expectations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty claim record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this claim (claimed, approved, reimbursed). Typically USD for domestic operations. Required for multi-currency financial reporting.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for claim denial or partial denial by the OEM or supplier (e.g., out-of-warranty, improper installation, normal wear). Supports supplier quality analysis and denial pattern tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_warranty|improper_installation|normal_wear|missing_documentation|coverage_exclusion|duplicate_claim|other — promote to reference product]']
  dot_inspection_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component failure was identified during or is related to a DOT (Department of Transportation) vehicle inspection. Supports regulatory compliance tracking and DOT out-of-service event correlation.']
  failed_component_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the specific component or assembly that failed (e.g., \'hydraulic pump\', \'CNG fuel injector\', \'compactor blade\'). Complements the part number for claim documentation.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized failure mode code from the maintenance failure code catalog (e.g., VMRS codes for fleet components). Enables structured failure analysis and supplier quality reporting.']
  failure_date date [note: 'The date on which the component failure or defect was first observed or reported. Used to validate that the failure occurred within the warranty coverage period.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the component failure or defect, including observed symptoms, conditions at time of failure, and any diagnostic findings. Supports OEM claim evaluation and root cause analysis.']
  labor_cost_claimed decimal(12,2) [note: 'The portion of the claimed amount attributable to technician labor hours expended on diagnosing and repairing the failed component. Supports detailed cost recovery analysis by cost category.']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the asset meter reading at failure (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for equipment hour meter). Required to correctly interpret the meter reading for warranty eligibility validation.']
  oem_claim_reference varchar [note: 'The claim reference or RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) number assigned by the OEM manufacturer or parts supplier upon receipt of the claim. Used for external tracking and correspondence.']
  oem_response_date date [note: 'The date on which the OEM manufacturer or parts supplier provided their formal response (approval, denial, or request for additional information) to the submitted claim.']
  oem_response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or rationale provided by the OEM or supplier in their claim response, including reasons for denial, partial approval justification, or conditions for reimbursement.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or internal part number of the failed component. Denormalized for claim correspondence and supplier communication without requiring a join to the parts master.']
  part_returned_date date [note: 'The date on which the failed component was physically returned to the OEM or supplier. Populated only when return_required_flag is True. Used to confirm return compliance and trigger reimbursement.']
  parts_cost_claimed decimal(12,2) [note: 'The portion of the claimed amount attributable to replacement parts and materials used in the repair of the failed component. Supports detailed cost recovery analysis by cost category.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The SAP purchase order number associated with the original procurement of the failed component. Used to validate warranty eligibility based on purchase date and supplier contract terms.']
  reimbursed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual monetary amount received from the OEM or supplier as reimbursement. May differ from the approved amount if partial payments were made. Used for accounts receivable reconciliation.']
  reimbursement_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the reimbursement payment was received from the OEM or supplier. Used for cash flow tracking, accounts receivable aging, and warranty recovery performance reporting.']
  return_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OEM or supplier requires the failed component to be physically returned as a condition of claim approval. Drives parts return logistics and core charge management.']
  source_claim_reference varchar [note: 'The native claim identifier from the originating source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the operational system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system from which this warranty claim record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between Infor EAM, SAP PM, and any manually entered claims.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the warranty claim was formally submitted to the OEM manufacturer or parts supplier. Used to measure claim submission timeliness and track aging.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty claim record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to claim status, amounts, or OEM response details.']
  warranty_agreement_number varchar [note: 'The warranty contract or agreement number under which coverage is claimed. References the specific OEM vehicle warranty, extended warranty contract, or supplier parts warranty agreement.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the applicable warranty coverage expires. Used to validate claim eligibility and confirm the failure occurred within the coverage window.']

  Note: 'Warranty claims submitted to OEM manufacturers or parts suppliers for defective components or premature failures covered under warranty agreements. Captures claim number, asset reference, failed component, failure date, failure description, warranty coverage type (OEM vehicle, parts, extended), claim submission date, claim amount, OEM/supplier response, approved reimbursement amount, reimbursement received date, and claim status (submitted, approved, denied, paid). Enables warranty cost recovery tracking and supplier quality management.'
}

Table maintenance.fluid_sample {
  fluid_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each oil and fluid analysis sample record in the predictive maintenance program. Primary key for the fluid_sample data product.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Boiler water chemistry samples must be tracked at the unit level for condition monitoring, corrosion prevention, and maintaining boiler efficiency per ASME guidelines and insurance requirements.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset from which the fluid sample was collected. Links to the asset master record in Infor EAM for asset lifecycle context.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Fluid samples from equipment operating at landfill sites should be tracked by site for equipment health monitoring and site-specific maintenance program effectiveness analysis. Enables site-level pred']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Fluid sampling programs are often permit-required (groundwater monitoring, stormwater sampling, underground storage tank testing). Samples must reference the driving permit for compliance demonstratio']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule that triggered this fluid sample collection. Links to the PM cycle definition for condition-based maintenance program tracking.']
  primary_fluid_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with the fluid sample collection event. Links to the work_order product for operational context and cost tracking.']
  tclp_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.tclp_test. Business justification: Oil analysis samples may require TCLP testing to determine if used fluids are hazardous waste, affecting disposal method and cost. Critical for waste characterization decisions, regulatory compliance,']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Turbine lubricating oil analysis requires unit-level tracking for predictive maintenance, bearing wear monitoring, and determining optimal oil change intervals to prevent catastrophic failures.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Fluid samples track oil analysis results by vehicle for predictive maintenance. Fleet maintenance planners need vehicle→fluid sample linkage for engine health monitoring and PM optimization. Asset_num']
  aluminum_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Concentration of aluminum wear particles in the fluid sample, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated aluminum indicates wear in pistons, bearings, or pump housings.']
  component_sampled varchar [note: 'The specific fluid system or component from which the sample was drawn. Identifies the type of fluid analyzed (e.g., engine oil, transmission fluid, hydraulic fluid, coolant, Diesel Exhaust Fluid (DEF)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine_oil|transmission_fluid|hydraulic_fluid|coolant|def|differential_fluid|gear_oil — promote to reference product]']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Overall fluid and component condition rating assigned by the laboratory based on analysis results. Drives maintenance urgency: \'normal\' = no action required, \'caution\' = monitor closely or schedule service, \'critical\' = immediate maintenance action required.']
  copper_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Concentration of copper wear particles in the fluid sample, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated copper indicates wear in bearings, bushings, thrust washers, or cooler cores.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fluid sample record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement for sample processing workflows.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Total engine operating hours recorded at the time of fluid sample collection. Used for equipment that tracks maintenance intervals by hours rather than mileage (e.g., off-road equipment, generators, compactors).']
  fluid_brand varchar [note: 'Brand or trade name of the fluid currently in service at the time of sampling (e.g., Shell Rotella, Mobil Delvac, Chevron Delo). Used to correlate fluid performance across brands and validate approved fluid usage.']
  fluid_grade varchar [note: 'SAE viscosity grade or specification of the fluid in service at the time of sampling (e.g., 15W-40, 5W-30, Dexron VI). Used to validate correct fluid specification and interpret viscosity analysis results.']
  fluid_hours_in_service decimal(10,1) [note: 'Number of operating hours the sampled fluid has been in service since the last fluid change. Key metric for assessing fluid degradation relative to OEM-recommended change intervals.']
  fluid_miles_in_service decimal(10,1) [note: 'Number of miles the sampled fluid has been in service since the last fluid change. Used alongside fluid_hours_in_service to assess oil life for on-road fleet vehicles.']
  fuel_dilution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of fuel contamination detected in the engine oil sample. Fuel dilution reduces oil viscosity and lubricating film strength, indicating injector leaks, ring blow-by, or short-trip operation patterns.']
  iron_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Concentration of iron wear particles in the fluid sample, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated iron levels indicate wear in cylinder liners, crankshafts, camshafts, or gears depending on the component sampled.']
  lab_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative comments provided by the laboratory analyst interpreting the fluid analysis results. May include trend observations, anomaly explanations, and specific component wear hypotheses.']
  lab_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external fluid analysis laboratory that processed the sample (e.g., Polaris Laboratories, Analysts Inc., Blackstone Laboratories). Used for vendor performance tracking and result traceability.']
  lab_received_date date [note: 'Date the fluid sample was received and logged by the external analysis laboratory. Used to calculate turnaround time and monitor laboratory service level performance.']
  lab_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the external fluid analysis laboratory to this sample submission. Used to cross-reference laboratory reports, chain-of-custody documentation, and result retrieval.']
  lead_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Concentration of lead wear particles in the fluid sample, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated lead indicates bearing overlay wear, particularly in connecting rod and main bearings.']
  odometer_miles decimal(10,1) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading in miles at the time the fluid sample was collected. Used to track fluid change intervals, correlate wear metal trends with mileage, and schedule condition-based maintenance.']
  particle_count_iso varchar [note: 'ISO 4406 cleanliness code representing the particle count per milliliter at 4, 6, and 14 micron thresholds (e.g., \'18/16/13\'). Used to assess hydraulic fluid cleanliness and filter effectiveness.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Maintenance action recommended by the laboratory based on fluid analysis findings (e.g., \'Change oil at next service\', \'Resample in 5,000 miles\', \'Immediate engine inspection required\'). Drives work order creation and PM schedule adjustments.']
  resample_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which a follow-up fluid sample should be collected, as recommended by the laboratory or maintenance planner. Populated when resample_required is True.']
  resample_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory or maintenance team has determined that a follow-up fluid sample is required to confirm findings, monitor a developing condition, or validate corrective action effectiveness.']
  results_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the fluid analysis results were received from the laboratory and made available for maintenance decision-making. Used to measure laboratory turnaround time and SLA compliance.']
  sample_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to extract the fluid sample from the component. Affects result interpretation as live-zone sampling provides more representative contamination data than drain-plug sampling.']
  sample_collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the fluid sample was collected from the asset. Provides higher granularity than sample_date for operational sequencing and integration with telematics data from Geotab.']
  sample_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the fluid sample was physically collected from the asset. Used as the principal business event date for trending, scheduling, and compliance reporting.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable identifier assigned to the fluid sample at time of collection. Used for cross-referencing with laboratory reports, work orders, and Infor EAM sample records. Typically formatted as a lab-assigned or field-assigned alphanumeric code.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fluid sample record, tracking progression from collection through laboratory analysis to closure. Drives workflow actions and follow-up maintenance decisions.']
  silicon_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Concentration of silicon in the fluid sample, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated silicon indicates dirt/dust ingestion through air filtration failures or gasket sealant contamination, accelerating abrasive wear.']
  soot_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of soot (combustion byproduct) measured in the engine oil sample. Elevated soot levels indicate incomplete combustion, EGR system issues, or extended oil drain intervals in diesel fleet engines.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which the fluid sample record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., Infor EAM, SAP PM, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the maintenance technician to record observations at the time of sample collection, such as visible fluid discoloration, unusual odor, or known recent repairs that may affect results.']
  total_acid_number decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total Acid Number (TAN) of the sampled fluid in mg KOH/g. Measures fluid oxidation and acidic degradation byproducts. Rising TAN indicates fluid life depletion and increased corrosion risk to engine components.']
  total_base_number decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total Base Number (TBN) of the sampled fluid in mg KOH/g. Measures remaining alkaline reserve in engine oil that neutralizes combustion acids. Declining TBN indicates oil life depletion and need for oil change.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fluid sample record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source systems, and audit compliance.']
  viscosity_cst_100 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Kinematic viscosity of the sampled fluid measured at 100 degrees Celsius, expressed in centistokes (cSt). Used to assess viscosity index and high-temperature fluid performance for engine and transmission fluids.']
  viscosity_cst_40 decimal(8,3) [note: 'Kinematic viscosity of the sampled fluid measured at 40 degrees Celsius, expressed in centistokes (cSt). Deviation from OEM specification indicates fluid degradation, contamination, or incorrect fluid grade.']
  water_content_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Water contamination level in the sampled fluid, expressed in parts per million (ppm). Elevated water content indicates coolant leaks, condensation ingress, or seal failures that can cause corrosion and accelerated wear.']

  Note: 'Oil and fluid analysis sample records supporting predictive maintenance programs for fleet engines, transmissions, and hydraulic systems. Captures sample ID, asset reference, component sampled (engine oil, transmission fluid, hydraulic fluid, coolant, DEF), sample date, mileage/hours at sample, laboratory reference, analysis results (viscosity, contamination levels, wear metals ppm, water content), condition assessment (normal, caution, critical), recommended action, and follow-up work order reference. Enables condition-based maintenance decisions.'
}

Table maintenance.facility_maintenance_request {
  facility_maintenance_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the facility maintenance request record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  emergency_response_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.emergency_response_incident. Business justification: Maintenance requests triggered by hazmat spills or releases must link to emergency response incidents for root cause analysis, corrective action tracking, and regulatory reporting. Required for incide']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed facility (transfer station, MRF building, landfill infrastructure, WTE plant, or administrative office) for which maintenance is requested. Links to the facility master record.']
  failure_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Facility maintenance requests should reference standardized failure modes for consistent problem classification and prioritization. Currently has failure_code (STRING) which should be replaced with FK']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fixed asset or equipment item within the facility that requires maintenance (e.g., conveyor belt, compactor, HVAC unit, leachate pump). Null if the request is for a structural/civil element rather than a tracked asset.']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Facility maintenance work requires Job Hazard Analysis before execution per OSHA requirements. Real business process: pre-work safety planning for confined space entry, elevated work, hazmat handling.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Maintenance requests for landfill site infrastructure (scale houses, site roads, fencing, environmental monitoring equipment) should link to specific landfill sites for site-specific maintenance track']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost center to which the maintenance costs for this request will be charged. Determines financial accountability and enables cost allocation reporting by facility, department, or business unit.']
