  parent_request_facility_maintenance_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent facility maintenance request when this request is a follow-up, sub-task, or related recurrence of a prior request. Enables request lineage tracking and recurring issue analysis.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Some facility maintenance requests are driven by permit conditions or compliance requirements. This link enables tracking of permit-mandated maintenance and ensures compliance obligations are met thro']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule that triggered this request, if applicable. Null for corrective or emergency requests. Links demand-side intake to the PM plan for schedule compliance tracking.']
  primary_facility_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who submitted the maintenance request. Used for accountability, communication, and follow-up notifications. Sourced from Workday HCM employee master.']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Facility maintenance at customer sites (compactor repair, container pad maintenance, scale calibration) is tied to service offerings for billing, cost allocation, and SLA compliance. Real WM operation']
  transfer_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.transfer_station. Business justification: Facility maintenance requests at transfer stations (tipping floor repairs, scale house HVAC, compactor issues) must reference which transfer station for work order routing, facility-specific maintenan']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order created to fulfill this maintenance request. Populated after WO creation; null while request is in pre-approval or pending state. Links the demand-side intake to the execution-side WO.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance work was actually completed and the request was resolved. Used for SLA compliance measurement, on-time performance reporting, and backlog aging analysis.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the maintenance request was formally approved for work order creation. Used for approval cycle time analysis and audit trail.']
  approved_completion_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the maintenance supervisor or planner as the target completion date after reviewing resource availability and priority. May differ from the requestor\'s requested completion date. Drives SLA compliance measurement.']
  confined_space_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance work requires entry into a permit-required confined space (e.g., leachate collection vault, underground utility vault, tank). Triggers confined space entry permit workflow per OSHA standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility maintenance request record was first created in the system of record (Infor EAM). Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data governance and lineage.']
  downtime_expected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance work is expected to require taking the affected asset or facility area out of service (downtime). Used for operational planning, production scheduling, and capacity impact assessment.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance issue has potential environmental impact such as leachate containment breach, LFG leak, chemical spill, or stormwater system failure. When true, triggers Enviance EHS notification and EPA/state regulatory reporting workflow.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Preliminary cost estimate for completing the requested maintenance work, including labor and parts. Used for budget approval routing, capital vs. expense classification, and maintenance budget forecasting. Expressed in USD.']
  estimated_downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of hours the affected asset or facility area will be out of service to complete the maintenance work. Used for operational impact planning and throughput loss estimation. Populated when downtime_expected_flag is true.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fixed facility for which maintenance is requested. Drives applicable regulatory requirements, maintenance standards, and routing rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transfer_station|mrf|landfill|wte_plant|admin_office|maintenance_shop|fueling_station — promote to reference product]']
  location_within_facility varchar [note: 'Specific area, zone, building section, floor, room, or equipment bay within the facility where the maintenance issue is located (e.g., \'Tipping Floor Bay 2\', \'Compactor Room\', \'Cell 4 Leachate Pump Station\'). Enables precise technician dispatch.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required to safely perform the maintenance work. When true, LOTO permit must be issued before WO execution. Critical for OSHA compliance.']
  problem_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the requestor describing the maintenance issue, observed symptoms, and any relevant context (e.g., \'Conveyor belt on tipping floor making grinding noise and slipping under load since Monday morning\'). Primary input for WO creation.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance request represents a recurring issue that has been reported previously for the same asset or location. When true, triggers root cause analysis workflow and may escalate priority. Supports reliability improvement programs.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance request is related to a regulatory compliance requirement such as a permit condition, DOT inspection finding, EPA consent order, or OSHA citation. When true, resolution is tracked against regulatory deadlines.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the approver when a maintenance request is rejected or returned to the requestor. Populated only when request_status is \'rejected\'. Supports process improvement and requestor feedback.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the maintenance request was formally submitted. Used as the principal business event date for SLA calculation, aging analysis, and backlog reporting.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable request identifier assigned at intake (e.g., FMR-2024-000123). Used for cross-system reference, verbal communication, and audit trails. Sourced from Infor EAM service request numbering sequence.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance request from initial submission through resolution. Drives workflow routing and SLA tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|rejected — promote to reference product]']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the maintenance request was submitted, including timezone offset. Used for exact SLA elapsed-time calculations and audit trail.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance request by nature of need. Corrective addresses existing failures; preventive is scheduled upkeep; emergency requires immediate response; safety and environmental types trigger regulatory notification workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrective|preventive|emergency|inspection|safety|environmental|capital_improvement — promote to reference product]']
  requested_completion_date date [note: 'Date by which the requestor needs the maintenance work to be completed, based on operational or regulatory requirements. Used as input to WO scheduling and SLA target-setting. May differ from the approved scheduled completion date.']
  requestor_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit of the employee who submitted the request (e.g., Landfill Operations, Fleet Maintenance, Environmental Compliance). Used for cost allocation and reporting.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who submitted the maintenance request, captured at time of submission for historical reference even if the employee record changes. Stored as a denormalized snapshot.']
  requestor_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the requestor for follow-up communication regarding the maintenance request status or clarification of problem details.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of how the maintenance issue was resolved, including corrective actions taken, root cause identified, and any follow-up recommendations. Populated upon request closure. Supports knowledge management and recurring issue analysis.']
  safety_hazard_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reported maintenance issue presents an immediate or potential safety hazard to personnel or the public. When true, triggers expedited review and OSHA notification workflow per safety management protocols.']
  source_request_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this maintenance request in the originating source system (e.g., Infor EAM service request ID, SAP PM notification number). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this maintenance request originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this facility maintenance request record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Requestor-assigned urgency classification indicating the business impact and speed of response required. Critical indicates immediate safety or operational risk; high indicates same-day response needed; medium and low allow scheduled response. Drives SLA target assignment.']

  Note: 'Maintenance requests submitted for fixed facilities including transfer stations, MRF buildings, landfill infrastructure, WTE plants, and administrative offices. Captures request number, facility reference, requestor name/department, request date, problem description, location within facility, urgency level, requested completion date, assigned work order reference, and resolution status. Distinct from fleet work orders — this captures the demand-side intake for facility maintenance needs before WO creation.'
}

Table maintenance.meter_reading {
  meter_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each meter reading record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the meter_reading data product.']
  boiler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.boiler_unit. Business justification: Boiler operating hour meters and steam flow meters must be tracked at the unit level for PM scheduling, performance monitoring, and calculating unit-level availability factors.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or depot where the meter reading was recorded or where the asset is based. Relevant for facility equipment meters (e.g., MRF balers, WTE turbines, landfill gas engines) and for fleet vehicles domiciled at a specific yard.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility equipment asset for which this meter reading was recorded. Links to the asset master in Infor EAM and SAP PM.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Meter readings may be permit-required (operating hours for air permits, throughput for waste permits, usage for water discharge permits). Readings must track permit compliance, support regulatory repo']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that this meter reading is associated with or triggers. Used to calculate next-due dates for meter-based PM cycles.']
  primary_meter_reader_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who recorded or submitted this meter reading. Applicable for manual entry readings; null for automated telematics or sensor sources.']
  turbine_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.turbine_generator. Business justification: Generator operating hours and MWh production meters require unit-level tracking for maintenance triggers, performance trending, and reconciling utility settlement data with internal generation records']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Meter readings track odometer and engine hours by vehicle for PM triggering and utilization analysis. Fleet operations managers need vehicle→meter reading linkage for maintenance scheduling and asset ']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Work Order (WO) during which this meter reading was captured, if applicable. Links meter readings taken at task execution time to the corresponding maintenance work order for cost and lifecycle tracking.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset being metered. Distinguishes fleet vehicles (collection trucks, transfer vehicles) from facility equipment (compactors, balers, WTE turbines, landfill gas engines) for utilization reporting and PM strategy segmentation.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the meter reading was confirmed and approved by the responsible supervisor or maintenance planner. Supports audit trail requirements for DOT and regulatory compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter reading record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter reading is associated with or required for a Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection event. DOT-linked readings are subject to additional retention and audit requirements under FMCSA regulations.']
  expected_reading_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The anticipated meter reading value based on historical usage rate and elapsed time since the last reading. Used as the baseline for variance calculation and anomaly detection in PM scheduling.']
  geotab_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Geotab telematics device installed on the vehicle that transmitted this meter reading. Populated only when reading_source is \'geotab_telematics\'. Enables traceability back to the specific telematics hardware unit.']
  geotab_trip_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Geotab trip or engine activity session during which this telematics-sourced meter reading was captured. Supports correlation with GPS route data and driver behavior events.']
  incremental_usage decimal(18,4) [note: 'Usage accumulated since the previous meter reading (current reading value minus previous reading value). Stored as a business field to support PM trigger evaluation and utilization rate calculations without requiring a self-join at query time.']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the meter or usage counter being read. Determines the unit of measure and PM trigger logic. Common types include odometer (miles), engine hours, hydraulic hours, cycle count (lifts/compactions), and tonnage processed (TPD). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: odometer|engine_hours|hydraulic_hours|cycle_count|tonnage_processed|fuel_consumed|pressurized_cycles|electrical_hours — promote to reference product]']
  pm_due_at_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The meter value at which the next Preventive Maintenance (PM) task is scheduled to be triggered for this asset and meter type. Calculated from the PM schedule interval and the last PM completion meter reading.']
  pm_trigger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter reading has triggered or is projected to trigger a Preventive Maintenance (PM) work order based on the asset\'s configured PM schedule thresholds. True when the reading value meets or exceeds the next PM due meter point.']
  previous_reading_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent prior confirmed meter reading for the same asset and meter type. Used to compute elapsed time and usage rate between readings for PM scheduling and utilization analytics.']
  previous_reading_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The most recent prior confirmed meter reading value for the same asset and meter type. Used to calculate incremental usage since last reading and to detect rollover or anomalous variance conditions.']
  reading_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the meter reading was taken. Used for day-level PM due-date calculations, utilization reporting, and DOT inspection scheduling. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reading_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the technician or driver at the time of reading. May include observations about meter condition, suspected inaccuracies, or contextual information relevant to variance investigation.']
  reading_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this meter reading record, sourced from Infor EAM or SAP PM. Used for cross-system traceability and audit reference.']
  reading_source varchar [note: 'Indicates the originating source or method by which this meter reading was captured. Manual entries are submitted by technicians or drivers; Geotab telematics readings are automatically transmitted via GPS/OBD integration; automated sensor readings come from onboard equipment sensors.']
  reading_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the meter reading record. Pending readings await validation; confirmed readings are accepted for PM calculations; rejected readings failed variance checks; superseded readings have been replaced by a corrected entry.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the meter reading was physically taken or recorded by the source system. This is the principal business event timestamp, distinct from record audit timestamps. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reading_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure corresponding to the meter type and reading value (e.g., miles for odometer, hours for engine/hydraulic meters, cycles for compaction counters, tons for tonnage processed). Aligns with Infor EAM unit of measure configuration.']
  reading_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual numeric value recorded on the meter or usage counter at the time of reading (e.g., 125,432.5 miles on odometer, 4,820.25 engine hours). This is the principal quantitative measurement for this event log record.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why a meter reading was rejected during validation. Populated when reading_status is \'rejected\'. Common reasons include meter rollover not flagged, value below previous reading, or variance exceeding tolerance.']
  remaining_until_pm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated remaining usage (in the meter\'s unit of measure) before the next PM task is due (PM due-at value minus current reading value). Supports proactive maintenance scheduling and fleet availability planning.']
  rollover_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter counter rolled over (reset to zero) between the previous and current reading, as can occur with odometers reaching maximum counter capacity. When true, incremental usage is adjusted for rollover in PM calculations.']
  source_reading_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this meter reading record in the originating source system (Infor EAM, SAP PM, or Geotab). Enables idempotent upserts and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this meter reading originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking across Infor EAM, SAP PM, Geotab telematics, and AMCS platform integrations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter reading record was last modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  variance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reading value deviates from the expected value beyond the configured tolerance threshold. True when the absolute variance exceeds the asset\'s defined variance tolerance, triggering a review workflow in Infor EAM.']
  variance_from_expected decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between the actual reading value and the expected reading value (actual minus expected). Positive values indicate higher-than-expected usage; negative values indicate lower-than-expected usage. Used to flag anomalous readings for review.']
  variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage deviation of the actual reading from the expected reading value, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Supports threshold-based alerting and trend analysis for asset utilization anomalies.']

  Note: 'Periodic meter and usage readings for fleet vehicles and facility equipment used to trigger meter-based PM schedules and track asset utilization. Captures asset reference, meter type (odometer miles, engine hours, hydraulic hours, cycle count, tonnage processed), reading value, reading date, reading source (manual entry, Geotab telematics, automated sensor), reader employee reference, and variance from expected reading flag. Feeds PM schedule due-date calculations and asset utilization reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each maintenance budget record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the maintenance_budget data product.']
  budget_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who formally approved this maintenance budget or the most recent budget revision. Required for SOX compliance and financial authorization audit trails.']
  budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically a Maintenance Manager or Fleet Manager) who owns and is accountable for this budget allocation. Used for approval workflows and spend governance escalation.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility (e.g., maintenance depot, transfer station, landfill, MRF) to which this budget is allocated. Enables facility-level maintenance spend governance and benchmarking.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Optional reference to a specific asset (vehicle, equipment unit, or facility component) when the budget is allocated at the individual asset level rather than at the asset class or facility level.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational cost center responsible for this maintenance budget. Enables financial accountability and cost allocation reporting by department or operational unit.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Maintenance budgets must allocate funds for permit-required activities (compliance inspections, permit-mandated equipment upgrades, monitoring programs). Budget line items track permit obligations, en']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Optional reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that drives the budgeted maintenance activity. Applicable when the budget is tied to a specific PM cycle rather than a general asset class or facility allocation.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: Maintenance budgets are allocated by standardized maintenance type categories. Currently has maintenance_type (STRING) which should reference the maintenance_type master table for consistent classific']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility. Business justification: Maintenance budgets are established at the WTE facility level for annual planning, cost center management, and tracking facility-level maintenance spend against revenue from tipping fees and energy sa']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual maintenance expenditure posted to date against this budget record, sourced from SAP FI/CO actual cost postings and Infor EAM work order actuals. Updated periodically via ERP integration.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the maintenance budget (or most recent revision) was formally approved by the authorized approver. Establishes the effective date of spend authorization.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the asset type covered by this budget allocation (e.g., fleet vehicles, facility structures, heavy equipment, containers, infrastructure). Drives asset-class-level budget analysis and benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fleet|facility|equipment|container|infrastructure|technology|other — promote to reference product]']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance budget record, used in financial planning documents, cost center reports, and cross-system references. Format: MB-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'Classification of the funding source for this maintenance budget: capital expenditure (CapEx), operating expenditure (OpEx), emergency reserve, regulatory compliance fund, or external grant. Drives financial reporting and EBITDA impact analysis.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance budget record, tracking progression from initial draft through approval, active spend, revision, and closure. Drives workflow approvals and spend authorization.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in SAP S/4HANA CO module. Used for direct cross-system reconciliation and financial reporting without requiring a join to the cost center master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance budget record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations and financial consolidation.']
  dot_inspection_budget_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record specifically covers Department of Transportation (DOT) mandated vehicle inspection and compliance maintenance costs. Supports DOT compliance cost tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  encumbered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of open Purchase Orders (POs) and open Work Orders (WOs) that have been committed against this budget but not yet invoiced or posted as actuals. Represents financial obligations that reduce available budget.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period granularity of the budget allocation — annual or a specific quarter (Q1–Q4). Supports both annual planning cycles and quarterly budget reviews and reforecasting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this maintenance budget allocation applies (e.g., 2024). Aligns with the company\'s financial calendar for annual planning and variance reporting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA FI module to which maintenance budget amounts are posted. Ensures financial traceability and alignment with the chart of accounts for GAAP/IFRS reporting.']
  labor_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total budget allocated for internal technician labor costs, including regular time and overtime. Supports workforce planning and labor cost governance within the maintenance function.']
  maintenance_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance type covered by this budget: fleet Preventive Maintenance (PM), fleet corrective/repair, facility PM, facility corrective, or subcontracted work. Core dimension for maintenance spend governance and KPI reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or commentary associated with this maintenance budget record (e.g., planned fleet expansion, anticipated regulatory changes, known large repair projects). Supports budget narrative documentation.']
  original_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial approved maintenance budget amount for the period, as established during the annual or quarterly planning cycle. Serves as the baseline for variance analysis and budget governance.']
  parts_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total budget allocated for spare parts, consumables, and materials (Bill of Materials — BOM items) required for maintenance activities. Supports parts inventory planning and procurement governance.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the budget period ends. Defines the boundary for spend authorization and variance calculation for the budget record.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the budget period begins. Used to align maintenance spend tracking with the fiscal calendar and to calculate time-phased budget consumption.']
  prior_year_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual maintenance spend for the equivalent period in the prior fiscal year. Used as a baseline reference for budget planning, year-over-year variance analysis, and trend reporting.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Operational region code to which this maintenance budget is assigned (e.g., NE, SE, MW, SW, W). Enables regional maintenance spend benchmarking and geographic budget governance across Waste Management\'s distributed operations.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget allocation is specifically designated to fund regulatory compliance maintenance activities (e.g., DOT vehicle inspections, EPA environmental equipment maintenance, OSHA safety system upkeep). True = compliance-mandated spend.']
  remaining_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Available budget balance calculated as revised (or original if no revision) budget minus actual spend minus encumbered amounts. Represents the uncommitted, unspent budget available for new work authorization.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The most recently approved revised budget amount, reflecting mid-period budget adjustments, supplemental approvals, or reforecasting. Null if no revision has been made. Used as the current authorized spend ceiling.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential counter tracking the number of budget revisions applied to this record. Zero (0) indicates the original approved budget with no revisions. Supports audit trail and change history analysis.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for the most recent budget revision, documenting the business justification (e.g., unplanned fleet breakdown, regulatory compliance requirement, emergency repair). Required when revision_number > 0.']
  source_budget_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this budget record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP CO internal order number, Infor EAM budget reference). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this budget record was sourced: SAP S/4HANA CO/PM module, Infor EAM, or manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  spend_to_budget_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of actual spend to date divided by the current authorized budget, expressed as a percentage. Provides a real-time utilization indicator for budget monitoring dashboards and KPI reporting.']
  subcontract_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total budget allocated specifically for subcontracted maintenance work (third-party vendors, specialized repair shops). Enables make-vs-buy analysis and subcontract spend governance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance budget record was most recently modified (e.g., budget revision, actual spend refresh, status change). Supports incremental data loading and change data capture in the Silver Layer.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary difference between the current authorized budget (revised or original) and actual spend to date. Positive value indicates underspend; negative value indicates overspend. Used for financial governance and management reporting.']
  variance_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Variance expressed as a percentage of the current authorized budget amount. Calculated as (variance_amount / current_budget_amount) * 100. Enables normalized comparison of budget performance across asset classes, facilities, and cost centers.']

  Note: 'Annual and periodic maintenance budget allocations by asset class, facility, cost center, and maintenance type. Captures budget period (fiscal year/quarter), asset class or facility reference, cost center, maintenance category (fleet PM, fleet corrective, facility PM, facility corrective, subcontract), budgeted amount, revised budget amount, actual spend to date, encumbered amount (open POs/WOs), remaining budget, and variance percentage. Supports financial planning and maintenance spend governance.'
}

Table maintenance.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each supply agreement record']
  parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the part in the master parts catalog that this supply agreement covers']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor who can supply the part under this agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this supply agreement. Active agreements are available for purchasing, Inactive are blocked, Pending are under negotiation, Expired require renewal.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master purchasing agreement or contract that governs the commercial terms for this vendor-part supply relationship. Links to contract management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement becomes active and the pricing/terms are valid from.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement expires and pricing/terms are no longer valid. Null indicates no expiration.']
  last_purchase_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Most recent actual purchase price per unit paid to a supplier. Used for cost trend analysis, standard cost updates, and procurement benchmarking. Expressed in USD. [Moved from parts_catalog: This attribute captures the most recent purchase price, but it doesn\'t indicate which vendor that price came from. In a multi-vendor sourcing model, procurement needs to track the current price from EACH vendor, not just the last price paid. This should move to supply_agreement as unit_price, allowing procurement to compare vendor pricing.]']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Most recent date when this part was purchased from this specific vendor. Used to track active supply relationships and identify stale source list entries.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected receipt for this vendor-part combination. Varies by vendor based on their inventory position, manufacturing capacity, and shipping distance.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this vendor for this part, based on vendor-specific packaging, economic order size, or contract terms.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred/primary supplier for this part. Used by procurement to prioritize vendor selection when multiple sources are available. Only one vendor per part should be marked preferred.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'The part number used by the preferred supplier in their catalog. Required for accurate purchase order line items and supplier communication. Corresponds to the Info Record in SAP MM. [Moved from parts_catalog: This attribute stores the part number used by the preferred supplier, but each vendor may use a different part number for the same part in their catalog. This is vendor-specific data that belongs in the supply_agreement association, not in the parts_catalog master record.]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of measure that this vendor charges for this specific part. Varies by vendor-part combination based on contract terms, volume commitments, and supplier relationships.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual supply relationship between a part in the parts catalog and a vendor who can supply that part. It captures vendor-specific pricing, lead times, minimum order quantities, and supplier preference status. Each record links one part to one vendor with commercial terms that exist only in the context of this specific supply relationship. This is the operational source list that procurement uses to determine which vendors to buy from for each part.. Existence Justification: In waste management fleet operations, parts are strategically sourced from multiple vendors to ensure supply continuity, competitive pricing, and risk mitigation. A hydraulic pump may be available from the OEM, two aftermarket suppliers, and an emergency local distributor—each with different pricing, lead times, and minimum order quantities. Conversely, each vendor supplies hundreds or thousands of different parts across engine, hydraulic, electrical, and body systems. Procurement actively manages these supply agreements as operational records, negotiating vendor-specific terms and tracking performance metrics per vendor-part combination.'
}

Table maintenance.incident_response_assignment {
  incident_response_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each incident response assignment record linking a technician to an emergency incident']
  emergency_response_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the emergency response incident record, identifying which hazmat incident this technician responded to']
  technician_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the maintenance technician assignment record, identifying which technician resource was deployed to the emergency incident']
  arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician arrived on scene at the emergency incident location, used for response time tracking and labor cost allocation']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this technician\'s emergency response assignment (dispatched, on scene, completed, medical evaluation required)']
  certification_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician\'s HAZWOPER certification was verified as current and valid before deployment to this emergency incident, required for regulatory compliance']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician departed from the emergency incident scene, used to calculate total time on scene and labor hours']
  exposure_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the technician\'s exposure level to hazardous materials during this incident response (none, minimal, moderate, significant), used for medical monitoring requirements and workers compensation documentation']
  hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours the technician worked on this specific emergency incident, calculated from arrival to departure timestamps, used for labor cost allocation and overtime tracking']
  incident_report_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this technician submitted their individual incident response report documenting actions taken and observations made during the emergency response']
  medical_clearance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician\'s medical clearance for hazmat response work was verified as current before deployment to this incident']
  ppe_used varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of personal protective equipment items used by this technician during the emergency response (e.g., Level A suit, SCBA, chemical-resistant gloves, face shield), required for OSHA safety documentation']
  response_role varchar [note: 'The specific role this technician performed during the emergency response incident (e.g., incident commander, hazmat specialist, equipment operator, decontamination technician, safety officer, support crew)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of maintenance technicians to hazardous material emergency response incidents. It captures the operational record of each technician\'s participation in an emergency response, including their specific role, time on scene, protective equipment used, and exposure documentation. Each record links one technician assignment to one emergency response incident with attributes that exist only in the context of this emergency response participation.. Existence Justification: Emergency response incidents require multiple technicians with specialized roles (incident commander, hazmat specialist, equipment operator, decontamination crew), and maintenance technicians respond to multiple emergency incidents over time. The business actively manages each technician\'s participation in each incident as a distinct operational record with role assignment, time tracking, PPE documentation, and exposure level recording for OSHA compliance and workers compensation purposes.'
}

Table maintenance.service_checklist_template {
  service_checklist_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_checklist_template']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or maintenance location where this checklist template is primarily used.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or business unit responsible for maintaining and using this checklist template.']
  parent_service_checklist_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_checklist_template (parent_service_checklist_template_id)']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.type. Business justification: Service checklist templates should reference the standardized maintenance type taxonomy. The template_type string field should be normalized to a FK to maintenance.type, which provides the authoritati']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether checklist completion requires supervisor or manager approval before the asset can return to service.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who approved this checklist template for active use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template was approved and authorized for operational use.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Category of asset or equipment this checklist template applies to within waste management operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the checklist template purpose, scope, and usage instructions.']
  documentation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this checklist requires formal documentation, sign-off, or record retention for compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this checklist template version becomes active and available for use.']
  equipment_required varchar [note: 'List of tools, diagnostic equipment, or safety gear required to complete the checklist.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes required to complete the checklist, used for scheduling and resource planning.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this checklist template version is no longer valid or should be retired. Nullable for templates without expiration.']
  external_template_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for this checklist template in the source system (e.g., Infor EAM template ID, SAP PM task list number) for integration and traceability.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval value for the frequency type (e.g., every 30 days, every 5000 miles, every 500 hours).']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'Defines how often or under what conditions this checklist should be executed.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the frequency interval defining the maintenance or inspection schedule.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this checklist template.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or comments related to the checklist template for technician guidance or administrative reference.']
  parts_list_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a bill of materials (BOM) or parts list is associated with this checklist for maintenance activities.']
  photo_evidence_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic evidence must be captured during checklist execution for audit or compliance purposes.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the checklist indicating urgency and importance for asset reliability and compliance.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulation, standard, or compliance requirement this checklist addresses (e.g., DOT 49 CFR 396.11).']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist is mandated by regulatory or compliance requirements such as Department of Transportation (DOT) inspections.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of certifications or qualifications required to execute this checklist (e.g., ASE Certified, DOT Inspector, HAZMAT Certified).']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Minimum technician skill or certification level required to perform this checklist.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist addresses safety-critical systems or components that directly impact worker or public safety.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) where this template was created (e.g., Infor EAM, SAP PM).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist template indicating its availability for use.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the checklist template used for external reference and system integration.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service checklist template describing its purpose and scope.']
  total_checklist_items varchar [note: 'Total number of inspection or verification items included in this checklist template.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the checklist template to track revisions and updates over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this checklist template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_checklist_template. Referenced by service_checklist_template_id.'
}

Table maintenance.task_list {
  task_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for task_list']
  parent_task_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on task_list (parent_task_list_id)']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.type. Business justification: Task lists represent standardized maintenance procedures and should reference the maintenance type taxonomy. The task_list_type string field should be replaced with a FK to maintenance.type for consis']
  applicable_asset_class varchar [note: 'Asset class or equipment type to which this task list applies (e.g., refuse truck, compactor, landfill equipment, transfer station machinery). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: refuse_truck|roll_off_truck|front_loader|rear_loader|compactor|baler|excavator|dozer|wheel_loader|transfer_station_equipment|landfill_equipment|recycling_equipment|facility_hvac|facility_electrical — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Equipment manufacturer or brand for which this task list is designed (e.g., Mack, Peterbilt, Caterpillar, John Deere). Null if task list is manufacturer-agnostic.']
  applicable_model_series varchar [note: 'Specific equipment model or series for which this task list is designed. Null if task list applies across multiple models.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the maintenance manager or technical authority who approved this task list for operational use.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this task list was formally approved for operational use.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this task list record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost estimates in the task list (typically USD for Waste Management operations).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the task list including scope, objectives, and procedural overview for maintenance activities.']
  dot_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task list includes Department of Transportation (DOT) mandated inspection activities for commercial fleet compliance.']
  downtime_expected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether execution of the task list will result in asset downtime requiring operational scheduling coordination.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this task list version is superseded or retired. Null for currently active task lists.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this task list version becomes effective and available for work order assignment.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task list includes environmental compliance activities such as emissions testing, hazardous waste handling, or EPA-mandated inspections.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated time in hours required to complete all tasks in the task list, used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor cost in USD for completing the task list, including technician time and overhead.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in USD for parts and materials required by the task list Bill of Materials (BOM).']
  estimated_total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in USD for completing the task list, including labor, parts, and other direct costs.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number for this task list in the source system (e.g., Infor EAM task list ID, SAP PM task list number).']
  frequency_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in days between scheduled executions of the task list for preventive maintenance (PM) cycles. Null for corrective or on-demand task lists.']
  frequency_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Standard interval in operating hours between scheduled executions of the task list for equipment preventive maintenance based on hour meter readings. Null for non-equipment assets.']
  frequency_interval_miles varchar [note: 'Standard interval in miles between scheduled executions of the task list for fleet preventive maintenance based on odometer readings. Null for non-fleet assets.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this task list record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information for technicians executing the task list.']
  permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether execution of the task list requires special work permits such as hot work, confined space entry, or excavation permits.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification indicating urgency and importance of the task list for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Minimum technician skill level or certification required to execute the task list safely and effectively.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision number of the task list, incremented when task list content or procedures are updated.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task list involves safety-critical activities requiring special permits, lockout-tagout (LOTO), or confined space procedures.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where this task list was originally created or is maintained (Infor EAM for CMMS, SAP PM for work order execution).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task list indicating availability for work order assignment and execution.']
  task_list_category varchar [note: 'Business category grouping task lists by asset domain: fleet (vehicles), facility (buildings), equipment (machinery), infrastructure (utilities), safety (compliance), or environmental (waste systems).']
  task_list_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the task list. Used for cross-system reference and work order documentation.']
  task_list_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the task list describing the maintenance activity or procedure.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for task_list. Referenced by task_list_id.'
}

Table maintenance.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for work_center']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent facility or site where this work center is physically located.']
  parent_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on work_center (parent_work_center_id)']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who supervises and manages operations at this work center.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the work center location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building number, suite, or unit.']
  average_labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average hourly labor rate for technicians at this work center, used for maintenance cost estimation.']
  backlog_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable backlog of work orders in hours before escalation is required.']
  capacity_hours_per_day decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total available labor hours per day for this work center, used for capacity planning and work order scheduling.']
  certification_requirements varchar [note: 'Required certifications or qualifications for technicians working at this work center (e.g., DOT, EPA, OSHA).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the work center is located.']
  cmms_system_code varchar [note: 'Work center identifier in the CMMS system (Infor EAM) for integration and work order routing.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for tracking maintenance expenses and labor costs associated with this work center.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the work center is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work center\'s purpose, capabilities, and scope of maintenance activities.']
  dot_inspection_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work center is authorized to perform DOT vehicle inspections and certifications.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the work center ceased operations or was decommissioned. Null for active work centers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the work center became operational and available for work order assignments.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for work center communications and work order notifications.']
  emergency_contact_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work center has 24/7 emergency contact capability for critical breakdowns.']
  equipment_capabilities varchar [note: 'Description of specialized equipment and tools available at this work center for maintenance operations.']
  erp_work_center_code varchar [note: 'Work center identifier in the ERP system (SAP PM) for financial integration and cost tracking.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work center is certified to handle hazardous materials and waste per EPA regulations.']
  last_safety_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety audit or inspection conducted at this work center.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was last modified or updated.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the work center (e.g., Fleet Maintenance Bay 3, Landfill Equipment Shop).']
  next_safety_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next safety audit or inspection at this work center.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments about the work center for operational reference.']
  number_of_technicians varchar [note: 'Current headcount of technicians assigned to this work center.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the work center (e.g., Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM).']
  parts_inventory_location varchar [note: 'Physical location code or identifier for the parts inventory storage area associated with this work center.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the work center.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the work center location.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Current safety performance rating of the work center based on incident history and compliance audits.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Operating shift pattern for the work center defining when maintenance activities can be performed.']
  specialization varchar [note: 'Primary technical specialization or trade focus of the work center (e.g., hydraulics, electrical, diesel engine, welding).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the work center is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the work center in its lifecycle.']
  utilization_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization percentage for the work center capacity, used for performance management.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the work center for business operations and reporting.']
  work_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center based on its primary maintenance function.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_center. Referenced by work_center_id.'
}

Table maintenance.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for maintenance_plan']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset class or equipment type this plan applies to, enabling template-based maintenance planning across similar assets.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific asset (vehicle, equipment, facility) covered by this maintenance plan.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this maintenance plan record in the system.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary technician or crew assigned to execute maintenance activities under this plan.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility or service center where the maintenance work will be performed.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this maintenance plan record.']
  superseded_maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_plan (superseded_maintenance_plan_id)']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.type. Business justification: Maintenance plans should reference the maintenance type taxonomy to classify the kind of maintenance work being planned (PM, corrective, inspection, etc.). The schema shows work_order_type_id which sh']
  work_order_type_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the type of work order that will be generated from this maintenance plan (e.g., PM inspection, lubrication, DOT inspection).']
  auto_generate_work_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders should be automatically generated by the CMMS system based on the maintenance schedule, or manually created by planners.']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., DOT inspections, EPA emissions testing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance plan record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset must be taken out of service to perform the maintenance, impacting operational scheduling.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance plan becomes active and begins generating scheduled work orders.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost in USD for executing this maintenance plan per occurrence, including labor, parts, and overhead.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Expected time in hours required to complete the maintenance activity, used for technician scheduling and downtime planning.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance frequency: time-based (days, weeks, months, years) or usage-based (miles, kilometers, operating hours, cycles).']
  frequency_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing the interval between maintenance activities (e.g., 30 for 30 days, 5000 for 5000 miles).']
  last_execution_date date [note: 'Date when maintenance under this plan was last completed, used to calculate the next scheduled maintenance date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance plan record was most recently updated in the system.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that work orders should be generated before the scheduled maintenance date to allow for planning and parts procurement.']
  meter_reading_trigger varchar [note: 'For usage-based plans, the meter reading (odometer, engine hours, cycles) at which maintenance is triggered.']
  next_scheduled_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next maintenance activity is due based on frequency and last execution date.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the maintenance plan.']
  parts_list_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan requires a bill of materials (BOM) or parts list for execution.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the maintenance plan for easy identification by maintenance staff and planners.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the maintenance plan, used in work orders and scheduling systems.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance plan. Active plans generate work orders; suspended plans are temporarily paused; inactive plans are permanently disabled.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance plan strategy: preventive (scheduled), corrective (reactive), predictive (data-driven), condition-based (triggered by asset state), time-based (calendar intervals), or usage-based (meter/hour intervals).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this maintenance plan, determining scheduling precedence and resource allocation when conflicts arise.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory standard or compliance framework this maintenance plan satisfies (e.g., DOT FMCSA 396.17, EPA CAA Title V).']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan addresses safety-critical systems or components where failure could result in injury or environmental harm.']
  season_restriction varchar [note: 'Seasonal constraint for when maintenance can or should be performed, relevant for weather-dependent activities or seasonal equipment.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the maintenance tasks and procedures to be performed under this plan, including inspection points and service activities.']
  warranty_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance is required to maintain manufacturer warranty coverage on the asset.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for maintenance_plan. Referenced by maintenance_plan_id.'
}

Table recycling.mrf_facility {
  mrf_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) record. Serves as the primary key and SSOT reference for MRF facility identity across the recycling domain.']
  emergency_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.emergency_action_plan. Business justification: OSHA 1910.38 requires facility-specific emergency action plans with unique evacuation routes, assembly points, and emergency coordinators. Each MRF must maintain its own EAP. Linking enables facility-']
  epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier registered in the EPA Facility Registry System (FRS). Required for federal environmental reporting, RCRA compliance submissions, and EPA inspection records. Serves as the federal regulatory identity of the facility.']
  facility_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: MRF facilities require designated facility managers for operational accountability, regulatory compliance reporting (EPA, state permits), safety oversight, and performance management. Facility manager']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Hazard registers are facility-specific risk management tools documenting unique hazards, controls, and residual risks. Each MRF maintains its own register based on equipment, processes, and environmen']
  lifecycle_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_lifecycle_stage. Business justification: Facility lifecycle stage (commissioned, operational, under expansion, decommissioned) determines regulatory reporting obligations, capital project prioritization, and operational budgeting. Required f']
  primary_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: MRF facilities procure maintenance services, supplies, and equipment parts from vendors. Tracking primary vendor per facility enables vendor performance management, procurement routing, and service co']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Safety programs (respiratory protection, hearing conservation, confined space) are facility-specific implementations based on unique hazards and operations. Each MRF must track which programs apply fo']
  tracked_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.tracked_site. Business justification: MRFs are emission sources tracked in sustainability reporting boundaries. Business need: ESG disclosure preparation requires linking operational facilities to tracked_site registry for GHG inventory c']
  accepts_cd_waste boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to process Construction and Demolition (C&D) waste streams including concrete, wood, drywall, and metals. C&D processing requires specialized equipment and separate permit conditions.']
  accepts_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to accept commercial and industrial recyclable materials. Drives commercial hauling route assignments in AMCS and contract eligibility in Salesforce CRM.']
  accepts_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to accept residential curbside recyclable materials. Drives routing logic in AMCS and service enrollment eligibility in Waste Logics.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the MRF facility. Used for regulatory filings, logistics routing, emergency response coordination, and vendor/hauler directions. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the MRF facility (suite, building, gate number, or additional location detail). Supplements address_line1 for precise facility identification in logistics and regulatory submissions.']
  baling_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of baling lines (balers) at the MRF facility used to compress and bind recovered commodities (paper, plastics, metals) into marketable bales. Determines bale production capacity and influences commodity sales throughput.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the MRF facility is located. Used for geographic reporting, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and service area mapping.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRF facility was officially commissioned and began commercial operations. Used for asset age calculations, depreciation schedules, permit anniversary tracking, and capital planning.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI/CO cost center code assigned to this MRF facility for financial cost allocation, P&L reporting, and budget management. Links facility operational costs to the general ledger (GL) for EBITDA and COGS reporting.']
  county varchar [note: 'County in which the MRF facility is located. Relevant for Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) jurisdiction, county solid waste authority reporting, and local permit compliance under SWIS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MRF facility master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail compliance, and record provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRF facility was permanently decommissioned and ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for asset retirement accounting, permit closure filings, and historical reporting.']
  design_capacity_tpd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Engineering-designed maximum daily throughput capacity in tons per day (TPD) as specified in the facility\'s original design documentation. May differ from permitted capacity due to regulatory limits or operational constraints. Used for capital planning and equipment sizing.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the MRF facility, used in operational systems (AMCS, SAP MM), regulatory submissions, and inter-system integration. Format: MRF-[STATE]-[SEQUENCE].']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the MRF facility as registered with regulatory authorities and used in customer-facing communications, contracts, and reporting (e.g., \'Houston North MRF\').']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MRF by processing methodology. Single-stream accepts all recyclables commingled; dual-stream separates fiber from containers; mixed-waste MRF processes unsorted MSW; source-separated handles pre-sorted materials; drop-off serves public drop-off programs.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility holds a current ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. ISO 14001 certification demonstrates systematic environmental management and is required for certain municipal contracts and sustainability reporting.']
  iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility holds a current ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Relevant for OSHA compliance, worker safety programs, and contract qualification requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MRF facility master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the MRF facility in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS-based routing in AMCS, geospatial analytics, emergency response mapping, and regulatory geographic submissions.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the MRF facility in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS-based routing in AMCS, geospatial analytics, emergency response mapping, and regulatory geographic submissions.']
  max_contamination_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination rate percentage for outbound commodity bales as specified in commodity offtake contracts and quality standards. Exceeding this threshold may result in load rejection, price penalties, or contract violations. Used in quality control and commodity sales management.']
  operating_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week the MRF facility operates (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT\'). Used for route scheduling in AMCS, customer service availability, and workforce planning in Workday HCM.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Standard daily end time for MRF facility operations in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for inbound vehicle scheduling, tipping fee window management in Oracle Revenue Management, and AMCS route dispatch timing.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Standard daily start time for MRF facility operations in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for inbound vehicle scheduling, tipping fee window management in Oracle Revenue Management, and AMCS route dispatch timing.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the MRF facility. Active indicates full operations; temporarily_closed covers planned or unplanned shutdowns; decommissioned indicates permanent closure; permitted_not_operating indicates permit held but no active processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|under_construction|temporarily_closed|decommissioned|permitted_not_operating — promote to reference product]']
  optical_sorter_count varchar [note: 'Number of optical sorting units (near-infrared or AI-vision sorters) installed at the facility for automated material identification and separation. Indicates automation level, influences contamination rates, and is tracked in Infor EAM for maintenance scheduling.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership or operational arrangement for the facility. Company-owned assets are on Waste Management\'s balance sheet; leased facilities carry lease obligations; joint ventures involve shared ownership; contracted facilities are operated under service agreements with municipalities or third parties.']
  permitted_capacity_tpd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted daily processing throughput in tons per day (TPD) as authorized by the facility\'s state environmental permit. This is the regulatory ceiling for inbound material acceptance and is used for compliance monitoring, capacity planning, and diversion rate reporting.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary operational phone number for the MRF facility. Used for inbound hauler coordination, customer service escalations, emergency contact, and regulatory agency communications.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM plant code corresponding to this MRF facility. Used for inventory management of recovered commodities, purchase orders (PO) for consumables, and materials management integration between the MRF and SAP MM module.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the MRF facility address. Used for geographic analysis, regulatory jurisdiction mapping, and logistics routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal Waste Management operational region code to which this MRF facility is assigned (e.g., \'SOUTH\', \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\'). Used for regional performance reporting, P&L allocation in SAP FI/CO, and organizational hierarchy management.']
  sort_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of active sorting lines (conveyor-based manual or automated sort systems) at the MRF facility. Directly determines sorting throughput capacity and staffing requirements. Used in capacity planning and equipment maintenance scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the MRF facility is located. Determines applicable state environmental regulations, permit requirements, and reporting jurisdictions.']
  storage_capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum on-site storage capacity in tons for processed and baled commodities awaiting outbound shipment. Used for inventory management in SAP MM, commodity sales scheduling, and market timing decisions.']
  target_diversion_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Facility-level target percentage of inbound material weight that is successfully recovered and diverted from landfill disposal. Expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used as a KPI benchmark for operational performance, EPA recycling reporting, and sustainability goal tracking.']
  tipping_floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total area of the tipping floor in square feet where inbound recyclable materials are unloaded from collection vehicles. Determines maximum simultaneous unloading capacity and influences throughput planning and safety compliance under OSHA.']
  total_floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total enclosed floor area of the MRF facility building in square feet, including tipping floor, sort lines, baling area, storage, and office space. Used for property tax assessments, lease calculations, and facility planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) operated by Waste Management. Captures facility identity, physical address, permitted capacity (TPD), facility type (single-stream, dual-stream, mixed-waste MRF), operational status, EPA permit number, state permit number, licensed processing capacity, tipping floor area (sq ft), baling line count, sort line count, ownership type, and commissioning date. SSOT for MRF facility identity across the recycling domain.'
}

Table recycling.inbound_load {
  inbound_load_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inbound material load received at the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Serves as the primary key for all load-level transactions and reconciliations.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, or municipal) associated with this inbound load. Used for tipping fee billing reconciliation in Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM account linkage.']
  diversion_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.diversion_record. Business justification: Each inbound load contributes tonnage to facility-level diversion metrics tracked for ESG reporting and regulatory compliance. Business need: Diversion rate calculation, ESG metric aggregation, tracea']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CDL-licensed driver who delivered the inbound load. Used for driver performance tracking, DOT compliance, and load accountability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF scale house operator who processed this inbound load weigh-in. Used for operator accountability, training compliance tracking, and audit trail requirements.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) that received this inbound load. Supports multi-facility operations and facility-level throughput reporting.']
  generator_epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration.
