Business justification: Contaminated bales exceeding commodity grade specifications and containing hazardous constituents require waste profiling before disposal or specialized treatment. Real business process: rejected bale']
  allocated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bale was allocated to a specific sales order or outbound shipment. Marks the transition from in_storage to allocated lifecycle status. Used for inventory turnover analysis and order fulfillment lead time tracking.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Human-readable barcode label affixed to the bale for physical identification and scanning during storage, quality inspection, and outbound shipment. Printed at time of production and scanned at each lifecycle checkpoint.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number assigned to the outbound shipment carrying this bale. The BOL is the primary freight transport document required by DOT regulations. Null until bale is loaded for shipment. Enables shipment tracking and dispute resolution with commodity buyers.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique quality certification document number issued for this bale upon passing inspection, confirming it meets the specified commodity grade and buyer quality requirements. Required for certain commodity buyers and export shipments. Null if no formal certification was issued.']
  commodity_subtype varchar [note: 'Specific material subtype within the commodity category (e.g., OCC for Old Corrugated Cardboard, HDPE for High-Density Polyethylene, aluminum, steel, clear glass, mixed paper). Drives commodity-specific market pricing and buyer contract matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OCC|ONP|mixed_paper|HDPE|PET|LDPE|aluminum|steel|tin|clear_glass|brown_glass|green_glass — promote to reference product]']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Primary commodity category of the bale. Classifies the recovered material stream for inventory management, market pricing, and EPA recycling diversion reporting. Core classification for MRF commodity recovery analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper|plastic|metal|glass|mixed|textile|electronics|organics — promote to reference product]']
  contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured percentage of non-target or non-recyclable material (contaminants) present in the bale, expressed as a percentage of total bale weight. Determined during quality inspection. Exceeding buyer-specified contamination thresholds results in grade downgrade, price deduction, or rejection. Key MRF quality KPI.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bale record was first created in the system, typically at the moment of bale production completion. Serves as the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage, Silver layer ingestion tracking, and SOX compliance recordkeeping.']
  days_in_storage varchar [note: 'Number of days this bale has been in MRF storage since production. Tracked to manage inventory aging, prioritize FIFO shipment sequencing, and identify slow-moving commodity inventory that may be subject to quality degradation or market price risk. Updated daily.']
  density_kg_per_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated density of the bale expressed as kilograms per cubic meter, derived from weight and physical dimensions. Indicates baler compression performance and material compaction ratio. Low density may signal baler maintenance needs or loose material feed. Used for baler performance KPI tracking.']
  estimated_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of this bale in USD, calculated as weight in tons multiplied by the applicable commodity market price per ton. Used for MRF commodity inventory valuation, revenue forecasting, and financial reporting in SAP FI/CO. Not a final invoiced amount — actual sale price is recorded on the sales order.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade assigned to the bale based on contamination level, moisture content, and material purity. Grade A represents premium quality meeting buyer specifications; Grade B is acceptable with minor deviations; Grade C is marginal; off-spec fails buyer specs but is saleable at discount; rejected is unsaleable. Directly impacts commodity sale price.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Combined with length and width for volume calculation, stacking safety assessment, and storage bay capacity planning.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent quality inspection for this bale. Pass indicates the bale meets all applicable grade and buyer specifications; fail results in rejection or rework; conditional pass indicates the bale is accepted with noted deviations that are within buyer tolerance.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent quality inspection was performed on this bale. Used for inspection age tracking, pre-shipment inspection validity windows, and audit trail for quality certification documentation.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality inspection performed on this bale. Production QC is conducted at time of baling; pre-shipment inspection occurs before loading; buyer-requested inspection is initiated by the commodity buyer; re-inspection follows a prior rejection or dispute. Determines inspection protocol and documentation requirements.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Used with width and height to calculate bale volume, verify baler performance, and plan trailer loading configurations for outbound commodity shipments.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the bale within its lifecycle from production through disposition. In_storage: produced and available in MRF inventory; allocated: assigned to a sales order or shipment; shipped: loaded and departed facility; rejected: failed quality inspection and removed from saleable inventory; on_hold: pending re-inspection or buyer dispute resolution.']
  market_price_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Prevailing commodity market price per ton in USD at the time this bale was produced or allocated to a sales order. Captured for commodity inventory valuation, revenue forecasting, and market price trend analysis. Reflects spot market or contracted price depending on buyer agreement type.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured moisture content of the bale expressed as a percentage of total bale weight. Excess moisture reduces net commodity value, increases transport weight without material value, and can cause mold or degradation in paper bales. Monitored against buyer moisture tolerance thresholds.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, quality observations, or special handling instructions associated with this bale. Used by MRF operators and quality inspectors to document non-standard conditions not captured in structured fields (e.g., visible damage, unusual contamination type, buyer special instructions).']
  production_shift varchar [note: 'MRF operating shift during which this bale was produced. Used for shift-level productivity analysis, quality trend analysis by shift, and workforce scheduling optimization. Correlates bale quality and throughput metrics to specific shift teams.']
  production_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bale was ejected from the baler and production was completed. The principal business event timestamp for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used for throughput calculations (Tons Per Day), shift productivity reporting, and bale age tracking for FIFO inventory management.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bale requires a follow-up quality inspection, typically after remediation of a failed inspection, buyer dispute, or conditional pass with noted concerns. True triggers a re-inspection workflow in the MRF quality management process.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for bale rejection when inspection_result is fail or lifecycle_status is rejected. Common reasons include excessive contamination, moisture above threshold, broken wires, incorrect commodity mix, or bale integrity failure. Required for root cause analysis and MRF process improvement. Null when bale passes inspection.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier embedded in or attached to the bale for automated tracking through MRF storage, loading dock, and outbound logistics. Enables real-time inventory visibility without manual scanning. Null if facility does not use RFID.']
  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bale was loaded onto an outbound transport vehicle and departed the MRF facility. Marks the transition to shipped lifecycle status. Used for inventory reconciliation, Bill of Lading (BOL) documentation, and commodity revenue recognition timing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this bale record was most recently modified. Tracks lifecycle status changes, quality inspection updates, allocation events, and shipment confirmations. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer and audit compliance.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the bale in kilograms as measured by the baler scale or floor scale at time of production. The principal quantitative measurement for this resource. Used for Tons Per Day (TPD) throughput calculations, commodity inventory valuation, sales order fulfillment, and EPA recycling tonnage reporting.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Combined with length and height for volume calculation, storage space planning, and trailer load optimization.']
  wire_count varchar [note: 'Number of wire ties or strapping bands securing the bale. Standard bale configurations use 4, 5, or 6 wires depending on commodity type and baler model. Deviations from standard wire count may indicate bale integrity risk during transport and are flagged during quality inspection.']

  Note: 'Master record for each commodity bale produced at a MRF facility, including production details and quality inspection history. Captures bale identity (barcode/RFID), commodity type, grade assignment, production timestamp, sort line and baler reference, physical measurements (weight, dimensions, density), operator ID, storage location, and bale lifecycle status (in-storage, allocated, shipped, rejected). Includes quality inspection records: inspection type (production QC, pre-shipment, buyer-requested), measured contamination and moisture percentages, pass/fail result, rejection reasons, and re-inspection flags. SSOT for bale identity, traceability from production through shipment, and quality certification.'
}

Table recycling.bale_inspection {
  bale_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bale inspection record. Primary key for the bale inspection transaction.']
  bale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bale being inspected. Links to the bale production record in the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) system.']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer for whom this inspection was performed. Populated for buyer-requested inspections or pre-shipment inspections for specific customers.']
  commodity_sale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity sales order associated with this inspection. Links inspection to outbound commodity sales transaction.']
  mrf_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment used for inspection measurements (e.g., moisture meter, scale). Links to asset management for calibration tracking.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility where the inspection occurred. Links to facility master data.']
  previous_inspection_bale_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior inspection record for this bale. Populated for reinspections to track inspection history and corrective action effectiveness.']
  primary_bale_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who performed the inspection. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  actual_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured weight of the bale in pounds at time of inspection. Used to verify production weight and calculate commodity value.']
  approved_for_shipment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bale is approved for shipment to buyer based on inspection results. True when inspection passes and all quality criteria are met.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued for bales that pass inspection and meet buyer specifications. Used for traceability and buyer dispute resolution.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Industry or regulatory standard against which the bale was inspected (e.g., ISRI specifications, buyer-specific requirements, EPA guidelines). Supports regulatory reporting and buyer certification.']
  contamination_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured percentage of non-target materials or contaminants in the bale. Critical metric for commodity grade determination and buyer acceptance. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  contamination_type varchar [note: 'Description of the primary contaminant types found during inspection (e.g., food waste, non-recyclable plastics, hazardous materials, textiles). Supports contamination tracking and source identification.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of corrective actions to be taken. May include rebaling, resorting, process adjustments, or equipment maintenance. Populated when corrective_action_required is true.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required based on inspection findings. True when bale must be reworked, reprocessed, or when process improvements are needed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the database. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  grade_assigned varchar [note: 'Quality grade assigned to the bale based on inspection results. Determines commodity pricing tier and buyer eligibility. Premium is highest quality, Grade A-C are standard tiers, off-spec requires rework, reject is unsaleable.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Time spent performing the inspection in minutes. Used for labor cost tracking and process efficiency analysis.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the inspection was performed. Production line for in-process QC, staging area for pre-shipment, loading dock for final verification, buyer facility for receiving inspection, third party lab for independent verification.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes captured by the inspector. May include observations about material quality, equipment issues, or process anomalies.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the inspection event. Format: INS-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where YYYYMMDD is inspection date and NNNN is sequence number.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the inspection. Pass indicates bale meets quality standards, fail requires corrective action, conditional pass allows shipment with buyer notification, pending retest requires additional verification.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bale inspection was performed. Principal business event timestamp for this quality control transaction.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection performed. Production QC (Quality Control) occurs during bale production, pre-shipment before delivery, buyer-requested per customer requirement, random audit for ongoing quality assurance, compliance verification for regulatory requirements, or post-complaint following buyer dispute.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the inspection record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Type of recovered material in the inspected bale. Aligns with commodity categories tracked for recycling diversion rates and commodity sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mixed_paper|cardboard|pet_plastic|hdpe_plastic|aluminum|steel|glass|mixed_plastics — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  moisture_content_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured moisture content in the bale as a percentage of total weight. Affects commodity value and grade classification. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  photo_documentation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic evidence was captured during inspection. True when photos are available for quality documentation or dispute resolution.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bale must be reinspected after corrective action. True when initial inspection failed or when buyer requires verification.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the bale failed inspection. Populated only when inspection_result is fail. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of samples taken from the bale for inspection. Larger sample sizes provide more accurate contamination and moisture measurements.']
  shipment_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why shipment was not approved. Populated when approved_for_shipment is false. Used for logistics coordination and customer communication.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the inspection data originated. AMCS for integrated MRF operations, SAP QM (Quality Management) for enterprise quality module, manual entry for paper-based inspections, mobile app for field inspections, third party lab for external certifications.']

  Note: 'Transactional quality control inspection record for a produced bale. Captures inspection timestamp, bale reference, inspector ID, inspection type (production QC, pre-shipment, buyer-requested), measured contamination percentage, moisture content percentage, actual bale weight, grade assigned, pass/fail result, rejection reason (if failed), corrective action required, and re-inspection flag. Supports commodity grade certification and buyer dispute resolution.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_inventory {
  commodity_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commodity inventory snapshot record.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recovered commodity type (paper, plastics, metals, glass).']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility where the commodity inventory is held.']
  allocated_bales varchar [note: 'Number of bales committed to pending sales orders or customer allocations.']
  allocated_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight in tons of commodity inventory allocated to pending sales orders.']
  available_bales varchar [note: 'Number of bales available for sale, calculated as total bales on hand minus allocated bales.']
  available_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight in tons of commodity inventory available for sale, calculated as total weight minus allocated weight.']
  average_bale_age_days varchar [note: 'Average age in days of all bales in inventory, calculated from production date to snapshot date.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Representative batch or lot number for traceability of the commodity inventory, particularly for quality tracking.']
  commodity_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade classification of the recovered commodity bales based on contamination levels and material purity standards.']
  contamination_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average contamination rate percentage across all bales in inventory, indicating non-recyclable material content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory snapshot record was first created in the data platform.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the commodity inventory indicating availability for sale or operational restrictions.']
  inventory_turnover_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days commodity inventory remains in storage before being sold, calculated over a rolling period.']
  inventory_valuation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total financial valuation of the commodity inventory on hand based on current market pricing or standard cost.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count performed for this commodity at this facility.']
  last_quality_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality inspection or certification performed on this commodity inventory.']
  market_price_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current market price per ton for this commodity grade, used for inventory valuation and sales pricing.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material master number for the commodity, used for integration with SAP MM inventory and financial systems.']
  maximum_capacity_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity in tons for this commodity type at this facility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the commodity inventory, including special handling instructions or quality observations.']
  oldest_bale_production_date date [note: 'Production date of the oldest bale currently in inventory, used to track inventory aging and FIFO management.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code representing the MRF facility for integration with SAP MM and FI/CO modules.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Effective date of the current market price per ton used for inventory valuation.']
  quality_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality certification or inspection for the commodity inventory against ISRI or customer specifications.']
  reorder_point_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory threshold in tons that triggers production planning or sales acceleration actions.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Business date for which this inventory snapshot was captured, representing the inventory position as of end of day.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when this inventory snapshot was captured from the source system.']
  storage_capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total MRF storage capacity utilized by this commodity inventory.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Primary storage location or zone within the MRF where the commodity bales are stored.']
  total_bales_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total count of commodity bales currently in MRF storage at the snapshot date.']
  total_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of commodity inventory on hand measured in tons.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure used for commodity inventory tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory snapshot record was last updated in the data platform.']
  valuation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for inventory valuation amount.']

  Note: 'Operational inventory snapshot of recovered commodity bales held in MRF storage at any point in time. Captures facility reference, commodity reference, commodity grade, total bales on hand, total weight on hand (tons), storage location summary, oldest bale production date, average bale age (days), allocated quantity (committed to pending sales orders), available quantity, and last physical count date. Integrates with SAP MM material inventory for commodity stock management and financial valuation.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_buyer {
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commodity buyer record. Primary key for the commodity buyer entity within the recycling domain.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buyer is currently active and eligible for commodity sales. True means active and available for transactions, False means inactive and excluded from sales processes.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the buyer\'s billing address including street number and street name. Used for invoicing and accounts receivable processing.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the buyer\'s billing address for suite, floor, or building information. Optional field for additional address details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the buyer\'s billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the buyer\'s billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the buyer\'s billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the buyer\'s billing address. Uses standard postal abbreviations.']
  buyer_established_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this commodity buyer was first established. Used for buyer tenure analysis and relationship management.']
  buyer_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the commodity buyer organization that purchases recovered materials from MRF operations.']
  buyer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commodity buyer based on their role in the recovered materials supply chain. Domestic mills process materials domestically, export brokers facilitate international sales, reprocessors transform materials into new feedstock, direct end-users consume materials in manufacturing, aggregators consolidate materials from multiple sources, and traders buy and resell commodities.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the buyer contact. Used for purchase orders, invoices, shipping notifications, and quality documentation.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the buyer contact. Used for order coordination, quality issues, and logistics communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity buyer record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance allowed for this buyer in USD. Used for credit risk management and sales order approval workflows.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit approval status of the buyer. Approved buyers can receive shipments on credit terms, suspended buyers require prepayment or letter of credit, declined buyers are not eligible for sales.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent commodity sale transaction with this buyer. Used to identify dormant buyer relationships and trigger reactivation campaigns.']
  minimum_order_quantity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum shipment size in tons that the buyer will accept per order. Used for load planning and sales order validation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity buyer record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional buyer-specific information such as special handling requirements, preferred contact times, facility access instructions, or historical relationship context.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining the number of days and conditions for payment (e.g., Net 30, Net 45, COD, Letter of Credit). Aligns with SAP FI payment terms master data.']
  preferred_commodities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of recovered material commodity types the buyer prefers to purchase (e.g., OCC, mixed paper, PET, HDPE, aluminum, ferrous metals, glass). Supports sales planning and material allocation decisions.']
  preferred_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Buyer\'s preferred logistics arrangement. FOB MRF (Free On Board at Materials Recovery Facility) means buyer arranges transport, delivered means seller delivers to buyer facility, buyer pickup means buyer collects from MRF, third-party carrier means mutually agreed logistics provider.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the buyer organization responsible for commodity procurement and contract management.']
  quality_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buyer requires quality certification documentation (e.g., contamination reports, bale composition analysis) with each shipment. True means certification is mandatory, False means standard documentation suffices.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD customer master record number linking this commodity buyer to the enterprise sales and accounts receivable system. Primary reference for commodity sales orders and revenue recognition.']
  sap_vendor_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM vendor master record number linking this commodity buyer to the enterprise procurement and accounts payable system. Used for purchase order and invoice reconciliation when buyer also supplies services or materials.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the buyer\'s shipping or delivery address. Used for logistics planning and Bill of Lading (BOL) documentation.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the buyer\'s shipping address for dock, gate, or facility-specific routing information.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City name for the buyer\'s shipping or receiving facility.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the buyer\'s shipping destination. Critical for export compliance and international logistics.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the buyer\'s shipping facility.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the buyer\'s shipping address.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Buyer organization\'s tax identification number (EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, or equivalent). Required for tax reporting and compliance with revenue recognition standards.']

  Note: 'Master record for commodity buyers and end-market purchasers of recovered materials sold from MRF operations. Captures buyer company name, buyer type (domestic mill, export broker, reprocessor, direct end-user), primary contact name, contact phone, contact email, billing address, shipping address, preferred commodities list, buyer credit status, payment terms, preferred delivery method (FOB MRF, delivered), active/inactive status, and SAP vendor/customer account reference. SSOT for commodity buyer identity within the recycling domain.'
}

Table recycling.market_price {
  market_price_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the market price record. Primary key.']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer organization in the customer master data. Links to Salesforce CRM or SAP SD customer accounts.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recovered commodity (paper, plastics, metals, glass) for which this price applies. Links to the commodity master data managed in SAP MM.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific MRF facility where this pricing applies. Some facilities may have different pricing based on location, processing capabilities, or local market conditions.']
  commodity_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade or specification of the commodity (e.g., Grade 1, Grade 2, Bale Grade A). Impacts pricing and marketability.']
  contamination_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base price to account for contamination levels in recovered materials. Values less than 1.0 indicate price reductions for contaminated loads. Critical for MRF quality control and revenue management.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the sales contract or pricing agreement governing this market price. Links to contract management system for terms and conditions.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this market price record. Supports audit trail and data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market price record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-currency pricing for international commodity sales.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms delivery terms defining when risk and cost transfer from seller to buyer. FOB (Free On Board) means buyer arranges transport, delivered means seller delivers to buyer location, ex-works means buyer picks up at MRF, CIF (Cost Insurance Freight), CFR (Cost and Freight).']
  market_condition varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the current market condition for this commodity. Strong indicates high demand and rising prices, weak indicates oversupply and falling prices, volatile indicates rapid price fluctuations, stable indicates consistent pricing.']
  minimum_order_quantity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum tonnage required for this price to apply. Buyers often require minimum loads for transportation efficiency. Measured in tons.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this market price record. Supports audit trail and data governance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this market price record was last modified. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about this market price (e.g., special conditions, market commentary, quality requirements, negotiation details).']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Standard terms are Net 30, Net 60, or Net 90 days. Impacts cash flow and accounts receivable management.']
  price_adjustment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this market price is reviewed and updated. Daily for spot markets, weekly or monthly for published indices, quarterly or annual for long-term contracts, as-published for index-linked pricing.']
  price_ceiling_per_ton_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum price per ton in contract agreements. Protects buyer from extreme market spikes. Nullable if no ceiling is negotiated.']
  price_change_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change in price compared to the previous pricing period. Positive values indicate price increases, negative values indicate decreases. Used for trend analysis and revenue forecasting.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business explanation for significant price changes (e.g., increased demand from China, mill shutdowns, contamination issues, seasonal demand, transportation cost increases). Supports market intelligence and strategic planning.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this market price becomes effective and applicable for commodity sales transactions. Start of the pricing validity period.']
  price_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this market price expires and is no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended pricing agreements. End of the pricing validity period.']
  price_floor_per_ton_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed price per ton in contract agreements. Protects seller from extreme market downturns. Nullable if no floor is negotiated.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the market index this price is linked to (e.g., OBM #11 Mixed Paper, RISI North American Boxboard Index). Used for index-linked pricing agreements.']
  price_per_ton_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Market price per ton of the commodity in US Dollars. Core pricing metric for MRF commodity sales valuation and revenue forecasting.']
  price_published_date date [note: 'Date when this market price was published or announced by the pricing source (OBM, RISI, etc.). May differ from effective date for forward-looking pricing.']
  price_region varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the market price. National for US-wide pricing, regional for multi-state areas (northeast, southeast, midwest, southwest, west), or local for specific market areas. Commodity prices vary significantly by region due to transportation costs and local demand. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: national|northeast|southeast|midwest|southwest|west|regional|local — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  price_region_code varchar [note: 'Abbreviated code for the pricing region (e.g., NE for Northeast, MW for Midwest, SW for Southwest). Used for reporting and system integration.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Source of the market price data. OBM (Official Board Markets) for paper pricing, RISI for pulp and paper market intelligence, internal contract for negotiated customer agreements, spot market for current trading prices, broker quote, exchange index, or customer contract. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OBM|RISI|internal_contract|spot_market|broker_quote|exchange_index|customer_contract — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this market price record. Active for currently applicable prices, expired for past prices, pending for future prices not yet effective, superseded for prices replaced by newer records, cancelled for withdrawn pricing.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing mechanism. Spot for immediate market prices, contract for long-term agreement prices, index-linked for prices tied to a market index, negotiated for custom pricing, published for industry-published rates, average for calculated averages.']
  transportation_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transportation costs are included in the price per ton. True if price includes delivery (delivered pricing), False if price is FOB (Free On Board) at MRF location.']

  Note: 'Reference record capturing current and historical commodity market prices used for MRF commodity sales valuation and revenue forecasting. Captures commodity reference, price effective date, price expiration date, price source (OBM — Official Board Markets, RISI, internal contract, spot market), price per ton (USD), price region (national, regional, local), currency code, and price type (spot, contract, index-linked). Supports commodity revenue management and diversion rate financial reporting.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_sale {
  commodity_sale_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commodity sale transaction. Primary key for the commodity sale record.']
  buyer_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Commodity buyers are often vendors in reverse logistics/circular supply chains. Linking buyer to vendor master enables consolidated vendor/customer management, rebate tracking, and closed-loop supply ']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer organization purchasing the recovered materials. Links to customer or vendor master data.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the commodity sale record in the system.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for this commodity sale transaction.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the commodity sale record in the system.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility where the commodity was processed and from which the sale originated.']
  recycling_buyer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master commodity sales contract or agreement governing this transaction. Links to contract terms and pricing schedules.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Export sales of recyclable commodities require EPA export notifications and international shipment declarations under RCRA and Basel Convention. Links commodity sales to regulatory export reporting wo']
  adjustment_amount_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any price adjustments, discounts, or surcharges applied to the sale in USD. Can be positive or negative.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number documenting the shipment of commodity materials. Legal document for transportation and proof of delivery.']
  contamination_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall contamination rate percentage for the commodity materials in this sale. Affects pricing and buyer acceptance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commodity sale record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the sale transaction. Typically USD for domestic US operations.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full delivery address where the commodity materials were shipped. Buyer facility or designated receiving location.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City name of the commodity delivery destination.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the commodity delivery destination. Typically USA for domestic sales.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the commodity delivery destination.']
  destination_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the commodity delivery destination.']
  freight_charge_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Freight or transportation charges in USD associated with the commodity sale. May be billed to buyer or absorbed by seller.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was generated for this commodity sale transaction.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number generated for this commodity sale. Links to billing system for accounts receivable tracking.']
  is_export_sale boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this commodity sale is an international export transaction requiring customs documentation and compliance.']
  market_index_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity market pricing index used for this sale. Examples include RISI, Fastmarkets, or regional market indices.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commodity sale record was last modified in the system.']
  net_sale_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final net revenue amount in USD after all adjustments and freight charges. Amount to be invoiced and collected.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the commodity sale transaction. May include special handling instructions, quality issues, or buyer requests.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for the commodity sale is due from the buyer, calculated based on payment terms.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date when payment was received from the buyer for this commodity sale. Null if payment is still outstanding.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms for the commodity sale. Defines when payment is due from the buyer.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Basis for determining the sale price. Indicates whether pricing is based on spot market rates, fixed contract terms, market indices, or negotiated rates.']
  quality_grade_certification varchar [note: 'Quality certification or grade verification documentation for the commodity materials. May reference third-party inspection reports.']
  sale_date date [note: 'Date when the commodity sale transaction was executed and ownership transferred to the buyer.']
  sale_order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the commodity sale order. Externally-known unique reference number used for tracking and reconciliation with buyers and billing systems.']
  sale_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity sale transaction. Tracks progression from order creation through payment completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|shipped|delivered|invoiced|paid|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sap_delivery_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD module delivery document number. Tracks the outbound delivery and goods issue for the commodity shipment.']
  sap_sales_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP SD module sales order document number. System reference for integration with ERP financial and logistics processes.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Date when the commodity materials were physically shipped or picked up by the buyer.']
  shipment_method varchar [note: 'Transportation method used to deliver the commodity to the buyer. Indicates logistics mode for the sale.']
  total_bale_count varchar [note: 'Total number of bales included in this commodity sale transaction across all line items.']
  total_gross_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight in tons including bale wrapping and any packaging materials. Used for transportation and logistics planning.']
  total_net_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total net weight in tons of all commodity materials sold in this transaction. Sum of all line item weights.']
  total_sale_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue amount in USD for the entire commodity sale transaction. Sum of all line item values before adjustments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of commodity sale transactions from a MRF to commodity buyers, including line-item detail for multi-commodity or multi-grade orders. Captures sale order number, sale date, facility reference, buyer reference, shipment method, sale status, payment terms, BOL number, and SAP SD reference. Line items capture commodity type, grade, bale count, net weight (tons), unit price (USD/ton), line total value, and bale ID references. Supports single-commodity and multi-commodity sale orders with full commodity-level revenue tracking, pricing reconciliation against market indices, and integration with billing domain for invoicing.'
}

Table recycling.outbound_shipment {
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound commodity shipment from MRF facility to buyer.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the trailer or container loaded with commodity bales for shipment.']
  carrier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Freight carriers are vendors requiring vendor master management. Links shipments to carrier vendor records for freight invoice matching, carrier performance tracking, and transportation spend manageme']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer organization purchasing the recovered commodity materials.']
  commodity_sale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity sale transaction that this shipment fulfills.']
  dispatcher_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Outbound commodity shipments require dispatcher assignment for logistics coordination, carrier scheduling, BOL preparation, and delivery tracking. Dispatcher accountability is critical for on-time del']
  fleet_fuel_consumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.fleet_fuel_consumption. Business justification: Outbound commodity shipments using company fleet consume fuel tracked for Scope 1 transportation emissions. Business need: Transportation emissions tracking, GHG inventory compilation for logistics, c']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Shipping incidents (loading accidents, vehicle collisions, bale securement failures) must reference the specific shipment for carrier liability claims, DOT incident reporting, and shipment delay docum']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility from which the commodity shipment originates.']
  transport_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Hazmat shipments of certain commodities (batteries in mixed loads, contaminated bales) require DOT transportation permits. Links shipments to permit compliance verification for hazardous material tran']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the truck or vehicle used to transport the commodity shipment.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date when the shipment was received and confirmed at the buyer destination facility.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Unique bill of lading number issued for this shipment, serving as the legal document between shipper and carrier.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association to identify the carrier.']
  commodity_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade classification of the commodity material based on purity, contamination level, and market specifications.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of recovered material being shipped (paper, plastics, metals, glass, or mixed commodities).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound shipment record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation number issued by the buyer upon successful receipt and acceptance of the shipment.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting delivery conditions, exceptions, damages, or special instructions recorded at delivery.']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the loaded vehicle departed the MRF facility gate.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the buyer facility where the commodity shipment is to be delivered.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City name of the buyer destination facility.']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the buyer destination facility.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the buyer destination facility.']
  destination_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code of the buyer destination facility.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Commercial driver license number of the driver, required for DOT compliance and traceability.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver operating the vehicle for this commodity shipment.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Projected date when the shipment is expected to arrive at the buyer destination.']
  freight_charges_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total freight transportation charges for this shipment in US dollars.']
  gross_weight_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the loaded shipment including vehicle, container, and commodity materials, measured in tons.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains or is classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  insurance_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Declared insurance value of the commodity shipment in US dollars for liability coverage.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound shipment record was most recently modified.']
  net_weight_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight of the commodity materials only, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight, measured in tons.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique security seal number applied to the container to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the commodity shipment was dispatched from the MRF facility.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound commodity shipment in the logistics workflow.']
  tare_weight_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty vehicle and container without commodity load, measured in tons.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation conditions.']
  total_bales_shipped varchar [note: 'Total count of commodity bales loaded onto this shipment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a physical outbound commodity shipment from a MRF facility to a buyer. Captures shipment date, MRF facility reference, commodity sale reference, commodity buyer reference, carrier name, vehicle/container ID, BOL number, total bales shipped, gross weight (tons), tare weight, net weight (tons), departure timestamp, estimated arrival date, actual arrival date, shipment status (scheduled, loaded, in-transit, delivered, confirmed), and driver name. Supports commodity logistics traceability and buyer delivery confirmation.'
}

Table recycling.residue_disposal {
  residue_disposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the residue disposal transaction record.']
  disposal_cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific landfill cell where residue was placed, if applicable. Used for landfill capacity tracking.']
  disposal_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Residue disposal to landfills or WTE facilities must comply with waste acceptance permits and manifesting requirements. Enables verification of permitted waste streams, manifest compliance tracking, a']
  disposal_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.disposal_purchase_order. Business justification: Residue disposal events must reference disposal service POs for three-way matching (disposal ticket, PO, invoice), accrual management, and disposal cost tracking - standard procurement control for dis']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver who transported the residue load to the disposal facility.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this disposal transaction for processing and payment.']
  ghg_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_emission. Business justification: Residue disposal (landfilling or WTE) generates Scope 1/3 emissions tracked in GHG inventories. Business need: Waste disposal carbon footprint tracking, emissions from non-diverted material, ESG repor']
  hauling_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle used to transport residue from MRF to disposal destination.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Residue handling incidents (exposure to hazardous waste, vehicle accidents, manifest violations) must link to the disposal event for EPA/OSHA cross-reporting, manifest tracking, and hazardous waste in']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill facility where residue was disposed, if applicable. Null if disposed via WTE or alternative method.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility that generated the residue material requiring disposal.']
  srf_production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.srf_production_line. Business justification: MRF residue (non-recyclable fraction) can be diverted to SRF production lines as feedstock instead of landfill disposal. This represents a real waste-to-energy diversion pathway tracked for sustainabi']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.tsdf_facility. Business justification: MRF residue containing hazardous contamination above de minimis levels must be disposed at RCRA-permitted TSDF facilities rather than standard landfills. Real business process: regulatory compliance f']
  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WTE facility where residue was sent for energy recovery, if applicable. Null if disposed via landfill.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this disposal transaction was approved for payment processing.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading document number tracking the shipment of residue from MRF to disposal facility. Required for chain of custody.']
  contamination_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of contamination level in the residue material. Impacts disposal cost and method selection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this residue disposal record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this transaction.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the residue material was disposed of at the destination facility.']
  disposal_destination_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility receiving the residue material for final disposal or treatment.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Specific method used for residue disposal. Determines environmental impact and regulatory reporting category.']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the residue disposal transaction in its lifecycle from scheduling through final acceptance.']
  disposal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the residue disposal transaction was completed at the receiving facility.']
  environmental_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee assessed for environmental compliance, remediation, or special handling requirements.']
  gross_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of loaded vehicle at weigh-in, measured in tons.']
  hauling_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Transportation cost for hauling the residue from MRF to disposal destination.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the disposal facility for this transaction. Links to accounts payable processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this residue disposal record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special instructions, or observations related to this disposal transaction.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment processing for this disposal transaction.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by disposal facility if residue load was rejected. Null if load was accepted.']
  residue_composition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of residue material composition, contamination types, and any notable characteristics observed during disposal.']
  residue_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of residue material disposed, measured in tons. Used for diversion rate calculation and cost allocation.']
  tare_weight_tons decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of empty vehicle at weigh-out, measured in tons. Net weight is calculated as gross minus tare.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Disposal facility-issued ticket or receipt number confirming acceptance and weighing of the residue load.']
  tipping_fee_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Charge per ton assessed by the receiving facility for accepting and disposing of the residue material.']
  total_disposal_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for this residue disposal transaction, including tipping fees, hauling, and any surcharges.']
  weigh_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loaded vehicle was weighed at the disposal facility scale upon arrival.']
  weigh_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the empty vehicle was weighed at the disposal facility scale after unloading.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the disposal of MRF residue (non-recoverable material) sent to landfill or alternative disposal. Captures disposal date, MRF facility reference, residue weight (tons), disposal destination (landfill site ID, WTE facility ID), disposal method (landfill, WTE, alternative), tipping fee per ton, total disposal cost, BOL or manifest number, hauling vehicle ID, and residue composition notes. Supports residue cost management, diversion rate calculation, and landfill domain integration.'
}

  last_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Tracks most recent maintenance work order for MRF equipment. Essential for equipment history tracking, MTTR analysis, and maintenance cost allocation. Operations teams need quick access to last servic']
  lifecycle_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_lifecycle_stage. Required for operational s']

Links equipment to standard spare parts material master for maintenance planning, inventory management, and equipment lifecycle co']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or capital cost of the equipment in USD. Used for asset valuation, depreciation calculations, and return on investment analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the equipment based on impact to MRF operations if the equipment fails. Used for maintenance prioritization and spare parts inventory planning.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the equipment asset over its useful life. Determines how asset value is expensed over time.']
  design_throughput_capacity_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer-rated processing capacity of the equipment measured in tons per hour. Used for capacity planning, bottleneck analysis, and performance benchmarking.']
  environmental_permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment requires specific environmental permits or air quality permits for operation. Used for EPA and state environmental compliance tracking.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the MRF equipment asset for operational identification and reporting.']
  equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique asset tag or equipment number assigned to this MRF processing equipment. Used for physical identification, maintenance tracking, and cross-system reference.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MRF processing equipment by functional category. Determines the equipment\'s role in the material recovery and sorting process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: baler|optical_sorter|conveyor|eddy_current_separator|magnetic_separator|shredder|glass_breaker|trommel_screen|air_classifier|compactor — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  floor_space_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical footprint of the equipment in square feet. Used for facility layout planning and space utilization analysis.']
  infor_eam_asset_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier to the corresponding asset record in the Infor EAM system. Enables integration for maintenance work orders, parts inventory, and asset lifecycle management.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was installed and commissioned at the MRF facility. Used for age tracking, depreciation calculations, and lifecycle planning.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment record is currently active in the system. Used for filtering active assets in operational reports and dashboards.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or operational inspection performed on this equipment. Used for regulatory compliance and safety audit tracking.']
  last_pm_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent preventive maintenance service was completed on this equipment. Used for maintenance compliance tracking and scheduling.']
  lockout_tagout_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment requires lockout/tagout procedures during maintenance or service per OSHA regulations. Critical for worker safety compliance.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average operational time between equipment failures measured in hours. Key reliability metric used for maintenance planning and equipment performance benchmarking.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the equipment when a failure occurs, measured in hours. Used for downtime planning and maintenance resource allocation.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model number or designation for this equipment type. Used for parts compatibility, technical specifications, and maintenance procedures.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required safety or operational inspection. Used for compliance planning and inspection scheduling.']
  next_pm_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance service on this equipment. Used for maintenance planning and downtime scheduling.']
  noise_level_db decimal(5,2) [note: 'Operational noise level produced by the equipment measured in decibels. Used for OSHA hearing protection compliance and facility noise management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, or operational notes about the equipment.']
  operating_hours_meter decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours recorded for this equipment since installation or last meter reset. Used for usage-based maintenance scheduling and lifecycle tracking.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the MRF equipment. Indicates whether the equipment is available for production, undergoing maintenance, or offline.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical power consumption rating of the equipment in kilowatts. Used for energy cost analysis, electrical capacity planning, and sustainability reporting.']
  safety_certification varchar [note: 'Safety certifications or compliance standards met by the equipment (e.g., UL, CE, OSHA). Used for regulatory compliance and workplace safety verification.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for this specific equipment unit. Used for warranty claims, service history, and asset tracking.']
  sort_line_assignment varchar [note: 'Designation of the specific sorting line or processing line within the MRF where this equipment is positioned. Used for throughput tracking and line balancing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment record was last modified in the system. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected operational lifespan of the equipment in years for depreciation and lifecycle planning purposes.']
  voltage_requirement varchar [note: 'Electrical voltage specification required for equipment operation (e.g., 480V 3-phase). Used for electrical infrastructure planning and safety compliance.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage for this equipment expires. Used for maintenance cost planning and warranty claim eligibility.']
  weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the equipment in pounds. Used for structural load calculations, rigging planning, and relocation logistics.']

  Note: 'Master record for MRF-specific processing equipment assets including balers, optical sorters, conveyors, eddy current separators, magnetic separators, shredders, and glass breakers. Captures equipment ID, equipment name, equipment type, manufacturer, model number, serial number, MRF facility reference, sort line assignment, installation date, design throughput capacity (TPH), operational status, last PM date, next PM due date, and Infor EAM asset reference. Distinct from fleet vehicles — covers fixed MRF processing plant equipment only.'
}

Table recycling.material_composition {
  material_composition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each material composition analysis record at the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  customer_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route from which the sampled inbound load originated. Enables contamination trending by geographic route or service area, supporting targeted customer education programs and route-level diversion rate analysis.']
  diversion_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.diversion_record. Business justification: Material composition analysis determines what percentage of input stream is diverted vs. landfilled, feeding diversion records. Business need: Diversion rate validation, contamination impact quantific']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility where the material composition analysis was performed. Links to the facility master record.']
  inbound_load_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound material load (truck delivery or collection route drop-off) that was sampled for this composition analysis. Nullable when analysis is performed on a sort session sample rather than a specific inbound load.']
  mrf_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Infor EAM asset record for the NIR (Near-Infrared) optical sorter or scanner used when analysis_method is nir_scan. Null for manual sort audit analyses. Supports equipment performance tracking and calibration compliance.']
  primary_material_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Workday HCM workforce record of the technician or analyst who performed the material composition analysis.']
  sort_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF sort line where the sample was collected or processed. Links to the recycling.sort_line product for sort line configuration and throughput context. Supports sort line performance benchmarking and contamination trending by line.']
  sort_session_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF sort line session from which the sample was drawn for composition analysis. Nullable when analysis is tied directly to an inbound load rather than a sort session.']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Material composition analyses detecting hazardous constituents (batteries, electronics, chemicals) trigger waste profiling requirements for proper characterization and disposal. Real business process:']
  aluminum_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of aluminum (primarily UBC — Used Beverage Cans) in the analyzed sample. Aluminum is the highest-value metal commodity recovered at MRFs. Tracked for commodity recovery, diversion rate, and SAP MM inventory valuation.']
  analysis_method varchar [note: 'The technique used to perform the material composition analysis. Manual sort audit involves physical hand-sorting of a sample; NIR (Near-Infrared) scan uses optical sensor technology; grab sample is a single-point collection; composite sample aggregates multiple grab samples across a shift or load.']
  analysis_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this composition analysis record, formatted as MC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-referencing with EPA recycling reports, audit documentation, and SAP MM commodity inventory records.']
  analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material composition analysis record. Draft indicates data entry in progress; pending_review awaits QC supervisor sign-off; approved indicates validated and accepted for reporting; rejected indicates failed QC review; voided indicates cancelled or superseded record.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time when the material composition analysis was physically performed at the MRF. Distinct from record creation audit timestamp. Used for contamination trending and EPA recycling reporting period alignment.']
  clear_glass_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of clear (flint) glass in the analyzed sample. Clear glass commands the highest glass commodity price and is tracked separately from colored glass for accurate market valuation and EPA reporting.']
  colored_glass_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of colored glass (amber, green) in the analyzed sample. Colored glass has lower commodity value than clear glass and is tracked separately for accurate diversion rate and market pricing analysis.']
  contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of non-recyclable contaminants (hazardous materials, food-soiled items, non-accepted materials, residue) in the analyzed sample. Critical KPI for MRF operations, commodity quality, and EPA recycling program compliance. High contamination triggers customer outreach and education programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this material composition analysis record was first created in the system. Populated automatically at record insertion. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer lakehouse processing.']
  diversion_rate_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated recyclable material percentage from this analysis that contributes to the facility diversion rate calculation (total recyclables recovered / total material received). Equals 100% minus contamination% minus residue%. Used for EPA recycling program reporting and circular economy goal tracking.']
  hazardous_material_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any hazardous or household hazardous waste materials identified in the sample. Populated only when hazardous_material_found is True. Supports RCRA incident reporting, Enviance EHS documentation, and DOT compliance.']
  hazardous_material_found boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any hazardous or household hazardous waste (HHW) materials were identified in the sample during analysis. True triggers mandatory incident reporting per RCRA and Enviance EHS protocols. Critical for worker safety (OSHA) and regulatory compliance.']
  hdpe_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene, Resin Code #2) plastics in the analyzed sample. HDPE includes milk jugs and detergent bottles. Tracked separately as a distinct commodity grade with its own market pricing.']
  material_stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material stream being analyzed. Single stream combines all recyclables; dual stream separates fiber from containers; source separated isolates specific commodities; MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) is mixed waste; C&D (Construction and Demolition) waste is construction debris.']
  mixed_paper_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of mixed paper (office paper, magazines, junk mail, paperboard) in the analyzed sample. Mixed paper is a lower-grade fiber commodity. Tracked for diversion rate reporting and commodity market pricing.']
  mixed_plastics_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of mixed plastics (Resin Codes #3-#7, excluding PET and HDPE) in the analyzed sample. Mixed plastics have lower commodity value and may be directed to waste-to-energy or residue streams depending on market conditions.']
  newspaper_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of Old Newspaper (ONP) in the analyzed sample. ONP is a distinct fiber grade with its own commodity market pricing. Tracked separately from mixed paper for accurate commodity recovery reporting.']
  occ_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of Old Corrugated Cardboard (OCC) in the analyzed sample. OCC is a high-value fiber commodity recovered at MRFs. Used for commodity recovery tracking, diversion rate calculation, and SAP MM inventory valuation.']
  organics_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of organic material (food waste, yard waste, compostable material) in the analyzed sample. High organics content in recyclable streams indicates collection program contamination and reduces commodity quality. Tracked for contamination trending and EPA reporting.']
  other_recyclables_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of other recoverable materials not classified in primary commodity categories (e.g., cartons, electronics, textiles, rigid plastics). Tracked to identify emerging commodity recovery opportunities and support diversion rate accuracy.']
  pct_sum_check decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sum of all material category percentages (fiber + plastics + metals + glass + organics + other recyclables + contamination + residue). Should equal 100.00% for a valid analysis. Used for data quality validation and QC review. Values deviating from 100.00 flag data entry errors.']
  pet_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of PET (Polyethylene Terephthalate, Resin Code #1) plastics in the analyzed sample. PET includes beverage bottles and food containers. High-value plastic commodity tracked for recovery rate and EPA recycling reporting.']
  qc_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the QC reviewer during the review process. Documents reasons for rejection, data corrections made, or observations about sample representativeness. Supports audit trail and continuous improvement.']
  qc_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC supervisor completed review of this composition analysis record. Null until QC review is performed. Supports audit trail for EPA reporting and ISO 14001 compliance.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The EPA or regulatory reporting period to which this composition analysis is attributed, formatted as YYYY-QN (quarterly) or YYYY-MM (monthly). Used to aggregate composition data for EPA recycling program reports, state environmental quality department submissions, and SWANA benchmarking.']
  residue_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of material directed to residue (landfill disposal) after MRF processing. Distinct from contamination percentage — residue includes unrecoverable fines, broken glass, and process losses. Used to calculate net diversion rate and landfill disposal costs.']
  sample_source_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the point in the MRF processing stream from which the sample was collected. Inbound load samples represent pre-processing material; sort line feed samples are taken mid-process; post-sort residue samples assess recovery efficiency; bale samples validate outbound commodity quality.']
  sample_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'The gross weight in pounds of the material sample drawn for composition analysis. Minimum representative sample weight per ASTM D5231 is typically 200 lbs for MSW streams. Used to validate statistical representativeness of the analysis.']
  steel_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage by weight of ferrous steel (tin cans, steel containers) in the analyzed sample. Steel is recovered via magnetic separation. Tracked for commodity recovery reporting and SAP MM inventory.']
  total_fiber_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate percentage by weight of all fiber materials (OCC + mixed paper + newspaper) in the analyzed sample. Derived field stored for operational convenience and EPA reporting. Supports fiber commodity recovery trending and sort line performance benchmarking.']
  total_glass_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate percentage by weight of all glass materials (clear + colored) in the analyzed sample. Stored for operational reporting and EPA recycling program compliance. Supports glass recovery trending.']
  total_metals_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate percentage by weight of all metal materials (aluminum + steel) in the analyzed sample. Stored for operational reporting and EPA recycling program compliance. Supports metals recovery trending and commodity revenue forecasting.']
  total_plastics_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate percentage by weight of all plastic materials (PET + HDPE + mixed plastics) in the analyzed sample. Stored for operational reporting and EPA recycling program compliance. Supports plastics recovery trending.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this material composition analysis record was last modified. Updated on every write operation. Supports change data capture (CDC) processing in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of material composition analysis performed on inbound loads or sort session samples at a MRF. Captures analysis date, inbound load or sort session reference, sample weight (lbs), fiber percentage (OCC, mixed paper, newspaper), plastics percentage (PET, HDPE, mixed), metals percentage (aluminum, steel), glass percentage (clear, colored), organics percentage, other recyclables percentage, contamination percentage, analysis method (manual sort audit, NIR scan), and analyst ID. Supports contamination trending, diversion rate accuracy, and EPA reporting.'
}
