Table recycling.recycling_buyer_contract {
  recycling_buyer_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the MRF commodity offtake agreement or sales contract record in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity buyer (counterparty) master record — the organization purchasing recovered materials from the MRF under this contract.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recovered commodity master record (e.g., OCC, HDPE, aluminum, glass) that is the subject of this offtake agreement.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Buyer contracts for commodity sales are specialized service agreements governed by the master contract framework. Operations teams track contract lifecycle, amendments, renewals, terminations, and com']
  contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management employee (typically MRF Commodity Manager or Regional Sales Manager) responsible for managing and maintaining this buyer contract.']
  export_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Export contracts for recyclable commodities require EPA export permits and international waste shipment permits (Basel Convention compliance). Enables verification of export authorization and tracking']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility originating this commodity sales contract. Identifies which processing facility is the selling party.']
  salesforce_contract_id varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM contract record identifier linked to this commodity offtake agreement. Used for cross-system traceability between the MRF data product and the CRM contract management module.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Commodity sales contracts may reference procurement contracts in closed-loop/take-back agreements (e.g., supplier takes back packaging as recyclable commodity). Enables integrated contract management ']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal within the renewal_notice_days period.']
  bale_weight_spec_lbs decimal(8,2) [note: 'The contractually specified target bale weight in pounds for commodity shipments under this agreement. Deviations from this specification may trigger price adjustments or load rejections.']
  base_price_per_ton decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contractually agreed base price per short ton for the commodity in US dollars. For fixed-price contracts, this is the locked price. For index-linked contracts, this may represent the base or floor price. Used in revenue recognition and commodity sales reporting.']
  ceiling_price_per_ton decimal(12,4) [note: 'The maximum price per short ton the buyer will pay, applicable when price_mechanism is \'floor_and_ceiling\'. Caps the MRF\'s upside in exchange for floor price protection. Null for non-floor-and-ceiling contracts.']
  committed_volume_tons_monthly decimal(12,3) [note: 'The contractually committed volume of recovered commodity in short tons per month that Waste Management agrees to supply to the buyer. Used for production planning, route optimization, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  contamination_penalty_basis varchar [note: 'The method by which contamination penalties are assessed: per_ton (rate applied to excess contamination weight), flat_fee (fixed charge per non-conforming load), price_deduction_pct (percentage reduction from invoice price), or load_rejection (load refused and returned at seller\'s cost).']
  contamination_penalty_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contractual penalty rate in US dollars per short ton applied to shipments that exceed the maximum allowable contamination percentage. Used to calculate contamination surcharge deductions from commodity revenue.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional contract terms, special conditions, buyer-specific requirements, or operational notes not captured in structured fields.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commodity offtake agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: binding and in force; Suspended: temporarily paused; Expired: reached end date without renewal; Terminated: cancelled before end date.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commodity sales agreement by duration and commitment structure. Spot contracts are single-transaction; short-term are typically under 12 months; long-term offtake agreements are multi-year committed volume arrangements; evergreen contracts auto-renew; trial contracts are provisional.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this buyer contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed delivery terms defining when title and risk of loss transfer from Waste Management to the buyer. FOB Origin: buyer takes title at MRF loading dock; FOB Destination: title transfers at buyer\'s facility; Ex Works: buyer arranges all transport.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed method for resolving disputes between Waste Management and the commodity buyer regarding contract terms, quality, or payment.']
  diversion_rate_credit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes provisions for diversion rate credits or incentives tied to the MRF\'s overall diversion rate performance. Supports EPA recycling reporting and circular economy goal tracking.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the commodity offtake agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended evergreen contracts.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract grants the buyer exclusive rights to purchase a specific commodity from this MRF facility, preventing Waste Management from selling that commodity to competing buyers during the contract term.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract was formally signed and executed by both parties. May differ from the effective start date for contracts with retroactive or future effective dates.']
  floor_price_per_ton decimal(12,4) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed price per short ton below which the buyer will not pay, applicable when price_mechanism is \'floor_and_ceiling\'. Protects the MRF from extreme market downturns. Null for non-floor-and-ceiling contracts.']
  force_majeure_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains a force majeure clause that suspends obligations in the event of extraordinary circumstances (natural disasters, regulatory shutdowns, pandemics). Relevant for EPA/OSHA emergency compliance scenarios.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'The U.S. state whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this commodity sales contract (e.g., \'TX\', \'CA\', \'FL\'). Relevant for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance.']
  invoicing_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which invoices are generated and submitted to the buyer for commodity shipments under this contract. Drives Oracle Revenue Management billing cycle configuration.']
  max_contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum percentage of contaminants (by weight) permitted in each shipment under this contract before contamination penalty clauses are triggered. Aligns with buyer\'s incoming quality acceptance criteria.']
  minimum_quality_grade varchar [note: 'The minimum acceptable quality grade or specification for the commodity as defined by ISRI or the buyer\'s specification sheet (e.g., \'OCC #11\', \'HDPE Natural Grade A\', \'Zorba 90/10\'). Shipments below this grade may trigger rejection or price deductions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for commodity invoices under this contract (e.g., \'Net 30\', \'Net 15\', \'2/10 Net 30\'). Governs accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting for MRF commodity revenue.']
  price_index_reference varchar [note: 'The published market index or benchmark used to determine the commodity price when price_mechanism is \'index_linked\' or \'floor_and_ceiling\' (e.g., \'RISI Yellow Sheet OCC #11\', \'LME Aluminum\', \'ISRI Zorba\'). Null for fixed-price contracts.']
  price_mechanism varchar [note: 'The pricing methodology governing how the commodity sale price is determined under this contract. Fixed: set price per ton; Index-linked: tied to a published market index; Floor-and-ceiling: bounded index pricing; Negotiated: periodic bilateral negotiation; Revenue-share: buyer and seller split net proceeds.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the contract end date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or not renew the agreement.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains a renewal option clause allowing either party to extend the agreement beyond the original end date.']
  sap_sd_contract_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA SD module contract document number (scheduling agreement or quantity contract) linked to this MRF commodity sales agreement. Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the commodity offtake agreement becomes legally binding and commodity deliveries may commence under its terms.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that expire naturally at end_date or remain active.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the contract was terminated before its scheduled end date. Populated only when contract_status is \'terminated\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: buyer_default|seller_default|mutual_agreement|market_exit|regulatory|force_majeure|non_renewal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transport_responsibility varchar [note: 'Identifies which party is responsible for arranging and paying for transportation of commodity bales from the MRF to the buyer\'s facility. Impacts logistics cost allocation and fleet planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this buyer contract record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  volume_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The allowable percentage variance (plus or minus) from the committed monthly volume before penalty or renegotiation clauses are triggered. For example, 10% means the MRF may deliver between 90% and 110% of committed volume without penalty.']

  Note: 'Master record for commodity offtake agreements and sales contracts between Waste Management MRF operations and commodity buyers. Captures contract number, commodity buyer reference, commodity reference, contract type (spot, short-term, long-term offtake), contract start date, contract end date, committed volume (tons/month), price mechanism (fixed, index-linked, floor-and-ceiling), price index reference, minimum quality grade required, penalty clauses for contamination, contract status (draft, active, expired, terminated), and SAP SD contract reference. Distinct from the contract domain — this is the MRF-specific commodity sales agreement.'
}

Table recycling.recycling_program {
  recycling_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the recycling program master record. Primary key for the recycling_program data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  circular_economy_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_economy_program. Business justification: Recycling programs are operational implementations of circular economy initiatives tracked for ESG reporting. Business need: ESG program tracking, circular economy performance reporting, linking custo']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Recycling programs (municipal/commercial collection and processing services) operate under master service agreements that define service commitments, pricing, SLAs, diversion rate targets, contaminati']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsoring commercial customer account or municipal authority account associated with this recycling program. Applicable for commercial and municipal contract programs. Null for open drop-off programs with no single account sponsor.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility that processes inbound recyclables collected under this program. Links collection operations to MRF processing for end-to-end diversion tracking.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Workday HCM employee identifier for the Waste Management program manager responsible for this recycling program. Accountable for contract compliance, diversion rate performance, and municipal relationship management.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Recycling programs are service offerings and should reference the service catalog to ensure consistency between operational programs and marketed services. This enables unified service management and ']
  accepted_materials_list varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of material categories accepted under this recycling program (e.g., \'OCC,ONP,PET,HDPE,aluminum_cans,steel_cans,glass\'). Drives contamination assessment, MRF sort line configuration, and public education materials. Sourced from Waste Logics container management.']
  amcs_program_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this recycling program as registered in the AMCS Platform for route optimization and fleet dispatching. Enables end-to-end traceability from collection route to MRF processing.']
  annual_tonnage_commitment decimal(12,3) [note: 'Contractually committed annual volume of recyclable material (in short tons) to be collected and processed under this program. Used for MRF capacity planning, commodity revenue forecasting, and contract compliance monitoring.']
  co2e_diversion_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'EPA-approved CO2e emission reduction factor (metric tons CO2e per short ton of material diverted) applied to this program for GHG reporting and sustainability disclosures. Varies by material stream type.']
  collection_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Scheduled day(s) of the week for collection under this program (e.g., \'Monday\', \'Monday,Thursday\'). Used for route planning in AMCS and customer communication via Waste Logics portal. May be a comma-delimited list for multi-day programs.']
  collection_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which recyclable materials are collected under this program. Drives route scheduling in AMCS and tonnage forecasting for MRF capacity planning.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Standard container type provided to participants under this recycling program. Drives container inventory management in Waste Logics and fleet loading configuration in AMCS (e.g., Automated Side Loader (ASL) vs Front End Loader (FEL) compatibility). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cart_96gal|cart_64gal|cart_32gal|bin|dumpster|roll_off|bag — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recycling program record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance.']
  diversion_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of collected waste volume to be diverted from landfill through recycling under this program. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used for EPA recycling reporting, circular economy KPI tracking, and contract SLA compliance.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the recycling program is scheduled to end or has ended. Null for open-ended programs. Used for contract expiry tracking, renewal planning, and EPA annual reporting period boundaries.']
  epa_program_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency assigned identifier for this recycling program, used in federal recycling rate reporting, RCRA compliance submissions, and Solid Waste Information System (SWIS) filings.']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recycling program requires Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reporting under EPA GHG Reporting Program or Clean Air Act (CAA) regulations. When true, CO2e diversion credits must be calculated and reported.']
  iso_14001_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recycling program is subject to ISO 14001 Environmental Management System requirements. True for programs operating under ISO 14001 certified facilities. Drives environmental audit scope in Enviance EHS.']
  lea_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) jurisdiction governing this recycling program. Used for regulatory compliance tracking in Enviance EHS and local solid waste authority reporting.']
  max_contamination_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination percentage for inbound loads under this program before surcharges or rejection apply. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Drives contamination surcharge triggers in Oracle Revenue Management and MRF quality control thresholds.']
  municipality_name varchar [note: 'Name of the municipal authority or local solid waste authority that sponsors or contracts this recycling program. Applicable for municipal curbside and drop-off programs. Null for purely commercial programs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, contract exceptions, or program-specific requirements not captured in structured fields. Used by program managers and MRF operations staff.']
  processing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to inbound loads from this program for MRF sort line scheduling. High priority programs (e.g., municipal contracts with SLA penalties) are processed before standard or low priority commercial loads.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the recycling program, used in AMCS route management, SAP MM commodity inventory, and customer-facing communications. Unique across all active programs.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the recycling program (e.g., \'City of Houston Curbside Recycling\', \'Downtown Commercial MRF Program\'). Used in reporting, customer portals, and EPA recycling disclosures.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the recycling program. Active indicates the program is currently collecting and processing materials. Pending indicates the program is approved but not yet operational. Suspended indicates temporary halt (e.g., due to contamination issues or contract dispute). Closed indicates the program has permanently ended.']
  public_education_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program contract or municipal agreement requires Waste Management to provide public education and outreach materials (e.g., accepted materials guides, contamination reduction campaigns). Drives marketing and communications obligations.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal Waste Management region code identifying the operational region responsible for this recycling program. Aligns with SAP organizational hierarchy and AMCS regional dispatching configuration.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which diversion rate, tonnage, and contamination reports must be submitted to the sponsoring municipality, state agency, or EPA under this program\'s contractual and regulatory obligations.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of net commodity sales revenue shared back with the sponsoring municipality or customer account under this program. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Null if no revenue-share arrangement exists. Tracked in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  service_area_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic boundary served by this recycling program (e.g., municipality name, zip code range, district name, or county). Supports service area reporting and route planning in AMCS.']
  service_area_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the primary state in which the recycling program operates. Used for state environmental agency reporting, permit alignment, and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  sla_response_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which Waste Management must respond to missed collection or service complaints under this program\'s SLA. Tracked in Salesforce CRM for SLA compliance monitoring.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the recycling program became or is scheduled to become operationally active. Used for contract compliance tracking, diversion rate reporting, and program lifecycle management.']
  state_program_code varchar [note: 'State environmental agency assigned identifier for this recycling program. Required for state-level recycling rate reporting and permit compliance with State Environmental Quality Departments.']
  tipping_fee_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contracted tipping fee rate charged per short ton of recyclable material received at the MRF under this program. May be zero or negative (revenue-share) for high-quality commodity streams. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management rate tables.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recycling program record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data processing, change tracking, and audit compliance.']
  vehicle_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of collection vehicle required to service this recycling program. ASL (Automated Side Loader), FEL (Front End Loader), REL (Rear End Loader), or roll-off. Drives fleet assignment in AMCS and Geotab telematics route planning.']

  Note: 'Master record defining recycling collection programs served by MRF facilities, including municipal curbside programs, commercial recycling programs, drop-off center programs, and special collection events. Captures program name, program type, sponsoring municipality or customer account reference, service area (geographic boundary), accepted materials list, program start date, program end date, diversion rate target (%), annual tonnage commitment, program status, and AMCS program reference. Links collection operations to MRF processing for end-to-end diversion tracking.'
}

Table recycling.epa_recycling_report {
  epa_recycling_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each EPA recycling report submission record in the Waste Management lakehouse silver layer.']
  epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRF facility that is the subject of this recycling report submission.']
  esg_disclosure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_disclosure. Business justification: EPA recycling reports provide source data for ESG disclosures (diversion rates, tonnage recycled, contamination rates). Business need: ESG disclosure preparation, regulatory-to-voluntary reporting ali']
  original_report_epa_recycling_report_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original epa_recycling_report record that this submission corrects or supersedes. Populated only when resubmission_flag is True. Enables lineage tracking of report amendment history.']
  primary_epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier for the MRF, used as the official facility reference on all federal recycling report submissions. Corresponds to the EPA facility registry identifier.']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: EPA recycling reports (state diversion reports, EPA WasteWise submissions) are regulatory submissions requiring confirmation numbers, agency acceptance tracking, and deadline management. Consolidates ']
  average_daily_throughput_tpd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average weight in short tons of material processed per operational day during the reporting period. Calculated as total_tons_received divided by processing_days. Key MRF capacity utilization metric reported to regulatory agencies and used for permit compliance verification.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the certifying official signed and certified the accuracy of the recycling report prior to submission. Must precede or equal the submission_date.']
  certifying_official_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized Waste Management official who certifies the accuracy and completeness of the recycling report submission. Regulatory submissions typically require a responsible official certification under penalty of law.']
  contamination_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Average contamination rate as a decimal percentage of inbound recyclable material that was non-recyclable or improperly sorted during the reporting period. High contamination rates impact commodity quality and diversion rates. Reported to state agencies in contamination reduction programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EPA recycling report record was first created in the Waste Management lakehouse. Populated automatically at record insertion. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  diversion_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of total inbound material weight that was diverted from landfill disposal through recycling or recovery during the reporting period. Calculated as total_tons_recycled divided by total_tons_received, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8750 = 87.50%). Core KPI for EPA and state recycling compliance reporting.']
  enviance_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the Enviance EHS system for the corresponding compliance submission record. Enables traceability between the lakehouse data product and the operational EHS system of record.']
  ghg_emissions_avoided_co2e_tons decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) greenhouse gas emissions avoided through recycling activities during the reporting period, calculated using EPA WARM model emission factors. Supports EPA GHG reporting, sustainability disclosures, and circular economy goal tracking.']
  glass_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of glass commodities recovered and diverted from landfill during the reporting period. Required for material-specific recovery reporting to EPA and state agencies.']
  late_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recycling report was submitted after the regulatory submission deadline. True when submission_date exceeds submission_deadline_date. Supports compliance risk monitoring and regulatory penalty tracking.']
  metal_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of ferrous and non-ferrous metal commodities (aluminum, steel, tin) recovered and diverted from landfill during the reporting period. Required for material-specific recovery reporting to EPA and state agencies.']
  organics_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of organic materials (food waste, yard waste, compostables) recovered and diverted from landfill during the reporting period. Increasingly required by state organics diversion mandates.']
  other_commodity_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of recovered materials not classified under paper, plastic, metal, glass, or organics categories (e.g., electronics, textiles, rubber) during the reporting period.']
  paper_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of paper and cardboard commodities (OCC, ONP, mixed paper) recovered and diverted from landfill during the reporting period. Required for material-specific recovery reporting to EPA and state agencies.']
  plastic_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of plastic commodities (PET, HDPE, mixed plastics) recovered and diverted from landfill during the reporting period. Required for material-specific recovery reporting to EPA and state agencies.']
  processing_days varchar [note: 'Number of operational days the MRF processed inbound recyclable material during the reporting period. Used to calculate average daily throughput (Tons Per Day) for regulatory performance benchmarking.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory body to which this recycling report is submitted. EPA indicates federal Environmental Protection Agency; state_environmental_agency indicates the applicable state environmental quality department; local_solid_waste_authority indicates a county or municipal solid waste authority; multi_agency indicates simultaneous submission to multiple regulatory bodies.']
  regulatory_agency_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the specific regulatory agency receiving this report (e.g., \'Texas Commission on Environmental Quality\', \'California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery\'). Provides the precise agency identity beyond the classification in regulatory_agency.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official tracking or confirmation number assigned by the regulatory agency upon receipt or acceptance of the recycling report. Used for correspondence, audit trails, and compliance verification with the agency.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason(s) provided by the regulatory agency for rejecting the recycling report submission. Populated when report_status is \'rejected\'. Used to guide resubmission corrections and track compliance issues.']
  report_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, explanatory comments, or supplemental context associated with this recycling report submission. May include explanations for anomalous tonnage figures, facility disruptions, or agency correspondence notes.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier assigned to this recycling report submission, used for tracking and correspondence with regulatory agencies. Typically formatted as a facility-year-sequence code (e.g., MRF-2024-001).']
  report_preparer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Waste Management employee responsible for preparing and compiling this recycling report. Required on regulatory submissions for accountability and agency correspondence.']
  report_preparer_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the Waste Management employee who prepared this recycling report (e.g., \'Environmental Compliance Manager\', \'MRF Operations Director\'). Required on regulatory submissions.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recycling report submission. Draft indicates report is being prepared; submitted indicates it has been sent to the regulatory agency; accepted indicates agency confirmation of receipt and compliance; rejected indicates agency returned the report for correction; under_review indicates agency is actively reviewing the submission.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recycling report by its regulatory purpose. Annual recycling tonnage reports total materials recovered; diversion rate reports percentage of waste diverted from landfill; material-specific recovery reports recovery by commodity type; quarterly performance reports interim operational metrics; facility summary reports aggregate MRF operational data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual_recycling_tonnage|diversion_rate|material_specific_recovery|quarterly_performance|facility_summary|other — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period covered by this recycling report. Combined with reporting_period_start_date defines the exact temporal scope of all tonnage and performance data in the report.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period covered by this recycling report. Combined with reporting_period_end_date defines the exact temporal scope of all tonnage and performance data in the report.']
  reporting_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the reporting period covered by this submission. Determines the temporal scope of the recycling performance data included in the report.']
  residue_tons decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of material received at the MRF that could not be recovered and was disposed of as residue (landfilled) during the reporting period. Residue rate is a key MRF efficiency metric reported to regulatory agencies.']
  resubmission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this report is a resubmission of a previously rejected or corrected report. True when this record represents a corrected resubmission; False for original submissions. Supports audit trail and compliance history tracking.']
  state_permit_number varchar [note: 'State-issued solid waste facility permit number associated with the MRF at the time of this report submission. Required on state recycling reports to link the submission to the permitted facility.']
  total_tons_received decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of all inbound material received at the MRF during the reporting period, including both recyclable and residue streams. Used as the denominator for diversion rate calculation.']
  total_tons_recycled decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total weight in short tons of all recovered materials processed and diverted from landfill during the reporting period across all commodity streams at the MRF. This is the primary quantitative outcome metric of the recycling report.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EPA recycling report record was last modified in the Waste Management lakehouse. Updated on every status change, data correction, or agency response update. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of EPA and state-mandated recycling performance reports submitted by Waste Management for MRF operations. Captures reporting period (annual, quarterly), MRF facility reference, regulatory agency (EPA, state environmental agency), report type (annual recycling tonnage, diversion rate, material-specific recovery), total tons recycled by commodity, diversion rate achieved, submission date, submission method, report status (draft, submitted, accepted, rejected), regulatory reference number, and Enviance EHS submission reference. Supports RCRA, state solid waste reporting, and EPA recycling program compliance.'
}

Table recycling.mrf_tsdf_disposal_agreement {
  mrf_tsdf_disposal_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each MRF-TSDF disposal agreement record. Primary key for the association.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Materials Recovery Facility that is party to this disposal agreement']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the TSDF facility that is party to this disposal agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this disposal agreement indicating whether shipments are authorized. Active agreements allow shipments; Suspended/Expired/Terminated agreements require resolution before shipments can resume.']
  annual_volume_commitment_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum annual tonnage of waste that the MRF commits to ship to this TSDF under this agreement. Volume commitments typically secure favorable pricing and capacity guarantees.']
  approved_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D, F, K, P, U series) that this specific MRF is authorized to ship to this specific TSDF under this agreement. Subset of the TSDF\'s overall permitted_waste_codes, tailored to the MRF\'s waste streams.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this disposal agreement became effective and the MRF was authorized to begin shipping waste to this TSDF under the agreed terms.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this disposal agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal. MRF must renegotiate or establish alternative disposal arrangements before this date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this disposal agreement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this disposal agreement record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_shipment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent waste shipment from this MRF to this TSDF under this agreement. Used to monitor active vs. dormant disposal relationships and contract utilization.']
  pricing_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contracted disposal fee in dollars per ton that the MRF pays to the TSDF for accepting and processing waste under this agreement. Pricing varies by MRF-TSDF relationship based on volume commitments, waste types, and distance.']
  primary_waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary waste stream that this MRF ships to this TSDF (e.g., contaminated residue, rejected bales, hazmat-contaminated materials, process water treatment sludge). MRFs may have multiple agreements with different TSDFs for different waste streams.']
  total_shipments_ytd varchar [note: 'Count of waste shipments from this MRF to this TSDF in the current calendar year. Used to track contract activity and compare against volume commitments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual disposal agreement between a Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and a Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF). It captures the business relationship where MRFs contract with TSDFs to dispose of contaminated residue, rejected bales, and hazmat-contaminated materials that cannot be recycled. Each record links one MRF to one TSDF with contract terms, approved waste codes, pricing, volume commitments, and shipment tracking that exist only in the context of this specific disposal relationship.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, MRF facilities maintain disposal contracts with multiple TSDF facilities to handle different waste streams (contaminated residue, rejected bales, hazmat-contaminated materials), and each TSDF serves multiple MRF facilities across the service region. The business actively manages these disposal agreements as contractual relationships with specific terms, pricing, approved waste codes, volume commitments, and shipment tracking that vary by MRF-TSDF pairing.'
}

Table recycling.buyer_tsdf_return_agreement {
  buyer_tsdf_return_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this buyer-TSDF return agreement record. Primary key.']
  commodity_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the commodity buyer who has established this contamination return agreement']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the TSDF facility authorized to receive contaminated returns under this agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this buyer-TSDF return agreement. ACTIVE agreements are in force, SUSPENDED agreements are temporarily inactive, EXPIRED agreements have passed their expiration date, TERMINATED agreements have been formally ended.']
  contamination_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination percentage in recovered material bales before rejection and TSDF disposition is triggered under this agreement. Varies by buyer quality standards and commodity type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this buyer-TSDF return agreement record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_cost_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines which party bears the financial responsibility for TSDF disposal costs for contaminated returns. Values: BUYER (buyer pays all costs), WM (Waste Management absorbs costs), SHARED (split per agreement terms), NEGOTIATED (case-by-case determination).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this buyer-TSDF return agreement became effective and operational. Used for determining which agreement terms apply to contamination events.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this buyer-TSDF return agreement expires or requires renewal. Null indicates an evergreen agreement with no fixed expiration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this buyer-TSDF return agreement record was last updated.']
  max_annual_volume_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum tonnage of contaminated material that can be sent to this TSDF facility under this agreement within a calendar year. Used for capacity planning and agreement compliance monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional terms, special conditions, or operational notes specific to this buyer-TSDF return agreement.']
  notification_protocol varchar [note: 'Describes the required communication and notification procedures when contaminated material is identified and routed to the TSDF under this agreement. May include contact sequences, documentation requirements, and timeline expectations.']
  preferred_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes that this buyer-TSDF agreement is specifically designed to handle. Subset of the TSDF facility\'s permitted waste codes, tailored to the buyer\'s typical contamination profile.']
  return_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity buyer must provide explicit written authorization before contaminated material can be sent to this TSDF facility. Affects operational workflow and liability.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual agreement between commodity buyers and TSDF facilities for handling contaminated recyclable material returns. It captures the business rules, cost responsibilities, and operational protocols that govern how rejected or contaminated bales from commodity buyers are disposed of through specific TSDF facilities. Each record links one commodity buyer to one TSDF facility with attributes that define the terms of their contamination disposition relationship.. Existence Justification: In waste management recycling operations, commodity buyers who purchase recovered materials from MRFs establish formal agreements with multiple TSDF facilities to handle contaminated or rejected bales that fail quality standards. Each buyer maintains relationships with multiple TSDFs to cover different contamination scenarios, geographic regions, and waste code requirements. Conversely, TSDF facilities serve multiple commodity buyers under separate contractual terms. The business actively manages these buyer-TSDF agreements as operational contracts with specific contamination thresholds, cost responsibilities, and notification protocols.'
}

Table recycling.facility_initiative_participation {
  facility_initiative_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each facility-initiative participation record']
  carbon_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carbon reduction initiative being implemented at the facility']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MRF facility participating in the carbon initiative']
  actual_reduction_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual measured annual CO2e reduction in metric tons achieved at this facility after initiative implementation. Null until post-implementation measurement is completed.']
  baseline_emissions_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Facility-specific baseline annual emissions in metric tons CO2e measured prior to initiative implementation, used to calculate actual reduction achieved at this facility.']
  facility_contribution_co2e_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected or actual annual CO2e reduction in metric tons attributable to this specific facility\'s participation in this initiative. Facility-level contribution that rolls up to the initiative\'s total target_co2e_reduction_tons.']
  facility_investment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Capital investment allocated to this specific facility for implementing this carbon initiative. Facility-level investment that rolls up to the initiative\'s total capital_investment_amount.']
  facility_project_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the facility-level project manager or site lead responsible for implementing this initiative at this specific MRF facility.']
  facility_role_in_initiative varchar [note: 'Role or phase designation for this facility within the initiative. Values include: pilot (testing/proof-of-concept site), full_implementation (standard rollout), phase_1/phase_2 (staged deployment), control_group (baseline comparison facility).']
  facility_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation status of this carbon initiative at this specific facility. Values: planned (scheduled but not started), in_progress (actively implementing), completed (fully implemented and operational), on_hold (temporarily paused), cancelled (discontinued at this facility).']
  implementation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing facility-specific implementation details, challenges, lessons learned, or deviations from standard initiative approach.']
  participation_end_date date [note: 'Date when this specific MRF facility completed implementation of this carbon initiative or ceased participation. Null if participation is ongoing.']
  participation_start_date date [note: 'Date when this specific MRF facility began participating in or implementing this carbon initiative. May differ from the initiative\'s overall planned_start_date due to phased rollout across facilities.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between MRF facilities and carbon reduction initiatives. It captures facility-specific implementation details, timelines, investment allocation, and CO2e reduction contributions for each initiative at each facility. Each record links one MRF facility to one carbon initiative with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific facility-initiative participation.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, carbon reduction initiatives are implemented across multiple MRF facilities in phased rollouts, and each facility participates in multiple concurrent sustainability programs (e.g., LED retrofits, solar installations, equipment efficiency upgrades). The business actively manages facility-specific implementation timelines, investment allocations, CO2e reduction contributions, and project status for each facility-initiative combination to support ESG reporting, ROI tracking, and initiative portfolio management.'
}

Table recycling.program_target_contribution {
  program_target_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the program-target contribution record. Primary key.']
  recycling_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recycling program that contributes to the sustainability target']
  reduction_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sustainability reduction target being supported by the program']
  actual_contribution_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured contribution value that this program has delivered toward the target, expressed in the target\'s unit of measure. Updated periodically based on operational data from the recycling program.']
  alignment_end_date date [note: 'The date when this recycling program stopped or is scheduled to stop contributing toward this sustainability target. Null for ongoing contributions. Used when programs are retired or removed from target scope.']
  alignment_start_date date [note: 'The date when this recycling program began contributing toward this specific sustainability target. May differ from program start date if target was established later or program was added to target scope after launch.']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The baseline measurement for this specific program in the context of this target, measured in the target\'s unit of measure. Represents the program\'s starting point performance in the baseline year for this target.']
  contribution_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the calculation methodology used to determine this program\'s contribution to the target. May reference specific formulas, data sources, or allocation methods used for this program-target combination.']
  contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the overall target achievement that this specific recycling program is expected to contribute. Used for proportional allocation of target responsibility across multiple programs.']
  last_measurement_date date [note: 'The date when the actual contribution value was last calculated and updated. Used to track data freshness for sustainability reporting and to schedule next measurement cycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting assumptions, exclusions, adjustments, or other relevant context for this program\'s contribution to this target. Used for audit trail and sustainability report footnotes.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this program\'s contribution reporting for this target. Active indicates ongoing tracking and reporting. Pending indicates contribution not yet started. Suspended indicates temporary exclusion. Completed indicates target achieved or program contribution ended. Excluded indicates program removed from target scope.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contribution relationship between recycling programs and corporate sustainability reduction targets. It captures how each recycling program contributes to achieving specific sustainability commitments, tracking baseline performance, actual contribution values, alignment timelines, and reporting status. Each record links one recycling program to one reduction target with attributes that quantify and track the program\'s role in achieving the target.. Existence Justification: In waste management sustainability operations, corporate reduction targets (e.g., 30% diversion rate increase, 50% Scope 1 emissions reduction) are achieved through the coordinated contribution of multiple recycling programs, and each recycling program simultaneously contributes to multiple sustainability targets. The sustainability team actively manages these contribution relationships, tracking each program\'s baseline performance, actual contribution value, contribution percentage, and reporting status for each target it supports. This is an operational management relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_acceptance {
  commodity_acceptance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each commodity-offering acceptance specification']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recovered commodity master']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service offering master']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this commodity acceptance under this service offering. Active = currently accepted, Suspended = temporarily not accepted (e.g., due to market conditions or contamination issues), Discontinued = permanently removed from offering.']
  accepted_grade_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or JSON list of ISRI grade codes accepted for this commodity under this specific service offering. Allows service offerings to restrict which grades are acceptable (e.g., residential service may only accept Grade A OCC, while commercial accepts Grades A and B).']
  contamination_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination percentage for this commodity under this service offering. May differ from the commodity master default to reflect service-level quality requirements (e.g., premium recycling programs have stricter thresholds than standard programs).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity acceptance specification record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity acceptance specification became or will become active for this service offering. Supports versioning of acceptance criteria over time.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity acceptance specification expires or was superseded. Null for currently active specifications. Supports historical tracking of acceptance criteria changes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this commodity acceptance specification record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity acceptance specification record was last updated.']
  minimum_volume_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum volume in pounds of this commodity required per pickup or billing period for this service offering. Null if no minimum applies. Used for commercial/industrial programs with volume commitments.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Method by which the commodity sale price or revenue share is determined for this commodity-offering combination. Values: spot (current market spot price), indexed (tied to published index), fixed (contracted fixed price), revenue_share (percentage of commodity revenue credited to customer), cost_plus (cost recovery plus margin).']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why this commodity is not accepted or was suspended under this service offering (e.g., MARKET_COLLAPSE, CONTAMINATION_ISSUE, EQUIPMENT_LIMITATION). Null for active acceptances.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of commodity sale revenue credited back to the customer under this service offering. Applicable when pricing_basis is revenue_share. Null for other pricing methods.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this commodity acceptance specification record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational acceptance criteria between recovered commodities and service offerings. It captures which commodities are accepted under which service offerings, along with offering-specific quality thresholds, pricing mechanisms, and grade restrictions. Each record links one commodity to one offering with attributes that define the acceptance terms for that specific commodity-offering combination.. Existence Justification: In waste management recycling operations, a single service offering (e.g., Commercial Recycling Program) accepts multiple commodity types (paper, plastic, metal, glass), and each commodity can be accepted under multiple service offerings (residential curbside, commercial front-load, industrial programs). The business actively manages commodity acceptance specifications per offering, including offering-specific contamination thresholds, accepted grade lists, pricing mechanisms, and volume requirements that vary by service tier and customer segment.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_supply_agreement {
  commodity_supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each commodity-vendor supply agreement record']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recovered commodity master, identifying which material type this supply agreement covers']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor master, identifying which supplier or buyer this agreement is with']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this supply agreement: active (currently in effect), inactive (temporarily suspended), pending (negotiated but not yet effective), expired (term ended), terminated (cancelled before term end)']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract or agreement reference number governing the commercial terms for this commodity-vendor relationship. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this supply agreement record was created']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement becomes effective and pricing/terms apply']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement expires or is scheduled for renegotiation']
  lead_time_days bigint [note: 'Number of days from order placement to delivery or pickup for this commodity from this vendor. Lead time varies by vendor based on logistics, processing capacity, and geographic location. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity (in commodity unit of measure) required per order for this commodity from this vendor. MOQ varies by vendor based on transportation economics and processing batch sizes. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms specific to this commodity-vendor agreement, may override vendor master default payment terms for this material type']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred supplier for this commodity based on price, quality, reliability, or strategic relationship']
  quality_grade varchar [note: 'Specific quality grade or specification required by this vendor for this commodity. May differ from the commodity master ISRI grade if vendor has stricter or custom requirements. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  unit_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of measure for this commodity from this specific vendor. Price varies by vendor based on market conditions, volume commitments, and quality requirements. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this supply agreement record was last modified']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual supply relationship between a recovered commodity type and a vendor who supplies or purchases that commodity. It captures vendor-specific pricing, lead times, minimum order quantities, quality grade requirements, and contract terms that exist only in the context of this specific commodity-vendor pairing. Each record links one commodity to one vendor with commercial terms negotiated for that specific material stream.. Existence Justification: In waste management recycling operations, commodities (recovered materials like OCC, mixed paper, plastics) can be sourced from multiple vendors through buy-back programs, commercial purchases, and third-party processors, while vendors supply multiple commodity types with varying pricing and quality terms. The business actively manages commodity supply agreements as contractual relationships with vendor-specific pricing, lead times, minimum order quantities, and quality grade requirements that vary by the commodity-vendor pairing.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_regulatory_applicability {
  commodity_regulatory_applicability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each commodity-regulation applicability mapping record. Primary key.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recovered commodity master record']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement record']
  commodity_regulatory_applicability_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this applicability mapping record. Active mappings drive compliance workflows; inactive mappings are historical.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing commodity-specific compliance considerations, interpretations, or special handling instructions for this regulation (e.g., \'Applies only to batteries containing lithium\', \'Requires pre-notification to state DEP\').']
  compliance_threshold varchar [note: 'Commodity-specific compliance threshold or trigger value that determines when this regulatory requirement applies (e.g., \'contamination >5%\', \'bale weight >1000 lbs\', \'hazardous material present\'). Thresholds vary by commodity type even for the same regulation.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity-regulation applicability mapping was created in the system.']
  documentation_standard varchar [note: 'Specific documentation or record-keeping standard required for this commodity under this regulation (e.g., \'EPA Form 8700-22 for hazardous waste\', \'Bill of Lading with ISRI grade certification\', \'Chain of Custody documentation\'). Documentation requirements vary by commodity type.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory requirement became applicable to this specific commodity. May differ from the regulation\'s general effective date if commodity-specific applicability was determined later or phased in.']
  exemption_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or citation for any exemption granted for this commodity under this regulation (e.g., EPA exemption letter number, state variance approval number).']
  exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether an exemption from this regulatory requirement has been requested or granted for this commodity. Tracks exemption lifecycle.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory requirement ceases to apply to this commodity, if applicable (e.g., due to commodity reclassification, regulation sunset, or exemption granted).']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review or audit confirming this commodity\'s adherence to this regulatory requirement. Used to track compliance verification currency.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this applicability mapping record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this applicability mapping record.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of this commodity against this regulatory requirement. Drives compliance calendar and audit scheduling.']
  requirement_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory requirement is mandatory, conditional (applies only under certain circumstances), recommended, or not applicable for this specific commodity. Drives compliance workflow routing.']
  responsible_compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the compliance officer responsible for managing this commodity\'s compliance with this specific regulation. Enables accountability and contact routing.']
  testing_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of compliance testing or sampling for this commodity under this regulation (e.g., TCLP testing frequency for hazardous commodities, contamination testing for recyclables). Frequency varies by commodity-regulation combination.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this applicability mapping record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory compliance mapping between recovered commodities and applicable regulatory requirements. It captures which specific regulations apply to each commodity type, along with commodity-specific compliance thresholds, testing protocols, and documentation standards that vary by commodity-regulation combination. Each record links one commodity to one regulatory requirement with attributes that define how that specific regulation applies to that specific commodity.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, a single recovered commodity (e.g., lithium batteries, contaminated cardboard, e-waste) is subject to multiple regulatory requirements from different agencies (EPA RCRA rules, DOT transport regulations, state solid waste codes, OSHA handling standards). Conversely, a single regulatory requirement (e.g., RCRA hazardous waste manifest requirements) applies to multiple commodity types (batteries, e-waste, contaminated materials, certain plastics). The business actively manages these mappings because compliance thresholds, testing protocols, and documentation standards vary by commodity-regulation combination—the same EPA regulation may require TCLP testing quarterly for batteries but monthly for contaminated plastics.'
}

Table recycling.equipment_certification {
  equipment_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this equipment certification record. Primary key for the association.']
  certifying_instructor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the instructor or supervisor who certified this employee on this equipment. Used for audit trail and training program management.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who holds certification to operate the equipment']
  mrf_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MRF processing equipment asset that requires operator certification']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current authorization status for this employee-equipment certification. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Determines whether employee can be scheduled to operate this equipment.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the employee was certified to operate this specific equipment. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this equipment certification expires and requires renewal. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Critical for compliance monitoring.']
  incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents recorded for this employee operating this specific equipment. Used for safety performance tracking and recertification decisions.']
  last_proficiency_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent proficiency assessment for this employee on this equipment. Used to track ongoing competency verification.']
  next_proficiency_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next proficiency assessment. Used for compliance scheduling and training program management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding restrictions, special conditions, or observations related to this certification (e.g., \'Authorized for day shift only\', \'Requires supervision for first 40 hours\').']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Assessed proficiency level of the employee operating this specific equipment. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Used for shift assignment and complex operation authorization.']
  total_operating_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours this employee has operated this specific equipment. Used for experience tracking and proficiency advancement decisions.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the employee completed required training for this equipment. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data. Used for OSHA recordkeeping.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the certification relationship between MRF processing equipment and employees authorized to operate it. It captures safety compliance, training completion, proficiency assessment, and authorization status for each employee-equipment pairing. Each record links one piece of MRF equipment to one certified employee with attributes that exist only in the context of this operational authorization relationship. Critical for shift scheduling, OSHA compliance, and safety management.. Existence Justification: In MRF operations, equipment certification represents a true many-to-many operational relationship. Each piece of specialized processing equipment (optical sorters, balers, eddy current separators) requires multiple certified operators across shifts to maintain 24/7 operations, and each MRF employee is typically certified on multiple equipment types to provide operational flexibility. The business actively manages these certifications with proficiency levels, expiration tracking, and authorization status as part of safety compliance and shift scheduling processes.'
}

Table recycling.facility_permit {
  facility_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-permit assignment record. Primary key.']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MRF facility that holds this permit']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the environmental permit held by the facility']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit was formally assigned or associated with this facility in Waste Management\'s compliance tracking system. Used for audit trail and historical tracking.']
  compliance_officer_assigned varchar [note: 'Name of the Waste Management compliance officer specifically assigned to manage this permit at this facility. May differ from the general permit compliance officer if facility-level assignments exist.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this facility with respect to this specific permit. Tracks whether the facility is meeting all conditions, limits, and reporting requirements imposed by this permit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit became effective and binding for this specific facility. May differ from the permit\'s original issue date if the permit was transferred or amended for this facility.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit expires for this specific facility. Critical for renewal tracking and compliance planning. May differ from the master permit expiration if facility-specific amendments exist.']
  facility_specific_conditions varchar [note: 'Additional conditions, limitations, or requirements specific to this facility\'s use of this permit. Supplements the general permit conditions with facility-specific operational constraints.']
  last_compliance_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance report for this permit at this facility was submitted to the regulatory agency. Used to track reporting frequency adherence.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection conducted for this specific permit at this specific facility. Used to track inspection frequency and schedule future inspections.']
  next_compliance_report_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next compliance report for this permit at this facility must be submitted to the regulatory agency. Critical for avoiding reporting violations.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Anticipated or scheduled date for the next regulatory inspection of this permit at this facility. Used for compliance planning and resource allocation.']
  permit_role varchar [note: 'The functional role or purpose this permit serves at this specific facility. A facility may hold multiple permits serving different regulatory purposes (e.g., one for air emissions, one for water discharge, one for waste handling).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit was terminated, revoked, or otherwise ceased to be applicable to this facility. Null if the permit is still active.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory permit assignment relationship between MRF facilities and environmental permits. It captures the specific permit-facility binding with effective dates, compliance status, and inspection history that exist only in the context of this regulatory relationship. Each record links one MRF facility to one permit with attributes tracking the lifecycle and compliance state of that specific permit at that specific facility.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, MRF facilities simultaneously hold multiple environmental permits (air quality, water discharge, stormwater, waste handling, RCRA hazardous waste) issued by different regulatory agencies, each with distinct effective dates, expiration dates, compliance requirements, and inspection schedules. Conversely, regional or multi-facility permits can cover multiple MRF facilities under a single permit number, particularly for air quality or stormwater permits. The business actively manages each facility-permit combination as a distinct compliance entity with its own lifecycle, tracking permit-specific compliance status, inspection history, renewal deadlines, and facility-specific conditions.'
}

Table landfill.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the landfill site. Primary key for all landfill operational, environmental, and compliance data anchored to this facility.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Large landfill sites operate as separate billing entities for host community agreements and inter-company transfers. Site generates internal invoices for corporate waste or bills host community for im']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Landfill sites are major stationary GHG emission sources requiring EPA GHGRP reporting under Subpart HH. Each site must be registered as an emission source for carbon accounting, regulatory compliance']
  regulated_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: Landfill sites are regulated facilities. Compliance domain maintains canonical regulated facility registry with EPA IDs, RCRA status, Title V designation, and compliance risk levels that landfill oper']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Landfill sites must implement site-specific safety programs for OSHA compliance (hazard communication, emergency response, confined space). Regulatory requirement and operational necessity for trackin']
  site_enviance_facility_id varchar [note: 'Facility identifier as configured in the Enviance EHS system, which is the system of record for environmental compliance, permit tracking, and incident management at this facility. Used for cross-system data integration and compliance record linkage.']
  site_epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier used for federal environmental reporting, RCRA compliance tracking, and CERCLA/Superfund obligations. Format: two-letter state code followed by 10-digit numeric identifier.']
  tracked_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.tracked_site. Business justification: Landfill sites are sustainability-tracked facilities for ESG reporting boundary definition, GHG inventory scope determination, and regulatory compliance (EPA GHGRP, state programs). Essential for CDP,']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the landfill facility. Used for regulatory correspondence, emergency response coordination, and logistics routing.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the landfill facility (suite, building, gate number, or additional location descriptor).']
  airspace_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engineering airspace survey used to calculate remaining airspace. Ensures remaining_airspace_cy reflects current conditions and supports audit trail for capacity reporting.']
  cap_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of final cover (cap) system installed or planned for the landfill. The cap is the final cover system placed over closed portions of the landfill to minimize infiltration and control LFG emissions. \'final_cap\' indicates permanent closure cap installed; \'interim_cap\' indicates temporary cover on inactive areas; \'none\' indicates active filling area; \'planned\' indicates cap design approved but not yet constructed.']
  cercla_status varchar [note: 'Current CERCLA/Superfund status of the facility. Indicates whether the site is listed on the National Priorities List (NPL) or subject to CERCLA remedial action obligations. Drives legal liability accruals and environmental compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_listed|npl_listed|remedial_investigation|remedial_action|completed|other — promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'Municipality or city in which the landfill facility is located. Used for jurisdictional determination, local enforcement agency (LEA) assignment, and host community agreement management.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory and operational classification of the landfill facility. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) accepts household and commercial waste; C&D (Construction and Demolition) accepts construction debris; monofill accepts a single waste type; industrial accepts non-hazardous industrial waste; hazardous is a RCRA Subtitle C facility. Drives applicable regulatory framework and permitted waste streams.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the landfill facility ceased accepting waste and initiated the formal closure process. Null for active facilities. Triggers post-closure care obligations under RCRA Subtitle D including 30-year monitoring requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the facility location (e.g., USA). Supports multi-country enterprise reporting and regulatory framework determination.']
  county varchar [note: 'County in which the landfill facility is located. Critical for determining applicable local solid waste authority jurisdiction, host community agreement obligations, and county-level regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the landfill site master record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per SOX internal controls.']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is subject to mandatory greenhouse gas (GHG) reporting under EPA 40 CFR Part 98 (Subpart HH for MSW landfills). Facilities exceeding the 25,000 metric ton CO2e threshold must report annually. Drives GHG data collection workflows in Enviance EHS.']
  groundwater_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is subject to groundwater monitoring requirements under RCRA Subtitle D or state permit conditions. Drives scheduling of monitoring well sampling events and Enviance EHS compliance tracking.']
  host_community_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the host community agreement (HCA) between the facility operator and the local municipality. HCAs govern tipping fee sharing, traffic mitigation, odor controls, and community benefit obligations. Links to contract management records in Salesforce CRM.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the landfill facility centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for geospatial analysis, route optimization, emergency response, and environmental monitoring zone mapping.']
  lea_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) responsible for routine inspection and enforcement at the facility. In California and other states, LEAs are county-level agencies delegated enforcement authority by the state. Required for compliance contact management and inspection scheduling.']
  leachate_management_type varchar [note: 'Type of leachate collection and management system installed at the facility. Leachate is the liquid that drains through the waste mass and must be managed to prevent groundwater contamination. Drives operational costs, permit conditions, and Clean Water Act (CWA) compliance obligations.']
  lfg_collection_system boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active landfill gas (LFG) collection and control system is installed at the facility. LFG systems capture methane for waste-to-energy (WTE) or renewable natural gas (RNG) conversion, and are required under EPA New Source Performance Standards (NSPS) for qualifying facilities.
