  inspection_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number, file path, or URL to the official inspection report issued by the regulatory agency.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the areas, systems, and operations included in the inspection scope (e.g., active face, leachate collection system, LFG system, groundwater monitoring wells, daily cover operations, liner integrity).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record (scheduled, in-progress, completed, closed, or pending response from facility).']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection based on its purpose and trigger (routine scheduled, complaint-driven, follow-up to previous findings, special investigation, unannounced, or pre-operational).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context related to the inspection that do not fit into structured fields, including inspector comments or facility explanations.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount assessed by the regulatory agency for violations identified during the inspection, in US dollars.']
  rcra_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall RCRA Subtitle D compliance status determination resulting from the inspection (compliant, non-compliant, or conditional compliance with corrective action plan).']
  regulatory_citations varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory code sections, permit conditions, or rule citations that were violated or referenced in the inspection findings (e.g., 40 CFR 258.28, state permit condition 4.2.1).']
  responsible_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the facility manager or compliance officer responsible for coordinating the inspection response and corrective action implementation.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Overall severity classification of the inspection findings (minor, moderate, major, or critical) based on environmental risk and regulatory impact.']
  swis_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this inspection must be reported to the EPA Solid Waste Information System (SWIS) (True) or not (False).']
  swis_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspection data was submitted to the EPA Solid Waste Information System for regulatory reporting.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct violations or deficiencies cited in the inspection report.']
  violations_cited varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any regulatory violations cited during the inspection, including specific non-compliance issues identified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of regulatory agency inspections conducted at a landfill site. Captures inspection date, inspecting agency (EPA, state, LEA), inspector name and credentials, inspection type (routine, complaint-driven, follow-up), areas inspected (active face, leachate system, LFG system, groundwater monitoring), findings and violations cited (with regulatory citation), corrective action required, corrective action due date, and inspection closure status. Supports RCRA Subtitle D compliance tracking and CERCLA/Superfund obligation management. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table landfill.closure_plan {
  closure_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the landfill closure and post-closure care plan record. Primary key.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific landfill cell covered by this closure plan. Nullable if the plan applies to the entire site rather than a single cell.']
  disposal_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.disposal_permit. Business justification: Closure plans are regulatory documents that must be approved by the permitting authority under the disposal permit. The closure plan defines cap system design, post-closure care period, monitoring sch']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill site for which this closure plan is defined.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Closure plans span 30+ years and require clear ownership for regulatory compliance, financial assurance, and knowledge transfer. Linking to employee (beyond responsible_party text field) enables accou']
  actual_closure_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which all closure construction activities were completed and the final cap system was installed.']
  actual_closure_start_date date [note: 'Actual date on which closure activities commenced.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) that approved the closure plan.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority approved the closure plan.']
  cap_barrier_material varchar [note: 'Type of impermeable barrier material used in the cap system (e.g., geomembrane, clay, composite liner).']
  cap_drainage_system varchar [note: 'Description of the drainage layer and system designed to manage stormwater runoff and prevent infiltration into the waste mass.']
  cap_inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of visual inspections of the final cap system during the post-closure care period (e.g., quarterly, semi-annually).']
  cap_layer_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct layers in the final cap system (e.g., barrier layer, drainage layer, soil layer, vegetation layer).']
  cap_system_design varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the final cover system design, including layer composition, materials, and engineering specifications.']
  cap_total_thickness_inches decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total thickness of the final cap system measured in inches, from the waste surface to the top of the vegetation layer.']
  cap_vegetation_type varchar [note: 'Type of vegetation to be established on the final cap surface (e.g., native grasses, low-maintenance groundcover).']
  cost_estimate_basis varchar [note: 'Methodology or standard used to prepare the cost estimate (e.g., third-party engineer estimate, regulatory cost formula).']
  cost_estimate_date date [note: 'Date on which the closure and post-closure cost estimates were prepared or last updated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this closure plan record was first created in the system.']
  engineer_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional engineering license number of the responsible engineer.']
  estimated_closure_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for completing all closure activities, including final cap installation, grading, and site preparation.']
  estimated_post_closure_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost for the 30-year post-closure care period, including monitoring, maintenance, and reporting activities.']
  financial_assurance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of financial assurance secured to cover closure and post-closure care costs.']
  financial_assurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or renewal date of the financial assurance instrument, if applicable.']
  financial_assurance_mechanism varchar [note: 'Type of financial assurance instrument used to demonstrate the ability to fund closure and post-closure care as required under RCRA Subtitle D.']
  financial_assurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the institution or entity providing the financial assurance instrument (e.g., bank, insurance company, surety).']
  groundwater_sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of groundwater monitoring well sampling during the post-closure care period (e.g., quarterly, semi-annually).']
  leachate_monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of leachate collection system inspections and monitoring during the post-closure care period.']
  lfg_monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of landfill gas monitoring activities during the post-closure care period, including Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) readings.']
  monitoring_schedule varchar [note: 'Detailed schedule of post-closure monitoring activities, including frequency and timing of inspections, sampling, and reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the closure plan.']
  plan_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL to the full closure plan document.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the closure plan, typically assigned by the regulatory authority or internal tracking system.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the closure plan in the regulatory approval and implementation process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|active|amended|superseded|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan scope: closure only, post-closure care only, or combined closure and post-closure plan.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the closure plan document, incremented with each revision or amendment.']
  planned_closure_completion_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for completion of all closure construction activities, including final cap installation.']
  planned_closure_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for commencement of closure activities for the landfill site or cell.']
  post_closure_care_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the post-closure care period, typically 30 years after closure completion unless extended by regulatory authority.']
  post_closure_care_period_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the post-closure care period in years, typically 30 years as required under RCRA Subtitle D.']
  post_closure_care_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the 30-year post-closure care period begins, typically following completion of closure construction.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory agency approval for this closure plan.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the professional engineer who prepared or certified the closure plan design.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name of the legal entity or organization responsible for implementing and funding the closure and post-closure care plan.']
  revision_history varchar [note: 'Summary of major revisions and amendments to the closure plan, including dates and reasons for changes.']
  settlement_survey_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of topographic surveys to monitor settlement and subsidence of the final cap during post-closure care.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this closure plan record was last modified in the system.']
  vegetation_assessment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of vegetation health and erosion control assessments on the final cap during post-closure care.']

  Note: 'Master record for the closure and post-closure care plan for each landfill site or cell, including the 30-year post-closure monitoring program. Captures plan version, regulatory approval status, planned closure date, final cap system design (cap layers, materials, thickness), post-closure care period and monitoring schedule, estimated closure and post-closure costs, financial assurance mechanism (trust fund, surety bond, letter of credit, insurance), financial assurance amount, post-closure monitoring activities (cap inspection, settlement survey, LFG monitoring, groundwater sampling, leachate check, vegetation assessment), and plan revision history. Required under RCRA Subtitle D for all permitted MSW landfills.'
}

Table landfill.stormwater_event {
  stormwater_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stormwater management event record.']
  compliance_environmental_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_environmental_monitoring. Business justification: Stormwater monitoring events are compliance environmental monitoring. Linking to canonical compliance monitoring record enables NPDES DMR reporting, exceedance tracking, and BMP effectiveness assessme']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or contractor who conducted the stormwater event inspection or monitoring.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Stormwater monitoring and BMP maintenance involve slip/fall hazards, confined space entry (outfalls), and chemical exposure. Incidents during stormwater events must link to specific monitoring activit']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill site where the stormwater event occurred.']
  outfall_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the discharge point where stormwater exits the landfill site, as designated in the NPDES (National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System) permit.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Stormwater discharges from landfills are NPDES-permitted. Each event must reference the permit establishing discharge limits for TSS, pH, BOD, and metals to track permit compliance and trigger correct']
  bmp_deficiencies varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any deficiencies or maintenance needs identified during BMP inspection, such as erosion, sediment accumulation, damaged controls, or inadequate coverage.']
  bmp_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Assessment result of the BMP inspection. Satisfactory indicates controls are functioning as designed, needs maintenance indicates minor repairs required, deficient indicates significant issues requiring prompt attention, and failed indicates BMP is not functioning and requires immediate corrective action.']
  bod_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of biochemical oxygen demand in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. BOD indicates the amount of organic pollution and oxygen depletion potential.']
  cod_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of chemical oxygen demand in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. COD measures total organic and inorganic oxidizable matter.']
  copper_mg_l decimal(10,6) [note: 'Concentration of copper in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Copper is a regulated heavy metal that can be toxic to aquatic life.']
  corrective_action_date date [note: 'Date when corrective actions were completed to address permit exceedances or BMP deficiencies.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions implemented in response to permit exceedances or identified deficiencies in stormwater management practices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stormwater event record was first created in the Enviance EHS system.']
  discharge_flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Rate of stormwater discharge measured in gallons per minute at the time of monitoring.']
  discharge_volume_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total volume of stormwater discharged from the outfall during the event, measured in gallons.']
  event_date date [note: 'Date when the stormwater event occurred or was observed.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the stormwater event, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stormwater event record in the compliance workflow.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the stormwater event occurred or monitoring was performed.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stormwater management event. Discharge monitoring tracks outfall water quality, BMP (Best Management Practice) inspection verifies erosion and sediment controls, spill response addresses contamination incidents, routine inspection covers scheduled site walkthroughs, corrective action implements remediation measures, and emergency response handles urgent environmental threats.']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether any monitored parameter exceeded the NPDES permit limits during this event. True indicates a permit violation requiring corrective action and regulatory notification.']
  exceedance_parameters varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific parameters that exceeded NPDES permit limits during this event (e.g., TSS, pH, lead). Used for compliance tracking and corrective action planning.']
  inspector_certification varchar [note: 'Professional certification or qualification held by the inspector, such as Certified Professional in Erosion and Sediment Control (CPESC) or Qualified Stormwater Manager (QSM).']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified individual who performed the stormwater inspection or monitoring, as required by NPDES permit.']
  lab_analysis_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory completed analysis of the stormwater sample and issued results.']
  lab_sample_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the analytical laboratory to the stormwater sample for chain-of-custody tracking and results reporting.']
  lead_mg_l decimal(10,6) [note: 'Concentration of lead in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Lead is a toxic heavy metal regulated under NPDES permits.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stormwater event record was last updated in the Enviance EHS system.']
  monitoring_location varchar [note: 'Specific location or area within the landfill site where the stormwater event monitoring or inspection was conducted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations, comments, or contextual information about the stormwater event that may be relevant for compliance documentation or operational decision-making.']
  oil_grease_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of oil and grease in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Indicates petroleum-based contamination from vehicles and equipment.']
  permit_limit_ph_max decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable pH level specified in the NPDES permit for this outfall.']
  permit_limit_ph_min decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable pH level specified in the NPDES permit for this outfall.']
  permit_limit_tss_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of total suspended solids specified in the NPDES permit for this outfall.']
  ph_level decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measure of acidity or alkalinity of the stormwater discharge on a scale of 0-14, with 7 being neutral. Critical parameter for assessing water quality and permit compliance.']
  precipitation_amount_inches decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total rainfall measured in inches during the monitoring period or prior to the discharge event, used to assess stormwater runoff volume and pollutant loading.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this stormwater event data has been submitted to the regulatory authority as part of NPDES permit compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the stormwater event data was submitted to EPA or delegated state authority for NPDES permit compliance reporting.']
  sample_collected_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a physical water sample was collected during this event for laboratory analysis.']
  total_nitrogen_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total nitrogen concentration in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Nitrogen is a nutrient pollutant that can cause eutrophication in receiving waters.']
  total_phosphorus_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total phosphorus concentration in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Phosphorus is a nutrient pollutant that contributes to algal blooms and water quality degradation.']
  tss_mg_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of total suspended solids in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. TSS is a key indicator of sediment and particulate pollution.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of the stormwater event, including precipitation type, intensity, wind, and temperature. Weather context is critical for interpreting discharge characteristics and permit compliance.']
  zinc_mg_l decimal(10,6) [note: 'Concentration of zinc in the stormwater discharge, measured in milligrams per liter. Zinc is a regulated heavy metal commonly found in stormwater runoff.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of stormwater management events at a landfill site including discharge monitoring, best management practice (BMP) inspections, and NPDES permit compliance checks. Captures event date, event type (discharge monitoring, BMP inspection, spill response), monitoring location, precipitation amount (inches), discharge volume (gallons), discharge point (outfall ID), analytical results (TSS, pH, BOD, metals), NPDES permit limit comparison, exceedance flag, and corrective action taken. Supports Clean Water Act NPDES stormwater permit compliance. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table landfill.compaction_record {
  compaction_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for compaction_record']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific landfill cell where compaction was performed. Links to the cell master record for the active working face.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator who performed the compaction operation. Links to workforce records for productivity and safety tracking.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Compaction operations are high-incident activities involving heavy equipment, struck-by hazards, and working face instability. Direct link between compaction records and incidents enables analysis of ']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Compaction is a core high-hazard landfill task requiring job hazard analysis for heavy equipment operation, working face hazards, and environmental conditions. JHA must be linked to compaction activit']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill site where the compaction operation was performed. Links to the site master record.']
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Compaction equipment breakdowns during operations generate work orders. Tracking which work order addressed equipment issues during specific compaction activities helps analyze equipment reliability, ']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compactor equipment unit used for this operation. Links to fleet asset master for maintenance correlation and equipment performance tracking.']
  achieved_compaction_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'The actual in-place density achieved during this compaction operation, measured in pounds per cubic yard. Calculated from waste tonnage and volume after compaction. Key performance indicator for operational efficiency.']
  compaction_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the compaction operation was performed at the landfill working face. Primary business event date for this transaction.']
  compaction_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the compaction operation was completed. Used to calculate duration and operational efficiency metrics.']
  compaction_method varchar [note: 'The operational method or technique used for compaction. Different methods achieve different density results and are selected based on waste type and operational goals.']
  compaction_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the compaction operation began at the working face. Supports operational efficiency tracking and shift analysis.']
  compaction_variance_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between achieved and target compaction ratios. Positive values indicate over-achievement, negative values indicate under-achievement. Used for operational performance monitoring.']
  cover_material_type varchar [note: 'The type of daily cover material applied after compaction, if applicable. Alternative Daily Cover (ADC) options may be used per permit requirements.']
  cover_material_volume_cy decimal(10,2) [note: 'The volume of cover material applied in cubic yards. Tracked for airspace consumption calculations and material inventory management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compaction record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance.']
  daily_cover_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether daily cover material was applied following this compaction operation. Required for regulatory compliance under RCRA Subtitle D.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or method from which this compaction record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and quality assessment.']
  equipment_hours_logged decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total equipment operating hours logged during this compaction operation. Used for preventive maintenance (PM) scheduling and asset lifecycle management.']
  fuel_consumed_gallons decimal(8,2) [note: 'The volume of fuel consumed by the compactor equipment during this operation in gallons. Sourced from Geotab telematics for cost tracking and environmental reporting.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'The type of fuel used by the compactor equipment. CNG = Compressed Natural Gas, RNG = Renewable Natural Gas. Critical for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions calculations and sustainability reporting.']
  interruption_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for any interruption or cancellation of the compaction operation. Captured for operational analysis and continuous improvement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this compaction record was last modified in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated moisture content percentage of the waste material being compacted. Affects compaction effectiveness and leachate generation potential.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional operational observations, special conditions, or contextual information about the compaction operation.']
  number_of_passes varchar [note: 'The count of compaction passes made over the waste material during this operation. Key operational metric for achieving target compaction ratios.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this compaction operation record. Tracks the lifecycle state of the operation from initiation through completion.']
  quality_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compaction operation passed quality control inspection. Used to identify operations requiring corrective action or re-work.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any safety incident occurred during this compaction operation. Triggers incident investigation and OSHA reporting workflows.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The operational shift during which the compaction operation was performed. Used for workforce planning and productivity analysis.']
  target_compaction_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'The design compaction ratio target in pounds per cubic yard that operations aim to achieve. Sourced from cell design specifications and permit requirements.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ambient air temperature in degrees Fahrenheit during the compaction operation. Temperature affects waste characteristics and equipment performance.']
  volume_after_compaction_cy decimal(12,2) [note: 'The measured volume of waste in cubic yards after compaction operations were completed. Critical for airspace consumption tracking and capacity planning.']
  volume_before_compaction_cy decimal(12,2) [note: 'The estimated volume of waste in cubic yards before compaction operations began. Used to calculate compaction effectiveness and airspace optimization.']
  waste_tonnage_compacted decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total weight in tons of waste material compacted during this operation. Sourced from tonnage receipt records for the working face during the compaction period.']
  waste_type_compacted varchar [note: 'The primary classification of waste material compacted during this operation. MSW = Municipal Solid Waste, C&D = Construction and Demolition Waste. Affects achievable compaction ratios and operational planning.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'The prevailing weather conditions during the compaction operation. Weather impacts compaction effectiveness, equipment performance, and operational safety. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Clear|Cloudy|Rain|Snow|Fog|Wind|Extreme Heat|Extreme Cold — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  working_face_height_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'The vertical height of the active working face in feet during this compaction operation. Critical for slope stability and operational safety monitoring.']
  working_face_length_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'The length dimension of the active working face in feet during this compaction operation. Used for operational planning and airspace calculations.']
  working_face_width_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'The width dimension of the active working face in feet during this compaction operation. Used for operational planning and airspace calculations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of compaction operations performed at the active landfill face. Captures compaction date, cell reference, equipment used (compactor model, equipment ID), number of passes, waste tonnage compacted, volume before compaction (cubic yards), volume after compaction (cubic yards), achieved compaction ratio (lbs/cubic yard), target compaction ratio, working face dimensions, and operator ID. Supports operational efficiency tracking, airspace optimization, and comparison against design compaction ratios. Sourced from AMCS Platform and field operations logs.'
}

Table landfill.post_closure_monitoring {
  post_closure_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the post-closure monitoring activity record.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific closed landfill cell being monitored. Nullable if monitoring applies to entire site.']

Business justification: Post-closure monitoring activities are performed according to the closure plan, which defines monitoring frequencies, parameters, and duration (post_closure_care_period_years). Each monitoring record ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or contractor who performed the monitoring activity.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Post-closure monitoring activities involve ongoing site hazards (cap integrity, gas migration, vegetation maintenance). Incidents during monitoring must be traceable to specific monitoring events for ']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill site where post-closure monitoring is performed.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Post-closure monitoring is permit-mandated (Subtitle D regulations). Each monitoring activity must reference the permit establishing monitoring frequency, parameters, and duration to ensure 30-year po']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Type of post-closure monitoring activity performed: cap inspection, settlement survey, Landfill Gas (LFG) monitoring, groundwater sampling, leachate system check, or vegetation assessment.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Current closure status of the site or cell at the time of monitoring.']

  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost in US dollars for conducting this monitoring activity, including labor, equipment, and laboratory analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring record was first created in the system.']

  maintenance_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance or corrective action is required based on monitoring findings.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments related to the monitoring activity.']
  photo_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to photographic documentation of monitoring activity and findings.']
  post_closure_care_year varchar [note: 'Year number within the 30-year post-closure care period (1 through 30) as required by RCRA Subtitle D.']
  precipitation_last_24h_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Precipitation amount in inches during the 24 hours prior to monitoring activity.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency overseeing post-closure care (EPA, state environmental agency, or Local Enforcement Agency).']
  regulatory_reporting_period varchar [note: 'Reporting period identifier for regulatory submissions (e.g., Q1-2024, Annual-2024).']
  report_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the monitoring report submitted to regulators.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date when monitoring report was submitted to the regulatory agency.']
  report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether monitoring results have been submitted to the regulatory agency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this record originated (e.g., Enviance EHS).']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the time of monitoring.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of monitoring activity (e.g., clear, rain, snow, wind).']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work Order number generated for maintenance or corrective actions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of post-closure care activities performed at closed landfill cells or sites. Captures monitoring date, site or cell reference, closure status (closed, post-closure care period), activity type (cap inspection, settlement survey, LFG monitoring, groundwater sampling, leachate system check, vegetation assessment), findings, maintenance actions taken, post-closure care year (1 through 30), regulatory reporting period, and compliance status. Required under RCRA Subtitle D for the 30-year post-closure care period. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table landfill.special_waste_approval {
  special_waste_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the special waste approval record. Primary key.']

Essential for contract compliance verification and special waste revenu']

The permit defines the permitted waste types and conditions under which special wastes may be accepted. Each approval decis']
  generator_epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Ne']
  hauler_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hauler or transporter responsible for delivering the special waste to the landfill site.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill site where the special waste is proposed for disposal.']

Business justification: Special waste approvals are triggered by specific service offerings requiring non-standard waste acceptance. Tracking the originating service offering is essential for approval workflow routing, prici']

  proposed_disposal_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific landfill cell proposed for disposal of the special waste stream.']
  waste_generator_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the entity that generated the special waste stream requiring approval.']

  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the special waste approval record was last modified or updated.']

  request_date date [note: 'Date when the special waste approval request was submitted for review.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the special waste approval request was received in the system.']

  tclp_results_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether TCLP analytical test results have been provided as part of the waste characterization data.']
  tclp_test_date date [note: 'Date when the TCLP analytical testing was performed on the waste sample.']
  waste_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the special waste stream based on RCRA Subtitle D criteria and state-specific waste acceptance standards.']
  waste_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the special waste stream including physical characteristics, composition, and any known contaminants or hazardous properties.']
  waste_origin_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the facility or location where the special waste was generated.']
  waste_origin_city varchar [note: 'City where the special waste was generated.']
  waste_origin_description varchar [note: 'Description of the industrial process, facility, or activity that generated the special waste stream.']
  waste_origin_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the location where the special waste was generated.']
  waste_origin_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the special waste was generated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of pre-approval decisions for non-standard or special waste streams proposed for disposal at a landfill. Captures approval request date, requesting customer or hauler, waste description, waste origin, estimated volume (tons or cubic yards), proposed disposal cell, waste characterization data (TCLP results, analytical profile), regulatory classification determination, approval or rejection decision, approved disposal conditions, approval expiration date, and approving authority. Ensures only permitted waste types are accepted and supports RCRA Subtitle D waste acceptance criteria compliance. Sourced from Enviance EHS and Waste Logics.'
}

Table landfill.site_authorization {
  site_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this service offering-landfill site authorization record. Primary key.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service offering that is authorized to use this landfill site']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the landfill site that is authorized to accept waste from this service offering']
  approved_waste_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of specific waste type codes from this service offering that are permitted for disposal at this site (e.g., MSW, C&D, yard_waste). Subset of offering\'s waste types filtered by site\'s permit restrictions.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this authorization. active=currently in use, suspended=temporarily disabled, pending=approved but not yet effective, expired=past end date.']
  average_haul_distance_miles decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average one-way haul distance in miles from the service offering\'s typical service area centroid to this landfill site. Used for route planning and environmental impact reporting.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was created in the system']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service offering-site authorization was or will be terminated. Null for currently active authorizations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this service offering-site authorization became or will become active for routing and disposal operations']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this authorization record']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was last updated']
  maximum_daily_tonnage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum tons per day that can be disposed from this service offering at this landfill site. Used for capacity planning and route optimization to prevent site overload.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this site when routing waste from this service offering (1=highest priority). Used by route optimization algorithms to prefer certain sites based on cost, distance, or capacity.']
  requires_special_handling boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waste from this service offering requires special handling procedures at this site (e.g., separate cell, additional inspection, manifest verification)']
  transportation_cost_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transportation cost in USD per ton to haul waste from this service offering\'s service area to this landfill site. Used for cost modeling and profitability analysis.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this authorization record']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational authorization between a service offering and a landfill site. It captures the business rules governing which waste streams from which service offerings are permitted to be disposed at which landfill sites, including capacity allocations, routing priorities, and site-specific cost structures. Each record links one offering to one site with attributes that exist only in the context of this disposal authorization relationship.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, service offerings are designed to serve multiple geographic markets and must be configured with multiple authorized disposal sites for capacity balancing, geographic coverage, and waste type specialization. Conversely, each landfill site accepts waste from multiple service offerings based on permit restrictions, capacity allocations, and commercial agreements. The business actively manages these authorizations as a disposal network configuration, tracking site-specific routing priorities, tonnage caps, transportation costs, and approved waste types for each offering-site pairing.'
}

Table landfill.cell_equipment_assignment {
  cell_equipment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each cell-equipment assignment record. Primary key for the association.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the landfill cell where equipment is assigned']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fixed asset (equipment) assigned to the cell']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment assignment to this cell ended or is scheduled to end. Null for currently active assignments. Used for historical tracking and equipment rotation planning.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this specific equipment assignment, including special operating conditions, performance observations, or assignment rationale.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was assigned to begin operations in this cell. Marks the beginning of the assignment period for cost allocation and utilization tracking.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the equipment assignment. Active: currently deployed; Scheduled: planned future assignment; Completed: assignment ended; Suspended: temporarily inactive due to maintenance or cell closure.']
  cost_center_override varchar [note: 'Optional cost center override for this specific cell-equipment assignment. When populated, overrides the equipment\'s default cost center for expenses incurred during this assignment period. Used for project-specific cost tracking.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was created in the system.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this assignment record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment record.']
  operational_role varchar [note: 'The specific operational function this equipment performs in this cell assignment. Examples: compaction (waste compactors), dozing (bulldozers for spreading), grading (final surface preparation), water_truck (dust control), support (auxiliary equipment). Same equipment may have different roles in different cells.']
  primary_equipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment is designated as the primary unit for this cell (true) or backup/supplemental equipment (false). Used for capacity planning and operational scheduling.']
  utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total operational hours the equipment has been utilized in this specific cell during the assignment period. Used for cell-specific cost allocation, equipment performance analysis, and maintenance scheduling.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this assignment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Equipment Assignment between landfill cells and fixed assets (equipment). It captures the operational deployment of equipment to specific cells for waste placement, compaction, and cell management activities. Each record links one cell to one piece of equipment with assignment period, role designation, and utilization tracking that exist only in the context of this relationship. Supports cost allocation, capacity planning, and equipment utilization analysis.. Existence Justification: In landfill operations, multiple pieces of equipment (compactors, dozers, water trucks) operate concurrently in a single cell during active waste placement, and each piece of equipment rotates across multiple cells throughout its operational lifecycle. The business actively manages these assignments with specific start/end dates, designates primary vs. backup equipment for capacity planning, and tracks cell-specific utilization hours for cost allocation and equipment performance analysis.'
}

Table landfill.cell_permit_authorization {
  cell_permit_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this cell-permit authorization record. Primary key for the association.']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the landfill cell being authorized for waste placement operations']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the environmental permit providing regulatory authorization']
  authorization_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, modifications, or clarifications related to this cell-permit authorization. Used by compliance officers to track authorization history, regulatory correspondence, or operational considerations.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this cell-permit authorization. Tracks whether the authorization is currently in force (Active), temporarily suspended by regulatory action (Suspended), has expired (Expired), has been revoked (Revoked), or is awaiting renewal approval (Pending Renewal). Distinct from both cell_status and permit_status as it represents the status of this specific authorization relationship.']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of what phase or activity this permit authorizes for the cell. Distinguishes between construction permits (authorizing cell construction), operation permits (authorizing waste placement), expansion permits (authorizing cell expansion), closure permits (authorizing final closure activities), and post-closure permits (authorizing post-closure monitoring and maintenance).']
  authorized_waste_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of waste types that this specific permit authorizes for placement in this specific cell. May be a subset of both the cell\'s permitted_waste_types and the permit\'s overall scope, representing the intersection of what the cell can accept and what this permit authorizes.']
  capacity_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum waste capacity (in cubic yards or tons) authorized for this cell under this specific permit. Tracks permit-specific capacity allocations when multiple permits authorize the same cell with different capacity limits or when a permit authorizes only a portion of the cell\'s total design capacity.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was created in the system. Supports audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit authorization becomes effective for the specified cell. Tracks when the cell is legally authorized to accept waste under this permit.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this permit authorization expires for the specified cell. May differ from the permit\'s overall expiration if the cell authorization is time-limited or if the cell reaches capacity before permit expiration.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was last updated. Tracks changes to authorization status, dates, or conditions.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory permit for this cell expires, requiring renewal or closure plan activation. [Moved from cell: This attribute in the cell product appears to reference a single permit\'s expiration date, which is inappropriate for a cell that can be authorized under multiple permits with different expiration dates. The expiration date belongs to the permit product itself (already exists as permit.expiration_date), not to the cell. This attribute should be removed from cell as it creates ambiguity about which permit\'s expiration is being referenced.]']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory permit for this cell was issued by the governing authority. [Moved from cell: This attribute in the cell product appears to reference a single permit\'s issue date, which is inappropriate for a cell that can be authorized under multiple permits. The issue date belongs to the permit product itself (already exists as permit.issue_date), not to the cell. This attribute should be removed from cell as it creates ambiguity about which permit\'s issue date is being referenced.]']
  regulatory_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions, restrictions, or operational requirements imposed by this permit for this cell. May include cell-specific monitoring requirements, operational constraints, reporting obligations, or special conditions that apply to this cell-permit combination but not to other cells under the same permit.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory authorization relationship between landfill cells and environmental permits. It captures which permits authorize which cells for waste placement operations, including authorization scope, effective periods, waste type restrictions, and capacity limits specific to each cell-permit combination. Each record links one cell to one permit with attributes that exist only in the context of this regulatory authorization.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, landfill cells require multiple types of regulatory permits throughout their lifecycle (construction permits, operational permits, expansion permits, closure permits), and each permit can authorize multiple cells within a facility or across facilities. The business actively manages these authorization relationships, tracking which permits cover which cells with specific effective dates, waste type restrictions, capacity allocations, and cell-specific regulatory conditions that belong to neither the cell nor the permit alone.'
}

Table landfill.collection_point {
  collection_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for collection_point']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific landfill cell where this collection point is installed, if applicable.']
  discharge_permit_id varchar [note: 'National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) or equivalent discharge permit identifier for this collection point.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent landfill site where this collection point is located.']
  upstream_collection_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on collection_point (upstream_collection_point_id)']
  action_level decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regulatory or operational action level threshold value that triggers response procedures when exceeded.']
  action_level_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the action level threshold (e.g., ppm, mg/L, percent, cfm).']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for equipment calibration at this collection point.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collection point record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the collection point was decommissioned or taken out of service, if applicable.']
  depth_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth of the collection point below ground surface measured in feet, applicable for wells and subsurface monitoring points.']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the collection point above sea level measured in feet.']
  enviance_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection point is integrated with the Enviance EHS system for automated compliance reporting.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the primary equipment installed at this collection point.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or instrumentation installed at the collection point for monitoring or collection purposes.']
  flow_capacity_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum flow capacity of the collection point measured in gallons per minute, applicable for leachate and stormwater collection.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between inspections at this collection point.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the collection point was installed and became operational.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection performed at this collection point.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the collection point location in decimal degrees.']
  lel_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower explosive limit threshold percentage for landfill gas monitoring points, triggering alerts when exceeded.']
  liner_integrity_status varchar [note: 'Current assessment status of liner integrity at or near this collection point.']
  liner_type varchar [note: 'Type of liner system associated with this collection point, relevant for leachate collection systems.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the collection point location in decimal degrees.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collection point record was last modified in the system.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for monitoring or sampling at this collection point as defined by regulatory requirements or operational procedures.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for inspection at this collection point based on regulatory or operational requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special instructions related to this collection point.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the collection point in its lifecycle.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit number associated with this collection point, if required by environmental regulations.']
  point_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the collection point, used in operational systems and reporting.']
  point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the collection point within the landfill site.']
  point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the collection point based on its primary environmental monitoring or collection function.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the collection point is currently in compliance with all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, team, or contractor responsible for monitoring and maintaining this collection point.']
  sample_method varchar [note: 'Standard method or protocol used for collecting samples or readings at this collection point.']
  treatment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether collected material from this point requires treatment before discharge or disposal.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for collection_point. Referenced by collection_point_id.'
}

Table landfill.monitoring_point {
  monitoring_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for monitoring_point']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific landfill cell or disposal unit associated with this monitoring point. Null for perimeter or off-site monitoring points.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified environmental laboratory responsible for analyzing samples collected from this monitoring point.']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill facility where this monitoring point is located.']
  parent_monitoring_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on monitoring_point (parent_monitoring_point_id)']
  access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any physical, safety, or regulatory restrictions affecting access to this monitoring point (e.g., confined space entry required, locked gate, hazardous area, private property).']
  aquifer_zone varchar [note: 'Hydrogeologic zone or aquifer unit monitored by this groundwater well: shallow (uppermost), intermediate, deep, bedrock, or perched water table.']
  background_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this point serves as a background or upgradient reference location (true) for comparison with downgradient monitoring results.']
  casing_diameter_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Inner diameter of the well casing in inches. Applicable to groundwater and gas monitoring wells.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring point record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring point was formally decommissioned or abandoned. Null for active points.']
  detection_monitoring_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this point is part of the RCRA Subtitle D detection monitoring program (true) or assessment/corrective action monitoring (false).']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Ground surface elevation at the monitoring point location measured in feet above mean sea level.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the primary monitoring equipment installed at this point for asset tracking and calibration management.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of monitoring equipment or instrumentation installed at this point (e.g., datalogger, gas analyzer, flow meter, weather station, piezometer).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring point was physically installed and became operational.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring equipment at this point was last calibrated or verified for accuracy.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring point record was most recently updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the monitoring point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  lel_alarm_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) at which an alarm or action is triggered for landfill gas monitoring points. Typically set at 25% LEL per OSHA and EPA guidance.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the monitoring point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  methane_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Methane concentration threshold (volume percent) for landfill gas monitoring compliance. Regulatory limit is typically 5% by volume at the facility boundary or 1.25% in structures.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required sampling or measurement frequency for this monitoring point as specified in the permit or monitoring plan.']
  monitoring_subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional detail on the monitoring point purpose (e.g., compliance well, detection well, extraction well, perimeter probe, interior probe, background station). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliance_well|detection_well|extraction_well|perimeter_probe|interior_probe|background_station|boundary_station|meteorological_tower — promote to reference product]']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of environmental monitoring performed at this point: groundwater quality, landfill gas (LFG) emissions, ambient air quality, leachate characteristics, surface water quality, or meteorological conditions.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification of monitoring equipment at this point.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, historical context, or special considerations related to this monitoring point.']
  parameter_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of environmental parameters or analytes measured at this monitoring point (e.g., pH, conductivity, VOCs, methane, benzene, dissolved oxygen, temperature).']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory permit or authorization number under which this monitoring point is required or approved.']
  point_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the monitoring point for regulatory reporting and field operations (e.g., GW-001, LFG-042, AM-105).']
  point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the monitoring point location (e.g., North Boundary Groundwater Well, Cell 3 Gas Probe, East Perimeter Air Station).']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether monitoring at this point is mandated by federal, state, or local environmental regulations (true) or is voluntary/supplemental (false).']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, contractor, or department responsible for monitoring activities at this point.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Standard operating procedure or EPA method used for sample collection at this monitoring point (e.g., low-flow purging, bailer, passive diffusion, direct-read instrument).']
  screen_interval_bottom_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth from ground surface to the bottom of the well screen interval in feet. Applicable to groundwater and gas monitoring wells.']
  screen_interval_top_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth from ground surface to the top of the well screen interval in feet. Applicable to groundwater and gas monitoring wells.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the monitoring point: active (in service), inactive (temporarily not monitored), abandoned (permanently closed), suspended (on hold), planned (not yet installed), or decommissioned (formally retired).']
  well_depth_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total depth of the monitoring well from ground surface to bottom of well screen, measured in feet. Applicable to groundwater and gas monitoring wells.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for monitoring_point. Referenced by monitoring_point_id.'
}

Table landfill.outfall {
  outfall_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for outfall']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landfill facility where this outfall is located.']
  upstream_outfall_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on outfall (upstream_outfall_id)']
  average_flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical average flow rate through the outfall in gallons per minute based on historical monitoring data.']
  best_management_practices varchar [note: 'Description of best management practices implemented to minimize pollutant discharge from this outfall.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the outfall discharge (compliant with permit limits, non-compliant, under regulatory review, or operating under consent decree).']
  construction_date date [note: 'Date when the outfall infrastructure was originally constructed and commissioned.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the responsible party managing this outfall.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the responsible party managing this outfall.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outfall record was first created in the system.']
  design_flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum design flow rate capacity of the outfall in gallons per minute as specified in engineering plans.']
  discharge_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the discharge source as point source (discrete conveyance) or non-point source (diffuse runoff).']
  discharge_limitation_type varchar [note: 'Type of effluent limitation applied to this outfall (technology-based standards, water quality-based standards, combined, or none).']
  discharge_method varchar [note: 'Method by which effluent is discharged from the outfall (gravity flow, pumped discharge, siphon, or overflow).']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the outfall discharge point above mean sea level in feet.']
  has_flow_meter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outfall is equipped with a flow measurement device for continuous or periodic flow monitoring.']
  has_sampler boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the outfall is equipped with an automatic water quality sampling device.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between physical inspections of the outfall as specified in permit or operational procedures.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this outfall record is currently active in the system for operational and reporting purposes.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection of the outfall structure and discharge point.']
  last_violation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent permit violation or exceedance event at this outfall.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the outfall discharge point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the outfall discharge point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of discharge monitoring and sampling as specified in the NPDES permit (continuous, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, or event-based).']
  monitoring_parameters varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of water quality parameters required to be monitored at this outfall (e.g., pH, BOD, TSS, metals, nutrients).']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection of the outfall.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special conditions, or remarks regarding the outfall configuration, history, or management.']
  outfall_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the outfall discharge point (e.g., North Leachate Discharge Point, East Stormwater Outfall).']
  outfall_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the outfall as designated in NPDES permit documentation and regulatory filings. Externally-known unique code.']
  outfall_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outfall based on the type of discharge (leachate, stormwater runoff, groundwater seepage, surface water, combined discharge, or process water).']
  permit_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the NPDES permit for this outfall became effective and discharge authorization commenced.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current NPDES permit expires and renewal or modification is required.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'NPDES permit number authorizing discharge from this outfall under the Clean Water Act.']
  pipe_diameter_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the outfall discharge pipe in inches.']
  pipe_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the outfall discharge pipe (HDPE, PVC, concrete, steel, ductile iron, or fiberglass).']
  receiving_water_body varchar [note: 'Name of the surface water body, stream, river, lake, or municipal system that receives discharge from this outfall.']
  receiving_water_classification varchar [note: 'State or federal classification of the receiving water body (e.g., Class A, Class B, impaired waters, TMDL-listed).']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the state or federal regulatory agency with jurisdiction over this outfall (e.g., EPA Region 5, State DEQ).']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or department responsible for operation and maintenance of this outfall.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the outfall in its lifecycle (active discharge, inactive, seasonal operation, decommissioned, under construction, or pending regulatory approval).']
  treatment_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of treatment system upstream of the outfall (e.g., leachate treatment plant, detention basin, oil-water separator, none).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outfall record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for outfall. Referenced by outfall_id.'
}

Table landfill.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for laboratory']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or regulatory body that granted accreditation to the laboratory (e.g., NELAP, A2LA, ANAB).']
  accreditation_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory accreditation became effective.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current laboratory accreditation expires and requires renewal.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or registration number issued by the accreditation body.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the laboratory indicating compliance with quality and competency standards.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the laboratory physical address including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the laboratory address for suite, building, or unit information.']
  audit_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings, non-conformances, or corrective actions identified during the most recent laboratory audit.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope of certification or accreditation defining the specific tests, matrices, and methods the laboratory is authorized to perform.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the laboratory is located.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the service contract with the laboratory expires or is scheduled for renewal (applicable for external/third-party laboratories).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the service contract with the laboratory became effective (applicable for external/third-party laboratories).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the laboratory is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was first created in the system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory is currently active and available for testing services.']
  is_mobile_laboratory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory is a mobile testing unit that can be deployed to different sites.']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the laboratory for identification in regulatory reporting and internal systems.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the laboratory facility or testing center.']
  laboratory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the laboratory based on ownership and operational model (internal company lab, external contracted lab, third-party independent lab, mobile testing unit, on-site facility lab, or reference lab).']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality audit or inspection conducted at the laboratory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was last updated or modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the laboratory location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the laboratory location in decimal degrees.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality audit or regulatory inspection of the laboratory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the laboratory operations, capabilities, or relationship.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the laboratory (e.g., Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM).']
  parent_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the parent company or organization that owns or operates the laboratory.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating of the laboratory based on quality, turnaround time, accuracy, and customer satisfaction metrics.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the laboratory location.']
  preferred_vendor_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory has been designated as a preferred vendor based on performance, pricing, or strategic relationship.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary laboratory contact for operational and regulatory communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person or laboratory director responsible for laboratory operations.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the laboratory contact.']
  quality_assurance_program varchar [note: 'Name or description of the quality assurance and quality control (QA/QC) program implemented by the laboratory.']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by regulatory agencies (EPA, state environmental agencies) for tracking and compliance purposes.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the laboratory is located.']
  testing_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of analytical testing capabilities and methodologies the laboratory is equipped to perform (e.g., leachate analysis, groundwater testing, air quality monitoring, soil contamination, landfill gas composition).']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time in days for the laboratory to complete testing and deliver results.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for laboratory. Referenced by laboratory_id.'
}

Table hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator {
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hazardous waste generator record. Primary key for the entity.']
  epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Hazardous waste generator is registered with EPA and has an EPA ID. The epa_id_number string is a denormalized copy of data in epa_id_registration. Adding FK to epa_id_registration normalizes this rel']
  generator_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Generator sites ARE facilities in waste management operations. Links generator regulatory records to facility asset master for permit tracking, inspection scheduling, facility utilization reporting, a']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Generator sites maintain hazard registers identifying site-specific hazards (waste types, storage areas, processes, chemical exposures). Links facility-level risk assessment to generator operations fo']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Generators operate under RCRA permits (40 CFR 262). Permit governs accumulation limits, reporting, and operational requirements. Core regulatory relationship for compliance tracking and inspection pla']
  responsible_official_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: EPA hazardous waste generator notifications require designated responsible official per 40 CFR 262.12. Tracking employee_id enables signature authority verification, certification tracking, regulatory']
  tracked_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.tracked_site. Business justification: Hazardous waste generators are sustainability tracking sites for ESG reporting. Corporate sustainability teams track emissions, waste metrics, and compliance by generator location. Standard ESG report']
  accumulation_start_date_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days hazardous waste may be accumulated on-site before shipment, based on generator category. LQG: 90 days; SQG: 180 days (or 270 if >200 miles from TSDF); VSQG: no time limit but quantity limits apply.']
  biennial_report_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the generator is required to submit biennial hazardous waste reports to EPA. Required for LQGs and some SQGs.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with RCRA and state hazardous waste regulations. Reflects findings from most recent inspection or enforcement action.']
  contingency_plan_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when the contingency plan was last reviewed and updated. Plans must be reviewed annually and updated as needed.']
  contingency_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document location for the site\'s hazardous waste contingency plan. Required for LQGs and SQGs.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated emergency response contact for incidents involving hazardous waste at this site.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: '24-hour emergency phone number for hazardous waste incidents. Must be staffed or monitored continuously.']
  enforcement_action_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal enforcement actions (notices of violation, administrative orders, civil penalties) issued against this generator.']
  federal_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the generator is a federal government facility (e.g., military base, federal laboratory). Federal facilities have specific RCRA compliance requirements.']
  generator_category varchar [note: 'EPA classification of generator based on monthly waste generation volume. LQG (Large Quantity Generator): ≥1,000 kg/month; SQG (Small Quantity Generator): 100-1,000 kg/month; VSQG (Very Small Quantity Generator): <100 kg/month; CESQG (Conditionally Exempt Small Quantity Generator): legacy term for VSQG.']
  generator_legal_name varchar [note: 'The official legal name of the hazardous waste generating entity as registered with EPA and state authorities.']
  generator_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and regulatory status of the hazardous waste generator registration.']
  initial_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the generator first notified EPA of hazardous waste generation activities and received EPA ID number.']
  inspection_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Expected frequency of regulatory inspections by EPA or state authorities, in months. Varies by generator category and compliance history.']
  last_biennial_report_year varchar [note: 'The reporting year of the most recently submitted biennial hazardous waste report (e.g., 2022 for the 2021-2022 reporting period).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection by EPA, state, or local enforcement agency.']
  last_notification_update_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent update to the generator\'s EPA notification information. Generators must notify EPA of changes within 30 days.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the generator site in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis and emergency response planning.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the generator site in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis and emergency response planning.']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address for correspondence and regulatory notifications if different from site address.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code identifying the primary business activity of the generator. Used for regulatory reporting and waste generation profiling.']
