Table billing.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing adjustment record. Primary key.']
  adjustment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated the adjustment. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  adjustment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who approved the adjustment. Required for adjustments exceeding automated approval thresholds. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.']
  ar_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.ar_account. Business justification: Adjustments (credits, debits, write-offs) directly impact AR account balances. While adjustment.invoice_id and adjustment.billing_account_id exist, a direct FK to ar_account is essential for AR sub-le']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account receiving the adjustment. Links to the billing account impacted by this adjustment.']
  contamination_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.contamination_event. Business justification: Contamination events trigger billing adjustments (surcharges when contamination exceeds contract thresholds, or credits for processing issues). Real-world process requires linking adjustments to conta']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Billing adjustments stem from contract disputes, SLA breaches, or rate corrections. Audit trail requires linking adjustment back to governing agreement for regulatory compliance, revenue recognition c']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute case that triggered this adjustment. Links adjustments to customer dispute resolution workflows.']
  ghg_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_emission. Business justification: Credit memos for service failures require corresponding emission allocation adjustments for accurate carbon accounting. Links billing adjustments to emission records, ensuring regulatory reporting acc']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: Safety incidents frequently trigger billing adjustments for service interruptions, equipment damage, liability claims, or route delays. Waste Management operations require tracking which adjustments s']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice being adjusted. Links to the invoice that this adjustment modifies or corrects.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Service-related adjustments (missed pickup credits, contamination charges, SLA breach credits) must reference the offering to categorize revenue impact, track service quality costs, and analyze adjust']
  reversed_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original adjustment being reversed by this adjustment. Maintains bidirectional linkage for audit trail and correction tracking.']
  service_exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.service_exception. Business justification: Billing adjustments for missed pickups, contamination, access issues, or service failures require linking to the documented service exception with root cause and resolution notes.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_request. Business justification: Credits and adjustments frequently originate from service requests (missed pickups, service failures, complaint resolutions). Tracking this relationship enables root cause analysis of revenue leakage,']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Billing adjustments frequently result from violation notices: credits for service interruptions due to compliance shutdowns, charge corrections for non-permitted waste acceptance, or penalty-related b']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the adjustment. Used for customer communication, dispute tracking, and audit trails.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the adjustment in the approval and posting workflow. Determines whether the adjustment has impacted financial statements and customer balances. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|posted|reversed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the adjustment indicating the nature of the correction or modification. Determines accounting treatment and approval workflow requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_memo|debit_memo|service_credit|write_off|bad_debt|rate_correction|small_balance_write_off|promotional_discount|dispute_settlement|billing_error_correction — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the adjustment. Positive values represent debits (charges to customer), negative values represent credits (refunds or reductions). Expressed in the account\'s billing currency.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the approval workflow for this adjustment. Tracks whether required management authorization has been obtained per SOX-controlled approval thresholds.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was approved by authorized personnel. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail documentation.']
  bad_debt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment represents a bad debt write-off or uncollectible balance. Used for bad debt reserve calculations per ASC 326 Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL) standard.']
  corrected_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Corrected invoice line item amount after adjustment. Used for billing error analysis and process improvement initiatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment amount. Supports multi-currency billing operations.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer was notified of the adjustment. Tracks communication timeliness and customer service performance.']
  customer_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified of this adjustment. Supports customer communication tracking and service quality management.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Business date on which the adjustment becomes effective for revenue recognition and customer billing purposes. May differ from posting date for backdated corrections.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source system or third-party system. Supports cross-system reconciliation and audit trail linkage.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the adjustment is posted. Determines whether the adjustment impacts revenue reversal, bad debt expense, or other GL accounts per GAAP requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment record was last modified. Tracks changes throughout the approval and posting workflow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes providing additional context, justification, or instructions related to the adjustment. Used for audit documentation and customer service reference.']
  original_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original invoice line item amount before adjustment. Provides context for variance analysis and billing accuracy metrics.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was posted to the general ledger and customer account balance. Marks the point at which the adjustment impacts financial statements.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific business reason for the adjustment. Used for root cause analysis, trend reporting, and process improvement initiatives.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the adjustment was necessary. Provides business context for audit, compliance, and customer service purposes.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount recovered from a previously written-off balance. Tracks bad debt recoveries for financial reporting and collections performance metrics.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously written-off amount was recovered. Used for bad debt recovery reporting and collections analytics.']
  recovery_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a written-off balance is eligible for future recovery attempts. Used by collections teams to prioritize recovery efforts.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount by which this adjustment impacts recognized revenue in the current accounting period. Used for revenue recognition reporting per ASC 606 and IFRS 15.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment reverses a previous adjustment. Used to track adjustment corrections and maintain audit trail integrity.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period to which this adjustment applies. Supports revenue recognition and period-specific billing corrections.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period to which this adjustment applies. Used for revenue recognition timing and period-specific adjustments.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the system of record that originated this adjustment. Supports data lineage tracking and system integration reconciliation.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment represents a write-off of an uncollectible or immaterial balance. Supports accounts receivable aging and collections management.']

  Note: 'Credit or debit adjustment applied to a customer account or invoice to correct billing errors, honor service credits, apply promotional discounts, settle disputes, or write off uncollectible balances. Captures adjustment type (credit memo, debit memo, service credit, write-off, bad debt, rate correction, small balance write-off), adjustment reason code, original invoice reference, adjustment amount, approval status, approver ID, GL impact (revenue reversal or bad debt expense), recovery status for write-off types, and approval workflow audit trail. Supports GAAP revenue recognition adjustments, SOX-controlled approval workflows, and bad debt management per ASC 326 (CECL).'
}

Table billing.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing dispute record. Primary key.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the container involved in the dispute, relevant for container size error or rental charge disputes.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that raised the dispute.']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Salesforce CRM case record associated with this dispute for tracking and resolution workflow.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Customer disputes often stem from inspection findings: contamination charges, service denials due to non-compliance, or disputed fees for regulatory violations. Linking disputes to triggering inspecti']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Disputes involve contract interpretation (rate disputes, service level failures, scope disagreements). Resolution teams need direct access to governing agreement terms, SLA thresholds, penalty provisi']
  dispute_approved_by_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager who approved the dispute resolution and associated financial adjustment.']
  dispute_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing analyst or customer service representative assigned to investigate and resolve the dispute.']
  dispute_escalated_to_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or supervisor to whom the dispute was escalated for review and decision.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver. Business justification: Customer disputes about service quality, missed pickups, or driver conduct require identifying the specific driver who performed the service. Essential for investigation, coaching, and resolution trac']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice being disputed by the customer.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice line item being disputed, if the dispute is line-item specific rather than invoice-level.']
  pickup_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.pickup_event. Business justification: Service disputes often reference specific pickup events; direct link enables investigation of RFID scans, timestamps, GPS coordinates, driver notes, and contamination flags for resolution.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route associated with the disputed service, relevant for missed pickup or service quality disputes.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service address associated with the disputed charges, relevant for location-specific billing disputes.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Disputes involving property damage, equipment malfunction, or service delivery issues require identifying the specific vehicle involved. Critical for insurance claims, maintenance investigation, and r']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.weight_ticket. Business justification: Tonnage and tipping fee disputes require direct access to the weight ticket with certified gross/tare/net weights, scale calibration date, and weighmaster certification for validation.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the dispute resolution requires management approval due to amount threshold or policy exception.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispute resolution was formally approved by authorized management.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the disputed invoice.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by the disputed invoice.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispute was formally closed in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount credited to the customer account as a result of the dispute resolution, in USD.']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo issued as part of the dispute resolution, if applicable.']
  customer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer contact for dispute correspondence and resolution communication.']
  customer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual customer contact who initiated or is associated with the dispute.']
  customer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the customer contact for dispute follow-up and resolution discussions.']
  customer_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction score (1-5 scale) collected via survey to measure dispute handling quality.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date the customer formally raised the billing dispute.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the dispute, used in customer communications and internal tracking.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute in the resolution workflow.']
  disputed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount being contested by the customer in the dispute, in USD.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was escalated to a higher authority for resolution.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times the dispute has been escalated to higher management tiers (0 = no escalation, 1 = first escalation, etc.).']
  financial_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial impact of the dispute and its resolution on the business.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the dispute record was last updated.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the dispute based on amount, customer tier, or business impact.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the primary reason for the dispute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incorrect rate|missed pickup|container size error|duplicate charge|tipping fee discrepancy|service not rendered|billing cycle error|proration error|tax calculation error|unauthorized charge|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer or customer service representative describing the nature of the dispute.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount refunded to the customer via payment reversal as a result of the dispute resolution, in USD.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was formally resolved and closed.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the investigation findings, actions taken, and rationale for the resolution decision.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'The outcome classification of the dispute resolution indicating how the dispute was settled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit issued|charge upheld|partial credit|invoice corrected|waived|refund issued|adjustment applied — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause identified during dispute investigation, used for process improvement and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing system error|rate table error|service delivery failure|data entry error|customer misunderstanding|policy exception|system integration issue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_date date [note: 'The date of the service event that is the subject of the dispute (e.g., scheduled pickup date for a missed pickup dispute).']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the dispute resolution exceeded the SLA target resolution timeframe.']
  sla_target_resolution_date date [note: 'The target date by which the dispute should be resolved per service level agreement commitments.']
  sox_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the dispute resolution followed SOX-compliant approval and documentation procedures for financial adjustments.']

  Note: 'Formal billing dispute raised by a customer contesting charges on an invoice. Captures dispute date, disputed invoice reference, disputed amount, dispute reason (incorrect rate, missed pickup, container size error, duplicate charge, tipping fee discrepancy), dispute status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution type (credit issued, charge upheld, partial credit), resolution date, and assigned billing analyst. Integrates with Salesforce CRM case management.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan {
  payment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment plan arrangement. Primary key.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account enrolled in this payment plan.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Payment plans are negotiated as part of contract terms. Collections and credit teams need to trace payment arrangements back to originating agreement for covenant compliance, early termination penalty']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method (credit card, bank account, etc.) on file for auto-pay or manual installment payments.']
  primary_payment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collections team member or agent responsible for managing this payment plan and following up on missed payments.']
  quaternary_payment_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this payment plan record.']
  tertiary_payment_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (customer service representative, collections agent, or system process) who created this payment plan record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the payment plan was approved by management. Null if no approval was required.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment plan required management approval before activation. Typically true for large balances, extended terms, or settlement plans.']
  auto_pay_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this plan. When true, installments are automatically charged to the customers payment method on file on each due date.']
  bankruptcy_case_number varchar [note: 'Court-assigned bankruptcy case number if this payment plan is part of a bankruptcy arrangement. Null for non-bankruptcy plans.']
  bankruptcy_chapter varchar [note: 'Type of bankruptcy filing if this payment plan is part of a bankruptcy arrangement. Chapter 7 is liquidation; Chapter 11 is business reorganization; Chapter 13 is individual debt adjustment. Null for non-bankruptcy plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment plan record was first created in the system.']
  default_threshold_count varchar [note: 'Number of missed installments allowed before the plan is automatically moved to defaulted status. Typically 2 for standard plans, may be higher for hardship programs.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial payment required at plan setup before installment schedule begins. May be zero for certain hardship programs.']
  down_payment_received_date date [note: 'Date the down payment was received and processed. Null if no down payment was required or if down payment is still outstanding.']
  enrolled_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the original balance that was enrolled into this payment plan. May be less than original balance if customer made partial payment or if certain charges were excluded from the plan.']
  final_installment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the last installment payment. Represents the planned completion date of the payment plan.']
  first_installment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the first installment payment. Subsequent installments are calculated based on this date and the installment frequency.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after installment due date before payment is considered missed. Standard grace period is typically 10 days.']
  installment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard amount due for each installment payment. Calculated as (enrolled_balance_amount - down_payment_amount) / total_installment_count. Final installment may differ to account for rounding.']
  installment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which installment payments are scheduled. Monthly is most common; weekly and biweekly are used for residential hardship programs; quarterly may be used for large commercial accounts.']
  interest_accrued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total interest charges accrued on this payment plan to date. Zero for interest-free plans.']
  interest_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the enrolled balance, if any. Most residential hardship plans have zero interest; commercial workout agreements may include interest. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard late fee charged for each missed installment payment. May be waived for hardship programs.']
  missed_installment_count varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled installments that were not paid by their due date. Used to determine default status and trigger collections escalation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment plan record was last modified. Updated whenever plan status, payment amounts, or other attributes change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special terms, customer circumstances, negotiation details, or other relevant information about this payment plan.']
  original_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total outstanding accounts receivable (AR) balance at the time the payment plan was established. Represents the full amount owed before any plan payments were made.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Actual date the payment plan was completed, defaulted, or cancelled. Null for active or suspended plans. For completed plans, this is the date the final payment was received; for defaulted or cancelled plans, this is the date the plan was terminated.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the payment plan, formatted as PP-NNNNNNNN.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Effective date when the payment plan became active and the installment schedule began. Typically the date the plan was approved and down payment (if required) was received.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment plan. Draft indicates plan is being set up but not yet active; active means plan is in effect and installments are being processed; completed indicates all installments paid successfully; defaulted means customer missed installments per default criteria; cancelled indicates plan was terminated before completion; suspended means plan is temporarily on hold pending resolution of dispute or other issue.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment plan based on the business program and customer segment. Residential hardship programs support customers facing temporary financial difficulty; commercial workout agreements are negotiated arrangements for business accounts; standard installment plans are routine payment schedules; seasonal deferral plans accommodate seasonal business fluctuations; bankruptcy arrangements comply with court-ordered payment terms; settlement plans represent negotiated reduced-balance agreements.']
  remaining_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance remaining on the payment plan after all payments received to date. Calculated as enrolled_balance_amount minus sum of all installment payments received.']
  settlement_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of discount applied to the original balance as part of a settlement agreement. Zero for standard payment plans.']
  settlement_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the original balance as part of a settlement agreement. Zero for standard payment plans. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20% discount).']
  total_installment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of scheduled installment payments in this plan. Typically ranges from 3 to 24 installments depending on plan type and balance amount.']
  total_late_fees_assessed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all late fees assessed on this payment plan due to missed installments.']
  total_paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all payments received against this payment plan, including down payment and all installment payments to date.']

  Note: 'Structured installment arrangement established for customers unable to pay their full outstanding AR balance, including individual scheduled installment records. Header captures plan start/end dates, total balance enrolled, number of installments, installment amount, frequency, down payment, plan status (active, completed, defaulted, cancelled), and collection agent ID. Each installment detail captures sequence number, scheduled due date, scheduled amount, actual payment date, actual amount paid, installment status (scheduled, paid, missed, waived), and days past due. Supports residential hardship programs, commercial account workout agreements, and enables collections teams to track compliance and trigger dunning on missed installments.'
}

Table billing.payment_plan_installment {
  payment_plan_installment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment plan installment record. Primary key.']
  applied_to_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice that this installment payment was applied against in the accounts receivable (AR) system. Used for revenue recognition and reconciliation.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent payment plan agreement under which this installment is scheduled.']
  actual_amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual amount paid by the customer for this installment in USD. May differ from scheduled amount due to partial payments or overpayments. Null if not yet paid.']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer actually made the payment for this installment. Null if not yet paid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this installment record was first created in the system, typically when the payment plan was established. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for this installment (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency billing for international operations.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'Number of days this installment is overdue, calculated as current date minus scheduled due date. Zero or negative if not yet due or paid on time. Used for aging analysis and dunning triggers.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning escalation level for this missed installment (e.g., 0=no action, 1=reminder, 2=warning, 3=final notice, 4=collections). Used to track collections intensity.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the scheduled due date before this installment is considered missed and dunning actions begin. Typically set at payment plan level but can be overridden per installment.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this installment within the payment plan (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Determines the order of scheduled payments.']
  installment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this installment: scheduled (not yet due or paid), paid (fully paid), partial (partially paid), missed (past due and unpaid), waived (forgiven by collections), cancelled (plan terminated).']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent dunning communication sent to the customer regarding this installment. Used to schedule next dunning action.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this installment record, such as status changes or payment application. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Late fee assessed on this installment due to missed or late payment, in USD. Null if no late fee applies or installment paid on time.']
  late_fee_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the late fee for this installment was waived as a customer service gesture or due to billing error. True if waived, False otherwise.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this installment, such as payment disputes, customer requests, or collections agent observations.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the customer submitted this installment payment (e.g., online portal, mobile app, phone). Null if not yet paid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used by the customer to pay this installment (e.g., credit card, ACH, check). Null if not yet paid. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ach|check|cash|wire_transfer|auto_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or transaction identifier from the payment processor or bank for this installment payment. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  scheduled_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The contractually agreed amount due for this installment in USD. Represents the planned payment obligation.']
  scheduled_due_date date [note: 'The date on which this installment payment is contractually due from the customer.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or system user who approved the waiver of this installment. Required for SOX audit trail when waivers are granted.']
  waiver_date date [note: 'Date on which the waiver for this installment was approved and applied. Null if not waived.']
  waiver_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this installment was waived or forgiven by collections or management. Null if not waived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_hardship|service_failure|billing_error|customer_retention|bankruptcy|deceased|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Individual scheduled installment within a payment plan, representing a specific payment obligation on a defined due date. Captures installment sequence number, scheduled due date, scheduled amount, actual payment date, actual amount paid, installment status (scheduled, paid, missed, waived), and days past due. Enables collections teams to track payment plan compliance and trigger dunning actions on missed installments.'
}

Table billing.collection_action {
  collection_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the collection action record. Primary key.']
  ar_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delinquent accounts receivable account against which this collection action was taken.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Collection strategies differ by contract type (municipal franchise vs commercial). Collectors need contract status, termination clauses, performance bond provisions, and relationship value to determin']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the delinquent receivable.']
  dunning_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dunning_sequence. Business justification: Collection actions are executed as part of structured dunning sequences. Each collection action (dunning letter, phone call, legal notice) follows a specific dunning sequence configuration that define']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or agent who performed or is responsible for this collection action.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice that is subject to this collection action, if action is invoice-specific.']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment plan established as a result of this collection action, if applicable.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party collection agency to which the account was referred, if applicable.']
  action_date date [note: 'Date on which the collection action was executed or initiated.']
  action_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the collection action, used for tracking and reference in communications and workflows.']
  action_outcome varchar [note: 'Result or outcome of the collection action, capturing whether payment was received, a plan was established, or further escalation is required. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: payment_received|payment_plan_established|promise_to_pay|no_contact|dispute_raised|account_escalated|service_suspended|legal_action_initiated|account_written_off|no_response — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the collection action in its lifecycle.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the collection action was executed, supporting audit trail and compliance requirements.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of collection activity performed. Includes dunning notices, collection calls, service suspension warnings, lien filings, and third-party collections referrals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dunning_notice|collection_call|email_reminder|service_suspension_warning|payment_plan_offer|lien_filing|legal_notice|third_party_referral|account_review|settlement_offer — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  call_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the collection call in seconds, if the action type was a phone call.']
  call_recording_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to the call recording, if the collection call was recorded for quality and compliance purposes.']
  communication_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the details of the communication or action taken, including customer responses and commitments.']
  compliance_verified_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who verified compliance for this collection action.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which compliance verification was completed for this collection action.']
  compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this collection action was reviewed and verified for compliance with FDCPA and internal policies.']
  contact_email_address varchar [note: 'Email address used to contact the customer during this collection action, if applicable.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Method used to contact the customer for this collection action.']
  contact_phone_number varchar [note: 'Phone number used to contact the customer during this collection action, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collection action record was first created in the system.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'Number of days the account was past due at the time of the collection action.']
  delinquency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of accounts receivable that was delinquent at the time this collection action was taken, in USD.']
  dispute_raised_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer raised a billing dispute during this collection action.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the dispute raised by the customer, if applicable.']
  dunning_letter_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal dunning letter was sent to the customer as part of this action.']
  dunning_letter_template_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dunning letter template used for this action, supporting compliance and audit requirements.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of the escalation stage in the collections process (e.g., 1=first notice, 2=second notice, 3=final notice, 4=legal action).']
  legal_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal action (lien filing, lawsuit) was initiated or threatened as part of this collection action.']
  next_follow_up_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next follow-up action or contact with the customer.']
  promise_to_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount the customer promised to pay, if a promise to pay was obtained during this action.']
  promise_to_pay_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer promised to make payment, if a promise to pay was obtained during this action.']
  service_suspension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether service suspension was initiated or threatened as part of this collection action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collection action record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Record of a collections activity taken against a delinquent AR account, including dunning notices, collection calls, service suspension warnings, lien filings, and third-party collections referrals. Captures action date, action type, action outcome, next follow-up date, collection agent ID, delinquency amount at time of action, and escalation level. Supports the end-to-end collections workflow for both residential and commercial accounts.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition {
  revenue_recognition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revenue_recognition']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.adjustment. Business justification: Revenue recognition events can be triggered by billing adjustments (credits, debits, corrections). Currently revenue_recognition has adjustment_reason (STRING) but no FK to the adjustment table. Addin']
  commodity_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.commodity_sale. Business justification: Commodity sales trigger revenue recognition events under ASC 606 accounting standards. Finance teams must link sales transactions to revenue recognition records for compliance, financial reporting, an']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the waste service contract from which this revenue is recognized.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which revenue is being recognized.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the revenue accountant or system user who approved this revenue recognition event.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Revenue recognition for facility-based performance obligations (landfill airspace consumption, transfer station throughput contracts) requires facility-level attribution for segment reporting, profita']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice associated with this revenue recognition event.']
  original_recognition_event_revenue_recognition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original revenue recognition event if this record is a reversal or adjustment. Null for original recognition events.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific performance obligation within the contract that this revenue recognition satisfies.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Revenue recognition for long-term contracts at permitted facilities must align with permit validity periods and compliance status. ASC 606 requires performance obligation assessment tied to regulatory']
  report_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.report_period. Business justification: SEC climate disclosure rules and integrated ESG-financial reporting require revenue recognition aligned with sustainability reporting periods. Enables CFO and sustainability teams to reconcile financi']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI accounting document number generated for this revenue recognition posting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this revenue recognition event was approved by the revenue accounting team or automated approval workflow.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for audit trail documentation supporting this revenue recognition decision, used for SOX compliance.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The business unit or division generating this revenue (e.g., Residential Services, Commercial Services, Recycling Operations).']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of performance obligation completion for percentage-of-completion recognition method. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The SAP cost center associated with the service delivery generating this revenue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this revenue recognition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the recognized revenue amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue deferred to future periods because performance obligations have not yet been satisfied. Represents unearned revenue liability.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which this revenue is recognized.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this revenue is recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP FI/CO general ledger account code to which this revenue recognition is posted.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this revenue recognition record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context or explanation for this revenue recognition event, including any special considerations or exceptions.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue recognition entry was posted to the SAP general ledger.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'The SAP profit center responsible for this revenue stream, used for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue is recognized in the general ledger according to GAAP/IFRS standards.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The method used to recognize revenue according to ASC 606 / IFRS 15 standards. Point-in-time for single service events, over-time for ongoing services, percentage-of-completion for long-term contracts.']
  recognition_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period over which revenue is recognized for over-time recognition methods.']
  recognition_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period over which revenue is recognized for over-time recognition methods.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the revenue recognition event in the revenue lifecycle: pending approval, recognized and posted, deferred to future period, reversed, or adjusted.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized in this event, posted to the general ledger. Represents earned revenue per GAAP/IFRS standards.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'The category of waste service revenue being recognized: collection (pickup services), disposal (landfill fees), recycling (MRF processing), rental (container leases), tipping fees, or hauling (transportation).']
  revenue_stream_type varchar [note: 'The billing model driving this revenue: subscription (recurring monthly), transactional (per-event), usage-based (tonnage/pickups), fixed fee, or variable fee.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this revenue recognition event is a reversal or adjustment of a previously recognized amount.']
  service_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the underlying waste service was delivered or performed, triggering revenue recognition eligibility.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'The business service line generating the revenue: residential waste collection, commercial services, industrial accounts, municipal contracts, or hazardous waste handling.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this revenue recognition event is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit requirements.']
  transaction_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total consideration allocated to this performance obligation under the contract, representing the expected payment amount.']
  unbilled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized but not yet invoiced to the customer. Represents contract asset or accrued revenue.']

  Note: 'GAAP/IFRS revenue recognition record capturing when and how revenue from waste service contracts is recognized. Tracks recognition date, recognition method (point-in-time, over-time, percentage-of-completion), recognized amount, deferred amount, contract performance obligation reference, revenue category (collection, disposal, recycling, rental), and SAP FI/CO revenue account posting. Supports ASC 606 / IFRS 15 compliance for multi-element service arrangements.'
}

Table billing.surcharge {
  surcharge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the surcharge definition. Primary key.']
  emission_factor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_factor. Business justification: Fuel and environmental surcharges must be calculated using documented emission factors for regulatory compliance and customer transparency. Links surcharge rates to emission factor methodology, enabli']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Environmental surcharges (host community fees, state environmental fees) are mandated by permit conditions and regulatory requirements. Surcharges must be traceable to the authorizing permit for compl']
  surcharge_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this surcharge definition record. Part of audit trail.']
  surcharge_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this surcharge definition for use in billing. Populated when approval_required is True and surcharge has been approved.']
  surcharge_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this surcharge definition record. Part of audit trail.']
  applicability_customer_type varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of customer types to which this surcharge applies (e.g., residential|commercial|industrial|municipal). Empty if applicable to all customer types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|municipal|government|non_profit|construction — promote to reference product]']
  applicability_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic scope where this surcharge applies. Can be state code, county name, city name, ZIP code range, or service district code. Empty if applicable to all geographies.']
  applicability_service_type varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of service types to which this surcharge applies (e.g., collection|disposal|recycling|hazardous_waste|roll_off). Empty if applicable to all service types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collection|disposal|recycling|hazardous_waste|roll_off|compactor|special_waste|medical_waste — promote to reference product]']
  applicability_waste_type varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of waste types to which this surcharge applies (e.g., MSW|C&D|hazardous|medical|e_waste). Empty if applicable to all waste types. MSW = Municipal Solid Waste, C&D = Construction and Demolition Waste.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether application of this surcharge to a customer invoice requires managerial approval. True if approval required, False if can be applied automatically.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge definition was approved for use in billing. Populated when approval_required is True and surcharge has been approved.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the surcharge amount: percentage_of_base (percentage of base service charge), flat_fee (fixed dollar amount), per_ton (rate per ton of waste), per_yard (rate per cubic yard), per_pickup (rate per pickup event), per_container (rate per container), tiered (tiered rate structure), formula (custom formula-based calculation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_of_base|flat_fee|per_ton|per_yard|per_pickup|per_container|tiered|formula — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge definition record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the surcharge rate (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this surcharge definition expires and can no longer be applied to new invoices. Nullable for open-ended surcharges. Part of the temporal validity range.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this surcharge definition becomes effective and can be applied to customer invoices. Part of the temporal validity range.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which revenue from this surcharge is posted in the financial system. Maps to SAP FI/CO chart of accounts.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Customer-facing description of the surcharge as it appears on invoices. May differ from internal surcharge_name to provide clearer customer communication.']
  invoice_display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence order in which this surcharge should appear on customer invoices. Lower numbers appear first. Used to control invoice line item presentation.']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge should be prorated for partial billing periods (e.g., mid-month service start/stop). True if prorated, False if charged in full regardless of period.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge is applied on a recurring basis (e.g., monthly fuel surcharge) or is a one-time charge. True if recurring, False if one-time.']
  is_regulatory_mandated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge is mandated by regulatory authority (EPA, state environmental agency, local solid waste authority) or is a discretionary business charge. True if regulatory-mandated, False if discretionary.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge is subject to sales tax or other applicable taxes. True if taxable, False if tax-exempt.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge definition record was last updated. Part of audit trail.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount for this surcharge regardless of calculation basis. Nullable if no maximum applies. Ensures surcharge does not exceed a cap value.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum dollar amount for this surcharge regardless of calculation basis. Nullable if no minimum applies. Ensures surcharge meets a floor value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, business rules, or special instructions for applying this surcharge. Used for internal documentation and billing team guidance.']
  rate_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Current rate value for the surcharge. Interpretation depends on calculation_basis: for percentage_of_base this is the percentage (e.g., 5.5 for 5.5%), for flat_fee this is the dollar amount, for per_ton/per_yard/per_pickup/per_container this is the unit rate.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this surcharge recurs: monthly (every month), quarterly (every quarter), annually (every year), per_service (each service event), one_time (single occurrence). Populated only when is_recurring is True.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or authority that mandates this surcharge (e.g., EPA, California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery, Local Enforcement Agency). Populated only when is_regulatory_mandated is True.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulation, statute, or ordinance that mandates this surcharge (e.g., RCRA Section 3001, California AB 939, County Ordinance 2023-15). Populated only when is_regulatory_mandated is True.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'High-level revenue classification for financial reporting: service_revenue (core service charges), surcharge_revenue (fuel and environmental surcharges), fee_revenue (administrative and regulatory fees), penalty_revenue (late payment and violation penalties), other_revenue (miscellaneous revenue).']
  surcharge_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the surcharge (e.g., FUEL_SUR, ENV_FEE, REG_REC, ADMIN_FEE, LATE_PAY). Used in billing systems and customer communications.']
  surcharge_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the surcharge as it appears on customer invoices (e.g., Fuel Surcharge, Environmental Fee, Regulatory Recovery Fee, Administrative Fee, Late Payment Fee).']
  surcharge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surcharge definition: active (currently in use), inactive (not in use), pending (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective end date), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the surcharge by business purpose: fuel (fuel cost recovery), environmental (environmental compliance fees), regulatory (regulatory recovery fees), administrative (administrative processing fees), late_payment (late payment penalties), disposal (special disposal fees), contamination (contamination processing fees), distance (distance-based fees), special_handling (special handling fees), other (miscellaneous surcharges). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fuel|environmental|regulatory|administrative|late_payment|disposal|contamination|distance|special_handling|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master definition of surcharges and fees that can be applied to customer invoices beyond base service rates. Captures surcharge code, surcharge name (fuel surcharge, environmental fee, regulatory recovery fee, administrative fee, late payment fee), calculation basis (percentage of base, flat fee, per-ton), current rate, effective date range, applicability rules (customer type, service type, geography), and regulatory mandate flag. Managed centrally to ensure consistent application across all billing runs.'
}

Table billing.usage_event {
  usage_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the usage event record. Primary key.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the waste container serviced during this event, tracked via RFID or barcode.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate schedule applied to calculate charges for this usage event.']
  container_rfid_scan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.container_rfid_scan. Business justification: RFID-based billing systems generate usage events from container scans; direct link provides proof-of-service, timestamp validation, and lift confirmation for automated billing and dispute resolution.']
  contract_service_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_commitment. Business justification: Usage events (pickups, weight tickets) must tie to specific service commitments to validate service delivery against contracted frequency, container counts, and SLA response times. Critical for operat']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account responsible for payment of this usage event.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the driver who performed the service event.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Usage events (scale transactions, gate entries, material processing) occur at specific facilities. Facility link is essential for operational reporting, facility utilization analysis, and facility-lev']
  ghg_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_emission. Business justification: EPA requires facility-level emission tracking tied to operational activity. Links collection events (pickups, tonnage) to emissions generated for Scope 1/3 reporting, carbon cost allocation, and regul']
  inbound_load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.inbound_load. Business justification: Inbound loads at MRFs generate billable tipping fee usage events. Real-world MRF operations require linking usage events to source loads for revenue reconciliation, dispute resolution, and contaminati']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice to which this usage event was posted, once billing status reaches billed. Null for unbilled or rated events.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Usage events (pickups, weighments, service deliveries) must reference the service offering being performed to enable usage-based billing, service verification, and SLA compliance tracking. Essential f']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Usage events (waste pickups, facility disposals) at permitted facilities must reference the operating permit for regulatory tonnage tracking, waste stream compliance, and permit condition verification']
  pickup_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.pickup_event. Business justification: Usage-based billing systems rate pickup events as billable usage; direct link enables automated rating, proof-of-service validation, and reconciliation between operational and billing systems.']
  rolloff_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.rolloff_order. Business justification: Rolloff services generate usage events for delivery, pull, swap, and disposal; direct link enables event-driven billing and reconciliation of service charges against order terms.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route on which this service event occurred, if applicable to route-based collection.']
  scale_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scale transaction record if this usage event originated from a weigh-in at a landfill or transfer station scale house.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the service was performed (customer site, landfill, transfer station, MRF).']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection or haul truck that performed the service.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period for revenue recognition in YYYY-MM format (e.g., 2024-03 for March 2024).']
  base_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated base charge for the usage event before surcharges, taxes, or adjustments. Computed as billable_quantity × unit_rate.']
  billable_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Normalized quantity used for rating and billing, expressed in the unit_of_measure. May differ from raw measurement due to rounding, minimums, or contractual adjustments.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the usage event in the billing workflow: unbilled (captured but not yet rated), rated (charge calculated), billed (included in invoice), excluded (non-billable), disputed (under review), reversed (charge reversed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this usage event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this usage event (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  environmental_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Regulatory or contractual environmental fee applied to this usage event (e.g., state recycling fees, landfill environmental fees).']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the service event, used for daily billing aggregation and reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the usage event, often printed on scale tickets or BOL documents.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billable service event occurred in the field (e.g., when the truck completed the pickup, when the scale recorded the weight). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billable service event: pickup (scheduled collection), weigh_in (landfill scale transaction), rolloff_delivery, rolloff_pull, recycling_dropoff, or container_rental.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Variable fuel surcharge applied to this usage event based on current fuel price index and contract terms.']
  gl_date date [note: 'Accounting date when revenue from this usage event is recognized in the general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate where the service event occurred, captured from Geotab Fleet Telematics.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate where the service event occurred, captured from Geotab Fleet Telematics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this usage event record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of waste material collected or processed: MSW (Municipal Solid Waste), recycling, yard waste, C&D (Construction and Demolition), hazardous, or special waste.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the usage event, entered by driver, dispatcher, or billing staff.']
  other_surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional surcharges applied to this usage event not captured in fuel or environmental fees (e.g., contamination fees, overweight fees, after-hours fees).']
  quantity_pickups varchar [note: 'Number of discrete pickup actions performed during this event (typically 1 for standard collection, may be higher for multi-container stops).']
  rating_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the rating process (e.g., MISSING_RATE, INVALID_QUANTITY, MANUAL_OVERRIDE). Empty if rating was successful.']
  rating_exception_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the rating exception, if any occurred during charge calculation.']
  rating_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the usage event was rated and charges were calculated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  revenue_recognition_status varchar [note: 'Status of revenue recognition for this usage event under GAAP/IFRS standards: pending (not yet recognized), recognized (posted to GL), deferred (recognition delayed), reversed (recognition reversed).']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier read from the container at time of service, used for automated container tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for this usage event: AMCS Platform, Locus Dispatch, Compuweigh scale system, onboard truck computer, or manual entry.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes calculated on the total charge amount.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Scale ticket number, BOL (Bill of Lading) number, or field service ticket number associated with this usage event.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charge for this usage event including base charge and all surcharges, before tax. Sum of base_charge_amount + fuel_surcharge_amount + environmental_fee_amount + other_surcharge_amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for billing this usage event: pickups (count), tons (weight), cubic_yards (volume), each (per item), or days (rental duration).']
  unit_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Per-unit price applied from the rate schedule (e.g., dollars per ton, dollars per pickup, dollars per cubic yard).']
  volume_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Volume of material collected, measured in cubic yards. Used for roll-off containers and volumetric billing.']
  weight_tons decimal(12,4) [note: 'Weight of material collected or disposed, measured in tons. Captured from scale systems at landfills, transfer stations, or onboard truck scales.']

  Note: 'Raw and rated usage record capturing a billable service event that drives usage-based billing — e.g., a completed waste pickup, a container weigh-in at a landfill scale house, a roll-off pull/delivery, or a recycling drop-off. Captures event date/time, service type, service location, quantity (pickups, tons, yards), container ID (CID/RFID), route ID, driver ID, ticket number (scale ticket or BOL), billing status (unbilled, rated, billed, excluded), applied rate schedule, rated amount, applicable surcharges, and rating exceptions. Source data from AMCS, Locus Dispatch, Compuweigh scale systems, and onboard truck computers. After rating, includes calculated charge amount and tax for invoice generation.'
}

Table billing.rated_usage {
  rated_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rated usage record. Primary key for the rated usage entity.']
  applied_surcharge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.surcharge. Business justification: Rated usage records capture priced usage events with fuel_surcharge_amount, environmental_fee_amount, and other_surcharge_amount. Adding applied_surcharge_id FK links to the surcharge master definitio']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific container associated with this usage event. Applicable for container rental and pickup usage types.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate schedule applied during the rating process. Defines the pricing structure used to calculate charges.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which this usage occurred. Determines applicable rate schedules and pricing terms.']
  contract_service_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_commitment. Business justification: Rated usage must reference the service commitment that defines rate schedule, service frequency, and overage thresholds. Critical for tiered pricing application, minimum commitment true-ups, variance ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which this usage was rated. Identifies the billable party.']
  diversion_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.diversion_record. Business justification: Validates billing accuracy against actual environmental performance for recycling services. Links rated recycling usage to diversion outcomes for performance verification, sustainability reporting, an']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management facility where usage was processed (landfill, transfer station, MRF). Applicable for disposal and processing usage types.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice to which this rated usage was aggregated. Null if not yet invoiced. Links rated usage to billing output.']
  load_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.load_ticket. Business justification: Transfer station tipping fees are rated from load tickets; direct link enables automated rating, reconciliation between operational and billing systems, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Rated usage for billing must link to the permit authorizing the service, especially for regulated waste streams. Ensures charges align with permitted activities and supports regulatory reporting of bi']
  pickup_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.pickup_event. Business justification: Rated usage records require direct link to the pickup event that generated the billable service for audit trail, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting of service delivery.']
  primary_rated_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the billing analyst who performed the manual override. Null for non-overridden records. Supports SOX compliance and audit requirements.']
  rate_component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific rate component within the rate schedule that was applied (e.g., base rate, overage rate, peak rate). Enables granular charge attribution.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route on which usage occurred. Applicable for pickup and collection usage types.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service location where usage occurred. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and geographic reporting.']
  tax_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tax_rule. Business justification: Rated usage records include tax_amount but no FK to tax_rule master. During rating, tax rules are applied based on service type, jurisdiction, and customer exemption status. Adding tax_rule_id FK link']
  usage_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the raw usage event that was rated. Links this rated record back to the originating operational usage event (pickup, tonnage, container rental day, etc.).']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.weight_ticket. Business justification: Tonnage-based rated usage requires direct link to weight ticket for audit trail, regulatory reporting, and reconciliation between operational weights and billed quantities.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period to which this rated usage is assigned for financial reporting. Format typically YYYY-MM or YYYY-Qn.']
  base_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated charge amount before surcharges, fees, and taxes. Computed as usage_quantity multiplied by unit_rate.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this rated usage record. May include override justification, special handling notes, or dispute resolution details.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rated usage record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rated usage record. Supports multi-currency billing for international operations.']
  environmental_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory or operational environmental fee applied to the usage. May include host community fees, state environmental fees, or landfill closure fees.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional charge applied to cover fuel cost fluctuations. Calculated based on fuel surcharge rate schedule and usage quantity.']
  gl_date date [note: 'Accounting date for general ledger posting. Determines the accounting period for revenue recognition. May differ from usage_date based on revenue recognition rules.']
  invoice_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number on the invoice where this rated usage appears. Null if not yet invoiced. Enables traceability from invoice back to rated usage.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rated usage record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  other_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional surcharges not categorized as fuel or environmental. May include administrative fees, special handling charges, or regulatory compliance fees.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rated usage was manually overridden by a billing analyst. True if rates or amounts were manually adjusted, false for standard rating engine output.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for manual override if override_flag is true. Null for non-overridden records. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manual override was performed. Null for non-overridden records.']
  rated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rating engine processed and priced this usage event. Represents the rating execution time, not the usage occurrence time.']
  rating_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the rating engine batch run that processed this usage event. Supports reprocessing and audit trail.']
  rating_error_code varchar [note: 'Error code if rating failed or encountered issues. Null for successfully rated records. Used for troubleshooting and reprocessing.']
  rating_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message describing why rating failed or what issue was encountered. Null for successfully rated records.']
  rating_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the rating process for this usage event. Indicates whether rating completed successfully or requires intervention.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Revenue recognition method applied to this rated usage. Determines timing of revenue recognition per GAAP/IFRS standards.']
  service_type_code varchar [note: 'Type of waste management service for which usage was rated. Categorizes the billable service line.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total charge amount before taxes. Sum of base_charge_amount plus all applicable surcharges and fees.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the rated usage. Includes sales tax, use tax, and any other applicable taxes based on service location and tax jurisdiction.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final billable amount for this rated usage event. Sum of subtotal_amount and tax_amount. This is the amount that will appear on the customer invoice.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the usage quantity is expressed. Aligns with rate schedule unit definitions.']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit applied from the rate schedule. The rate component value used in the charge calculation.']
  usage_date date [note: 'Date when the actual usage event occurred (pickup date, disposal date, rental day). Business event date for revenue recognition and reporting.']
  usage_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Measured quantity of usage that was rated. Value depends on usage type: number of pickups, tons of waste, days of container rental, etc.']
  usage_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the usage event being rated. Distinguishes between different billable usage categories (pickups, weight-based, time-based, fees).']

  Note: 'Priced and rated version of a usage event, representing the output of the rating engine that applies the appropriate rate schedule to a raw usage event to produce a billable charge amount. Captures rated date, applied rate schedule, applied rate component, rated quantity, unit rate, calculated charge amount, applicable surcharges, tax amount, and rating status (rated, pending, error, override). Bridges raw operational usage data and the invoice generation process.'
}

Table billing.tax_rule {
  tax_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax rule configuration. Primary key.']
  primary_tax_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this tax rule configuration for production use.']
  superseded_by_rule_tax_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tax rule that replaces this rule when it expires or is updated, maintaining audit trail of rule changes.']
  applicable_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of waste management service types to which this tax rule applies (e.g., residential collection, commercial hauling, landfill disposal, recycling, hazardous waste). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential_collection|commercial_collection|landfill_disposal|recycling|hazardous_waste|transfer_station|waste_to_energy|rolloff_service — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the tax rule configuration: pending review, approved for use, or rejected.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tax rule was approved (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the tax: percentage of base amount, flat fee, tiered rate structure, per-unit charge, or compound (tax on tax).']
  compounding_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which this tax is applied when multiple taxes compound on each other. Lower numbers are applied first.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax rule record was first created in the system (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for tax amounts (typically USD for U.S. operations).']
  customer_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Customer segments to which this tax rule applies (all, residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, government).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this tax rule expires or is superseded by a new rule. Null indicates an open-ended rule (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this tax rule becomes active and begins applying to transactions (format: yyyy-MM-dd).']
  exemption_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of exemption categories that exclude customers or transactions from this tax (e.g., nonprofit, government, recycling-only, tax-exempt certificate holders).']
  exemption_certificate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid tax exemption certificate must be on file to apply the exemption (True/False).']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount charged per transaction or unit when tax is assessed as a flat fee rather than a percentage rate.']
