  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in SAP FI/CO where tax revenue or liability is posted for financial reporting and reconciliation.']
  gl_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal allocation and management reporting of tax-related revenue or expenses.']
  jurisdiction_county varchar [note: 'County name where the tax rule applies, if applicable to county-level taxation.']
  jurisdiction_municipality varchar [note: 'City or municipality name where the tax rule applies, if applicable to municipal-level taxation.']
  jurisdiction_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code defining the geographic boundary for tax applicability, used for address-based tax determination.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the tax rule applies (ISO 3166-2:US format).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax rule record was last updated (format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  maximum_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum tax amount cap per transaction or billing period, limiting tax liability for high-value transactions.']
  minimum_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum tax amount to be charged regardless of calculated tax, ensuring a floor for tax collection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special instructions, or business rationale for the tax rule configuration.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the statute, ordinance, or regulation authorizing this tax (e.g., California Revenue and Taxation Code Section 6051, City Ordinance 2023-045).']
  remittance_due_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) or days after period end when tax remittance payment is due to the authority.']
  remittance_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which collected taxes must be remitted to the tax authority: monthly, quarterly, annually, or on-demand.']
  reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for regulatory tax reporting and filing with state or local authorities (e.g., sales tax jurisdiction code, waste tax category code).']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Accounting rule for when tax revenue is recognized: immediate upon billing, deferred to service delivery, prorated over service period, or milestone-based.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the tax rule, used for external reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tax rule including applicability conditions, exemptions, and business context.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the tax rule for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax rule: draft, pending approval, active, suspended, expired, or superseded.']
  tax_authority_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the tax authority (e.g., state FIPS code, county code, municipality tax ID).']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental entity or tax authority imposing the tax (e.g., State of California, Cook County, City of Houston).']
  tax_authority_type varchar [note: 'Level of government or jurisdiction imposing the tax (state, county, municipality, special district, federal, tribal).']
  tax_base varchar [note: 'The base amount or unit upon which the tax is calculated: gross invoice amount, net amount after discounts, tonnage disposed, number of pickups, container count, or service days.']
  tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Tax rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0825 for 8.25% tax rate). Supports up to six decimal places for precision.']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Tax rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.25 for 8.25% tax). Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax being applied: sales tax, excise tax, solid waste tax, environmental surcharge, tipping fee tax, or franchise fee.']
  waste_stream_applicability varchar [note: 'Types of waste streams subject to this tax rule (e.g., MSW, C&D, hazardous, recyclable materials). Municipal Solid Waste (MSW), Construction and Demolition (C&D) waste classification.']

  Note: 'Reference configuration defining tax applicability rules for waste management services by jurisdiction. Captures tax authority (state, county, municipality), tax type (sales tax, excise tax, solid waste tax, environmental surcharge tax), applicable service types, tax rate, effective date range, exemption categories, and tax account GL mapping. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance for Waste Management\'s nationwide operations.'
}

Table billing.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable write-off record. Primary key.']
  ar_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AR account from which the balance is being written off. Links to the receivables ledger account.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Write-off approval thresholds and GL treatment vary by contract type and customer segment. Finance needs contract context for bad debt reserve calculations, customer lifetime value assessment, and win']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this write-off. Links to the customer account that holds the uncollectible balance.']
  final_collection_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.collection_action. Business justification: Write-offs occur after collection efforts are exhausted. Write_off table has collection_attempts_count and last_collection_action_date but no FK to the actual collection_action records. Adding final_c']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that is being written off. May be null if write-off applies to multiple invoices or an account-level balance.']
  primary_write_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the write-off. Required for SOX segregation of duties compliance. Links to the user/employee who has write-off approval authority.']
  tertiary_write_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this write-off record. Used for change accountability and SOX compliance.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI/CO document number generated when the write-off was posted to the general ledger. Used for GL reconciliation and audit trails.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period in which the write-off is recorded. Format: YYYY-MM. Used for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount written off as uncollectible bad debt. Represents the AR balance that is being removed from the books.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level of the approver. Values: supervisor (up to $1K), manager (up to $10K), director (up to $50K), vp (up to $250K), cfo (over $250K). Ensures write-offs are approved at appropriate authority level per dollar thresholds.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the write-off request. Values: pending (awaiting approval), approved (write-off authorized), rejected (write-off denied). SOX control point.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the write-off was approved. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance documentation.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to supporting documentation for the write-off decision. May include collection notes, legal documents, or bankruptcy filings.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The business unit or division responsible for the original revenue and subsequent write-off. Used for segment reporting and performance analysis.']
  collection_attempts_count varchar [note: 'Total number of collection attempts made before the write-off decision. Demonstrates due diligence for SOX compliance.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center code associated with the write-off for management accounting purposes. Used for operational performance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this write-off record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the write-off amount. Typically USD for domestic operations.']
  days_past_due_at_write_off varchar [note: 'Number of days the invoice was past due at the time of write-off. Used for aging analysis and collection effectiveness metrics.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the write-off is recorded. Used for annual financial reporting and trend analysis.']
  gl_ar_contra_account varchar [note: 'The GL contra account code used to reduce the AR balance. Paired with bad debt expense account for double-entry accounting.']
  gl_bad_debt_expense_account varchar [note: 'The GL account code to which the bad debt expense is posted. Used for financial statement reporting and GAAP compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this write-off record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, context, or special circumstances related to the write-off. Used for operational and audit documentation.']
  original_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original total amount of the invoice(s) before any payments or adjustments. Provides context for the write-off magnitude.']
  outstanding_balance_before_write_off decimal(18,2) [note: 'The AR balance outstanding immediately before the write-off was applied. Used for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the write-off transaction was posted to the general ledger. May differ from write_off_date due to period-end processing.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the write-off. Values: bankruptcy (customer filed bankruptcy), customer_dispute_settled (dispute resolved with write-off), small_balance (balance below collection threshold), statute_of_limitations (collection period expired), business_closure (customer business closed), uncollectible (deemed uncollectible after collection efforts).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances leading to the write-off. Provides additional context beyond the reason code.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount recovered after the write-off was recorded. Used to track bad debt recovery and adjust bad debt expense. Null if no recovery has occurred.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'Date when recovery payment was received. Null if no recovery has occurred. Used for bad debt recovery reporting.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which the write-off was reversed. Null if not reversed. Used when write-off was recorded in error or customer subsequently pays.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this write-off has been reversed. True if reversed, False if active. Used for financial statement accuracy.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the write-off was reversed. Required when reversal_indicator is True. Used for audit documentation.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'The service line associated with the original revenue. Values: residential_collection, commercial_collection, recycling, landfill, hazardous_waste, waste_to_energy. Used for bad debt analysis by service type.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this write-off is subject to SOX internal control testing. True for material write-offs requiring enhanced documentation.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the AR balance was officially written off as uncollectible bad debt. Used for financial reporting and GAAP revenue recognition.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the write-off transaction. Format: WO-NNNNNNNN. Used for audit trails and external references.']
  write_off_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the write-off record. Values: written_off (initial write-off recorded), partially_recovered (some amount recovered post-write-off), fully_recovered (entire amount recovered), reversed (write-off reversed due to error or policy change).']
  write_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the write-off transaction was recorded in the system. Used for audit trails and SOX compliance.']

  Note: 'Record of an AR balance written off as uncollectible bad debt. Captures write-off date, write-off amount, original invoice references, write-off reason (bankruptcy, customer dispute settled, small balance, statute of limitations), approval authority, GL bad debt expense account, and recovery status (written off, partially recovered, fully recovered). Supports SOX-controlled bad debt management and GAAP bad debt expense recognition.'
}

Table billing.prepayment {
  prepayment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the prepayment or deposit record. Primary key.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract or agreement that required or governs this prepayment, particularly for municipal mobilization fees or commercial service agreements.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that made the prepayment. Links to the customer account master record.']
  prepayment_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager who approved the prepayment for posting, required for SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) segregation of duties controls.']
  prepayment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who recorded the prepayment receipt in the system.']
  prepayment_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the prepayment record, supporting SOX audit trail requirements.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-01) when the prepayment was recognized in the financial statements.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original total amount of the prepayment received from the customer before any applications or adjustments.']
  application_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for automatic application of this prepayment against invoices when multiple prepayments exist for the same customer account. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of the prepayment that has been applied against invoices or service charges to date.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the prepayment was approved for posting to the general ledger.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prepayment should be automatically applied to new invoices (true) or requires manual application by AR staff (false).']
  bank_account_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account where the prepayment funds were deposited.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the prepayment record was first created in the database.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the prepayment transaction (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Date when the prepayment funds were deposited into the company bank account, which may differ from receipt date for checks or delayed transfers.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the prepayment is no longer valid for application and may be subject to refund or forfeiture per contract terms. Null for prepayments with no expiration.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the source system for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  forfeiture_date date [note: 'Date when the prepayment was forfeited and recognized as revenue per contract terms (e.g., non-refundable deposit after service cancellation). Null if not forfeited.']
  forfeiture_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or contract clause justifying the forfeiture of the prepayment (e.g., customer cancellation, breach of contract, expiration). Null if not forfeited.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code for the unapplied cash or customer deposit liability account where the prepayment is recorded.']
  gl_date date [note: 'Accounting date when the prepayment was posted to the general ledger, determining the fiscal period for financial reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the prepayment record was last updated, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, special instructions, or business notes related to the prepayment (e.g., customer request details, special handling requirements).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Financial instrument used to remit the prepayment: check, wire transfer, ACH (Automated Clearing House), credit card, cash, or money order.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the payment instrument (check number, wire confirmation, ACH trace number, credit card authorization code) for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  prepayment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the prepayment transaction, typically system-generated or manually assigned for tracking and reference purposes.']
  prepayment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prepayment: active (available for application), partially applied (some balance used), fully applied (entire amount consumed), refunded (returned to customer), expired (past validity period), or forfeited (retained per contract terms).']
  prepayment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prepayment purpose: deposit (refundable security), advance payment (applied to future invoices), retainer (ongoing service commitment), mobilization fee (municipal contract startup), security deposit (credit risk mitigation), or container deposit (roll-off equipment).']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the prepayment was received from the customer. This is the business event date for the cash receipt.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the prepayment was received, including time zone information for multi-region operations.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the customer from this prepayment. Null if no refund has been issued.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date when the prepayment or remaining balance was refunded to the customer. Null if no refund has been processed.']
  refund_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the prepayment is eligible for refund to the customer (true) or is non-refundable per contract terms (false).']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method used to return funds to the customer: check, wire transfer, ACH, credit card reversal, or account credit. Null if no refund has been processed.']
  refund_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the refund transaction (check number, wire confirmation, ACH trace) for reconciliation purposes. Null if no refund has been processed.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unapplied balance available for future invoice applications. Calculated as prepayment_amount minus applied_amount minus any refunds or adjustments.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Expected or actual start date of services for which this prepayment was collected, relevant for advance payments and mobilization fees.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating system that created the prepayment record (e.g., ORACLE_AR, SALESFORCE, WASTE_LOGICS) for data lineage and integration tracking.']

  Note: 'Customer prepayment or deposit held on account before services are rendered, common for new commercial accounts, roll-off container rentals, and municipal contract mobilizations. Captures prepayment amount, receipt date, payment method, prepayment type (deposit, advance payment, retainer), applied amount, remaining balance, expiry date, and refund status. Managed in Oracle AR as an unapplied cash balance until offset against future invoices.'
}

Table billing.billing_account {
  billing_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing account. Primary key.']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_area. Business justification: Billing accounts are domiciled in service areas for franchise compliance, tax jurisdiction determination, service territory management, and regulatory reporting. Essential for municipal contract compl']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master account in the customer domain. A single customer may have multiple billing accounts for different service locations or business units.']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'FK to billing.billing_cycle']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary service location associated with this billing account for service delivery and route assignment.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the billing account, often matching the customer legal name or a business unit designation.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable billing account number displayed on invoices and customer communications. Externally visible identifier.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing account indicating its operational state and billing eligibility.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing account based on customer segment and service model.']
  autopay_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is enrolled in automatic payment processing on invoice due date.']
  autopay_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the customer enrolled in automatic payment processing.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for billing correspondence and invoice delivery.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, apartment, or unit number for billing correspondence.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for billing contact to receive invoices and payment notifications.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary billing contact person responsible for invoice receipt and payment coordination.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for billing contact for payment inquiries and dispute resolution.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the billing address.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business unit or division code for organizational hierarchy and reporting segmentation.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account was closed and billing operations ceased.']
  close_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for account closure (e.g., customer request, non-payment, service termination).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal financial reporting and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for the account before service suspension or payment requirement.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit assessment classification used for credit limit determination and collections risk management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all billing transactions on this account.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation level in the collections process, indicating severity of delinquency and collection actions taken.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code in SAP FI/CO for revenue recognition and accounts receivable posting.']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Most recent date when a dunning notice or collection communication was sent to the customer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing account record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for special billing instructions, account-specific arrangements, or internal comments.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account was first established and activated for billing operations.']
  oracle_account_number varchar [note: 'External account identifier in Oracle Revenue Management system used for billing and invoicing integration.']
  paperless_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted for electronic invoice delivery instead of paper statements.']
  paperless_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the customer enrolled in paperless billing.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due, defining the standard payment window.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Default payment instrument type preferred by the customer for invoice settlement.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code for segment reporting and business unit performance tracking.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is exempt from sales tax or other applicable taxes on waste management services.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued certificate number validating the tax-exempt status of the account.']
  tax_exemption_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the tax exemption certificate expires and requires renewal.']

  Note: 'Billing-specific account entity representing the financial relationship between Waste Management and a customer for invoicing and payment purposes. Distinct from the customer master (owned by customer domain) — this entity captures billing address, billing contact, preferred payment method, autopay enrollment, paperless billing flag, billing currency, tax exemption status, tax exemption certificate number, credit rating, and Oracle Revenue Management account number. A single customer may have multiple billing accounts (e.g., separate accounts per service location or business unit).'
}

Table billing.billing_program_enrollment {
  billing_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_program_enrollment']
  account_billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the billing account enrolled in the program']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to billing.billing_account identifying the customer account enrolled in the program']
  circular_economy_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to sustainability.circular_economy_program identifying the program']
  program_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this enrollment record. Primary key.']
  actual_diversion_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual volume of material (in tons) diverted by this billing account through this program since enrollment. Updated periodically based on operational data.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal contract or service agreement governing this program enrollment, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was created.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The date when the billing account enrolled in this circular economy program. Used for program tenure analysis and eligibility determination.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'The date when the enrollment ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing enrollments.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment indicating whether the customer is actively participating, suspended, completed the program, or cancelled.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial incentive amount (in USD) provided to the customer for participation in this program. May be a rebate, credit, or performance-based payment.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent performance review for this customer-program enrollment, used to track review cadence and customer engagement.']
  participation_tier varchar [note: 'The service level or commitment tier for this enrollment (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Determines performance targets, incentive rates, and reporting frequency.']
  performance_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'The customer-specific diversion or participation target (in tons) for this enrollment. Used to measure customer performance against commitments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment contract between a billing account and a circular economy program. It captures customer participation in sustainability initiatives including enrollment terms, performance tracking, and financial incentives. Each record links one billing account to one circular economy program with attributes that exist only in the context of this participation relationship.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management operations, billing accounts (customers) can enroll in multiple circular economy programs simultaneously (e.g., a commercial customer participates in both organics composting and electronics take-back programs), and each program has multiple enrolled customers. The business actively manages these enrollments as operational entities, tracking enrollment dates, participation tiers, customer-specific performance targets, actual diversion volumes, and financial incentives for each customer-program combination. This is not a derived analytical relationship but an operational business process where enrollment records are created, monitored, and updated as part of program management and customer sustainability reporting.'
}

Table billing.dunning_sequence {
  dunning_sequence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dunning_sequence']
  escalation_dunning_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dunning_sequence (escalation_dunning_sequence_id)']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether management approval is required before assigning this dunning sequence to customer accounts.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the manager who approved this dunning sequence for operational use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning sequence was approved for operational use.']
  auto_escalation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dunning sequence automatically progresses through escalation steps without manual intervention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning sequence record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary thresholds in this dunning sequence.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the dunning sequence including its purpose, escalation strategy, and intended use cases.']
  dispute_pause_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dunning sequence automatically pauses when a customer files a billing dispute.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this dunning sequence is retired and no longer available for new assignments. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this dunning sequence becomes active and available for assignment to customer accounts.']
  final_action_type varchar [note: 'The ultimate collection action taken when all dunning steps are exhausted without payment resolution.']
  gaap_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning sequence aligns with GAAP revenue recognition and accounts receivable management standards.']
  ifrs_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning sequence aligns with IFRS revenue recognition and accounts receivable management standards.']
  initial_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice due date before the first dunning action is triggered.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this dunning sequence configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dunning sequence record was last updated in the system.']
  legal_hold_pause_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dunning sequence automatically pauses when an account is placed under legal hold or bankruptcy protection.']
  minimum_balance_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum outstanding account balance amount required to trigger this dunning sequence. Balances below this threshold are excluded from dunning.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to deliver dunning notices to customers in this sequence.']
  payment_plan_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this dunning sequence are eligible to request payment plan arrangements to avoid further escalation.']
  sequence_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the dunning sequence used in billing and collections operations.']
  sequence_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the dunning sequence describing its purpose and escalation strategy.']
  sequence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dunning sequence based on customer segment or service type.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Escalation severity level of the dunning sequence indicating the aggressiveness of collection actions.']
  sox_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning sequence meets Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance requirements for revenue recognition and financial controls.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dunning sequence indicating whether it is available for assignment to customer accounts.']
  step_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days between consecutive dunning steps in the escalation sequence.']
  total_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of escalation steps defined in this dunning sequence from initial reminder to final collection action.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the dunning sequence configuration used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this dunning sequence configuration.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for dunning_sequence. Referenced by dunning_sequence_id.'
}

Table billing.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for credit_memo']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account to which this credit memo applies.']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific container associated with the credit memo, if applicable (e.g., rental credit).']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the credit memo record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the credit memo.']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute that resulted in this credit memo, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the credit memo. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice being credited, if applicable. Nullable for standalone credits.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the credit memo record.']
  originating_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credit_memo (originating_credit_memo_id)']
  refund_transaction_id varchar [note: 'External transaction identifier for the refund payment, if applicable (e.g., check number, ACH trace number).']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route associated with the service being credited, if applicable.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service location associated with the credit memo, if applicable.']
  voided_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who voided the credit memo, if applicable. Nullable if not voided.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the credit memo that has been applied to invoices or account balances. May be less than net_credit_amount if partially applied.']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo was applied to the customer account or invoice. Nullable if not yet applied.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo was approved by authorized personnel. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period to which this credit memo applies, if applicable.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period to which this credit memo applies, if applicable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal financial allocation and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the credit being issued to the customer, before any adjustments.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the credit memo, typically formatted as CM-XXXXXXXX.']
  credit_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit memo in the billing workflow.']
  credit_memo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit memo based on the reason for issuance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit memo amounts (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the credit memo becomes effective for accounting and billing purposes.']
  general_ledger_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this credit memo is posted for financial reporting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo was officially issued to the customer.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was last modified in the system.']
  net_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net credit amount after all adjustments and taxes, representing the final credit value applied to the customer account.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments regarding the credit memo for internal reference.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the credit will be returned or applied to the customer (credit to account, refund check, etc.).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason for issuing the credit memo (e.g., SVCFAIL, OVERCHG, DISPUTE).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the credit memo was issued.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date on which the refund was issued to the customer, if applicable. Nullable if no refund issued.']
  refund_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a monetary refund has been issued to the customer (True) or credit applied to account (False).']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unapplied credit balance remaining on the credit memo, available for future application.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue adjustment from this credit memo is recognized in financial statements.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the credit memo, if applicable.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the credit memo was voided, if applicable.']
  voided_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit memo was voided, if applicable. Nullable if not voided.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for credit_memo. Referenced by credit_memo_id.'
}

Table billing.payment_method {
  payment_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_method']
  superseded_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_method (superseded_payment_method_id)']
  available_for_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method is available for commercial customer accounts.']
  available_for_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method is available for residential customer accounts.']
  chargeback_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments made with this method are subject to chargeback disputes.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on regulatory compliance requirements, restrictions, or certifications applicable to this payment method (e.g., PCI DSS, NACHA, SOX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment method record was first created in the system.']
  customer_facing_description varchar [note: 'Customer-friendly description of the payment method displayed during checkout and billing.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the display order of payment methods in customer-facing interfaces.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this payment method was discontinued or deprecated. Null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this payment method became available for use.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code in SAP FI/CO for posting transactions using this payment method.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the icon image representing this payment method in user interfaces.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes and processing instructions for staff handling this payment method.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default payment method pre-selected for customers.']
  is_electronic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method is processed electronically (true) or requires manual handling (false).']
  maximum_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount allowed for this payment method, in USD. Null if no maximum applies.']
  method_category varchar [note: 'Broader business category grouping payment methods by processing characteristics.']
  method_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the payment method type (e.g., CC_VISA, ACH_DEBIT, CHECK, CASH).']
  method_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the payment method (e.g., Visa Credit Card, ACH Direct Debit, Paper Check, Cash).']
  method_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the payment method instrument type.']
  minimum_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount allowed for this payment method, in USD. Null if no minimum applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment method record was last updated.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party payment processor or gateway used for this payment method (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, Chase Paymentech).']
  processing_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of business days required to process and settle a payment using this method.']
  processor_merchant_code varchar [note: 'Merchant account identifier assigned by the payment processor for this payment method.']
  requires_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method requires real-time authorization before acceptance (e.g., credit card authorization).']
  requires_bank_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method requires customer bank account information (e.g., ACH, wire transfer).']
  requires_card_details boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method requires credit or debit card details.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Revenue recognition treatment for payments received via this method, per GAAP/IFRS standards (e.g., immediate, deferred, milestone-based).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for fraud and chargeback exposure associated with this payment method.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment method. Active methods are available for customer selection and transaction processing.']
  supports_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method can be used for recurring subscription billing.']
  supports_refund boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment method supports refund transactions.']
  transaction_fee_flat decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount charged per transaction for this payment method, in USD.']
  transaction_fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage-based fee rate applied to transaction amount for this payment method (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_method. Referenced by payment_method_id.'
}

Table billing.refund_transaction {
  refund_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for refund_transaction']
  original_refund_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on refund_transaction (original_refund_transaction_id)']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing adjustment record that triggered this refund, if applicable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was approved for processing. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or system that authorized the refund for processing.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period for which the refund applies.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period for which the refund applies.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was successfully completed and funds were delivered to the customer.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code responsible for the refund, typically the service location or business unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this refund transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount. Typically USD for Waste Management operations.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account receiving the refund.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute case that resulted in this refund, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this refund transaction is posted for financial reporting.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this refund is associated with.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund has tax implications requiring reporting or adjustment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this refund transaction record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments about the refund transaction for internal reference.']
  original_payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment transaction being refunded, if applicable.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment processor or bank for tracking the refund payment (check number, ACH trace number, transaction ID).']
  processing_date date [note: 'Date when the refund payment was initiated or sent to the payment processor.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount being refunded to the customer in US dollars.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund will be issued: original_payment_method (reverse to source), check (mailed check), ach (electronic bank transfer), credit_memo (applied to future invoices), account_credit (balance adjustment).']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique refund transaction number used for customer communication and tracking.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific business reason for the refund.']
  refund_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the refund was issued, including any supporting context or customer communication notes.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized for processing), processing (payment in progress), completed (refund issued), cancelled (request withdrawn), rejected (not approved).']
  refund_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the refund transaction was recorded in the system. Primary business event timestamp.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund reason: overpayment (customer paid more than owed), service_credit (service quality issue), billing_error (incorrect invoice), rate_adjustment (pricing correction), cancellation (service termination), dispute_resolution (resolved customer dispute).']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the refund request was initially submitted.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that initiated the refund request (customer service representative, automated system, customer portal).']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the refund transaction was reversed or voided.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this refund transaction has been reversed or voided.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the refund was reversed, if applicable.']
  service_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service location associated with the original service that is being refunded.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the refund amount that represents tax being returned to the customer.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for refund_transaction. Referenced by refund_transaction_id.'
}

  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Waste service offerings require specific operating permits (e.g., hazardous waste collection requires RCRA Part B permit, medical waste requires state-specific permits). Operations cannot execute offe']
  required_asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Drives asset eligibili']

Business justification: Hazmat, medical waste, and specialized services require specific certifications (HAZWOPER, CDL-A, DOT hazmat endorsement). Drives crew assignment eligibility, service delivery compliance, and regulato']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Service offerings often require vendor partnerships for delivery (e.g., TSDF disposal, specialized equipment rental). Real business process: vendor selection and capability assessment for service deli']

  wte_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to energy.wte_facility.
