  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency involved (e.g., EPA, OSHA, DOT, state environmental quality department, local enforcement agency) if the action was triggered by a regulatory event.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation, code section, or permit condition that the corrective or preventive action addresses (e.g., 40 CFR 258.21, OSHA 1910.120, permit condition 3.2.1).']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area responsible for implementing the action (e.g., Operations, Maintenance, EHS, Fleet, Engineering).']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis performed to identify the underlying cause of the nonconformity or potential nonconformity. May reference methodologies such as 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, or Fault Tree Analysis.']
  safety_impact_category varchar [note: 'Category of occupational health and safety impact addressed by the action (e.g., hazardous waste exposure, equipment safety, PPE compliance, confined space, fall protection, vehicle safety).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the corrective or preventive action was originally created and managed (e.g., Enviance EHS, SAP EHS module, manual entry).']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or required date by which the corrective or preventive action must be completed. May be driven by regulatory deadlines or internal commitments.']
  triggering_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the source event that triggered this action (e.g., inspection ID, incident ID, audit finding ID, violation notice number). Enables traceability to the root cause.']
  triggering_event_type varchar [note: 'The type of event that initiated this corrective or preventive action (e.g., regulatory inspection, violation notice, environmental incident, internal audit finding, customer complaint, or proactive self-identification).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness of the corrective or preventive action was verified. Null if verification has not yet occurred.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the effectiveness of the corrective or preventive action (e.g., follow-up inspection, document review, testing, monitoring data analysis, management review).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the verification process indicating whether the action has been verified as effective, ineffective, or is pending verification.']
  verified_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who verified the effectiveness of the corrective or preventive action.']

  Note: 'Corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) initiated in response to inspection findings, violation notices, incidents, or internal audit observations. Tracks action ID, triggering event reference, action type (corrective/preventive), description of required action, responsible party, target completion date, actual completion date, verification method, and closure status. Supports the full CAPA lifecycle from identification through verification and closure. Integrates with Enviance EHS CAPA module and SAP EHS. Supports ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 continual improvement requirements.'
}

Table compliance.ehs_incident {
  ehs_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EHS incident record. Primary key.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Incidents may be discovered during compliance inspections or internal audits. This FK links incidents to the inspection that identified them, supporting inspection follow-up and corrective action trac']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee involved in the incident if the injured party is a company employee. Null for contractors or third parties.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the incident occurred (landfill, MRF, transfer station, WTE plant, or administrative office).']
  involved_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Incidents frequently involve vendor personnel or equipment (contractor injury, vendor vehicle accident). Essential for vendor safety performance tracking, insurance claims, and vendor qualification de']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: When incidents occur during tasks with JHAs, linking enables root cause analysis of whether JHA controls were adequate or followed. Essential for JHA effectiveness review and continuous improvement. R']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: EHS incidents (injuries, environmental releases, near-misses) may involve permit violations or occur during permitted activities. This FK links incidents to relevant permits, supporting permit complia']
  regulatory_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_corrective_action. Business justification: Incidents trigger corrective and preventive actions (CAPA). This FK links incidents to the corrective actions taken to address root causes and prevent recurrence, enabling CAPA tracking and closure ve']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: EHS incidents should link to the safety program under which the incident occurred for program effectiveness tracking and continuous improvement. Enables analysis of which safety programs are preventin']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet vehicle involved in the incident if the incident type is vehicle accident. Null if not a vehicle incident.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'Specific body part(s) injured in the incident (e.g., left hand, lower back, eyes). Null if no personal injury occurred.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which all corrective actions were verified as complete. Null if actions not yet complete.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Summary of corrective actions identified to address root causes and prevent recurrence. Null if no corrective action required.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which corrective actions must be completed. Null if no corrective action required.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions were identified as necessary to prevent recurrence of the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident record was first created in the EHS system.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured employee was unable to work as a result of the incident. Zero if no time lost. Used for OSHA DART rate calculation.']
  days_of_job_transfer varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured employee was transferred to another job as a result of the incident. Zero if no transfer occurred.']
  days_of_restricted_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured employee was on restricted or light duty as a result of the incident. Zero if no restriction. Used for OSHA DART rate calculation.']
  environmental_media_affected varchar [note: 'The environmental medium impacted by a release or spill incident. Applicable only to environmental release incidents.']
  immediate_cause varchar [note: 'The direct, proximate cause of the incident as observed at the time of occurrence (e.g., equipment failure, slip and fall, vehicle collision).']
  incident_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the incident occurred.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative description of what happened during the incident, including sequence of events, conditions, and immediate observations.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique incident tracking number assigned by the EHS system for external reference and reporting.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident record. Open indicates initial reporting; under investigation means root cause analysis in progress; corrective action pending means investigation complete but actions not yet implemented; closed means all actions complete and incident resolved.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the incident occurred, including timezone information.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident based on severity and regulatory reporting requirements. OSHA recordable requires formal logging; first aid is minor treatment; near-miss had potential but no actual harm; environmental release involves spills or emissions; property damage affects assets; vehicle accident involves fleet collision.']
  injured_party_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person injured or affected by the incident. Null if incident did not involve personal injury.']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical injury sustained. Null if incident did not result in personal injury. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laceration|fracture|sprain|burn|contusion|amputation|respiratory|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal incident investigation was completed and root cause analysis finalized. Null if investigation not yet complete.']
  investigator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EHS professional or manager who led the incident investigation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident record was last updated in the EHS system.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific location within the facility where the incident occurred (e.g., tipping floor, sorting line, landfill cell 5, maintenance bay).']
  material_released varchar [note: 'Description of the hazardous or non-hazardous material released during an environmental incident (e.g., diesel fuel, leachate, hydraulic fluid). Null if not an environmental release.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency was notified of the incident. Null if no notification required.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify the regulatory agency (phone, email, online portal, written report, in-person). Null if no notification required.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA criteria for recordability on the OSHA 300 log (death, days away from work, restricted work, medical treatment beyond first aid, loss of consciousness, or significant injury/illness diagnosed by healthcare professional).']
  ppe_in_use_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the injured party was wearing required personal protective equipment at the time of the incident. Null if not applicable.']
  ppe_type varchar [note: 'Description of the PPE that was (or should have been) in use at the time of the incident (e.g., hard hat, safety glasses, gloves, respirator, steel-toed boots). Null if not applicable.']
  property_damage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of property damage resulting from the incident in USD. Null if no property damage occurred.']
  regulatory_agency_notified varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency (EPA, OSHA, DOT, state environmental agency, local fire department) that was notified of the incident. Null if no notification required.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident triggers a mandatory regulatory reporting obligation to EPA, OSHA, DOT, or state/local agencies.']
  release_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated quantity of material released during an environmental incident. Null if not an environmental release.']
  release_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the release quantity (gallons, liters, pounds, kilograms, cubic yards, tons).']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who initially reported the incident to the EHS department or management.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was first reported to the EHS system or management.']
  root_cause_classification varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the underlying root cause determined through incident investigation. Human error includes training gaps or procedural non-compliance; equipment failure is mechanical breakdown; process deficiency is inadequate procedure; environmental condition is weather or site hazard; management system is organizational control weakness.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the root cause analysis findings, including contributing factors and systemic issues identified.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names of individuals who witnessed the incident. Used for investigation purposes. Null if no witnesses.']

  Note: 'Environmental, health, and safety incident records capturing workplace injuries, near-misses, environmental releases, spills, fires, and property damage events. Tracks incident ID, incident type (OSHA recordable, first aid, near-miss, environmental release, property damage), date/time and location, description, injured party or affected media, immediate cause, root cause classification, OSHA recordability determination, days away from work, regulatory reporting obligation triggered, and notification status. Sourced from Enviance EHS. Distinct from violation_notice (agency-issued) — this is the internal incident record. Supports OSHA 300 log, EPA emergency notification, and DOT accident reporting.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_environmental_monitoring {
  compliance_environmental_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental monitoring measurement record.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record initiated in response to this monitoring result, if applicable.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Environmental monitoring stations measure emissions from specific physical sources (stacks, vents, landfill gas wells). Compliance monitoring programs require linking sample data to the emission sourc']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management facility where the environmental monitoring measurement was taken.']
  laboratory_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Monitoring programs use contracted laboratories and sampling services. Links lab certification verification, chain-of-custody, performance evaluation, and payment processing. Laboratory_name is denorm']
  monitoring_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental monitoring program under which this measurement was collected.']
  monitoring_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_station. Business justification: Environmental monitoring records are collected at fixed monitoring stations (groundwater wells, air monitoring stations, leachate sampling points). This FK replaces the denormalized monitoring_locatio']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental permit (air, water, waste) that mandates this monitoring activity.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Environmental monitoring is conducted to satisfy regulatory requirements (Clean Water Act discharge monitoring, landfill groundwater monitoring under Subtitle D). This FK links monitoring records to t']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the physical sample collected, used for chain-of-custody tracking and laboratory analysis correlation.']
  analysis_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory analysis was completed and results were finalized.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or contextual information about the monitoring event, sample collection, or result interpretation.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the monitoring result triggered the need for corrective action due to exceedance, trend analysis, or other compliance concern.']
  data_qualifier varchar [note: 'Code or flag indicating special conditions affecting the measurement result, such as J (estimated value), U (non-detect), B (blank contamination), or other EPA-standard qualifiers.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this monitoring record was sourced, typically Enviance EHS or SAP EHS module.']
  detection_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum concentration or value that the analytical method can reliably detect for this parameter. Values below this threshold may be reported as non-detect or below detection limit.']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value exceeded the regulatory limit, triggering potential compliance action or reporting requirements.']
  laboratory_certification_number varchar [note: 'The certification or accreditation number of the laboratory that performed the analysis, demonstrating compliance with quality assurance requirements.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric value of the environmental parameter measured during the monitoring event.']
  monitoring_event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the monitoring event, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency at which this monitoring parameter must be measured as specified by the permit or regulatory requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|event_based — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  monitoring_program_type varchar [note: 'Type of environmental monitoring program. Ambient air monitors outdoor air quality, stack emissions track facility discharge, NPDES (National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System) effluent monitors wastewater discharge, groundwater well tracks subsurface water quality, LFG (Landfill Gas) LEL (Lower Explosive Limit) methane monitors landfill gas concentrations, leachate monitors liquid drainage from landfills, soil monitors ground contamination, surface water tracks nearby water bodies, and stormwater monitors runoff quality. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient_air|stack_emissions|npdes_effluent|groundwater_well|lfg_lel_methane|leachate|soil|surface_water|stormwater — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parameter_measured varchar [note: 'The specific environmental parameter or pollutant measured in this monitoring event. Examples include CO2e (Carbon Dioxide Equivalent), methane, BOD (Biochemical Oxygen Demand), pH, VOCs (Volatile Organic Compounds), TSS (Total Suspended Solids), heavy metals, nitrogen oxides, sulfur dioxide, particulate matter, and other regulated substances.']
  quality_assurance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement passed quality assurance and quality control (QA/QC) checks including calibration verification, blank analysis, duplicate samples, and spike recoveries.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring record was first created in the Enviance EHS system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring record was last modified in the Enviance EHS system.']
  regulatory_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum allowable concentration or value for this parameter as specified by the applicable environmental permit, regulation, or standard. Used for compliance comparison.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this monitoring result must be included in regulatory reports submitted to EPA, state agencies, or local authorities (e.g., SWIS, air quality reports, discharge monitoring reports).']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The regulatory reporting period to which this monitoring result applies, typically expressed as a month, quarter, or year (e.g., 2024-Q1, January 2024).']
  sample_collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the environmental sample was collected or the measurement was taken in the field.']
  sampler_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team who collected the environmental sample or performed the field measurement.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'The EPA-approved or industry-standard method used to collect and analyze the environmental sample. Examples include EPA Method 8260 for VOCs, EPA Method 25 for stack emissions, Standard Methods for water quality, and ASTM methods for soil analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed. Examples include ppm (parts per million), ppb (parts per billion), mg/L (milligrams per liter), ug/m3 (micrograms per cubic meter), percent LEL, pH units, tons per day, and other standard environmental measurement units.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of sample collection, relevant for ambient air, surface water, and stormwater monitoring where meteorological factors affect results.']

  Note: 'Environmental monitoring measurement records capturing air emissions, water quality, groundwater, leachate, landfill gas (LFG), and soil monitoring data collected at Waste Management facilities. Tracks monitoring event ID, monitoring program type (ambient air, stack emissions, NPDES effluent, groundwater well, LFG LEL/methane), monitoring location/station, sample date and time, parameter measured (CO2e, methane, BOD, pH, VOCs), measured value, unit of measure, detection limit, regulatory limit for comparison, exceedance flag, sampling method, and laboratory reference. Supports permit condition compliance verification and regulatory reporting (SWIS, state air reports). Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table compliance.monitoring_station {
  monitoring_station_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental monitoring station. Primary key for the monitoring station entity.']
  equipment_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Monitoring equipment is vendor-supplied with maintenance contracts. Tracks equipment warranty, calibration service scheduling, and vendor performance for critical compliance infrastructure.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management facility where this monitoring station is located (landfill, MRF, WTE plant, TSDF, etc.).']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Monitoring stations (groundwater wells, air quality monitors, meteorological stations) are capital assets requiring depreciation, maintenance scheduling, insurance, and lifecycle management. Links com']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental permit that mandates or governs this monitoring station (air permit, water discharge permit, solid waste permit, etc.).']
  access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any physical access restrictions, safety requirements, or special procedures needed to reach or service this monitoring station (e.g., \'Requires confined space entry permit\', \'Active landfill area - escort required\', \'Locked enclosure\').']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring station was activated and began collecting compliance data. May differ from installation date if there was a commissioning period.']
  analyte_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or reference to the suite of parameters, chemicals, or constituents monitored at this station (e.g., \'Appendix I VOCs\', \'Appendix II metals\', \'LFG composition\', \'pH, conductivity, turbidity\'). Defines the scope of environmental monitoring.']
  casing_diameter_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Inner diameter of the well casing in inches. Determines sampling equipment compatibility and flow capacity. Applicable to wells only.']
  casing_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the well casing. Affects chemical compatibility and longevity. Applicable to wells and probes.']
  communication_method varchar [note: 'Method by which monitoring data is transmitted from the station to central systems (cellular modem, satellite uplink, radio telemetry, hardwired connection, or manual collection).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring station record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_logger_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the electronic data logger or telemetry device that collects and transmits measurements from this station. Used for data integration and troubleshooting.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring station was deactivated, abandoned, or decommissioned. Null for active stations. Marks the end of the station\'s operational lifecycle.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the monitoring station was deactivated (e.g., \'Permit requirement ended\', \'Well damaged beyond repair\', \'Facility closure\', \'Replaced by new station\'). Null for active stations.']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the monitoring station above mean sea level in feet. Critical for groundwater flow analysis and air quality modeling.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the primary monitoring equipment at this station. Used for warranty tracking, calibration records, and maintenance history.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type or model of monitoring equipment or instrumentation installed at the station (e.g., \'Continuous Air Monitor Model XYZ\', \'Submersible Pump\', \'Flow Meter\'). Used for maintenance planning and calibration tracking.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required interval in days between physical inspections of the monitoring station, as specified by permit, regulation, or internal policy.']
  installation_contractor varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor or vendor who installed the monitoring station. Used for warranty tracking and quality assurance.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring station was physically installed and became operational. Marks the beginning of the station\'s monitoring history.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration or verification of monitoring equipment at this station. Critical for data quality assurance and regulatory compliance.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection of the monitoring station for structural integrity, accessibility, and proper operation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring station record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the monitoring station in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for spatial analysis, regulatory reporting, and GIS mapping.']
  longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the monitoring station in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for spatial analysis, regulatory reporting, and GIS mapping.']
  monitoring_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary purpose or phase of the monitoring program this station supports (detection monitoring, assessment monitoring, corrective action, etc.). Determines sampling frequency and analyte list.']
  monitoring_station_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the monitoring station. Determines whether the station is included in active monitoring schedules and compliance reporting.']
  monitoring_zone varchar [note: 'Designated monitoring zone or area within the facility (e.g., \'Upgradient\', \'Downgradient\', \'Cell 3 Perimeter\', \'North Boundary\'). Used for spatial grouping and compliance zone tracking.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification of monitoring equipment. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special conditions, historical context, or operational notes about the monitoring station that do not fit in structured fields.']
  regulatory_designation varchar [note: 'Official regulatory identifier or designation assigned by permitting authority (e.g., NPDES outfall number, well ID per state permit, EPA monitoring point ID). Used in compliance reporting and permit tracking.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the person, team, or contractor responsible for maintaining and sampling this monitoring station. Used for accountability and work assignment.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Required or planned frequency for collecting samples or measurements at this station, as specified by permit or monitoring plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|hourly|daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|semiannual|annual|event_based — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  screen_interval_bottom_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth to the bottom of the well screen interval from ground surface in feet. Defines the lower boundary of the monitored zone. Applicable to wells only.']
  screen_interval_top_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth to the top of the well screen interval from ground surface in feet. Defines the upper boundary of the monitored zone. Applicable to wells only.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the monitoring station, often used in regulatory reporting and field operations. May include facility prefix and station type abbreviation.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation for the monitoring station (e.g., \'North Boundary Well MW-12\', \'LFG Probe GP-05\', \'NPDES Outfall 001\').']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring station based on the environmental medium and purpose. Determines the types of measurements collected and regulatory requirements applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: groundwater_well|lfg_extraction_well|lfg_monitoring_probe|air_quality_station|stormwater_outfall|leachate_collection_point|npdes_discharge_point|surface_water_monitoring|soil_gas_probe|meteorological_station — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  well_depth_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total depth of the monitoring well from ground surface to bottom in feet. Applicable only to groundwater wells and LFG extraction wells. Null for other station types.']

  Note: 'Master record for fixed environmental monitoring stations and sampling locations at Waste Management facilities. Covers groundwater monitoring wells, LFG extraction and monitoring probes, air quality monitoring stations, stormwater outfalls, leachate collection points, and NPDES discharge points. Tracks station ID, station type, facility association, geographic coordinates (lat/long), installation date, regulatory designation (e.g., NPDES outfall number, well ID per permit), active status, and associated permit. Provides the spatial and regulatory context for all environmental_monitoring measurement records.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_submission {
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory submission record. Primary key.']
  data_provider_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Submissions require vendor data (Tier II reports use vendor SDS, disposal reports need TSDF certifications). Tracks vendor data quality and timeliness for regulatory deadlines and audit trails.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management facility for which this regulatory submission is filed. Links submission to the operating location.']
  original_submission_regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original regulatory submission that this record amends, if applicable. Links amended submissions to their original filing.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the environmental permit associated with this regulatory submission, if applicable. Links submission to the governing permit.']
  primary_regulatory_prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who prepared the regulatory submission. Tracks accountability for report preparation.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory submissions fulfill specific regulatory reporting requirements (annual emissions inventory, discharge monitoring reports, hazardous waste manifests). This FK replaces the denormalized regul']
  scheduled_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.scheduled_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory submissions fulfill scheduled compliance obligations. This FK links submissions to the scheduled obligation that triggered them, enabling obligation fulfillment tracking and deadline compli']
  tertiary_regulatory_submitted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who executed the submission to the regulatory agency. Tracks final submission action.']
  agency_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or feedback provided by the regulatory agency regarding the submission. Captures agency instructions, deficiency notices, or acceptance notes.']
  agency_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Level of government jurisdiction for the filing agency (federal, state, local, or regional).']
  agency_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency provided a formal response to the submission (acceptance, rejection, or request for additional information).']
  agency_response_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory agency\'s response to the submission. Tracks whether the filing was accepted, rejected, or requires follow-up.']
  amendment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is an amendment to a previously filed report. True if this is an amended submission, false if original.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the original submission was amended. Documents the business justification for the corrected filing.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the authorized official certified the regulatory submission. Establishes legal accountability for the report contents.']
  certification_statement varchar [note: 'Text of the certification statement signed by the authorized representative, attesting to the accuracy and completeness of the submission under penalty of law.']
  certifying_official_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized company official who certified the regulatory submission. Typically a facility manager, environmental manager, or corporate officer.']
  certifying_official_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the authorized official who certified the submission (e.g., Environmental Manager, Facility Manager, Vice President of EHS).']
  compliance_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which compliance is being reported. Facilitates year-over-year compliance tracking and trend analysis.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number issued by the regulatory agency upon receipt of the submission. Serves as proof of filing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory submission record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  document_file_path varchar [note: 'File path or storage location of the submitted regulatory report document. Enables retrieval of the original submission for audit and reference.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the submitted regulatory report (PDF, XML, Excel, etc.). Identifies the technical format used for submission.']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the submission must be filed. Used to track compliance with filing timelines.']
  facility_epa_number varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned identification number for the facility covered by this submission. Required for RCRA and other federal reporting.']
  filing_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or regulatory body to which the report is submitted (e.g., EPA, state environmental quality department, local enforcement agency).']
  late_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission was filed after the regulatory deadline. True if late, false if on time. Used to track compliance with filing timelines.']
  late_submission_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the submission was filed late, if applicable. Documents the circumstances that caused the delay.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the regulatory submission. Captures context, special circumstances, or internal coordination details.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'The regulatory program under which this submission is required. Identifies the governing environmental or safety framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RCRA|SWIS|NPDES|Title V|CERCLA|EPCRA|OSHA|GHG Reporting — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory report being submitted. Identifies the specific regulatory program and reporting requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RCRA Biennial Report|SWIS Report|NPDES DMR|Title V Certification|CERCLA Tier II|EPCRA Tier II|OSHA 300A Summary|GHG Emissions Report|State Environmental Report|Air Quality Report|Water Discharge Report|Hazardous Waste Manifest|LFG Monitoring Report|Leachate Analysis Report — promote to reference product]']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this regulatory submission. Defines the close of the compliance reporting window.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this regulatory submission. Defines the beginning of the compliance reporting window.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory report was submitted to the filing agency. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the report was submitted to the regulatory agency (electronic portal, paper mail, email, etc.).']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the regulatory submission, typically assigned by the submitting organization or regulatory agency. Used for external reference and tracking.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory submission. Tracks the submission through preparation, filing, and agency review stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Accepted|Rejected|Under Review|Amended|Withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory submission record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']

  Note: 'Records of formal regulatory reports and submissions filed with government agencies by Waste Management. Covers annual RCRA biennial reports, SWIS solid waste reports, NPDES discharge monitoring reports (DMR), Title V annual compliance certifications, CERCLA/EPCRA Tier II chemical inventory reports, OSHA 300A annual summaries, GHG emissions reports (EPA Subpart HH/TT), and state-specific environmental reports. Tracks submission ID, report type, regulatory program, filing agency, reporting period, submission date, submission method (electronic/paper), confirmation number, and submission status. Sourced from Enviance EHS and SAP EHS. Provides the audit trail for all external regulatory filings.'
}

Table compliance.training_certification {
  training_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training certification record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who holds this certification. Links to workforce domain employee master data.']
  training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_requirement. Business justification: Training certifications fulfill specific training requirements (HAZWOPER, CDL, OSHA 40-hour, DOT HazMat). This FK replaces the denormalized certification_type STRING field with a structured relationsh']
  training_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Certifications are vendor-provided (OSHA HAZWOPER training, equipment certifications). Links vendor qualification, training cost tracking, and regulatory compliance verification. Training_provider is ']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'The file path or URL to the scanned copy of the certification document stored in the document management system. Used for audit and verification purposes.']
  cdl_class varchar [note: 'For CDL certifications, the license class indicating the type and weight of vehicle the driver is authorized to operate. Class A: combination vehicles over 26,001 lbs; Class B: heavy straight vehicles over 26,001 lbs; Class C: vehicles designed to transport 16+ passengers or hazmat.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate or license number issued by the certifying body. Examples include CDL number, HAZWOPER certificate number, OSHA card number.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification. Active indicates valid and in good standing; Expired indicates past expiration date; Suspended or Revoked indicates regulatory action; Pending Renewal indicates renewal process initiated.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category of training or certification. Includes Commercial Driver License (CDL), Hazardous Waste Operations and Emergency Response (HAZWOPER) 40-hour initial and 8-hour refresher, Department of Transportation (DOT) hazmat transport, Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) 10-hour and 30-hour safety, confined space entry, forklift operator, Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Risk Management Plan (RMP), and ISO 14001/45001 internal auditor certifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDL|HAZWOPER_40HR|HAZWOPER_8HR_REFRESHER|DOT_HAZMAT|OSHA_10HR|OSHA_30HR|CONFINED_SPACE|FORKLIFT_OPERATOR|EPA_RMP|ISO_14001_AUDITOR|ISO_45001_AUDITOR|FIRST_AID_CPR — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee successfully completed the training or certification program.']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is a mandatory regulatory or company compliance requirement for the employees role. True for required certifications (e.g., CDL for drivers, HAZWOPER for hazmat handlers); False for optional professional development.']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred for this training or certification, including tuition, materials, travel, and exam fees. Used for training budget tracking and cost allocation.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the training cost. Waste Management primarily operates in USD.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  endorsement_codes varchar [note: 'For CDL certifications, the endorsement codes indicating specialized vehicle or cargo authorization. Examples: H (Hazardous Materials), N (Tank Vehicle), T (Double/Triple Trailers), P (Passenger). Pipe-separated if multiple endorsements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and requires renewal. Null for certifications with no expiration (e.g., some OSHA 30-hour cards).']
  instructor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the instructor or trainer who conducted the training session, if applicable.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was officially issued by the certifying body. May differ from completion date due to processing time.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'The organization or regulatory authority that issued the certification. Examples include state Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV), OSHA Training Institute Education Centers, EPA-approved training providers, ISO registrars.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'For state-issued certifications (e.g., CDL), the two-letter state code of the issuing jurisdiction. Examples: TX, CA, IL, NY.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special conditions, or comments related to the certification. May include details on accommodations, retake history, or special circumstances.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employee passed, failed, is currently in progress, or did not complete the certification assessment.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory body or standard-setting organization governing this certification. Examples: Department of Transportation (DOT), Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), State Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV), International Organization for Standardization (ISO), or Internal company policy.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The number of months between required renewals. Examples: 12 months for HAZWOPER refresher, 24 months for First Aid/CPR, 60 months for CDL renewal. Null if no renewal required.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requires periodic renewal. True for CDL, HAZWOPER refresher, DOT hazmat; False for one-time certifications.']
  restriction_codes varchar [note: 'For CDL certifications, the restriction codes limiting driving privileges. Examples: L (No Air Brake Equipped CMV), Z (No Full Air Brake Equipped CMV), E (No Manual Transmission). Pipe-separated if multiple restrictions.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The numerical score or percentage achieved on the certification exam or assessment, if applicable. Null for certifications without scored assessments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this certification record originated. Primary sources are Enviance EHS for compliance training and Workday HCM for general employee training records.']
  training_delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the training was delivered. In-person classroom, online e-learning, blended (combination), or on-the-job practical training.']
  training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The total number of instructional hours completed for this certification. Examples: 40 hours for HAZWOPER initial, 8 hours for HAZWOPER refresher, 10 or 30 hours for OSHA safety training.']
  training_location varchar [note: 'The physical location or facility where the training was conducted. May be a Waste Management facility, training center, or external provider location. Null for online training.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was last verified by Waste Management HR or EHS personnel. Used for audit trail and compliance validation.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the authenticity and validity of the certification. Document review of physical certificate, online registry check with issuing body, third-party verification service, or employee self-attestation.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee ID of the Waste Management personnel who verified the certification. Typically an EHS coordinator or HR specialist.']

  Note: 'Records of compliance-required training completions and professional certifications held by Waste Management employees. Covers CDL (Commercial Driver License) endorsements, HAZWOPER 40-hour and 8-hour refresher certifications, DOT hazmat transport training, OSHA 10/30-hour safety training, confined space entry certification, forklift operator certification, EPA RMP training, and ISO 14001/45001 internal auditor certification. Tracks certification ID, employee reference, certification type, issuing body, completion date, expiration date, training hours, pass/fail status, and renewal requirement. Distinct from workforce domain employee records — this is the compliance-specific training obligation and certification record. Sourced from Enviance EHS and Workday HCM.'
}

Table compliance.training_requirement {
  training_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training requirement record. Primary key.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Training requirements implement regulatory requirements (OSHA 1910.120 HAZWOPER training, DOT 49 CFR 172.704 HazMat training). This FK replaces the denormalized regulatory_citation STRING field with a']
  superseded_by_requirement_training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the training_requirement_id that replaces this requirement when regulatory standards change or training programs are updated. Null if not superseded.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that must accredit the training provider (e.g., OSHA, EPA, State DMV, IACET, ANSI). Null if no specific accreditation required.']
  applicable_facility_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of facility types where this training is mandatory (e.g., Landfill, MRF, TSDF, Transfer Station, WTE Facility, Fleet Maintenance Yard, Hazardous Waste Storage).']
  applicable_job_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of job roles/positions to which this training requirement applies (e.g., CDL Driver, Hazmat Handler, MRF Sorter, Landfill Operator, Maintenance Technician, Environmental Compliance Manager).']
  applicable_regulatory_programs varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory programs or permits that trigger this training requirement (e.g., RCRA Part B Permit, Air Quality Permit, NPDES Permit, DOT Hazmat Transportation, OSHA PSM).']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why this training is required, including regulatory mandate, risk mitigation, operational necessity, or industry best practice rationale.']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion must result in a formal certification or credential (True) or if attendance/completion record is sufficient (False).']
  competency_assessment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal competency assessment (exam, practical demonstration, skills test) is required in addition to training attendance.']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per employee to complete this training requirement, including tuition, materials, travel, and lost productivity. Used for compliance budget planning.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who created this training requirement record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training requirement record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_retention_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years training records must be retained to satisfy regulatory and audit requirements (e.g., OSHA requires 3 years for most training, EPA RCRA requires duration of employment plus 3 years).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this training requirement becomes mandatory and enforceable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this training requirement is no longer mandatory. Null for ongoing requirements.']
  frequency_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required training completions for recurring requirements. Null for one-time or event-driven training.']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'Recurrence pattern for the training requirement (e.g., initial upon hire, annual refresher, triennial recertification, event-driven upon role change). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial|annual|biennial|triennial|quinquennial|one_time|event_driven — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after expiration during which the employee may continue working while completing recertification. Null if no grace period allowed.']
  iso_14001_competence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training requirement is part of the ISO 14001 Environmental Management System competence framework.']
  iso_45001_competence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training requirement is part of the ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System competence framework.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Languages in which training must be available to ensure comprehension by all applicable employees (e.g., English, Spanish). OSHA requires training in language understood by employee.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this training requirement record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training requirement record was last updated.']
  minimum_passing_score_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required on competency assessment to satisfy the training requirement. Null if no assessment or no minimum score defined.']
  minimum_training_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum contact hours required to satisfy the training obligation (e.g., 40 hours for HAZWOPER initial, 8 hours for annual refresher, 10 hours for OSHA General Industry).']
  prerequisite_training_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of training_type_code values that must be completed before this training can be taken (e.g., HAZWOPER_40HR must precede HAZWOPER_SUPERVISOR).']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Legal or regulatory reference mandating this training (e.g., 29 CFR 1910.120 for HAZWOPER, 49 CFR 172.704 for DOT Hazmat, 40 CFR 265.16 for RCRA personnel training, OSHA 1910.178 for forklift operation).']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the training requirement. Active requirements are enforced in compliance gap analysis.']
  third_party_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification must be issued by an external accredited body (True) or can be issued internally (False). Examples: CDL requires state DMV, HAZWOPER may require accredited training provider.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the training requirement domain.']
  training_delivery_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of acceptable delivery formats (e.g., classroom, online, blended, hands-on, on-the-job, simulator). Some regulatory training may restrict delivery methods.']
  training_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the training program (e.g., 40-Hour HAZWOPER Initial Training, Commercial Driver License Class A Certification, OSHA 10-Hour General Industry Safety).']
  training_provider_type varchar [note: 'Classification of acceptable training provider sources for this requirement.']
  training_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific training program (e.g., HAZWOPER_40HR, CDL_CLASS_A, OSHA_10HR, DOT_HAZMAT, RCRA_HANDLER, CONFINED_SPACE, FORKLIFT_CERT).']

  Note: 'Reference catalog defining mandatory compliance training obligations applicable to specific job roles, facility types, and regulatory programs at Waste Management. Maps training type to the regulatory citation requiring it (e.g., HAZWOPER required by 29 CFR 1910.120 for hazmat workers), required frequency (initial, annual, triennial), minimum training hours, applicable employee roles (CDL driver, hazmat handler, MRF sorter, landfill operator), and whether a third-party certification is required. Used to identify training gaps by comparing requirements against training_certification records. Supports ISO 45001 competence management.'
}

Table compliance.air_emission_event {
  air_emission_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the air emission event record. Primary key for tracking individual emission episodes at Waste Management facilities.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Emission events may be discovered during regulatory inspections or internal compliance audits. This FK links events to the inspection that identified them, supporting root cause analysis and inspectio']
  detected_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracking which employee detected emission events supports training effectiveness analysis, incident response performance evaluation, and recognition programs for proactive environmental monitoring in ']
  emergency_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.emergency_action_plan. Business justification: Air emission events trigger emergency response procedures. This link documents which emergency plan was activated and ensures regulatory reporting includes emergency response actions. Required for Tit']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific emission unit or source at the facility (e.g., flare, stack, combustion unit, landfill gas collection system, fugitive source). Corresponds to permit-defined emission points.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management facility where the air emission event occurred. Links to landfill, waste-to-energy plant, transfer station, or materials recovery facility.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Emission units (engines, flares, stacks, vents) are fixed assets. Linking emission events to asset records enables maintenance trigger analysis, asset performance tracking, regulatory reporting by equ']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Air emission events are governed by air quality permits. Each emission event occurs under the authority of a specific permit that defines allowable emission limits. The permit_id FK enables tracking w']
  regulatory_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_corrective_action. Business justification: Emission events that exceed permit limits or regulatory thresholds trigger corrective actions. This FK links emission events to the corrective actions taken to address the deviation, enabling tracking']
  responsible_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Emission events result from vendor equipment failure or service issues (control equipment malfunction, contractor error). Essential for vendor accountability, warranty claims, and performance evaluati']
  agency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency representative or inspector who was contacted regarding the emission event.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency notified (e.g., U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, State Department of Environmental Quality, Local Air Quality Management District).']
  agency_notified_date date [note: 'Actual date when the regulatory agency (EPA, state air quality agency, or local enforcement agency) was notified of the emission event. Used to verify compliance with notification deadlines.']
  air_emission_event_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the emission event record in the EHS management system. Open indicates active event; under investigation means root cause analysis in progress; corrective action pending means actions identified but not completed; reported means regulatory notification submitted; closed means all actions complete and event resolved.']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the root cause of the emission event. Includes equipment failure details, operational upset conditions, weather events, human error, or other contributing factors. Required for regulatory deviation reports.']
  community_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the emission event resulted in off-site impacts, odor complaints, or community concerns. True if complaints were received or visible plume extended beyond facility boundary.']
  complaint_count varchar [note: 'Number of community complaints received related to this emission event. Includes odor complaints, visible emission concerns, and health-related inquiries.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of immediate and long-term corrective actions implemented to stop the emission event and prevent recurrence. Includes equipment repairs, operational adjustments, enhanced monitoring, and preventive maintenance measures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this emission event record was first created in the Enviance EHS system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance tracking.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the emission event was first detected or identified. May differ from event start time if detection was delayed.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the emission event constitutes a deviation from permit requirements under Clean Air Act Title V. True if permit limits were exceeded or monitoring/recordkeeping requirements were not met.']
  emission_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated emission quantity. Typically pounds or tons for mass-based pollutants, cubic feet or cubic meters for volumetric measurements.']
  enforcement_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the emission event resulted in regulatory enforcement action such as Notice of Violation (NOV), penalty assessment, consent order, or compliance schedule. True if formal enforcement was initiated.']
  estimated_emission_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated quantity of pollutant released during the event. Calculated using emission factors, continuous emission monitoring system (CEMS) data, or engineering estimates. Reported in units specified by emission_quantity_unit.']
  event_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the emission event in hours. Calculated from start and end timestamps. Used for regulatory reporting and compliance assessment.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the air emission event concluded or was brought under control. Used to calculate total event duration and emission quantity.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the air emission event began. Critical for calculating duration and determining regulatory notification deadlines.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the air emission event. Excess emission indicates permit limit exceedance; SSM (Startup Shutdown Malfunction) covers operational transitions; fugitive emission refers to uncontrolled releases; emergency release covers unplanned incidents; routine release covers normal operations; opacity exceedance indicates visible emission violations.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the person who last modified this emission event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this emission event record was last updated in the Enviance EHS system. Tracks changes to event details, corrective actions, or status updates.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect and quantify the emission event. CEMS (Continuous Emission Monitoring System) for real-time data; parametric monitoring for surrogate parameters; visual observation for opacity; calculation using emission factors; engineering estimate for unmonitored sources.']
  notification_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which regulatory notification or deviation report must be submitted to the appropriate agency. Calculated based on event type and applicable regulations (typically 24 hours for emergencies, 30 days for deviations).']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify the regulatory agency of the emission event (phone call, email, written report, electronic submission portal, in-person notification).']
  notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the emission event triggered a regulatory notification requirement to EPA, state air quality agency, or local enforcement agency. True if immediate notification (within 24 hours) or deviation reporting is required.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by regulatory agency for the emission event violation, if applicable. Reported in U.S. dollars.']
  permit_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the permit limit (e.g., pounds per hour, tons per year, parts per million, percent opacity, grains per dry standard cubic foot).']
  permit_limit_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The regulatory permit limit for the pollutant and emission unit that was exceeded or approached during this event. Used to determine if event constitutes a deviation or violation.']
  pollutant_code varchar [note: 'EPA-standardized code for the primary air pollutant involved in the emission event (e.g., PM10, PM2.5, SO2, NOx, VOC, CO, CO2e, CH4, H2S). May reference multiple pollutants for complex events.']
  pollutant_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the air pollutant released during the event (e.g., Particulate Matter 10 micrometers, Sulfur Dioxide, Nitrogen Oxides, Volatile Organic Compounds, Carbon Monoxide, Methane, Hydrogen Sulfide).']
  preventive_action_planned varchar [note: 'Description of planned preventive measures to avoid similar emission events in the future. May include capital improvements, enhanced training, revised procedures, or increased inspection frequency.']
  report_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or tracking identifier assigned by the regulatory agency or internal EHS system for the deviation report or notification associated with this emission event.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Internal classification of emission event severity based on magnitude of exceedance, duration, community impact, and regulatory significance. Minor events have minimal impact; critical events pose significant environmental or health risks.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of the emission event. Relevant for fugitive dust events, odor complaints, and dispersion modeling. Includes wind speed, wind direction, temperature, precipitation.']
  wind_direction varchar [note: 'Wind direction at the time of the emission event (e.g., N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW, or degrees from north). Used to assess potential off-site impacts and community exposure.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind speed in miles per hour at the time of the emission event. Critical for fugitive emission events and dispersion analysis.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the person who created this emission event record in the EHS system.']

  Note: 'Records of air emission events including scheduled and unscheduled emission releases, excess emission episodes, startup/shutdown/malfunction (SSM) events, and fugitive dust events at Waste Management facilities. Tracks event ID, facility and emission unit, event type (excess emission, SSM, fugitive), pollutant(s) affected, event start and end datetime, estimated emission quantity, cause description, corrective actions taken, regulatory notification requirement triggered, and agency notification date. Supports Clean Air Act Title V deviation reporting and state air quality agency notification obligations. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

  spill_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spill or release incident record.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Spills may be discovered during compliance inspections or internal audits. This FK links spill records to the inspection that identified them, supporting inspection follow-up and corrective action tra']
  emergency_action_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.emergency_action_plan. Business justification: Spill releases trigger emergency action plans. Required for SPCC and RCRA contingency plan documentation. This link documents which emergency plan was activated, ensures response actions followed appr']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility where the spill occurred, if applicable.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broader environmental, health, and safety incident record if the spill is part of a larger incident event.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Spills and environmental releases may violate permit conditions (e.g., stormwater discharge permit, hazardous waste storage permit). This FK links spill records to the permit that was violated, enabli']
  regulatory_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_corrective_action. Business justification: Spills trigger corrective actions to address root causes and prevent recurrence. This FK links spill records to the corrective actions taken, enabling CAPA tracking and closure verification for enviro']
  responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Spill incidents require employee accountability for response actions. Linking enables root cause analysis tied to personnel, training gap identification, performance management, and regulatory reporti']
  responsible_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Spills involve vendor materials or services (fuel delivery overfill, contractor chemical handling error). Links vendor liability determination, insurance coordination, and performance tracking. Cleanu']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Reportable spills that exceed regulatory thresholds may result in violation notices from EPA or state agencies. This FK links spill records to the resulting violation notice, enabling enforcement acti']
  affected_media varchar [note: 'Environmental media impacted by the spill (e.g., soil, surface water, groundwater, air, pavement, multiple media).']
  agency_notified varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or agencies notified of the spill (e.g., National Response Center, state environmental agency, local emergency response).']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS registry number uniquely identifying the chemical substance released, if applicable.']
  cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause of the spill (e.g., equipment failure, human error, natural event, third-party action, unknown).']
  cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause and contributing factors that led to the spill incident.']
  cleanup_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which all cleanup and remediation activities were completed and the site was restored.']
  cleanup_start_date date [note: 'Date on which cleanup and remediation activities commenced.']
  containment_actions varchar [note: 'Description of immediate containment and response actions taken to control the spill (e.g., deployment of absorbent materials, berms, booms, or other containment measures).']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Description of corrective and preventive actions implemented to prevent recurrence of similar spill incidents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spill release record was first created in the system.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'The date on which the spill or release was first discovered by personnel.']
  disposal_facility_epa_number varchar [note: 'EPA identification number of the disposal facility where contaminated waste was sent.']
  disposal_facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF) where contaminated waste was sent for final disposal.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost of the spill incident including cleanup, disposal, regulatory fines, and remediation expenses.']
  estimated_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated volume or mass of material released during the spill incident.']
  facility_epa_number varchar [note: 'EPA-issued identification number for the facility where the spill occurred, used for regulatory tracking and reporting.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the spill location in decimal degrees.']
  location_address varchar [note: 'Street address or specific location description where the spill occurred.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City where the spill occurred.']
  location_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the spill occurred.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location where the spill occurred (e.g., facility, roadway, transfer station, landfill, Materials Recovery Facility, customer site).']
  location_zip_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code of the location where the spill occurred.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the spill location in decimal degrees.']
  material_released varchar [note: 'Detailed description or name of the specific material or substance that was released during the spill (e.g., diesel fuel, hydraulic fluid, leachate, hazardous waste).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or comments related to the spill incident and response activities.']
  notification_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or reference number provided by the regulatory agency upon notification of the spill.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which regulatory agencies were notified of the spill incident.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the estimated quantity of material released (e.g., gallons, liters, pounds, kilograms, tons, cubic yards).']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spill is reportable to regulatory agencies under federal, state, or local requirements.']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material released during the spill incident (e.g., leachate, fuel, hazardous material, stormwater, chemical, oil).']
  reportable_quantity_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spill quantity exceeded the CERCLA Reportable Quantity threshold requiring federal notification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which the spill release record originated (e.g., Enviance EHS).']
  spill_date date [note: 'The date on which the spill or release incident occurred.']
  spill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spill incident (e.g., reported, contained, cleanup in progress, cleanup complete, closed).']
  spill_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the spill or release incident was discovered or occurred.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials transported, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spill release record was last modified or updated.']
  waste_disposed_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of contaminated material or waste generated and disposed of as a result of the cleanup activities.']
  waste_disposed_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity of waste disposed (e.g., gallons, liters, pounds, kilograms, tons, cubic yards).']

  Note: 'Records of chemical, fuel, leachate, and hazardous material spills and environmental releases at Waste Management facilities and during transport operations. Tracks spill ID, release type (leachate, fuel, hazmat, stormwater), location (facility, roadway, transfer station), date and time, material released, estimated quantity, affected environmental media (soil, surface water, groundwater), immediate containment actions, regulatory reportability determination (CERCLA RQ threshold, state spill reporting), agency notification date and confirmation, and cleanup completion status. Supports CERCLA Section 103, EPCRA Section 304, and Clean Water Act spill reporting. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table compliance.scheduled_obligation {
  scheduled_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the scheduled compliance obligation. Primary key for the scheduled obligation entity.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the facility responsible for fulfilling this scheduled obligation. Links to the facility master data.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the environmental permit associated with this obligation. Applicable when the obligation is driven by a specific permit condition.']
  reduction_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.reduction_target. Business justification: Scheduled compliance obligations (permit-mandated emission reduction deadlines, phase-out schedules) may be driven by or aligned with corporate reduction targets. Tracks regulatory milestones that sup']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the regulatory requirement that drives this scheduled obligation. Links to the master regulatory requirement catalog.']
  responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory obligations must be assigned to employees for accountability. Linking enables workload planning, automated reminders, performance tracking, and audit documentation showing who is responsibl']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours spent completing this obligation. Used for performance tracking and future estimation refinement.']
  agency_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the regulatory agency contact person. Organizational contact information classified as confidential.']
  agency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency contact person for this obligation. Used for questions or clarifications regarding compliance requirements.']
  agency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the regulatory agency contact person. Organizational contact information classified as confidential.']
  completed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who completed this obligation. May differ from the originally assigned responsible party.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when this obligation was completed. Null if the obligation is still pending or in progress.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the obligation was marked as completed in the system. Provides audit trail for compliance verification.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number received from the regulatory agency upon submission. Provides proof of submission for audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scheduled obligation record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for compliance tracking.']
  days_until_due varchar [note: 'Calculated number of days remaining until the obligation due date. Negative values indicate overdue obligations. Used for prioritization and escalation.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of the documentation, reports, or records that must be prepared or submitted to fulfill this obligation. May include forms, certifications, monitoring data, or supporting evidence.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which this scheduled obligation must be completed to maintain regulatory compliance. Critical for compliance calendar management and deadline tracking.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date when this obligation was escalated to management or senior leadership. Null if no escalation has occurred.']
