  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation has been escalated to management due to approaching deadline, complexity, or risk. True if escalated, false otherwise.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete this obligation. Used for resource planning and workload management.']
  next_occurrence_date date [note: 'The due date of the next scheduled occurrence of this recurring obligation. Automatically calculated based on recurrence frequency and pattern.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this scheduled obligation. Used for context and collaboration.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scheduled compliance obligation, including specific requirements, deliverables, and any special instructions for completion.']
  obligation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the scheduled obligation, formatted as OBL- followed by 8 digits. Used for tracking and reference in compliance communications.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheduled obligation. Tracks whether the obligation is awaiting action, being worked on, completed, or past due.']
  obligation_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the scheduled compliance obligation. Provides a human-readable summary of the task.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduled compliance obligation. Defines the nature of the regulatory task that must be completed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: permit_renewal|monitoring_report|regulatory_filing|compliance_certification|inspection|sampling|training_certification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this obligation based on regulatory risk, penalty severity, and business impact. Critical obligations require immediate attention.']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this obligation recurs. Defines the schedule pattern for recurring compliance tasks such as quarterly monitoring reports or annual certifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|biennial|as_needed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Detailed recurrence pattern specification for complex schedules. May include specific days of month, week numbers, or custom scheduling rules.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'The government agency or regulatory body to which this obligation must be reported or submitted. Examples include EPA, state environmental departments, or local enforcement agencies.']
  regulatory_driver varchar [note: 'The specific regulation, permit condition, or regulatory citation that mandates this obligation. May reference EPA regulations, RCRA requirements, state environmental codes, or specific permit conditions.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The department or functional area within the facility responsible for completing this obligation. Examples include Environmental Compliance, Operations, Safety, or Landfill Management.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the regulatory and business risk associated with non-compliance. Based on potential penalties, environmental impact, and reputational consequences.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned date when work on this obligation should begin. Used for proactive task planning and resource allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this scheduled obligation record originated. Typically Enviance EHS Compliance Calendar Module or SAP S/4HANA EHS.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date when compliance documentation was submitted to the regulatory agency. Null if submission has not yet occurred.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The method by which compliance documentation must be submitted to the regulatory agency. Examples include electronic portal, certified mail, or in-person delivery.']
  submission_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires formal submission of documentation or reports to a regulatory agency. True if submission is required, false otherwise.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized training or certification is required to complete this obligation. True if training is required, false otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scheduled obligation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']

  Note: 'Scheduled compliance obligations and recurring regulatory tasks that Waste Management must fulfill on defined frequencies. Covers permit renewal deadlines, quarterly monitoring report submissions, annual regulatory filings, semi-annual compliance certifications, monthly LFG monitoring rounds, and periodic waste characterization sampling. Tracks obligation ID, obligation type, regulatory driver (permit condition or regulation citation), responsible facility and department, due date, recurrence frequency, assigned responsible party, completion status, and days until due. Acts as the compliance calendar and task management backbone. Sourced from Enviance EHS compliance calendar module.'
}

Table compliance.audit_program {
  audit_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit program record. Primary key.']
  audit_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate or regional EHS manager who owns and oversees this audit program. References workforce or employee master data.']
  primary_lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  applicable_facilities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of facilities covered by this audit program (e.g., \'All MRF facilities\', \'Landfill sites in Region 3\', \'TSDF facilities\').']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the executive or management authority who approved this audit program (e.g., \'VP of EHS\', \'Chief Compliance Officer\').']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit program was formally approved by management.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which audits under this program are conducted (Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Biennial, Ad-Hoc).']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the audit methodology and approach used in this program (e.g., \'Process-based audit\', \'Risk-based audit\', \'Compliance checklist audit\').']
  audit_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or identifiers of additional audit team members assigned to this program.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit program by its primary focus and regulatory or standards framework (ISO 14001 internal audit, RCRA compliance audit, OSHA VPP audit, operational compliance review, ISO 45001 internal audit, integrated EHS audit).']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget amount allocated for this audit program, covering auditor time, travel, training, and related expenses.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget allocated amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external certification or accreditation body associated with this audit program, if applicable (e.g., \'BSI\', \'DNV\', \'UL\').']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions are mandatory for findings identified under this audit program (True/False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit program record was first created in the source system.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of roles, departments, or individuals who receive audit reports and findings from this program.']
  documentation_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of documentation and records required to be reviewed or produced as part of audits under this program.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit program is scheduled to end or be reviewed for renewal. Nullable for ongoing programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit program becomes effective and audits under this program may commence.']
  external_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit program supports external certification or accreditation (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001 certification) (True/False).']
  follow_up_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether follow-up audits are required to verify closure of corrective actions (True/False).']
  iso_clauses varchar [note: 'Specific ISO standard clauses addressed by this audit program (e.g., \'ISO 14001:2015 Clause 9.2\', \'ISO 45001:2018 Clause 9.2\').']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent management review of the audit program.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next management review of the audit program to assess effectiveness and make adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context related to the audit program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the audit program, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows organizational coding standards.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit program (e.g., \'ISO 14001 Internal Audit Program FY2024\', \'RCRA Compliance Audit Program\').']
  program_objectives varchar [note: 'Stated objectives and goals of the audit program, including compliance verification, process improvement, risk mitigation, and certification maintenance.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit program (Active, Inactive, Suspended, Under Review, Archived).']
  regulatory_standards varchar [note: 'List of applicable regulatory standards, frameworks, and requirements covered by this audit program (e.g., \'RCRA 40 CFR Part 264\', \'ISO 14001:2015\', \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910\', \'Clean Air Act Title V\').']
  reporting_template varchar [note: 'Name or reference to the standard reporting template or format used for audit reports generated under this program.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level associated with the areas covered by this audit program, used for prioritization and resource allocation (Critical, High, Medium, Low).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit program scope, including applicable facilities, operations, processes, and regulatory or standards requirements covered.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this audit program record originated (e.g., \'Enviance EHS\', \'SAP EHS\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit program record was last updated in the source system.']

  Note: 'Master records for internal EHS audit programs conducted by Waste Management\'s corporate and regional EHS teams. Defines the audit program name, audit type (ISO 14001 internal audit, RCRA compliance audit, OSHA VPP audit, operational compliance review), audit scope and applicable facilities, audit frequency, lead auditor assignment, applicable regulatory standards and ISO clauses, and program status. Parent entity for individual inspection records generated under the program. Supports ISO 14001 Clause 9.2 internal audit requirements and corporate EHS governance. Sourced from Enviance EHS.'
}

Table compliance.chemical_inventory {
  chemical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical inventory record. Primary key for the chemical inventory product.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.emission_source. Business justification: Chemicals stored/used at facilities can be emission sources (fugitive VOC emissions, refrigerant leaks). Tracking which chemicals are associated with which emission sources supports air quality compli']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management facility where the chemical is stored or used. Links to the facility master data.']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Chemicals in inventory represent hazards that must be in the hazard register. This link ensures all chemical hazards (flammability, toxicity, reactivity) are captured for risk management. Required for']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Chemical storage and usage at waste management facilities is governed by permits (RCRA hazardous waste permits, air permits for VOC storage, etc.). This FK links inventory records to the permit that a']
  supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Chemicals sourced from vendors with regulatory certifications. Links vendor qualification for hazmat supply, SDS management, and regulatory reporting. Manufacturer_name already listed for removal unde']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Chemical inventory records at facilities should link to approved waste profiles for proper hazmat classification and handling procedures. This ensures consistency between compliance tracking and hazma']
  average_daily_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Average amount of the chemical present at the facility on a daily basis during the reporting year. Used for EPCRA Tier II reporting.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numerical identifier assigned by the Chemical Abstracts Service to every chemical substance. Used for precise identification in regulatory reporting.']
  cercla_reportable_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'CERCLA reportable quantity in pounds. Releases of this amount or more within a 24-hour period must be reported to the National Response Center. Null if no RQ established.']
  confidential_location_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specific storage location is withheld from public disclosure in Tier II reporting due to trade secret or security concerns. Requires substantiation to state authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this chemical inventory record was first created in the Enviance EHS system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  ehs_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is listed as an Extremely Hazardous Substance under EPCRA Section 302. EHS chemicals trigger emergency planning and release reporting requirements.']
  ehs_threshold_planning_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'EPCRA Section 302 threshold planning quantity for the EHS chemical in pounds. Facilities storing amounts at or above the TPQ must notify the SERC and LEPC. Null if not an EHS.']
  epcra_section_313_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is a listed toxic chemical under EPCRA Section 313 requiring annual TRI reporting if threshold quantities are manufactured, processed, or otherwise used.']
  ghs_environmental_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS environmental hazard classification (e.g., hazardous to the aquatic environment acute category 1, chronic category 2). Derived from Section 2 of the SDS.']
  ghs_health_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS health hazard classification (e.g., acute toxicity category 3, carcinogenicity category 1A, skin corrosion category 1B). Derived from Section 2 of the SDS.']
  ghs_physical_hazard_class varchar [note: 'GHS physical hazard classification (e.g., flammable liquid category 2, oxidizing solid category 1, explosive division 1.1). Derived from Section 2 of the SDS.']
  hmis_rating varchar [note: 'HMIS rating in format Health-Flammability-Physical-PPE (e.g., 2-3-1-B). Used for workplace labeling and employee right-to-know programs.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the chemical in facility inventory (active, depleted, discontinued, pending removal). Used to maintain accurate reporting and trigger re-ordering or disposal actions.']
  last_inventory_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory verification for this chemical. EPCRA Tier II requires annual inventory updates; more frequent verification may be required for PSM-covered chemicals.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the chemical manufacturer or supplier as listed on the SDS and product label. Used for product traceability and supplier management.']
  maximum_quantity_stored decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum amount of the chemical present at the facility at any time during the reporting year. Used for EPCRA Tier II threshold determination.']
  nfpa_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 diamond rating in format Health-Flammability-Instability-Special (e.g., 2-3-1-OX). Used for emergency response and facility placarding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the chemical inventory record, including special handling instructions, regulatory exemptions, or facility-specific considerations.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical form of the chemical at ambient conditions (solid, liquid, gas, sludge, or compressed gas). Determines storage and handling requirements.']
  product_identifier varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s product code, catalog number, or SKU for the specific chemical product. Used for procurement and inventory tracking.']
  psm_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the chemical is present in quantities that trigger OSHA Process Safety Management requirements (10,000 lbs threshold for most flammables, specific thresholds for listed chemicals).']
  psm_threshold_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'OSHA PSM threshold quantity for the chemical in pounds. Facilities with amounts at or above this threshold must implement PSM program elements. Null if not a PSM-covered chemical.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for maximum and average daily quantities (pounds, kilograms, gallons, liters, cubic feet, cubic meters). Must be consistent with EPCRA reporting requirements.']
  rcra_generator_status_impact varchar [note: 'Impact of this chemical on the facility\'s RCRA hazardous waste generator status classification (none, conditionally exempt, small quantity, large quantity). Based on monthly generation quantities.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this inventory record applies. EPCRA Tier II reports are due by March 1 for the previous calendar year.']
  sds_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the electronic copy of the Safety Data Sheet in the Enviance EHS document management system. SDS must be readily accessible to employees.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision of the Safety Data Sheet for this chemical. SDS must be updated within three months of new hazard information becoming available.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this chemical inventory record originated (typically Enviance EHS chemical management module). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions required for the chemical (ambient, refrigerated, heated, pressurized, inert atmosphere). Derived from Section 7 of the SDS.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Specific location within the facility where the chemical is stored (e.g., building number, room, tank ID, outdoor storage area). Used for emergency response planning.']
  storage_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or storage system used for the chemical (tank, drum, tote, cylinder, bag, bulk storage, other). Determines inspection and containment requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tank|drum|tote|cylinder|bag|bulk_storage|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tier_ii_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical must be included in the annual EPCRA Tier II inventory report submitted to SERC, LEPC, and local fire department (threshold: 10,000 lbs or EHS TPQ).']
  un_na_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit identification number assigned by the United Nations or Department of Transportation for hazardous materials transport. Used on shipping papers and placards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this chemical inventory record was last modified in the Enviance EHS system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Facility-level inventory of hazardous chemicals and substances stored or used at Waste Management sites, maintained for EPCRA Tier II reporting, RCRA generator status determination, and process safety management (PSM) threshold tracking. Tracks chemical name, CAS number, EPA waste code if applicable, physical and health hazard classifications (GHS/SDS), maximum quantity stored, average daily quantity, storage location within facility, container type, and whether the chemical exceeds CERCLA reportable quantity or EPCRA Section 312 threshold. Updated at least annually for Tier II submission. Sourced from Enviance EHS chemical management module.'
}

Table compliance.ghg_regulatory_submission {
  ghg_regulatory_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the greenhouse gas regulatory submission record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management facility subject to GHG reporting requirements.']
  ghg_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ghg_inventory. Business justification: GHG regulatory submissions (EPA GHGRP, state programs) are prepared from compiled GHG inventories. The submission references the underlying inventory data that was verified and certified. Standard reg']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: GHG emissions reporting is required under air permits (Title V, EPA Subpart HH for landfills). This FK links GHG submissions to the air permit that governs emission reporting requirements, enabling pe']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: GHG regulatory submissions are a specialized type of regulatory submission. This FK links the detailed GHG calculation record to the parent regulatory submission record, enabling unified submission tr']
  responsible_party_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GHG submissions require employee ownership for data quality and regulatory accountability. Linking supports audit trails, workload management for sustainability staff, and performance tracking for cli']
  activity_data_input varchar [note: 'Description of the activity data inputs used in the emission calculation (e.g., waste in place tonnage, fuel consumption volume, vehicle miles traveled).']
  activity_data_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the activity data (e.g., metric tons, gallons, cubic feet, vehicle miles).']
  activity_data_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the activity data used in the emission calculation.']
  calculated_co2e_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total greenhouse gas emissions calculated and expressed in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) using EPA-specified global warming potentials.']
  calculation_methodology varchar [note: 'EPA-approved calculation methodology tier used to quantify GHG emissions (Tier 1-4, direct measurement, mass balance, or continuous emissions monitoring system). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|tier_4|direct_measurement|mass_balance|cems — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calculation_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, assumptions, or explanations regarding the GHG emission calculation methodology and data sources.']
  co2e_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the calculated CO2e quantity, standardized as metric tons CO2e per EPA requirements.']
  corporate_sustainability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is also used for corporate sustainability GHG inventory and ESG reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GHG regulatory submission record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the quality and reliability of the activity data and emission factors used in the calculation.']
  emission_factor_applied decimal(18,8) [note: 'EPA-approved emission factor applied in the GHG calculation (e.g., methane generation rate constant, CO2 emission factor per fuel type).']
  emission_factor_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the emission factor (e.g., kg CO2e per gallon, metric tons CH4 per metric ton waste).']
  emission_source_type varchar [note: 'Category of emission source being reported: landfill gas methane (Subpart HH), waste-to-energy combustion (Subpart C), stationary combustion, mobile fleet sources, or fugitive emissions.']
  epa_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation number issued by EPA e-GGRT portal upon successful submission of the GHG report.']
  epa_subpart varchar [note: 'Specific EPA 40 CFR Part 98 subpart under which the emissions are being reported (e.g., Subpart HH for landfills, Subpart C for stationary combustion).']
  facility_epa_number varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier used for regulatory reporting and tracking.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility type for GHG reporting purposes (landfill, waste-to-energy, materials recovery facility, transfer station, fleet depot, treatment storage disposal facility).']
  ghg_pollutant varchar [note: 'Specific greenhouse gas pollutant being reported: methane (CH4), carbon dioxide (CO2), nitrous oxide (N2O), hydrofluorocarbons (HFC), perfluorocarbons (PFC), sulfur hexafluoride (SF6), or nitrogen trifluoride (NF3). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CH4|CO2|N2O|HFC|PFC|SF6|NF3 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  global_warming_potential decimal(10,2) [note: 'EPA-specified global warming potential factor used to convert the GHG pollutant to CO2 equivalent (e.g., CH4 = 25, N2O = 298).']
  reporting_threshold_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility met the EPA reporting threshold for mandatory GHG reporting (typically 25,000 metric tons CO2e per year).']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which greenhouse gas emissions are being reported.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the GHG submission if the report has been revised or resubmitted after initial filing.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the GHG submission was revised or resubmitted after the initial filing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which the GHG emission data was sourced (e.g., Enviance EHS, SAP EHS, sustainability domain feeds).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the GHG emission report was submitted to EPA e-GGRT portal.']
  submission_deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline date by which the GHG report must be submitted to EPA (typically March 31 following the reporting year).']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the GHG regulatory submission assigned by the reporting system or EPA e-GGRT portal.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GHG regulatory submission in the EPA e-GGRT workflow.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GHG regulatory submission record was last modified in the system.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the GHG emission calculation and submission were verified or quality-assured.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of third-party verification or internal quality assurance review of the GHG emission calculation and submission.']
  verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party verifier or internal auditor who verified the GHG emission calculation.']

  Note: 'Greenhouse gas (GHG) emission calculation and reporting records for Waste Management facilities subject to EPA Mandatory Reporting Rule (40 CFR Part 98). Covers landfill gas (LFG) methane emissions (Subpart HH), WTE combustion emissions (Subpart C), fleet mobile source emissions, and stationary combustion sources. Tracks emission record ID, facility, reporting year, emission source type, GHG pollutant (CH4, CO2, N2O), calculation methodology, activity data inputs, emission factor applied, calculated CO2e quantity (metric tons), and verification status. Supports EPA e-GGRT annual submission and corporate sustainability GHG inventory. Sourced from Enviance EHS and sustainability domain feeds.'
}

Table compliance.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental, health, and safety risk assessment record.']
  assessed_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material. Business justification: Risk assessments evaluate hazards of specific materials. Procurement must consider findings when sourcing alternatives or approving new materials. Links risk management to procurement decisions for sa']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Risk assessments may be triggered by inspection findings that identify hazards or compliance gaps. This FK links risk assessments to the inspection that triggered them, enabling inspection-driven risk']
  conducted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Risk assessments require qualified employees. Linking enables competency verification, workload tracking for EHS staff, audit trails for ISO 14001/45001 compliance, and quality assurance of assessment']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal corrective action record created to address risks identified in this assessment. Links to the regulatory_corrective_action product for tracking implementation of recommended controls.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management facility where the assessed operation, process, or activity occurs. Links to landfill, Materials Recovery Facility (MRF), transfer station, Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plant, or other operational site.']
  hazard_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hazard_register. Business justification: Risk assessments feed the hazard register. This link ensures identified compliance risks are captured in the facility\'s master hazard inventory. Required for ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 integration, demon']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific EHS incident that triggered this risk assessment. Applicable when the assessment is conducted as part of incident investigation or root cause analysis.']
  jha_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.jha. Business justification: Risk assessments often identify hazards that require JHA development or updates. This link tracks which JHAs were created or revised based on risk assessment findings. Essential for demonstrating cont']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental or operational permit under which this risk assessment is conducted. Applicable when the assessment is required by permit conditions (e.g., air permit, water discharge permit, RCRA permit).']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Risk assessments evaluate compliance with regulatory requirements under ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 frameworks. This FK replaces the denormalized regulatory_citation STRING field with a structured relatio']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was conducted or completed. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this assessment record.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the risk assessment, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  assessment_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who reviewed and approved the risk assessment. Typically a senior EHS manager or facility director.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment. Draft indicates initial preparation, In Review indicates management or EHS review in progress, Approved indicates formal acceptance, Active indicates current operational use, Superseded indicates replaced by newer assessment, Archived indicates historical record retention.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk assessment methodology applied. Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) evaluates task-level hazards, Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) evaluates process-level risks, Environmental Aspect and Impact Assessment evaluates environmental consequences, and Operational Risk Review evaluates broader operational risks.']
  associated_risk_or_impact varchar [note: 'Description of the potential consequence or impact if the identified hazard or environmental aspect is realized. For safety, examples include injury, illness, or fatality. For environmental, examples include air quality degradation, water contamination, soil pollution, or contribution to climate change (CO2e emissions).']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment has identified the need for formal corrective action to address unacceptable residual risk. True if corrective action is required, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  environmental_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental impact associated with the identified environmental aspect. Used for ISO 14001 environmental aspect and impact assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air_emissions|water_discharge|soil_contamination|waste_generation|noise|odor|greenhouse_gas|resource_depletion — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  existing_controls varchar [note: 'Description of current control measures, safeguards, or mitigation strategies already in place to manage the identified hazard or environmental aspect. Examples include engineering controls (guards, ventilation), administrative controls (procedures, training, permits), or Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) requirements.']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the identified hazard or environmental aspect. Physical hazards include struck-by, caught-in, fall hazards. Chemical hazards include exposure to hazardous substances. Biological hazards include exposure to pathogens. Ergonomic hazards include repetitive motion or lifting. Environmental aspects include emissions, discharges, waste generation. Operational hazards include equipment failure or process upset. Regulatory hazards include non-compliance risk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical|chemical|biological|ergonomic|environmental|operational|regulatory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  hazard_identified varchar [note: 'Description of the specific hazard, environmental aspect, or risk factor identified during the assessment. For occupational safety, examples include struck-by hazards from moving vehicles, exposure to hazardous waste, ergonomic strain, or confined space entry risks. For environmental aspects, examples include air emissions, wastewater discharge, soil contamination, or greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.']
  iso_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ISO 14001 or ISO 45001 clause that this risk assessment fulfills. Typically Clause 6.1 (Actions to address risks and opportunities) or Clause 6.1.2 (Hazard identification and assessment of risks and opportunities).']
  likelihood_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating representing the probability or frequency that the identified hazard or environmental aspect will occur. Typically scored on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is rare and 5 is almost certain. Used in conjunction with severity rating to calculate risk priority.']
  management_review_date date [note: 'Date when this risk assessment was reviewed by senior management as part of the ISO 14001 or ISO 45001 management review process.']
  management_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment requires formal management review due to high residual risk level or significant operational impact. True if management review is required, False otherwise.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or reassessment of this risk assessment. ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 require periodic review of risk assessments to ensure continued relevance and accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context related to this risk assessment. May include stakeholder input, lessons learned, or special considerations.']
  operation_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the specific operation, process, activity, or work area covered by this risk assessment. Examples include residential collection route operations, landfill cell compaction, MRF sorting line operations, hazardous waste handling, fleet maintenance activities, or leachate management.']
  ppe_required varchar [note: 'Description of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required to manage the identified hazard. Examples include hard hats, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, hearing protection, high-visibility vests, or chemical-resistant suits.']
  recommended_additional_controls varchar [note: 'Description of recommended additional control measures, safeguards, or mitigation strategies to further reduce residual risk to an acceptable level. May include engineering improvements, enhanced training, revised procedures, or additional PPE.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Reference to applicable regulatory requirement, standard, or permit condition that drives this risk assessment. Examples include EPA regulations, OSHA standards (29 CFR 1910), DOT requirements (49 CFR), RCRA requirements, Clean Air Act provisions, or state environmental regulations.']
  residual_risk_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the remaining risk level after existing controls are applied. Low indicates acceptable risk with current controls. Medium indicates risk requiring monitoring. High indicates risk requiring additional controls. Very High indicates unacceptable risk requiring immediate action.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment was formally reviewed and approved by management or EHS leadership.']
  risk_priority_number varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority score, typically the product of likelihood rating and severity rating. Used to rank and prioritize risks for control implementation. Higher RPN values indicate higher priority for risk mitigation.']
  safety_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the occupational health and safety impact associated with the identified hazard. Used for ISO 45001 hazard identification and risk assessments.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Numerical rating representing the magnitude of harm or impact if the identified hazard or environmental aspect is realized. Typically scored on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is negligible and 5 is catastrophic. Used in conjunction with likelihood rating to calculate risk priority.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this risk assessment data originated. Typically Enviance EHS or SAP EHS module.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment has identified the need for specific training to manage the identified hazard or environmental aspect. True if training is required, False otherwise. Examples include HAZWOPER training, CDL training, confined space entry training, or equipment-specific training.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']

  Note: 'EHS risk assessments conducted for Waste Management operations, facilities, and activities under ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 frameworks. Covers job hazard analyses (JHA), process hazard analyses (PHA), environmental aspect and impact assessments, and operational risk reviews. Tracks assessment ID, assessment type, facility and operation scope, assessment date, identified hazard or environmental aspect, associated risk or impact, likelihood rating, severity rating, risk priority number (RPN), existing controls, residual risk level, and recommended additional controls. Supports ISO 14001 Clause 6.1 and ISO 45001 Clause 6.1 hazard identification and risk assessment obligations.'
}

Table compliance.regulated_facility {
  regulated_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulated facility record. Primary key for compliance tracking and regulatory reporting. [ROLE: MASTER_RESOURCE]']
  primary_regulated_epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA Facility Registry Service (FRS) identifier. Unique federal identifier assigned by EPA for tracking environmental compliance across all federal programs.']
  active_violations_count varchar [note: 'Current number of unresolved regulatory violations at this facility. Calculated from violation_notice records with status = open or pending. Used for compliance risk assessment.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the regulated facility. Used for regulatory correspondence, inspection scheduling, and emergency response coordination.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building number, suite, or unit. Optional field for additional location details.']
  applicable_regulatory_programs varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of federal and state environmental programs applicable to this facility. Examples: RCRA Subtitle D, Title V Air, NPDES Water, SPCC, EPCRA Tier II, SWIS. Determines compliance obligations and reporting requirements.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the design capacity value. TPD = Tons Per Day (common for landfills and WTE), cubic yards (transfer stations), tons per hour (MRFs).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the facility is located. Used for local jurisdiction compliance and LEA coordination.']
  compliance_risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall compliance risk assessment for the facility. Low = no violations, good compliance history; Medium = minor violations or aging permits; High = multiple violations or major non-compliance; Critical = enforcement action pending or operations at risk. Used for prioritizing compliance resources.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the facility location. Determines applicable national environmental regulations and international reporting requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulated facility record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  design_capacity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted operational capacity of the facility. Units vary by facility type: TPD (Tons Per Day) for landfills and WTE plants, cubic yards for transfer stations, tons per hour for MRFs. Used for permit compliance and capacity planning.']
  facility_closure_date date [note: 'Date the facility permanently ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for post-closure monitoring and long-term stewardship tracking.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier code for the facility. Used for cross-system integration with SAP EHS, Enviance, and operational systems.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the regulated facility as registered with regulatory agencies. Used for permit applications, inspection reports, and regulatory correspondence.']
  facility_opened_date date [note: 'Date the facility first began operations. Used for facility age analysis, permit history tracking, and closure planning.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility based on its primary operational function. Determines applicable regulatory programs and compliance requirements. MRF = Materials Recovery Facility, WTE = Waste-to-Energy, TSDF = Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: landfill|mrf|transfer_station|wte_plant|tsdf|hauling_depot|collection_yard|maintenance_facility|administrative_office — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. True = certified, False = not certified. Certification demonstrates commitment to environmental performance improvement.']
  iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. True = certified, False = not certified. Certification demonstrates commitment to worker safety.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection conducted at this facility. Used for compliance tracking and inspection frequency analysis.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, environmental monitoring zone determination, and emergency response planning.']
  lea_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Name of the Local Enforcement Agency with jurisdiction over this facility. LEAs conduct inspections and enforce state solid waste regulations at the local level.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, environmental monitoring zone determination, and emergency response planning.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code classifying the facility\'s primary business activity. Used for regulatory applicability determination and industry benchmarking. Common codes: 562111 (Solid Waste Collection), 562212 (Solid Waste Landfill), 562213 (Solid Waste Combustors and Incinerators), 562920 (Materials Recovery Facilities).']
  next_scheduled_inspection_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of the next regulatory inspection. Based on inspection frequency requirements and agency scheduling. Used for preparation and resource planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory or operational notes about the facility. Used for documenting special conditions, historical context, or unique compliance considerations.']
  operating_permit_number varchar [note: 'Primary operating permit number issued by the state or local regulatory agency. Required for legal operation of the facility. Links to detailed permit records in Enviance EHS.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the facility independent of regulatory status. Operational = actively processing waste, Non-operational = temporarily idle, Seasonal = operates during specific periods, Standby = ready but not active, Decommissioned = permanently shut down.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the primary operating permit. Critical for compliance tracking and permit renewal planning. Facilities cannot operate beyond this date without renewal.']
  permitted_waste_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of waste types the facility is permitted to accept. Examples: MSW (Municipal Solid Waste), C&D (Construction and Demolition), industrial, hazardous, medical, e-waste, universal waste. Determines operational scope and compliance requirements.']
  post_closure_monitoring_end_date date [note: 'Date when post-closure monitoring obligations end. Typically 30 years after closure for landfills. Null if monitoring not required or facility still active.']
  post_closure_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility requires post-closure environmental monitoring after operations cease. True = monitoring required (typical for landfills), False = no monitoring required. Determines long-term compliance obligations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the facility location. Used for geographic analysis and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  primary_regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary regulatory contact. Used for regulatory correspondence, inspection notifications, and compliance alerts.']
  primary_regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary internal contact responsible for regulatory compliance at this facility. Typically the facility manager, EHS manager, or compliance officer.']
  primary_regulatory_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary regulatory contact. Used for urgent regulatory communications and emergency response coordination.']
  rcra_generator_status varchar [note: 'RCRA hazardous waste generator classification. LQG = Large Quantity Generator (≥1000 kg/month), SQG = Small Quantity Generator (100-1000 kg/month), VSQG = Very Small Quantity Generator (<100 kg/month). Determines hazardous waste management requirements.']
  regional_epa_office varchar [note: 'EPA regional office with jurisdiction over this facility. Determines which EPA regional office handles permits, inspections, and enforcement actions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: region_1|region_2|region_3|region_4|region_5|region_6|region_7|region_8|region_9|region_10 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the facility. Active = operational and compliant, Inactive = temporarily non-operational, Pending closure = in closure process, Closed = permanently closed, Suspended = operations halted due to violations, Under review = regulatory investigation in progress.']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit SIC code for legacy regulatory reporting. Still required by some state agencies and EPA programs. Common codes: 4953 (Refuse Systems), 4212 (Local Trucking Without Storage).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this regulated facility data. Enviance EHS = primary compliance system, SAP EHS = enterprise EHS module, Manual entry = data entered directly by compliance staff.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code where the facility is located. Determines applicable state environmental regulations and reporting requirements.']
  title_v_designation varchar [note: 'Clean Air Act Title V operating permit classification. Major source = emissions exceed threshold limits, Minor source = below thresholds, Synthetic minor = voluntarily limited emissions. Determines air quality monitoring and reporting requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulated facility record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Compliance-specific master record for each regulated facility or operational site within Waste Management\'s portfolio, capturing the regulatory identity and classification attributes needed for EHS compliance management. Tracks facility ID, facility name, physical address, EPA facility ID (FRS ID), state facility ID, facility type (landfill, MRF, transfer station, WTE plant, TSDF, hauling depot), RCRA generator status (LQG/SQG/VSQG), Title V major/minor source designation, applicable regulatory programs, primary regulatory contact, and compliance status. Distinct from the asset domain\'s facility master — this record owns the regulatory classification and agency-facing identity of each site. Sourced from Enviance EHS and SAP EHS.'
}

Table compliance.facility_audit {
  facility_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility audit record. Primary key.']
  audit_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audit program under which this facility audit was conducted']
  regulated_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulated facility that was audited']
  audit_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was completed and findings were finalized.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the audit was conducted at this facility. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the audit report generated from this facility audit.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Overall result or outcome of the facility audit. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this facility audit execution. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  auditor_assigned varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the auditor assigned to conduct this specific facility audit. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  certification_outcome varchar [note: 'Certification status resulting from this facility audit, if applicable to external certification programs. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical or high-severity findings identified during this facility audit. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of findings identified during this facility audit. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next audit of this facility under this audit program. Explicitly identified in detection phase.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the execution of an audit program at a specific regulated facility. It captures the audit event including scheduling, execution, findings, and outcomes. Each record links one audit_program to one regulated_facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific audit execution.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management EHS compliance operations, audit programs are executed across multiple facilities, and each facility is subject to multiple audit programs (ISO 14001, RCRA, OSHA VPP, operational reviews). Compliance managers actively schedule facility audits by selecting facilities for each program cycle, assign auditors, track execution, and manage findings. The facility audit is a recognized operational entity with substantial relationship data including audit dates, results, findings counts, auditor assignments, and certification outcomes.'
}

Table compliance.permit_requirement {
  permit_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this permit-requirement mapping record. Primary key.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the permit that must satisfy this regulatory requirement']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement that applies to this permit']
  applicability_determination varchar [note: 'Documented determination of how and why this regulatory requirement applies to this specific permit. Captures the legal and operational reasoning for the mapping.']
  citation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific citation or section reference from the regulatory requirement that applies to this permit. Allows for granular tracking when only portions of a requirement apply.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this permit with respect to this specific regulatory requirement. Tracks whether the permit holder is meeting the obligation.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this permit-requirement mapping was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory requirement became applicable to this specific permit. May differ from the requirement\'s general effective date based on permit-specific circumstances.']
  exemption_claimed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exemption from this regulatory requirement has been claimed for this permit. If true, exemption documentation should be maintained.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory requirement ceases to apply to this specific permit. May be due to permit expiration, requirement sunset, or exemption grant.']
  last_compliance_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which compliance with this requirement was last verified for this permit through inspection, audit, or self-assessment.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this permit-requirement mapping was last modified.']
  monitoring_frequency_override varchar [note: 'Permit-specific monitoring frequency that overrides the default monitoring requirement. Captures permit conditions that impose more or less frequent monitoring than the base requirement.']
  next_compliance_verification_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance verification activity for this permit-requirement combination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting compliance activities, issues, or special circumstances related to this permit-requirement mapping.']
  reporting_frequency_override varchar [note: 'Permit-specific reporting frequency that overrides the default reporting requirement. Captures permit conditions that impose different reporting schedules than the base requirement.']
  responsible_officer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance officer or facility manager responsible for ensuring this specific permit meets this specific requirement.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory compliance mapping between permit and regulatory_requirement. It captures the specific requirements that each permit must satisfy and tracks compliance status, applicability determinations, and requirement-specific monitoring and reporting obligations. Each record links one permit to one regulatory_requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In environmental compliance operations, permits must satisfy multiple regulatory requirements simultaneously (e.g., a landfill permit implements RCRA Subtitle D requirements, state solid waste codes, air quality standards, and stormwater management rules). Conversely, each regulatory requirement applies to multiple permits across different facilities and permit types. Compliance officers actively manage these mappings during permit application, renewal, and ongoing compliance monitoring, tracking status, applicability determinations, and requirement-specific obligations for each permit-requirement combination.'
}

Table compliance.monitoring_program {
  monitoring_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for monitoring_program']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the waste management facility where this monitoring program is conducted.']
  parent_monitoring_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on monitoring_program (parent_monitoring_program_id)']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental permit that requires or authorizes this monitoring program.']
  action_level decimal(18,6) [note: 'Threshold value that triggers corrective action or enhanced monitoring when exceeded, typically set below the regulatory limit.']
  analytical_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory analytical method or technique used to analyze collected samples, typically referenced by EPA method number or standard protocol.']
  chain_of_custody_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal chain of custody documentation is required for sample handling and transport.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the monitoring program based on recent monitoring results and regulatory requirements.']
  corrective_action_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for any corrective action plan associated with non-compliance or exceedances detected by this program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this monitoring program record was first created in the system.']
  data_management_system varchar [note: 'Name of the software system or database used to store, manage, and report monitoring data (e.g., Enviance EHS, SAP EHS).']
  data_validation_level varchar [note: 'EPA data validation level applied to analytical results, indicating the rigor of quality assurance review.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring program is scheduled to terminate or was terminated, nullable for ongoing programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring program became or will become active and monitoring activities commenced.']
  estimated_annual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected annual cost in USD for conducting this monitoring program, including sampling, analysis, reporting, and labor.']
  iso_14001_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this monitoring program is part of the organizations ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification scope.']
  iso_45001_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this monitoring program supports ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System requirements.']
  laboratory_certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification or accreditation number of the laboratory authorized to perform environmental analyses.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certified laboratory performing analytical testing for samples collected under this program.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection or audit of this monitoring program.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this monitoring program record was most recently updated or modified.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this monitoring program record.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency at which monitoring activities must be performed as specified by regulatory requirements or permit conditions.']
  monitoring_location_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct sampling or monitoring locations included in this program.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled or anticipated date for the next regulatory inspection or audit of this monitoring program.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or renewal of the monitoring program requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to the monitoring program.']
  parameter_monitored varchar [note: 'Specific environmental parameter, pollutant, or constituent being measured by this monitoring program (e.g., PM2.5, BOD, lead, methane).']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the monitoring program for regulatory reporting and internal tracking.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the monitoring programs objectives, scope, and methodology.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the monitoring program describing its purpose and scope.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the monitoring program indicating whether it is operational, suspended, or terminated.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring program based on the environmental medium or parameter being monitored.']
  public_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether monitoring results must be made available to the public through regulatory reporting portals or community right-to-know requirements.']
  quality_assurance_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the Quality Assurance Project Plan (QAPP) governing data quality objectives and procedures for this program.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the federal, state, or local regulatory agency that oversees this monitoring program and receives compliance reports.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary federal or state regulatory framework that mandates or governs this monitoring program.']
  regulatory_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration or level for the monitored parameter as specified by regulatory standards or permit conditions.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting monitoring results to regulatory agencies or internal stakeholders.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, department, or contractor responsible for executing and managing this monitoring program.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Standardized methodology or protocol used for collecting environmental samples, including EPA method numbers or industry standards.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measurement for the monitored parameter (e.g., mg/L, ppm, µg/m³, dBA).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for monitoring_program. Referenced by monitoring_program_id.'
}

Table compliance.environmental_sample {
  environmental_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for environmental_sample']
  collected_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who physically collected the sample.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the waste management facility where the sample was collected.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory facility that will analyze or has analyzed the sample.']
  monitoring_station_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific monitoring location or station where the sample was collected.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental permit under which this sample was collected.']
  sampling_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broader sampling event or campaign of which this sample is part.']
  analysis_completed_date date [note: 'Date when laboratory analysis was completed.']
  analysis_due_date date [note: 'Date by which laboratory analysis must be completed to meet holding time requirements.']
  blank_sample_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a quality control blank sample used to detect contamination.']
  chain_of_custody_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number for the chain of custody documentation accompanying the sample.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to collect the environmental sample.']
  collection_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the sample at the time of collection.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the environmental sample was physically collected in the field.']
  compliance_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any analyte in this sample exceeded regulatory limits.']
  composite_sample_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample is a composite of multiple sub-samples collected over time or space.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sample record was first created in the system.']
  duplicate_sample_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample is a quality control duplicate of another sample.']
  holding_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable time between sample collection and analysis completion, as specified by regulatory method.']
  ph_level decimal(4,2) [note: 'Acidity or alkalinity measurement of the sample at collection, on a scale of 0-14.']
  preservation_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to preserve sample integrity during transport and storage.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Federal or state environmental program under which the sample is required.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the sample was rejected by the laboratory or deemed invalid, if applicable.']
  reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample result must be included in regulatory reporting submissions.']
  sample_depth_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth below surface at which the sample was collected, applicable for groundwater, soil, and sediment samples.']
  sample_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the sample appearance, odor, or other observable characteristics at collection.']
  sample_matrix varchar [note: 'Physical state or composition of the sample material.']
  sample_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the sample collection, handling, or analysis.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the environmental sample, used in regulatory submissions and laboratory tracking.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the environmental sample from collection through analysis completion.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental medium being sampled.']
  sample_volume decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of sample collected, measured in the unit specified by volume_unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sample record was last modified.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the sample volume.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions at the time of sample collection, relevant for outdoor sampling.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for environmental_sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

Table compliance.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for laboratory']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or certification body that granted accreditation to the laboratory (e.g., NELAP, A2LA, state environmental agency).']
  accreditation_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory accreditation became effective and valid.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current laboratory accreditation expires and requires renewal.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or accreditation number issued by the accrediting body.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the laboratory indicating its certification standing with regulatory bodies.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the laboratory facility location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information of the laboratory facility.']
  certification_iso_14001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory holds ISO 14001 certification for environmental management systems.']
  certification_iso_45001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory holds ISO 45001 certification for occupational health and safety management systems.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the laboratory facility is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory ceased operations or was decommissioned, if applicable.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the laboratory is located (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was first created in the system.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory was established or first became operational.']
  hazardous_waste_handler_id varchar [note: 'EPA-issued identification number for the laboratory as a hazardous waste handler, required for laboratories that generate or handle hazardous waste.']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code assigned to the laboratory for external reference and regulatory reporting.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the laboratory facility or testing center.']
  laboratory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the laboratory based on its primary testing focus and specialization.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external quality audit conducted at the laboratory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the laboratory facility location in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the laboratory facility location in decimal degrees.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required quality audit of the laboratory.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments regarding the laboratory, including special conditions, restrictions, or operational considerations.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory indicating whether it is actively conducting testing or temporarily unavailable.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the laboratory is owned and operated internally by Waste Management or is an external contracted facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the laboratory facility address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary laboratory contact for official communications and coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person or laboratory director responsible for laboratory operations.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the laboratory contact or operations center.']
  quality_management_system varchar [note: 'Name or description of the quality management system framework implemented by the laboratory (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO/IEC 17025, NELAP QA Manual).']
  scope_of_accreditation varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the testing methods, analytes, and matrices for which the laboratory is accredited.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the laboratory facility is located.']
  testing_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comprehensive list or description of analytical testing capabilities and methods available at the laboratory (e.g., metals analysis, volatile organic compounds, microbiology).']
  turnaround_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time in days for the laboratory to complete testing and deliver results.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for laboratory. Referenced by laboratory_id.'
}

Table compliance.sampling_event {
  sampling_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sampling_event']
  actual_sampling_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the sample was physically collected in the field. This is the principal business event timestamp for the sampling event.']
  ambient_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature at the time of sampling, measured in degrees Celsius, relevant for certain environmental monitoring requirements.']
  analysis_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when laboratory analysis was completed and results were finalized.']
  analysis_requested varchar [note: 'Description of the analytical tests or parameter suite requested for this sample (e.g., VOCs, metals, pH, BOD, total coliform).']
  chain_of_custody_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number for the chain of custody documentation that accompanies the sample from collection through analysis, ensuring sample integrity and legal defensibility.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sampling results require corrective action per permit conditions or regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sampling event record was first created in the system.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the sampling event, typically formatted with facility code prefix and sequential number for external reference and regulatory reporting.']
  exceedance_detected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any analytical results from this sampling event exceeded regulatory limits or action levels, triggering potential corrective action or reporting requirements.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the waste management facility where the sampling event occurred (landfill, transfer station, recycling center, or treatment facility).']
  field_conductivity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of the sample measured in the field, in microsiemens per centimeter, used as an indicator of dissolved solids in water samples.']
  field_dissolved_oxygen decimal(6,2) [note: 'Dissolved oxygen concentration measured in the field, in milligrams per liter, critical for water quality assessment.']
  field_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'pH measurement taken in the field at the time of sample collection, required for water and leachate samples as pH can change during transport.']
  field_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the sample medium measured in the field at time of collection, in degrees Celsius, required for water quality monitoring.']
  holding_time_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample was analyzed within the required holding time specified by the analytical method. Non-compliance may affect data usability.']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified analytical laboratory that will perform or has performed the sample analysis.']
  laboratory_sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the analytical laboratory to track the sample through their internal processes.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the sampling location in decimal degrees for spatial analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the sampling location in decimal degrees for spatial analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sampling event record was last modified in the system.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of sampling as specified in the permit or monitoring plan: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or event-based (triggered by specific conditions).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, deviations from standard procedures, or other relevant information about the sampling event.']
  parent_sampling_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sampling event if this is a quality control sample (duplicate or split) associated with another primary sample.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the environmental permit under which this sampling event is conducted (air quality, water discharge, waste handling permit).']
  preservation_method varchar [note: 'Chemical or physical preservation technique applied to the sample to maintain integrity during transport and storage (e.g., acidification, refrigeration, freezing).']
  quality_control_type varchar [note: 'Indicates if this is a quality control sample: field blank, trip blank, equipment blank, field duplicate, matrix spike, or none (regular sample).']
  regulator_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the sampling results were officially submitted to the regulatory agency.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Federal or state environmental program under which the sampling is required: RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act), CERCLA (Superfund), CAA (Clean Air Act), CWA (Clean Water Act), NPDES (National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System), SWIS (Solid Waste Information System), or state-specific program.']
  reported_to_regulator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sampling results have been submitted to the appropriate regulatory agency as required by permit or regulation.']
  sample_container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container used to collect and preserve the sample (e.g., glass bottle, plastic jar, Tedlar bag, stainless steel canister) as specified by analytical method requirements.']
  sample_depth decimal(8,2) [note: 'Depth at which the sample was collected, measured in feet or meters below ground surface or water surface, critical for groundwater and soil sampling.']
  sample_depth_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for sample depth: feet, meters, or inches.']
