Pre-employment tests are required before hiring; random tests are unannounced selections from the pool; post-accident tests follow qualifying incidents; reasonable suspicion tests are based on observed behavior; return-to-duty tests are required before reinstatement after a violation; follow-up tests are part of a monitoring program.']
  violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test result constitutes a DOT drug and alcohol program violation (positive result, refusal, or adulteration). True if violation occurred; False otherwise.']
  workday_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this drug test record to the corresponding event or case in Workday HCM for HR tracking and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks DOT-mandated drug and alcohol testing events for CDL drivers and safety-sensitive employees at Waste Management. Captures test type (pre-employment, random, post-accident, reasonable suspicion, return-to-duty, follow-up), test date, specimen type, laboratory, result (negative, positive, refusal, cancelled), MRO review status, and SAP/Workday reference. Mandatory under DOT 49 CFR Part 382 and FMCSA regulations for all CDL holders.'
}

Table workforce.job_requisition {
  job_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job requisition record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the position\'s labor costs will be allocated.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or location where the position will be based (landfill, MRF, transfer station, office, etc.).']
  job_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_position. Business justification: Job requisitions should link to the authoritative job position definition to ensure consistency in job requirements, qualifications, certifications, and compensation. The job_position_code and all req']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Requisitions opened to support new service offerings or service expansion. Drives growth planning, service launch staffing, and capacity expansion. Essential for aligning hiring pipeline with service ']
  primary_job_hiring_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the hiring manager responsible for this requisition.']
  tertiary_job_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved the requisition (typically senior manager or HR director).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the requisition by management and HR (pending, approved, rejected).']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total approved budget amount for the position(s) covered by this requisition, typically annual salary or hourly rate budget.']
  approved_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of positions approved for hire under this requisition.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition was approved.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approved budget amount (e.g., USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the department or organizational unit requesting the hire.']
  education_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required for the position (high school, associate degree, bachelor degree, master degree, doctorate, or none).']
  filled_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of positions that have been filled to date under this requisition.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the job responsibilities, duties, and requirements.']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for the position.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about the requisition, hiring requirements, or special considerations.']
  posting_channel varchar [note: 'Channels where the job requisition is posted (e.g., company website, Indeed, LinkedIn, internal job board).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level for filling this requisition (low, medium, high, critical).']
  requisition_close_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was closed (filled, cancelled, or otherwise finalized).']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the job requisition, externally visible and used in recruiting workflows and communications.']
  requisition_open_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was officially opened and made available for recruiting.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job requisition (draft, open, on-hold, in-review, filled, cancelled, closed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|on_hold|in_review|filled|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Type of requisition indicating the reason for the hire (backfill for departure, new headcount expansion, seasonal demand, temporary project, contractor conversion, internal transfer).']
  salary_range_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum salary or hourly rate for the position.']
  salary_range_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum salary or hourly rate for the position.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this requisition record originated (e.g., Workday HCM).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this requisition in the source system.']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Desired start date for the new hire(s) to begin employment.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of work location for the position (on-site at facility, field-based routes, remote office, hybrid).']

  Note: 'Tracks open job requisitions for Waste Management workforce hiring. Captures requisition number, job position, org unit, facility, hiring manager, requisition type (backfill, new headcount, seasonal), required CDL class, required certifications (HAZWOPER, OSHA), target start date, approved headcount budget, requisition status (open, on-hold, filled, cancelled), and source system reference from Workday HCM recruiting module.'
}

Table workforce.labor_cost_allocation {
  labor_cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the labor cost allocation record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which the labor cost is allocated, representing the organizational unit or operational function.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system process that created the labor cost allocation record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility (landfill, MRF, WTE plant, transfer station) to which labor costs are allocated.']
  landfill_cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific landfill cell to which labor costs are allocated for cell management and operations.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system process that last modified the labor cost allocation record.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Labor costs allocated to service offerings for profitability analysis. Drives service line P&L, pricing model validation, and cost-to-serve analysis. Essential for understanding true service profitabi']
  primary_labor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee whose labor cost is being allocated.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route to which labor costs are allocated, applicable for drivers and collection crew.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO activity type code representing the type of work performed, such as collection, sorting, landfill operations, or maintenance.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date when the labor cost allocation was processed and recorded in the financial system.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate labor costs to the cost object, such as direct assignment, percentage split, driver-based allocation, activity-based costing, or standard rate application.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the employee\'s total labor cost allocated to this specific cost object, used when an employee\'s time is split across multiple cost centers or activities.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the labor cost allocation record in the approval and posting workflow.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the labor cost allocation was approved for posting to the financial system.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business unit (residential collection, commercial collection, recycling, landfill, hazardous waste) for segment reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the labor cost allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the labor cost amounts. Waste Management primarily operates in USD.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which the labor cost allocation applies.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the labor cost allocation applies for financial reporting and period closing.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI General Ledger account code to which the labor cost is posted for financial reporting.']
  hazard_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional pay amount for working in hazardous conditions such as handling hazardous waste or working in confined spaces.']
  hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours worked by the employee during the pay period that are being allocated to the cost object.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO internal order number used for tracking costs related to specific activities or initiatives.']
  job_classification varchar [note: 'Job classification or title of the employee whose labor is being allocated, such as CDL driver, MRF sorter, equipment operator, or environmental engineer.']
  labor_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to calculate the labor cost for this allocation, including base wage and loaded costs such as benefits and taxes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the labor cost allocation record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context or explanation for the labor cost allocation, such as special circumstances or adjustments.']
  overtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked during the pay period.']
  overtime_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Labor cost amount for overtime hours worked.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the pay period for which labor costs are allocated.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the pay period for which labor costs are allocated.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO profit center code representing the business unit or segment for EBITDA reporting and profitability analysis.']
  regular_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of regular hours worked during the pay period, excluding overtime.']
  regular_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Labor cost amount for regular hours worked, excluding overtime and premium pay.']
  sap_posting_reference varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO document number or posting reference generated when the labor cost allocation is posted to the financial system.']
  shift_differential_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional pay amount for working non-standard shifts such as night, weekend, or holiday shifts.']
  total_labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total labor cost allocated to the cost object, calculated as hours worked multiplied by labor rate.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union to which the employee belongs, if applicable, for labor cost analysis by union contract.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure element for capital projects to which labor costs are allocated, enabling project cost tracking.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order number to which labor costs are allocated for maintenance, repair, or project activities.']

  Note: 'Records the allocation of employee labor costs to SAP FI/CO cost objects including collection routes, landfill cells, MRF processing lines, WTE facility cost centers, and overhead pools. Captures employee, pay period, hours worked, labor rate, total labor cost, SAP cost center, SAP WBS element (for capital projects), activity type, and allocation percentage. Enables COGS analysis by operational function and supports EBITDA reporting by business unit.'
}

Table workforce.union_agreement {
  union_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the collective bargaining agreement record.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or contract number assigned to this collective bargaining agreement for tracking and reference purposes.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collective bargaining agreement indicating its operational state and validity.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the collective bargaining agreement indicating its scope and nature within the labor relations framework.']
  arbitration_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the collective bargaining agreement includes binding arbitration as the final step in the grievance resolution process.']
  bargaining_unit_size varchar [note: 'Total number of employees represented by this collective bargaining agreement at the time of ratification or most recent count.']
  base_wage_rate_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly base wage rate in US dollars (USD) for the highest tier or most senior position covered under this agreement.']
  base_wage_rate_minimum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum hourly base wage rate in US dollars (USD) for the lowest tier or entry-level position covered under this agreement.']
  cdl_premium_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'Additional hourly pay rate in US dollars (USD) for employees holding a valid Commercial Driver License (CDL) as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  covered_job_classifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of job classifications, titles, and positions covered under this collective bargaining agreement (e.g., Commercial Driver License (CDL) drivers, Material Recovery Facility (MRF) sorters, equipment operators, facility workers).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collective bargaining agreement record was first created in the system.']
  document_attachment_path varchar [note: 'File system path or document management system reference to the full signed collective bargaining agreement document and any amendments or side letters.']
  double_time_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked per day or per week after which double-time rates apply according to the collective bargaining agreement provisions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the collective bargaining agreement becomes binding and enforceable for all covered employees and management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the collective bargaining agreement terminates and requires renegotiation or renewal.']
  grievance_filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days after an alleged contract violation within which an employee or union must file a formal grievance to preserve their rights under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  grievance_procedure_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of formal steps defined in the grievance resolution procedure outlined in the collective bargaining agreement before arbitration.']
  hazard_pay_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'Additional hourly pay rate in US dollars (USD) for employees working in hazardous conditions or handling hazardous materials as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  health_insurance_employer_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of health insurance premium costs that the employer agrees to contribute for covered employees under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  lead_negotiator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary management representative who led negotiations for this collective bargaining agreement.']
  management_rights_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the collective bargaining agreement includes a management rights clause reserving certain operational decisions to management discretion.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collective bargaining agreement record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  negotiation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which negotiations concluded and a tentative agreement was reached between management and union representatives.']
  negotiation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which formal negotiations for this collective bargaining agreement commenced between management and union representatives.']
  no_strike_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the collective bargaining agreement includes a no-strike clause prohibiting work stoppages during the term of the agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special provisions, or contextual information about the collective bargaining agreement not captured in structured fields.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base wage rate for overtime hours worked (e.g., 1.5 for time-and-a-half, 2.0 for double-time) as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  overtime_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked per day or per week after which overtime rates apply according to the collective bargaining agreement provisions.']
  paid_holidays_count varchar [note: 'Number of paid holidays per year guaranteed to covered employees under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  paid_time_off_accrual_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Rate at which covered employees accrue paid time off hours per hour worked or per pay period as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  pension_employer_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of employee wages or fixed dollar amount per hour that the employer contributes to the pension plan for covered employees.']
  pension_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of retirement pension plan provided to covered employees under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  probationary_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days that a newly hired employee must complete before gaining full union membership rights and seniority accrual under the collective bargaining agreement.']
  ratification_date date [note: 'Date on which the union membership voted to approve and ratify the collective bargaining agreement.']
  seniority_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of seniority system defined in the collective bargaining agreement that governs bidding rights, layoff order, recall rights, and shift preferences.']
  shift_differential_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'Additional hourly pay rate in US dollars (USD) for employees working non-standard shifts (evening, night, weekend) as defined in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  union_dues_deduction_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount or percentage of wages deducted from covered employee paychecks for union membership dues as authorized in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  union_dues_deduction_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which union dues are deducted from covered employee paychecks as specified in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'Local chapter or affiliate number of the union representing the specific bargaining unit (e.g., Local 174, Local 1021).']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the labor union or labor organization representing the employees (e.g., International Brotherhood of Teamsters, Service Employees International Union, International Union of Operating Engineers).']
  union_representative_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary union representative or business agent who led negotiations for this collective bargaining agreement on behalf of the union membership.']
  wage_scale_tier_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct wage scale tiers or pay steps defined in the collective bargaining agreement for covered positions.']

  Note: 'Master record of collective bargaining agreements (CBAs) and labor relations activity for unionized Waste Management employees including Teamsters (CDL drivers), SEIU (facility workers), and IUOE (equipment operators). Captures union name, local number, agreement effective date, expiration date, covered job classifications, wage scale tiers, overtime rules, seniority provisions, grievance procedure reference, ratification date, and active grievance tracking (filing date, type, CBA article cited, step in procedure, resolution). Drives payroll rules, scheduling constraints, and labor dispute management for covered employees.'
}

Table workforce.grievance {
  grievance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the labor grievance record. Primary key.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility or work location where the grievance incident occurred.']
  grievance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the grievant employee who filed the grievance.']
  grievance_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the grievance record.']
  grievance_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the supervisor or manager involved in or responding to the grievance.']
  grievance_union_steward_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the union steward representing the grievant.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or organizational unit where the grievance incident occurred.']
  related_grievance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of another grievance that is related to or consolidated with this grievance.']
  union_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.union_agreement. Business justification: Grievances cite specific CBA articles and must be processed according to the grievance procedure defined in the collective bargaining agreement. Linking to the authoritative union_agreement ensures th']
  arbitration_date date [note: 'Date the arbitration hearing was conducted.']
  arbitration_decision varchar [note: 'Written decision issued by the arbitrator, including findings and remedies.']
  arbitration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance proceeded to binding arbitration.']
  arbitrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the neutral arbitrator assigned to hear the grievance if it proceeded to arbitration.']
  back_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of back pay awarded to the grievant as part of the grievance resolution.']
  cba_article_cited varchar [note: 'Specific article or section of the collective bargaining agreement cited as the basis for the grievance.']
  confidential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grievance contains confidential or sensitive information requiring restricted access.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grievance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts. Waste Management operates primarily in USD.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the grievance was formally filed by the employee or union representative.']
  grievance_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique grievance tracking number assigned by labor relations or union steward.']
  grievance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grievance in the resolution process.']
  grievance_type varchar [note: 'Category of the grievance indicating the nature of the complaint.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date of the incident or event that gave rise to the grievance.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the incident or circumstances that led to the grievance being filed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the grievance record was last updated or modified.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Detailed written response from management addressing the grievance claims and proposed resolution.']
  management_response_date date [note: 'Date management formally responded to the grievance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or internal observations related to the grievance case.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the grievance based on severity, impact, or urgency.']
  remedy_description varchar [note: 'Description of any remedies awarded to the grievant, such as back pay, reinstatement, or policy changes.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date the grievance was formally resolved or closed.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the final resolution, including any remedies, corrective actions, or settlements agreed upon.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or decision on the grievance after resolution process completion.']
  step varchar [note: 'Current step in the multi-step grievance resolution procedure as defined in the CBA.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal labor grievances filed by unionized Waste Management employees under collective bargaining agreements. Captures grievance number, filing date, grievant employee, union steward, grievance type (discipline, scheduling, safety, wage), CBA article cited, step in grievance procedure (Step 1 through arbitration), management response, resolution date, and outcome. Supports labor relations management and CBA compliance tracking.'
}

Table workforce.workers_comp_claim {
  workers_comp_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workers compensation claim record. Primary key.']
  ehs_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this workers compensation claim to the corresponding incident record in the Enviance EHS incident management system.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management facility where the injury occurred, if applicable.']
  medical_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.medical_case. Business justification: Workers comp claims and medical cases track the same workplace injury through insurance and clinical lenses. Links claim financial data to clinical treatment details for case management and return-to-']
  primary_workers_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who filed the workers compensation claim.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collection route where the injury occurred, if the incident happened during route operations.']
  adjuster_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance claims adjuster assigned to evaluate and process the claim.']
  adjuster_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the insurance claims adjuster handling the claim.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'Primary body part or region affected by the occupational injury or illness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: head|neck|back|shoulder|arm|hand|leg|foot|chest|abdomen|multiple|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claim_filed_date date [note: 'Date when the formal workers compensation claim was filed with the insurer or state authority.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique claim number assigned by the insurer or internal claims management system for tracking and reference.']
  claim_reserve_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial reserve amount set aside by the insurer to cover anticipated future costs for this claim.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the workers compensation claim indicating its processing stage.']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions implemented to prevent recurrence of similar injuries or illnesses.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this claim record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workers compensation claim record was first created in the system.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Specific location where the injury or illness occurred, such as facility name, route number, or work site address.']
  indemnity_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total indemnity or wage replacement payments made to the employee for lost time due to the injury or illness.']
  injury_date date [note: 'Date when the occupational injury or illness occurred. This is the principal business event timestamp for the claim.']
  injury_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of how the injury or illness occurred, including circumstances, location, and contributing factors.']
  injury_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity level of the injury or illness based on medical assessment and impact.']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of occupational injury or illness sustained by the employee. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: musculoskeletal|laceration|chemical_exposure|vehicle_accident|slip_fall|struck_by_object|caught_in_equipment|burn|respiratory|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurer_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier assigned by the insurance carrier for their internal claim tracking.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the workers compensation insurance carrier handling the claim.']
  lost_time_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days the employee was unable to work due to the injury or illness, excluding the day of injury.']
  lost_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the injury resulted in lost work time beyond the day of injury.']
  medical_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total medical expenses incurred for treatment of the injury or illness, including hospital, physician, therapy, and medication costs.']
  medical_facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the hospital, clinic, or medical facility where treatment was provided.']
  medical_treatment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the level of medical treatment provided for the injury or illness.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this workers compensation claim record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to the workers compensation claim.']
  osha_case_number varchar [note: 'Case number assigned to this incident in the OSHA 300 log if it is recordable.']
  osha_recordable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the injury or illness meets OSHA criteria for recordability on the OSHA 300 log.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date when the employee or supervisor reported the injury or illness to management.']
  restricted_duty_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days the employee worked on restricted or light duty due to the injury or illness.']
  restricted_duty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee was placed on restricted or light duty as a result of the injury.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date when the employee returned to full or modified duty following the injury or illness.']
  return_to_work_status varchar [note: 'Status of the employees return to work, indicating whether they resumed full duties, modified duties, or have not yet returned.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis conducted to identify underlying factors that contributed to the injury or illness.']
  total_incurred_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the workers compensation claim, including medical expenses, indemnity payments, and administrative costs.']
  treating_physician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physician or medical provider who treated the injured employee.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Names of any witnesses who observed the incident or can provide information about the injury circumstances.']

  Note: 'Tracks workers compensation claims filed by Waste Management employees for occupational injuries and illnesses. Captures claim number, injury date, injury type (musculoskeletal, laceration, chemical exposure, vehicle accident), body part affected, OSHA recordable flag, lost time days, restricted duty days, medical treatment type, claim status, insurer reference, total incurred cost, and return-to-work date. Integrates with OSHA 300 log recordkeeping and Enviance EHS incident management.'
}

Table workforce.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cost_center']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department that owns or manages this cost center.']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business division to which this cost center belongs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical facility or location associated with this cost center (e.g., landfill site, MRF, transfer station).']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the responsible manager or supervisor for this cost center.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent cost center in a hierarchical cost center structure, enabling roll-up reporting.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual or period budget allocation for this cost center in USD.']
  category varchar [note: 'Business function category that the cost center supports within waste management operations.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for closing or inactivating the cost center (e.g., \'Project Completed\', \'Facility Closed\', \'Reorganization\').']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the cost center used in financial reporting and labor allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial amounts associated with this cost center.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost center\'s purpose, scope, and operational responsibilities.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this cost center was closed or became inactive. Null for currently active cost centers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this cost center became active and available for labor and expense allocation.']
  general_ledger_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which labor and expenses for this cost center are posted.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether labor and expenses charged to this cost center are billable to external customers or contracts.']
  is_capital_project boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center represents a capital project requiring capitalization of labor and expenses.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost center record was last updated or modified.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the cost center (e.g., \'North Region Collection Operations\', \'MRF Sorting Line 2\', \'Fleet Maintenance\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the cost center for internal reference.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated profit center code for revenue-generating cost centers, used in profitability analysis.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the cost center operates (e.g., \'NE\' for Northeast, \'SW\' for Southwest).']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time and expense charges to this cost center require managerial approval before posting.']
  sap_cost_center_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this cost center in SAP ERP system for labor cost allocation and financial integration.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center indicating whether it is operational and accepting labor charges.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by its functional role within the organization.']
  workday_cost_center_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this cost center in Workday HCM system for integration and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cost_center. Referenced by cost_center_id.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_plan']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or benefits provider administering this plan. Null for self-insured plans.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number assigned by the insurance carrier for this benefit plan. Used for claims processing and carrier communication.']
  cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage when an employee experiences a qualifying event such as termination or reduction in hours.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of covered medical expenses the employee pays after meeting the deductible. For example, 20% means employee pays 20% and plan pays 80%.']
  copay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee pays for specific covered services such as office visits or prescriptions. Typical for medical and dental plans.']
  coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum benefit amount or coverage limit provided by the plan. For life insurance, this is the death benefit. For disability, this is the maximum monthly benefit.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Defines who is covered under the benefit plan: employee only, employee plus spouse, employee plus children, or full family coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount that must be met before the plan begins paying benefits. Applicable primarily to health insurance plans.']
  dependent_coverage_age_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum age at which dependent children are eligible for coverage under this benefit plan. Typically 26 years for ACA-compliant health plans.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan terminates or is no longer available for new enrollments. Null for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes effective and available for employee enrollment. Marks the beginning of the plan\'s coverage period.']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee contributes per pay period for this benefit plan. Null if contribution is percentage-based or employer-paid.']
  employee_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total premium or salary that the employee contributes for this benefit plan. Null if contribution is fixed-dollar or employer-paid.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employer contributes per pay period for this benefit plan. Used for labor cost allocation and financial reporting.']
  employer_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total premium or salary that the employer contributes for this benefit plan. Used for labor cost allocation and financial reporting.']
  enrollment_eligibility_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule or criteria defining which employees are eligible to enroll in this benefit plan, such as full-time status, job classification, or tenure requirements.']
  is_aca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this health benefit plan meets the minimum essential coverage and affordability requirements under the Affordable Care Act.']
  is_erisa_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is subject to the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) regulations and reporting requirements.']
  is_hsa_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this health plan qualifies as a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) and is eligible for Health Savings Account (HSA) contributions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  open_enrollment_end_date date [note: 'End date of the annual open enrollment period. Changes made during this period become effective at the start of the next plan year.']
  open_enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the annual open enrollment period when employees can enroll in, change, or cancel benefit plans.']
  out_of_pocket_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an employee will pay out-of-pocket for covered services in a plan year. After this limit, the plan pays 100% of covered expenses.']
  plan_administrator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plan administrator for employee inquiries and benefits administration communication.']
  plan_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department responsible for administering this benefit plan, handling enrollments, and addressing employee questions.']
  plan_administrator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the plan administrator for employee inquiries and benefits support.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of benefit plans for reporting and administration purposes. Broader classification than plan type.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the benefit plan in HR systems and employee communications. Used for plan enrollment and payroll deduction processing.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the official plan document, Summary Plan Description (SPD), or benefits guide for employee reference.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the benefit plan as displayed to employees during enrollment and on benefit statements.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or administrative comments about the benefit plan that do not fit in structured fields.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit plan indicating whether it is available for employee enrollment and active for coverage.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefit plan indicating the type of coverage or benefit provided to employees.']
  plan_year_end_date date [note: 'End date of the plan year for benefits administration. Deductibles and out-of-pocket maximums typically reset at the start of the next plan year.']
  plan_year_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the plan year for benefits administration, typically aligned with open enrollment periods and deductible reset dates.']
  total_premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total premium cost per pay period for this benefit plan, including both employer and employee contributions. Used for budgeting and cost analysis.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days an employee must wait after hire date or eligibility date before they can enroll in this benefit plan. Zero indicates immediate eligibility.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_plan. Referenced by benefit_plan_id.'
}

Table workforce.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_period']
  prior_pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pay_period (prior_pay_period_id)']
  approval_deadline_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which supervisors must approve timesheets and payroll adjustments for this pay period.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1-12) in which this pay period primarily falls.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this pay period falls (e.g., 2024).']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which paychecks or direct deposits are issued to employees for this pay period.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period was closed and locked from further time entry or changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this pay period.']
  finalized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when payroll processing was completed and paychecks were finalized for this pay period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) to which this pay period belongs.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this pay period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'The frequency at which employees are paid during this period (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly).']
  holiday_count varchar [note: 'The number of company-recognized holidays that fall within this pay period.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period is designated for payroll adjustments, corrections, or off-cycle payments (True/False).']
  is_year_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the final pay period of the calendar or fiscal year, requiring special year-end processing (True/False).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this pay period, such as special processing instructions, holidays, or exceptions.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period was opened for time entry and payroll processing.']
  payroll_system_code varchar [note: 'The code or identifier used by the payroll system (Workday HCM, SAP) to reference this pay period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the pay period (e.g., \'PP01-2024\', \'January 2024 - Period 1\').']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the pay period within the fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 1, 2, 3...26 for bi-weekly).']
  processing_batch_code varchar [note: 'The batch identifier used during payroll processing for this pay period, linking to payroll run logs.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this pay period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay period (draft, open for time entry, processing payroll, closed, finalized, archived).']
  time_entry_cutoff_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which employees must submit their time and attendance records for this pay period.']
  total_days_count varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in this pay period.']
  working_days_count varchar [note: 'The number of standard working days (excluding weekends and holidays) within this pay period.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pay_period. Referenced by pay_period_id.'
}

Table workforce.department {
  department_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for department']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code used to track and allocate labor costs, expenses, and budget for this department in the general ledger and enterprise resource planning systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this department record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used as a business identifier for the department in operational systems and reporting.']
  department_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the department by its primary functional area within the waste management organization.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the department\'s purpose, responsibilities, and scope of operations within the organization.']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the division or business unit to which this department belongs in the broader organizational structure.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this department was or will be closed, merged, or otherwise ceased operations. Null for currently active departments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this department was officially established and became operational within the organization.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the department used for official correspondence and communication.']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'The current number of filled full-time equivalent positions in the department, reflecting actual staffing levels.']
  headcount_budgeted varchar [note: 'The approved number of full-time equivalent positions allocated to the department in the current fiscal year budget.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether labor hours from this department can be directly billed to customer contracts or projects, used for revenue recognition and cost allocation.']
  is_customer_facing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this department have direct interaction with customers as part of their primary job responsibilities.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical location or facility where the department is based, such as a landfill, transfer station, materials recovery facility, or administrative office.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the department manager or head, responsible for oversight and decision-making.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this department record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the department as recognized in organizational charts and human resources systems.']
  parent_department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent department in the organizational hierarchy, enabling multi-level departmental structures. Null for top-level departments.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the department used for internal and external communication.']
  requires_cdl boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this department require employees to hold a valid Commercial Driver License for operating waste collection vehicles.']
  requires_hazwoper boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this department require HAZWOPER certification for handling hazardous waste materials and emergency response situations.']
  requires_osha_training boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this department require specific OSHA safety training certifications beyond general workplace safety.']
  safety_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the department based on the level of safety risk associated with its operations, used for determining personal protective equipment requirements, training frequency, and insurance premiums.']
  sap_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center identifier for this department in the SAP enterprise resource planning system, used for financial reporting and labor cost allocation.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard work shift pattern for employees in this department, indicating whether they work day shifts, night shifts, rotating schedules, or around-the-clock operations.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or shortened name of the department used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces where space is limited.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the department indicating whether it is actively functioning, temporarily suspended, or planned for future activation.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union representing employees in this department, if applicable. Null for non-unionized departments.']
  workday_department_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for this department in the Workday Human Capital Management system, used for integration and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for department. Referenced by department_id.'
}

Table workforce.division {
  division_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for division']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the division\'s mailing or physical address (street number and name).']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the division\'s address (suite, building, floor, or other secondary address information).']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit this division belongs to for financial and operational reporting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the division is located.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code used to track costs and expenses associated with this division. Integrates with SAP for labor cost allocation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX) where the division is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this division record was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the division in operational systems and reporting. Typically 2-10 characters.']
  division_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the manager or director of this division.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the division.']
  division_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the division based on its primary function within the organization (e.g., operational, administrative, support, regional, functional, business unit).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date this division ceased operations, was closed, or merged into another division. Null for active divisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date this division became operational or was established.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for division-level communications.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the division, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_account_code varchar [note: 'Primary general ledger account code associated with this division for financial reporting and consolidation.']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'The current number of filled full-time equivalent (FTE) positions in this division.']
  headcount_authorized varchar [note: 'The approved number of full-time equivalent (FTE) positions authorized for this division.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this division record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the division.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the division (e.g., Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM).']
  osha_establishment_id varchar [note: 'OSHA establishment identifier for this division used for regulatory reporting and compliance tracking.']
  parent_division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent division in the organizational hierarchy. Null for top-level divisions.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the division.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the division\'s address.']
  primary_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary facility or headquarters location for this division.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code used to track revenue and profitability for this division.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic region this division operates within.']
  safety_incident_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The division\'s safety incident rate per 100 full-time employees, calculated per OSHA standards.']
  service_area_description varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic service area or operational scope covered by this division (e.g., counties, municipalities, or facility types served).']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code (e.g., TX, CA, ON) where the division is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the division indicating whether it is actively operating, temporarily suspended, or permanently closed.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the division\'s primary location (e.g., America/Chicago, America/New_York).']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'The local union chapter number or identifier if employees in this division are unionized.']
  union_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this division are represented by a labor union (True/False).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for division. Referenced by division_id.'
}

Table asset.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for all fixed assets owned or leased by Waste Management including containers, stationary equipment, facility infrastructure, and administrative buildings.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Fixed_asset currently has asset_class and asset_subclass as STRING attributes, but asset_class table exists with full classification hierarchy, depreciation rules, GL accounts, and compliance requirem']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Fixed assets are physically located at facilities (landfills, MRFs, transfer stations, maintenance shops). Currently fixed_asset has plant_code STRING, but facility table exists with facility_code and']
  lifecycle_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_lifecycle_stage. Business justification: Fixed_asset has operational_status STRING (e.g., \'active\', \'inactive\', \'under_maintenance\', \'retired\'), but asset_lifecycle_stage defines standardized stages with rich metadata (stage_sequence, is_dep']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the asset. Used for accountability, maintenance coordination, and asset custody tracking.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized since the asset was placed in service. Used to calculate current book value and remaining depreciable base.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost to acquire the asset including purchase price, delivery, installation, and any other costs necessary to place the asset in service. Basis for depreciation calculations.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired by Waste Management through purchase, lease, or other means. Used for asset age calculations and lifecycle planning.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the fixed asset including make, model, capacity, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the asset. Used across SAP FI-AA, Infor EAM, and operational systems for asset tracking and reference.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity value. Must align with industry standards and operational reporting requirements.']
  capacity_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated capacity or size of the asset in appropriate units. Examples: cubic yards for containers, tons per day for processing equipment, square feet for buildings.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date the asset was placed in service and capitalized on the balance sheet. Marks the start of depreciation calculations per GAAP/IFRS standards.']
  cid varchar [note: 'Container Identification number used for tracking dumpsters, roll-offs, and carts. Unique identifier printed on container and linked to RFID tags for automated tracking.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical condition assessment of the asset based on inspection and maintenance history. Used for replacement planning and risk assessment.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost center to which the asset is assigned. Used for depreciation expense allocation and management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  current_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the asset after accumulated depreciation. Calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Updated with each depreciation run.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense. Must comply with GAAP/IFRS standards and IRS regulations for tax reporting.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was retired, sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Marks the end of the assets lifecycle and triggers final accounting entries.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was disposed of. Used for accounting treatment, tax reporting, and asset lifecycle analysis.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount received from sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal for financial reporting.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset requires environmental compliance monitoring per EPA, RCRA, or state regulations. Used for regulatory reporting and inspection scheduling.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,8) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the assets current or primary location. Used for asset tracking, route optimization, and geographic analysis.']
  gps_longitude decimal(11,8) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the assets current or primary location. Used for asset tracking, route optimization, and geographic analysis.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering the asset. Used for risk management, claims processing, and insurance compliance tracking.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection or condition assessment of the asset. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance scheduling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset master record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  lease_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract number for leased assets. Links to lease agreement master data for payment tracking and lease accounting compliance.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed physical location description of where the asset is deployed or stored. Examples: customer site address, facility name, yard location, route assignment.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured or produced the asset. Used for warranty claims, parts sourcing, and vendor management.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or designation for the asset. Used for parts compatibility, maintenance procedures, and specifications lookup.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection or condition assessment. Used for preventive maintenance planning and regulatory compliance.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership status of the asset. Determines accounting treatment, balance sheet classification, and lease accounting compliance per ASC 842 or IFRS 16.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Used in depreciation calculations to determine depreciable base.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the asset. Used for warranty tracking, parts ordering, and equipment identification.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years for depreciation purposes. Determined by asset class and regulatory guidelines per IRS Publication 946 or IFRS standards.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage expires. Used for maintenance cost planning and warranty claim eligibility.']

  Note: 'Master record for all fixed assets owned or leased by Waste Management, including containers (dumpsters, roll-offs, carts identified by CID/RFID), stationary equipment (compactors, balers, scales), facility infrastructure, and administrative buildings. Tracks asset number, description, asset class, acquisition date, capitalization date, useful life, salvage value, current book value, depreciation method, cost center, plant/location, serial number, manufacturer, model, condition rating, and operational status. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA) and Infor EAM as the authoritative SSOT for all fixed asset master data.'
}

Table asset.class {
  class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset class. Primary key for the asset class reference hierarchy.']
  parent_asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset class in the classification hierarchy, enabling multi-level asset categorization (e.g., Containers > Roll-Off Containers > 20-Yard Roll-Offs).']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the asset class aligned with Waste Management operational divisions. Containers include dumpsters, roll-offs, carts; stationary equipment includes compactors, balers, scales; infrastructure includes landfill cells, liners, caps, leachate systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|stationary_equipment|landfill_infrastructure|mrf_equipment|wte_equipment|transfer_station_equipment|building|land|vehicle_attachment|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the asset class used in SAP and financial reporting. Typically a 4-12 character alphanumeric code aligned with chart of accounts structure.']
  asset_class_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the asset class including scope, typical assets included, and business purpose.']
  asset_class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the asset class (e.g., Roll-Off Containers, Front-End Loaders, Compactors, Landfill Infrastructure, MRF Equipment).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific type within the asset category providing granular classification (e.g., Front-End Loader, Rear-End Loader, Automated Side Loader, Stationary Compactor, Baler, Weighbridge, Landfill Cell, MRF Sorting Line).']
  capitalization_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum acquisition cost required for an asset in this class to be capitalized rather than expensed, aligned with GAAP/IFRS policies and company materiality thresholds.']
  capitalization_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the capitalization threshold amount (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset class in the master data system. Active classes are available for new asset creation; deprecated classes are retained for historical assets only.']
  cost_center_default varchar [note: 'Default cost center code for assets in this class, used for management accounting and operational expense allocation in SAP CO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification for assets in this class based on operational impact of failure. Critical assets require immediate response; low criticality assets can tolerate longer downtime.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Default depreciation calculation method for assets in this class. Straight-line is most common for buildings and infrastructure; units of production may apply to equipment with measurable usage (e.g., compactors by cycle count).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this asset class configuration is no longer valid for new asset creation. Null for currently active configurations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this asset class configuration becomes effective for new asset creation and depreciation calculation.']
  energy_consumption_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether energy consumption (electricity, natural gas, diesel) is tracked at the asset class level for sustainability reporting and cost allocation.']
  environmental_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of potential environmental impact from failure or improper operation of assets in this class. High for landfill liners and leachate systems; medium for compactors and balers; low for administrative assets.']
  epa_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class must be included in EPA regulatory reporting (e.g., landfill gas collection systems, leachate treatment equipment, air emission control devices).']
  ghg_emission_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are direct sources of greenhouse gas emissions requiring monitoring and reporting (e.g., landfill gas collection systems, WTE combustion equipment, stationary engines).']
  gl_accumulated_depreciation_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger contra-asset account number for accumulated depreciation of assets in this class. Maps to SAP FI chart of accounts.']
  gl_asset_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number for capitalizing assets in this class on the balance sheet. Maps to SAP FI chart of accounts.']
  gl_depreciation_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number for recording depreciation expense for assets in this class. Maps to SAP FI chart of accounts.']
  gl_gain_loss_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number for recording gains or losses on disposal or retirement of assets in this class.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in days between required inspections for assets in this class. Null if periodic inspection is not required.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class must carry specific insurance coverage beyond general property insurance (e.g., environmental liability insurance for landfill cells, equipment breakdown insurance for WTE plants).']
  insurance_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance coverage required for assets in this class (e.g., Environmental Liability, Equipment Breakdown, Pollution Legal Liability, Property and Casualty). Null if no specific insurance required.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Default maintenance approach for assets in this class. Preventive for scheduled maintenance; predictive for sensor-monitored equipment; reactive for low-criticality items; condition-based for inspection-driven maintenance.']
  manufacturer_warranty_months varchar [note: 'Typical manufacturer warranty period in months for new assets in this class. Used for warranty tracking and maintenance planning during warranty period.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this asset class record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset class record was last modified.']
  rcra_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are subject to RCRA hazardous waste management regulations (e.g., hazardous waste storage containers, treatment equipment, TSDF infrastructure).']
  requires_gps_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class require GPS tracking devices for location monitoring. May apply to mobile equipment and high-value portable assets.']
  requires_periodic_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class are subject to mandatory periodic safety or compliance inspections (e.g., pressure vessels, lifting equipment, hazardous waste storage units).']
  requires_rfid_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this class must be equipped with RFID tags for automated tracking and inventory management. Typically true for containers (CID tracking), false for fixed infrastructure.']
  salvage_value_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected residual value as a percentage of original cost at end of useful life. Containers and equipment may have 5-15% salvage value; buildings typically higher; landfill cells near zero.']
  serialized_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual assets in this class must be tracked by unique serial number in addition to asset ID. True for high-value equipment and containers; false for bulk infrastructure.']
  useful_life_years_default varchar [note: 'Standard useful life in years applied by default when creating new assets in this class, typically the midpoint or most common value within the min-max range.']
  useful_life_years_max varchar [note: 'Maximum expected useful life in years for assets in this class, providing a range for depreciation planning based on usage intensity and maintenance quality.']
  useful_life_years_min varchar [note: 'Minimum expected useful life in years for assets in this class, used for depreciation calculation and asset lifecycle planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this asset class record.']

  Note: 'Reference classification hierarchy for fixed assets aligned with SAP asset class configuration and GAAP/IFRS capitalization policies. Defines asset categories such as containers, compactors, balers, weighing scales, landfill infrastructure, MRF equipment, WTE plant equipment, transfer station equipment, and buildings. Each class carries depreciation method defaults, useful life ranges, capitalization thresholds, GL account assignments, and RCRA/EPA reporting flags. Enables consistent depreciation calculation and regulatory asset reporting across all Waste Management facilities.'
}

Table asset.asset_container {
  asset_container_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the waste container. Primary key. This is the system-generated surrogate key that uniquely identifies each container record in the enterprise data warehouse.']
  approved_waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Containers must be approved for specific waste profiles based on material compatibility, DOT ratings, and waste characteristics. Links containers to approved waste profiles for safe deployment, regula']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Container types (front-load, rear-load, roll-off) should be classified within the asset class hierarchy for consistent depreciation rules, capitalization thresholds, and GL account mapping. Currently ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the customer account that is currently being serviced by this container. Links to customer.customer_account. Null if container is not currently assigned to a customer.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Container is a specialized type of fixed asset. Containers have acquisition_date, acquisition_cost, depreciation_method, useful_life_years, accumulated_depreciation, net_book_value, sap_asset_number, ']
  last_maintenance_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Container maintenance history tracking requires linking to the actual work order that performed the last maintenance. This supports container lifecycle management, warranty tracking, and regulatory co']
  lifecycle_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_lifecycle_stage. Business justification: Container deployment_status (e.g., \'deployed\', \'in_yard\', \'in_repair\', \'condemned\') should map to standardized asset lifecycle stages defined in asset_lifecycle_stage. This normalizes status tracking ']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the collection route that services this container. Links to collection.route. Used for route optimization and service scheduling.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the service address where this container is currently deployed. Links to customer.service_address. Null if container is not currently deployed (e.g., in inventory or maintenance).']
  service_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Containers are serviced by specific vehicles on routes. While route_id exists, direct vehicle linkage enables container-to-vehicle assignment validation, RFID read reconciliation for billing disputes,']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total depreciation expense recognized to date for this container asset in USD. Used to calculate net book value. Confidential financial data.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The original purchase price or capitalized cost of the container in USD. Used for depreciation calculations, asset valuation, and financial reporting. Confidential business financial data.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'The date the container was acquired by Waste Management, either through purchase or lease inception. Used for depreciation calculations and asset lifecycle tracking.']
  amcs_container_reference varchar [note: 'The external identifier for this container in the AMCS Platform system. Used for integration and cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data warehouse and the operational AMCS system.']
  capacity_cubic_yards decimal(6,2) [note: 'The volumetric capacity of the container measured in cubic yards. Standard sizes include 2, 4, 6, 8 cubic yards for front/rear-load dumpsters; 10, 20, 30, 40 cubic yards for roll-off boxes; and 32, 64, 96 gallons (converted to cubic yards) for residential carts. This is the principal quantitative measurement for the container resource.']
  cid varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for the container, typically an alphanumeric code printed on the container and used by field operations, customer service, and AMCS Platform for tracking. This is the operational identifier used in day-to-day business processes.']
  color varchar [note: 'The exterior color of the container, often used to designate waste stream type (e.g., blue for recycling, green for organics, black for MSW). Color coding helps drivers and customers identify the correct container for each waste stream. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: green|blue|black|gray|brown|yellow|red — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compaction_ratio decimal(4,2) [note: 'The volume reduction ratio achieved by compactor units. For example, a ratio of 4.0 means the compactor reduces waste volume by 4:1. Only applicable to compactor_unit container types. Used to calculate effective capacity and optimize service frequency.']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the container based on inspection findings. Excellent means like-new; good means minor wear; fair means functional with visible wear; poor means needs repair soon; critical means unsafe or non-functional.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the container by its operational design and collection method. Front-load dumpsters are serviced by Front End Loader (FEL) trucks, rear-load by Rear End Loader (REL) trucks, roll-offs by specialized roll-off trucks, residential carts by Automated Side Loader (ASL) trucks, and compactor units are stationary compression equipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this container record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Immutable after initial creation.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'The date this container was most recently deployed to a customer service address. Used to track deployment history and calculate time-in-service metrics.']
  gps_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the GPS tracking device installed on high-value containers (typically roll-offs and compactors) for real-time location monitoring via Geotab Fleet Telematics or similar systems.']
