  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., each, gallon, kilogram). Aligns with inventory and procurement systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|set|pair|gallon|liter|pound|kilogram|meter|foot|box|roll|sheet — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warranty_months varchar [note: 'Duration of manufacturer warranty coverage for the component in months from date of installation. Used for warranty claim tracking and cost recovery.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the person who created this BOM record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials (BOM) for complex fixed assets and stationary equipment, defining the component parts and sub-assemblies that make up each asset. Tracks parent asset, component part number, component description, quantity, unit of measure, manufacturer part number, criticality rating, lead time for replacement, and whether the component is a consumable or capital spare. Sourced from Infor EAM BOM management and SAP PM equipment BOM. Enables preventive maintenance planning, spare parts inventory management, and total cost of ownership analysis for compactors, balers, WTE boilers, and MRF sorting equipment.'
}

Table asset.rfid_tag {
  rfid_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RFID tag record. Primary key.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.container. Business justification: RFID_tag currently has container_id_cid STRING attribute storing the container CID, but container table exists with cid as a unique identifier. This should be a proper FK relationship (rfid_tag.contai']
  assigned_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the asset (container, equipment, or other trackable item) to which this RFID tag is currently attached. Null if the tag is in inventory and not yet assigned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The system user ID or employee identifier of the person who last modified this RFID tag record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  primary_rfid_installed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee who physically installed the RFID tag on the asset. Used for quality control, training assessment, and accountability.']
  replaced_by_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the new RFID tag that replaced this tag. Creates a chain of custody for container tracking history. Null if the tag is still active or was retired without replacement.']
  assigned_asset_type varchar [note: 'The category of asset to which this RFID tag is attached. Container is most common for waste management operations. Equipment includes compactors, balers, and scales. Vehicle tags are used for non-fleet tracking (fleet has separate DOT-compliant systems).']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date when this RFID tag was assigned to a specific asset (container, equipment, or other trackable item). Marks the beginning of the tag-asset relationship for tracking and billing purposes.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer batch or lot number for this RFID tag. Used for quality control, recall management, and performance analysis by production batch. If a batch shows high failure rates, all tags from that batch can be identified and replaced proactively.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RFID tag record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  encryption_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag uses encrypted communication protocols. True for tags with built-in encryption capabilities. Encryption enhances security for sensitive applications and prevents unauthorized tag cloning.']
  environmental_rating varchar [note: 'The IP (Ingress Protection) rating indicating the tags resistance to dust and water. Waste container RFID tags typically require IP67 or higher for outdoor exposure, pressure washing, and harsh environmental conditions.']
  epc_code varchar [note: 'The unique EPC identifier encoded in the RFID tag, typically a 96-bit or 128-bit hexadecimal value conforming to GS1 EPC standards. This is the actual data read from the tag during scanning operations.']
  expected_lifespan_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'The manufacturer-specified expected operational lifespan of the RFID tag in years under normal operating conditions. Passive tags typically last 10-20 years; active tags with batteries last 3-7 years. Used for replacement forecasting and total cost of ownership calculations.']
  frequency_band varchar [note: 'The radio frequency band on which the tag operates. UHF (860-960 MHz) is standard for waste container tracking due to long read range and fast read rates. Frequency band determines reader compatibility and regulatory compliance.']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date when the RFID tag was physically installed or affixed to the asset. May differ from assignment_date if the tag was pre-assigned before physical installation.']
  installation_location varchar [note: 'Describes where on the asset the RFID tag is physically mounted (e.g., front panel, lid interior, side wall, handle). Important for troubleshooting read failures and ensuring consistent tag placement across the fleet.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RFID tag record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  last_read_location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate where the RFID tag was last successfully read. Captured from GPS-enabled mobile readers on collection vehicles or fixed readers at facilities.']
  last_read_location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate where the RFID tag was last successfully read. Used in conjunction with latitude for geospatial analysis and container location tracking.']
  last_read_reader_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RFID reader device that performed the last successful read. May be a vehicle-mounted reader, handheld scanner, or fixed portal reader. Used for reader performance analysis and troubleshooting.']
  last_read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The most recent date and time when this RFID tag was successfully read by an RFID reader during a collection event, yard inventory scan, or other tracking operation. Used to identify potentially damaged or lost tags.']
  memory_capacity_bytes varchar [note: 'The total memory capacity of the RFID tag in bytes. Typical passive UHF tags have 96-512 bits (12-64 bytes). Larger memory allows storage of additional data such as service history, container specifications, or maintenance records directly on the tag.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, observations, or special handling instructions related to this RFID tag. May include installation challenges, special mounting requirements, or historical context.']
  operating_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the RFID tag is rated to operate reliably. Important for tags on containers exposed to direct sunlight, hot climates, or high-temperature waste streams.']
  operating_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the RFID tag is rated to operate reliably. Critical for tags deployed in extreme cold climates or refrigerated waste applications.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number under which this RFID tag was procured. Links to procurement records for vendor management, cost tracking, and audit trails.']
  read_failure_count varchar [note: 'The total number of attempted reads that failed for this RFID tag. High failure counts indicate potential tag damage, environmental interference, or reader issues requiring investigation.']
  read_success_count varchar [note: 'The total number of successful reads recorded for this RFID tag since installation. Used to assess tag performance, durability, and expected remaining lifespan.']
  replacement_date date [note: 'The date when this RFID tag was replaced with a new tag. Marks the end of the tags active service life. Used in conjunction with installation_date to calculate actual tag lifespan for performance analysis.']
  replacement_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the RFID tag was replaced or retired. Common reasons include physical damage, read failures, container disposal, tag upgrade, or end of service life. Used for root cause analysis and tag durability improvement.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(5,2) [note: 'The signal strength of the last successful RFID tag read, measured in dBm. Typical values range from -70 dBm (weak) to -30 dBm (strong). Declining signal strength over time may indicate tag degradation or environmental factors affecting readability.']
  tag_manufacturer varchar [note: 'The name of the company that manufactured the RFID tag hardware (e.g., Alien Technology, Impinj, Avery Dennison, Confidex). Important for warranty tracking, performance characteristics, and replacement sourcing.']
  tag_model varchar [note: 'The specific model or part number of the RFID tag. Different models have varying read ranges, durability ratings, and environmental tolerances critical for outdoor waste container applications.']
  tag_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the RFID tag. Active tags are in service and readable. Damaged tags have been identified as non-functional during read attempts. Replaced tags have been swapped out. Retired tags are decommissioned. Lost tags cannot be located. Quarantined tags are temporarily suspended pending investigation.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'The technology type of the RFID tag. Passive UHF tags are most common for container tracking in waste management, offering long read ranges (up to 40 feet) without battery power. Active tags include battery for extended range and sensor capabilities.']
  unit_cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'The purchase cost of the individual RFID tag in USD. Used for asset valuation, inventory accounting, and cost-benefit analysis of RFID tracking programs. Passive UHF tags typically cost $0.10-$2.00; active tags cost $10-$50.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the manufacturer warranty for this RFID tag expires. Used for warranty claim processing and replacement planning. Typical RFID tag warranties range from 1-5 years depending on tag type and application.']
  write_protected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag memory is write-protected (locked). True means the tag is read-only and cannot be reprogrammed. False means the tag can be rewritten. Write protection prevents accidental or malicious data modification.']

  Note: 'Master record for RFID tags assigned to containers and other trackable assets. Captures RFID tag ID (EPC code), tag type (passive UHF, active), tag manufacturer, tag status (active, damaged, replaced, retired), assignment date, asset it is attached to, and last read timestamp. Enables automated container identification during collection events, yard inventory counts, and customer billing verification. Integrates with AMCS Platform container tracking and Waste Logics customer portal for container-level service confirmation.'
}

Table asset.rfid_read_event {
  rfid_read_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each RFID tag read event captured during container service operations or facility gate transactions.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical container (dumpster, roll-off, cart) associated with the RFID tag at the time of the read event.']
  collection_stop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific route stop where the RFID read occurred. Null for non-route events such as facility gate scans.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the container at the time of the read event. Used for billing confirmation and service verification.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver operating the vehicle at the time of the RFID read. Null for stationary facility readers. Used for driver performance tracking and service accountability.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility (landfill, MRF, transfer station, maintenance shop) where the RFID read occurred. Null for mobile route-based reads.']
  rfid_tag_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier encoded in the RFID tag affixed to the container. Typically a 96-bit or 128-bit EPC (Electronic Product Code) represented as hexadecimal string.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route being executed when the RFID read occurred. Null for facility gate or yard scan events.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service address where the container was read. Used to validate that service occurred at the correct location per the service agreement.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection vehicle on which the RFID reader was mounted at the time of the read event. Null for stationary readers at facilities.']
  antenna_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific antenna on the reader device that captured the tag read. Multi-antenna readers use this to determine read direction and position.']
  billing_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this read event qualifies for billing confirmation based on read quality, location accuracy, and business rules. True if eligible, False if not.']
  duplicate_read_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this read event is a duplicate of a previous read within a defined time window (typically 60 seconds). True if duplicate, False if unique read.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFID read event indicating the business context: service confirmation during collection, yard inventory scan, facility gate entry/exit, manual handheld scan, or exception handling scan.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or anomaly detected during the read event. Empty string if no exception. Used for quality control and dispute resolution.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the exception or anomaly, if any. Null if no exception occurred. Provides context for operational review and customer service.']
  gps_accuracy_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters at the time of the read. Lower values indicate higher precision. Used to assess reliability of location data for billing disputes.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the raw RFID read event was processed and loaded into the lakehouse silver layer. Used for data lineage and latency monitoring.']
  read_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of vehicle or container movement at the time of read: inbound (entering facility), outbound (exiting facility), stationary (no movement), or unknown (direction not determinable).']
  read_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) captured by GPS at the time of the RFID read. Used for service verification and geospatial analytics.']
  read_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) captured by GPS at the time of the RFID read. Used for service verification and geospatial analytics.']
  read_quality_score varchar [note: 'Normalized quality score (0-100) calculated from signal strength, GPS accuracy, and reader diagnostics. Higher scores indicate more reliable reads suitable for billing confirmation.']
  read_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this read within the route execution or facility session. Used to reconstruct the chronological order of service events and detect duplicate reads.']
  read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the RFID tag was read by the reader device. Captured in UTC and converted to local time zone for reporting.']
  reader_device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the RFID reader device that captured the tag read. Typically mounted on collection trucks, facility gates, or yard scanners.']
  reader_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the firmware running on the RFID reader device at the time of the read. Used for troubleshooting and ensuring device compliance with operational standards.']
  service_date date [note: 'Business date (local time zone) on which the service event occurred. Used for partitioning, billing cycle alignment, and operational reporting. Derived from read_timestamp.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Received signal strength indicator (RSSI) of the RFID tag read measured in dBm. Higher values (closer to zero) indicate stronger signal. Used for read quality assessment and troubleshooting.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the read event record in the source operational system. Used for traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originated the RFID read event: AMCS Platform, Geotab Fleet Telematics, Locus Dispatch, or MANUAL for handheld scanner entries.']
  tag_memory_bank varchar [note: 'RFID tag memory bank from which the data was read: EPC (Electronic Product Code), TID (Tag Identifier), USER (user-defined data), or RESERVED (access/kill passwords).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each RFID tag read event captured during container service operations. Records read timestamp, RFID tag ID, reader device ID (mounted on collection truck or at facility gate), GPS coordinates at time of read, read signal strength, associated route and stop reference, and event type (service confirmation, yard scan, gate entry/exit). Provides the raw event stream for container service verification, billing confirmation, and chain-of-custody tracking. High-volume append-only table partitioned by read date.'
}

Table asset.gps_telemetry {
  gps_telemetry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GPS telemetry record. Primary key for time-series GPS position and telemetry data from GPS-equipped stationary assets and containers.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the asset being tracked. Links to the asset master record for the container, roll-off, portable compactor, or other GPS-equipped stationary asset.']
  accuracy_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated accuracy radius of the GPS position reading in meters. Represents the confidence interval within which the true position lies. Lower values indicate higher precision.']
  altitude_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation above sea level in meters at the time of GPS reading. Used for terrain analysis and asset placement verification in landfill cells or hilly terrain.']
  battery_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Remaining battery charge level of the GPS device as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Used for proactive device maintenance and battery replacement scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this telemetry record was first inserted into the data platform. System-generated audit field in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the GPS tracking device hardware unit. Used to identify the specific GPS tracker installed on the asset, distinct from the asset itself.']
  device_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the GPS tracking device. Active = functioning normally, Inactive = powered off or not transmitting, Maintenance = undergoing service, Fault = malfunction detected, Decommissioned = removed from service.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GPS position and telemetry data was captured by the device. Represents the real-world observation time in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the GPS device firmware at the time of transmission. Used for device management, compatibility tracking, and firmware update planning.']
  geofence_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the asset relative to its assigned geofence zone. Inside = within authorized zone, Outside = beyond authorized zone, Entering = crossing into zone, Exiting = crossing out of zone.']
  geofence_zone_code varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the geofence zone the asset is currently within. Null if asset is outside all defined geofences. Used for authorized location verification and breach detection.']
  hdop decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measure of GPS position accuracy. Lower values indicate better precision. <1 = Ideal, 1-2 = Excellent, 2-5 = Good, 5-10 = Moderate, 10-20 = Fair, >20 = Poor.']
  heading_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Compass direction of asset movement in degrees (0-360). 0/360 = North, 90 = East, 180 = South, 270 = West. Null if asset is stationary.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the GPS device was installed on the asset. Used for device age tracking, warranty management, and replacement planning.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the GPS device last underwent maintenance, battery replacement, or firmware update. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and device lifecycle tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the asset position in decimal degrees. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000. Positive values represent North, negative values represent South.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the asset position in decimal degrees. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000. Positive values represent East, negative values represent West.']
  movement_alert_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether unauthorized movement was detected for a stationary asset. True = unexpected movement detected, False = asset stationary or movement authorized. Used for theft detection.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Communication network used to transmit the telemetry data. Cellular = 3G/4G/5G mobile network, Satellite = satellite communication, WiFi = wireless local area network, LoRa = long-range low-power network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context about the telemetry reading. Used for documenting anomalies, investigation findings, or special circumstances.']
  satellite_count varchar [note: 'Number of GPS satellites the device was connected to at the time of reading. Higher count indicates better position accuracy. Minimum 3 required for 2D fix, 4 for 3D fix.']
  signal_quality varchar [note: 'Quality of the GPS satellite signal at the time of reading. Excellent = 4+ satellites with strong signal, Good = 3-4 satellites, Fair = 2-3 satellites, Poor = 1-2 satellites, No Signal = 0 satellites.']
  speed_kmh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Instantaneous speed of the asset in kilometers per hour. For stationary assets, non-zero values indicate unauthorized movement or theft. Used for tamper detection and movement alerts.']
  tamper_alert_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the GPS device detected tampering or unauthorized removal attempt. True = tamper detected, False = no tamper. Triggers security alerts and theft investigation.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature reading from the GPS device sensor in degrees Celsius. Used for device health monitoring and environmental condition tracking. Null if device does not have temperature sensor.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of what triggered this telemetry transmission. Scheduled = routine periodic update, Event Triggered = geofence breach or movement alert, On Demand = manual query, Emergency = tamper or critical alert.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this telemetry record was last modified in the data platform. System-generated audit field in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']

  Note: 'Time-series GPS position and telemetry records for GPS-equipped stationary assets and containers with active tracking devices. Captures device ID, asset ID, timestamp, latitude, longitude, altitude, geofence zone, battery level, tamper alert flag, and signal quality. Distinct from fleet telematics (which is owned by the fleet domain via Geotab) — this table covers non-rolling assets such as roll-off containers with GPS trackers, portable compactors, and temporary site equipment. Enables real-time asset location visibility, theft detection, and unauthorized movement alerts.'
}

Table asset.lease {
  lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lease contract record. Primary key.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the business unit or operating division responsible for managing this lease. Supports lease portfolio analysis by business unit.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Lease has leased_asset_category STRING describing the type of leased asset, but this should map to the asset_class hierarchy for consistent classification. Adding asset_class_id FK enables lease accou']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the asset master record in the asset management system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Links the lease contract to the specific asset instance being leased. May be null if lease covers multiple assets or asset not yet registered.']
  lessor_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Vendor master record identifier for the lessor in the SAP S/4HANA MM module. Links lease contract to vendor master for procurement and accounts payable processing.']
  classification_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease was classified as operating or finance lease per ASC 842/IFRS 16 criteria. Typically the lease commencement date or the date of initial recognition.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity that is the lessee under this lease contract. Used for financial consolidation and reporting in SAP S/4HANA FI module.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the lease contract as assigned by the lessor or internal procurement system. Referenced in SAP lease contract module and vendor communications.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center to which lease expenses are allocated for internal management accounting and cost control. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA CO module cost center hierarchy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the scanned lease contract document stored in the enterprise document management system. Provides access to the original signed lease agreement for audit and reference purposes.']
  end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the lease contract term. May be extended if renewal options are exercised. Null for perpetual or indefinite leases.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting lease expense or ROU asset amortization. Aligns with the chart of accounts in SAP S/4HANA FI module.']
  implicit_interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Implicit interest rate in the lease contract, if determinable. Used to discount lease payments to present value for ROU asset and lease liability calculation. If not determinable, the lessee\'s incremental borrowing rate is used instead.']
  incremental_borrowing_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The rate of interest that the lessee would have to pay to borrow funds on a collateralized basis over a similar term to obtain an asset of similar value. Used as the discount rate when the implicit rate is not readily determinable.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lease contract. Active indicates the lease is in force; terminated indicates early termination; expired indicates natural end of term; renewed indicates transition to a new lease term.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease as operating lease, finance lease, or capital lease per ASC 842 (US GAAP) and IFRS 16 accounting standards. Determines accounting treatment for right-of-use asset and lease liability recognition.']
  leased_asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the asset(s) covered by this lease contract. May include containers (dumpsters, roll-offs, carts), compactors, balers, scales, or other equipment used in waste collection and processing operations.']
  liability_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding lease liability balance on the balance sheet. Represents the present value of remaining lease payments. Reduced by principal portion of each lease payment and increased by interest accretion.']
  modification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent lease modification that changed the scope or consideration of the lease. Lease modifications require remeasurement of the lease liability and ROU asset per ASC 842/IFRS 16.']
  modification_description varchar [note: 'Description of the most recent lease modification, including changes to lease term, payment amounts, or scope of leased assets. Null if no modifications have occurred.']
  monthly_lease_payment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly payment amount due to the lessor under the lease contract. Excludes variable payments based on usage or performance. Denominated in the currency specified in payment_currency_code.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the lease contract. Used for capturing information not covered by structured fields.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for lease payment amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). All monetary fields in this lease record are denominated in this currency.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of lease payment obligations. Most leases are monthly; some may be quarterly or annually depending on contract terms.']
  purchase_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes an option for the lessee to purchase the leased asset at the end of the lease term or during the lease period. True if purchase option exists; False otherwise.']
  purchase_option_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed purchase price or formula for calculating the purchase price if the lessee exercises the purchase option. Null if no purchase option exists or price is not predetermined.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes an option for the lessee to renew the lease term beyond the initial end date. True if renewal option exists; False otherwise.']
  renewal_option_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed description of renewal option terms, including renewal period duration, payment adjustments, and conditions for exercising the renewal. Null if no renewal option exists.']
  rou_asset_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial right-of-use asset value recognized on the balance sheet at lease commencement per ASC 842/IFRS 16. Calculated as the present value of lease payments plus initial direct costs. Amortized over the lease term.']
  sap_lease_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document ID for the lease contract in SAP S/4HANA lease accounting module. Used for integration and reconciliation between lakehouse and SAP system of record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the lease contract. Marks the commencement of the lease term and the beginning of lease payment obligations and right-of-use asset recognition per ASC 842/IFRS 16.']
  term_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the lease contract in months from start date to end date. Used for lease accounting calculations and amortization schedules.']
  termination_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lessee has the right to terminate the lease before the scheduled end date. True if early termination option exists; False otherwise.']
  termination_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty or fee payable by the lessee if the lease is terminated early. Null if no termination penalty applies or if termination is not permitted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Master record for operating and finance leases on assets used by Waste Management, including leased containers, compactors, balers, and facility equipment. Captures lessor name, lease type (operating/finance per ASC 842/IFRS 16), lease start and end dates, monthly lease payment, right-of-use (ROU) asset value, lease liability balance, implicit interest rate, renewal options, purchase options, and SAP lease contract reference. Supports IFRS 16/ASC 842 lease accounting compliance, lease vs. buy analysis, and lease portfolio management.'
}

Table asset.warranty {
  warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset warranty record. Primary key.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset or equipment covered by this warranty. Links to the asset master record in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the warranty provider in the vendor master system. Links to SAP MM vendor master or procurement system.']
  claim_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total dollar amount that can be claimed under this warranty over its lifetime. Null indicates unlimited coverage. Critical for high-value equipment like MRF sorting systems and WTE boilers.']
  claim_limit_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the claim limit amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  claims_approved_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims that were approved and paid by the warranty provider. Used to calculate claim approval rate and assess warranty provider responsiveness.']
  claims_filed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed against this warranty to date. Used for warranty utilization tracking and vendor performance assessment.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for warranty claim submission and service coordination.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the warranty provider for claim submission and service coordination.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for warranty claim submission and service requests. Critical for emergency equipment failures at MRF and WTE facilities.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the warranty contract document stored in the document management system. May be a file path, document ID, or URL to the warranty certificate and terms.']
  cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost paid for the warranty coverage. Zero for manufacturer warranties included with asset purchase; non-zero for extended warranties and service contracts. Used for warranty ROI analysis.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the warranty cost (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  coverage_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of warranty coverage expressed in months. Standard manufacturer warranties are typically 12-36 months; extended warranties may range from 12-120 months for high-value equipment.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when warranty coverage expires. Critical for proactive warranty utilization planning and determining when assets transition to out-of-warranty maintenance.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when warranty coverage becomes effective. Typically the asset acquisition date for manufacturer warranties or the purchase date for extended warranties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warranty record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount that must be paid by Waste Management before warranty coverage applies to each claim. Zero indicates no deductible. Common for extended warranties on high-value equipment.']
  deductible_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deductible amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Specific items, conditions, or circumstances not covered by the warranty. Common exclusions include consumables, normal wear and tear, misuse, unauthorized modifications, and environmental damage from landfill leachate or corrosive waste.']
  labor_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty covers labor costs for repairs and service. True means labor is covered; false means labor must be paid separately.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this warranty record. Links to Workday HCM user master for audit purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warranty record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes about the warranty including special conditions, claim history, provider performance issues, or maintenance requirements to maintain warranty validity.']
  parts_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty covers replacement parts costs. True means parts are covered; false means parts must be purchased separately.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company or organization providing the warranty coverage. May be the original equipment manufacturer (OEM), third-party warranty provider, or service contractor.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number used to procure the extended warranty or service contract. Links to SAP MM procurement documents. Null for manufacturer warranties included with asset purchase.']
  renewal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this warranty is eligible for renewal upon expiration. True means renewal option is available; false means warranty cannot be renewed.']
  renewal_notification_date date [note: 'Date when Waste Management should be notified about warranty renewal options. Typically 60-90 days before coverage end date to allow procurement lead time.']
  service_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Guaranteed maximum time in hours for warranty provider to respond to a service request. Part of warranty Service Level Agreement (SLA). Critical for high-availability equipment like MRF sorting lines and WTE boilers.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full text or summary of warranty terms, conditions, exclusions, and limitations. Includes coverage scope, maintenance requirements, claim procedures, and exclusions (e.g., misuse, environmental damage, normal wear).']
  total_claims_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total claims value (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  total_claims_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar value of all approved warranty claims paid under this warranty. Used for warranty ROI calculation and vendor performance assessment.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be transferred to a new owner if the asset is sold or relocated. True means warranty transfers with the asset; false means warranty is non-transferable.']
  travel_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty covers technician travel and trip charges for on-site service. Particularly relevant for remote landfill sites, transfer stations, and MRF facilities.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'Unique warranty certificate or policy number issued by the warranty provider. Used for warranty claim reference and tracking.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty. Active indicates coverage is in effect; expired indicates coverage period has ended; cancelled indicates warranty was terminated early; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending activation indicates warranty purchased but not yet effective.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of warranty coverage. Manufacturer warranty is included with asset purchase; extended warranty is purchased separately; comprehensive covers parts and labor; parts-only covers replacement parts; labor-only covers service labor; service contract is ongoing maintenance agreement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this warranty record. Links to Workday HCM user master for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Warranty records for fixed assets and equipment, tracking manufacturer and extended warranty coverage. Captures warranty provider, warranty type (parts, labor, comprehensive), coverage start and end dates, warranty terms and conditions, claim limit, deductible amount, warranty contact information, and number of claims filed. Enables proactive warranty utilization before expiration, warranty claim tracking, and vendor performance assessment. Particularly relevant for high-value MRF sorting equipment, WTE boilers, compactors, and balers.'
}

Table asset.capital_project {
  capital_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capital expenditure project. Primary key.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit or division sponsoring and funding the capital project.']
  carbon_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_initiative. Business justification: Capital projects implementing carbon reduction initiatives (LFG-to-RNG systems, solar installations, fleet electrification, energy efficiency upgrades) must link to carbon_initiative to track capital ']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Capital_project has asset_class STRING attribute indicating the asset class of the asset being constructed/improved, but asset_class table exists with full classification hierarchy and accounting rule']
  contractor_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Vendor master identifier for the primary contractor executing the capital project.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary facility or site where the capital project is being executed (landfill, MRF, transfer station, WTE plant, maintenance shop).']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset master record created when the capital project is capitalized and transferred to the asset register.']
  project_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the project manager assigned to this capital project.']
  strategic_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate strategic initiative or program that this capital project supports (e.g., sustainability program, fleet modernization, digital transformation).']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the project was completed and ready for capitalization or operational handover.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenditure incurred to date on the capital project, including committed and posted costs.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when project execution commenced (first work order issued or first cost posted).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual or committee that approved the capital project budget (e.g., CFO, Board of Directors, Capital Committee).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the capital project budget and scope were formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital expenditure budget approved for the project by the investment committee or board. Represents the authorized spending limit.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date when the completed project was capitalized and transferred to fixed assets, triggering depreciation.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount committed via purchase orders and contracts but not yet invoiced or paid.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract or purchase order number governing the terms and conditions of the capital project execution with the contractor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this project (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  environmental_permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capital project requires environmental permits from EPA, state, or local authorities (e.g., air quality permit, water discharge permit, solid waste permit).']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of capital funding for the project: internal cash (retained earnings), debt financing (bonds/loans), equity (shareholder capital), grant (government/foundation), lease (capital lease arrangement), or public-private partnership.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who last modified the capital project record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was last modified.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number issued by the regulatory authority for environmental or construction permits required for the project.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required environmental or construction permits: not required, pending (application submitted), approved (permit issued), denied (application rejected), expired (permit lapsed).']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for project completion as defined in the approved project schedule.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for project execution to begin, as defined in the project plan.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the capital project (e.g., \'Landfill Cell 7 Construction\', \'MRF Baler Upgrade\').']
  project_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing important project milestones, issues, decisions, or other relevant information for audit and knowledge management.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique project number or code assigned to the capital project for external reference and tracking.']
  project_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority ranking of the capital project: critical (regulatory/safety), high (strategic), medium (operational improvement), low (discretionary).']
  project_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the capital project scope, objectives, deliverables, and expected business outcomes.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capital project: planning (pre-approval), approved (budget authorized), in_progress (active execution), on_hold (temporarily suspended), completed (construction finished), cancelled (terminated before completion), closed (financially settled and capitalized). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planning|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital project by nature of work: new construction (greenfield facility), expansion (capacity increase), rehabilitation (major repair/refurbishment), technology upgrade (system modernization), equipment replacement (asset renewal), or environmental compliance (regulatory-driven improvements).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment of the capital project considering technical complexity, regulatory exposure, schedule constraints, and financial impact.']
  sap_internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP internal order number used for cost collection and tracking for smaller capital projects not requiring full WBS structure.']
  sap_wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP WBS element identifier for complex capital projects structured with hierarchical work breakdown for cost and schedule control.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Schedule variance in days calculated as actual completion date minus planned completion date. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early completion.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget variance calculated as actual spend minus approved budget. Positive values indicate over-budget, negative values indicate under-budget.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budget variance expressed as a percentage of approved budget: (actual spend / approved budget - 1) * 100.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who created the capital project record.']

  Note: 'Tracks capital expenditure (CapEx) projects for asset construction, major improvements, and infrastructure expansion at Waste Management facilities. Captures project name, project type (new construction, expansion, rehabilitation, technology upgrade), sponsoring facility, approved budget, actual spend to date, project manager, start date, expected completion date, project status, and SAP internal order or WBS element reference. Manages the lifecycle from CapEx approval through asset capitalization, including landfill cell construction, MRF equipment upgrades, and WTE plant expansions.'
}

Table asset.capital_project_expenditure {
  capital_project_expenditure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capital project expenditure line item. Primary key.']
  budget_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the capital budget line item against which this expenditure is tracked. Enables budget vs. actual variance analysis and commitment tracking.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'The business unit or operating division responsible for this capital project expenditure. Enables segment reporting and divisional performance analysis (e.g., Residential Collection, Commercial Hauling, Landfill Operations, Recycling).']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent capital project (WBS element or internal order) against which this expenditure is posted. Links to the capital project master record tracking the overall asset construction or acquisition initiative.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'The internal cost center code that originated this expenditure for internal labor, overhead, or allocated costs. Used when the expenditure is not vendor-sourced but represents internal resource consumption charged to the capital project.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the facility or site where the capital asset is being constructed or installed. Links expenditure to the physical location (landfill, MRF, transfer station, WTE plant, maintenance shop).']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the vendor or supplier who provided the goods or services for this expenditure. Null for internal cost postings (labor, overhead allocation).']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'The asset class or category to which the capitalized expenditure will be assigned upon asset creation. Determines depreciation method, useful life, and general ledger account assignment (e.g., Buildings, Machinery, Vehicles, Landfill Infrastructure, MRF Equipment).']
  budget_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between this expenditure and the budgeted amount for the associated budget line item. Positive values indicate over-budget spending; negative values indicate under-budget. Supports project cost control and variance reporting.']
  capitalization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this expenditure is eligible for capitalization as part of the fixed asset cost basis. True indicates the cost will be capitalized; False indicates it will be expensed. Drives the asset acquisition cost calculation and financial statement treatment.']
  capitalization_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for the capitalization eligibility determination. Values include: direct_material (materials incorporated into the asset), direct_labor (labor directly attributable to asset construction), freight_delivery (transportation costs), installation (installation and commissioning), site_preparation (site work required for asset placement), engineering_design (design and engineering services), permits_fees (regulatory permits and fees), interest_during_construction (financing costs during construction period), overhead_allocation (allocated indirect costs), non_capitalizable_expense (operating expense not eligible for capitalization), maintenance_repair (routine maintenance or repair costs). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_material|direct_labor|freight_delivery|installation|site_preparation|engineering_design|permits_fees|interest_during_construction|overhead_allocation|non_capitalizable_expense|maintenance_repair — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commitment_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number representing the financial commitment (purchase requisition, purchase order, or contract) that reserved budget for this expenditure. Supports commitment accounting and funds reservation tracking.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The company code (legal entity) to which this expenditure is posted. Supports multi-entity financial consolidation and legal entity-level reporting.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'The cost element or general ledger account code to which this expenditure is classified. Represents the nature of the cost (e.g., materials, labor, equipment rental, professional services, freight). Aligns with the chart of accounts and cost accounting structure.']
  cost_element_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the cost element, providing business context for the type of expenditure (e.g., Construction Materials, Engineering Services, Equipment Purchase, Site Preparation).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this expenditure record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the expenditure amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency capital projects and foreign exchange tracking.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the originating source document (invoice date, goods receipt date, timesheet date) that triggered this expenditure posting. Used for audit trail and variance analysis between document and posting dates.']
  expenditure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of this expenditure line item in the transaction currency. Represents the gross cost posted to the capital project before any adjustments, taxes, or allocations.']
  expenditure_date date [note: 'The date on which this expenditure was posted to the capital project in the financial system. Represents the accounting date for cost recognition, distinct from the document date or goods receipt date.']
  expenditure_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier or line number for this expenditure transaction within the project accounting system. May correspond to a journal entry line, goods receipt line, or invoice line.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The goods receipt document number that confirmed physical receipt of materials or completion of services. Links expenditure to the three-way match process (PO, GR, Invoice) and inventory movements.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date on the vendor invoice. Used for payment terms calculation, aging analysis, and variance tracking between invoice date and posting date.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor invoice number associated with this expenditure. Supports accounts payable reconciliation and audit trail for payment processing.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this expenditure record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this expenditure record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about this expenditure. May include justification for capitalization decision, variance explanations, or special handling instructions.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which payment was made to the vendor for this expenditure. Supports cash flow analysis and working capital management for capital projects.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'The financial document number for the payment that settled this expenditure. Links to accounts payable payment run and cash management.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'The current posting status of this expenditure in the financial system. Values: posted (successfully posted to GL and project), pending (awaiting approval or posting), reversed (posting has been reversed), cancelled (transaction cancelled before posting), parked (saved as draft, not yet posted).']
  purchase_order_line_item varchar [note: 'The specific line item number within the purchase order that this expenditure relates to. Provides granular linkage to the ordered material or service specification.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number that authorized this expenditure. Links the expenditure to the procurement process and enables traceability to contract terms, pricing, and delivery schedules.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods or services received for this expenditure line item. Used in conjunction with unit_of_measure for materials management and unit cost analysis.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which this expenditure posting was reversed. Used for period-end reconciliation and financial statement adjustments.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this expenditure posting has been reversed (True) or is active (False). Supports audit trail and correction tracking.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the reversal transaction if this expenditure has been reversed. Provides audit trail linkage between original and reversal postings.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (sales tax, VAT, GST) associated with this expenditure. Supports tax accounting, compliance reporting, and determination of capitalizable vs. expensed tax costs.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction or tax code applied to this expenditure. Determines tax rate, tax type, and tax account assignment.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of measure for this expenditure. Calculated as expenditure_amount divided by quantity. Supports unit cost benchmarking and price variance analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA for each, TON for tons, HR for hours, M3 for cubic meters, FT for feet). Standardizes quantity reporting across diverse materials and services.']
  work_breakdown_structure_element varchar [note: 'The WBS element code within the project hierarchy to which this expenditure is assigned. Provides granular project phase or deliverable-level cost tracking (e.g., Design Phase, Site Preparation, Equipment Installation, Commissioning).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this expenditure record. Supports accountability and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Line-level expenditure records posted against capital projects, capturing individual cost transactions that accumulate toward asset capitalization. Records expenditure date, cost element, vendor or internal cost center, amount, purchase order reference, goods receipt reference, and capitalization eligibility flag. Supports project cost tracking, budget variance analysis, and the determination of total capitalized cost when the asset is placed in service. Integrates with SAP FI-CO project accounting and the procurement domain PO/GR process.'
}

Table asset.container_inventory {
  container_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the container inventory snapshot record.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Container_inventory tracks inventory by container_type and container_size, but these should map to asset_class for consistent valuation and accounting. Adding asset_class_id FK enables inventory valua']
  supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary vendor or supplier for this container type and size.']
  yard_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the yard, maintenance shop, or staging area where the container inventory is held.']
  average_daily_deployment decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average number of containers of this type and size deployed per day over the last 30 days.']
  container_size varchar [note: 'The size or capacity of the container (e.g., 2-yard, 4-yard, 6-yard, 8-yard, 10-yard, 20-yard, 30-yard, 40-yard, 64-gallon, 96-gallon).']
  container_type varchar [note: 'The type of container being inventoried (e.g., front load, rear load, roll-off, cart, compactor, recycling bin).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for inventory valuation (e.g., USD, CAD).']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between scheduled cycle counts for this container type and size.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of days the current available inventory can support based on average daily deployment rate.']
  inventory_date date [note: 'The date for which this inventory snapshot was recorded.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this inventory record (active, inactive, frozen, under review).']
  last_physical_count_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee who performed the last physical inventory count.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'The date when the last physical inventory count was performed for this container type and size at this location.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The typical lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this container type and size.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material master number or SKU for this container type and size.']
  next_scheduled_count_date date [note: 'The date when the next physical inventory count is scheduled for this container type and size.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding this inventory record.']
  quantity_available varchar [note: 'Number of containers available for immediate deployment to customer sites.']
  quantity_condemned varchar [note: 'Number of containers marked as condemned or awaiting disposal due to irreparable damage or end of useful life.']
  quantity_in_repair varchar [note: 'Number of containers currently undergoing maintenance or repair.']
  quantity_in_transit varchar [note: 'Number of containers in transit between facilities or being relocated.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total number of containers of this type and size currently in stock at the yard location.']
  quantity_reserved varchar [note: 'Number of containers reserved for scheduled deployment or customer orders.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'The minimum inventory level that triggers a reorder or procurement action for this container type and size.']
  reorder_quantity varchar [note: 'The standard quantity to order when inventory falls below the reorder point.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'The specific storage location or bin code within the yard facility where containers are stored.']
  total_inventory_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total value of the inventory on hand (quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost).']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The average cost per container unit for this type and size, used for inventory valuation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this inventory record was last updated.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'The difference between the system inventory count and the last physical count (positive or negative).']

  Note: 'Snapshot inventory records of container stock held at Waste Management yards, maintenance shops, and staging areas. Tracks yard location, container type, container size, quantity on hand, quantity reserved for deployment, quantity awaiting repair, quantity condemned, and last physical count date. Enables container inventory management, reorder planning, and deployment scheduling. Supports the AMCS Platform container pool management and Waste Logics container availability queries for customer service representatives.'
}

Table asset.condition_history {
  condition_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each asset condition history record. Primary key for the asset condition history log.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset whose condition is being tracked. Links to the asset master record in SAP PM or Infor EAM.']
  inspection_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order under which this condition assessment was performed. Links to Infor EAM or SAP PM work order records.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or technician who performed the condition assessment. Links to Workday HCM employee master data.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'The method or technique used to assess the asset condition. Distinguishes between manual inspections, automated sensor data, and predictive analytics.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations from the condition assessment. Provides detailed context for condition changes and recommended actions.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the condition assessment was performed or recorded. Principal business event timestamp for this log entry.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment based on its business purpose. Supports compliance tracking and maintenance strategy analysis.']
  compliance_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment was performed to satisfy regulatory compliance requirements (EPA, OSHA, DOT, state environmental agencies).']
  condition_change_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or root cause for the change in asset condition. Captures factors such as wear and tear, environmental exposure, accident damage, or maintenance improvements.']
  condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric condition score on a standardized scale (typically 0-100) representing asset health. Enables quantitative trend analysis and predictive maintenance modeling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this condition history record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  critical_defect_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any critical or safety-related defects were identified during this assessment. Triggers immediate corrective action workflows.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated repair cost. Supports multi-currency financial reporting.']
  current_condition_grade varchar [note: 'The new condition rating assigned during this assessment. Used for asset health monitoring and replacement prioritization.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Number of defects or issues identified during this condition assessment. Quantifies asset health problems for prioritization and trend analysis.']
  document_attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents (inspection reports, test results, certifications) attached to this condition assessment.']
  environmental_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of environmental risk posed by the asset in its current condition. Critical for landfill liners, leachate systems, and hazardous waste containers.']
  estimated_remaining_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of years the asset can continue to operate in its current condition before replacement is required. Supports capital planning and budgeting.']
  estimated_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to repair or remediate the identified condition issues. Supports maintenance budgeting and repair-versus-replace analysis.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate where the condition assessment was performed. Supports field inspection verification and mobile workforce tracking.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate where the condition assessment was performed. Supports field inspection verification and mobile workforce tracking.']
  inspection_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or document identifier for the inspection report or assessment record. Supports traceability to source documentation.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the technician or inspector who recorded the condition change. Provides human-readable reference for audit and accountability.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this condition history record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this condition history record was last modified. Tracks updates and corrections to assessment data.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next condition assessment based on inspection frequency requirements and asset criticality. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  operational_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of how the current asset condition affects operational performance and service delivery. Supports asset replacement prioritization.']
  photo_attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of photos or images attached to this condition assessment record. Supports visual documentation of asset condition.']
  previous_condition_grade varchar [note: 'The condition rating of the asset immediately before this assessment. Enables tracking of condition degradation or improvement over time.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'The recommended maintenance or lifecycle action based on this condition assessment. Drives work order generation and capital planning decisions.']
  recommended_action_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for the recommended action. Determines scheduling and resource allocation for corrective maintenance.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or authority for which this compliance inspection was performed (e.g., EPA, OSHA, state environmental department).']
  safety_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of safety risk posed by the asset in its current condition. Aligns with OSHA and EPA safety compliance requirements.']
  sensor_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the IoT sensor or monitoring device that captured automated condition data. Applicable for sensor-based assessments on compactors, scales, and facility equipment.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'Source system or application that recorded this condition assessment. Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this condition history record. Supports audit trail and data quality accountability.']

  Note: 'Historical log of condition rating changes for fixed assets over their lifecycle, providing a longitudinal view of asset health degradation or improvement. Each record captures the assessment date, previous condition grade, new condition grade, assessment method (inspection, sensor, visual), technician or system that recorded the change, and any associated work order or inspection reference. Enables predictive maintenance modeling, asset replacement prioritization, and lifecycle cost analysis for containers, compactors, and facility equipment.'
}

Table asset.utilization {
  utilization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset utilization measurement record.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit that owns or operates the asset. Used for organizational reporting and asset portfolio analysis across divisions.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility where the asset is located during the measurement period. Links to landfill sites, MRF facilities, transfer stations, WTE plants, or maintenance shops.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset being measured. Links to the asset master record in SAP PM or Infor EAM.']
  actual_throughput_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the actual throughput value. Must be compatible with the rated capacity unit for utilization rate calculation.']
  actual_throughput_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual measured throughput or usage during the measurement period. For containers, this is average fill level. For compactors and balers, this is actual tons processed. For scales, this is actual weighing transactions completed.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'The category of fixed asset being measured. Determines which utilization metrics are applicable (e.g., containers track fill level, compactors track operating hours and cycles, scales track transaction count). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|compactor|baler|scale|transfer_station|landfill_cell|mrf_equipment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  average_fill_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average fill level percentage for containers during the measurement period. Calculated from RFID sensor readings or visual inspection data. Used to optimize service frequency and identify oversized or undersized containers.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center responsible for the asset during the measurement period. Used for financial allocation of asset utilization costs and ROI analysis. Sourced from SAP FI/CO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this utilization measurement record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  cycles_completed varchar [note: 'Total number of operational cycles completed during the measurement period. Applicable to compactors (compaction cycles) and balers (baling cycles). Captured from equipment control systems or manual logs.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or method used to capture the utilization measurement. Identifies whether data came from automated sensors (RFID, GPS), manual entry, or integrated systems (AMCS, Infor EAM, SAP PM). Critical for data quality assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rfid_sensor|gps_telematics|manual_entry|amcs_platform|infor_eam|sap_pm|scale_system|equipment_controller — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  idle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the asset was idle (not used or serviced) during the measurement period. Critical metric for container rationalization and redeployment decisions.']
  idle_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total idle hours (non-operating time) during the measurement period. For containers, this represents days without service. For equipment, this is available hours minus operating hours. Used to identify underutilized assets for redeployment.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'The method used to obtain the utilization measurement. Automated sensor indicates RFID or IoT device readings, manual inspection indicates visual assessment, system calculated indicates derived metrics from operational systems.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the utilization measurement period. Defines the conclusion of the observation window for calculating utilization metrics.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the utilization measurement period. Defines the beginning of the observation window for calculating utilization metrics.']
  measurement_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Data quality score for the utilization measurement, ranging from 0.00 to 1.00. Higher scores indicate more reliable data. Factors include data source reliability, completeness, and timeliness. Used to weight measurements in analytics.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the utilization measurement was captured or recorded. For sensor-based measurements, this is the sensor reading timestamp. For manual entries, this is the data entry timestamp.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system account that last modified this utilization measurement record. Used for change tracking and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this utilization measurement record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about the utilization measurement. May include explanations for anomalies, special circumstances affecting utilization, or operational observations.']
  operating_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total operating hours recorded for the asset during the measurement period. Applicable to compactors, balers, and other powered equipment. Captured from equipment hour meters or telematics systems.']
  peak_fill_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum fill level percentage recorded during the measurement period. Indicates whether the container size is adequate for the customer\'s waste generation rate.']
  rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated utilization rate expressed as a percentage of rated capacity. For containers, this is average fill level percentage. For equipment, this is operating hours divided by available hours. Used for asset rationalization and redeployment decisions.']
  rated_capacity_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the rated capacity value. Varies by asset type: cubic yards for containers, tons per day for processing equipment, transactions per day for scales, operating hours for machinery.']
  rated_capacity_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum rated capacity of the asset during the measurement period. For containers, this is volume in cubic yards. For compactors and balers, this is throughput in tons per day (TPD). For scales, this is maximum weighing transactions per day.']
  redeployment_candidate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is a candidate for redeployment based on utilization metrics. True if asset is underutilized at current location and could be more effectively deployed elsewhere.']
  roi_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this utilization measurement has significant impact on asset ROI analysis. True if utilization is outside acceptable thresholds and requires management attention for redeployment or disposal decisions.']
  service_frequency_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the asset was serviced during the measurement period. For containers, this is the number of pickups. For equipment, this is the number of maintenance or operational service events.']
  total_weight_processed_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight in tons processed through the asset during the measurement period. Applicable to compactors, balers, scales, and processing equipment. Sourced from weight tickets and equipment logs.']
  utilization_status varchar [note: 'Categorical assessment of the asset\'s utilization level. Optimal indicates efficient use, underutilized flags candidates for redeployment, overutilized indicates need for additional capacity, idle indicates no usage, out_of_service indicates asset is unavailable.']
  weighing_transactions_count varchar [note: 'Total number of weighing transactions processed by a scale during the measurement period. Key utilization metric for landfill scales, transfer station scales, and MRF scales.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system account that created this utilization measurement record. Used for data lineage and accountability.']

  Note: 'Periodic utilization measurement records for fixed assets, capturing how intensively each asset is being used relative to its rated capacity.
