For containers, tracks fill level percentage, service frequency, and idle days. For compactors and balers, tracks operating hours, cycles per day, and throughput (tons). For scales, tracks weighing transactions per day. Captures measurement period, utilization rate, rated capacity, actual throughput, idle time, and data source (sensor, manual entry, AMCS). Supports asset rationalization, redeployment decisions, and ROI analysis.'
}

Table asset.impairment {
  impairment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset impairment assessment record. Primary key.']
  class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Asset_impairment has asset_class STRING for reporting impairment losses by asset class (required for financial statement disclosures under ASC 360 / IAS 36). Adding asset_class_id FK enables asset-cla']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility where the impaired asset is located. Particularly relevant for landfill sites approaching capacity limits or WTE plants subject to regulatory changes.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset being assessed for impairment. Links to the asset master record in SAP S/4HANA or Infor EAM.']
  appraisal_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the external appraisal report supporting the impairment assessment.']
  appraiser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the independent appraiser or valuation firm that performed the asset valuation, if an external appraisal was obtained.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the impairment assessment was formally approved by the approving authority.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the executive or authority who approved the impairment assessment and write-down. Typically CFO or Controller for material impairments.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation, work papers, and audit trail materials that substantiate the impairment assessment for internal and external audit purposes.']
  carrying_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net book value of the asset immediately before the impairment loss is recognized, calculated as acquisition cost less accumulated depreciation.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA) to which this impairment belongs.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center responsible for the impaired asset, used for management accounting and cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this impairment record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this impairment record.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount rate applied in calculating the present value of future cash flows for value-in-use calculations, expressed as a percentage.']
  environmental_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an environmental incident (e.g., leachate release, air quality violation, groundwater contamination) contributed to the impairment assessment.']
  fair_value_less_costs_to_sell decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated market value of the asset less the costs that would be incurred to dispose of it. Used in determining recoverable amount under IFRS.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the impairment loss is recognized.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the impairment loss is recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The effective date on which the impairment loss was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.']
  gl_posting_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number in the general ledger system (SAP FI) that records the impairment loss journal entry.']
  impairment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the impairment assessment, typically generated by the financial system for tracking and audit purposes.']
  impairment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the impairment assessment in the approval and posting workflow.']
  impairment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of whether the impairment is permanent (not reversible) or potentially reversible under future conditions. GAAP generally prohibits reversal; IFRS allows reversal under certain conditions.']
  landfill_remaining_capacity_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'For landfill assets, the remaining permitted capacity in tons at the time of impairment assessment. Approaching capacity limits is a common impairment trigger for landfill infrastructure.']
  loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount by which the carrying amount exceeds the recoverable amount. This is the write-down amount recognized in the financial statements.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this impairment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this impairment record was last modified in the data platform.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or context regarding the impairment assessment, including management judgment, assumptions, and future considerations.']
  recoverable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The higher of the asset\'s fair value less costs to sell or its value in use. This is the amount the asset is expected to recover through use or sale.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific regulatory changes, permit modifications, or compliance requirements that triggered or contributed to the impairment (e.g., EPA permit restrictions, RCRA changes, state environmental regulations).']
  reversal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of impairment loss reversed, if applicable under IFRS. Limited to the amount that would restore the asset to its depreciated historical cost.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which the impairment loss was reversed, if applicable under IFRS. Null if no reversal has occurred.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this impairment has been subsequently reversed. Applicable under IFRS IAS 36; not permitted under GAAP ASC 360.']
  sox_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the impairment loss is material for SOX financial reporting and internal control purposes, requiring enhanced documentation and executive certification.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which the impairment assessment was performed. This is the principal business event timestamp for the impairment evaluation.']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'The business event or condition that triggered the impairment assessment. Common triggers include market decline, physical damage, regulatory changes, technology obsolescence, landfill capacity limits, or environmental incidents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: market_decline|physical_damage|regulatory_change|technology_obsolescence|capacity_limit|operational_closure|environmental_incident — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific circumstances that triggered the impairment assessment, including relevant facts, dates, and business context.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to determine the recoverable amount or fair value of the asset for impairment testing purposes.']
  value_in_use decimal(18,2) [note: 'The present value of future cash flows expected to be derived from the asset through continued use and eventual disposal. Used in determining recoverable amount under IFRS.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this impairment record.']

  Note: 'Records formal asset impairment assessments and write-downs performed under GAAP ASC 360 or IFRS IAS 36. Captures impairment test date, triggering event (market decline, physical damage, regulatory change, technology obsolescence), carrying amount before impairment, recoverable amount, impairment loss recognized, GL posting reference, approving authority, and whether the impairment is permanent or potentially reversible. Particularly relevant for landfill assets approaching permitted capacity limits and WTE plant equipment subject to regulatory changes.'
}

Table asset.asset_insurance {
  asset_insurance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset insurance coverage record. Primary key.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset covered under this insurance policy. Links to the asset master record in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  additional_insured_parties varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional parties named as insured under this policy. May include lessors, contractors, or municipalities requiring coverage.']
  appraisal_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the appraisal report. Links to document management system for detailed valuation documentation.']
  appraiser_name varchar [note: 'The name of the certified appraiser or appraisal firm that conducted the last valuation. Used for audit trail and credibility assessment.']
  broker_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the insurance broker. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  broker_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the insurance broker. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  broker_name varchar [note: 'The name of the insurance broker or agent who arranged this coverage. Used for policy servicing and renewal negotiations.']
  business_interruption_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy includes business interruption coverage for lost revenue if the asset is damaged and operations are disrupted. Critical for WTE plants, MRF facilities, and landfill operations.']
  certificate_of_insurance_issued_date date [note: 'The date when the certificate of insurance was issued. Used to verify current coverage for contract compliance and vendor requirements.']
  claims_count varchar [note: 'The total number of insurance claims filed against this policy for this asset. Used for loss history analysis and premium negotiation.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of loss that the insured must bear if the asset is underinsured. Typically 80% or 90% coinsurance clause requiring asset to be insured to that percentage of value.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'The date when insurance coverage expires for this asset. Triggers renewal workflows and coverage gap alerts.']
  coverage_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount the insurance policy will pay for a covered loss. May differ from insured value due to policy caps or sub-limits.']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'The date when insurance coverage becomes effective for this asset. Used to determine coverage eligibility for claims.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the insurance coverage. Active indicates coverage is in force; expired indicates coverage has lapsed; cancelled indicates policy was terminated; pending renewal indicates coverage is up for renewal; suspended indicates temporary hold.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'The type of insurance coverage provided. Property covers physical damage to assets; equipment breakdown covers mechanical/electrical failure; inland marine covers mobile equipment and goods in transit; general liability covers third-party claims; environmental liability covers pollution incidents; business interruption covers lost revenue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance coverage record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this insurance record. Waste Management primarily operates in USD with some CAD and MXN operations.']
  deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deductible amount that must be paid out-of-pocket before insurance coverage applies. Used in claims processing and financial planning.']
  environmental_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy includes environmental liability coverage for pollution incidents, contamination, or remediation costs. Essential for landfills, hazardous waste facilities, and transfer stations.']
  insured_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The declared value of the asset covered under the insurance policy. Used to determine coverage limits and premium calculations. Represents replacement cost or agreed value.']
  insurer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person at the insurance carrier for policy inquiries and claims.']
  insurer_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the insurance carrier contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the insurance company providing coverage for this asset.']
  insurer_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the insurance carrier contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  last_appraisal_date date [note: 'The date when the asset was last appraised to determine insured value. Used to track valuation currency and trigger reappraisal workflows.']
  loss_payee_name varchar [note: 'The name of the entity designated to receive insurance proceeds in the event of a loss. Typically the asset owner or lender with a security interest.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this insurance coverage record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_appraisal_due_date date [note: 'The date when the next asset appraisal is due. Ensures insured values remain current and aligned with replacement costs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, exclusions, or coverage details not captured in structured fields.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The unique policy number assigned by the insurance carrier. Used for claims processing and policy lookup.']
  policy_renewal_date date [note: 'The date when the insurance policy is scheduled for renewal. Triggers renewal workflows and premium renegotiation processes.']
  premium_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to allocate premium costs to this specific asset. Direct assigns full premium to one asset; proportional allocates based on insured value; usage-based allocates by utilization; blanket covers multiple assets under one policy.']
  premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total premium amount paid for this insurance coverage. May be annual, semi-annual, or allocated portion for this specific asset.']
  premium_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which insurance premiums are paid. Used for cash flow planning and accounts payable scheduling.']
  renewal_notice_sent_date date [note: 'The date when renewal notice was sent to stakeholders. Used to track renewal workflow progress and ensure timely policy continuation.']
  replacement_cost_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the policy provides replacement cost coverage (true) or actual cash value coverage (false). Replacement cost pays to replace with new; actual cash value deducts depreciation.']
  risk_assessment_rating varchar [note: 'The risk rating assigned to this asset by the insurer or internal risk management. Influences premium rates and coverage terms. High-risk assets include WTE boilers, hazardous waste equipment, and landfill gas systems.']
  total_claims_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount paid by the insurer for all claims on this asset. Used for loss ratio analysis and risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Insurance policy coverage records for fixed assets, tracking which assets are covered under which insurance policies. Captures policy number, insurer name, coverage type (property, equipment breakdown, inland marine), coverage start and end dates, insured value, deductible, premium allocation, and last appraisal date. Enables insurance renewal management, coverage gap analysis, and claims processing support. Particularly important for high-value assets such as WTE boilers, MRF sorting lines, landfill liner systems, and large compactor fleets.'
}

Table asset.lifecycle_stage {
  lifecycle_stage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset lifecycle stage reference record. Primary key for the asset lifecycle stage table.']
  allowed_source_stages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of lifecycle stage codes from which an asset can transition INTO this stage. Defines valid state machine transitions. Empty for initial stage (Ordered). Used for lifecycle transition validation.']
  allowed_target_stages varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of lifecycle stage codes to which an asset can transition FROM this stage. Defines valid state machine transitions. Empty for terminal stage (Retired). Used for lifecycle transition validation.']
  amcs_deployment_status varchar [note: 'Corresponding container deployment status in AMCS Platform for container assets (dumpsters, roll-offs, carts). Maps lifecycle stage to AMCS container tracking status. Null for non-container assets.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether management approval is required before an asset can transition into this lifecycle stage. True for Condemned and Retired stages due to financial impact; False for routine operational transitions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle stage reference record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display order of lifecycle stages in user interface dropdowns, reports, and dashboards. Lower numbers appear first. Allows custom ordering independent of stage_sequence.']
  disposal_method_applicable varchar [note: 'Indicates the typical disposal method applicable when assets reach this lifecycle stage. Only populated for terminal stages (Condemned, Retired). Used to guide asset disposition process and environmental compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sale|scrap|donation|landfill|recycle|transfer|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  eam_status_mapping varchar [note: 'Corresponding asset status code in Infor EAM system. Enables synchronization of lifecycle stage between Waste Management\'s enterprise data model and Infor EAM operational system. Used for bidirectional integration.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this lifecycle stage definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active stage definitions. Supports temporal versioning and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this lifecycle stage definition became active and available for use in asset management systems. Supports temporal versioning of lifecycle stage definitions.']
  environmental_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage are subject to active environmental compliance monitoring and reporting requirements (EPA, RCRA, air/water permits). True for In-Service stage at regulated facilities; False for non-operational stages.']
  gl_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transitions into or out of this lifecycle stage trigger General Ledger postings in SAP FI. True for capitalization (Received), disposal (Retired), and impairment (Condemned) stages; False for operational transitions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle stage definition is currently active and available for assignment to assets. False for deprecated or retired stage definitions. Used for reference data filtering.']
  is_active_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle stage represents an active operational state where the asset is available for use or deployment. True for In-Service and Idle stages; False for all others.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage can be billed to customers for service. True for In-Service stage when asset is deployed at customer location; False for all other stages including maintenance, transit, and disposal.']
  is_depreciable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage are subject to depreciation calculations. True for capitalized stages (Received, In-Service, Idle, Under-Repair); False for pre-capitalization (Ordered, In-Transit) and post-disposal (Condemned, Retired) stages.']
  is_insurable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage require active insurance coverage. True for stages where asset has value and risk exposure (In-Transit, Received, In-Service, Idle, Under-Repair); False for pre-acquisition and post-disposal stages.']
  is_terminal_stage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a terminal lifecycle stage from which no further transitions are allowed. True only for Retired stage; False for all others. Used for lifecycle completion validation.']
  maintenance_strategy_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether preventive maintenance (PM) strategies and work orders should be active for assets in this lifecycle stage. True for In-Service and Idle stages; False for all others. Drives PM scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or business rules related to this lifecycle stage. Used for documentation and knowledge management.']
  notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated notifications should be sent to stakeholders when an asset transitions into this lifecycle stage. True for exception stages (Under-Repair, Condemned) and terminal stages (Retired); False for routine operational transitions.']
  requires_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage require periodic safety and compliance inspections. True for In-Service and Idle stages; False for non-operational stages. Drives inspection scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  requires_location_tracking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this lifecycle stage require active GPS or RFID location tracking. True for In-Service, Idle, and In-Transit stages; False for stationary stages (Received, Under-Repair) and disposal stages.']
  sap_system_status varchar [note: 'Corresponding system status code in SAP Asset Accounting (FI-AA) module. Maps lifecycle stage to SAP\'s asset master status field. Used for SAP integration and financial reporting alignment.']
  sla_duration_days varchar [note: 'Target maximum number of days an asset should remain in this lifecycle stage before transitioning to the next stage. Used for lifecycle aging analysis and exception reporting. Null for stages without time constraints (e.g., In-Service).']
  sla_warning_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before SLA breach when warning alerts should be triggered for assets remaining in this stage. Typically 70-80% of sla_duration_days. Used for proactive lifecycle management.']
  stage_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of lifecycle stages into major phases: acquisition (ordered, in-transit, received), operational (in-service, idle), maintenance (under-repair), disposal (condemned, retired). Used for lifecycle analytics and reporting.']
  stage_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the lifecycle stage (e.g., ORD, INTRANS, RECV, INSVC, IDLE, REPAIR, COND, RET). Used as a business key for system integration across SAP FI-AA, Infor EAM, and AMCS Platform.']
  stage_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the lifecycle stage, including business meaning, typical duration, and operational implications. Provides context for asset managers and system users.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the lifecycle stage (e.g., Ordered, In-Transit, Received, In-Service, Idle, Under-Repair, Condemned, Retired). Primary display label for user interfaces and reports.']
  stage_owner_role varchar [note: 'Business role or department responsible for managing assets in this lifecycle stage (e.g., Procurement for Ordered, Operations for In-Service, Maintenance for Under-Repair, Finance for Retired). Used for workflow routing and accountability.']
  stage_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of lifecycle stages representing typical progression through asset lifecycle. Lower numbers indicate earlier stages. Used for lifecycle progression reporting and validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifecycle stage reference record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  utilization_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether asset utilization metrics (hours operated, cycles completed, tonnage processed) should be tracked for assets in this lifecycle stage. True for In-Service stage; False for all others. Drives utilization reporting and ROI analysis.']

  Note: 'Reference table defining the standardized lifecycle stages for Waste Management fixed assets, from acquisition through retirement. Stages include: Ordered, In-Transit, Received, In-Service, Idle, Under-Repair, Condemned, Retired. Each stage carries a description, allowed transitions (state machine rules), SLA for stage duration, and whether the asset is billable to customers while in this stage. Enables consistent lifecycle status management across SAP FI-AA, Infor EAM, and AMCS Platform, and supports asset aging and lifecycle reporting.'
}

Table asset.facility_access_authorization {
  facility_access_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this customer-facility access authorization record. Primary key.']
  account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer account that has been granted facility access']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to customer_account. Links this authorization to the specific customer account.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Waste Management employee or system user who granted this facility access authorization. Used for audit trail and authorization governance.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to facility. Links this authorization to the specific waste management facility.']
  access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Specific restrictions or conditions on this customer\'s access to this facility (e.g., \'hazardous waste only\', \'weekdays only\', \'max 5 tons per delivery\', \'requires escort\'). Null if no restrictions apply.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was granted access authorization to this facility. Establishes the start of the customer-facility relationship.']
  authorization_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this facility access authorization expires, if applicable. Null for indefinite authorizations. Used for temporary access grants or contract-bound authorizations.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the facility access authorization. Active = customer can currently use facility; Suspended = temporarily blocked; Revoked = permanently removed; Expired = authorization period ended.']
  delivery_count varchar [note: 'Total number of waste deliveries from this customer account to this facility since authorization. Incremented by delivery transaction systems. Used for customer-facility relationship analytics.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent waste delivery from this customer account to this facility. Updated by delivery transaction systems. Used to identify inactive authorizations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this customer-facility authorization, including special arrangements, historical context, or operational instructions.']
  preferred_facility_rank varchar [note: 'Ranking of this facility in the customer\'s preference order (1 = most preferred). Used by routing systems to optimize delivery assignments when multiple authorized facilities are available.']
  tipping_fee_override decimal(10,2) [note: 'Customer-specific tipping fee per ton for deliveries to this facility, overriding the facility\'s standard rate. Null if standard facility rate applies. Used for negotiated pricing agreements with large commercial/industrial customers.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the authorization and preference relationship between customer accounts and waste management facilities. It captures which facilities a customer is authorized to use, their preference ranking, facility-specific pricing overrides, access restrictions, and delivery history. Each record links one customer account to one facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this customer-facility relationship.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, large commercial and industrial customer accounts are authorized to deliver waste to multiple facilities based on waste type, geographic proximity, and facility capacity. Each facility serves thousands of customer accounts. The business actively manages facility access authorizations as operational records, tracking customer-specific preferences, pricing overrides, access restrictions, and delivery history for each customer-facility pair.'
}

Table asset.facility_safety_program_implementation {
  facility_safety_program_implementation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-program implementation record. Primary key.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the facility where the safety program is implemented']
  program_coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who coordinates this specific safety program at this facility (facility-level coordinator, distinct from the enterprise program owner). Responsible for local training, compliance, and recordkeeping.']
  program_safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the safety program being implemented at the facility']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the safety program being implemented at this facility.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'The certification or maturity level achieved by this facility for this safety program. Reflects depth of implementation, training completion rates, and compliance history.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this safety program implementation at this facility. Indicates whether the facility meets all requirements of the program (training, equipment, recordkeeping, audits).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this implementation record was created in the system.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility has been granted an exemption from this safety program due to operational characteristics (e.g., administrative office exempt from confined space program).']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why this facility is exempt from this safety program, if exemption_flag is true. Documents regulatory or operational justification.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'The date on which this safety program was deployed and became operational at this specific facility. Tracks facility-specific rollout timeline.']
  implementation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting facility-specific implementation details, challenges, customizations, or special considerations for this safety program at this location.']
  incident_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents related to this program at this facility in the current calendar year. Key performance indicator for program effectiveness.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which this safety program was audited at this specific facility. Facility-level audit date, distinct from enterprise program review.']
  last_incident_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which a safety incident related to this program occurred at this facility. Used for risk assessment and program effectiveness tracking.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this implementation record was last modified.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance audit of this safety program at this facility. Used for audit planning and compliance tracking.']
  training_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of required employees at this facility who have completed training for this safety program. Used to track training compliance at the facility level.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the implementation and compliance tracking of safety programs at specific facilities. It captures the operational deployment of OSHA-mandated and internal safety programs (LOTO, confined space, hazmat handling, etc.) at each landfill, MRF, transfer station, or TSDF. Each record links one facility to one safety program with implementation-specific attributes including deployment dates, facility-level coordinators, compliance status, audit results, and certification levels that exist only in the context of this facility-program relationship.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management operations, each facility (landfill, MRF, transfer station, TSDF) must implement multiple safety programs (LOTO, confined space, hazmat handling, respiratory protection, etc.) based on the operations conducted at that site. Conversely, each safety program is deployed across multiple facilities where applicable. The business actively manages facility-specific implementation with deployment dates, facility-level coordinators, compliance status tracking, audit schedules, and certification levels that are unique to each facility-program combination.'
}

Table asset.safety_compliance {
  safety_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-safety program compliance record. Primary key.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fixed asset enrolled in the safety program']
  last_inspection_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the most recent compliance inspection or audit for this asset-program combination.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for ensuring this asset remains compliant with this safety program. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the safety program in which the asset is enrolled']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the certification or qualification for this asset under this safety program (e.g., LOTO certification for a compactor, confined space certification for a tank). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific asset within this specific safety program. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when this asset was enrolled into this safety program. Tracks the start of the compliance relationship.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'If compliance_status is Exempt, the documented reason why this asset is exempt from this safety program.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit performed for this asset under this safety program. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit of this asset under this safety program. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding this asset\'s compliance with this safety program, including corrective actions, findings, or special conditions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between fixed assets and safety programs. It captures the enrollment of specific assets (containers, compactors, vehicles, facility equipment) into formal OSHA-mandated and internal safety programs, tracking program-specific compliance status, audit history, certifications, and responsible personnel for each asset-program combination. Each record links one fixed asset to one safety program with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance relationship.. Existence Justification: In waste management operations, fixed assets (compactors, balers, containers, vehicles, confined space equipment) must be enrolled in multiple OSHA-mandated and internal safety programs simultaneously (LOTO, Confined Space Entry, Fall Protection, Hazmat Handling, Respiratory Protection). Each safety program applies to multiple assets across facilities. The business actively manages compliance status, audit schedules, certifications, and responsible personnel for each asset-program combination as a distinct operational record.'
}

Table asset.inspection_checklist {
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection_checklist']
  parent_inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_checklist (parent_inspection_checklist_id)']
  applicable_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions, states, or jurisdictions where this inspection checklist is applicable.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the authorized person who approved this inspection checklist for operational use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection checklist was formally approved for use.']
  asset_subtype varchar [note: 'Specific subtype or category of asset within the asset type classification (e.g., roll-off, front-load, compactor for containers).']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Type of asset this inspection checklist applies to within the waste management infrastructure.']
  checklist_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the inspection checklist used across systems and documentation.']
  checklist_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the inspection checklist including its objectives, scope, and application context.']
  checklist_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the inspection checklist describing its purpose and scope.']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the inspection checklist to track revisions and updates over time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection checklist record was first created in the system.']
  critical_items varchar [note: 'Number of items in the checklist that are classified as critical for safety or compliance purposes.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or file path to the detailed inspection checklist document or form template.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this inspection checklist version becomes active and available for use.']
  escalation_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of failed critical items that triggers automatic escalation to management or safety personnel.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected time in minutes required to complete an inspection using this checklist.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this inspection checklist version is no longer valid or has been superseded by a newer version.']
  failure_action_required varchar [note: 'Mandatory action to be taken when an inspection item fails or does not meet acceptance criteria.']
  inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Recommended or required frequency for performing inspections using this checklist.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection this checklist supports (preventive maintenance, safety audit, regulatory compliance, etc.).']
  integration_system varchar [note: 'Enterprise asset management or maintenance system that this checklist integrates with for work order generation and tracking.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language of the checklist content.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who most recently updated this inspection checklist.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection checklist record was last updated in the system.']
  mobile_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist is available for use on mobile devices or field tablets.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review and update of this inspection checklist.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, instructions, or contextual information about the inspection checklist usage and application.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional area responsible for maintaining and updating this inspection checklist.']
  photo_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether photographic documentation is required as part of the inspection process.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, code section, or standard citation that requires this inspection checklist.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or authority that mandates this inspection if applicable (e.g., EPA, OSHA, state environmental agency).']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection checklist is mandated by regulatory or compliance requirements.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of certifications or qualifications required for personnel performing inspections with this checklist.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory reviews of this inspection checklist to ensure continued relevance and accuracy.']
  safety_equipment_required varchar [note: 'Personal protective equipment (PPE) and safety gear required when performing inspections using this checklist.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inspector signature or digital approval is required upon completion of the checklist.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection checklist indicating its availability for use.']
  total_items varchar [note: 'Total number of inspection items or checkpoints included in this checklist.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who originally created this inspection checklist.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for inspection_checklist. Referenced by checklist_id.'
}

Table asset.strategic_initiative {

  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the strategic initiative was completed or closed.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred to date for the strategic initiative in USD.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when execution of the strategic initiative commenced.']
  alignment_to_corporate_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of how the strategic initiative aligns with and supports overall corporate strategic goals and objectives.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the strategic initiative was formally approved by executive leadership or governance board.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for the strategic initiative in USD.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Rationale and business case for the strategic initiative, including expected benefits and return on investment.']
  change_management_plan varchar [note: 'Summary of the organizational change management approach and activities planned to support adoption of the strategic initiative.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the strategic initiative record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the strategic initiative, including objectives, scope, and expected outcomes.']
  environmental_impact_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the strategic initiative by its primary environmental sustainability impact area.']

  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach and coverage area of the strategic initiative.']
  impacted_asset_count varchar [note: 'Number of physical assets (containers, facilities, equipment) expected to be impacted or upgraded by the strategic initiative.']
  initiative_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the strategic initiative, used for reporting and cross-system reference.']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the strategic initiative.']
  initiative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the strategic initiative.']
  initiative_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the strategic initiative by its primary business objective.']
  is_cross_functional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the strategic initiative requires collaboration across multiple departments or business units.']
  key_performance_indicators varchar [note: 'List of measurable metrics used to evaluate the success and performance of the strategic initiative.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent governance review or status assessment of the strategic initiative.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Documentation of key insights, challenges, and best practices identified during execution of the strategic initiative.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the strategic initiative record was last updated in the system.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next governance review or status assessment of the strategic initiative.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the strategic initiative is planned to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the strategic initiative is planned to begin execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority ranking of the strategic initiative relative to other initiatives.']
  program_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the program manager assigned to lead the execution of the strategic initiative.']
  regulatory_driver varchar [note: 'Identification of any regulatory requirements, mandates, or compliance obligations driving the strategic initiative.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment level for the strategic initiative based on probability and impact of potential issues.']
  scope_statement varchar [note: 'Formal definition of the boundaries, deliverables, and constraints of the strategic initiative.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the executive sponsor responsible for the strategic initiative.']
  stakeholder_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of key stakeholders involved in or impacted by the strategic initiative.']
  success_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific conditions and thresholds that must be met for the strategic initiative to be considered successful.']
  technology_platform varchar [note: 'Primary technology platform, system, or solution being implemented or leveraged by the strategic initiative.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for strategic_initiative. Referenced by strategic_initiative_id.'
}

Table asset.budget_line_item {
  budget_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for budget_line_item']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific asset (container, facility, equipment) for which this budget line item is allocated.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent budget or financial plan to which this line item belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for this budget line item expenditure.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific facility (landfill, MRF, transfer station, WTE plant) to which this budget line item is allocated, if facility-specific.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or organizational unit that owns or is responsible for this budget line item.']
  parent_budget_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on budget_line_item (parent_budget_line_item_id)']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capital project or initiative to which this budget line item is allocated, if applicable.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred or contracted vendor associated with this budget line item, if a specific vendor relationship exists.']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount spent or expended against this budget line item to date, based on posted transactions.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the budget line item indicating whether it is in draft, awaiting approval, approved, rejected, or under revision.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role who approved this budget line item.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget line item was formally approved, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  budgeted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned or allocated monetary amount for this budget line item in the base currency.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the budget line item indicating the nature of expenditure: capital investment, operational expense, maintenance, administrative overhead, regulatory compliance, or environmental program.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount that has been committed or encumbered through purchase orders, contracts, or other binding obligations against this budget line item.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'The contract or agreement number associated with this budget line item, if spending is tied to a specific contractual obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget line item record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the budget line item amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget line item expires or is no longer valid for spending, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Null for open-ended line items.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this budget line item becomes effective and spending can commence, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period within the fiscal year (e.g., Q1, Q2, M01, M12, FY for full year) to which this line item is allocated.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget line item applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  general_ledger_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which this budget line item is posted for financial reporting.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line item is currently active (true) and available for spending, or inactive (false) and closed or suspended.']
  is_capital_expenditure boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line item represents a capital expenditure (true) or an operating expenditure (false), used for financial statement classification.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line item represents a recurring expenditure (true) that repeats across fiscal periods or a one-time expenditure (false).']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business justification or rationale for this budget line item, explaining the need, expected benefits, and alignment with strategic objectives.']
  line_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the purpose, scope, and nature of this budget line item expenditure or allocation.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier or sequence number for this budget line item within the parent budget.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget line item record was last modified or updated, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or annotations related to this budget line item for internal reference and collaboration.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this budget line item, used for resource allocation decisions and budget cut scenarios.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned or budgeted quantity in the specified unit of measure, applicable when the line item tracks physical volumes or counts.']
  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The adjusted or revised budget amount after mid-year adjustments, reallocations, or amendments. Null if no revision has occurred.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory or classification within the primary category, providing granular segmentation for reporting and analysis.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned cost per unit of measure, calculated as budgeted_amount divided by quantity, used for variance analysis and cost control.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for quantifiable budget line items (e.g., tons, cubic yards, hours, units, gallons) when the budget tracks physical quantities in addition to monetary amounts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for budget_line_item. Referenced by budget_line_item_id.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key sourced from SAP MM vendor master (LFA1).']
  accounts_payable_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the vendor\'s accounts payable contact.']
  accounts_payable_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the vendor\'s accounts payable or billing contact for invoice and payment inquiries.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the vendor\'s business location or remittance address.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date the vendor was approved and activated in the vendor master, marking completion of onboarding and compliance checks.']
  city varchar [note: 'City name for the vendor\'s business address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary business classification of the vendor based on goods or services provided. Used for spend analytics and vendor segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fleet_parts_supplier|fuel_distributor|ppe_vendor|container_manufacturer|chemical_supplier|equipment_oem|facility_supply_vendor|professional_services|tsdf_operator|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the vendor\'s business address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for vendor transactions and invoices.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet, used for vendor credit checks and risk assessment.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for vendor communication and invoice delivery.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Business fax number for the vendor, if applicable.']
  insurance_certificate_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current certificate of insurance (COI) is on file for the vendor, required for risk management and contract compliance.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s insurance certificate on file. Vendors with expired insurance may be blocked from receiving new purchase orders.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was last updated, used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  minority_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise (MBE) for diversity spend reporting.']
  name_2 varchar [note: 'Additional name line for vendor, used for extended legal names or doing-business-as (DBA) names.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Status of the vendor onboarding process, tracking completion of required documentation, insurance certificates, and compliance checks.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for remitting payment to the vendor.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining the number of days for payment and any early payment discounts (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30).']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating for the vendor based on quality, delivery, responsiveness, and compliance. Used for vendor scorecard and sourcing decisions.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the vendor.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code for the vendor\'s business address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary vendor contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the vendor for procurement and account management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the primary vendor contact.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary contact at the vendor.']
  small_business_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a small business for government contracting and diversity reporting.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the vendor\'s business address.']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'IRS tax classification of the vendor entity, determines tax reporting requirements and withholding rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporation|s_corporation|partnership|sole_proprietor|llc|non_profit|government — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number for the vendor, used for tax reporting and 1099 processing.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal business name of the vendor as registered with tax authorities and used on contracts and purchase orders.']
  vendor_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the vendor, used in procurement documents, purchase orders, and invoices. Unique across the enterprise.']
  vendor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor record. Active vendors can receive purchase orders; blocked vendors cannot transact.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor provides goods, services, or both.']
  veteran_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a veteran-owned business enterprise for diversity spend reporting.']
  w9_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a completed IRS Form W-9 is on file for the vendor, required for tax reporting and 1099 processing.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s corporate website URL for reference and supplier portal access.']
  woman_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is certified as a woman-owned business enterprise (WBE) for diversity spend reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers and vendors providing goods and services to Waste Management, including fleet parts suppliers, fuel distributors (diesel, CNG/RNG), PPE vendors, container manufacturers, chemical suppliers, equipment OEMs, facility supply vendors, professional services firms, and third-party disposal/TSDF operators. Serves as the SSOT for vendor identity, classification, tax information, payment terms, and onboarding status. Sourced from SAP MM vendor master (LFA1/LFB1).'
}

Table procurement.vendor_contact {
  vendor_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor contact record. Primary key.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent vendor organization this contact represents. Links to the vendor master record in SAP MM.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor contact\'s business address (street number and name). Used for correspondence and physical document delivery.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor contact\'s business address (suite, floor, building). Optional field for additional address details.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the vendor contact\'s business address.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact. Inactive or terminated contacts are excluded from active routing and communication workflows.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s role within the vendor relationship. Determines routing and escalation workflows.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the vendor contact\'s business address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or division within the vendor company where this contact works (e.g., Sales, Compliance, Technical Support, Customer Service).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact ceased to be active or left the vendor organization. Null for currently active contacts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contact became active and available for vendor communication. Used for contact history tracking and audit trails.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the vendor contact. Used for Purchase Order (PO) confirmations, compliance notifications, and general correspondence.']
  extension varchar [note: 'Telephone extension number within the vendor\'s phone system for direct routing to this contact.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the vendor contact. Used for formal Purchase Order (PO) transmission and compliance documentation where required.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the vendor contact person.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete formatted name of the vendor contact as it should appear in correspondence and reports.']
  is_authorized_signer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has authority to sign contracts, Purchase Orders (PO), or other legally binding documents on behalf of the vendor.']
  is_emergency_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should be reached for emergency situations such as hazardous waste incidents, equipment failures, or urgent supply shortages.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor relationship. Used for default routing of Purchase Orders (PO) and escalations.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official position or title of the contact within the vendor organization (e.g., Regional Sales Manager, Compliance Director, Technical Support Lead).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the contact\'s preferred language for business communication (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish, FRA for French).']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this vendor contact. Used for relationship management and contact freshness tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this vendor contact record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the vendor contact person.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the vendor contact person.']
  mobile_phone_number varchar [note: 'Mobile telephone number for the vendor contact. Used for emergency escalations and after-hours support coordination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information about the vendor contact, such as special handling instructions, availability constraints, or relationship history.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent procurement matters and vendor coordination.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the vendor contact\'s business address. Used for mail routing and geographic analysis.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred method of communication for routine business matters. Guides procurement workflow routing and notification preferences.']
  secondary_email_address varchar [note: 'Alternate email address for the vendor contact, used for backup communication or escalation purposes.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the vendor contact\'s business address. Uses standard postal abbreviations (e.g., TX, CA, ON).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact\'s primary work location (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago). Used for scheduling calls and coordinating delivery windows.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a vendor record — account managers, sales representatives, technical support contacts, compliance officers, and emergency contacts. Tracks name, title, phone, email, preferred communication channel, and role classification per vendor relationship. Supports vendor communication workflows and escalation routing.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_certification {
  vendor_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor certification record. Primary key.']
  attachment_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document management system record containing the scanned or uploaded copy of the certification, permit, or insurance certificate. Links to document repository.']
  primary_vendor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Waste Management employee who performed the most recent verification. Links to user or employee master data.']
  tertiary_vendor_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this vendor certification record. Links to user or employee master data.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor holding this certification. Links to the vendor master record in SAP MM.']
  certificate_holder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the entity designated as the certificate holder or additional insured on insurance certificates. Typically Waste Management or a subsidiary. Null for non-insurance certifications.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate or permit number issued by the certifying authority. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'A description of the scope, coverage, or limitations of the certification. For EPA TSDF permits, this includes waste types and treatment methods authorized. For DOT hazmat carriers, this includes hazard classes authorized. For insurance, this includes coverage limits and exclusions. For ISO certifications, this includes the facilities or processes covered.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification. Active certifications are valid and in good standing. Expired certifications have passed their expiration date. Suspended certifications are temporarily invalid due to compliance issues. Pending renewal indicates renewal application submitted but not yet approved. Revoked certifications have been permanently withdrawn by the issuing authority. Pending approval indicates initial application under review.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category of certification held by the vendor. Includes regulatory permits (EPA TSDF, DOT hazmat carrier, RCRA compliance, state environmental permits), quality/safety certifications (ISO 14001 environmental management, ISO 45001 occupational health and safety), business designations (MWBE minority/women-owned business enterprise), insurance certificates (general liability, workers compensation, auto liability), and bonding (performance bond, payment bond). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA_TSDF_PERMIT|DOT_HAZMAT_CARRIER|RCRA_COMPLIANCE|ISO_14001|ISO_45001|MWBE_DESIGNATION|GENERAL_LIABILITY_INSURANCE|WORKERS_COMP_INSURANCE|AUTO_LIABILITY_INSURANCE|PERFORMANCE_BOND|PAYMENT_BOND|STATE_ENVIRONMENTAL_PERMIT|LOCAL_BUSINESS_LICENSE — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_gate_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this certification is a mandatory compliance gate for vendor qualification. True indicates that the vendor cannot be approved or used for relevant procurement categories without this certification being active and valid. False indicates the certification is supplementary or optional.']
  coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary coverage limit for insurance certificates or bonding amounts. Expressed in USD. Null for non-financial certifications such as permits or quality certifications.']
  coverage_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the coverage amount. Typically USD for U.S. operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor certification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  deductible_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deductible amount applicable to insurance certificates. Expressed in the same currency as coverage amount. Null for non-insurance certifications.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification becomes valid and enforceable. This is the start date of the certification period.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Nullable for certifications with indefinite validity or one-time designations.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the regulatory body, government agency, or standards organization that issued the certification. Examples include U.S. EPA, state environmental quality departments, DOT FMCSA, ISO certification bodies, insurance carriers, and bonding companies.']
  issuing_authority_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic or regulatory jurisdiction of the issuing authority. Examples include federal (USA), state (e.g., California, Texas), regional (e.g., EPA Region 5), or international (e.g., ISO global).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor certification record was most recently updated. Audit trail field.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was most recently renewed. Null for certifications that have never been renewed.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next certification renewal. May differ from expiration date if renewal must be initiated before expiration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the certification. May include details on restrictions, conditional approvals, or follow-up actions required.']
  policy_holder_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the policy holder for insurance certificates. Should match the vendor legal name. Null for non-insurance certifications.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'The procurement category or service line for which this certification is required or relevant. Examples include hazardous waste disposal, fleet parts, fuel supply, PPE, third-party hauling, landfill services, recycling services. Multiple categories may be pipe-separated if the certification applies broadly.']
  renewal_alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before expiration when the system should trigger a renewal alert to procurement and vendor management teams. Typical values range from 30 to 90 days depending on certification type and renewal complexity.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification was last verified by Waste Management procurement or compliance teams.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the authenticity and validity of the certification. Document upload indicates vendor provided certificate copy. Third-party verification indicates independent verification service used. Registry lookup indicates verification via public regulatory database (e.g., EPA TSDF registry, DOT carrier database). Self-attestation indicates vendor declaration without independent verification. On-site audit indicates physical inspection or audit conducted.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory and business certifications held by vendors, including EPA-permitted TSDF status, DOT hazmat carrier authorization, RCRA compliance certifications, ISO 14001/45001 certifications, minority/women-owned business (MWBE) designations, insurance certificates (general liability, workers comp, auto), and bonding status. Captures certification type, issuing authority, effective date, expiration date, and renewal alert thresholds. Critical for vendor qualification and compliance gating in hazmat and environmental services procurement.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_performance {
  vendor_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor performance evaluation record.']
  primary_vendor_evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who conducted the vendor performance evaluation.']
  sourcing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.sourcing_contract. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations are often contract-specific, measuring vendor performance against specific contract terms and SLAs. This FK enables tracking performance metrics at the contract level, s']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor being evaluated in the vendor master.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor performance evaluation was formally approved.']
  contract_compliance_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transactions that adhered to negotiated contract terms including pricing, payment terms, and service level agreements (SLA) during the evaluation period.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the vendor must complete corrective actions to address performance deficiencies.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor performance evaluation identified deficiencies requiring formal corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor performance evaluation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total spend amount.']
  environmental_compliance_violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of environmental compliance violations or non-conformances identified during the evaluation period. Tracked per U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) requirements.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor performance evaluation was completed and recorded.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments and observations from the evaluator regarding vendor performance, strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance evaluation period.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance evaluation period.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor performance evaluation in the approval workflow.']
  fill_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase order line items completely fulfilled without backorders or shortages during the evaluation period.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of invoices received without pricing errors, quantity discrepancies, or other billing issues requiring correction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor performance evaluation record was last updated.']
  lead_time_days_avg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order (PO) placement to delivery during the evaluation period.']
  next_evaluation_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic vendor performance evaluation.']
  on_time_delivery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for vendor reliability.']
  overall_scorecard_rating decimal(4,2) [note: 'Composite vendor performance score calculated from weighted performance metrics. Scale typically 0-100 or 0-5 depending on organizational scoring methodology.']
  quality_defect_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of received goods or services that failed quality inspection or were rejected due to defects during the evaluation period.']
  response_time_days_avg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of days for the vendor to respond to inquiries, requests for quotation, or issue resolution during the evaluation period.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of safety incidents involving the vendor\'s personnel, equipment, or materials during the evaluation period. Tracked per Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) requirements.']
  total_invoice_count_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of vendor invoices reviewed during the evaluation period for accuracy assessment.']
  total_purchase_orders_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase orders (PO) included in the evaluation period sample for performance calculation.']
  total_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total procurement spend with the vendor during the evaluation period in USD. Used for spend analytics and cost control reporting aligned with Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) metrics.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic vendor classification tier based on performance evaluation results. Determines eligibility for preferred pricing, contract renewals, and sourcing priority.']

  Note: 'Periodic vendor performance evaluation records capturing on-time delivery rate, fill rate, invoice accuracy, quality defect rate, safety incident count, environmental compliance violations, and overall scorecard rating. Supports strategic sourcing decisions, contract renewals, and preferred vendor tier management. Aligned with NWRA and SWANA supplier quality standards. Sourced from SAP MM and manual evaluation workflows.'
}

  material_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material master record. Primary key.']

Business justification: Materials used in operations (PPE, chemicals, supplies) generate specific waste streams requiring proper characterization and disposal. Real business process: waste characterization, disposal planning']
  material_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_group. Business justification: Material classification FK. material.material_group is a STRING code; need FK to material_group.material_group_id for proper normalization. Remove material_group code column as it becomes redundant (c']
  safety_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_program. Business justification: Hazardous materials and PPE procurement requires verification that safety program requirements (OSHA compliance, training, handling procedures) are met. Material master must reference governing safety']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'ABC classification for inventory management prioritization. A = high-value/high-usage, B = moderate, C = low-value/low-usage.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for inventory, ordering, and usage tracking. Common values: EA (each), GAL (gallon), LB (pound), TON, KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter), FT (foot), BOX, CASE, DRUM, PALLET. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|GAL|LB|TON|KG|L|M|FT|BOX|CASE|DRUM|PALLET — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
